comment: Overhated imo, boring lectures and tests are hard but lots of extra credit and I got a decent grade without taking any stats classes in high school. Just give an average amount of effort and you'll be fine. I  just gave a 5 to cancel out all the 1's lol realistically I'd say 3
Honestly, I never went to any lectures and I got a B in the class. Just do the exam reviews and you'll be alright. Not a bad class just horribly organized.

She makes it very hard to take this as an online class. Made us buy a spearate keyboard for the exams, timings for test was 6am-6am 24 hours... 6am? why is that a thing and for a summer class why isn't a weekend long? Excel activities are so annoying She goes out of her way to make it very hard for students. if I could give a -, I would.

This professor makes it very hard to take this as an online class. Made us buy a spearate keyboard for the exams, timings for test was 6am-6am 24 hours... 6am? why is that a thing and for a summer class why isn't a weekend long? Excel activities are so annoying She goes out of her way to make it very hard for students. if I could give a -, I would.
People make it seem like its impossible. Just do outside of class extra prep for exams. Take practice exam and understand each one. Not that hard tbh.

She is not as bad as the other reviews say. Go to lecture to get extra credit for exams, and you get a note sheet on the exams. My least favorite part of the class were the Excel activities, but you will get through them. Pay attention, and you will be fine.

Cant "avoid her at all cost" if she's the only one teaching this class. get the extra credit by attending and grind through the slide deck + review banks on your own time. The lectures are ineffective. Homeworks were tedious but doable. exams were fair. make sure to use AI if you dont understand a concept.
Pam is a ok professor. She is over hated because the class is just hard.

Whenever I brought up this course to someone who took it they always say the same thing, "just get through it, 30305 is so much easier". I feel that her style of teaching style in combination of TA's who don't teach but instead just give the answers on how to fill out the excel activity with the right answers, makes learning the subject all on you.
A quote from the Professor, "I purposefully make the tests different from the practice problems and homework". Who does that? Purdue needs to hire more than one teacher for STATS 30301.
Not a good professor.

Has to be the worst professor at Purdue. She could care less about her students. She intends the class to be hard, and she loves it. The exams are very hard, and the review practice she gives does not help. The fact she has not been removed from Purdue has me speechless. The day she gets fired, Purdue will become a much better campus overall.

I genuinely don't understand why she gets so much hate. Yes the class is difficult but it is nowhere near as bad as the calc classes here. She is not the best lecturer I have ever had but she is well spoken and explains concepts reasonably well. Just put in the effort and you will be fine. She literally gives extra credit for attending class

Pam is a wonderful person. Apart from that, this is genuinely impressive how bad this class is. It is as bad as people are saying, and anyone who says otherwise needs to stop lying. There is no hope. Exams are legitimately bad. Good luck. :)

I'll be honest, shes not as bad as people always complain about. The exams are very difficult, but lots of resources are given to prepare for them. You can get extra credit points just for attending, so make sure to go to the lecture, even if you don't pay attention. You can go through the slides in your own time.
The slides and lecture suck but if you've done stats before it's not that bad. The excel activities are really confusing but they're not on the exams.

Pam isn't the best, but she is fine. So many people claim exams are hard and then brag about the fact they only studied for 15 minutes. Assignments are easy, exams aren't hard at all if you put in the bare minimum amount of studying. Don't listen to reviews. Maybe if people studied instead of drinking 4 nights a week... super easy intro class.

This is hands down the worst class on Purdue's campus. The majority of each lecture class has to retake the course because the second exam is intentionally made to be difficult and "weed out students". I do not understand the value of her teaching method, and am incredibly frustrated with the amount she weighs on these exams.

The lecture slides are organized terribly. She does not elaborate on the content and simply reads off slides.The few times she actually attempts to go in depth on a concept, her explanation only complicates things more. I'm retaking this class, and she still hasn't incorporated feedback. Stats is general is not hard, but she makes it impossible.

genuinely laughable course.
exam review is 6 questions.
best of purdue btw
Not good at all
Cannot teach I ended up having to teach stat to myself

Professor Porterfield is a genuinely kind and caring person. Unfortunately, her tests are EXTREMELY tough, and her lectures aren't very informative. Excel activities get confusing, and there's no curve for the exams, although the average is well within the 60s. Good luck taking this class!
Her lectures are occasionally confusing and can be very boring. The tests are really only ok because you get cheat sheets. Just do the homework and have a really good cheat sheet for exams.

She's a nice person, but she'll teach you the bare minimum when it comes to what is needed for the exam. She'll give a set of review problems, but there's no guarantee that it'll be on exam. She'll also put the most random concepts that she skips past during lecture that would make you think aren't important. All in all, good luck.
Dr. Carter made the lectures really digestible and ensured that every student's opinion mattered in his class. I would highly recommend taking his classes in Purdue.

BCHM 561. Grade heavily based on exams/quizzes, only a couple sets of homework problems. Lot of  content, but 99% of that tested is lecture material and fair game. Carter does a phenomenal job of relaying the content and making it relatable. If you take the time to study and know everything presented to you, there will be no surprises
Great to chat with. Grades relatively easy. This was his first year teaching BCHM 221. Attendance only mandatory for lab. Pop quizzes in lab. No extra credit. No textbook.
Summer class, lots of work, fun experience

Professor Duffy makes you laugh because of how passionate he is about what he does, almost to a point where he needs to stop because of how much he wants to teach us. The class is presentation heavy which can be daunting but it is mostly to convey course content so they are low pressure.

Professor Duffy helped his students to immerse themselves in the class content. He was incredibly helpful and took feedback from students well. The class was very homework heavy for the tight three week timeline.

I did the "AI in Safety" Study Abroad Course with Professor Duffy Summer 2026. He was incredibly caring and cares about student's wellbeing. He is very knowledgeable and I would recommend taking a course with him anyday!

Professor Duffy is great. He helped me be a successful student during the Study abroad trip. I highly recommend taking any of his courses, especially abroad! 100% would take more of his classes in IE.

I enjoyed Professor Duffy a lot. He made lectures entertaining. I always wanted to go to his class and the work load was manageable. I took the "Summer 2026 Study Abroad" course in Rome. This was the best decision and location for a 3 credit class. I 100% recommend taking any of Dr. Duffy's classes at Purdue. He helped me be a successful student.

I took a study abroad course with him. He was a great teacher, always caring if we were following along and about our success in the course. Of course, this is easier in a class of 20-something students, so I don't know how this would translate to a lecture hall but very kind. He was a bit disorganized and didn't always plan deadlines effectively..

Took AI and Safety in Systematic Review in Italy with him, had the best time. Made class time really interesting and fun, lots of presentations and group work, cared about the students and our understanding of the material, passionate about his work, very talented and used textbooks he wrote! Would definitely take a class with him again!
I took a study abroad course with this teacher, and the class was properly balanced with exploring the culture of Italy.
Effective course
Professor gave good materials to learn from.
Good materials to learn from.
Suggested good materials to learn.
Amazing professor, very approachable and helpful!!
Nice guy, good class. The organization of material and expectations could have been better. He knows his stuff and it awesome to hear him teach.
He is a good professor. Clear with what is expected.
Smart guy and good communicator

Good professor who knows the material well. Class can be challenging, but fair and manageable if you stay on top of things. Lectures are clear and organized, and he gives opportunities like extra credit.

Prof. Duffy is a solid instructor who clearly knows the material and wants students to succeed. The course can be challenging at times, but if you pay attention and stay on top of assignments, you'll be fine. Lectures are organized and he explains concepts in a practical way. He also offers opportunities like extra credit, which helps.
Amazing Professor!!
Amazing lectures and resources
Clear and structured
The course management is somewhat lacking in structure. However, overall, I think it is a good class.
Prof. Duffy is one of the most approachable and understanding professors out there. I highly recommend opting for his safety engineering course.
Explains concepts clearly and with engagement. Strong participation component to class but is good about offering extra credit/make up assignments if you do miss class.

I took a look at Monica's ratings on Rate My Professor,   and I would want to check that the students who submitted ratings actually took the class because I did not recognize this professor from the comments.

Easily one of my favorite professors this semester. She is very kind and caring and does a great job teaching the material. While not mandatory, lectures are heavy with content due to the density of the material. Homeworks can be long, but the textbook has an answer key, and exams are fair with many opportunities for extra credit. I'd recommend!
not the worst professor ever but lots of room for improvement. if you want a professor who actually teaches you instead of telling you, maybe look for someone else
Sometimes she would write on the board which was fine. What was not awesome was when she put her notes on the projector and read them to us. Not a great intro to analysis.

I stayed in class up until after the midterm. She said she wants to avoid tedious problems, but that is all the homework is. Her lectures were absolutely boring, and she was adamant about not using electronics in class. She was hard to understand under her mask and accent. The class was so easy, but I hated every second of it.
Lectures have good examples. Homework is a lot of quantity but not difficult. Exams are straightforward and follow the lectures and homework from the textbook.

Pretty average experience overall. Tests aren't too bad, very straightforward and not integration heavy on purpose (she often said this isn't a Calc II class so we don't need to focus on hard integrals). Sometimes there's a lot of homework though.
Her accent was very strong but after a little while, you'll get used to it. Very lecture heavy and sometimes the notation that she uses is different than others that I have seen so beware!

Strong Accent, Writes on board, Lecture was more theory a lot of the time didnt always align with Webassign, 3 matlab projects (in which matlab is never taught in class), grades for exams were never posted online (only handed back in class) so didnt know how you were doing until final grades, no quizzes, not mandatory attendance,

This professor was one of the worst math professors I have had. I couldn't understand her during some lectures (accent) and I always had to get lecture notes online because hers are a mess. She does not prepare you for the difficulty of her exams.
She was wonderfully helpful. Clearly puts in a lot of work into her classes and is always available for discussion after class or during office hours. Very friendly and clearly knows her stuff!

She always shows in class the proof for the methods to be used on the homework, I liked that and though it gave me a better understanding. She was very helpful in scheduling a makeup exam and additionally was useful to me in office hours.  She is good if you like to have a thorough understanding of the math behind the methods you are using.

Terrible lecturer, terrible lesson planner, terrible textbook and exam author, terrible problem choice. Never attempted a single homework assignment herself, assigning all the problems in an incomplete textbook many of which were outside the scope of the section, class, or possibility. Copied textbook onto handwritten sheets and then to the board.

The lecture is not very helpful. She puts up the textbook examples during class and reads off of them, sometimes even photocopies of the actual book pages. She is not very helpful outside of class and gets annoyed with students. She does not allow phones in class.
She is difficult to understand.  She lost my exam, making me almost drop the class, then found in 3 days later. And her office hours are pointless.

With the other comments I was expecting the class to be fairly easy and enjoyable to attend. I have to say I haven't had either. She is very hard to understand with a thick accent, doesn't go over material well at all, and is very impatient with the class and doesn't want to explain answers fully.
She was very good at explaining the material and was always available to answer questions if we had them.

She mostly just rewrites the textbook on paper, puts it on the overhead, and then reads it to the class. The textbook is fairly dense as it is, it takes time to digest the material, and having someone read it to you isn't helpful. She is skipping important sections (differentiation), but still the results. Avoid if you are not strong in Analysis.
I enjoyed her class.  As long as you do the homework and put the effort forth, you should get an A.
She is very entertaining, but at the same time, clearly explains all concepts and wants to see everyone in the class succeed.  She is always open to answering questions and saying funny things.

She didn't tell us much that wasn't in the textbook, and as I didn't understand the textboook to begin with, her classes weren't at all helpful. I could tell other students were confused as well. She was teaching a class geared toward engineers in a thoroughly conceptual, rather than physical, way, which I thought was a mistake.

The summer section of the class is well organized. The lectures are comprehensive, though sometimes you need to look at the textbook for some niche details asked in the homework. Exam is similar to the practice exam, and is open notes. Prof. Baeza also tells you what you need to and don't need to know for the exam.
Philip is outstanding. Nice dude, best prof I've ever had. Anyone would be lucky to have him as a teacher.

MGMT 245, he spends his lectures talking about what seems like everything but excel and on certain assignments he told us "I told my TAs to not help on this homework" which leaves you without a support system sometimes. He also took pride in making the class very hard until the first exam average was a 60%.Its a freshman level class not a 500 level

I had him for MGMT245 but it wasn't an option. Baeza is pretty chill, however I do think a lot of us struggled in the class because this course is basically CS-type hard (im not cs tho so dont @ me) but we were lowkey expecting it to be a basic excel course. the first unit is easy but it advances FAST. make sure u pay attention in lectures. lock in

Phillip is horrible. He reads off the slides like a middle schooler doing a presentation for the first time and proceeds to take points off on your project if you do the same. He also goes on random tangents and doesn't teach almost any relevant material to the exams or homework which makes exams extremely difficult. This class is a waste of time.

**MGMT 310** Outstanding professor who cares and wants his students to succeed. The material in this class is super useful in the real world. Lectures are interesting and have I-clickers in almost every class.

I took 310 with Prof. Baeza, he is one of my fav prof at Purdue, yes the class can be challenging but he makes time for you to actually understand the material. If you can, try to get to know him outside of class!!

He is a great professor. He checks all the boxes. He cares about his students and has very reasonable expectations.  This is the most well-thought-out class and probably the best-taught class I have taken at Purdue. The only drawback is that the material leans towards the boring side, but the guest lecturers and prof B's enthusiasm make up for it.

Had him snr year and despite it only being his second-year teaching at Purdue, he was a great professor. Dr. Baeza is a different level of passionate about his studies, he shares his own research but keeps it interesting and quick. One of my favorite professors at Purdue, you can tell he cares and enjoys what he is teaching about. Highly recommend!
He is the goat. Nothing else to say.

Super nice professor, took his class last semester and he was so helpful and willing to accommodate if I ever had any problems with HW/class. Going to class will definitely help you learn the material and do better in the class, I didn't go the first half but did the second and did significantly better. Overall a decent class and great teacher.

Attendance doesn't really matter as long as you understand the homework and are actually completing it. He curved everyone if they completed every single homework to get at least a C even when failing exams. You also get a cheat sheet for each exam.
Professor Baeza is a kind man. You can tell he is very passionate about his subject.

I can't imagine who else is better at teaching this course other than Drew. His lectures are very clear and the weekly worksheet helps a lot in practicing the materials we learned. Exams are difficult, but the grading is very lenient and they are only ~50% of the total grade. Homework is biweekly and doesn't take long,

Great guy who does the notes in class so you can follow along. Posts supplemental slides. Combination of in-class worksheets and homeworks that aren't too bad. Syllabus grade break-down is in your favor. Liked his dry sense of humor and lectures are very casual.
One of my favorite Stats profs at Purdue, he does an amazing job breaking stuff down for you.

Professor Yarger's course is very well designed. The lectures, assignments, and worksheets are all clear and helpful for understanding the material. The worksheets in particular are well-structured and reinforce key concepts effectively.

Professor Yarger is honestly one of the best stat professors I've had at Purdue. He's really good at breaking stat theory down to plain english, and bringing in a conceptual layer where it would seem otherwise impossible. He's also pretty lively during lectures, promotes asking questions and really does not let it get boring in class.
Good prof, typically makes sense but idk much after the midterm and still got an A.

A large portion of the material I'd finally understand after looking up how to do it while attempting the homework, rather than understanding during lecture. He would often explain some of the simple concepts quite thoroughly while breezing past the difficult concepts, especially near the end of semester. Not a bad professor, but I'd prefer others.

Midterm worth 20% , final 20% and homework 45%. His midterm was like the practice midterm. He has a fun personality and is a chill guy. Topics can be confusing. Homework consists of 5-6 problems. Only 7 homeworks all semester, lowest one dropped. Not a tough grader. He is the only reason I actually went to class.

He is a great guy. very helpful in office hours. lectures are a little messy but you can self learn most of the topics. the grade breakdown was 60% homework and 40% exams. should be very easy to pass. exams are like the practice exam. hes such a chill guy.
Ive done this dance with Mrs fox a couple times now and every time she keeps surprising me on how unorganized and un communicative a professor can be. Do not take for your own sake
As an engineering student most of her lectures could have just been emails. Her class was disorganized but in that mess she'll give you the benefit of the doubt.

Very well organised in terms of laying out deadlines for assignments and tests were very easy due to being multiple choice. Explained the content well enough and without a lot of math. Explains everything well but course has a lot of busywork.

Low-key, take these reviews with a heavy grain of salt. I personally thought she was a great professor who wanted us to do well. She taught well & homework weren't bad, but class was boring at times. It was well structured & the exams are ok. It does cover spoken topics, but it takes extra outside work to understand concepts to prepare 4 exams;

The worst professor I have ever taken at Purdue. The questions she puts on her exams do not correspond with her lectures about 80% of the time. Most of the time, she has no idea what she is doing.  I took a different professor for this class, and they helped me understand Microeconomics so much better. Not sure how she is still employed.
Maxistan deported her for a reason

In the words of One republic "Boy, run, run, run, run, run, yeah, run run run." If the fox catches up to you. Prepare yourself for a semester of blood, sweat, and tears. If your not religious pick one and start praying. But if your fast I repeat Run away, Run fast, and never ever look back!
Just so bad!!! I got a 5 on AP macro and failed this course!!! This class is designed to teach but rather to make you fail. If your seeing this during open registration chose a different prof!!!

The lectures are very important but not very difficult of concepts. There is a no technology policy for the most part, though you are allowed to use iPads. The exams are pretty difficult, reading the textbook is kind of important.

Rigor is one thing; arbitrary difficulty is another. I recommend avoiding this professor; she's on some power trip where she believes that making tests insanely difficult for her ENTRY level class is valid, and it's not. You can understand everything important in macroeconomics and still do poorly on exams since they test random niche concepts.
One of my harder classes I've taken. The exams were short answer and fill in the blank.

When one first goes into college they expect that the whole point is to prove you have learned your skills. One class with Dr. Fox will make you realize that is simply not the case. Exams are designed to make your fail and lectures are simply not helpful. If you practice hard you'll learn but not any info that is useful come test time.

Avoid this professor at all costs!!! If this is a required class and she's the only one available, take it next semester. If that doesn't work switch majors. This Econ class was my 13th 14th 15th 16th… 67th reason why. Hours upon hours of studying. Staying up 36 hours in a row graced me with a D+. I'll be taking my talents to a diffrent prof.

imo: great lecturer, very approachable, always answered questions and explained concepts throughly if something's not clicking in class or office hrs. 

however long you think you'll need to study for a regular exam, double it for this class. exams are insanely difficult relative to other intro classes and there's always grading errors

Lectures are barely relevant and boring since she talks fast. Exams are tough with vague questions and few multiple-choice options, so you need to thoroughly understand your knowledge. She has taught for a while, but her teaching style still weirds me out. HWs and activities are worth little, while exams are worth so so so so much.

Very difficult cumulative final that is like really intense and on so many modules about so many topics doesn't clearly tell us to focus on for anything. SO DIFFICULT TO FIND WHAT ASSIGNMENTS ARE DUE WHEN SHE DOESN'T SAY WHEN HALF THE THINGS ARE DUE.

Don't bother trying to take notes she talks so fast and then will call you out you if you try and use your laptop to take notes. 2nd exam was nothing like the practice exams, a girl sitting next to me during the exam asked the TA if there was a typo because there was concepts that she never mentioned in class
She sucks mucho
Speaks a lot inn lectures goes too fast i think and most of her class are like exam heavy most of the grades are based on exams not that much heavy oriented for participation or hws

She treats you like a child. She's nice in person and I didn't feel that the homework or exams were unnecessarily difficult, but I didn't enjoy her acting like a helicopter parent. She has a strong no laptop policy and has zero issue with yelling at students. Acted like she was the best teacher, but openly admitted to incorrectly teaching content.

I love Dr. Fox as a professor. She is super kind and interesting to talk to. She is one of the best teachers that I've ever had. With that, the grade is 80% exams which is super annoying. The exams are also super hard, but she gives a generous curve. It is hard, but she made me fall in love with econ. If you like econ, take her!

Prof. Fox is ok. She is clearly knowledgeable, but runs her lectures a bit like high school. No laptop, no phone (except iClicker), annoying assignments. The exams are NOT multiple choice, and they are very frustrating. Poor exam grades are common, even with hours of studying. She also basically refuses to give partial credit. Avoid if possible.

This class will have you wanting to take a one way trip to Maxistan and never come back (ifykyk) The course content itself is not hard theres just 1000 hops you have to jump in order to get through it... Hours upon hours of studying graces you with TERRIBLE test scores. She does curve so ill give her that. For your own sake chose a diffrent prof!

Dr. Fox was an amazing lecturer and made the content understandable. A lot of the grade is based on exams and a combined class participation/attendance/homework grade. Overall, the work was not that difficult, and the class was easy, but she is a little blunt and not super approachable.

The class is just so poorly set up. The course is over 75% graded on exam performance and Mrs Fox (yes that was said on purpose) is nowhere to be found during them. TA's had to correct 5 questions mid exam and 2 weeks of vigorous studying graced me with a 62%. Expect for exam grading to take 3 weeks at the minimum.
The lectures were short, but sometimes didn't talk about everything that you were tested on. You're probably supposed to actually read the textbook. Exams are 50% of grade.
he is not helpful and caring at all
*PROFESSOR I HAVE EVER MET!!!!!MY FIRST LOW RATING is for this unwilling at resolving any of your issues or helping with the difficulties you run into, and he is extremely rigid

Prof. Kammen was a good lecturer, and even at 7:30am would teach very well. His exams were incredibly easy, with some questions coming directly from the practice tests, word for word. Exam curves were common. This class was an easy A with him.

Professor Kammen requires attendance at lectures as well as recitation each week. You are given multiple resources to help you do well in the class. If you show up to lecture, do the work, and study for exam's then you should do well. Professor Kammen himself is a nice guy and he is clearly passionate about Econ.

Lectures have mandatory attendance for points. Quizzes weren't super hard, but I relied heavily on recitation to learn content. He tends to teach what he wants and not what is actually important to know. You can tell he really likes what he does, but unfortunately it doesn't make up for how useless the lectures tend to be.

He had lectures every week with lots of Seinfeld references. Quizzes were alright, but super tedious. Some weeks we would have a midterm and quiz in the same week, and that doesn't include the iClicker he does every single class.
Quizzes EVERY WEEK is killer.

anyone critizing him for 360 has a problem with the course imo. He is a great prof and very willing to help. econometrics is supposed to be challenging. no final exam just project. if you follow all advice given in class its not hard to do a good job on it.
exams were very challenging with stuff we didn't expect and a 54% average on the first class. he's nice and wants us to do well but is a harsh grader.
Outstanding professor for ECON 385. Also, one of the most interesting ECON classes I've taken so far.

Teaches what he wants, regardless of what will be on the exams. Quizzes and homework is super easy, in-class discussions are easy yet also pointless, and exams come out of left field compared to lectures. Daily iclicker questions got really annoying really quick. Interact with online textbook to best prepare for exams. Was too energetic for 7:30am.
Very chill guy. i believe he changed the syllabus. Exams are not mandatory and he seems to be open for any help needed, and he is willing to adapt to requests. Great prof.
I fail to understand previous ratings. Anyone would be lucky to have him as a professor.
He wasn't great. Lectures were boring and somewhat hard to follow. Tests were manageable but had to study pretty hard. Definitely would not say I enjoyed this class.

1. The things he taught in class, like Stata is not needed in the homework nor the exams
2. There were only two practice exam questions given. For both exams, there is no cheat sheet. 
3. The Final Project is a massive chunk of your grade, and he grades harshly.
4. You are forced to watch those 2 hours prerecorded lectures on top of going to class

This guy does not teach in class, and will tell you to watch the lecture videos that are uploaded. What's the point of me going to class then? His main objective is to crack jokes. It's been 5 weeks in the semester and not for a single lecture has he taught anything.
Buddy never shuts up but never talks about anything important ever

I hate to give Van Kammen a bad rating as he is a genuinely a good person that clearly cares about students and loves to teach. However, the way the class is setup is terrible. The HW are hard given that he has no examples, the lectures don't relate to much of what we do, and studying for exams is redundant and really just luck of the draw.

Not a terrible professor, but he is pretty awkward and doesn't know how to properly engage the class, especially at 7:30 in the morning. Mandatory attendance with Hotseat questions every lecture was BRUTAL (again because the lecture was at 7:30 in the morning).
Professor Van Kammen is a nice person, but an awful professor. The exams were completely different from the practice and reve

Later homework is challenging and he only goes over it after it's finished. Material is uninteresting. Be careful because practice exams are not as difficult as the actual exams. Lectures can be a bit awkward, he makes a lot of dad jokes. He is enthusiastic but I'm sure you won't be. The curve is generous at least
Funny guy, lots of extra credit/insane exam curves, and fairly reasonable grading.

Professor Kammen is a fine person but he is just an awful professor. Lectures felt basically useless where he didn't teach things that were on the homework and exams were unreasonably difficult in comparison to what was shown in class and on homework. Do yourself a favor and take this class with any other professor.

Professor Van Kammen is a really nice person, but unfortunately, this amazing personality is completely outweighed by the fact that he is completely incapable of teaching. The class structure I was disorganized, and the exams are nothing like the homework and review, which makes studying redundant. Please take a different class if you want to excel
Goated dude
No tests, just show up!

Prof. Kantor was an amazing teacher. When I occasionally see him around campus he still greets me by name. His classes consisted of him giving a lecture on a philosophical topic from the few books he assigned. I didn't read any of the books and still did good. Overall, it is an easy class, and a great professor.

I took Dr. Kantor as my first English professor at Purdue and the class is heavy on him lecturing the class, but he does encourage people to talk and discuss in class. As a professor he is a bit intense and seems unapproachable, however, he does want to help and jokes a lot in class which makes the class entertaining. P.S. You can pass w/o reading

You learn to love him. Very few assignments. Go along with his conversations in lectures, it makes the class fun. Assignments were sometimes hard to find what he was expecting even after studying the syllabus, but I always got good scores.
Assignments were fairly easy, with one research paper as the final and a presentation. Lectures were pretty repetitive, but class was overall easy.

Even though it comes off as a class you will need to focus a lot on, you can get by just fine without putting in a lot of attention. Grading criteria for the few assignments are very clear, and he is very lenient with his grades. It may not always seem like it, but you can tell he hopes for his students to succeed.

He mainly lectures for the whole class so just make sure that you can stay awake during the whole time. He has no problem with lack of speaking up for most people and makes his lectures funny quite often. His grading is also very fair and I didn't have to put in too much time into all the assignments except for the final project.
Honestly a very funny and engaging professor. While subject matter can get slightly boring at some times he is very fair and helpful.

The class was pretty easy, just bi weekly journals and a research paper. Kantor was funny, engaging, and personable. Made me genuinely interested in topics. Sometimes books we were told to read were barely covered though.

Honestly I loved Dr. Kantor. There were biweekly journals we had to do, but it was just over our thoughts about the lectures and readings. It was difficult at times because we discussed a lot of philosophy based content, ut also was super interesting. Prof really cares about all the students and has a funny sense of humor, once you catch on :)

If you show up to class, and make a solid attempt on the homeworks, you'll get at least an 80. Do well enough on the quizzes and you have a great grade. However, nearly native hebrew speakers in the class will be in, and speed up, the course so if you are a first time learner as myself, you will find yourself constantly in a state of catching up.

Kantor was not a bad professor by any means, easy to get a good great if you put in the work. He's just absurdly boring, the class just consists of him talking about his own philosophical beliefs and we probably talked about the exact same thing 30 times over and over again.

Prof. Kantor was really passionate about the material that he gave lectures on. He was very knowledgable aobut the subjects and was able to convey the information in an engaging way. As long as you put effort in on the few assignments and turn them in on time, you should do well in his SCLA 101 class (the few assignments are weighted heavily).

Professor Kantor's class is fairly light in writing with only a couple of journal entries and a large end-of-the-year research paper. He cracks a fair amount of jokes in class which helps with lengthier lectures. Additionally, he is very responsive to emails and questions in and out of the class. Overall, is a nice guy.

Professor Kantor is a really great person and a lecturer, using popular culture references and silly anecdotes to keep the class engaged. Compared to other SCLA101 sections, this class is not essay heavy and has few opportunities for gradings. If you enjoy philosophy, Professor Kantor will not disappoint.

The class felt like a joke, I didn't even read more than a quarter of the assigned readings and got a 100 on every CS. The lectures were extremely boring, and the presentations were "talk for the minimum amount of minutes and get an A+." TLDR: Easy credit, but boring.

Professor Kantor is super nice and one of the most approachable professors I've had thus far. He keeps his lectures interesting by throwing in the occasional joke or modern reference and moves from point to point at a moderate speed. This class requires a decent amount of writing and a few speeches, but other than that it is quite manageable.

Super nice professor to have and he is incredibly knowledgable in many subjects which is awesome so I learned much more than just Hebrew in the class. Most important thing to succeed in the classes are to know you vocabulary! But that is a given in a language course.

Professor Kantor is a great person and is very caring. I wouldn't say that he's the greatest at teaching a language. Classes don't have a strict structure and cracking jokes, as long as he's included, is always welcome. Basically, be ready to teach yourself out of a workbook but have a good laugh in class.
Good professor,  does a very good job of making the material easy to understand. TA's are also very helpful and grade very fairly, they don't just hand out A's, but are very fair.

Definitely a fun class. I would say I learned some hebrew, but it wasn't the most 'serious' language course I've ever taken. If you want a fun course with a cool professor, mixed in with a difficult schedule, take this class. I would call it an easy A.
No Comments
The material is hard, but the study guides are amazing. Kenji is amazing

Kenji is a great professor and he makes a 400+ person lecture hall feel like a classroom. He teaches concepts well, but the exams for Calc 2 are brutal and the averages were in the 60s for each. Make sure to get all of the homework and quiz points that you can and attending lecture can stop you from falling behind. Good luck.
Best Math prof at Purdue! Take 165/166 if you can!!!!!!
Absolutely class prof. Make sure to attend lecture tho.

Had him for MA165 and MA 166 he is a dog. As long as you study his study guides and watch his in class lecture videos you will get a good grade. Definitely the best professor I have had at Purdue. I would let him have my children.
I luv u Kenji Matsuki

Honestly I dont know why this prof hypes up his study guide so much. Not that its bad but like I solved 1/4th of it before the exam and i think I did quite well without going thru the entire thing. Great prof tho, lectures are amazing and concepts are taught clearly. Curious to know from other folks why he over-hypes the study guide.

One of the best teachers ive ever had. 165 is an incredibly unfair and unnecessarily difficult course, but Kenji really cares about helping you succeed. He puts some of the exam questions on the study guide and if you ask for help he refuses to let you leave w/out understanding the concept. Lectures are hilarious, the guy has no filter at all

He is a good teacher who tries his best, just do the work and pay attention you will do well. You can tell he cares about his students and class by entertaining because he often make jokes, and also sometimes gives questions that will be on the test in class.

Kenji is a fantastic professor! He cares for his students and truly wants them to succeed. During the lecture, he always makes jokes to make the content more engaging and is very funny. He teaches the content well. If you do the homework and exam study guide, you will perform extremely well. The content is similar to Calc AB, the class is harder.

To succeed on the exams, simply go through the study guide and do every problem in good faith, then circle back to the problems you found most challenging. 
His lectures are incredible and he has a way with words - don't miss the lectures.

he doesnt explain things well and i always left lectures more confused than when i came in. i eventually stopped showing up to lectures and just watched chenflix instead and saw an improvement. the exams are unnecessarily difficult, be ready to study excessively, theyre harder than the hw's or quizzes.
Great professor, simplifies concepts and gives a good curve :)

Purdue math is notoriously difficult, but having Kenji as your professor helps a lot. He is entertaining and it is easy to pay attention to his lectures. His study guides are essential for the exams, but be prepared for the exam average to be around a 65 no matter how hard you study. If there's a way for you to have Kenji for Calc 1 or 2, do it!

I loooooove Kenji! His lectures are really entertaining and he explains topics really well. The homework and quizzes are a bit tedious, but good for points. His study guides, on the other hand, are insanely clutch--do everything in them at least once! Also attend his lectures before exams, the problems he reviews are really helpful!

Kenji is a very engaging and fun math professor. He's clearly very passionate about the math concepts he's talking about, and tries to make lectures as fun as possible. Sometimes, his explanations are vague or too simplified, but he makes up for it with very clear study guides and homework assignments. Also cares a lot about student success.

Great professor. It is possible to do well in the class without attending lectures but going to them makes the course so much easier. He makes sure that classes are engaging and everything that we learn in class is on the test. The study guide is a godsend and as long as you do that and the other homework you should get at least a B.

Very interesting professor. He is using his tenure to his full potential. Very funny and engaging during lectures, and pays attention to the needs of the students. His exams are fairly difficult, but the study guide he provides encompasses the entirety of the exam. Weekly commitments include 2-3 homeworks and one quiz that can be retaken infinitely

Kenji is an amazing lecturer and professor, but there is a huge time investment required to excel in this course. The problems he creates for exams are very difficult but the study guides make them fair. Be prepared to spend over 20 hours on them since there are probably over 100 problems on there. He does post them weeks in advance though.

He is my favorite professor! He consistently pushes me to be the best student I can be. I struggled at the beginning of the semester until I went to his office hours, it made things clearer. Treat him with respect and would definitely recommend for those taking MA 165. He is definitely one of my favorite people at Purdue and he is brilliant! 10/10!

Matsuki provides study guides for each exam, but what you see on the tests will 100% test your working knowledge. As someone who did well in BC calc, be prepared to work hard; this is a top 10 engineering school, no?

Took calc AB and BC in high school and thought I knew what I was doing.... until I took Kenji calculus. This man makes calculus the hardest thing to understand. Exams are hard for no reason.. His study guides are 200+ pages long, so good luck finding 40 hours to study for a midterm. And kenji used AI to write the final. Make this make sense kenji.

hes the best. super funny and explains things well, definetly go to office hours if you are struggling. hes one of the only professors ive had that really cares about office hours. and he makes study guides so the exams r super light.
Difficult integral… Matsuki has been here.

This dude has some good lectures and study guides, but he has flipped me off almost every day. No professor has ever done that to me but this guy does it almost everyday. Goated and hilarious prof tho. Must take if you get the chance. 
I could also say that besides the study guide solve the past exams and boiler exams as well. Just dont tell him.
Great class, loved him.

I enjoyed his vibe while teaching, but was defiently a tought grader. learned a lot but had to work a lot harder to get a good grade in the class than other SCLA professors. Required to have physical books and I'd think the class would be pretty hard if you were not actually reading them. Consider a different professor if you just want an A.

Professor Bennett, unlike most professors at Purdue, has a great personality and shows a keen interest towards teaching his classes. If you come with the mentality of wanting to learn, you would enjoy this class very much. The lectures, too, are fun as the professor brings in a positive energy everyday. Great guy!

Prof. Bennett was a very engaging professor to take class with. He was open to all sorts of ideas no matter how absurd they were. I will admit, if you're taking SCLA101 for the credit and only care about grades, this prof is not for you. However, if you're willing to put in that extra work to read and analyze, his class is very rewarding.

He has his own interpretations of the books being read, and says he values “pushback” and “alternative viewpoints”, but then instead of supporting forming your own views, he tries to disprove them to make his point “correct”. 

This makes people not want to even consider having their own thoughts, which is the whole purpose of being a teacher
good at lecturing, clear on expectations at every point in the course
He's the goat. I usually hate English/Literature classes but his enthusiasm, dedication, and passion for this study makes class SO much better. Love this guy

Professor Bennett is passionate about the course readings and curious about his students' ideas. He is a pretty jumpy guy and brings a positive energy to class. Make sure to read and attend class. If you stay on top of the workload and complete it in good faith, you will be fine.

Change proffessors. There is too much work in this class for no readon whatsoever. There are many other scla proffessors that will not give you this much work to do. SCLA should not be that much work. He is a very tough grader. He is a good person person though.
Love his teaching, he is just a bit strict at times, and is a pretty tough grader.

Really cool guy! would love to take another class from him again, a lot of homework and reading so be prepared for that. Group project is fun. I find he is a forgiving grader for small mistakes as long as you don't make to many. Definitely take his class!

Prof. Bennett was very fun to have in class. He encouraged a lot of in-depth discussion on the books we read. He especially encourages you to come to him with your own analysis and he will listen to you earnestly. If you are taking SCLA101 for credit for, say, engineering, I'd recommend avoiding him however because he grades somewhat harshly.

Professor is very intense. I just need to say that he showed me a deep kindness and understanding as I was going through a tough time mentally during the semester. I would take his class again if I could.
the no-device policy--while unusual--is fair and reasonable. the professor is by far the most caring one I had so far  purdue.

This guy makes the class so much harder than it should be. It's a 101 english class and he treats it like a PhD class. He will also probably falsely accuse you of using AI when you didn't, and he is extremely against any form of technology. Definitely take this with another professor .

Hardest grader I've had at Purdue so far. It might be a 101 class, but he grades like we're publishing a doctorate paper. Also, he's creepy and makes going to class such a drag. Do not take his class switch immediately
He will make you WORK for a grade in a 101-level class. I would avoid if you could. Good guy, bad grader.
Prof. Bennett is an amazing professor. I would definitely recommend him for SCLA 10100 if you're passionate to learn a lot.

It was a lot of work, but the prof. is fun to listen to and he's always willing to help.

This professor is genuinely one of the sweetest I have ever had. He is extremely understanding, responds to emails quick, and you can tell he is passionate about his work. DR NICK IS THE BEST. My favorite prof I've ever had, I genuinely look forward to his class. He is funny and makes things easy to understand. If you have a question and email him, he will explain until you understand! Actually the GOAT

his lectures are fire, he explains really well and his classes arent boring

Dr. Nicholas George Lohr, AKA the Prince of Tippecanoe, was a historic signing by the Purdue Math Department; if the Big10 had a linear algebra league, Dr. Lohr would undoubtedly lead Boilermaker Mathletes to win more championships than our football team wins games. Really great lectures, explained all the concepts really well
Even as a math major, he is by far the best math teacher I've ever had. Insanely funny, knows his stuff, and gives easy to understand lectures. Can't emphasize it enough.

Professor Nick is excellent! He does a great job at making lectures engaging and easy to understand. He understands when topics are very abstract and gives as many examples and analogies as you need. He also makes an effort to get to know the students in his class and memorize all their names.

This professor cannot be glazed hard enough. He could lead Purdue football to a Big10 championship if given half a chance. Undisputed goat. Dr. Lohr > LeBron.

Is not the coordinator for the course, so I cannot blame her for the assignments/exams or class difficulty. But as a professor, always gave very relatable examples to understand more complex topics. Professor Diyab makes distasteful "jokes" in lecture that stereotype women as being vain/bad at math. She spends 20-30 mins "joking" at the start of class, so we were always very behind. I took CS159 online this summer. Professor Diyab was very kind and cared about her students. Her lectures were consistently clear and easy to understand.

Class overall sucks because exams are super hard and expect you to be a calculator. For mandatory lectures she was pretty helpful and if you need to take this class I think she is better than the other options. She is super sweet and probably one of the best professors for CS159. She cares about how her students do, gives great lectures which are also posted after, and answers questions well in class, and is easily accessible after lecture.

Course taken Spring 2024. Overall, professor Diyab just talked a lot and didn't seem that interesting at all, but maybe that was just me. Very nice professor, and was very understanding when I was too sick to attend a lab.

Prof. Diyab definitely gave a lighthearted and calming feeling to a tough/intimidating class like CS 159. Professor Diyab does her best to make CS 159 engaging and her lectures have a lot of student interaction. She also does example problems at the beginning of every class very thoroughly, so taking good notes and paying close attention will take you far in this class.
Professor Diyab was really fun! She made the content pretty enjoyable to learn, definitely recommend taking her class whether you know programming or not.

I loved professor Diyab! She was so caring and made C easy to learn, although I did have prior coding experience. Everyone told me this class would be so hard but I couldn't disagree more. She is such a sweet lady and hands out chocolate and candy if you answer questions right in lecture.

Probably the most caring professor I've ever met. She is really good at breaking everything down into more general examples, so absolutely recommend if this is your intro to coding. Lectures can feel slow if you know what you're doing, but that's better than being lost. Great help in office hours, USE THEM if lost or you will fall behind.

Professor Diyab certainly can make lecture engaging and fun with her stories and jokes of the beginning of lectures. It does definitely help transitioning towards a heavy lecture. However, beware that this CS 159 class intends to see in the perspective of an interpreter and compiler rather than building good programming skills and standards.

This class was pretty hard, and Prof. Diyab didn't help to make it that much easier. She had some good explanations at times, but overall, some of the lectures were long and a little confusing. Be ready to study a ton for the exams because they are the majority of your grade and they are really hard.

Professor Diyab did a great job explaining the basics of CS159 and always included participation in the class.  She really cares for your grade and understanding of the C language, and sets you up well to succeed in the class.

Eman Diyab is a very good choice for CS159. She does a very good job explaining the fundamental concepts of C, and she gives in class lecture quizzes for help. Make sure to show up and do the homework, and you'll be fine. Overall, the class was a pretty good experience, and she had generally very good lectures.

Explains concepts very well, although can be quite slow at times. The class is pretty easy if you have programmed before and has few assignments, although the tests can be difficult due to the more advanced conceptual questions.

I was afraid of CS159 based on the comments I had heard from other people, but even though this course was definitely not a piece of cake, I think Dr. Diyab made it so much easier than I expected. She cares so much about her students and is always willing to help. She even gives candies to students who participate in lecture!

While CS 159 may be a terrible course that should be avoided, if possible, Diyab's a safe bet at getting through it. Does a great job at explaining examples in class, most which did pop up on exams. A great professor. I took the summer online course and there were a lot of lectures (to watch on your own time) but they were very interesting. There were just three short writing assignments and three exams that were all easy and he gave great feedback and graded easily.
I mean... he teaches a rock and roll class... Does anything else need to be said?
This dude is the best. Take 371

Amazing teacher. Three homework assignments throughout the class that aren't too time-consuming. His lectures are worth going to.

I took Hist152 as a student that has had very formal treatment of American history before hand but Dr. Atkinson, through his humorous and passionate "sermons," helped increase my appreciation and love for the subject. Come to class, take notes, connect your ideas, and have fun! Dr. Atkinson takes the effort to get to know his students.

I took HIST 152 as part of Purdue's Early Start program. I could not have asked for a better first class and professor at Purdue.

Great professor, hilarious guy. My favorite class I've ever taken at Purdue. While attendance isn't mandatory, you should go to the lectures as you will fall behind if you skip. One of the best faculty members in the history department. Through his lectures you can really tell how passionate he is about history! His TA this semester was great as well, every exam and the paper for the class always had great feedback.

HIST 371 was the best decision I've ever made. Professor Atkinson was absolutely hilarious and charismatic. I seriously wish his classes were twice the length so I could listen to him speak. Professor Atkinson obviously cares about his students and makes an effort to connect with them, and he also has a great sense of humor.

Take good notes during lectures (which is easy because they're so interesting) and you'll basically know how to most of the classwork without studying. Definitely the best class I've ever taken at Purdue, and it's mostly thanks to Professor Atkinson and his incredible charisma. He makes you wish power hours were longer so you can keep listening.

Dr. Atkinson is God's gift to Purdue. His lectures were really interesting and he is clearly passionate about the subject. There are few grades but if you do the reading and put in a good amount of effort you'll be fine. One of the best classes I've ever taken at Purdue!

Professor was great! Honestly, he was very fun and engaging. The workload was really manageable, but make sure to do the reading reflections because there are so few assignments, they will hurt your grade.
Professor Atkinson is the best! I actually go to his lectures because they are interesting, and he is an amazing lecturer. Exams are easy if you prepare and only three assignments the whole semester.

Professor Atkinson restored my faith in professors at Purdue. I was an FYE student at the time and didn't enjoy any of my professors. Luckily, my SCLA teacher was Prof. Atkinson who had thought-provoking class discussions, added to my understanding of classic literature, and made me enjoy my time in class.

Atkinson is clearly passionate about rock and roll and enjoys lecturing. His dry sense of humor makes every lecture very entertaining. 3 very short writing assignments and 3 essay exams. Possible exam questions provided. Write out answers and memorize them. Morrison left large shoes to fill and Atkinson does an excellent job continuing his legacy.

David is AMAZING. I have never enjoyed lectures more in my life. You have to take GOOD notes, but it's really fun to listen to and therefore easy to remember because it's so fascinating. HOWEVER. The exams are weighted really heavily, so if you're taking this as a gen-ed like I am, take it pass or fail. It makes the class much less stressful.

Professor Atkinson was my favorite Purdue professor. 5 years later he still remembers my name and we swap updates. His lectures are interesting and relatable. Provided the opportunity, I would take every one of his classes.
Great Teacher, Amazing lecturer

Atkinson is extremely knowledgable. He always finds a way to make the material relatable and interesting. I never wanted to miss a class because the lectures were so interesting - plus his dry sense of humor made the lectures that much more enjoyable.

Atkinson can grade difficultly on exams and papers of the few he assigned, but overall he was an excellent professor that made class material interesting and made it so that I wanted to go to class each day. I would definitely take another one of his courses given the chance.

He is an exciting lecture, engaging, and make his class interesting. He proctored class discussions that were actually interesting. The class consisted a two essay exams but there were not difficult even without reading the text books. My favorite aspect is that even after a year and a half, he still remembers my name when I passed him in a hallway

SCLA 102. Very passionate professor, but somewhat too passionate. Way too much reading, while she acts as if she's giving you the bare minimum. Does not give anything higher than an A- in participation, so be ready. Great person, but manages to make a GPA booster difficult. Avoid taking this class with her.

I had her for my SCLA 102 class; she is one of the best professors I ever had. Very friendly to students, and brought up good discussion topics that were very thought-provoking. Definitely take her classes.

Dr. Irvine is very passionate about what she teaches. She will do everything she can to help you succeed in her class. She will not send out notes or lecture slides, but she will take the time to help you find a way to pass the class.

She is very fast-paced and will not send out slides or let you take pictures of them, but she is an amazing professor. There is some homework but it is the same format every time so it gets easier. She is very personable and takes the time to get to know you and genuinely cares. She is extremely charismatic and keeps you engaged.

He's a great professor. It's clear that he wants his students to succeed. Don't be afraid to email him for help, he's super responsive and will go above and beyond to help you. He's actually really kind. His lectures are fun as well! 270 ended up a cakewalk. He's great.

Zuel is a great guy, and it was clear that he took extra care to make sure his lectures were interesting, and more importantly, applied well to career-related stuff. 270 is most of the Sec+ exam and some of Net+, so it's great prep. Lab section started rocky but got much better once I learned the ropes and grading patterns (CHECK THE RUBRICS!)

Ok professor. He mostly reads off the slides, and lectures don't always match the labs, which is annoying. His course policies are good. He allows resubmission of the first lab report, offers plenty of extra credit, and grades assignments quickly. No mandatory attendance, but some in-class work requires attendance.

Pretty mid professor. He mostly reads off the slides, and lectures don't always match the labs, which is annoying. His course policies are good. He allows resubmission of the first lab report, offers plenty of extra credit, and grades assignments quickly. No mandatory attendance, but some in-class work requires attendance.

Pretty mid professor. He mostly reads off the slides, and lectures don't always match the labs, which is annoying. His course policies are good. He allows resubmission of the first lab report, offers plenty of extra credit, and grades assignments quickly. No mandatory attendance, but some in-class work requires you to be there.
Reads off slides, isn't very present throughout the course, and doesn't respond to emails unless you send a follow up.

I was not that bad bro. Yes labs are kinda tough but it does get better. Highly recommend spending each day working on your lab cause the reports actually can't be done at the last minute. As for the teacher, he is a nice dude. He does read of the slides and there is pop ALA's but the content is not that hard. you will be fine, trust.

He's an AI Bro. He loves LLMs and will find a way to shove them into every lecture and every assignment. Instead of teaching us how a powershell script that he used for an in class demo worked he instead taught us his Claude prompt wrote to generate the script. I am convinced that he's forgotten every piece of knowledge he ever learned in college

He just reads off his slides and does not go into detail much. He has many ALAS. There have been occasions which he assigns them and does not tell the class which unless we look, we would have no clue. The lab assignments are hard and are not very in-depth with how their manuals are written and doesn't much instruction.

No attendance mandatory, does a lot of ALAS but they are fun to do. Will never regrade. His TA, Shetty, is the worst try to avoid him at all costs. He will never reply to emails and will not help you in labs. Tough grader and will not give you clear feedback even in person. The professor is always on the TA side. Just stay away from this TA.

attendance not required but some activities missed if you don't come. LABS R SO MUCH WORK. they have strict guidelines and are confusing. manuals and rubrics don't align. you spend hours trouble shooting to only get 50% on labs because it didn't work for you. Overall if you put your blood, sweat, and tears you can get the A. Exams are online.
I really liked him, his lectures were easy to understand. 270 labs just get a good lab partner. From all 200 classes in CNIT, easiest and most fun class.

Zuel was a pretty good lecturer and pretty chill. Would never lecture for the entire 50 minutes. The labs at first were kind of hard to get through, but they got easier as the semester went along and were actually kind of fun. There is a ton of extra credit too.
Great professor, ended this class with over an A+ because of the extra credit.
very rude and hard!! never choose
Class overall was pretty easy. Plenty of extra credit. The labs take a while but as long as you don't procrastinate you'll be fine. Lectures weren't mandatory but were still good to go to.

Took 270 during Professor Zuel's First semester teaching this course. Definitely not as hard as 242 but Professor Zuel and his TA's made this class really hard to navigate. Attendance is not mandatory but his system of taking exams was really stupid as it was open note and open browser (fully online) but we had to take it in our 7:30 am lecture
Dr. Wei is highly knowledgeable, patient, and compassionate. He cares about students' needs and is always willing to provide additional office hours to support them.

Great instructor. Great course on Tableau. Helpful for a beginner like me. The assignments are useful for learning visualization skills. The only downside is that the professor had to spend quite some time disciplining my class.

It would be helpful to focus more on course material and limit unrelated discussions. Comparisons like “even my daughter's teachers find her class more attentive” can be demotivating and may not contribute positively to the class atmosphere. Additionally, spending over 20 minutes addressing discipline may not be necessary.

Professor Wei did a good job delivering the content, which I find useful. I did not know Tableau before the class. After the semester, I'm pretty comfortable using it. Professor Wei was always willing to answer my questions. I understand that all Krannert courses are subject to the Krannert curve thing, which may have pissed off some students here.

Professor was a good professor and did a good job covering content in class. The class itself was super easy too. He imposed the Krannert Curve on the day grades were due which rightfully pissed a lot of students off since the cutoffs were extremely harsh - which I guess is a consequence of the course being so easy. But overall, he did a great job.
One of the best professors I have take courses with at Krannert. Useful content. Willing to work with students.

Professor Wei is one of the best professors I've had for the MGMT 479 class. However, the grading does not make sense. A lot of people did the HW and Exams wrong but not penalized. Although this is good to some extent, the lack of penalty for those mistakes means that the Krannert Curve will destroy grades. A is almost impossible due to this.

MGMT 382 is a blend of technical skills like systems development and databases as well as traditional business topics like marketing and economics.  Prof. Wei gives engaging lectures, but be prepared to write down things he says verbally, as they show up on exams even though they never appeared in the lecture slides.  He gives you help if you ask.

Took online over summer. Literally a waste, and I mean a waste, of time. The entire course is done on Connect: HW, quizzes, exams, etc. No actual interaction with the professor and the material is literally so boring that you will have trouble learning it. Complete waste of tuition money and a grand reflection of Krannert. Have fun.

Zaiyan is probably one my favorite professor I've had at Purdue. While attendance and participation is required, he makes his lectures interesting and engaging so it doesn't feel like a chore. There's three group projects but no homework. Overall, I really enjoyed this class and the professor.
He is so nice and professional. We dont have hw and his lectures were clear, organized and easily to follow. I will take his class again if I have a chance. Go take his classes.
He is a fantastic professor, a lot of material to cover for exams.

Great teacher, really funny, you must pay attention in the lectures and take good notes to do well. No need for a textbook, but everything he says is up for game on the tests. However, He is a tough grader
One of the best professor I have ever had in Krannert. Good lectures. clear assignments, and cares about students

He is willing to meet with students or correspond over email. His projects are clearly laid out and he is willing to help explain. He asks questions at the beginning of each class that cover the last class; these question will be a lot like what is on the test. The final is not cumulative. He is nice and his class is easier than Dr. Dee's.
One of the few classes I have enjoyed thus far in my Purdue University MSAI program. Great course, great feedback. I would enroll in another course with Dr. Naimi without hesitation.
Dr. Naimi is an awesome professor. Truly cares about her students and will work with you if need be. Definitely recommend fellow students to take a class with her if they get a chance.

Dr. Naimi has great knowledge about leadership theory and it's application across multiple fields. On top of this, she truly cares about her students and regularly goes the extra mile to address their concerns and ensure that they understand course material.
Dr. Naimi is the best! Her classes are great and she's incredibly nice. She always goes the extra mile to help her students.

Fantastic person. Good Professor. Make sure that you go to all her lectures, and actually get to know her. Not only is she an insightful person, but she is very flexible with grading if you can show her that you've put in the work and effort necessary to succeed!

Dr. Naimi is a superb professor. She is intelligent and extremely knowledgable. She is more than helpful and caring and has a unique teaching style. She is engaging and makes you think critically. She wants all of her students to be successful and does everything in her power  to make that happen.
Dr. Naimi is a very professional and interesting individual.  She is always willing to help a student with anything. I had no problem passing the class with a little effort on my part.

Dr. Naimi is an award winning professor and one of the best professors I have ever had at Purdue. She is fair, caring, intelligent, and very helpful. She is also an attorney and helps us with legal questions and problems.

Dr. Naimi is the best professor I have ever had BY FAR! She is kind hearted and fair. The class is easy as long as you take it seriously. She takes a personal interest in every student and has an unusual but fantastic teaching style. The ethical dilemmas that she poses really help the students to learn more about their own ethics.
She dosent explain anything..

Professor Galarza grades very fair and helps students when they need help. She talked about how her time as a student motivated her to get a degree in teaching and this shoes. Anytime you have grade disputes you can go up to her and she provides ample help and feedback. I highly recommend Professor Galarza for anyone looking to take ENGR 132.

She is a nice person, and the class is not difficult. However, it is quite badly managed, with almost no communication about logistics or assignment expectations. Instructions for the team project are scattered across various documents. Grading is somewhat picky but if you have reasonable attention to detail it should be fine.

One of the most caring profs I've had at Purdue. I remember near the end of the semester she told me that my smile genuinely brightened her day. Said at the beginning of the semester that she would always take our side no matter what. Genuine unconditional kindness that you don't see often elsewhere (but should).

ENGR 131 isn't a hard class. It's not too exciting either, but Prof. Galarza did her best to make classes kinda fun or have us practice an engineering soft skill (teamwork). 
Prof. Galarza is one of the nicest, most caring professors at Purdue. She also bumped my A- up to an A because I asked (the FYE 3.2 GPA, iykyk), because I got to know her.

Professor Galarza is super caring and always makes sure to asks students how they are doing. She is also very helpful when answering students' questions. The class curriculum itself is slow and tedious though so I would recommend taking ENGR 133 over ENGR 131 if you're taking a lighter course load.

Probably the nicest professor I have ever had. She cares so much about all of her students and is willing to help everyone. She has no problem giving points back to students if they ask and she is always willing to give good feedback and listen to problems you may have in this class.

I am very grateful to have gotten Professor Galarza as my ENGR 131 professor. She very obviously cares about her students education and their future. The classes consist mainly of review of pre-lecture videos with group work after.

She might be one of the most caring professors at this university. She's always available for her students and she truly wants you to succeed. ENGR 131 wasn't a hard class but I had a 7:30, and I'm really thankful that I had her class because I didn't often dread getting up to go to it. She also gives you a lot of studio time to work on projects.

ENGR 13100 I think is a pretty tedious class because the course fits a lot into a few months. Dr. Galarza made the class so much more tolerable. She is a sweet and supportive teacher and is the first person at Purdue who made me feel like I belonged. She makes effort to know all of her students personally and really cares about their well-being.
Lovely person, so supportive and kind. Will happily explain anything you're struggling with, gives a lot of extra credit opportunities

ENGR131 itself is tedious but she helped by giving extra credit opportunities and solving any doubt from the assignments or projects, exam criteria is unclear but she is fair when reviewing exams and if you explain why you deserve the points for an unclear question, she gives at least some or all of them. I'm taking her ENGR132 class next semester.

She is very helpful if you have any questions you wanna ask directly in person. Her lectures are boring but I don't know who could possibly make this class any less boring. She is bad at answering emails though. I still recommend!
Boring, rambling lectures reading straight from slides. Accent not bad at all. Recommend someone else for 131.

Great class! Always fun but with a lot of useful concepts discussed. Group projects very useful, although at times wish we could pick our teams. Feedback provided on every project allows us to keep improving as future professionals.
He makes his lectures very engaging, the group projects are very fun but you do not get to choose your group, and there are lots of assignments to make up the grade.
Love this professor, I signed up to take his 325 course as well. Love that class too. 10/10 would recommend.
Great guy. I am taking his next class. Entr 325. Excited to be in his class again!
Always accesible and a fun class to attend.

Jorge cared about how we learned in his class. He wanted us to get to see all different kinds of entrepreneurs, and it was helpful to hear how they all got to the place that they are now. The one beef I do have with the class is how much the group assignments effect your grade. Good luck with a bad group because you're going to do all the work.

Genuinely my favorite professor at Purdue. I took this course to satisfy a business elective but actually had no interest in the topic before the class. His passion and teaching style made me more interested in the topic and enjoy a class that I expected to hate. He is fun and passionate about the topic which helps keep students engaged

Jorge is a great teacher and made class a good experience. I really appreciated that if he called on you randomly he didnt pressure you to guess the answer if you didn't know it. Jorge was great at
making the class a joint experience and trying to engage everyone in discussion

Professor Romero-Day has a ton of experience doing real entrepreneurship and his connections made for really good and insightful guest speakers in class. He does grade projects quite harshly and you need to make sure to follow his instructions to a T, or else you will lose points.
Very funny and interesting discussions during his class! Gives plenty of cool extra credit opportunities also

If you're not a business major or thinking about becoming one, don't take this class. Jorge is nice and cares about students but the projects are difficult and he grades hard. I took this to fulfill a pre-med requirement and I definitely felt out of place. I never really understood what was going on and the group projects get really annoying.
Easiest A as a first year student, lots of group projects but they are relatively straightforward. Weekly quizzes but they are open book. Highly recommend Jorge.
Great professor, he really cares about his students and wants them to succeed.
Absolutely amazing prof. Loved his lectures, though he is a bit strict with deadlines, he is a great teacher and gives really good feedback. Extremely entertaining classes too!
Very good professor. I loved his lectures! Classes are super engaging!
Favorite class this semester!
Very engaging, fun classes, absolutely loved him!
Good professor! I really enjoyed his classes! He is very passionate about what he teaches.

Lecture slides that he has varies when it comes to depth of detail and also different in structure-wise (lecturing order is different from the slides that are given)-> posted lectures tends to get confusing a lot of times. 
Harsh grader overall.
Says that this class is "open-discussion" style but always has his own answer and strict about it.
Tough grader and highly group project based. Slides are a mess

Didnt learn much in class.
lecture slides are made with bare minimum with student slide and his slide not being the same.
Easy class but over complicated.
Not the class to take in part if you want to open your business.
No clear rubric for grading. Comments are often the same all through out the students.
Never gives A+. Very few A. B is common.
Professor is available each week during the course office hours. I benefited by visiting the office hours to talk about broader implications of learning to design. I use the course in my work

Content is out of date, sometimes by over a decade. Much of the content comes from HBR, which is mostly editorials. For a course structured like a humanities course, there is a surprising lack of critical thinking in any of the assignments. You can pass by making stuff up, course rates well because it is in fact an easy A for a grad class.

Overall, I enjoyed the class content, though I feel it was more applicable to potential entrepreneurs than it was professionals in big business. Which is ok! I did learn a lot, it's just not what I expected. The online course delivery was very good and I appreciated the
obvious about of work that went into providing the content.

He made  sure  the  course  was  well  structured,  so  that  students  from  all  backgrounds  could  understand  the  course  concepts.  The ones who did not like the class were the ones who did poorly on interviews. He does provide guidance. I  hope  this  method  of  teaching  and  the  quizzes  after  reading  some  awesome  HBR  articles.

Horrible course. Outdated syllabus, frequent last-minute changes, and unclear expectations. Modules and assignments were added with little notice. Busy work. Horrible interview tests. No responses to emails or Slack. Instructor did not care at all. Feedback was from ChatGPT. Save your money buy a harvard business subscription.
Easy-going Professors and very enriching course content. Of course, it takes some effort to get good grades, but it is not that challenging as it gives fair grading and feedback.
