But I will say that she explains the content to the best of her ability and geniunely does alright, she seems to be truly considerate of her students' success. The course introduces new concepts very slowly, which also definetly helps.
Ms. Zhou is a great lecturer and really helped me understand concepts thoroughly. She's also very kind and approachable. Don't be afraid to meet her in office hours if you have questions.

Ms. Zhou is an excellent lecturer who explains concepts very clearly. She is also extremely approachable and very kind. I regret not utilizing her office hours more, as her willingness to help and patience in explaining were outstanding. The exams in the class are pretty difficult, but if you do practices you should be ready.
She's a nice person, but she can be difficult to understand a lot.

The accent for non-Chinese speakers takes time to adjust but the content delivered is clear. Her pacing during lectures is good and reserves time to answer many questions asked by students. Professor Zhou is all business, no play, so the lectures will require high concentration. She also cares about her students and hopes they succeed.
It was a good class. She's nice and uses coding demos and quizzes to explain the material
Her lectures are very dry and boring but she seems to genuinely care about the class doing good. This class is pretty easy if you have prior programming experience so lecture isn't that important

I liked Professor Zhou's lectures. This is an entry-level class, but the labs and exams can be somewhat challenging. She is flexible and willing to work with students when help is needed. Overall, I had a good experience in this class.

Professor Zhou genuinely cares about her students and puts a lot of effort into teaching. CS159 is hard, but she explains concepts clearly and makes it easier to understand. Office hours are very helpful, and the lecture quizzes after each topic really help reinforce the material

Lectures are dry. The packet's practice tests are essential for the grade. Don't buy the textbook; it's rarely used. Rigid classroom: assigned seating and no talking. Communication via email and in-class questions is difficult. Quizzes run long. Coordinate with your Lab TA for help.

Her lectures are not helpful. Long lecture quizzes (20–30 minutes) leave little time to learn new concepts. She doesn't explain code clearly and often can't answer questions due to the next class. Learning relies on TAs and self-study. The workload is manageable, but if you want to learn during lectures, I don't recommend taking her.
Ended my college career with Naik. He is a really great professor who got me really passionate about thermofluids when I had him for ME 200 in sophomore year. I owe a lot to this guy.

Naik made a 7:30 AM Thermodynamics class become my favorite course of the semester. He's super passionate about his work, and if you show up to lectures and do the homeworks properly, you WILL get a good grade as long as you put in the effort. Absolute thermo GOAT.

I'll be honest, I was not in Naik's section. I attended his lectures anyway because his lectures were miles above any other professor's. His handouts made a daunting class enjoyable and interesting. Professor Naik is a rare case of a professor that excels in teaching and clearly isn't just here to pursue his research.

Professor Naik is able to take very complex ideas, thoughts, and concepts, and is able to bring them to a very straight forward lecture that I had not experienced in many other courses. The early lectures are beyond worthwhile to ensure thorough understanding of the content.
Probably the best professor I've ever had. Absolutely incredible at what he teaches and truly made me enjoy the subject. If only he taught all of my classes.
The goat. amazing teacher, makes topics seem easy. Super fair exams as well, and great review videos before each one.

Naik is the best professor I've had at Purdue. His lectures are thorough, and I can tell he cares about his students and what we're learning. He does examples in class, and is clear about the application and approach of thermo. He posts his lectures online, there are no worries about missing class. Although, you should totally go to his lectures.
His lectures are amazing. Also offers recordings if you miss his lectures or decide not to go. Best professor I've had at Purdue. Genuinely cares about his students.
Phenomenal teacher. You want him as your professor for sure.

Dr. Naik made me love thermodynamics, so much so that it became my favorite class. His lecture videos are extremely insightful, clear, and helpful. He is extremely caring and respectful during his office hours as well. He really just wants to see his students succeed. Overall, if you have the chance, and are in ME200, taking Naik is a necessity.

Thermo was very manageable thanks to Prof. Naik. He teaches concepts very effectively and pragmatically that you begin to realize the course just completely revolves around the 3 basic equations and thermo property tables.

I took ME 200 over the summer with Naik after failing it during the year with a different professor. I was dreading taking it again, but I ended up actually enjoying it. The course was well organized, the instructions and grading criteria were laid out clearly, and the lectures were great (though I did watch them on 1.25 speed). Highly recommend.
I recommend going to his class even if you're not in his section.

Naik is one of the best professors I've had at Purdue. After struggling a lot early on in ME200, I decided to go to his office hours and he helped me turn things around. Now I owe it to him for his lectures are excellent, and he truly cares about student success. He may seem intimidating at first, but he's a genuine and supportive professor.

Great lecturer that makes the content easy to understand. Gives help on homework and exam review. Holds extra office hours close to exams and genuinely cares. If you want to learn and do well his section is the one to be.
Professor Naik is an excellent lecturer who makes thermodynamics much more digestible. Use Unstuck study and the free textbook for great study prep - his hints on exams are super helpful too.

The best professor I've had at Purdue by far. Teaches in a way that is very easy to understand, provides a lot of extra materials to help students succeed, and is definitely worth waking up to attend his lectures. 100% recommend.

Amazing teacher. The class is still difficult, but he sets you up for success with the lectures and homework. If you redo the homework problems before the test, you will have a good chance of succeeding in the class. He also provides hints for the homework which make them a lot easier to solve on a first work through.
great
Probably one of the best professors at Purdue.

Professor Naik is a special professor. He goes above and beyond to make sure everyone can understand the content. He will STRONGLY HINT what questions/topics will be on the exam, and helps with every homework with hints. Thermo is not an easy class, but his teaching and personality will make me remember this class in a positive light.

Naik might be the best professor in Purdue mechanical engineering. His effort to organize the course effectively through great homework hints and printing out notes for all students is completely unmatched by any other course or professor. Naik provides all the materials you need to succeed and goes above and beyond expectations.
Pretty Good
Excellent Lecturer, made the topic really clear and easy to follow.

Not in Naik's section but attending his lectures and office hours. Purdue/ME is lucky to have instructors like him who put heart into their teaching. He simply said "Good start - let's see how you do in the next one; hope you will continue" about the high average. Don't throw shade at an amazing instructor. He isn't rude at all. Mad props to Naik!
I really liked her class she made it really easy to have discussions as a class and open up to classmates, strict grader through have to put in good effort for an A

Pretty good overall, I have no complaints. I do think the grading is slightly harder than it should be for a COM class but she's good at what she does. An A is still easily achievable for the most part.

prof albitz is an understanding person, she is silly and easy to talk to. her classroom has an open and lighthearted vibe. she is more of a tough grader and i would say you definitely need to put in effort to get a good grade on your speeches, but the rubric is pretty clear.

My opinion of Professor Albitz changed a ton throughout the semester. While she might be on the harder end of grading speeches, getting a good grade in her course is still very achievable. I found she genuinely cares about her students, and even though the course itself is a little flawed, I would still definitely recommend her section!

Albitz is a great person, always bringing energy and good vibes to class. In terms of grading, I always felt it was very fair. There were clear rubrics, and I understood why points were lost on speeches. Also, she is very accessible if you have a question on a speech, project, or homework.

While she may be one of the "harder" comm graders, that's really exaggerating. I was not the best at speeches, but I averaged a 90 on them and didn't do much preparation. Lectures can be boring sometimes, but that's not her fault it's the department's fault. She truly cares about the students and gets to know them
thumbs up!
Genuinely cared about performance and improvement in the class. Gave good feedback and was very approachable but does grade a bit harsh. Diffidently approachable and sometimes let us out early.

I'm not quite sure what these other reviews are on about but when I had her she was fair and amicable. She cares for her students and wants the best for their academics. I loved this class and it was one of the ones that I always showed up to. Which says something because I don't go to class ever.
This lady had the lowest A rate out of all com 114 professors last semester. All my stem classes had a higher gpa. Horrible person who thinks that her class is the only thing that matters
She told students in class go rate high on rmp for bonus points which is messed up. Terrible professor who made a required gen ed unnecessary stressful. Avoid at all cost
Worst professor of all time avoid at all costs

Half of these reviews are fake. She might be approachable, but is by far one of the worst professors you can have if you are taking this class for a good grade. Average GPA  is the lowest for this semester

Mrs. Albitz was truly a joy. Even with my class taking place at 4:30 pm, including Fridays, I was always excited to go to class. She is a very kind, understanding, and flexible person, and in my opinion, the perfect person to teach this course.

Professor Albitz is a great instructor.  I looked forward to coming to class. She taught us great communication skills and really cares about her students. I would definitely recommend this lady. I wish she taught other classes too.

10/10 if you can take com114 with her, do it! She is such a caring, kind and funny lady. She makes you want to come to class! I told her i would write this review because she truly deserve the best! Class is really easy, you're given the final group project at the start of the semester, so if you can, start early and get it out of the way!
If you go to class and communicate with the professor/ follow her guidelines, this class should be cake! Wish she taught more classes because I like how she sets things up.
Love this lady. Put the work in and get the grade! Great lectures, easy to communicate with
She responds quickly to emails. She is a fair grader. Class was fun and engaging.
Engaging professor. Interesting material. Tries to apply content every major.

Docks unreasonable amount of points for small errors, really hard to get a good grade. When the grades get put in, the percentage doesn't actually reflect your grade. Syllabus isn't updated and is point-based instead of percentage based, so it doesn't actually reflect your grade either. She doesn't retain your attention when lecturing in class.

Kircher is a great professor. His presentations are very organized and he posts his recorded lectures from previous years if you prefer watching them at home. Easily one of the best professors I've ever had.

This prof was fantastic. He made me want to go to class. Everything you need to be successful is in his lectures, just need to practice the topics and concepts through homeworks and the practice problems. If you do not have this professor, find someone who does and request the lecture slides.

He's overall just a very good ME200 lecturer. His slides are amazing. Puts a lot of material/videos online to help students succeed. Provides good insight into what to study for the exams. Overall seems like a chill dude, who asks how to improve the class and seems to really care about students answers. Would take Thermo with him again.
Very caring and understanding of his students, dives into more abstract, interesting concepts within thermodynamics rather than just sticking to formulas and derivations. Fair grader.

To my experience, professor Kircher in ME200 is amazing! Approachable and passionate about teaching, his style focuses on proofs, great for understanding mechanics. This is very beneficial for students who learned by understanding the math and mechanic. Plus, he's easy to talk to, so students are always welcomed to ask question after class.

I mean, the classes (ENGR 161/162) itself are a pain if you come in with no prior calculus/physics/basic robotics experience. Whalen moves fast and doesn't teach the content properly to people who haven't learned it before. The course is designed poorly in that aspect, Whalen himself is chill enough just can't teach.
Bipolar. Seems to really dislike questions.

Whalen was a trash teacher but a funny guy. I swear the grading is black magic though. The best way to get a good grade is to be friends with him because what you score on assignments and exams doesn't really matter. Get prepared for insufferable homework. This class is worse than 161.

Very disorganized class. Impossible to predict your grade until the end of the semester, there are too many labs and exercises that do not impact the final grade at all. Whalen is an ineffective teacher at best when it comes to physics and other technical subjects of the class

Prof Whalen is a cool dude. He might be scary and can get angry a little easily, but inside is a cool dude who cares about students and their success. I have screwed up BIG multiple times and he allowed me to make stuff up. The class is hard and has a lot of work, but Whalen teaches pretty well too.
He explains difficult concepts well and clearly cares about the success of his students. The class itself is terrible, though, and an absolute pain to get through.
Very good professor, great at explaining difficult concepts. This class is a complete pain, but Dr. Whalen does a good job at teaching it, even if he does move relatively quickly.

If you put in enough effort the class isn't that difficult, but the main problem is the group work. If you get a group with people that don't put in work then you have so much to stress over. He doesn't get involved unless there are criminal charges and is mildly unsympathetic. He's a good lecturer but still keep in mind the content is difficult.
Whalen is insufferable and so is his class. There is way too much work given.
Bro doesnt GAF and last project is made for you to fail it. Demeaning guy too.

He is a little bit cranky at times but he knows what he is talking about. Its normal to just stop listening if its a 2 hour lecture and go over the slides during a study session later on. If anything all the courses after this one are either the same difficulty or easier. Go to office hours because homework is graded on correctness.

Dr Whalen does a great job at explaining content, and he is a pretty cool person to talk to. I will note that ENGR162 requires a lot of time for projects and homework, and the tests are no joke. Be prepared to spend a lot of time on this class. Whalen teaches the topics great, but some of the topics covered feel out of place or not very useful.
This class is designed terribly. Whalen does the best with what the course gives.
Dr. Whalen is a bit intimidating but super knowledgeable and gives great feedback.

He is a very good professor. He is knowledgeable when he is talking and answering questions. If you get something wrong he will point it out, not to be mean, but to make sure you grasp a better understanding of the material. If something is late, he his a stick to the syllabus type of teacher and rarely makes exceptions. Tests are difficult.

Dr. Whalen is by far the most qualified to be teaching this course. He can come off as condescending for sure but if you respect him, then he is more than happy to answer your questions and gives the best explanations to questions. It is very obvious that he truly cares about his students.

ENGR 161 is a tough course, but Dr. Whalen is an excellent instructor for it. Lectures are very clear (but move quickly) and he always is open to answering questions. Regardless of the professor, expect to put a lot of time in   if you haven't taken more advanced engineering/physics classes before. Highly recommend Dr. Whalen for this class.

I could rant and complain about how stupid ENGR 161/162 (previously 141/2) is. But as far as Dr. Whalen's instructor role, he is great. By far the best lecturer for the 161/2 team. But for freshman reading, beware this class takes a lot of effort. Big projects take lots of time outside class. The physics portion is like high school. Exams are bad.

He definitely cares a lot and means well, but when he lectures he goes off on these long tangents that are not necessary and if anything confuse the topic further. Lots more lecturing than lab/project work time, and basically all group work is done outside of class. Exams and homework are intense, but the class prepared me so I'd take it again.

Although Dr. Whalen can come across as a little condescending when answering questions in class, he's a good professor for ENGR 161/162. The class is a ton of work and not very well organized, but it's not Dr. Whalen's fault. An A is definitely achievable, but expect to go to office hours a LOT. The TAs for this class will save you!

Dr. Whalen's lectures are quite informative, but he delights in withholding information from students regarding course material and scheduling. Engr 162 is an exceptionally confusing class, and Whalen is no help with this. Grading rubrics are hidden and constantly changing, so ask your grading TA to look over all reports before the deadline.

ENGR 161/162 are the worst classes I have taken. The teacher is rude and condescending, and the lectures are boring and poorly explained. He absolutely expects you to come in with calc II and physics knowledge. Don't try to ask a question he will not help you instead he will explain why you shouldn't need help.

It takes an impressive amount of skill to organize and teach a class as poorly as Dr Whalen did. Assignment descriptions were incorrect and he contradicted himself when asked for clarifications. Don't expect a useful response if you ask question, all I got was "you should know" and "I know the answer but won't tell you" before he stalked off.
Dr. Whalen is incredibly kind and cares about his students. He gives very detailed lectures that are boring but are often helpful in completing the work.
ENGR 161/162 are the most poorly organized classes I have ever taken. Dr. Whalen was rude when answering questions and this class is so poorly taught.

While she is not the most approachable, Professor Ficht is clearly very intelligent and taught me a lot, even more than 3 credit classes. While she isn't the nicest or most approachable, the class and her grading are clear and fair. The lectures were really engaging, and I really got a lot out of this class.
Feels like she is scolding you every lecture, goes off on lots of tangents about her own law career, content isn't hard but some quiz questions are subjective and grades group projects harshly, avoid
Any 5 you see here is an obvious self rank. Begged our class for good course evals to keep her job. If you want to be marked absent for going to class, take this one.
Can be very condescending and disrespectful towards students.

Attendance is mandatory but the exams are based off of the lectures so if you go and pay attention you will succeed. She cares about the students and the material and will help you if you need it and give helpful feedback.

One of my favorite professors I have ever had. She genuinely cares about the class. Tough grader but I feel like the feedback I got on my projects were constructive and helped me learn in the class. I always looked forward to going to her class.

Ficht made me look forward to class. she cares about her students more than any professor I've ever had. provides extensions when needed, takes students out for coffee, and actually cares that we understand the material. I had her for 254 and the class itself was good. the tests were online open note. one assignment a week. take her over anyone.
Can't take criticism

The workload in this class is overwhelming. Every week, we have to write a full single-spaced page in Times New Roman 12, keep up with the readings, handle four exams, do group projects. This one class feels like more work than three of my other classes combined. I understand that it's the law, but does it really need to be this difficult?
Just don't.

(25400) Don't have an issue with discussing political events in a business law class but she does get way too personal in lectures. This may be a college-wide organization issue, but we'd sometimes have entire lectures just going over misleading quiz questions (there is a group project but no weekly homework).

The worst professor at Purdue. She is disrespectful toward students, brings up her irrelevant personal politics, and genuinly just does not like students. There is no reason she still has a job. There is piles of work for a one credit class. DO NOT take this class.

She took off points from a project for "not having paragraphs"—Didn't give me back the points until I contacted course coordinator for the complex task of looking at my submission and seeing paragraphs. Otherwise, horrible self-obsessed teacher. Spends more time talking about how she used to be successful instead of the material she was given—Avoid
THE BEST!!!!
A hamster spinning in a wheel could teach better.
I did not wear my seatbelt on the way to class in hopes that I wouldn't make it.
Never take her

She isn't great.. She brings up political views often which is unnecessary, also spends very minimal time talking about actual lecture material and instead goes on tangents about her career and how amazing she was. Quizzes and assignments aren't that hard though and grading criteria is clear ill give her that

Removed 5 attendance pts (3.5% of final grade!) despite attending every class
Has no respect for students or other staff—assumes everyone else is stupid
Spends more time talking about her life than class—went on a trip to see a judge, went to LA during Katrina, was in military, wrote a law
Barely goes over material weekly quizzes
Grades arbitrarily

To start, during lectures she doesn't stay on topic and is heavily opinionated. She constantly brings up political views and talks about random stuff that has no bearing on the class. Normally I wouldn't mind this but when you have an extremely difficult tests at the end of every week its not helpful. Don't take this class!

Besides the fact that she sounds like mickey mouse with a cold, her lectures were constantly interrupted by her need to make herself feel important. I dont think there was a week in the entire semester where she didn't bring up the law she wrote, her fridge, or belittle students. There is a reason she is the lowest rated at professor at Purdue

Attendance is mandatory, but I don't see the point of going to her lectures since she often goes off topic and doesn't cover material related to the quizzes. If the quizzes weren't so difficult, she'd be a good professor for anyone who enjoys listening to stories.
I'd rather actually get by a car if it means i never get to take her class ever again.
It is a waste of energy to explain everything wrong with her teaching style. I don't understand how she was hired and how she has managed to keep this job; she needs to change fields.

So many papers and studying for a ONE credit hour class. Lectures are useless & mandatory. She always strays off topic, into a personal/political rant. Very rude tone as well and assumes the worst of people. You have to extensively analyze textbook for quizzes. She doesn't even know what's on her quizzes and blames others for poor questions.
Very caring, teaches well (slides), and grade is mostly group projects.

I really enjoyed this class, Dr. Nies is a great professor who clearly cares about his students and their learning. Semester long group project is the majority of the class, so having a good group makes a huge difference.
A really, really great professor. Definitely take him if you can!
Dr. Nies is great! 10/10 recommend taking his class.

This is my second class with Dr. Nies as the professor. His lectures were amazing and this was one of the most useful classes I took this semester. However, if your group does not pull their weight for the projects, the class feels like a LOT of work.

Professor Nies is a fairly easy-going professor. The class is based around group projects so if you have bad group then it'll be more stressful than it needs to be. He is educated on the subject, but seems close minded if you do not agree with the things he says. Could do a better job at communicating deadlines and giving feedback on assignments

Dr. Nies is one of the best professors at Purdue. He truly cares about students and runs his class in an engaging, unconventional way. Take the time to speak with him if you can, he has a lot of great insight and knowledge. If you put in the effort, you'll get an A no problem. Make sure to do well on group projects and you'll be fine.

Dr. Nies is a wonderful professor who makes sure that students learn what is actually important and relevant- he doesn't waste time with any stuff that he believes to be unimportant. He gave many opportunities for us to boost our grades, and his lectures really held my attention.

My biggest issue was that he expected everyone to be very proficient with chemistry and stayed away from explaining anything with chemistry. This was a covid semester so he was really nice and made a certain amount of mini exams and hw skippable for everyone. The exams and hw had a very fair difficulty. He focuses on real world applications.

Professor Nies does a good job teaching the course, but is very difficult and stubborn if you want to deal with him after class. Those who propose new ideas are not taken into consideration. He gets upset very easily, and did not do a good job of hiding it. Overall I would not recommend professor Nies to any upcoming engineers.

Pretty decent professor however he is teaching an annoying class. ENGR 131 is one of the most grindy classes I've taken, prepare to spend several hours a week doing group projects. His lectures are pretty boring but he also tells you what's going to be on quizzes the class before.
He kept talking about critical thinking and cultural gap and stuff, but he kept using strange slangs that nobody understands on grading on a "what do you think" type question and give you a zero.
Dr. Nies is respected and really make you think critically. The course is easy. However, the TAs that he chose are super biased, which is ironic.
Not much to say. Decent as a lecturer and pretty fair in course evaluations.
Nies is a great professor and he really encourages his students to think about sustainability through a critical, but optimistic, lens. This course is extremely group project-oriented.

Oh boy was 131 a time. IDK if it was because of the 7:30 lecture times or because this class was vague. I tried my best in what is supposed to be an easy A class but the engineering team and professor love grading super harshly. The lectures were not exciting and you just go by.

Overall, having Professor Nies and EEE 355 has been an interesting experience. He is kind of goofy and awkward, and encourages thinking rather than memorizing information which I appreciate. The class is extremely group project-heavy and all work is done out of class. Big time commitment but the work is never hard.

He is very solid. He tells you exactly what will be on his "mini-exams" before the exam so there are no surprises. Show up to class since it is mandatory, do a little bit of studying before the exam and you will easily get a B. He is pretty funny too and really wants his students to do well in his class.

As long as you schedule weekly meetings with your group, you'll do great in the class. He is very understanding and willing to provide help or extensions. He is funny and passionate about sustainability and makes the class very interesting.

Professor Nies has a very inflated ego. It is clear from day one, given how much he feels the need to emphasize how most students "love" his class, and only a handful do not. This is simply not true. Most students would agree the amount of group work is ludicrous, and it is all expected to be done outside of class. I do not recommend this class.

Best professor I've ever had. Ever. He's a little nerdy, but he's funny and makes the class interesting & easy to understand. He's clear with his expectations, but you still have to work for the grade. The class is also very non-traditional so it keeps things fun & interesting.
Great Professor! Always here on time to teach 131 especially at 7:30. Definitely made the 7:30 class a lot more pleasant. Would definitely recommend him.
Very good prof, responsive and interesting. The whole picture of the class doesn't come together until the end, so don't write it off as useless too early.

He was always very helpful whenever I had a question. I once talked to him after class for an hour about how I was struggling with a couple classes. If it weren't for him, I wouldn't be at Purdue still today.
Super entertaining guy. Super ethical, great morals, he's all about helping students learn other than fail them. Great guy, so much respect for him

My favorite professor yet. He is funny and engaging, and makes you feel included no matter what takes you have. He remembers every name is so passionate. Remember to read every time he assigns things and you'll be great. As long you read, you'll be okay, don't forget that.

Professor Heth is obviously very knowledgeable and enthusiastic about what he teaches in class. His lectures are very enjoyable and informative to context surrounding the time period and history of the books read in class. Participation in class is a necessity, but it is structured in a way such that it is not painful to speak up.
Prof Heth was cool and taught well, but the class was way too hard. Lots of reading and you have to talk in class.

The professor made the material genuinely engaging. He is incredibly knowledgeable, which leads to great class discussions. The workload is very fair. Highly recommend for those who want an interesting but manageable course.

I had Prof. Heth last semester and I loved his class! The only thing he emphasizes is that participation matters. The discussions in class are really interesting and prof Heth is just brilliant. Would definetly take this class again.

Lots of writing, but he was literally the only professor who knew my name. He seems like he cares about his students, which wasn't the case for my other classes. You'll read lots of old stuff, but he tries to make it fun.

Nice guy and lectures are engaging if you keep up with the reading. His AI policy feels overly strict and not fully thought through, especially given how unreliable AI detectors can be. Probably the least boring SCLA class you could take.

Good class, but too hard. He expects you to have read for every single class and talking matters. He also has a blanket no-AI policy that he takes way too seriously. I liked it but I'm sorry gen eds should be easier.
I took Heth for a online class.

I took Professor Heth for SCLA 101. I was frustrated about having to take the class since I'm here for engineering, but it wasn't bad. We read lots of old stuff like Gilgamesh and the Bible. We read some newer stuff too, but he knows a lot about the ancient world and made those books really interesting. I signed up for another class with him.
I was going to leave a review about how he's a good teacher but then I saw the stuff below about his forearms and yea good forearms.

Dr. Heth is the ideal instructor. He is very passionate about what he teaches and very knowledgeable yet very humble and down to earth, it's really easy to get along with him. He is also very aware that most of his students are STEM majors and is very lenient with grading and course work. I love his sense of humor and am very glad I met him.
great professor. he also has big forearms
Professor Heth's class was fun. We only read ancient stuff in there, but it wasn't as boring as it could have been. I joined the class late and he made sure I was caught up. Nice dude.

Prof heth loves his work and it's very visible in class. He's extremely engaging with his students. He knows that his class mostly consists of STEM majors, and the length of assignments and homework match. Participation is a big chunk of your grade, but as long as you do the assigned readings before every class and speak up once, you'll be fine.

Professor Heth is clearly very passionate about his work and puts a lot of effort into his lectures. He is engaging with students and as long as you do your part with the readings, you'll be prepared. He's extremely lenient with grading on his writing assignments and papers, and as long as you speak 1-2 times per class, you'll get an A.

Prof Heth gives amazing lectures. He teaches historical texts in his area of expertise, and gives knowledge that give so much context behind them and allow his students to understand them more fully than if he had just assigned the readings and had us discuss them like normal teachers would.
Beyond lenient on grading and shows a great amount of respect towards students. Of course I would recommend Professor Heth, even at 7:30 am :)

SCLA 101. We read a bunch of super old stuff but the class was cool. Mostly just long discussions in class and papers. You have to read and participation is a big part of the grade. Just talk and your straight.
Dr, Heth was really nice. He has lots of office hours, so if I didnt get something I would go talk to him and he would help. All the books you read are super ancient but the class is interesting.

Best professor I had this sem and I loved the class. I usually despise writing and speaking out loud so I thought I would hate SCLA but it ended up being the only class which I enjoyed. He is funny, kind and brilliant in his field. He also sets realistic expectations as he is aware that 99% of his class is STEM students and are not here to write.

Professor Heth is the best. It is my favorite class by far. Professor Heth mostly uses ancient literature(mostly Mesopotamian and Greek) in his class, however he is really passionate about these texts and loves discussing them. Most of his classes are spent discussing the readings, as he enjoys discourse and hearing the thoughts of the class.
My favorite teacher i had this year.

Review written during his ongoing first semester for reference to spring students 
Great guy, and he adores his field. Class covers ancient texts. Grades fair and has fun lectures. Will need to discuss the texts in class. Manageable reading assignments + 250 words response paper each week + 1 750 word Creative writing + 1 2000 word research paper

Incredibly difficult grading. If you do what's asked of you on the outline she'll still give you a 50% and take points off anyways. Tests were ok but you had to put the exact wording from the textbook otherwise you'd get zero points. Homework and exams were released at odd times and they were all due at 12PM EST no exceptions.

Dr. B was super easy and a nice lady overall. Homework were annoying because you have to get the answer perfect from book or she counts it wrong. Had three exams worth most of the grade, and 10 or so homework assignments and that was it for grades. Listen to lecture, take good notes and you'll be fine.

She takes attendance but it's not for points.
Tests are most of your grade and there's not a lot of room for messing up but they're close to the slides (which aren't posted)
Good lectures, and she's really understanding, if you're going through something definitely contact her, she'll help
Homework can be confusing

Bonnie was really cool. Her lectures are kinda long and her homework can be tedious but easy. Her exams are easy if you use the study sheet she gives us, and she always adds points to boost your score. She gives deep examples that help and is just really fun to watch sometimes.
The exams are directly from her lectures, if you take notes and study the slides you will do fine. Her lectures are long and drawn out but not difficult.

Overall Dr. B. is an okay professor, she just sets up the class poorly. I didn't do great on the first exam which ruined my grade because she doesn't make lectures available until after homework is due, which was unclear in the syllabus. Class is very exam heavy (7 exams) and homework assignments are due two days after exams. Good intro class still

Be ready to read. She sets up the class so you don't know how the questions will be framed and then provides no help with constant reminders that everything opens and closes at noon. She does not care and lectures couldn't be more boring. She has a monotone voice and seems like she just does not care.
Just no. She never communicated well with us about anything. Everything is due at odd times and is just not an enjoyable prof.

Bonnie was AMAZING. While the class was online, she always offered to give us help and feedback whenever she could. If you watch the lecture videos and take good notes, you are set to do well. Straight forward class with a manageable amount of work. Bonnie really cares about her students and even offered some extra credit for us.

I took HK100 with her, however, it was online due to COVID. I recommend writing the examples and other information she says during lectures. The majority of the exams are applied questions and she uses her own examples. I found the lectures interesting and the homework is not too bad. She is passionate about her job and loves kinesiology.

Her class is challenging at times only due to her using concepts covered in class in ways that are not explicitly said in her slideshows. As long as you go to class, pay attention, and write notes on the slides AND what she tells you, you should end up with a decent grade in her class. Her voice can get monotonous at times.
Zero enthusiasm. Students always bored in class. WAY TOO strict on grading the reading assignments.
HK 100 is a requirement for most students and I believe she is the only instructor. Good Luck
Had this class last semester. Avoid Blankenship at all costs, material isnt difficult but lecture info if dramatically different from what is on the exams.
Take notes based on the power point then write more about the info based on what she said. Use the study guide while preparing for the exams! Go to class so you can get these notes!!
All exams are from the textbook- Her notes/lectures do not go along with them. Much more difficult than I was expecting for a 100 level course. I would not take a course with her again.

She uses the same PowerPoint every year, dont just write down whats on the slides, make sure to write down what shes saying and try to understand what she means. She gives a study guide out that you fill out so I find it helpful to do while in class as she goes. Every point counts so do well on homework and study for the tests.

3 Exams, worth 90% of your grade. The way she grades them is nice. Say 50 questions and everyone misses 5 of those. She will decide to throw out 5 questions but you keep the points if you go them right. so if you missed all 5, then you are now at a 45 out of 45. But if you got them all right you end up with a 50 out of 45 on the exam.

I love her so much! She is really engaging in class and never dreaded going. With that said, I felt that the textbook and slides were more effective at learning everything needed for the course. However, she was great at answering questions in class and clearly values teaching and her students. This class is very material heavy, so read and show up
Very content heavy class but she was a very fair grader and I liked her lectures. She always gave real life examples and stories. Do the extra credit

Professor Gavia is a great instructor who clearly knows her subject matter. While the class itself isn't overly difficult, many of the assignments are tedious. Expect weekly discussion posts and quizzes that require LockDown Browser. You should be prepared for a significant amount of group work, as well as a demanding midterm and final exam.
She is so awesome and so nice. But the class honestly is just so hard. The exam is just is so much content and its overwhelming.

The actual content of MGMT 254 is really difficult, you have to cover a lot of chapters and reading the textbook is very important. However, Mrs. Gavia is really kind, a great lecturer, and tries to give you a lot of grace knowing how hard the course is. She is a really great professor to take for this course, especially with how hard it is.

Professor Gavia is truly an amazing professor-- super engaging and makes the content relatable to her students. Her experience as a lawyer makes her an amazing teacher for this course. I would take this class with her a million times as she is the sweetest person, truly cares about her students, and helps you succeed in the course.

She is such a kind person. She wants every single student to do their best in the class. The class can be challenging due to the quizzes and the exams with lots of questions but she is very aware of this. She offers other assignments and lots of time to talk to her to make sure you understand the material. I would take this class with her!

Gavia is an absolute sweetheart and does her best to explain a very content-heavy course. Quizzes can be a bit difficult than needed at times, and I found the simulation project frustrating due to a poor group (pick a good one)! My biggest gripe is that she is the slowest grader ever! Our final is in two days, and I still don't know my grade.

As a pre-law business major, I found the material interesting. However, it was very content heavy at times. Gavia has great past experience which leads to cool stories in lectures. The class is primarily graded by weekly quizzes. These quizzes are honestly tricker than they should be but still very manageable as long as you have the textbook.
Class content is rather hard due to volume of it but gavia does a great job explaining the content during lectures. Attendance is required but you get multiple excused absences.

The textbook is available to find online, which you are able to use for the quizzes.

Attending lectures is the easiest way to pass the class. Slides have the general topics and points, but class lectures clearly explain and provide examples to help understanding.
Semester long group project is generally easy, but be sure to get a good group.

Loved her!!! She is a super sweet person. Lectures can be a little boring, but that's more of the content than anything. Prof Gavia is very helpful and is great at answering any questions you have about content or instructions for the business simulation assignments.

Professor Gavia is super sweet. She is very understanding of her students and will listen to your situation and try to work something out with you. The class itself is pretty challenging, but as long as you read the textbook you should be fine.

Professor Gavia is a really sweet person but lectures can be a little messy at times. She came up with a presentation project spontaneously. You'll need to go over the textbook (take notes) days before the weekly quiz to score well, slides aren't enough. The Business Simulation is really straight forward. The final is really tough and really large.

Professor Gavia cares about her students a lot. Super easy to communicate with, and she is always smiling when talking to you. She will listen to your situation and provide whatever help she can. Class itself is pretty hard, but as long as you keep up, there are clear materials for you to grind.

A quiz every week that makes up for 50% of the final grade. No midterms, only final exam that is 21%. Group project is easy and accounted for 17.5%. There are attendance and participation points. Reading the textbook helps a lot. Final is very hard.

Professor Gavia is an extremely kind and caring person. She loves her students and always comes into class with a smile on her face. The course is set up very quiz/test heavy which is somewhat difficult. She does very helpful reviews before quizzes and awards attendance. I recommend this professor but be prepared for a lot of hard work!

I really was dreading Business Law (for obvious reasons) but I had a blast with Professor Gavia. She was hilarious, gave great examples, was lenient, understanding, and treated us all like humans. In my section, there were no midterms but instead one very large final. There were weekly quizzes and discussions too, but that was it. I miss her class!

I am such a fan! Professor Leitch is very good at what he does, smart, and cares a lot about his students and teaching. MGMT 223 is pretty work heavy class (for my semester there were 2 weekly assignments that took a decent amount of time, a weekly lockdown quiz, in addition to 5 presentations). Grading is tough, but I learned a lot!
Really nice professor who offers a lot of good insights into marketing but holy is there a lot of work. Be prepared to do a bunch of group projects and hours of homework each week.
He is a nice teacher and always willing to help but there is a lot of work for an entry level class.
Be prepared to present with the same group for more than 5 times. There is one solo prerecorded presentation.

I had Professor Leitch for MGMT 42130, Consumer Behavior. AMAZING class. Participation is important, and you have to present quite a bit. However, you can participate by asking questions too. Material was super interesting, lectures were engaging, and he is super understanding. Great class, definitely recommend taking it!

Leitch is a great professor, he has the best explanation of concepts in my opinion and the class isn't hard if you apply yourself. Attendance is not required but you have to participate in class to get points for things.
I have had him every year I have been at Purdue and he is an amazing Professor

I'm taking his HTM 173 online class right now. This class is 4 weeks long and we are doing 16 weeks worth of class work in 4 weeks. He doesn't really answer his emails. I was originally told that since it is 4 weeks long the class work would fit 8 weeks but it turned out we are doing 16 weeks worth of class work.

Leitch is one of the most interesting professors I've ever had. His classes are easy, but you still have to do work. He is understanding and helpful, along with a fun to be around. I would recommend him over and over.

If you're taking it online in summer I want to let you know you'll be on your own. There are no zooms or anything you have to teach yourself everything. It wasn't hard but it was a lot of work and very tedious. Projects are due every week along with multiple quizzes, assignments, and tests. Leitch also takes forever to reply to emails. Easy class.

Absolutely fantastic person. You can tell he cares about students and is not going to give you pointless material that you won't get anything out of. He has a huge passion for teaching and it really comes through in all of his classes. I would have Stephen teach all of my classes if possible. You will work hard but walk away with so much.

Stephen wrote his own textbook for this class. Pay attention in class and you should have no problem passing with high marks. Easy going teacher who gives the benefit of the doubt if you put in the work.

He's a good professor and you can tell he really enjoys teaching. Grading criteria is clearly spelled out and is followed. Be prepared to study for tests, but with some work put in you will get a good grade. He also tells entertaining stories to make lecture go by quickly.

I loved this class and the way Professor Hughes taught it. I found the essays to be really fun and engaging to write. Her organization of the class flowed nicely throughout the semester, and lectures were concise and informative.
Professor Hughes clearly cares about our learning and make an inclusive environment. The homework and papers were pretty easy, overall chill and fun class

I was very worried going into ENGL 108, but Professor Hughes created such a relaxed and comfortable environment that I loved going to class every day. You can tell she cares about all her students and wants to get to know you. She makes what could have been a terribly boring class pretty interesting. So so nice and funny. I am so glad I got her!!
dr. valero-pena saved my life

Solid professor overall. The class is fully immersive in French and she tries to make lectures engaging and get students to contribute. The homework and quizzes are straightforward if you understand the lecture concepts. More reading and writing practice would have been good, but the class was enjoyable overall. She is very receptive to questions.

Had a vocab quiz that was just a grammar quiz (wasted time studying). Essay...brutal. 6+ mistakes, you got a D or lower. The first time she graded our writing was for the essay as well. I learned some new things, but I wish we had in-class assignments to learn more and practice instead of just her lecturing. Little writing and speaking practice.
Goes of on random tangents, disorganized, doesn't explain directions clearly, harsh grader

I had a great experience with Valero Pena for FR 301 this semester. She dropped the textbook and the homework assignments were very light and straight forward. The quizzes make up a large portion of your grade but they are pretty easy if you read somewhat carefully. Valero Pena is very willing work with you individually if you have any concerns.

I've had Pena for 301,302, and 401 and she's a great prof overall. If you're havingproblems with brightspace or have difficultly accessing something, just speak up she will be understanding. I think 301 was by far the most difficult because adapting to a French speaking classroom was a HUGE challenge but then it got easier. Read the rubrics!
This class is very manageable and honestly I enjoyed her dedication to only speaking in French

I had Valero Pena for FR 301 & 302; both classes were very manageable. There are only a few homework assignments and the quizzes aren't difficult. The learning concepts were mostly review if you studied French in the past. The class is immersive (no English). I found lectures engaging and understood her French most of the time.

I have had her for FR301 and am currently in FR302 because I didn't know she was the only prof and I would highly suggest AVOIDING this class if possible. Her teaching style is very unclear and if you already have an understanding of French, can be very boring. She is not organized at all. Both classes with her have been overall a bad experience.

Don't take this professor. I feel like I didn't learn anything. During class we would just work on examples from the textbook. A lot of it was also stuff I had already gone over in my previous French classes. She is also very disorganized. At the end of the semester we had an essay that was due that she canceled the day before it was due.

Overall, she is a good professor. The course itself only has like 6 quizzes, which in total adds up to 50% of your grade. This kinda sucks because even 1 mistake in the quiz costs you 1%. But overall, I've seen worst and better.

She's not the most interesting when it comes to professors, but she's not bad. She's good with feedback and is pretty easy to work with in and outside of class given the whole remote learning thing, but her grading can be pretty harsh. A typical experience, I've seen both better and worse.
Avoid at all costs
Best professor at Purdue!

All her classes are great, and her lectures are very interesting and have a lot of activities that don't feel like work at all. Probably one of if not my favorite professors at Purdue. Shes very caring and takes her time to get to know every one of her students as long as you let her. Also gives us free food.
Made me spend money and time on an extra credit opportunity which only counted for 0.2 percent of my final grade. An extremely difficult grader with unclear grading criteria
An enthusiastic lecturer and a fair grader.
This was highkey the hardest grader I ever done had in my life, chill though.
Probably one of the best professors I have ever had. I thoroughly enjoyed her class, and she takes time to get to know her students.
Dr. Cuthbertson was the highlight of my first year. Best SCLA professor. Great discussions and very accommodating with grading.

Course taken Spring 2024. Dr. Cuthbertson during lecture made class not boring, and showed a lot of emotion and would sometimes talk a lot during lectures. I would zone off, and be completely fine, the course was very easy even with the papers and presentations. Group discussions are a big part, and making sure you talk is an important part.

Dr. Cuthbertson's class was a highlight of my week. She was always ready to make class fun but also a place where we could learn and ask questions. The class is based entirely around readings done outside of class, but they usually aren't super long. Participation is important and she'll try to get everyone to talk, even if it's only one thing.

The professor is very caring and understanding. She gives opportunities for extra credits and have a very fun personality. Participation matters a lot, you should contribute to class discussion semi-frequently to do well in class. I love that the books she requires for class is accessible, cheap, and have rich literary history and importance.

Every week we had a small 150 word reading response that needed to be quote based. Participating in class discussions is not mandatory, but beware that participation is 10% of the final grade. She's super caring, offers extensions when needed and is super fair when grading.

Super understanding and caring, willing to make exceptions and help you in any way necessary. refuses to give a paper above a 94, even if the comments say it was a perfect essay. You don't need to do the readings but at least read the SparkNotes and talk once a week in class.

Dr. Cuthbertson is an awesome English professor. She is very caring and offers students extra help outside of class if needed. Each class revolves around discussions of the reading, and while some of the readings were quite philosophical, she brought unique insights into the discussions. Overall, it was a fun class.
Her discussions were always very fun and attentive, she always made everyone feel included.

Professor Cuthbertson is the GOAT.
You need to do the readings and participate in class discussions, but they're pretty fun and you can really say anything. She's very understanding and always ready to help students with the writing, willing to give extensions, lets you resubmit an essay after working in the feedback.
Best part of my day!

So far my favorite professor so far. She always makes sure students are having a good time in her class and isn't shy from deviating away from the agenda if the class discussion is interesting and unique.

Professor Cuthbertson has been my favorite professor at Purdue so far. She is unbelievably smart and so passionate about her passions that it helped inspired me to be more involved in the class. The books required can be a bit difficult to understand sometimes, but she does a great job explaining in class. I would absolutely take this class again

This class was a blast, and Dr. Cuthbertson was a joy to have the whole time. She is so passionate about her work and it is clear to see. She is always wiling to help if you need advice on one of the three papers in that class. Everything was fully manageable and I never felt overwhelmed. She was amazing!

Other reviews said she was a strict grader, but she really was not. I got nothing lower than an 86 and I am a terrible writer. She cares lots about her job and even more about her students. She can talk for a while, but she encourages class participation, and it is nothing boring. She was probably by best professor this past semester.

Dr. Cuthbertson was easily my favorite professor of my first semester. She is super kind and caring, and values everyone's voice in her class. She grades fairly and allows you to resubmit papers based on her feedback, which is awesome.

Dr. Cuthbertson is probably the best professor I have ever had. She is caring, passionate, and very knowledgeable. Though she is a tough grader, she is more than willing to help her students and provides the means for them to succeed. Get ready to have a fun, philosophical, and mostly stress-free class!

Prof Cuthbertson is a very caring prof. She encourages group discussions a lot which can be good or bad depending on whether or not you're a person that likes that. I found her grading pretty fair. If you think she's a harsh grader she still gives a) tons and tons of feedback and b) allowed me to resubmit essays I did really poorly on.

Professor Cuthbertson's class is rather easy as there is only one assignment a week and three essays throughout the entire semester, however she can be a bit of a harsh grader at times. It does help though as she ensures that you are becoming a better writer. She is really kind and understanding and is super accessible outside of class.

This course is a very easy A, although people say she grades essays harshly, it doesnt matter because I got B's on 2 of my 3 essays and still ended the class with a 98%. Her harsh grading does not affect your grade but it is also to genuinely help you learn to write. Most of the assignments are for completion anyways so just make sure to do them.

Dr Cuthbertson's class is very mediocre. she goes on tangents a lot and is an extremely difficult grader regarding the 3 essays that are required for the course. She is a very genuine person, but an ok professor. Be prepared to talk as she makes everyone provide a thought or comment about the class readings. She takes attendance very seriously.

I honestly think her class is virtually a free ride if you attend class and just write stuff. Weekly reflection papers are cake. I hate writing, so 3 big essays were painful but manageable. You really don't have to do a thing during the class other than showing up. She is a very likable person though.

Professor Cuthbertson is great. You can tell that she is very passionate about what she does. The way that she runs her class is everyone gets in small groups to discuss the reading, she then shares her personal thoughts, and then we have one large class discussion. The class is very chill with only 3 papers the entire semester.

She speaks at a steady pace and stays aligned with the slides. The lectures move quickly, but the exams are straightforward and closely follow the study guides. I'm not sure what class everyone else has been taking.
She's nice, but the class structure is awful. Not many resources, and it takes a lot of time out of other classes. I'm doing a minor in Psychology and this was one of my busiest classes.

You can tell she's passionate & knowledgeable about I-O psych. However, Her exams are extremely difficult & are worth 60% of your grade. They're lockdown browser & camera on. There are 6 throughout the semester, but only your top 5 count. The exam preps are barely helpful. The rest of the work isn't bad. Exams are killer though.
Considering she just read off the slides, I think a high schooler could teach this class better. I would rather retake Chem 129 than deal with this professor every again.

I've never had a professor as awful as this one. The rage I felt taking notes is an anger I've never felt toward an educator. All she does is read off the slides & FLY through them. Do not be fooled when it says 30 minutes, it will take you 3x that. The exams are incredibly difficult and worth 60% of your grade. I don't know how she got this job.

A 200 level psych class should NOT be this hard. I think she needs to start caring about her students success and understand that none of the material on her exams make sense. She is just one of those young teachers that will make the online class super difficult for no reason. Flies through lectures and is hard to follow. You've been warned.
This class was so much harder than it should've been. The online tests were so difficult. The lectures and notes took hours. I studied for the tests for 10 hours and still didn't do well.

This was for Moral Minds. She is very passionate, and the course topics were genuinely interesting. I think she really excels as a philosophy professor. She even baked us cookies and brought cupcakes for the class. I will say she can have a deadpan expression when students give speeches, but don't let that intimidate you.
Taken for PHIL 114. She is very kind and offers tons of extra credit. Funny professor.

Very interesting reading assignments and lectures. The class mainly focuses on large group discussions so participation is very important. Professor Waggoner is a very fair grader and is always open to new ideas from her students. This course is very easy to be successful in if you put in the time and effort.
Not bad at all. Nice and communicative. I will say this class is christianity heavy.

Not very understanding or easy to work issues out with. Almost every in class quiz taken throughout the semester was a pop quiz, questions are mostly straightforward but something to be aware of nonetheless. Participation is very important and if you are not fully paying attention and participating every class you will receive points off.

The class itself is pretty easy, it's just a lot of busy work. Class isn't mandatory but labs are. The homework is easy if you need help, ask TA's. All of the labs are based on correctness. The individuals labs show your TA before you submit and they will help. Group projects are easy just follow guidelines. Tests are fair, and also only 30% grade.

Chenzhong who also goes by Frank, does not make lecture slides. Lecture is pretty much just him reading the textbook. I just started going to a different STATS lecture. That being said, he does seem to actually care about his students and is very responsive to emails. Additionally, he doesn't take attendance.
Super friendly, engaging and entertaining, even at 7:30 in the morning

Honestly a great professor! He has very engaging lectures that make the class much easier than it might otherwise be. Only thing to watch out for is SONA research credits, however if you are diligent you can knock that out within the first five weeks no issue. He does sing, can confirm that as true in my experience. Classwork on Tophat and easy.
Genuinely the most chill and flexible professor ever. Truly baffling how the course is so unrestrictive while still teaching its content effectively. Phenomenal.

Incredible professor. Expectations for the course are very clear and attendance is not mandatory. The lectures that I watched were both entertaining and educating. Professor Hollich clearly cares about his students.
work assigned every week, very easy, professor is very lenient with due dates and attendance
One of the best!

Professor Hollich is an amazing professor who keeps the class engaging. I had a 7:30 with him, but attendance isn't mandatory, and he makes it easy for you to learn the material. His textbook was interesting and actually helped with learning the material.

TAKE THIS CLASS WITH DR.HOLLICH! He is the BEST professor I have had in my 4 semesters. Class was only offered at 7:30 AM, but he posted everything online and includes a zoom link if you are not able to make it in. Bonus- there is no exams! Class work is very manageable, and he offers EC for doing your SONA credits early.
Professor Hollich is really fun! (prepare to be sang to at 7:30 am lol) The class work was all done online, and there are no exams. It was a super easy class!

Dr. Hollich is easily one of the most passionate teachers I've ever had, and the enthusiasm he brings to his lectures carries off in the actual material. Most of the material is from curriculum that he wrote himself, so very straightforward. I found the required research credits to complete pretty interesting as well, just start early on it.

Lectures are recorded, all homework is just multiple choice questions and writing, not hard at all.  No exams (there are "quizzes" which are online, untimed, 15 MCQ, open note). Very chill definitely take Prof. Hollich for PSY 120 over other profs!!  Also just a very entertaining and skilled lecturer.

Very accessible for any questions via email and responds in a timely manner. The class is not difficult by any means as long as you stay on top of work. I would recommend working ahead since he usually posts multiple weeks' content and also getting your research credits done early so you don't have scramble at the end during finals.

One of the best professors I've ever had at Purdue. I took both Psych 120 and Psych 235 with the professor and it's the easiest class you'll take. Need to pay for TopHat subscription and textbook for each course but other than that, its very easy homework and no exams.
One of the best professors I have ever learnt from. Made this introductory course extremely interesting and engaging for all the students.

Loved professor hollich! Super caring guy you can just tell he's passionate about what he teaches. Homework and quizzes are super easy, attendance isn't mandatory so I stopped going after the first week. Everything is online through the textbooks. I recommend going though as he's such a cool prof! Make sure to do your SONA projects early!

No exams, but lots of discussion boards. All assignments are online and lectures are recorded. Attendance is not mandatory, just have to do the participation discussions. Few midterm essays, but rubric is clear and graded mostly on completion. Lots of writing discussions. Professor sings in lectures sometimes. Would highly recommend.

Trust me guys if you are taking psy 120 get it with Hollich, even if it is at 7:30. All my friends that had different professors for psy 120 had exams but Hollich did not give us one singular exam. He also records every lecture so you do not have to worry about making it to the lecture. Homework was very straightroward.
He is literally such a chill professor. He is so funny and a very sweet guy.
7:30 AM lectures are not mandatory, as they're available via Zoom. He's a very nice guy who's easily accessible outside of class. As long as you do the bare minimum, you will pass.

Prof Hollich is amazing. I took PSY 120 with him, and I loved it so much that I made sure to take PSY 235 with him as well. He is engaging and fun; it is clear he loves what he does. The 3 assignments are super easy, and he is an easy grader. I got a 100% on everything. There is a bit of work/reading, but it is easy and engaging.

I dropped the course later in the semester since I realized I didn't need it and I procrastinated too hard, but Dr. Hollich is definitely an awesome instructor. He creates a comfortable environment at his lectures and a lot of the class is graded on participation/completeness. Very respectable and likable teacher.

Great professor, super engaging and even sings in class. He wrote the textbook we used, which made me respect him even more. Was the first class I took here at Purdue, and made me know psychology was the way to go. A lot of homework involves reading and discussions online. Get ready for some busy work, but an overall great time.
One of the best professors. The course was fun and easy, and on top of that, he's very passionate about the subject. He is nice and approachable, and very funny.
Great teacher

Bless this man! Prof Hollich is the most passionate and dedicated professor I have ever seen teach at Purdue. He genuinely cares about your learning and goes above and beyond to make sure you get a grade that you are happy with. He is the most understanding with students and wish him all the best. My experience in Purdue was better thanks to him

His lectures are entertaining and well-paced, as well as good at answering questions and conveying the core concepts of calc 3. Homeworks and recitations were pretty easy. He was the CC when I took the course, and his tests were challenging but with the backlog of previous tests + Boilerexams it was totally manageable if you put in the time.

Mummert was a terrific and funny lecturer who made calc 3 an enjoyable class. However, his exams were very conceptually heavy and more difficult than previous semesters. If you go to his lectures and actually understand the concepts instead of trying to memorize formulas and train yourself like an LLM with previous exams, you'll be fine.

Does not work well for online students. Sent him four emails throughout the semester - no response. Online format is intended to be self-paced, according to Purdue goal, he enforces tight deadlines and releases lectures sequentially forcing online students in pace with face-to-face students. Additionally, test difficulty is inconsistent.

This is the greatest math teacher of all time. His lectures actually were useful and attending didn't feel like a chore. If you go to lecture and take notes during his lecture, you'll be fine. He's very funny and keeps you engaged in the lectures.
i dont understand
Basically, for a math class exam, mathematicians can play tons of math tricks to make the exam extremely hard. And he is one of those who play these kinds of tricks on students.

Dr. Mummert was the first professor I met in college. He gave me the best first impression of Purdue, and he really knows his stuff. My single gripe is that I noticed he tends to have difficulty understanding a student's query if they're not 100% precise. Super funny dude, literally friends with him to this day.
Funny guy, would take again.
Truly a gift of a man, he's involved in more than you know
I liked this professor; he was a decent lecturer. Be warned of the exams and quizzes, though, they're rough!
Very good lecturer. The homework he gives is a lot but is also the same as all other calc 3 sections.

He's the goat. Amazing lectures, witty, very knowledgeable about more than just math, like lowk calc3 lectures were often the highlight of the day. The class is a good heat check if u think ur smart. If u really are you'll do great, if u don't it's cuz ur not - no excuses/blaming the professor, and tests are very fair. Truly enjoyable and rewarding

he is a great professor and a very sassy diva, which makes lectures both informative and engaging. his exam writing is slightly evil, but overall great experience would recommend (just study hard and go to lectures).
He was really good at explaining concepts and always answered students questions. I feel like the best way to prepare for exams is to do boilerexams and the past exam archive.

MA 261 is heavy on conceptual understanding. Mummert did an excellent job explaining the concepts. Does a good job drawing 3d surfaces to help you visualize the concepts. Exams were tricky. Go through the past exams to study; lots of past exams.

Mummert is a good lecturer. He is clear and will answer questions in class. He writes tests that are very different from previous years tests, which makes them tough to study for. The homework problems were not particularly helpful for the tests themselves, as they were often unlike the types of questions on exams.

Mummert does his best to get all of his students through tricky concepts in Multivariate Calculus. Gives you EVERYTHING you need in the lectures so be sure to listen up. As long as you pay attention to everything (and he will often hint at things that might be on a test), you will be fine.

Mummert makes otherwise difficult calc 3 concepts simple and intuitive to understand. His lectures are extremely engaging and easy to follow, with the right amount of actually funny jokes. Exams are very difficult but I don't think that's in his control.

Professor Mummert is an excellent lecturer. He makes complex concepts easily digestible within each 50 minute lecture. Homework is manageable, but in my opinion, not very helpful for the six option multiple choice exams. Use the past exam library as your study guide.

This professor is a great lecturer. He is great at teaching the concepts and using visuals to explain the topics. Sometimes he is pretty fast in his lectures, so make sure to reread your notes so you understand everything. The only complaint is that the tests are REALLY hard.
Amazing professor. Insanely difficult exams as they focus more on conceptual understanding

Professor Mummert is an amazing professor. His lectures are easy to follow and he often starts with a review of a calc 1 concept and then connects it to calc 3. He assigns lots of homework but if you do it, the homework really helps in preparation for the tests. The tests can be difficult but if you do practice questions you will be fine.
Genuinely he was born to lecture. He is wonderful at teaching you how and why things work, as long as you pay attention.
The exams in this class were very tough. You could spend two weeks prepping for them and still do poorly. Overall just  very unfair class

If Isaac Newton and God had a baby, it would be this man. Because of him, math is never about rote memorization but pure, intuitive internalization. Literally a one in a billion talent. I would name all my kids after him. My GPA is saved, my soul is ascended. We are truly not worthy. The absolute goat. 11/10.

The textbook is annoying, considering that I rarely referenced it except for the quizzes and take home tests. Otherwise, seems like a chill and understanding person, passionate about CAD and PDM. Do all the textbook quizzes at the very start of the course, don't procrastinate assignments, attend TA office hours, easy A.

The professor is not bad, and the course itself is very easy. The lectures are not helpful at all but are mandatory, there is a lot of CAD to do every week but also plenty of guided videos and instruction.
He's a good speaker; his lectures are just pointless as they don't relate to the assignments.

Dont rly know where all the hate is coming from. Sure its time sink work and way too much for a 2 credit class, but really isnt that bad. Assignments are straightforward and have walkthrough videos . Lectures maybe arent necessary but interesting if you're genuinely interested in the engineering industry. Only big issue is the book price
Grading is so clear. There's a lot of work, but you can get it done during lab since it's pretty much work time. Honestly an organized and easy class.

The previous reviews were based on the beta testing of this class. The class now seems to be a lot more organized and manageable. There is a lot of work to do in this class but as long as you put in effort, you'll be okay. Prof. Fuerst is actually very understanding and funny and wants the best for his students.

The negative things I heard about this professor going into MFET were honestly unwarranted. That's not to say prior experiences weren't as rough as people say, but the class has been improved substantially since then. They are very nice and always want feedback to improve the class more. Yes, 200 for a book is rough, but it's not wholly unusual.
The class has a steep learning curve and can be very difficult at the start, but you are given plenty of resources, and it gets much easier.

Professor Fuerst's older reviews seem tied to the previous software, which I understand was frustrating. With Siemens NX and Teamcenter, the course feels much more useful and real-world applicable. This was my favorite class was my favorite this semester. The required book was not very helpful, but Mazumdar was an excellent TA.

I went into this class with zero CAD background, so I was kinda freaked out about this class, but it wasn't bad at all.  Learned a lot of NX and Teamcenter stuff and it actually helped me get an internship.  Textbook is expensive, but it did help me understand CAD with the practice exercises.

As difficult as he seems to be, He is transparent on his grading scale, expectations for students, as well as course schedules. The assignments can feel redundant and tedious, but at the end of the class I knew NX more than ever. This is due to the learning environment of finding out for myself instead of people telling me how to do something.
Overall, I found this course very beneficial, particularly for those without previous CAD experience. Although the workload is heavy, the assignments are incredibly useful for learning the material.

This class has a bad rep, mostly because it was a new class with a lot of moving parts. Now, it seems to be smoother and more polished, and I have hardly run into any issues. If you want to be a mechanical engineer and get into CAD, this class is a necessity and will provide you with the skills needed.
The content isn't too bad but the class is very, very tedious. He assigns a lot of busy work, making a 2 credit hour class feel like 4.
Makes you buy his textbook, which he wrote. Got most assignments wrong and had to change them 1-2 days before the due date

Lectures were interesting and the new Teamcenter site was good to learn. Going to office hours and making sure to follow the rubric really helped me do well. Most important thing is getting on top of assignments and not being against asking for help, as the software can mess up at times.

Not a great lecturer, TAs carry the course. He gives extra credit assignments but downcurves at the end of the year based on distribution. So even if you get an A according to the syllabus you could end up with a B. Those extra credit assignments are really just normal assignments. He's the worst and is not transparent about it.

Honestly one of my favorite classes so far. The lectures gave a solid look at how engineering design works in industry, and the labs made the CAD and PDM stuff click. Fuerst is super approachable and helpful with both class questions and career advice. Not sure why people hate on it.

0 lenience & mercy, doesn't care about his students.Not structured to be a 2-credit course,rather heavier than 4-credit.Didn't give extension for a legit technical fault,& asked me to drop the entire course for the 2nd time.I have no motivation to take it for the 3rd time now.
