He's doing whatever he wants as he's the only professor for this course.

Initially, I was unsure about Fuerst considering the less-than-stellar reviews, but he seemed completely fine. The course itself is super easy if you have any previous CAD experience although there is a lot of homework. Other than the cost of the textbook, I have no complaints.

This class isn't hard as long as you do all the work and get every extra credit. By the “final” practical exam, I had so many points I could have skipped and still gotten an A-. Take every assignment seriously and don't be afraid of going to office hours (the lab office hours run by TAs) and and the class will be way easier than you think.
Useful lectures and homework. The content in mfet163 helped me achieve proficiency with CAD and plm in a few months.

I took MFET 10301.

Mandatory $200 textbook, extremely buggy software that barely worked on the school computers, useless information about PDM, and average CAD training made this class really more difficult than it should have been.

If you can avoid taking this class, do so. The TA's made it tolerable and they did the best they could.
85% of the class gets an A so even though its a lot of work can't complain, 200$ textbook is a bit annoying but apart from that pretty chill class

Participating in labs helped me get some extra credit, have to say, got good feedback each time I lost points. He is strict on grading but definitely follows Industry standards. Overall, a tough one but a good one

Some annoying parts to the course, like the Monday quizzes, but the extra credit makes up for it. Very nice in office hours. Emailed him about submitting something wrong and kindly gave an excuse for it and got full points. Do all the extra credit, study the tweaks, pray that the final isn't as bad as F25 it should be an easy A.

While PHYS 172 is a tough class, professor Rebello gives you everything you need to succeed. From the large amounts of extra credit, to the tweak videos for the exams and problem quizzes, he truly wants success for his students. Don't be afraid to ask questions and put in the work for this class, it is very easy to get overwhelmed with everything.

I mostly watched Dr. Rebello's pre-lectures and post-lectures to learn the material in the course. He does a pretty good explaining those concepts. He also seems like a pretty cheerful dude, never met him IRL or attended his lectures tho.

Professor Rebello is great at simplifying heavy concepts so everyone is on the same page. He genuinely cares about how students do in the class, if you reach out to him about struggles in phys he will do what he can to put you on the right path and help you move forward and be  better in the future! Great prof!

Professor Rebello is an incredibly nice guy and truly cares about his students' success. PHYS 172 is a course with a large workload and tricky exams, but the extra credit make it so that it's quite manageable to get a good grade. Make sure to watch his online lectures, as I generally found them very helpful for succeeding in the class.

Didnt have him personally, but he was coordinator for 172. Gives out tons of extra credit and has detailed videos explaining every topic in the course. Gives out tons of extra credit in the second half of the semester that makes it impossible to fail if you do it. The final was extremely difficult and unlike any practice material or past exams.

Rebello is reasonable and genuinely wants students to succeed. The workload is heavy, with frequent problem quizzes and assignments, so you need to stay on top of things. Exams were challenging, but there was a lot of extra credit offered throughout the semester. If you're prepared to put in consistent effort, the class is demanding but manageable.

Sanjay has incredibly easy practice exams and incredibly hard exams. The weekly problem quizzes are ridicusly tough as well, everyone I know cheats on them because there is no way.
Horrible professor, makes an easy class hard, and made me hate physics.
STAY AWAY

Dr. Rebello organizes this class in the worst way. It's impossible to find any of the material that you need to succeed and the problem quizzes have ridiculously little content to review. For what is really not that hard of a class, he makes it one to try and convince the school that this class is hard enough. Take AP Physics C and get out of this.

When I tell you, I put in absolutely 0 effort into this class, I mean it. I didn't pay attention to any lecture, never watched any videos, and didn't study till the day before the tests. There is so much extra credit available; Sanjay sets the class up in a way that is impossible to fail. He is fr the GOAT.

Class has loads of extra credit, which is nice.
Not very organized between recitation, lab, lectures and homework (We would often see stuff in the homework that wasn't in the lectures).
Course Brightspace is confusing, as he uses a whole load of colors, making it actually hard to read anything.
Prepare to teach yourself. Explains *very* poorly.
makes a bunch of videos that if you watch and understand ur guaranteed an a basically
Prof. Robello spends a lot of time working on videos that teaches us the contents. Although they are long, they really helped with exams and quizzes

Great professor!! He is so wonderful to talk to in office hours, and really cares about how we are doing in his class. The videos he makes for lectures and problem quizzes are super helpful. Would take again!

Course workload was very demanding. Expect a quiz, lab, recitation, 2 HW, & 2 lectures per week. DO NOT fall behind. Rebello had videos for each quiz and midterm, however they're long. Watching these videos are key for midterms & heavily pulls questions from practice exams and will hint at what problems to know how to solve. Lenient grading/curve.

You learn basically all the content in lecture so if you go and pay attention you'll be fine. For labs you work with the same group all semester for every lab so try to sit with competent people. Most labs take about an hour, but a couple take the full class time. Professor and TA hold your hand through everything.

I feel that Dr. Rebello makes incredible and engaging videos that help me learn a large amount. They are so informative that most of the time I don't even learn as much from the actual mandatory lectures. His videos are also very helpful for when reviewing for quizzes and tests, and I like that he provides review documents for exams
Class has a very heavy workload and quizzes can be very difficult at times. However Professor Rebello does a good job making plenty of videos and other online resources to help you succeed.

Professor Rebello answers questions sent through email quickly, understands student's situations, and makes it very clear what he plans to test on problem quizzes and tests. At some points the course can be challenging but if you watch all the provided videos and study it isn't that bad.
He wasnt very good

Do you like reading emails highlighted in ten different colors? 
Do you like a nice curve?
This class is for you!

While difficult and ill-organized, it's highkey designed for you to succeed. You can mess up a whole exam and still end up with an A-level grade. Be prepared to teach yourself, though. The lecture was useless to me.

I took PHYS 172 in Fall 2023 and because there were mistakes on the exams, the curve was extremely good. Approximately 50% or more of the class ended the semester with an A- or better. The Pre Class videos were dry so I didn't watch them. Make sure you go to Lectures, Labs, and Recitations because if you go then it is a guaranteed 100% usually.

This man loves to make everything as confusing as possible. Not to mention half of the stuff he posts is in all caps. Which kinda defeats the purpose. I cant even go on the Brightspace page without being overstimulated (in the worst way) by colors and CAPS FOR EVERYTHING.

I love Dr. Rebello! He is a very easygoing professor and is very understanding. The course was not too hard even if you do not have a background in physics (just thinking sometimes and not overcomplicating does the trick). Having helpful TAs also made the class much more efficient.

He jam packs the course with too many content and the copious amount of work assigned out of class is simply too much to keep up with. We also never had enough time to properly master the material because we're made to take 4 exams for an entire semester. He does however answer emails quickly and is generous with curving people's grades.

She definitely comes off intimidating early, but she explains concepts well and is easy to ask questions to. Pretty homework intensive and tests can be tricky, but there are enough resources that you can memorize some question structures and brute force your way through the tricky true or false and theory questions.

Rlly good lecturer. Took MA 265. She goes really fast but I didn't find her hard to understand at all, & the benefit of this is she ends up covering almost everything needed to complete the homework for every lecture. I remember our class finishing all the content about a week before every exam, which helped  me to start studying early for exams

This is my first review. She might come off as intimidating at first but she is very caring. She is one of the best math professors at Purdue. The only 'bad thing' would be her accent (its not bad, but it takes getting used to since she goes at a pretty fast pace).
Terrible professor

Amazing professor. She explains the concepts and not just how to solve the problem. All lecture notes are posted before the first day of class. She posts solutions to past exams and puts together questions which are relevant to the midterms. Midterm grades were posted within 6 hours. She appears to talk with a harsh tone, but she's really nice.

Lectures are very fast pace and zero intuition is ever explained for what you are doing. More of a reflection on the course organizers but I couldn't get anything out of lecture so I just watched Chenflix and 3B1b on my own time. Nice person but lecture just didn't work out for me. Very quick turnaround on grades like night of which I appreciated.

Honestly, the best math professor that I've had at Purdue. She can seem a little scary at first, but once you go to a few lectures, she's amazing! She's super understanding and really nice. She made me love this class, honestly. Get her at all costs if you are taking MA 265.

Her lectures were decent, but sometimes she could be hard to follow, and you would have to learn some things by yourself. Midterm review was mostly helpful, but boilerexams was the best resource overall.

Honestly, one of the best math professors here at Purdue... She cares about her students, and makes Linear Algebra quite fun and easy to understand. At first, she can seem kinda scary but once you keep attending lectures shes pretty top-tier as far as Purdue math goes.

I really couldn't recommend it.  The lectures are incoherent.  The tests and quizzes aren't taken from the homework on pearson, it's a whole different kind of question prompt.  So you're expected to review old exams and guess what will be on it.  Worst of all, the time given to do quizzes and tests isn't enough for cautious test takers.

Ying Zhang was pretty good at teaching, she has a noteable accent but her explanations are pretty clear. She kinda scary at first like asian tiger mom kinda, but she's rly good, her sections always average highly.

Ying zhang is possibly the best math professor Ive had at purdue.  If you can get past her accent (which few people did not), her lectures and notes are very well structured and organized.  Another bonus is that she writes the exams for the course (i think???) and lots of theory from her notes will appear word for word on the exams.

Explains concepts clearly. You will get used to her accent after a week. Notes are thorough and some statements will appear word for word on the exams. Exam grades will be available when you wake up the following morning.

Professor Zhang's lectures had a lot of information but were difficult to follow at times. She went through the lecture material very fast and it was often hard to understand her accent. The notes she posted online sometimes didn't match the notes she wrote down during class.
Professor Zhang is the best Purdue math professor I've had. Her lectures are good, and she gives exam grades back less than 6 hours after each exam ends. She is also accessible outside of class.

Taken during the summer; for a summer class, broke 266 down very well to core concepts. Some lectures are hard to understand with her camera problems, but she explains things well. 

I took 265 and 266 at the same time, and learned more about 265 from her 266 class than I did my 265 lecturer. Lol. Great professor. Hard class.

I found Dr. Zhang to be very knowledgeable about linear algebra. However, sometimes her lectures were very difficult to understand and make sense of the concepts. Her handwriting and accent can be difficult to understand at first, but it isn't too bad. Make sure to understand the key concepts that are introduced at the beginning of the course.

Dr. Zhang is a very fair grader and more understanding with any situations that may arise. She's fair and lenient, which can't be said for a lot of professors. She also sits with students to explain the concepts thoroughly. At the same time, her lectures are a bit harsh to get through due to bad camera quality and scribbly handwriting. Good prof.

Great professor. Covers everything thoroughly, and answers all questions very clearly, so students don't have any doubts afterwards. The lectures are a bit fast (due to the amount of course material), but being online solves that problem by allowing the lecture to be paused to fill out the notes. Solid professor overall.
She does care about her students and the online transfer is very smooth. However, the language barrier prevents her from explaining the concepts clearly and the lectures are not super structured.
Extremely bad accent and doesnt really care much about her students. She just cares about completing the course material and then boasting about herself.
Zhang is a pretty good professor. Minor accent but not so much as to detract from lectures. Answers questions pretty well.

She is a great professor! It takes a couple of weeks to get used to her accent, but she is really easy to understand after that. She also has really clear handwriting. Sometimes she goes a little fast, but she kind of has to because the class is so full of material. She also was helpful in office hours. Would definitely take another class with her

After you get used to her accent, she is a great professor. She covers the necessary content thoroughly, and gives clear instruction on the theories. The lectures are fast paced, but clear and easy to understand. She made diffEQ fairly easy

This class is perfect. She's lecture is clear and logical and the review cession helps a lot with the exam. Though the attendence is not required, I strongly recommend not to miss class, lectures are really great. The metiarials covered are complex and there is a work load. A good grade is not free, but as long as you work hard, you can get an A

I thought the books for the course were pretty interesting and the quizzes were really easy if you read them. Participation and speaking matters, but as long as you give it your best shot you'll do well. Very kind and facilitates discussions really well.

she's a very easy grader on assignments but it's very important that you participate in class discussions somehow. personally i found her difficult to pay attention to, not very good engaging the class, but the course was light and instructions are clear.

Participate a lot, one sentence during discussions won't cut it. She will dock you if you don't speak enough. Even 1 minute late, she will dock you. Presentations are easy, just stick to the rubric. Quizzes are really tough; SparkNotes won't cut it. You actually have to study really hard to get a decent quiz grade. Not like other SCLA102 classes!

Very kind person. Attendance adds up for a large portion of your grade. Sylabus isn't very organized, hard to know how many assignments there are + what each assignment is worth, tbh idk if she has it set in stone at the start of the semester. Get a good relationship with her, and she will be more generous to you with your speech grades.

She does not seem to care much for her students in general. She opened the year by stating that she had written her attendance policy in such a way that if we missed class and she could prove we weren't sick, she would take us to the dean for academic dishonesty. A lot of the class time is spent on long winded tangents that move at a snails pace.

She is very harsh on attendance, if you do not participate multiple times per class you will get marked down, and if you have any electronics out when not supposed to (which includes computers to take notes), you will be marked absent. Overall pretty easy class but it is necessary to read the books as summaries online will not do the trick.

Great professor who seems very caring and makes adjustments based on students' needs. Just know that attendance, as well as class participation, are for points. She offered many extra credit options that were extremely helpful, and she maintained a good class pace.

I took SCLA 102 with Prof Watkins and enjoyed the class, she cares about her students and is clear when explaining expectations and assignments. The in class discussions were always fun and the tests are not very difficult if you actually read the books. As long as you show up, participate in class discussions, and read, you should end up with an A

She was quick on grading and responding to emails. Class was enjoyable. She gained the student's respect from the beginning. Class for SCLA 102 was pretty easy. Make sure you read the books, (cliff notes may not work for quizzes), participate in class discussions as well as do the other assignments, you'll be fine. Great teacher.

Dr. Watkins is a good professor, she is very chill. However, the speeches were a little difficult, but thankfully I still managed to finish with a respectable grade. As long as you put in the effort you will be fine.

Professor Watkins is a phenomenal professor. She is very chill but makes sure you are engaged. There is a decent amount of reading but it is very managle. Class participation is important but not answering questions in class all the time won't tank your grade. Her 102 class that i took had 4 total speeches, 1 is a group speach.

She is a very lenient grader when it comes to the speeches. I am a horrible speaker and was still able to get an A in the class. The speeches were all spaced out really well so that there were at least 3 weeks between each one. Overall a great professor.
An extremely kind teacher! Awesome discussion during class! Truly a joy to have
Professor Watkins is ver kind and her classroom atmosphere is always calm and inviting. She grades reasonably and the class is not difficult at all.
Good teacher

Professor Watkins is a great person who truly cares about her students. She wants to see everyone succeed. I would recommend taking her class as long as you do every assignment on time and participate in class you will do well.

Prof Watkins is a great prof. She is incredibly nice and caring, and her classroom has a great aura because of this. Class isnt too tough, and she is very flexible. Would definitely take this again, Watkins was probably my best prof this semester

Very good lecturer and I like how he breaks down problems instead of just clicking through slides. I went to his office hours and he was very down to Earth and great to talk to! I liked his practical demonstrations and how he says "square root"

Tyler was a funny and knowledgeable professor and I enjoyed every lecture. He did spend a lot of time doing algebra when solving some problems. But he still made PHYS 172 a pretty fun learning experience. I really enjoyed the whole class applauding after every demo.
Highly recommend Tyler for PHYS172. He is a super nice guy and engaging lecturer. I especially like his slower pace when solving problems.
Great lecturer. Made sure that everyone understood what was going on. He always makes an effort to clarify his explanations if they are confusing.
He made the lectures very engaging. I thought he explained the material well and I liked his demonstrations. He was also very easy to talk to and a nice person.

Nice guy sure but not that great of a teacher. Phys 172 is already known as a very poorly designed course, but he skipped over actual concepts/understanding of material and spent obnoxiously long on practice problems just doing algebra. Like lecture should be opposite if anything. ChatGPT was my exam prep. Luckily easy A class
Listens to student feedback and shows real interest in what he's teaching. Very engaging professor.
Great lectures! Liked his slower pace when explaining problems. Very nice person.
Out of all the physics profs, he is the most interesting and the least likely to put you to sleep. I also thought he was kind of cute
I love this guy. Funny and teaches the class in a great way. Able to understand the course because of him.

Ellison taught the class in the most understandable way out of all the PHYS 172 lecturers. He made his class feel significantly more welcoming and comfortable, making the learning environment enjoyable. He taught in a way that actually made sense and taught the material even if he often took a slightly longwinded way to get an answer.

Tyler Denman Ellison is the absolute GOAT. You wouldn't even know this was his first year teaching PHYS 172 let alone his first year teaching at Purdue. He does a great job breaking down and explaining concepts and encourages his students to ask questions during lecture. We clapped after all his demos so please please please do the same.

Ellison felt like a awesome second grade teacher who's kind and gentle.  However his lectures usually were paced too slowly for me (some students preferred this!) and his presentations always derivated the long way and sometimes didn't cover equations and concepts that would appear in the homework and on exams.
He is perfect for PHYS172. He is patient, well-paced, and his teaching is very clear. Highly recommend!!
Super helpful and clear explanations. Grading is standardized, so the lecturer doesn't matter, but I recommend going to Tyler's lectures even if you don't have him!!
Does not follow APA7, invented his own writing guide called ARWE.

It starts with hating the course, but eventually, we will understand that the course improves our writing skills. The only drawback is that assignments have redundant questions of the same kind, so we have to write the same stuff but in a different format again.

He claims that the class uses APA and prepares you for writing the thesis/dissertation with the template already styled. You will have to make significant changes before submitting your proposal or research paper to the graduate school because the template is off by miles. He will deduct many points for not following his instructions.

A politician in an engineering department. This course has barely anything to do with MET but here we are taking it as a mandatory course that occupies over 1/2 of my weekly school work time. Also the final project is as cringey as it is time consuming.

This guy is so full of himself. 'As a Full Professor, it is my job to be extra hard to protect the reputation of Purdue.' And "If you miss 2 classes you lose a letter grade', seriously there are 32 classes that account for 10% of the grade, how does that math work. Do NOT take this guy if you like yourself.
Tons of reading and writing Very hard on writing requirements. The course states that it uses APA but don't buy the APA book. The instructor has his own version of APA he enforces.
Went thru slides quickly and a little bit monotone, but funny and knowledgeable about content.

Be ready to move fast. Slideshows are good to show intuition and derivations. While you should understand the intuition behind the formulas, focus more on knowing the formulas themselves. Otherwise, you will fall behind. Peterson is funny and knows his stuff. Success in the course requires engagement in lecture. The textbook is also very useful.

I thought that professor Peterson's lectures were alright, and mostly came from the slides. However, the slides were pretty detailed and were always posted, and it was his first time teaching the course. I would say he was pretty understanding as well.

Spring 2026 semester was an off semester so Dr. Sun was the course coordinator. Course was made mistakenly easy, but exams still required ample amount of studying and were fair. Lectures often ended early and Dr. Sun made sure to stick around for any remaining questions. Great prof!
Don't know what happened with 301, but goated for 203

He's great at cutting out fluff in the class and focusing on what you need to know for the exams. Even though he ends class early often, he's always available at the end of lectures for students to ask questions if needed. We finished content for each exam well ahead of schedule so we also had extra time to study/prepare.

He's pretty soft spoken but even with his accent his lectures are pretty clear and easy to follow. We got through all the course material 3 weeks before the final. He loves to be thorough with practice problems and is very clear on what he expect from you on tests. Unfortunately tests comprise 90% of the grade but he prepares you really well.

Prof. Sun is great at skimming out the extra info in lecture. He really cuts down the information that is completely irrelevant or we have all know since middle school to make time to go over homework at the end. In my opinion, he teaches with a lot of clarity. Really wish I didn't sell my A in this class.

bahaha what a semester. Of the 5 tests, only 2 happened. Cuz of more grading things, I didn't have to take the final and got an A. No curve, but 4% free EC and lots of freebies. Sun ended class like pretty early usually, I thought it was nice, he was a chill dude. He kinda dgaf abt his students or office hrs tho.

Horrible professor. Speeds through a 75-minute lecture in 20-30 minutes consistently. Changed the syllabus frequently with quiz cancellations because we were very behind on the course content. In the end, half of the syllabus material was never even taught. Even with an A, I don't feel like I learned anything in the course.
Good Grading but very, very disorganized

4% EC given in class, no curve. Lecture slides are well formatted, but Sun only teaches for 50 mins max in a 75 min class. HWs are fair and graded well, you just have to know what you're doing and not be afraid of office hours. Exams are difficult & computationally heavy but very similar to HWs. No study guide & exams have comprehensive EQ sheets.

Finish a 75min lecture in 15 min reading off 30+ poor-format slides crammed with useless info. Speed is way too fast to learn anything. Unable to solve his own practice problems if his TA doesn't do it for him. No exam prepare at all while no grades are curved. No partial credit for exams only correct answer count. Heavy homework graded nitpicking.

Professor Sun is a solid choice. Extremely helpful, gives lots of example problems and has extra credit opportunities. Only critique is that he is very soft spoken and monotone in lectures, but pay attention and he gives you actually helpful information.
DSun is an actual god, take him for 203 and you'll understand everything. This man gives you the ravioli and the formuoli

Professor Sun makes a difficult subject much easier to comprehend. He provides lots of worked examples in class, is readily available outside of class as needed, and he adds helpful calculation shortcuts in lecture that you won't find in the class text/notes. If you put for the effort in his class, he'll go the distance to help you too.
If you want to learn about steel design his textbook and his lectures are the best source. Glad to have him in all design courses. One of the best at Purdue!
I learned so much in his class. One of the very best professors at Purdue CCE program.
Fantastic lectures!
Great lectures.
He is a good professor but sometimes very strict and harsh. The exams were very hard, but since 50% of the class is homework and labs, it is doable to pass this class

He's a good lecturer, homeworks/labs are doable but can be very time-consuming, exams are very difficult but not outrageous. Doesn't grade attendance but complains regularly about people not showing up to early morning lectures.

Prof is harsh about attendance and rants to students about those who don't show up. He “lost his appetite” grading. He implies that if students aren't getting good grades they aren't trying hard. This is insulting to the those putting in a lot of effort. He is very knowledgeable but he often seems too critical of his students who are trying hard.
The best course I have aver taken in my life! Amazing professor, very knowledgeable.

People who dislike this guy are those who incapable of putting effort to succeed. he gives so many opportunities for you to help not only your learning, but your grade as well. Anyone with below a 4 rating on this thread is coping.
Although the lecture time is early in the morning but he is doing a great job engaging the students. Knowledgeable and cares about his students. I will take any courses he offers at Purdue.
He cares about his students and always available to help them. Very knowledgeable!
Amazing Professor! One of the best I have ever seen at Purdue. Cares about students!
By far, the best professor I have had at Purdue. He knows his stuff and cares about his students.

I found this class to be unreasonably hard. The homeworks were impossible to get above a C on. During our 8:30 am lecture, the prof would frequently yell at the class about the majority who weren't there. TAs were unhelpful and rude during exams.

This professor frequently speaks about how the student's confusion disgusts him and causes him to 'lose his appetite,' although the confusion stems from the fact that the lectures do not correlate with the exams and that the homework is genuinely impossible to do well on. No amount of studying could make you prepared for exams. Never again.

Exams are very difficult, and don't correlate to the notes. He tends to rant a lot in lecture especially about people who don't come to class, which is annoying bc he's talking to the people who are there. He gets upset if people don't know how to solve things and blames the bad averages on students 'not studying enough.' He "loses his appetite."

Dr. Soleimani takes himself and his class very seriously. I see lots of ratings for his higher level courses, and I fully believe that he is a solid professor for those. For CE270, his exams and averages have been extremely tough, to the point that even our TA's have asked him to make exams shorter, but he fully blames the class for low grades.
An excellent professor to teach design courses!
He knows his stuff. You learn a lot from him, one of the best professors I met at Purdue.
Fantastic Professor, one of the best at LSCCE!

CE 473 - Likes to gripe about how underpaid the industry is. Likes even more to point out the biggest mistakes he sees in design, as well as the textbooks as well. Still can't hate him, because he definitely acts this way for a reason - he knows his stuff. This professor could teach any structural class.
He is a great teacher and knows the material well. Take any courses he offers!

He is very knowledgable in his field and is good at lectures. However, the exams were outrageous, they were so much harder than any lecture or homework assignment, having you solve problems in a way that you have never done before and having no way to prepare for that level. I failed the class just because of how heavy the exams were weighted.
One of the best professors I have ever had. Such an easy class!
I will take any courses he offers, you learn quite a lot in his class!

I took his Finite Element Analysis in Chemical Engineering (CHE 697) course, and it was one of the best courses I have ever taken. It was truly inspiring. He is an outstanding professor whose lecture notes were exceptionally well prepared. He is also a genuinely kind and supportive person.
Good yes f
Get ready to learn a lot.

There is no question Osman Basaran is a great professor, however as a teacher he is terrible. He is passionate and wants you to learn but appears unable to condense the material down to the basics. Fluid Mechanics should be about granting an engineer a basic understanding of fluids, pipping systems, and pumps. I left his class with none of that.

Just do the homework assignments and try to learn (because he really wants everyone to learn), then everything will be more than fine. He loves teaching, he is so much passionate about science, he is a good mentor to everyone, he cares about students and he is super inspirational. He is good that's why he is teaching the toughest classes.

Dr. Basaran is an inspirational professor. His passion toward his subject is infectious and motivates you to pursue research in fluid mechanics! The subject material he covers finds direct use in understanding many aspects of research in diverse fields. He is tough but fair. I wholeheartedly recommend taking any courses he teaches, totally worth it

Professor Basaran was a great professor who really made the course content interesting and delivered great lectures. However, the homeworks and exams were extremely difficult with very low average scores. Overall great professor who made the class interesting and was always willing to help, but the content of the course was very difficult.

Easily one of the smartest professors in CHE. Do well on the homework and above average on the exams (average is about 5% or lower.) And you will get an A.  Go to class and show you are interested and ask questions. More effort you put better your grade will be without worrying too much about doing well

He's probably the best and the toughest professor I've had in my life! But he's fair... And he really wants one to learn the material more than anything else! He will help more than anything... DO THE HOMEWORK AND YOULL BE FINE WITH A B... But actually though he's awesome!!!! He provides notes cause off which you don't need the text book!

Basaran is a brilliant man, but I feel he has a hard time connecting with the students on what we actually understand. Most of the homeworks and exams are impossible, BUT he just wants to see you try. Put effort into the class and take time out to show him that you're trying. Don't drive yourself mad over the class. Put in effort and you get a B!

Basaran knows what he's talking about, but you never will. He moves quickly through material, gives WAY too much homework, and the exams are impossible. But hang in there, apparently everyone gets a B in the end.

This is the HARDEST class I have ever taken, like ever. There is an immense amount of material, and unfortunately its all rather difficult. He is very helpful, but unfortunately it is almost impossible to get a hold of him. Don't be discouraged if you fail the exams, if everyone fails, no one fails!

Learned more in this class than in all my other ChE courses I took in the last year.  You have to be ready to learn fast and keep on top of your toes otherwise you'll start falling behind before the second week of class.  He assumes you know everything from physics and calculus.  If you're stuck on concepts he'll gladly help you at office hours.
Not that great of a professor.  He likes to think he is the best though.  Pretty arrogant, and easy to trip up.  Just ask him a question to prove one of his statements and watch the hilarity ensue.
Extremely brilliant Prof. Doesn't grade a single homework or assignment but so much to learn from him.

Professor was a very very intelligent and caring man. He just fails to connect well with students at times. He knows what he is talking about, but I don't. Available for help and openly welcomes questions. Hard exams. I got a 13% and was pleased I was above average.
Best professor i have had at purdue. an all around great guy who knows the material very well.

Amazing that time cannot dull his issues. Obviously brilliant man, but no real connection with the students or understanding of what he is (or in most cases, is NOT) clearly conveying. Very obvious from his test averages- in my class 13%, 21%, 24% and a whopping 30% on the final exam.  Its Purdue, you do have a choice in professors.  Chose wisely!

Can you say Neopolean Complex???  This is a little man has major attitude combined with disregard for whether his students are actually learning!!!  He called our whole class stupid when we couldn't get his teaching....  horrible teacher.
No Comments
systematic, and sincere teacher... challenges the students with tough questions.  Extremely hard exams.  sets high standards for himself and for the students...  excellent class notes!
He is a good teacher.  Very smart.  Yet, his exams are hard.. average 13%
Explains clearly. Exams are extremely hard!

I love Prof. Vian! Yes, she was hard to understand sometimes but she's very kind and gives students a lot of chances for extra credit and easy points. As long as you go to class and labs, you'll get at least a B in this class. I recommend getting the lab manual before school starts because it sells out fast. This class was fun overall!

Professor Van is one of the most inconvenient and lackluster professors I've had at Purdue. Her lectures are hard to follow (accent), and the material often feels disconnected from the materials or engineering. MET 143 feels like wasted lecture time twice a week, plus a two-hour lab that somehow feels just as pointless, unhelpful, and frustrating.

Labs were wonderful and felt revelant to MET as a whole, lectures were related to the labs and at times she can be difficult to understand but i would say she is a average lecturer. Attendance quizzes after almost every class, but she prepares her students for the tests, and I feel she genuinely cares about what they learn in her class.

The professor is hard to understand, has very bad lectures, and does not work to help her students learn more. There are graded attendance quizzes almost every class. I haven't had an updated grade in that class since the biggening of the semester.

“Lectures” equate to skipping half of info-heavy slides, and tangents on others. Doesn't know how to actually help students in labs and refuses to demonstrate labs that may be incredibly dangerous if done incorrectly. (Hides behind students when another student is using a blowtorch which she refused to demonstrate how to handle first).
She can be difficult to follow at times. However, if you make an effort (asking questions, office hours, etc.) he course will be rewarding. She's also hillarious!
Tough and hard to understand but caring. If you go to her office hours, she is friendly and funny. She will teach you 1 on 1 and make the content way more understandable and easy.
Wei is very kind. She obviously cares about her students; however, she is a poor instructor. Expect to teach yourself most of the information.

Could not be a more unenjoyable professor. Lectures have absolutely nothing to do with exams or labs but she makes you go since there is an attendance quiz every class. Exams are basically impossible due to the fact that she purposefully misleads you on the study guide and writes questions that make no sense.

Professor Vian was a very nice professor, she clearly cared, however, this does not mean she was a good professor. She has a very archaic way of teaching, she writes everything on the chalk board, and writes everything from a paper she has. This means no slides are posted, and she just writes down and doesn't do too much explaining.

Good lecturer, but also likes to lecture students (and TAs) about life and career, despite having only a smattering of industry experience outside Purdue and not having a PhD in CS. His course materials, knowledge, perspectives, and really everything, are just outdated, but he somehow refuses to admit anything, gets aggressive when corrected.

hes not the worst in the world but homeworks were VERY long. probably spent at least 7 hrs a week in office hours getting help from TAs. exams are extremely fair if you actually do the hw. i would not want to talk to the prof one on one but other than that it was fine.

Professor Turkstra's lectures were organized and yet disorganized at the same time. He would randomly go on tangents about things that was never spoken of again. The homeworks take an incredibly long time and there is a shortage of TAs in office hours. Besides that he attempted to be funny and even hosted weekly lunches. Exams aren't too bad.

I mean he is not that great of a lecturer and nothing correlates to hws. I spent hours in office hours with TAs just to get the homework done. I just recommend taking a lighter semester whenever you take this class if you want a good grade and want to learn. He is not the worst person ever, but i would never want to interact with him one on one

Yes he can be rude, but he is a good lecturer and I found his lectures very engaging. Homework is a beast but doable. Textbook is "required" but you can get by without it. Dual grade cutoff is annoying. As long as you do the work and understand the material, you'll be fine. Remember, the easiest way to not get caught cheating is to not cheat.
Richard Quinn of our age.

this class says its 3 credits, but it's more like 9 credits and 6 of those credits are insomnia credits due to how long the homework is. No wonder so many people used ai. It's possible to be good at all the concepts and applications IRL and just do poorly due to how the homework and exams are structured. 5/5 it's pure purdue GRIT, would take again.

I think some comments here are too harsh. Yes he thinks he is a super villian and yes he has a superiority complex and it shows. But he is here to teach. He is not here to make us happy and hold our hands and sing. Just do what you are supposed to do, avoid interacting with him as you can. That's all you need to do.

He needs to improve his attitude. Gets paranoid of the smallest things, including picking on a student who used their phone to CLEARLY help take notes. Has the most demotivating grading scale and doesn't lecture well at all. Homework takes so long to do and has the most egregious coding styles. Please avoid at all costs, no matter what.

He was just alright and lectures weren't particularly helpful. 180 is so easy that it doesn't matter. Midterms were fair, but the final exam all multiple choice felt like a joke as the questions felt like random facts he didn't teach.
Ridiculous grading scheme. Tough for no reason.

Acts like he cares for his students then proceeds to provide homework that can take a minimum of 30+ hours that must be completed within 1 week (3 credit class btw). Furthermore, tests things on the exam that were BARELY mentioned in class.
Homework is extremely difficult and long. Poor teaching style and lack of care for the students.
Lectures are ok, homeworks take about 15-20 house a week, exams are fair, course itself is structured not very well

This class and its AI policies literally messed up my period cycle and shortened it to 3 days (its an avg of 4-5 days). The high levels of corsitol I experienced changed my physiology. Take that as u will.
Second-worst Jeff to get media coverage. Here is your requested professor review. If you would like a more firm revised version or need anything else let me know!
Too many babies... Yes, many of my classmates use A.I. I've seen it first hand... Everyone is complaining because they don't put in the work.

CS students shouldnt use AI to fully complete homework assignments but its not worth trying to expel students over using AI for help. Have any suspected student sit down with the professor or other depatment head and code the alleged cheated homework live on an offline PC. This helps them learn and would allow the proessor to teach better.

While I do not agree with how he enforced the AI policy, I believe he did the right thing. However, saying "this is an easy situation, that's just how the world is" to people who he falsefully accused by mistake is not a way to go. He expects people to show respect, but evidently he does not have any. Good class though.
He isn't even that bad, you just should not use Artificial Intelligence for learning. He allows use of AI in 307, so just be patient and learn the fundamental concepts.
Was not accomodative of anything. Has a superiority complex.

He knows what he is teaching and is very good at it. But I feel like he is very insecure, given how he treats people around him (including his own TAs), hates everyone if something does not move in a way he intended. He is a good lecturer though, you will learn a lot.

Turkstra and CS 240 is a complete mess. Acknowledged that he completely messed up in the way he worded a 40 point question on one of the midterms. Same can be said about his HWs which take at least 20 hrs a week. Has an overall superiority complex. Plus he has a grating laugh and people only go to lecture in case he drops a pop quiz
Turkstra makes CS 240 so much harder than it needs to be. The content itself isn't too bad, but the course structure and extra requirements make it a nightmare.

DO NOT TAKE TURKSTRA. He does not have a teaching attitude about things. He is hostile towards his students. Lectures are ok. Homeworks are a mess. Take north of 20+ hrs/week without AI. TAs can only help you to a small extent and you are on your own. Cares more about failing his students than teaching them.

Professor Haq makes this course pretty manageable if you do alright on the lab exams There are lecture exams, but they are done online. The lab exams are tough and require some studying, but they are not overly difficult. As long as you don't forget to do the connect assignments, post lecture quizzes, this course should be too difficult.

Prof. Haq makes this class an easy A. Lectures were quite boring, but iClicker forces you to be there. Exams were all online and open notes. Lab exams required a bit of preparation, but otherwise easy. Homework/reading/attendance were not put on Brightspace until the last day, but only 2/3 of it counted. Final exam was optional.

this guys lectures r super boring but i dont blame him, the content is just bland imo. exams r online and homework is pearson so rly easy to do well. labs r rly fun lab exams r fine except 2. i felt bad for Haq a lot because he talked quiet and ppl always talked over him. if that was u, be ashamed.

Lectures can be a bit boring, but class exams are pretty easy if you pay attention, or even study before them. Labs are tedious and you have to show up to it every single week, and it can get a bit annoying. Lab exams are not super easy but doable. LOTS of dropped points when it comes to Iclicker and assignment grades (but not labs). Overall easy.
boring. so very boring. i don't know a single person who actually paid attention in class. luckily that's fine because nothing he talks about really matters. seems like a decent guy though

His lectures are pretty clear-cut, but slow and often cover information exactly from the textbook. The class overall isn't bad at all - homeworks are easy, labs are collaborative and aren't bad as well, lecture exams are fine, and lab exams can be tricky but aren't bad if you study a bit. The professor seems like a nice guy, but a dry lecturer.
Extremely lecture heavy. Be prepared to listen to monotone speaking for an hour.

Not too much work: online hw, iClicker, exams. Lecture exams are easy as they are online and you can reference your notes + other content. Lab exams are manageable if you study and understand the weekly lab handouts. HIGHLY recommend attending the evening review held by the TAs for lab exam 2 because they cover everything. Overall very easy class.

His lectures are not particularly interesting, and most students only show up for the iClicker points. He also assigned homework through McGraw Connect, which cost a lot, and we had to purchase/rent an iClicker as well. Overall, taking EAPS 111 with him is an easy A if you just do the work and study for the lab exams.
Very hard to get in contact with. I took his EAPS 116 class and there was not TA listed on the syllabus or other means of contact. Average email response time was 2 months.

Dr. Haq is getting a bit of hate here, but I honestly enjoyed him. He's got a very dry sense of humor which I found really funny and while he doesn't look up from his slides, he knows his stuff. If you just pay attention in class, do homework, maybe take some notes, it's an easy A. Only reason I took a point off is because he never answers emails

Dr. Haq is one of the most boring lecturers I've ever witnessed. He stares at his own PowerPoint presentation the entire class and reads it in the most monotone voice possible. This class is supposed to be an easy, fun and interesting Lab Science but he makes the exams extremely hard to do well on.

You can get an A but it's not worth the effort.
Lab exams are REALLY difficult and over-all the lab section of the course is a pain. Lectures are boring. He's a nice dude outside of class but this course stinks.

The professor takes teaching the intro level EAPS class very seriously. Be sure to show up to class and do the quizzes. The materials can be a bit of work, but the class is straightforward overall for a lab science.

He often looks to be done with this class, very dry lectures, reading off the slides. The class uses McGraw Connect that costs $70+ and iClicker, and you need to do homework and iClicker quizzes before/during lectures. Exams are pretty easy, although labs are fairly hard. Clear grading criteria, he drops a lot of assignment and 90.5 was A.

Overall, I enjoyed the class. The lectures were informative, but not super exciting or engaging. It's important to attend lectures for iClicker questions. The homework was through McGraw Hill Connect which was really convenient and helped me learn the concepts pretty well.
Very boring lecture but you need to attend for clicker questions. Also very disorganized.

Mr. Haq was a nice professor to talk to outside of class. He worked with me to improve my grade and I ended with a B. However, I found this class extremely difficult. Mr. Haq's lectures were unorganized, full of grammar errors, and he did not seem excited about teaching. He was very nervous while teaching and lacked confidence.

Very disorganized, doesnt seem to know whats happening most of the time. Has graded absolutely nothing from anything this semester. The class itself is extremely easy if you need to complete a requirement, but dont expect to actually learn from this guy.
He tries to make a class which is supposed to be one of the most easiest classes at Purdue hard for no reason. He wants to appear professional but is completely disorganized and rude at times!

Pretty easy class, just study a day or two before the exams. Homework given is way too much and points are taken off for wrong answers. Pop quizzes in class but you can discuss with neighbors. Professor does not look up while lecturing, easy to fall asleep. Easy A if you're looking to get rid of a Gen Ed req or an STS req.

Professor Haq is very nervous and won't answer students email-- which is so annoying. His slides have lots of errors. He gives out extra information on exam reviews and covers too much material in one class period. Because of this, students are unable to adequately take notes during the lectures and he does not post full lectures on Blackboard.
He was a good professor and very professional. Show up to class and do the exam reviews.

Textbook mandatory for online homework access. Easy class, very easy material. Is very clear on what he grades and how. Homeworks are incredibly easy. Exams are medium difficulty, but not hard if you study for a couple of hours. EAPS 106 and 100 are easier if you're just looking to fill an elective.
Good Lectures

Professor Matangi is a great professor who tries his best to teach difficult material to the students. Lectures get boring at times, so be prepared. The exams are manageable if you understand the practice exams. There is extra credit on exams that is a lifesaver. Homework and assignments are annoying, but multiple attempts are given on them.

He's very helpful when you have questions. He is not the coordinator so all the 350 lectures just teach off the old slides from the coordinator. They're not too helpful and go into too much derivations. He's also very sweet and offered good policies to recover after a poor midterm 1.

Super knowledgable! Gets through lectures quickly and cancels class a lot. Does a good job warning you about the tough parts of the exam. He is also very laid back with logistical stuff; and this class is not bad if you took AP stats. They made it less work compared to previous years. I think his rate my professor is super harsh.

Prof. Matangi was very sweet and I enjoyed his teaching. The slides were very old and hard to follow, and the labs were close to impossible in my opinion, but he did his best to help us out. The exams were very clearly written by AI, though, which was very disappointing and not conductive to learning.

One of the nicest professors I have had, he is always willing to help and responds very quickly. He really cares about his students and even cancels class sometimes if we get ahead. There's not too much homework either.
Professor was good. Grading on exams were fair, but lecture slides were a bit so,so and unclear. The textbook was better at explaining and problems on exams were easier than homework.

I feel the other reviews here are harsh; he did his best with the course material that he had to teach. He's a very caring and approachable professor. It's not his fault that STAT350 is designed very poorly.

professor was difficult to understand but the slides were somewhat useful. i felt he was good at answering questions. the course has a lot of overly difficult homework that wasn't very related to exams. exams were easier than homework.
The worst professor at Purdue

He used another lecturer's slides and recordings for "quality-control purposes", but I believe he should have given lectures himself. For exams, we were told to type our work and answers into lockdown browser, but we were told that we could upload pictures after I had already submitted. There was a discussion forum that he almost never answered.

Pros
- Insane curve, you won't fail this class
- Book is easy to read
Cons
- Required purchase of online homework service
- Horrible test logistics (typing your math into a non-LateX text window)
- Took someone else's notes and lectures (which were completely not worth watching)
- Doesn't reply to discussion forms

imo the class itself was kinda strange with all the R assignments. tests were graded very nicely (extremely generous with points), but lectures were honestly a waste of time to attend. i don't like the tophat assignments that were due weekly as the content he taught generally deviated/was more specific so that entire platform was a waste.
Why I'm still alive?

prob worst teacher ive had here. hes rly sweet so i hate saying that but i had to teach myself every single concept on my own. he reads off slides and doesnt do example problems at all. coding homework and 3 other homeworks /week that are irrelevant to the exam topics. dont go to class. its a waste of time. pretty close to hs AP stats but worse

He was a very nice and passionate teacher. The lectures involved him mostly reading off of slides without many example problems, so you would have to learn on your own too. He's always open to help if you reach out to him though.

Does not teach, just reads out complicated math out of slides. You will have to learn to code in R by yourself, and its HARD. Additionally, no curve at all and exams are tough (cheat sheet allowed tho). Don't recmend.

He worked under a course coordinator, so a lot of criticism cannot be attributed to him. He had a lot of material to cover each class. Lectures were very boring, but he is an incredibly kind and helpful man who you can access outside of class if you need. Class itself is doable if you do the assignments and study.

Professor Matangi is incredibly kind and understanding. The workload in this class is very manageable as long as you keep up with the lectures and stay organized. One of the great aspects of his class is the practice exams he provides before each test (very similar structure to actual exam). Highly recommend!

I would argue that Dr. Matangi did the best he could under the conditions of the course. There is a lot of content, the course powerpoints are confusing, and there's little to no overlap between hw, computer assignments, and exams. The content isn't too difficult to teach to yourself and he was extremely helpful and kind outside of class.

Probably the worst professor I have had. It is amazing how someone is able to make a class so disengaging. It wasn't even worth it to show up as I learned much more on my own than I did ever coming to class. He just reads off PowerPoints, often skipping over steps when it comes to giving examples. His tests are also so poorly structured. Avoid!

Professor Matangi seems to genuinely care for his students and is a nice guy. However, this instruction for STAT 511 was quite terrible. He would just read from a PowerPoint and not show any process. I stopped watching lectures after the first week and learned on my own after seeing the lack of effort given on his part. Exams were fair.

He made this class way harder than it needed to be because he doesn't know how to write exams. He expected you to show your work, but the exams were online with only a text entry box. So you had to type everything out while trying to also solve the problems. It took forever and made them way harder. He doesn't know how to run a class like this.
He just read out the PowerPoint from 2001, and his course design is awful. Maybe he's good at statistics but he knows nothing about teaching.

I have done awful with math classes at Purdue but I consider STAT 511 relatively easy compared to other math classes. It was not easier because this prof was good. He did not go through a single practice problem step by step which is obviously important. He just reads off a powerpoint. I am astounded how he never could understand student questions.

Attendance isn't mandatory but is recommended to stay up to date with the material. Homework is very easy and the project is simple enough. Exams are similar to practice exams; just make sure you are comfortable with the concepts.
Do not recommend. Lectures are not engaging and make difficult content harder to learn. 50% of your whole grade is the final exam. Memorization of formulas is expected.

Lectures are very boring. He goes over long proofs for simple math that shouldn't require this much effort. His exams were pretty easy and reflected the practice exam, but he worded the equations weird to the point where i got half of them wrong and I'm a math major. He's also super unhelpful when it comes to questions, self admittedly.

Professor Hansen is a very respected, serious professor at Purdue. He will teach you a lot, and do it well. Attendance was not required, but going to class is very important to clearly understand material. Overall, if you show up and pay attention, you will receive a good grade.

He's ok. Not bad as finance professors go. He's not exactly teaching the most interesting material and understands that, so he tries to make his lectures fun, but 75% of the class stopped going after a few weeks. Exams are hard but curved pretty well so it ends up being ok.

In my opinion, 310 is very easy material. Mr. Hansen cares for you and is willing to teach the materials. Attendance is optional, and the exams are curved. His lectures are very informative and fun, better than other professors in the business school. I would take him if you get the chance. It's very easy to get an A in the class if you put effort.

Hansen is a pretty good professor, he makes the lectures interesting and entertaining. My only complaint is that the course is pretty test heavy and he aims for the exam average to be around 50-60, but he does curve. The exam questions are usually in a form we haven't practiced solving, which makes them difficult but doable.

Overall, MGMT 310 was not a terrible class. The material was easy if you took the time to learn it by yourself. The lectures seemed to acked a sense of structure. When questions are asked, you are made to feel dumb and any email that was sent to the professor was responded in a rude manner. I would recommend finding a different professor.

Prof. Hansen was a good lecturer and helpful during office hours, albeit a little arrogant sometimes. Most of my problems with this class have nothing to do with the professor - exams are 90% of your semester grade, with the other 10% being homework. The exam averages were in the low 70's and mid 60's respectively. Avoid. Hard for no good reason.
Emailed him for some help and extra questions for the exam (just a copy of the 2nd midterm). His response was "No.".

The exams were incredibly difficult.  Exam 1's average was around a 54% while Exam 2 was over 85.  After we took the second exam he said he was upset because we "Clearly weren't challenged enough".  He's a nice person and a fun lecturer, but awful at writing exams.  I don't even think he graded our finals, because I clearly did not get an A

A terrible professor who is so self-centered. Class average for the first exam was around 50% and he was proud of that. He said he had a professor who told him the making of a good exam means the class scores around 50%. He enjoys making the class and exams hard. His ability to lecture is subpar but I would cut him slack in that area as he is new.
i really liked his lectures and quiz explanations were so helpful. i didnt realize how good they were till i had turkstra for 240 where we had no quiz explanations. also head ta is amazing
Very Good Professor. I don't think he makes the 180 curriculum so he should not be the one to blame about the tests and homework.

Yes, the class (especially Spring 2026) can be difficult, but he does a great job in his lectures, has well thought out explanations, gives well made attendance quiz problems, and I would say his teaching does serve to improve the average student's understanding of the course. Good professor.

I found his lectures boring and hard to stay focused on, but you can tell he knows his stuff and is willing to answer questions. Pop quizzes sound scary but there are enough drops to where it's basically a glorified attendance score (I had an 89% average on the quizzes and got full points in the gradebook). HW is mostly pretty chill.

Had for CS 180. Professor can ramble and it is sometimes easy to get distracted then become lost (especially during examples). Provides decent explanations of concepts. The rest here is specific to CS 180. TAs and lab instructors have a bad rep and can be unhelpful. Also, there is a HUGE leap in difficulty after the first exam so be locked in.
good professor

Prof. Bergstrom was a good lecturer. The class difficulty ramps up after the first midterm. Labs were time sinks but otherwise the class content helped you learn OOP very well. Very similar to AP CSA if you took that before. Exams had low averages but if you practice they are easy.

Overall, he's not a bad professor. He seems to care about his students and is knowledgeable about the subject. The lectures are alright, but you'll need to review the slides after class and when studying for exams to fully understand everything. The midterms were manageable, and the final wasn't too bad.

Professor Bergstrom is a fairly decent teacher—that being said, he has a heavy emphasis on keeping multiple quizzes during each lecture which WILL contribute to your grade so make sure you're attentive at all times. The projects aren't too bad, but how your final group project goes really depends on the group members you end up with (good luck!)

Take note of everything in the slides (be able to recall/explain and write any code/concept). He does slip some important concepts in the lecture implicitly, so note everything. 

If you do this, you can do well on the midterms. However, the final is genuinely unfair and will tank your grade. 

It helps so much to have prior java/coding experience!
lectures were okay. i never really paid attention in class so i had to self-study for the exams, which were pretty tough btw. the final grade was generously curved, so i can't complain.

Not a bad professor. He seems to care about his students and clearly cares about what he's teaching and knows what he's doing. Lectures are alright, not amazing but not too bad either, you will probably need to review the slides after each class to actually understand everything. The midterms were FRQ and not too bad, but the MCQ final was brutal.

Very Meh. Boring lecturer, multiple days where I just tuned him out. Did not explain complex topics well, so had to self-teach for the final. Labs and homework were usually not time-intensive, and you can complete the occasional homework in lab. Projects were graded easily. Final was a lot harder than the practice exam he provided us.

I don't really mind Bergstrom as a professor. He was accessible outside of class and clearly wants to make sure every student does well. Problem is with CS180's structure as a whole. The work you have to do outside of class is insane and you have to be on top of everything just so you don't fall behind. Use the labs; they're pretty helpful.

Bergstrom is a good professor imo. Was accessible after class for questions. Lectures were a bit confusing at times but I know his lectures were better than Turkstra's (apparently he didn't upload his coding examples from class to Brightspace).

This is more of a rating on CS180 Fall 2025. The class structure is set up well in a way to learn the content. Labs are a good way to learn the content. Class EXTREMELY picks up the pace after the first exam. The difference between weeks 1-6 and 7-16 are night and day. Start projects early as they do take time if you start late. Huge Group Project.
