There were fewer homework assignments than I would have like to prepare for the difficulty of the exams, but since the class was graded on a curve, it was fine in the end. Lectures were also a little boring, but they were instructive and clear.
Took for Phys 221. Switch out of his section and into Urbas section. The class is difficult and he shows little to no interest in lecturing or helping students.

He just reads off the slides in a thick accent that's hard to understand. He doesn't explain anything well at all, and his lectures are very confusing. I'd try to avoid him if possible, the other phys 272 professors are MUCH better. Everyone just shows up for attendance and then watches the other lectures because they make so much more sense.
He spends most of his lecture time reading slides, and it is very hard to understand his lecture too.
interesting demos and daily like connections. love it.
He read right off the lecture slides and the exams were difficult. The material in class didn't relate well to exam content. He was hard to understand at times but seemed passionate about physics.
He explains things pretty thoroughly, but it's difficult to stay focused for the whole fifty minutes of lecture as he drags things on quite a lot.
He has accent and covers most of the material, and you must study hard after class
teaching with great passion

I know a lot of reviews are emphasizing that a theory-based class is always going to be hard to teach intuitively, and I understand that argument. But, I still don't think Prof. Sellke did a great job. She rarely explained how she derived the next step in her work and exams are brutal. She is very kind and caring and will answer your 1-1 question.

I'm not sure if the issues with the course are because of how the class works or with Sellke, but the lectures are extremely hard to follow and don't explain concepts well at all. You'll be lost quickly even if you are paying attention. She is nice and tries to make things clear, but be ready to spend more time learning everything online yourself.

Spring 2026. Homework is challenging. Quizzes are easy. Exams are definitely tough, but if you study, should be able to shoot above the mean and median. Content can seem easy, but the applications in the homework are very tough compared to simple practice problems. Sellke was not the best at teaching and often skipped important steps.
She was a really poor lecturer who clearly didn't review her slides beforehand. She spent a lot of time reviewing unimportant material and her class was a struggle to get through.
She seems approachable and pleasant to talk to, but the quality of her lectures is quite poor. She would often read off of the slides. I would suggest not taking this professor for this class.

Listen, the class is difficult because the content is hard and you either need to be naturally gifted at math or study a ton. That won't change based on what professor you have. If we are examining how good of a lecturer she is, I really think she teaches the content in a clear, informative way and you would not significantly benefit from another.

Was always open to help after class or outside it and very good at explaining the concepts in this class as she simplified things a lot for her students. Gave fair exams though not a lot of guidance on how to prepare for them.

Be prepared to handle the course load, as the homework could be difficult. The course is sometimes unorganized, making it difficult to know what to study for the tests. I didn't feel prepared for the final exams or midterm, but I believe it's a combination of things, like my own responsibility to study and not knowing what to study.

Decent professor who really cares about her students. I even remember how she showed up to the very first PSO after pulling an allnighter to make sure everything was fine (idk why but i guess she really cares about us). She might only go through the slides a bit slower than most of the other profs, but that's the only downside i can think of.

She is an incredibly nice professor who cares a lot about each of her students and is willing to talk individually after class. While her lectures go quickly, she covers all of the topics needed for the class efficiently. She focuses on examples, which helped me gain deep conceptual understanding, making the specifics much easier to learn.
she is terrible. go to grama's lectures if you have her. she doesn't know what she is talking about.

Midterm and Final were 65% of final grade so you need to study. Very sweet woman and solid lecturer. Sometimes brought candy for the lecture. No attendance so definitely can skip, but you 100% need to take notes to succeed on the exams.
Not great lectures but very nice person
Professor Sellke has a way of making every lecture feel valuable and fun. She super knowledgeable and always ready to help, making learning feel less like a chore and more like an opportunity.
Not accessible outside of class, very nitpicky about assignments, very long and tedious assignments, not very respected by the students.
Very nice professor, but she sometimes rambles on the slides without much coherence, so you'll have to do some reviewing on your own.

I enjoyed her class. I always looked forward to lectures. She is amazing explaining tough concepts so clearly, and her enthusiasm for the subject makes everything feel more interesting. My friends from different section go to her lectures. She was approachable and supportive, which made it easy to ask questions and stay engaged.

Sellke is an exceptionally knowledgeable and engaging instructor who effortlessly breaks down complex concepts into digestible, thought-provoking ideas. She fosters an environment where critical thinking and student participation thrive. A brilliant educator who genuinely cares about student success,

By far, subpar lecturing. Lack of responsibility about quality of lecture slides. Visible lack of deeper understanding of topics discussed. Heavy homework assignments. Excessively time-constrained tests. Caring, to an extent. Tries to make an effort. You will learn much more from the textbook than from her, and the TAs will help much more than her.

I had an exceptional experience with this course. The instructor was knowledgeable, engaging, and always available to provide support. I feel much more confident in the subject thanks to the clear explanations and thoughtful guidance throughout."

Slides used in the class were often confusing, lectures often left me confused as the explanations were not clear as well. Homeworks took quite a bit of time as well, quizzes were pretty easy though. Exams were time consuming and difficult as well. Very badly planned class.
She was a good professor. The class was organized. I enjoyed going to PSO session.
She was very nice but made no sense to me. make sure to go to class trust.

Sellke is a nice professor, and always answers questions from students in lecture. CS182 isn't easy, but she made graphs and probability much easier to understand. The homework/slides are shared for all CS182 professors so there's not much to say other than they're fine. Definitely go to office hours for homework.

A generous curve in the course. Exams were fair and based on the lectures but homework was bit more difficult than lectures. Sellke is a sweet person however her head TA is completely opposite and as far as I understood he makes the majority of final decisions. It would be much better if she assigns someone who has more humanity.

Fracture is probably one of the few rare classes that gives a direct insight into real applicable engineering problems that is everywhere but not a lot of people are familiar with. He does a good job explaining the concepts, although a little bit too much theory, but it pays off at the end. Very reasonable expectations, highly recommend.
Fracture mechanics is a fun class, learned a lot from it. Very reasonable expectations, and prioritizes learning the material. Very quick to answer on Piazza.
Learned a lot, and it's relevant to industry as a big plus. Good expectations, and taught lectures pretty well. Would recommend taking.
He is very hard. His class is impossible.

This was a good class, valuable information taught, very reasonable expectation with homeworks/exams, and learned a lot. His teaching is mediocre sometimes, as he likes to brush through certain topics fast with minimal explanations leaving it up to you to understand it to the depth required to do the homeworks. But it's not impossible and doable.

Vikas is a pretty basic but good professor. Lectures are sometimes tough to understand but are handwritten, so the pace is easy to follow. Exams are straightforward, with few tricks. He's a pretty chill guy too and keeps things real with the class. Sometimes he will joke during lectures and catch you off guard. Just don't bug him about crib sheets.

He is a good professor but admittedly took some getting used to.  I learned a lot, this class was hard.  The good thing is it counts as a math class.  I would say he is not very good at answering questions in class, either repeating what he already has stated or answering a question not asked.  Apart from that he was a good prof.
Good Professor, really wants to make sure the students learn. Responds quickly to questions.
He is nice.

Dr Michalski is amazing.  I took EAPS 104 online as an 8 week course. He is so chill, he kept opening quizzes for us after their due date, If he gave one student extra time, he gave it to everyone.  I had questions about some of the material and emailed him about it, he emailed back with the answer within 2 hours, it was 10pm at night.

As a student who enjoys math and came into the course motivated, I was disappointed with how the material was introduced at the beginning of the semester. The professor's teaching style made it difficult to clearly understand expectations and foundational concepts early on. The lack of structure and clarity made it harder to build confidence.

Such an unhelpful professor. Nothing is online so I missed so many assignments because I had no idea they even existed. Doesn't answer emails and has such boring lectures that cover such random and niche topics for this to be a university required class. Brightspace incredibly unorganized. Genuinely the most frustrating teacher I've ever had.

This professor is very unorganized with everything. He consistently posts copies of the same assignment and then will go after the due date and scrap one of the copies. He won't fix them. He also laughs at his students whenever they get a question wrong, don't know the answer or he doesn't agree with their statement. So nobody raises their hand.

The Brightspace is the most disorganized I have ever seen. Due dates not changed from past years (I took this course in 2025 and an assignment had a 2023 due date). We had project presentations during dead week AND a final. Expectations are very unclear and he laughs at sudents when someone doesn't know the answer to a question.

This is the most disorganized class I've taken. The lectures are very monotonous and the majority of your grade is based on a couple "homework" assignments. There is one midterm that is worth about 8% of your grade, everything else comes from a lot of tiny assignments.

Prof. Michalski was ok. One issue is that he talks very monotonously, so it's fairly easy to get bored. Also, make sure you carve out a lotta time for the 'projects' and 'videos'. 9/10, the projects are just really long assignments where you have to search around on websites and the videos are hr long documentaries.

He is very difficult to ask him questions out of class. He never responds to emails and if he does its just yes or no. Usually does not answer them in a timely manner. He has no due dates on the individual projects they are easy it just he does not mention it in class either so usually found out by brightspace giving the 24 hr warning.

If you can avoid taking a class with Michalski, do it.  ESPECIALLY if you're not an EAPS major.  I've already taken numerous EAPS courses, and this one was overly-complicated for what it should've been.  The quizzes, exams, Brightspace announcements, and lecture notes are full of typos that sometimes make it unreadable.  Labs are easy at least.

The lectures were very boring and the slides used were plagued with typos. Labs and quizzes were also plagued with typos. He did not show up to many labs and did not have a TA to go to them, but the labs are really easy. He also does not respond to emails or submit grades quickly at all.

Spelling errors are incredibly constant and it makes it difficult to understand what he is saying/asking. Rarely responds to emails and labs are all over the place. He is also rude and doesn't care about his students' questions.
He does not respond to emails and is never available. He does not show up to labs. His lectures are super dull and are filled with typos.

Lectures, labs, and quizzes are filled with typos and run-on sentences. It is really difficult to understand what he is asking at literally any given time. It takes 5 minutes to understand a question. He's nice but this class has no organization. I love the topics in the class as well but I have no idea what he is asking us to do most of the time.

It's an easy class.  Most of your grade is projects, which are usually very easy but somewhat tedious.  He makes numerous spelling and continuity errors though which make it difficult to understand the projects and grading system.  He also teaches some obviously incorrect information and gets irate when students try to correct him.  TA is clueless.

He doesn't answer emails at all, this class is based off of a bunch of busy work, the TA barely knew what was going due to the lack of communication from the prof. There's iClickers randomly throughout the year, honestly if you need an easy A, with EAPS104 with him just take it. Such a blow off class. No big complains other than the emails lol.
He is a good professor in terms of flexibility. However, he sometimes forgets stuff, but he is open to emails and always responds them.
Make sure you read the book and study the power point questions, this helps a ton with the tests/quizzes. If you pay attention and put in effort you will end up with a decent grade.
Average professor. Class assignments are easy. Easy A

One of the fantastic water lessons at Purdue. You can get all the answers about projects and quizzes from the Internet. Easy to take. You don't need to spend much time on that. I usually started the assignment on the due date and write something down. A peer review is easy. I always recommend this. but I heard it will change content this semester

Lots of long lectures every week, quizzes are very specific and recorded with no chance to use notes, professor does not answer questions but tells you to put them in the discussion part of blackboard so that other students can answer them but that often doesnt work.

So much homework, very unorganized. You have to turn in all of your papers into the assignment on blackboard, but you also have to turn them into a separate plagiarism check assignment on blackboard. If you forget, then you get a 0 for the paper. I don't understand this part because there is a plagiarism check built in on blackboard. Regret taking.

I took EAPS 104 and it was seriously the most unorganized class I've ever taken. It was a pretty easy class overall but it was extremely messy and unprofessional. There were so many typos in the quizzes and exams and the lecture videos that we had to watch (because there were in lecture questions we had to answer) looked like they were from 2004.

Don't know if something changed when I took it but the class got worse as the semester progressed. All quizzes and tests they video you so you can't use notes, and the questions were really specific details from the videos. Also, the videos went from 30 minutes where he actually explains to just having us watch hours of boring Blue Planet videos.

Really good experience. What I did was completing the assignment in advance. Usually about 15 days before the deadline. The TA commonly finished the grading within one day after I submitted it. If I got some questions wrong and don't know how to fix it, I would email the TA and ask. And I still get chance to redo the assignment before the deadline
so kind and funny. He is a big fan of group work and icebreakers to help the class get to know each other. He leaves enough time to ask questions and even help with assignments.
Fantastic professor very chill and relatable to the rest of the students. Always a fun time to be in his class
Josh was a really great professor. He knew our names, always said hello and kept the class flowing and met us where we were at. He also brought candy before breaks!

Josh is one of the kindest professors. He made class entertaining while still helping students learn proper speech & communications skills. He asked every student how they were doing every single morning, and was always so helpful with any questions.
Joshua is a really good professor, he helps a lot and will respond to emails really quickly. He is amazing and will do everything to help his students
Josh really cared about how we were doing inside and outside of his class. He was also really fast to respond to emails.
I took an asynchronous version of PSY-120 and Kessler was always available if we needed help. She's great!

I LOVE PROF LIM-KESSLER!!! This is my second semester with her as my professor, and I've thoroughly enjoyed her classes. She posts lecture videos with the homework answers if you missed class and need to catch up. She clearly communicates the exam content and sends out plenty of reminders for due dates. She's also very sweet outside of class.
Super great and easy class. All tests were open note online.
No comments. She is simply the best and makes this class VERY engaging. Would take this class again in a heartbeat.

Dr. Lim-Kessler is always very bubbly and her lectures are genuinely enjoyable. The class is easy. everything is online, homework is nonexistent, and there were only like 2 quizzes. She's very understanding too and let me make up an exam when I was sick once.

An absolutely amazing professor! Genuinely one of the sweetest and most caring people ever. For PSY201 she goes through all the homework for you, so as long as you come to class she will flat out give you the answers. All the quizzes/test were incredibly easy. Attendance not mandatory but honestly just go for her. Sweetest Prof Ever.

I really like Lim-Kessler's energy and I think she is super sweet, but her lectures didn't really add anything to the course. She kind of just reads off the slides and reiterates like she's making new points. I stopped going after about a week and still got an A by just reading the slides.

Professor Lim-Kessler is one of the kindest professors I have ever had. If students are confused she will take time to stop her lecture and thoroughly explain it again. She is a relatively easy grader and gives extensions if you have a valid reason. I would 100% take a class with her again.

She is an amazing teacher. This class can be hard if you do not go to lectures or read over the slides, but overall, it is easy. Tests and quizzes are taken online, and on days that you have tests/quizzes, there is no class. She loves this topic and wants her students to learn and do well.

She is such a great teacher. Many homework assignments, but she does the homework with you in class. Tests and quizzes are taken online, and on days you have tests/quizzes there is no class. She is so kind and caring. She gave us candy on Halloween, math-themed pencils, and erasers in different shapes.

Dr. Lim -Kessler is amazing. She is so sweet and is passionate about what she teaches. She is the most caring professor ever and every exam is open note and online. Attendance is not mandatory, but her lectures are enjoyable, so I recommend going. Sometimes she throws in some extra credit if you attend.
you don't really have to go to class

I absolutely loved her class! She very clearly cares about all of her students and she makes an effort to connect with everyone that comes to class. She occasionally also would give out extra credit to the students who attended class. Overall, she was a great professor!

She's an absolute sweetheart. She walks through the homework with us and really explains it in a way that is easy to understand. Her lectures were recorded, and her exams/quizzes were administered online. She was easy to reach outside of class. On the last day of class, she brought little gift bags for us all

Does all of the homework with you in class, at least for PSY 201 which makes life so much easier but also does make it harder to retain because I feel like I didn't have to try as hard. She's very sweet and tries very hard to make Psych statistics as easy as possible especially since it's super tough for some.

I absolutely love professor Lim-Kessler and this class was super easy. Attendance isn't required, her lectures are recorded and all the tests and quizzes are open note/open book. As long as you have the notes down, there's no reason you shouldn't get a great grade in the class. She really cares about her students and is always available to help.

I adored taking Psy 222 with Dr. Lim-Kessler. Although lecture slides were posted and she recorded lectures, I never skipped a class because of how much I loved her class. Even if the exams and quizzes weren't open notes, she makes the content so interesting and easy to learn I'd still do okay because she just explains things so well.
Prof Lim-Kessler is literally so amazing, she's so sweet and really cares about her students. She's also really passionate about the material and just overall a great professor.

Best professor I have ever had! I don't think one can ask for a better teacher!! She is the most caring teacher ever. She always checks up on people and understands that college is hard. I just cant say enough about how great she is. I recommend her to anyone who has the option of taking a class with her!

Professor Lim-Kessler is one of the kindest educators I have ever encountered. She is very passionate about behavioral neuroscience and just wants students to learn and do their best. All tests and quizzes are open note and pull information from the lectures and videos from class. She is the best and I'd love to take another one of her classes!

I have taken two courses with this professor and she is so great! She is caring about her students and will do her best to make the lectures easy to understand. In her Intro to statistics in psychology course she goes through the homework problems and makes them easy to understand. We had a book, but rarely used it.

Literally one of the best professors ever.  She is fantastic and super knowledgable.  She really cares about all of her students and makes complex math/ideas easy to understand.  I'm taking PSY222 this semester just to have her as a professor.  If you have an opportunity to take a class she teaches, do it.
Dr. Lim-Kessler really cared about her students learning and the class was very engaging if you are interested in the topics. She was also very sweet and always made me smile.

This is a very easy & interesting class. Even though attendance isn't mandatory, there are questions on exams from class discussion that are not included in her slides and you can get a bunch of extra credit for attending.
Dr. Lim-Kessler is amazing. I don't particularly enjoy stats, but she made the class bearable and easy to understand. She's very caring and personable as well.
Prof. Tosun is the GOAT at Indianapolis campus.

Professor Tosun is the best at Purdue Indianapolis campus. Everyone talks about her. She was the highlight of my freshman year. Don't miss her classes at all if you end up going to Purdue's new campus. So sad she is not teaching 102

I had taken her for my 2025 fall semester as my SCLA 101 professor. The way she teaches and engages the class is what I loved. Even though we had 3 Essay midterms and a final, she made sure that we felt comfortable writing them by providing cheat sheets and sites for further understanding. I would 100% recommend taking Dr. Tosun's class!

I took SCLA 102 with Prof. Tosun. Although it was an online class, she did a great job communicating with her students and organizing the class. She was on top of the assignments, pre-recorded lectures, grading, and communicating. She provided a great deal of detailed feedback, which you do not normally get at Purdue. She is amazing.

Simply the best. Dr. Tosun was the best part of my freshman year at Purdue Indianapolis. She is respected and loved by all her students. Very famous and has a high reputation among Indianapolis campus students.

Dr. Tosun was the highlight of my freshman year at Purdue's new campus. Not only she is a wonderful human being, but she is an expert on Shakespeare and very passionate about everything we discuss. Great, fun, and safe class environment. She is very popular among the students at Purdue's Indianapolis campus. I hope she can teach SCLA 102, too.
Best English professor ever! She has a reputation for being the best at Purdue's Indianapolis campus. Do whatever you can and take SCLA 101 with Dr. Tosun.
Dr Tosun tries very hard to make the class an open discussion and available for everyone. She does a great job helping others and makes it very clear what she expects from each student

Dr. Tosun is known as the best SCLA 101 professor. Very caring, understanding and accessible both in and outside the class. The class environment was very friendly. Clear expectations and grading criteria. Great discussion. We went to galleries and met real actors from London to practice Shakespeare.

Dr. Tulin was the highlight of my freshman year. There is no doubt why she is phenomenal among Purdue students. Clear expectations and clear grading criteria. We had so many in-class writing workshops, which made me a better writer.
very easy class and she takes a lot of interest to make the classes interactive and interesting as well. Would definitely take the class again.

Dr. Tosun is the best SCLA 101 professor. It is so sad that she is not teaching 102-only if you are lucky to take it in the summers with her. She is very respected and loved among her students. Gives great feedback. get ready to write but also to learn how to write. She is also very passionate about Shakespeare.

Dr. Tosun is a phenomenal professor. I enjoyed her overall effort and passion for the course; this experience was very fun. Dr. Tosun gives great feedback on papers! I liked that she allowed students to send in papers before the deadline to have them proofread before grading.
good professor

Enjoyed summer SCLA 102 with her, she is strict on late work but the deadlines are generous overall. Takes time to give meaningful feedback on assignments and created a high amount of engagement with the class despite it being online. Would recommend her online course to anyone.

Dr. Tosun is very respected by her students. She is very caring and always does her best to help her students. She reached out to me several times when I was not able to go to class and encouraged me to bring the best version of myself and worked with me step by step. She would be the best thing and professor you would ever get at Purdue.

Dr. Tosun is known as a the best SCLA 101 prof on Purdue campus and as you stepped in her class, you feel it. She is very caring, very inclusive and an amazing human being. Is she a tough grader? Yes! But she makes you learn a lot! All the detailed feedback she gives makes you a better writer. She helped me to be a better writer.

Fun and great class. She is very kind and respected. Dr. Tosun gives good and detailed feedback on everything and her grading criteria is very clear. participation matters and they are rewarding. There are pop quizzes but they are already in the syllabus so not sure why people act like they are caught by surprise. Read the syllabus people.

Although definitely a fun and interesting class compared to other SCLA courses, Dr. Tosun has a hard time conveying her expectations for assignments and then grades them as if they were very clear so its very hard to understand what she is looking for. Key to success: do assigned readings, study rubrics to a T, don't miss class, and ask questions.

fun class, great readings and lots of writing. She provides great feedback with drafts. always available outside the classroom. makes you feel valued and understood. She sends daily and weekly reminders on Brightspace. always there to listen if you have any questions. Do take SCLA 101 with her! She is a big name and her previous students love her!

Great class. She is always very helpful and always there to help. Gives great feedback on writing. great lectures and engaging classroom. Had a lot of fun in her class. Will I take another class with her? Oh, yes!

Super great lady, neat class. Lots of reading, but not unreasonable. My one gripe is that grades could take a while to be entered, but it felt like later assignments were graded on a curve to adjust for this.

Dr. Tosun is among the best profs I have had at Purdue! She is always very excited about her topic. very knowledgeable on Shakespeare. I bet she is the best SCLA Prof! She is very inspirational and caring! get ready to explore the campus and galleries at Purdue!
This class was tough when it came to writing papers, but if you set up times to meet with her, it helps a lot. Professor Tosun was the highlight of my first semester at college.

As long as you participate in class discussions you'll do fine. There is only one weekly homework assignment, and that's it besides the course-mandated essays. She's a great teacher and also a great person. I'd put money that she's the best SCLA 101 teacher.

Professor Carter made me feel more connected to an online course than some of my in person classes this semester. He frequently kept in touch and reminded students of due dates and important milestones. He and his TAs were great help during office hours. Take the class if you're willing to learn something new and willing to put in the work.

Prof. Carter made me feel more to an online course than some of my in-person classes this semester. He frequently kept in touch and reminded students of due dates and important milestones. He and his TAs were great help during office hours. Take the class if you're willing to learn something new and willing to put in the work.
Just a great guy who did a great job. Dont take if you're not going to stay on task.

Professor Carter made me feel more engaged than some of my in-person classes this semester. He frequently checks in on all the students and replies quickly to emails. His video lectures are very informative and kept relatively brief. I didn't do any of the readings and still did well in the course because I kept up with the milestones. He's great!

Fantastic Professor with great lectures. Online POL231 was a bit too textbook heavy for my liking but the Dr. Carter was fantastic. Always reaches out to ensure students are on track and offers flexible office hours for his students should they need more help.

Honestly, great teacher because he explains concepts well. But this is an online 101 class, and the amount of work required is not proportional to the difficulty it should have. Lecture exams on lockdown, essays and assignments are honestly harshly graded, should be easier to score on them. Good if you're passionate but not an easy class.

Professor Carter cares about the progress of his students in the courses he teaches. He provides constant reminders about upcoming deadlines on important assignments, course milestones, projects, etc. Courses require proper time management as there is a lot of quizzes but if you do them properly, you'll be all set for the exams as well.

Way too much work for an online 101 class. He's a great teacher who really helps you understand what you need to pass. But, three essays, a group project, lockdown browser, and harsh grading makes this really unbearable.

Dr. Carter is a great professor, but he made this online course way harder than it should have been. There were lots of assignments, and they were all graded harshly. The exams are lockdown. Additionally, the last assignment was a group project, which proved to be extremely difficult in an online course. He did offer one extra credit assignment.
He is easy to work with and does a great job explaining content.

Overall an enjoyable and educational lecturer. Knowledgeable with unique perspectives due to his military experience. Has you play this fun international relations simulation over the course of a few weeks. It's like a more academic version of Civ. His lectures can drag as he answers superfluous questions and overexplains concepts.

Carter was teaching this class for the first time, so some hitches to the class but it was organized and he asked our feedback, and actually took it into account. POL430 had lots of research papers to read, with assignments over them, and then you write your own research paper, which was hard but he supports you well with progress assns and OHs

Very knowledgeable and prepared prof. Very pragmatic, focusing on what's really relevant. No long papers, only 2 page papers. They'll be toughly graded but he'll let you know and give you good feedback. Weekly online quizzes with unlimited attempts, really help study for the exam. You need to read the textbook and go to class. A is max grade, no A+

Professor Carter is clearly very knowledgable and passionate about foreign policy and political science. He is very accommodating, and lectures are always very interesting and engaging. Easily my favorite professor so far. You do have to go to class and read the textbook, but it is not difficult work and if you are interested, you will love it.

He is very passionate and knowledgeable about the topic. You have to read the textbook and go to class. The papers are a little difficult but doable. On tests, you can use your notes. He gives 5-minute breaks during class. He is very accessible outside of class and wants to help you.

You can tell this professor cares about teaching, and puts a lot of effort in the lectures. Can be a little chaotic with the constant adjustments in the assignments and lectures but the trade off with his ability to explain concepts well is well worth it. 10/10 would recommend taking him.

One of the best professors I've had for undergrad and grad. He really cares about teaching the nontrivial material in a way that's easy to digest. A lot of work needs to be put in to understand and do the homework but he makes it up with his teaching effort. It can take a while to get feedback on assignments but don't fret as long as you put effort

He does a very good job in lectures, and the content was very interesting throughout the course. The course has long problems in the nature of itself, but he did a good job covering the content. Great professor to take, but the homework grades did come back pretty late. If you want to learn cool stuff take this; if you want an easy course, don't.

Professor G. is an amazing dynamics instructor, is very accessible, and is extremely good at breaking down complicated topics in the course. He is honest about what topics are difficult and require more practice, and will help everyone out who asks for it. The course itself is coordinated, heavier on HW, and has a required lecture book. Exam heavy.

Very reasonable with expectations, difficulty, and the pace of the course. Exams were timed and scheduled well in advance. Exam material was always something that was previously covered in class, and the grading was flexible as long as you showed your learning and thought process.

The course was my grade booster. The course is easy and the professor helps to under the concepts easily. Professor gives very little homework's and lot of time to complete them. Had him online so he had a lot of technical problems. Average professor but definitely has the potential to be great
Missed class once every two weeks, gave quizzes that closed before class ended or never opened, horrible handwriting and lecture style. Watch CMK.

Great guy, interesting and engaging lectures, very understanding, and the class is not really that difficult.
Love Mr. Freed, truly a one of a kind professor in this day and age.

I really liked his class and how accessible he made himself. He would show up at both of the campuses to hang out with the students and show us meteorites that he had. I really liked his lectures and style of teaching and the advice he gave before each lecture.

I loved his class. He was a very approachable teacher, and he made sure to go out of his way to make himself available to students. He would hold sessions on both the Indy and WL campuses so that all the students could reach him. His lectures were really interesting.
Greatest prof. of all time. GPOAT.

Dr. Freed is a passionate teacher. The class wasn't difficult, and I found myself learning and retaining more than I would have expected. Took this class online, never watched a lecture video and did the whole semesters homework assignments in January. Gave me the core curriculum science requirement and was a very easy class. Definitely recommend.
i really enjoyed being in his lecture even though I did not need his class for credit.

The absolute GOAT

Lecture videos are alright and HW is given biweekly. The class only gets busy once group project started, and instructions and expectations for the project isn't very clear. Exam is not difficult as long as you understand the topics instead of memorizing, and both exams are only 30% of the grade.

Project's expectation isn't clear and rubric was not released until quite late. Homework is assigned biweekly and not long (5 - 7 hours). im so mad shes the only prof for this class, i cant even understand half of what she says and she makes things so confusing. her class is organized soooo badly, it takes me 30 mins to just decipher what her instructions are saying and her standards are still so high.

professor hiday is really knowledgable and understanding and you can tell he really loves this course, however the TAs can either make or break your grade in this class, really strict grading on the later half of lab reports to "prepare you for 242" by some TAs and other ones give everybody full credit. overall, decent class

please quit

He's clearly passionate and knowledgeable about IT, but he's really hard to follow during lectures. Labs, in my opinion, were pretty easy but if you're not careful, you can mess up one small thing and fail them. How well you do in this class is heavily dependent on your TA.

Great professor. Great course. Great reintroduction to a major I switched out of then back into.
bad professor

Prof. Salem is probably one of the best professors I've had. 155 was a breeze, and even if you don't have prior coding experience he makes functions and their purposes and uses easy to understand. He is so caring and he truly cares that his students understand the subject. Best coding teacher I ever had and more coding teachers should be like him. This class is also a piece of cake if you have coding experience and if you dont, not worry cause it is still an easy class.

Professor Salem is the way to go for those that don't necessarily have a lot of programming experience. HW and Lab assignments are super calm, and the TAs are great!

I thought this professor did a good job explaining programming concepts, and lectures were pretty clear overall with some humor mixed in. The class moved at a steady pace that worked well for beginners. Labs didn't take very long and exams matched what we covered in class.

Great professor just go to lectures, lab and study for the exams and you will pass. If the labs are tricky the ta's can help you some. be sure to do all homework as it is best at teaching you the content.

Professor Salem was amazing. He is engaging in lecture and has a good sense of humor. TAs are very lenient in grading. Labs and assignments are very straight forward. 10 assignments 12 labs. Professor Salem is THE best teacher for people who do not know how to code. If you how up to lectures/labs/do assignments, you'll get an A easy. The exams are.. god awful Ill be real, but everything else makes up for it.

Class is very easy, even without previous Python knowledge. As long as you read the lecture slides and pay attention in the lectures, there is no reason to read the book. He is such a good professor and wants everyone to learn. Loved having him as a professor, he made funny jokes here and there and overall hes just a chill guy teaching a python course, cant get mad at him!

did not excuse medical issue, ignored emails intentionally

Salem is all around a great professor. The lectures were one of the best things. He is pretty funny and really knows his stuff. Our entire section loved the guy. Great professor. He knows what he is doing and he is very helpful. One of the most enjoyable professors I have had in a while, because he knows the course is easy and treats it as such. Go to lab and this is an easy A if you do.

I love this professor. CNIT 155 is not a serious class, and the score may be different if the class was serious, but Dr. Salem is one of the most enjoyable lecturers I've listened to. He has an incredibly good spirit and maybe the widest smile I've ever seen.

He is a very fun professor and most of the code he goes over is posted so you can copy and paste into lab. Salem is definitely excited about teaching the course but misses A LOT of things that are on the final. The book you rent isn't mandatory but just explains a few topics deeper.

The professor is extremely helpful and excellent at teaching programming. I did not do well during high school in programming and was fearful of taking this course. But this time, I not only did really well but also understood the information and enjoyed learning to program.

Couldn't understand a single thing this guy said. Not like I needed to. His lectures are useless. You can go, but you'll end up being more confused than if you just didn't go. Attend labs and you'll be fine. His labs are a lot better because he is never there, and you can get help from the TA's.

its a pretty good introduction to coding if you haven't coded before. he's kind and understanding. the grading criteria is clear. never miss labs, attendance is not always mandatory but going to class does help. the quizzes and the written tests don't make you a better programmer at all and are just mostly trick questions.

Class is classified as a beginner class but lots of students know what they are doing. Assignments are managable. Labs are managable. Exams are difficult. Lectures are boring and he posts them online.

I didn't go to lectures, however, I have 1 thing to say which brought him down to a 2, he made us buy a 40$ online book which we never used! He said we'd have mandatory readings, but those were never assigned! Very easy A though

Excellent class-- great team projects throughout the semester
Great guy, tough grader. Funny guy too, makes the class super interesting.

Not a very difficult course but one you get a lot out of. Professor Rios does a great job making the content fun and easy to understand. Tends to let us out early at times. Prof. Rios is just an incredibly funny and relatable guy. He makes class entertaining and is just as averse to boring lectures as students are. Professor Luis Rios disappoints with unclear schedules, erratic teaching, and delayed grades. Low participation marks with no response to inquiries reflect a lack of commitment. Think twice before enrolling.

He has such a great sense of humor and cares so much about his students! Very personable and a very easy grader. Most of the course is dominated by a group project split into parts throughout the semester. Pick a good group of classmates and you will be very successful in the course.

Pick a good group for this class, could end up make or breaking it for you. Prof. Jones is a great prof. he's willing to explain things multiple times and does not discredit opinions when they are brought up during discussions like some other professors do. genuinely cares about his students and the material. class is mandatory and loves to play with legos

Amazing professor, hilarious, cares about his students. Lecture-heavy class, but they are super easy to follow. Nice teacher. Very engaging and involved. Wish he explained the math a bit more. But I understand its ML course, so stats and math is a part. I liked him over the other prof, as he was more caring and serious about teaching. Overall great choice.

Nice guy. Content is Technical, so i understand why it can get mathy sometimes. I attended both lectures. The lectures are unbearably slow and attendance is mandatory (and tracked through Codon, which is one of the worst platforms to ever exist). Content on the exams is more difficult than what is taught in class, and there's a lot of work (homework and a quiz every week).

Lectures are pretty basic, likely due to the class format change. The assignments for class are in all different places, but exams are multiple choice and not too bad. If you know the slides well and do the Codon assignments you will be good. Just know there can be a lot of lull time in lecture and that there are quizzes on Fridays.

This class is currently making me want to change my major. It is SO confusing what they want us to know and for the homeworks, there are 10 different TAs grading so some people fail and other people get an 100 for doing the same thing. Its basically a flipped classroom because you have to learn everything yourself and do "activities" in class.

The slowest and most boring professor you will ever take. He talks so much and yet nothing he says makes any sense. It's a waste of time coming to class, but you still have to because he now grades based on attendance.
Lecture Slides and Exams were identical. Every detail on slide were on exams; study the slides to great depth, using his lectures. His lectures are verbatim on the exams.

Ended the class with a 99.2%. As long as you are a dedicated student, this class is extremely straightforward and an easy A. While Camarillo isn't a great lecturer, I'd say it's still important to watch his lectures to gauge how well you need to know the material. Take detailed notes, write a LOT for the homework FRQs, and start studying early!

Don't bother showing up to lectures. They are extremely boring. The best way to do well in this class is to watch the lectures they post, put them on 2x speed, and take notes. The TAs are horrible and write horrible tests, with some questions having no correct answer. There are about 15 TAs, but it will take a month to get your scantron test back.
Worst professor I've had so far by a long shot.

Actually so bad can't believe he has a job... Get ready to RUMBLE your way to a failing grade with no fault of your own. Worked hours upon hours to receive a B in this class and was forced to take a dip in others. I went to every lecture even when I came down with the flu in order to pass this class only to find myself teaching him basic technology

This professor might have been the absolute worst teacher I've ever had in my entire career as a student. He was extremely monotone and had 0 passion for this class. He showed complex diagrams and failed to explain them, and would provide a summary slide but would instantly click away. I've also never had to wait so long for a test grade to return
Genuinely so bad. One of the worst professors. Super nice guy but actually cannot with this class.

Camarillo is funny and a sweet guy but the lectures feel like a waste of time. Also:

- Exam grading takes eons (scantron??)
- Unprepared TAs
- Weekly homework graded harshly
- Entire class just feels disorganized

On the bright side, the exams and quizzes aren't too hard if you watch the lectures online or read. The study guide is very clear.

He teaches very slowly and the concepts are not explained thoroughly. The TAs are very underprepared. The lectures are a waste of time, the homework is graded harshly and the homework can tank your grade. There was extra credit but it was harshly graded and not worth a lot. The study guides just have wrong answers and there is no curve to the class

Dr. Camarillo is a nice guy, but his lectures aren't very helpful. I really disliked the grading system as I ended up with an 89.5, which counts as a B (not even a B+). He gave us extra credit which was nice, but I don't think it's fair to assign the same grade to people who averaged 90s on exams to people who averaged 75s.

This guy...... 
He mumbles, stumbles, and fumbles through every lecture. 
His study guides. 
Are full of deceit and lies. 
His explanations.
Deserve damnation.
We miss Minchella.
We don't like this fella.
He's not funny. 
He thinks he's punny. 
He talks about breasts. 
I just want to rest.

No plus grades or curves, but some extra credit they begrudgingly offered. TAs are unfriendly and create bad practice problems with ChatGPT that didn't have any correct answer choices. My least favorite course I have ever taken purely because of the bland lectures, TAs, and instructor. Nice enough guy but awful as a lecturer.

He tells you to just study the content on study guides and you will do great on the exams, but then half of the exam isn't from the guide. One exam took a month to get graded (a scantron exam), and you have to request a regrade on homework often because the TA's can't grade. Exam seating comes out way too late and got messed up during the final. :(

I didn't go to lectures and instead taught myself everything. His lectures are incredibly boring and while the content itself could be interesting, the way he teaches it is not. Before every exam, he spent time trying to get us to chant "let's get ready to rumble." I have never wanted to rumble just give me the exam. The TA's are useless as well.

Terrible lecturer, slides were awful and barely explained the topics. Difficult exams, and way too picky on the HW grading. Fight for your points. Unfortunately this class is required, and I don't think anyone else teaches it.

One of the best faculty members I have had. Extremely enthusiastic about teaching and make the class very engaging. Lectures are clear, well-organized, and very helpful for understanding the core concepts deeply rather than just memorizing material.
Excellent professor!
You seriously need to study in order to get good grades and understand the material.

I had her for 30411 and can say that she is genuinely the worst professor I have ever had. Her lectures are hard to follow and hardly go over the material required for homework and exams. She provided zero review materials for the exams nor gave information about how it is structured. The average on the second exam was 17%... avoid her if you can.
Terrible instructor, does not care about students, accuses students of things. Hard to communicate hard to understand. Avoid this professor at all costs
Her teaching is okay, but she also does not organize the class very well, and she is not very helpful for students. I think the exam avg. in this class were one of the lowest.

This class is notoriously hard, and she did a pretty good time lecturing. She encouraged participation but never forced anyone who didn't want to participate to do so. I never reached out to her individually, but she seemed like she would be receptive to outside questions. Overall worth taking, and the later 2:30 lecture time was an added bonus.

Her examples are amazing and make a lot of sense. She will often give real world examples of a concept in class (lightning like a capacitor). This is the second time I am taking this class, and I am very grateful that I got her as my professor the second time around. She has completely changed my feeling towards my major choice for the better.

Make sure to go to class everyday to do your course participation, she also gives out very helpful extra credit which she only announces in class, if you miss it there are no makeups. Exams were pretty hard to me bc I'm not a good coder but the extra credit helped tremendously. There is a final proj which isn't hard but you have to present.

Professor Crowe is an excellent lecturer and fair grader. She offers significant office hours to help students and gives extra credit opportunities. Additionally, she has helped to make CS 177 the most organized class I have taken at this school. Everything felt reasonable to do with knowledge from lecture, and she gives a lot of grace to students.

Professor Crowe is an excellent lecturer who grades quite fairly, with clear grading criteria. She clearly cares about her students and offers frequent office hours as well as extra credit. Additionally, she has helped to make the class structure more fair to students. Almost every assignment is easily done with lecture slides or the course manual.
Really nice professor who goes above and beyond to make sure her students understand the material. I took her for CS 235(MGMT 235) and she was also awesome. 100% recommend.

Professor Crowe is great at explaining complex topics clearly, even for those new to CS. She is very helpful and allows assignment redos if you attend office hours. Her background as a school teacher makes her quite lenient, which is a plus. The class is enjoyable, but be prepared for a heavy workload!

This class was originally CS 235. Professor Crowe is kind, and she is lenient with due dates, especially since there is so much work in this class. Use lab time to your advantage to get ahead! The only bad part is that at the end of the semester, there is a project involving coding in Python, and WE WERE NEVER TAUGHT PYTHON. Good luck on that one.

Crowe is great, but 235 is so much more work than the other excel options. People in the other classes looked at my workload and thought it was nuts. Crowe is super nice tho. Very kind lady. Pointless group project and unnecessary workload dock points tho. Take one of the other excel options if able.

Took Marta for CS 177, and she was amazing. She was extremely accessible outside of class with basically 24/7 help available through all the TAs and herself. The labs have mandatory attendance but were the same as the prelab. Lecture videos were posted same day with the course participation being due at midnight that night.

Prof Crowe geniunlly cares about her students and wants us to do well. She's always willing to help outside of class and got an A with no effort even though I got C's on all the exams and knew nothing about excel

Marta is a great professor. Her lectures aren't mandatory, and you can always watch videos from class online. Labs are mandatory, but there is usually homework so it's a good time to get that done. A semester long group project takes up a big part of your grade, but it's nothing bad. Tests are long but as long as you did the HW you're fine.

I suppose this review doesn't matter since this is the last semester of CS235, BUT character-wise, she seems very nice, attendance was required, but you could do it on your own time (after class) as she posted lecture videos. I stopped attending mostly because she uses a mic to speak, and it was always popping. Seems nice, though!
Dense course, but she organizes it effectively and provides what you need to earn a good grade. Great for mastering Excel and teamwork.

Marta is a great professor. She is very clear in explaining things and wants her students to do genuinely well in her classes. The course was structured really well. It sucks that this is the last semester she is teaching this course. Overall, if you get her as a professor, you'll do well. Just make sure to do the work and ask questions.
she is okay prof
Its a merge class,so attendance is basically free.
The lectures are boring, but they are recorded. Nice person and very understanding. Exams are difficult, even if you take the time to study and take notes.

The class is super easy, and if not there are lots of resources to make sure you do well. You can do assignments as many times as you want until you get a 100%. The exams are also just like the labs and HW. The prof is very understanding and accommodating.

Class was a bit of work, but if you just get things done you can easily get an A in the class. Can rewatch lectures and redo every assignment except exams. To prep for exams, go to her reviews they are very similar to the test. For extra exam practice I just redid labs and lab preps. She is a nice lady and very helpful.

The class had a lot of homework, but as long as you pay attention in class you'll have a good foundation for all of the work that you do outside of the course participation. Prof Crowe is very sweet and very accessible outside of class. She is patient and is willing to work with you outside of class to make sure you are understanding the content.

Great overall, the class was easy and if you have any questions, the TAs respond very quick, and you can even do zoom with them. Very helpful the grades are like this:LAB CPS: 5%, LAB PREPS: 10%,LABS:20%, EXAMS:35%, TEAM PROJETS:15% lecture CPS; 15%(She posts lectures onbrightspace so if you get caught up in something you don't have to go to class,
Crowe is the most caring professor I have ever had. I had never worked on Excel before and her class was super accommodating. She cares about her students better than any professor I have had.
She really cares, and is super easy to keep up with in class.

Marta was very caring and overall the class was extremely helpful, however I find it much more beneficial to watch the lecture recordings instead of attending lecture because she moves really fast and if you have a Mac it is more complicating. There's a group project that lasts the whole semester and it is sort of confusing but graded very easy.

Firstly, her voice is bad. The major reason why I do not go to her class is because of her voice. Her voice is too crying voice like sheep. And she speaks like shouting, Her lecture is too fast to catch up. There was nothing I learned in class. I learned myself through lab time.

Professor Crowe does a great job of helping and explaining new topics, especially if you have had no experience with Excel before. Exams are fair, but packed with tons of work, so be sure to have everything perfected. Homeworks is easy and late credit is given if needed.

MGMT/MIS 382 - A very nice guy but insanely unorganized and surprisingly difficult projects.  He changes due dates all the time, and uses AI to make most of his stuff.  The exams cover so much content, so studying is a necessity.  On top of that, he gives coding projects - he does not teach you how to code and then grades it very harshly.

Highly request all of you to not take his MGMT 382 class.I have suffered immensely in this class. All other 382 sections have such an easy exam and project, most of my other friends got through it with an easy A, but my grades fell and how. Not organized at all, very difficult exams, no curve scale, and everything is wrong about it.

This is my favorite professor I have had at Purdue! I don't even like econ, and am not good at it. I just needed to take this course. He truly cares about his students and gives so many opportunities to earn points. I remember him bringing donuts & starbucks. His kids passed out our exams. He is so caring and funny and an amazing professor!

Easily one of my favorite professors I have had while at Purdue! He is always willing to meet outside of office hours and if a very interesting lecture ( I actually wanted to go to his class and I do not even like Econ)!

Hands down one of the best professors I've ever had. Really cares about your learning and makes every effort from his side to make sure you're doing okay. I've never asked him for help and not got my problem resolved, whether academic or logistical.
Honestly one of the best professors. The course is not easy but Joe is a really good professor and super caring about his students.

Joe is seriously one of the best professor I have ever had. Always patient with answering questions. Super caring about students. His lectures are REALLY interesting. His exams are really similar to the practice exams and are super reasonable. Too bad I cant rate him higher than a 5.

Joe is an amazing professor and I definitely recommend taking Industrial Organization with him. By far the best professor I have had thus far at Purdue, and attend all the lectures you can, it is definitely worth it!

Prof. Mazur is the best professor ever! As an international student, I was kinda worried about the participation points at first, due to my weak language. But Joe gave us so many bonus points, make sure we could earn enough. Plus he is accessible outside the class, go to his office hour and you'll get a lot of help for quizzes and homework.

Prof. Mazur brought a lot of energy to lectures that made the subject engaging. He did an amazing job at taking a technical subject and making it come alive. Ask Qs in class if you are lost. He is helpful and gracious in his responses. The course isnt easy and you need to stay up with the material, but if you put in he work, you will be rewarded.

Prof. Mazur is by far one of the best professors Ive ever had. He is extremely caring, will always help you when stuck and offers plenty of opportunities to achieve a good grade. The way the class is structured makes it extremely enjoyable, as he uses case studies along the usual theory. One of the best classes Ive ever taken all thanks to him

So Joe is definitely one of the best professors I've ever had. You can tell he genuinely cares about you and you getting the best grade you can. His lectures are super interesting! The exams are reasonable, the practice exams are basically the exam he gives, they are fair and actually test understanding rather than being too hard. I love Joe!
He is a great professor who really looks out for his students.

Joe is the best Econ lecturer I have ever met. His lectures are interesting, though the content is hard itself. He is willing to listen to students' feedbacks and trying to improve himself and the lectures. You will benefit from attending the lectures, even the attendance is not mandatory. Exams are not hard. Understanding is the point.

Not a ton of homework, very charismatic and enjoyable, like you actually want to attend his lectures, weird. His tests are hard as balls, but he gives you all the resources and information you need to study. Participation is mandatory, but super easy, answer 9 questions through the whole semester and you'll get 91% minimum
Awesome. Serious. Informative
Professor Mazur did a great job of making the lectures interesting and relating material. He was always interested. Class didn't have any homework and it wasn't that bad.
There was no reason to have closed-book exams, too many concepts, examples, models... you need to remember everything for the exam! There was no way a student can meet his requirement for exam.

Records all lectures so you don't have to a attend if you don't want to. The course is curved a good amount which is already stated in the rubric, do not miss any in class assignments or homework's, those are easy points compared to the exams. I didn't do well on exams (3 total) but ended up with a B+.
Her lectures are great, and exams are chill as long as you do your work!
A wonderful professor from Oxford, exam is not easy, but the lecture is easy to understand although it is also hard. Really responsible to students kindness and patient.

Took ECON 463 with Prowse (No option to choose 463 here in rate my professor). Probably the best professor I've had in any economics class here in Purdue. Excellent lectures, hilarious, easy to understand slides, and very accessible outside of class. The course is overall very well designed with exercises in-between to practice STATA.

Prof. Prowse's lectures are very, very clear. She repeats much of what she teaches multiple times to make sure everyone fully understands the concepts. I was surprised by how little it seemed we learned and how easy the homeworks and exercises were, but the tests are pretty derivation-heavy and can be challenging.

Excellent professor. Transparent about course expectations, has her lecture slides organized intuitively, and always prepared for class. Very accessible by email (and after move to online, Piazza) for thorough explanations of what can be challenging material.
Approachable, always willing to help student. Makes difficult material much easier to understand. One of the best professor I had

Econometrics can be a beast of a class, but Professor Prowse teaches in a way to make it more simplistic and easier for undergraduate students to understand. Class is mandatory 1 day a week for in-class exercises, but these are all specified in the syllabus you get the first day. There are not frequent homework assignments, but they are worth a lot

He does not do lecture slide, he just talks about the topic the whole class and occasionally writes stuff on the board. All hw is group work, tests are kinda tough bc there are no slides to review, only the text book. He is very nice tho

Prof Stokes was overall great! Understanding, good lectures, fair exams, optional attendance, and easy to find outside of class via office hours and/or email. The class content was interesting. There's also potential for a small final grade boost. My only complaint was that Brightspace was not updated very frequently with deadlines or instructions.
This man is the real estate GOAT, if you're going to take MGMT370 do it with Stokes

This guy is a good professor, he knows a lot about real estate and is funny in class. Homeworks are done in groups of at least 2 people, there are 3 exams, and 1 alumni interview project. On exams they are a mix of MC and FRQ and we use a blank Excel sheet for calculations. Prof Stokes is nice and sometimes gives us an extra 5 min during exams.

Prof Stokes is an alright professor, clearly knowledgeable about real estate and can be funny but sometimes lecture is confusing. Homework has to be completed in groups. Attendance is not mandatory but participation counts a bit (why??). Assignments and instructions are not updated well on Brightspace, exams are announced ~1 week away from the day.

Would recommend another professor if possible, exams were closed note and extremely difficult, the rules for exams were confusing. Lectures are not very helpful, attendance is not mandatory but she gets upset when attendance is low. Has extra credit questions in exams about her like her kids names. Posts study guides late that are't helpful anyways

Dr. McAleer is passionate but I would advise choosing another professor is possible. Lectures are okay, exams are extremely hard, in person and heavy on free response. No cheat sheet, werid exam rules, posts study guides late, and posts confusing announcements. Attendance is not mandatory but dislikes low attendance, would not take her again.

Dr. McAleer is passionate but I would advise choosing another professor is possible. Lectures are okay, exams are extremely hard, in person and heavy on free response. No cheat sheet, claims she's sick, posts study guides late, and posts confusing announcements. Attendance is not mandatory but dislikes low attendance, would not take her again.

GREATEST PROFESSOR EVER! She is so nice and fair, she makes the class feel like a family. Lectures are really interesting, and many real-world examples are used to help learn concepts. She even takes examples that you give in class, and puts them on the exam, making it a little more personalized. Very engaging class, YOU MUST TAKE A CLASS WITH HER!
Her class is fair and very well taught.

Genuinely such a kind person. She knows the names of almost every student across the 3 sections she teaches and is a phenomenal lecturer. If you do the work and pay attention in class you will be fine.

Rosokha is a great professor. Her lectures and synchronous sessions are engaging, and she truly wants you to succeed in her course. In some projects, it was difficult to understand what exactly she was looking for. Nevertheless, she was always available if we had questions. Would definitely recommend.

She will learn every person's name instantly and she genuinley cares about you as a human. I wanted to show up, and it's the first class I have ever felt like that. I am moving into HR because of her. I can not say enough good things about her and her class.

Prof. Rosokha is one of the best profs I've ever had. She is super intentional and caring. She also makes sure to try to build a connection with every student in a large lecture which is usually rare. I would really recommend this prof.
best professor ever shes so sweet. take her class if you have the chance!

She is the sweetest and best professor ever! Very kind, caring and tries to get to know all her students.
The content is very easy and actually interesting. As long as you read the textbook, you can get an A.

I liked her in the sense that she is such a caring professor and has very interesting lectures. Hw is easy but I would say the practice exams do not prepare you well for the test. The test is way more difficult and includes information that isn't on the practice exam or the review game we do in-class. Tests should be easier for how easy class is

Dr. Rosokha is one of the best professors I've ever had at Purdue. Her lectures are interactive and engaging. She is super sweet and really cares about her students! Loved her class and would totally take another class with her.
Very nice and friendly, fair grader, very passionate about the content she teaches

This course has been one of my favorite courses at the business school. Professor Rosokha genuinely cares about her students, and even learns everyone's names. It's a pretty easy class, I would just recommend attending class regularly and reviewing the study guide before the exam.

Super easy class with minimal homework. There's in class assignments, so you generally have to go to class, but usually the lectures have plenty of discussions/videos to keep them interesting. As long as you pay attention in class, you can easily get at least a B on the exams. The professor is also very sweet and genuinely cares about you.
Never take this Professor
She was super sweet and a great lecturer. HWs and group project are pretty easy, and if you pay attention to lecture and do the practice exams you will be fine for the exams.

Loved this class; she took our feedback, and we students altered the course of the class. The assignments are heavily based on discussions in lectures and in-class participation matters (15% of the grade). She is not strict about deadlines; just get it done. Assignments are about 0.5-1 page long.

The class isn't too difficult, and Prof. Smythe is a very caring teacher. However, the lectures feel too surface level and repetitive a lot of the time to be engaging. She can also be a bit pretentious at times and gives pretty bad feedback. That said, she does care about the success of her students and will throw the class bones a lot.

GREAT lecturer. Does not give feedback when grading and not clear when outlining assignments. Kind of has an attitude with you if you don't speak much in class. Don't miss lectures! She makes up assignments and due dates as she goes and you'll have to work around it. I was excited at first, but the lack of organization and clarity ruined it.

The class was wonderful I really enjoyed going. At first it had simple homework assignments, short easy quizzes, and essays. Halfway into the semester she changed the structure, which made the class easier but it was confusing for the class to completely change. If you rely on Brightspace, it may be hard for you because she uses it very little.
Best professor ever! I wish I could retake this class just to sit in her lectures. It was the most fun class i've taken. She is extremely chill and witty.
I love Stacey
Best class ever, all because of her
She's a queen we stan.

Stacey is one of the most genuine, passionate professors that I have had at Purdue. She did a tremendous job of explaining art history in an understandable way to someone who has never taken an art class before.

She is an excellent lecturer, however, her PowerPoint slide bullet points were very small and hard to read. Definitely get a seat toward the front and take good notes. The textbook is helpful, but not completely necessary if you take good notes.

I had Stacey for Italian last semester and she was absolutely wonderful. She was always there when you needed help and loved to see her students succeed. She was also very understanding when it came to missing class and grading as well. I definitely recommend having her as a professor! Plus she loves to bake ;D

I am bad at writing papers and I thought this class was going to be tough, but I actually really enjoyed it. She likes to ramble about her day and she's really funny. I only had to write like four papers and none of them were long. She grades easily and gives good feedback on your rough drafts so you can make improvements. She also brings in food!

Our professor was fired half way through the semester, and he was the replacement. I don't understand how his ratings are so high. He has a really strong accent, making him very difficult to understand, and his lectures are not engaging at all. He is available for extra help though. Overall, I would avoid him.

He is hands down one of the best professors ive had. Although his hw is very heavy and the course material is a bit difficult, he breaks everything down very simply in his lectures and also incorporates some geopolitics. he also offers a ton of extra credit and he is really kind, helpful and understanding.

Can't lie, I think he's slightly overrated but is pretty good. His homeworks are long and tedious but the exams are super easy and there is 5% extra credit between attendance and 2/3 of the class doing course evals. His lectures are sooo boring and intuitive though (mostly bc its an intro class), especially if you took Micro first.

His exams are pretty hard, but overall he is a very approachable professor. He will answer any questions you have and breaks down topics very well with examples during lectures. Only complaint i have is the lack of practice exams. Overall great professor!
Boring lectures and fell asleep multiple times. Exams were fair but the MCQ format was annoying, though macroeconomics is just boring tbh can't really fault the professor.

This was an ok professor, but his lectures are basically him reading presentations. He doesn't make content easy, and I would learn the same amount of things through the textbook or youtube. He says attendance isn't mandatory, but uses iClicker for points. Not the best if you want to be genuinely engaged during lectures.

Dr Vargas is one of the greatest economics professors and lectures I've had! he is super kind and very helpful. His lectures are well organized with lots of examples and always willing to answer questions before, during, and after class. I've had him for macro too and although the topics can be quiet hard he can help you through understanding well!

Dr Vargas is a ok professor. I personally found Macro a little bit more interesting compared to Micro. His exams are not bad as long as you do some studying. His lectures can be a bit boring but attendance is not mandatory and is counted as extra credit via iClicker. Homeworks can take some time since they are done on Pearson. Way better than Fox.

I love Vargas, I am actually planning to take as many economics classes as possible with him. He cares about his students and their success, and he is so accessible. He gives out extra credit for attending lecture, and he is kind and honestly hilarious. While I earned an A-, let that not reflect him in any way, just study more than me :) goat!!
Breaks down difficult statistics topics and does a great job at explaining them.

Vargas is clearly very knowledgeable and passionate about teaching. The class material doesn't always line up perfectly with the exams, but the exams and homework aren't too difficult. Overall good professor, and especially way better than the alternatives (Fox) for intro Econ classes.
honestly a super kind professor, but his material doesn't match up with exam. Lots of homework and material is difficult to understand.
The GOAT ECON Professor!

Professor Vargas was by far my best instructor this semester. He takes time to help his students and his lectures are very efficient, covering everything on exams. He offers optional in-class help sessions for the harder homework assignments, which makes a huge difference. He is always willing to answer questions. Easy A if you do the assignments.
Dr. Vargas is awesome. The material can be a bit tricky, but he breaks it down well. Tests aren't too hard.

Vargas is an amazing econ teacher. His lectures are very clear, and he is willing to talk with students who don't understand the material after class. The homework assignments are due every week are manageable but they may take time. He provides a practice exam with material similar to the real exams, and they helped us focus on what to study.

Vargas is a smart lecturer who knows what he's talking about and has taught this class for many years. Nonetheless, his lecture pacing can get very boring. Although it's likely because he's not native to the U.S., his voice lacks enthusiasm and isn't great at making the topic interesting. Overall, the class is moderate, slightly harder than 251

Vargas is good! Personally I thought the class was boring. Final and second exam are much harder than first so do well on the first one. Study and know concepts. He does care and explains questions well.
Good class, lectures dragged out a bit but in a good way since it helped us understand the concepts.

lectures were boring but not terrible. homeworks r on pearson, never read the textbook. he seems like a nice guy but i never went to class because it was boring, still got an ok grade. if u alr took 251 this will be pretty easy. second midterm is randomly hard.

The most incomprehensible exam writing ever. Someone needs to proof read him. For exams being 80% of your grade, its kind of unacceptable. Also be prepared for every question to not be a topic thats important at all to Macroeconomics but rather about random theories he didnt even mention in lectures.
He uses lots of visual aides while teaching like videos and real world data that is extremely helpful.

Vargas is a great professor for ECON 252, and he offers extra credit for showing up to class and answering an iClicker question or two correct. Gives weekly homework and tests are challenging but he does post practice exams which help. He does have an accent but you can still easily understand him. Would definitely take another one of his classes

Pretty good class with lots of extra credit. Attendance is extra credit. Homework could be time consuming and annoying but you can redo questions you get wrong. You don't really have to pay attention/do anything in class, but it does make the exams a bit difficult. The lectures are interesting and the professor is nice.
Dr. Vargas is a good professor, and I enjoyed his class. He was able to break down complicated topics, making them easier to understand.
