Meh. His English is such that it's very hard to know when one sentence stops and the next begins; or whether he's asking a question or making a statement. This makes it difficult to follow the logic or his train of thought. This is not helpful, especially when talking about vector spaces. And his exams are much harder than HW practice tests.

I was not a huge fan of how he explained a lot of concepts and had a tough time understanding him in general. watched another profs lectures to compensate. Exams were a mix of easier procedural problems and some very difficult conceptual (70ish averages). I think he would be better in person.
Amazing lecture. Very friendly. Makes hilarious comments often. Keeps the class engaging and happy

I took MA 341. The lecture was clear and slow so most people could catch on. Quiz and exams are fairly easy. If one is understanding all the materials, he or she never need to worry about that. But THIS COURSE IS STILL DIFFICULT. There're a lot homework and materials. So you really need to work hard to get a good grade.

I took his class for MA 265. He can be pretty difficult to understand and he struggles to teach concepts, but the class's applied material is easy. The tests aren't too bad despite requiring some conceptual understanding, they resemble the practice tests you are given.
Poor communication skill yet passed the message reasonably well, but needs improvement.

Had him for MA265. It helps that the content of this course is easy, but he is easy to understand. Usually gives a quiz every other week. From my experience he based the tests off of his practice exams that he creates so study those. Overall, you definitely could have a worse professor at purdue math.

He has trouble understanding questions. He assigns a lot of homework which is good. He prides himself on giving the tests based on your fundamental knowledge, which he does not teach well. My advice would be to take the class only if you feel confident before starting. If you are questionable in areas, don't count on him to clear things up for you.
Wonderful lecture

He actually speaks really good English and all of his lectures were very clear. First math class at Purdue that I got a solid A. He really makes sure that you are learning the material and his quizzes and tests are very fair and he gives a lot of partial credit. I absolutely loved this class and I have a lot of friends who struggled with other prof

Great lectures and a really well organized and paced class. The grading scale is also very forgiving. The only frustrating element of the class was having to get up at 8:30 for lectures. Overall incredible class.

He was very open to feedback and was accessible outside of class. He did give quizzes every Friday, so participation is mandatory on Fridays. However, his lectures were very engaging and you could tell he is very passionate in his work. Additionally, he was understanding of our stresses, such as having a Friday quiz during an exam week

Great guy. Wants to know you and always says hello if you see him outside of class. One of the most genuine people at Purdue. You can tell he is such a distinguished person who is truly passionate about his work.

He quizzes every week, but they are easy if you listen to him and do your homework. If you go a step more and listen on Fridays, you'll have near a 100 in this class. Dr. Wasserman makes sure his class is enjoyable even with some pretty boring topics.
Great guy and a great prof. The kind of person you'd expect and want as your first Purdue professor. Can't recommend enough.

Love love love Wasserman. He's so kind and you can tell he cares so much about the students. You need an 85% to get an A, with 3-4 dropped quizzes and either 1 dropped midterm or half-dropped final. Only necessary attendance is Friday quizzes, every week. Would recommend actually doing the reading, but it's not checked. Overall loved the class!

Dr. Wasserman is da GOAT. He wants to get to know and his lectures have amazing demos and clear notes. You only need an 85 to get an A and he lets you drop 4 quizzes, 1 midterm or half the final, and 1 lab. Dr. Wasserman sets you up very well for higher level chem no matter if you plan to go into grad, medical, dental, or whatever career!

Dr. Wasserman was probably one of my favorite professors this semester. He's really creative with his lectures and is happy to answer any questions that his students have. The only thing that gets me are the Friday quizzes, which can be a bit stressful at times to prepare for. There's a lot of outside work, however. But Wasserman is awesome!
His lectures are nice with demos and stuff, I just wish he provided more review material for tests throughout the course. Chill professor.

GO TO THE LECTURES FOR CHM 136. Quizzes and exams have lecture based questions that aren't covered by the homework or textbook. CHM 136 was pretty easy, though. TONS of partial credit was given on exams and lots of things get dropped at the end of the semester.

Dr. Wasserman is a nice guy but I found his lectures to confusing and somewhat irrelevant. Instead of explaining concepts he often derived complicated equations we didn't have to know as a way to teach the concepts. But he posts relevant study guides and practice questions for each test so the exams are very managable- TAs are lenient graders too.
Literally my favorite professor so far, he's so sweet. This class was graded super generously as well.
Pros: -You learn a lot -Interactive class -Connections -Fair grader Cons: -Can be tough sometimes (concepts) -Kinda wants u to attend class badly

He is clearly very passionate about the subject and wants his students to learn. He makes up games and activities instead of lectures on Fridays and uploads lots of resources on Blackboard. Almost every lecture had a demo, which made the morning class better.

I had wasserman for the second half of chem 116. His online lectures were interesting and Straight to the point. Him and the professor for the first half of chem 116 wanted everybody to be graded fairly so an A was roughly a 75% due to only 12 multiple choice questions on the exams. The exams were hard but i hear the TA's right them and not him.

He is very organized and gives very clear explanations. We did a lot of problems in class and he went step by step explaining  it. He is also very helpful and wants students to understand the material.
I felt like Dr. Wasserman did a great job of helping visualize some of the more complex topics. Great teacher.
Nice guy, thick accent but is pretty understandable. Again, be prepared to work a lot of math problems since the exams in this course tend to be about 70% math problems.

Due to my own lack of motivation, I didn't personally interact with him but I notice that he helps students a lot who want to learn. His lectures were clear and I love his accent (kept me awake unlike Robinson...) didn't go to many lectures but the tests aren't horrible. Just keep up and you can get an A.
Terrible professor. Writes ridiculous exam questions. His style is just weird.

I had Robinson for the first half of CHM 116, and he was a good professor, but then I got Wasserman for the second half, and suddenly had a renewed interest in Chemistry. He is an excellent lecturer. He's the best I've had so far. He's good at answering questions and he works through problems by hand in lecture, which is really helpful.

Dr. Wasserman is a good lecturer. When I took CHM116, Dr. McMillan does the first half of the semester and Dr. Wasserman does the second half. Dr. Wasserman is still pretty new to Purdue so that may be why. He is a better lecturer than Dr. McMillan, but harder to understand due to his Colombian accent. Does a lot of neat in-class experiments.

Amazing professor (idk why everyone throws hate at him in RMP). Easy grader, has extra credit in every quiz and lab. GOOD lectures and have PPT posted before each class. He NEVER reads from the PPTs. He asks interesting questions to the class, which makes you engage. He is also always open to feedback.

This professor has been absolutely delinquent in his class policies. He refuses to take students' unique circumstances into account. Typical juvenile behavior from someone who is more interested in his personal career than educating the students who are unfortunate enough to take his required class for their tracks

He's a nice guy, but he isn't invested in teaching. He simply reads off the slides, which he borrows from other professors. Sometimes he even gets confused about what's on the slide. The curve is decent, though.

Honestly, this class was a mess because the whole structure set us up to fail. His lectures were not helpful and the projects were way too complex. He was also very unflexible with the midterm regrade deadline and barely gave us any resources. This is the worst CS class I have ever taken and I wish I checked the reviews before taking it.
Awful course, labs do not align with the content covered in the course. Wish I had looked into reviews before taking this course with Pedro.

Words cannot express how much I dislike this professor. No clear grading criteria, coming out of our last midterm. He didn't let us discuss the exam after the regrade deadline, which was set during spring break so TAs didn't reply to all of them. What is his deal omg.

A very hard class and the projects are very confusing. You will need to keep communicating with the TA and professor to get clarifications. The exams are hard. I have to read 2 textbooks (OSTEP is such a great book) to do well in the class. The curve is generous but I really hope our professor can learn from this semester and improve in the future

Slides are unorganized. Like to test on skew questions and contents not on slides. A lot of readings (3 textbooks). 3 labs take up 50% of the final grade but the lab handouts are very confusing, requirements change two hours before the deadline, the earlier you start the more time you waste fixing bugs in starter code. 100h+ wasted in this course.

You're not gonna learn a thing from the lab because they're hot garbage. I've never seen such an ill-scheduled, sloppy, lazy and irresponsible lab before and it's honestly a waste of time. I would rather spend a month making a VFS that actually makes sense rather than this piece of hot garbage that has 0 practical usages. 0/10 AVOID AT ALL COST

Everything that could've been said is already said by other reviews. Poorly organized labs, no test cases, midterms test materials that were not covered, last lab is unreasonably huge and people did not have time to complete it. Please for the sake of your sanity, avoid at all cost. I only gave 1/5 because there was no lower options.
Don't take this course, the labs are not good, the TAs nor the professor knows what's going on. This course is a total mess, I guess the professor doesn't really care about anyone in the course.

He just doesn't care about this class, or his student, or his TAs. If he wants to focus on his research, how about using old labs which has clear instructions and solution? Instead, he wants to make new labs, but instead of he design it, he throws it to the TAs and never cares about it anymore, leading to unclear and ridiculous lab handouts.

This is probably one of the worst OS courses out there. 

Expect :
- A "Learning" OS from the 90s, which won't compile on your system
- Really old Hardware that fail on a whim (No emulation possible!)
- Ambiguous project questions, with no grading criteria or transparency
- Extremely hard midterm with a really bad curve
- Unending frustration

No transparency in grading
Expect to get 0/160 in labs or less than 50% in midterm
No test cases were given for ambiguous labs. Lab specification changes often without prompt notification and no partial grading. Marks dedicated based on fixed rubric. Most of the lecture is just reading from the slides or answering student questions.

The labs are very ambiguous and sometimes even make no sense, and we ended up constantly changing the lab handout, which sometimes creates even more confusion. There are no test cases given, no grading criteria, so each project is an endless time sink. It's not even funny that even the processor doesn't have a solution for the lab.

Very badly structured class. Professor did not know how to use Brightspace or Piazza. Lectures have nothing to do with projects. A lot of the material is out of the Software Testing scope or too complex for undergraduate level. Exams were not related to lectures or projects either. Very little explanation in lectures and a lot was required from us

I've had plenty of difficult and poorly run CS classes at Purdue but this is just ridiculous. Staff lied about exam format. Piazza TA answers sometimes directly contradict each other. Projects are way too in depth for time given, impractical, and mostly irrelevant to lecture. Every person I've spoken to that's in this class is incredibly frustrated

Most difficult professor I've had at Purdue. This is a software testing class, but two of the projects seemed like they should be way beyond the scope of this class. It is impossible to do the projects given the amount of resources we get. The TA's have single-hour office hours for four days of the week, which is just not enough.

The projects are focused on things not fully explained in class. Timelines were messy & handouts had wrong info/got changed multiple times. Students had to clarify these mistakes on Piazza for them to get corrected. There are only 2 TAs & not a lot of office hours. Prof expects you to be well informed on topics not relevant to testing. Unorganized.
Lecture is simple but labs are hard. Labs are ill-scheduled and you dont have enough time to finish him labs all by yourself.
His lecture was monotone, boring, and lacking enthusiasm, he did not seem to want to be there.

Lectures are very boring, and the homeworks, while somewhat rare (every ~2 weeks) each one is easy, but long and annoying. The grading scale was 50 points from exams (with questions copied from the homework), 50 points from the homework, and 20 points from participation. However, you are only graded out of 100 points. Overall boring but easy.

He is very full of himself as shown by his recored lectures. He may be a big deal in the climate society but we are here to learn about climate, not your achievements. Its fine if he were discussing how his findings are relevant to the course, but instead he wants to brag instead of showing his students in an educational and informative way.

He has a huge ego, respect him as much as you want and he'll still be very vague and won't help with much. His tests are bs as they're completely from the homeworks that are extremely long, vague, and pointless towards anyone's education. For the most part the course is fairly easy but very useless. Need some easy credits to get over with, take 117
Solid dude, your grade is 50% homework and 50% exams. There is only 2 exams and 5 homeworks. Also has an extra 20% in extracredit.

This was a very easy course. The grading scheme is 120 possible points graded out of 100. Almost all of the assignments are group work so you are set if you have a good group. It might be more difficult otherwise. I failed the only exam in the class and still ended with A+.

Professor Huber is vague in his assignments, to the point where there have to be clarifying questions/assumptions on everything. The class is easy if you just do the assignments, but they are often long with nothing to gain. Additionally, he has a huge ego and is rude to students. Nice emails are met with one word replies and rude remarks.
The lectures are very engaging. Most of the work in the class is with a group and the workload is very light. Grade is 120/100 so it is very easy to do well.

Professor Huber is not bad as a lecturer, but his assignments are awful. Each one is uniquely vague and requires several rounds of clarification. For the final project, even after submission I was still unsure I did anything correctly. Luckily he's not too bad of a grader but it was much more of a pain than it needed to be.

Worst class I have ever taken, mainly for one reason: Huber In the beginning, Huber is alright. He progressively gets worse and worse and is a very tough/unfair grader. Lectures are dragged on, assignments are vague, and he has such a big ego. Expect to put several hours of work into the course each week, yet get nothing out of it.

Pretty easy course, even though the grading is a little weird. 4 hotseat questions are worth the same amount of points as a homework that takes 2-3 hours. But besides that, Dr. Huber was a very laid back yet interesting professor, and I enjoyed going to his lectures.

Great at making earth science more clear to non-majors! The rest of the course uses these topics to learn about climate change and some politics surrounding it. He does a good job incorporating current events to lecture, but sometimes he can talk on one topic for an extended time! Overall easy workload and he is passionate teaching climate change.
Professor Huber is a pretty laid-back, approachable professor. He goes into good detail the material discussed in class, and the class isn't very difficult since it's a slower-paced class.

I took this class (climate change and society) as a requirement for my great issues in science requirement and i'm glad i did. His lectures are interactive and very interesting. He is very knowledgeable on the subject matter and brought up issues about climate change that i hadn't thought about.

This professor is very unspecific.  He will give you the rubric for an assignment only for you to follow the rubric and get a 2/"8" on the assignment. He expresses that science is ambiguous and that you need to expand on concepts; however, if you expand too much or write more than he wanted you loose points.  Horrible grading system/won't help you.

He is really strict and make no sense about grading. What he and his grader will said "There is no full score for assignment, your grade depend on your effort". His instruction about paper is totally useless and make no sense. I suggest don't take his class and I am going to report this poor prof.
No Comments
AHHHHHHHHH... If it weren't for my friends in the class I would have stabbed myself in the eye with a sharp object.
Very smooth and well organized course. Went through the basics of probability and random variables clearly.
A weird professor…Lecture is boring and not accessible in office hours

I really enjoyed his lectures as he explained the topics very well, even the more difficult topics later in the course.
Grading was quite lenient and exams were of a reasonable difficulty and similar to the practice exams that he sent and the course homeworks.
Would definitely recommend.

Buzzard is a great professor with clear notes and expectations. That said his exam problems are extremely challenging and have a lot of curve balls. However he is 100% open to helping and giving clarification. To succeed at 519, do all the problems in Ross both graded+ungraded and you'll do well

Great prof overall. If you can get him, go for it - and select early. I messed up my final exam pretty bad (MCQs) and ended up with an overall score ~ 84 out of 100. Still got an A-.
Advice: Attend his lectures in person. You will never get time again to watch the lectures online. It doesn't happen. You don't have to pay attention-just attend!!!
Great professor! Exams are tough but he is a reasonable grader.
Great lectures. Makes the content easy to follow. You can tell that he cares that students are learning. Overall a great experience taking the course with him.

I really enjoyed Buzzard as a professor. He shows great examples and gives time for you to try things on your own. I found it very helpful to go to office hours since the class was hard but he does a great job of explaining things, especially during 1 on 1. Funny guy and really enjoyed

Buzzard does a good job overall. His penmanship won't win any awards, but is still mostly readable. His lectures are useful, but I watched Chen's lectures on BoilerCast instead because I liked his style more. The exams sucked all the fun out of the course for me and I actually CODOed out of math. Prepare to work hard. Good luck. You will need it.

He is very approachable as a person. All of his lectures are very well organized. He always starts conceptual, and then moves on to examples that are useful in the online homework and in exams. He tries to interact in lecture, and if a student gives a wrong answer, he looks at it from their perspective and explains where they were right and wrong.

Wonderful professor. Always smiles and makes the lectures fun, keeps the class alive. I wasn't enrolled just wanted to sit in his class and he was super supportive of this, his attitude really helped motivate me.
Teaches concepts well. Very helpful. Makes it fun sometimes
Awesome professor! Speaks clear English and is very clear in class. His exams had difficult questions, but they were curved very fairly.

Fantastic. Best math professor I have had. I love math but I feel that my success in this class was in large part, due to Buzzard. He speaks clear english, is very accessible outside of class, and is very willing to help. The way he presents material is well though out and organized.
Very good! Lectures are easy to follow, very accessible in and out of class.

The professor is very bad at teaching and explaining things. I haven't learned anything from this class. He cannot teach or explain any concept and does not offer resources to help students succeed. In my opinion, he should not teach undergraduate classes. Do not take this class.

Tough course, graded by 12 quizzes (+1 dropped). Some quizzes rocked, others sucked horrifically. Inconsistent, either breezed through slides or took forever to explain one thing. I would appreciate the addition of programming assignments to lessen the impact of bad quiz grades.

Not recommend professor for PhD and also course, basically disorganized and also it is hard to understand him. The class all gives unuseful staff. Overall, don't recommend his class. Also for his PhD.

Homework is graded with unclear rubric. Attendence is worth 5% and is not extra. He didn't even put down students' names. Scores were given arbitrarily. If you use his starter code in python, you should prepare to debug for him. The system was completely down for a week right before deadline. He barely could answer only a few questions on piazza.

This is the most ridiculous instructor I have ever seen in my life and let me know how awful a professor receive 1 on RMP can be. The ridiculous single-threaded autograder for the project, which was down during most of the time, the slides with the examples that calculated wrong and confused you, and mysterious grading criteria of participation.

5 percent of your grade is from class participation and it's extremely hard to get. Each endorsed answer of piazza gives you 1% and each bug found in project gives 2%, so good luck if you used Java for the compiler where most of the bugs have already been taken care of. Gotta say people are for real upset about this.

Really really bad professor. The baddest professor I have ever met in my life. Especially the way to grade your attendance. This is the only prof who said "Attendance is not based on how often you came to class". If you attend all classes, good luck, you may still get a 0 for your attendance grade. RUN! do not take this class! RNM tui qian.

Actually the worst professor I've had at Purdue. The lectures make no sense and the course is very disorganized. The source code has bugs in it that make your work twice as difficult, and he can't debug his own code (it's another professor's work that he pawns off as his own, he couldn't be bothered to put in effort). Do not take compilers w/ him.

During the lecture, he cannot explain everything clearly, his slides make no sense, and you can't really learn from his lecture. His project is a disaster, you have to debug for him(takes about a day) since his code is pretty buggy and I bet he didn't test his code before he publish it to the class. (Maybe he just can't find or fix his bug^^)
Horrible and unclear grading scheme. Unprepared during office hours and lecture. Students gave up asking questions after a few lectures.

The only prof I have met so far in ECE who seems to think free-response exams should be graded with no partial credit. Ridiculous project step instructions, with basically no support if you do the Python version, then putting the work of fixing their bugs on students. Never take this class unless Kulkarni is teaching it.

His lectures are not very helpful, and he comes off as insensitive and condescending to students. One student asks him for clear instructions on project on Piazza, he replied "we didn't mean to babysit." You should know what you're getting into before taking his 468/595/573, they're basically the same class, but you need to do more in 573.
Very badly designed project. The office hours are extremely useless. The TAs are not even familiar with the codes and are even more confused than you.

The professor attacks you if you ask for an extension even though he didn't even release the assignment until much later. So in order to keep the original due dates, he makes 2 steps due the same week. He is condescending and will constantly remind you that you have poor time management skills even though it's his fault.
The project guidelines are so confusing, and it takes so long to understand anything. Highly recommend not to take this class.

Lectures give you a good understanding of the subject. Furthermore, the professor is very helpful in his office hours and really tries to make sure you know how to solve the problems. Exams were challenging but fair; as long as you did them yourself you would do good. He also gives a participation bonus so you don't have to worry about grades.

Dr. Su's lecture is clear and concise, and welcome questions that students have. The exams aren't easy to complete in the given time but is fair and doable. The exam grader is also very lenient on giving partial credit. He gives plenty of extra credits for participation in class which makes learning less stressful.

Jianxi Su is a great professor who provides a bunch of extra credit points just for asking questions in class. He is an easy grader on exams, and the first two midterm exams contain questions that are exact copies from the HWs.

super sweet guy and really great professor. taught everything very simple and clearly, exams VERY closely resemble homework sometimes with word for word questions. extra credit given if u participate. very easy to get a good grade. love this guy, def take him if ur actuarial
Dr. Su is a great professor for this course. His hw and exams are fair and lectures help you understand the material easily.

Dr. Su is what every professor in that stats department should strive to be. He does an incredible job of explaining difficult concepts clearly and engaging with the class to confirm they are being absorbed. Exams can be tough to complete within the given time period but are more than fair.

Su is an excellent professor and very helpful with questions. He had an ec policy where if you went to OH or asked questions in class you would get a 10% overall boost. This made the class a lot more laid back and easier. The course content can get hard near the end but if you do all the homeworks well you can manage.

Dr. Su's notes might seem hard to understand at first, but as you get on in the semester, it starts to make more sense. He definitely challenges you to think outside the box on the homework, but he helps you when you need it. The homework is more than enough to help you prepare for the exams, and he is a very lenient grader.

Amazing prof. Really tries to get you to understand probability at an intuitive level. The tests are difficult, and weight a lot, but very fair overall. Lots of extra credit. If he sees people are busy, he will allow you to take an extension. Always tries to show you how you can apply what you learn in research or in practice.
Amazing professor. Material can get a little difficult near the end.

I accidentally signed up for actuarial section of STAT416 with Professor Su. In spite of that I really enjoyed the class, and thought the actuarial applications of the theories were really interesting. The class was run very well, and there was phenomenal communication throughout. I only wish he taught STAT417.
Dr. Su is a good lecturer. He gives a lot of EC opportunities as well. Overall great guy.

Very organized, concise lectures. Usually very easy to follow. No textbook or online notes, so going to lecture is the only way to learn the material. Good sense of humor. Homeworks and exams are fine - what you'd expect from a college math course.
Sweet guy and a super easy grader

Highly recommend going to class. 381 is the hardest CS course I took and he was a brilliant professor and made all the concepts as clear as he could. Gave brilliant examples in class and always open to discuss after class. He is also really good about not making students feel bad if they don't get concepts and actually cares about students.

CS 381 was a time-intensive course, but Professor Dey was able to break down the concepts extremely well. He took time to answer questions during and after class, and he is very clear in what he is trying to say during the class. I also appreciated that he goes at a pace that is easy to keep up with during the lecture.

Lectures were engaging and he cared that students were understanding the content. The class itself is of course extremely difficult. Homeworks are time sinks and exams will be difficult to pass if you do not understand the content well. Definitely would recommend going to lecture

I found Dey's lectures difficult to follow and boring. The content itself was also hard for me, so that contributed to it. He would often times in lecture say things like "look at this on your own later" or "just think about it until you understand it" which was funny but unhelpful. Reading the textbook isn't required but is pretty beneficial.

Good professor, but the lectures would sometimes be boring or difficult to follow. The homeworks are near impossible to do well on if you miss out on the PSOs or lectures, and the recordings are not as good as attending in person. The exams are reasonable if you practice the concepts well. The material is not intuitive and difficult to learn.

Lectures were great and content was clearly presented. HW took a decent amount of time each week but wasn't terrible and was great practice for the exam. Nothing to complain about except maybe that you had to go to lectures and PSO or you would have a hard time catching up (recorded lectures isn't the same as going in person).

I took CS 182 with Professor Dey, and it was pretty good. Professor Dey really explained the contents well although the materials were not easy. He really understands what he is explaining. He is also fair and makes students involved during the lectures by making us think as much as possible. HWs were hard. Definitely enjoyed it when I look back.

I am taking CS182 with Dey and Sellke. Majority of the class was taught by Dey and a few lectures Sellke gave lecture. The class is very well organized. Too much homework though (Expect to have a homework due every Friday).

Took CS182 with Prof.Dey. The class, by far, is the most organized class I have ever had. Homework are posted at a regular time every week and returned in less than a week. The exams are fair. He teaches material well. Q/A forum response rate is pretty fast. He doesn't tolerate plagiarism (I liked that Dey caught some students cheating).

1) He takes great interest in answering questions. He is very careful with answering them and follows up with students in the later lectures with even more details! Wow!
2) He puts emphasis on the points that are critical during lectures.
3) He also ecnourages and appreciates students for their class involvement and makes the lectures enjoyable.

The lecture of Prof.Dey is amazing, he always explain everything in a clear and understandable way. The exams are not so hard compared to those from other professors on CS580. But the TAs are awful, they are hard to communicate and sometimes give wrong hints, always neglect the students' thoughts, hold terrible attitudes when asking for regrade.
Took the class as an elective (Part 61) student. Best class I've taken at this school and enjoyed going to the lectures so much. Prepared well for FAA exam and was very flexible on deadlines.
Incredibly knowledgeable and cares about the subject matter in the course.

I really enjoyed having Deb as a professor. She is very accommodating and will give extensions for pretty much anything. Do the work and you will pass the class. Every test is taken straight from FAA test questions so it helps you study for the written test. Towards the end the class becomes more like a study hall as people finish their written.
Great teacher and prepared me well for faa written
She is the best lecturer at Purdue by far. Give her a raise!

She is super super nice and really cares about each individual. The quizzes are the FAA questions which makes it clear if you need to study more. She'll extend your deadlines if you have some sort of issue. She'll tell engaging stories to help with the material. One of the best professors I've had hands down.
Professor Henneberry is a wonderful teacher. Always prepared for lectures and has funny stories.

Deb is awesome and will extend any due date for any reason at all. Her stories are funny, interesting, and relevant. Just show up to class and you will be okay. She doesn't try to make commercial any more complicated than it is - her quizzes are the exact FAA questions.
Super knowledgeable and experienced professor. Funny and caring as well. Brings balance to the force :)

Deb is an amazing professor and a wonderful addition to SATT - she genuinely cares about each of her students, and is always around to help, give advice, or just to chat and joke around with. While class can get boring at times (it is basically PPL ground, just more in-depth) she has some amazing and funny stories to keep your attention.

Super down to earth person. Will extend any deadline you need and is very friendly. Lectures go off topic but in a good way and her experience stories in aviation are priceless. Would definitely just listen to her talk about some niche topic again even if I wasn't in the class.

Dr. Henneberry is hilarious.  She makes subtle references to great music and connects with students. She clearly knows what she is talking about and has a really interesting professional background. She genuinely cares about the students and their success, so pay attention and do it is asked of you and you won't have problems.

She did a good job in explaining the concepts clear with a combination of outlines, lecture notes and example problems. I wish she can spend more time on the the exam practice problems before each exam.

Very great lecturer in general. It is evident that she knows what she is talking about and does not just read the slides. She has some nice insights and perspective from research which helps give some context. Her very understanding nature and flexibility as a professor is helpful.
Class lectures are well organized. She cares and is willing to help inside and outside of class. Homework is difficult, but her homework section helps.
Have to learn from the book
Never-ending lectures with basically mandatory office hours that offer very little in the way of help. They mostly just serve to finish the lecture she didn't finish during class time.

I had Tian Li Fall 2020 when she first arrived. She is a brand new professor but she still has a lot to learn. Maybe in the future she could improve but at current state is advised to seek other professors
Terrible experience. Can not explain the course material properly, and the homework is really tough and confusing.
Thick accent and does not give you enough material in order to accomplish and complete your homework.
Good professor, tests are heavily dependent on lecture slides. Attendance credit is based on accuracy not completion.
His lectures are engaging and he always motivates concepts before introducing them, which isn't that common in CS. He's also a really nice and approachable guy.

He was a great professor even though it was his first time teaching the class. His lectures are engaging and he will ask the class questions throughout lecture. He is very accessible at his office hours and he was always very helpful at answering questions.

Prof. Ameranis is goated! I found value in his lectures, and his style of teaching is very open and flexible. The accent was a bit hard to understand at times, but otherwise no complaints. Exams were tricky, but nicely curved. Do get started early on the projects tho. Also, his lectures on graphs & hashing were some of the best ive ever seen

Professor Ameranis speaks clearly. Even though he is "new" at teaching, he gets to the point in lectures, he is not monotone or repetitive which makes the 50 minute lectures a breeze. If you have never programmed in your life, well lets just say you will need to put in a lot more effort than those kids who have been programming since 6th grade.

He's a new professor, but by no means unexperienced. I really enjoyed the setup of the lectures, as the concepts explained in class clicked very quickly apposed to if I had to miss a class and learn the information outside of class. 

Main thing: If you show up to classes, put in effort in class, and think critically you will do well.

I had Prof. Paulson for 251, it just wasn't an option. Hard rate since I think the class is structured abysmally but he basically just read the slides he was given so I figured a 3 made sense until I went from a 97 to a 90 on the final project and he ghosted me and my entire group's emails. They just never did a regrade on a different assignment.

Able to explain the material in a clear and concise way. His lectures were really fun, he had demos to bring to class. His office hours are super helpful and you can tell he genuinely cares about his students. Extremely chill guy and great person to talk to outside of class. Overall great professor!
Nice person and good at explaining material. Went a little fast though

I had Professor Paulson for his first ever semester of teaching and he was phenomenal. Knows his stuff, cares about your learning, what more could you want? However, he forgot to square the pi term in the critical buckling equation, which subtracts 8 points from my rating so he gets a -3/5. Otherwise great teacher.

Great lecturer. Paulson explains all of the material very thoroughly and provides sufficient example problems. Helpful during office hours, answers all questions, and is overall a nice guy to talk to outside of class.
First time lecturing, great effort

Really good professor, difficulty is mainly from how the class is structured. Super homework heavy with a bunch of team projects, but there are a ton of resources to help you. Most coursework in the semester I had ENGR133, especially for 2 credits, but nothing was super overwhelming. Pretty easy class if you know Python.
She is very knowledgeable about the topics and is incredibly helpful. One of my all time favorite professors.
Will help with your code if your confused, but will not feed the answer. She is a very good professor.

These ratings are harsh it was not as difficult as I expected. The in person lectures are not helpful, do online if you can. She is very long winded and over explains every detail. She has given a lot of grace compared to other classes I've taken. If you have taken any STEM class at Purdue, this class will not be a problem for you. Project heavy

Took a class on technical writing... instead got a class *entirely* about accessibility—and somehow, it's the least accessible class I've ever taken. I also can't understand how someone can be so long-winded and write so unsubstantively while teaching a class on **technical writing**. What an absolute joke.

Tracy Clark, while well-intentioned, is a subpar professor whose course and material did not teach me about--nor reflect principles of--technical writing. Her Brightspace is bloated, with redundant class assignments taking longer to explain than complete. You can do well, but sitting through her mandatory lectures is tiring. Go online if you can.

Tracy's Brightspace setup is confusing; assignments are giant text blocks that require multiple tabs to decode. Lectures are long with little substance—she once spent 5+ mins explaining how to click a button. Attendance is strict; missing over three classes drops your grade by multiple letter grades even with perfect HW grades. Avoid this class.
TOO HARD. AVOID

like everyone else said, she talks *so much* without conveying much of substance. she attempts to make her BrightSpace navigable and fails since her attempts at clarity result in an overwhelming amount of information. the course is easy, the grading's light, the late "policy" is laughable, and she makes you do things in class that never get graded.

Tracy is like a disorganized teenager. She is exceedingly passive aggressive, talks excessively without saying anything of substance, and bends her own rules when its to her own convenience. 

Given her role as an English teacher, I am surprised by her lack of proficiency in the writing. Purdue should discontinue her employment. Advice? Avoid her.

The worst teacher you can ever have. I have had middle school teachers who could teach better than her. Her lectures are all over the place and she is never on point. It just feels like she has no teaching plan for her lectures. Its impossible to understand the assignments and what she is looking for due to her explanation being all over the place.

She likes to talk and talk and talk. Make sure you don't have a class right after this class you will be late every single day. Although she talks and talks all the students still have no clue on what to do. She takes extra time to explain something useless and takes no time to explain the work we need to do.

This professor wrote 10 pages when 1 page could do the job. I was often lost on the course page trying to find instructions for assignments. Despite being longwinded, instructions were also general/nondescript. Feedback is similar. Not insightful and overwhelming amounts of text. Expect to be torn apart over details you never expected to matter.

She is very wordy with everything she posts. To the point where you aren't clear of what to do because she says so much. Is very critical of assignments even if you have all the required information. Overall, a terrible professor.

Really terrible learning experience, course materials are confusing and rambling. Feedback/grading is impossible to predict (you'd lose points for using the word "Note" instead of "Hint") and critical in weird ways ("Purdue already decided to do blah blah 3 years ago, so your report isn't useful to Purdue"). She's unexpectedly passive aggressive.

Ironically, she's terrible at writing, which mattered significantly when all of her communications were through Brightspace posts during an asynchronous summer class. Describes otherwise simple assignments in absurdly long, rambling, and confusing essays. For example, her syllabus is over 5000 words, and you are quizzed on it. Pointless, dumb work.

One of the worst professors I have taken at Purdue. Took the summer portion of ENGL 421 and did not receive 75% of my grades until the grade deadline set by Purdue. The irony of it being a technical writing class is that she is a terribly incoherent writer. The entire course revolves around Brightspace and her Brightspace is terribly laid out.

Worst class I have ever taken. The class content was focused entirely around Brightspace and how great it is. I then found out she was one of teachers who got us using Brightspace. Weekly busywork assignments dedicated to using Brightspace and helping students and professors like Brightspace. A completely useless class. The class is organized poor

T/R Power Hour. Talked the entire time, yet still managed to not provide very few examples for how to complete projects and even fewer grading criteria or instructions for them. Discussion board participation counted for a laughably high portion of the grade. Very difficult to take the course seriously because of how painfully frustrating it was.

Too verbose in her teaching. Does not have clear and concise expectations. Passive aggressive in her feedback, almost to the point of insulting and disparaging. 0/10 for this class. Can not stress how much I do not recommend this class with Professor Clark.
Avoid her is the only advice.

One of the most appalling professors I have had. For a technical writing course her course set up on BrightSpace was both laughable and very unironic of how poorly formatted it was, making it extremely difficult to navigate among the plethora of course content. She put below minimum effort into course and certainly did not lead by example.

This class was absolutely awful. We had long online lectures where she talked constantly and repeated herself every 30 seconds, total waste of time. Grading criteria was never clear, group projects were terrible, and overall I learned nothing about technical writing. Don't bother taking this class with her.

Tracy likes to talk...a lot. We have 1.25 hour classes and she talks the entire time yet we are all still confused as to what is expected of us. She is very wordy in her explanations yet conveys nothing. I don't feel like I am in a health and human sciences writing class. She is teaching like she's teaching a multimedia/technical writing class. :(

Absolutely awful. Requires mountains more work than any other professor teaching this class. I've learned nothing, despite spending way more time on this class than it deserves. Her project descriptions and syllabus are long, unclear, and (ironically) terrible examples of technical writing. Take this course with anyone else.

At first, I had an open mind after viewing RateMyProfessor, but after starting and going through the class I agreed with the comments. She didn't provide clear guidelines, due dates, or grading criteria. If you are a tech or engineering student, request to have another professor.

While you may find that some of the lectures are a bit dull, attendance for this class is mandatory. Clark does talk a lot during lectures and goes on a lot of tangents but the material is helpful. No quizzes or tests, you will be graded on 3 projects and a combination of other stuff for the last percentage of your grade.

So Tracy seems like a person who took a crash course on ENGL 421 and now teaches it in just the way she knows how. She is not flexible at all when it comes to her rubric and almost comes off as arrogant with the way she likes a project to be done. However, she is very quick with her feedback and her regrades are very generous points wise.

His lectures had a lot of information that sometimes could be overwhelming, but his explanations after reading each point helped a lot. His quizzes were about a single aircraft system, instead of multiple all at once. This made this easier to study.

This class was online, so I didn't really meet this professor in person. But his lecture slides were filled with a lot of information and easy to understand. Whenever I had a question, I knew I could email him and get a response within a day. I hope to have him again.

This professor was great! He helped me understand the aircraft systems better than other professors. We had an amazing opportunity to go down to the Flight Simulators and learn to start an engine and taxi. That's where he really excelled at teaching. I learned that's one of the things he did during his time in the airlines. Great Professor!

He did a pretty good job teaching this class. I heard this was his first semester teaching 307 and based on that, he did good. His lectures were filled with a lot of information, but were easy to understand. He had a lot of quizzes, but they were over an individual system, not multiple systems. That made it easier. I would take this class again.

The course felt disorganized, with lectures lacking clear progression and connections between topics, making it hard to build a strong foundation. Slides were dense and often just read aloud. Exams emphasized unclear details, and limited feedback made it difficult to improve.

He didn't know the material he was teaching and only ever lectured with PowerPoints from other teachers. When asked a question he almost never knew the answer. Also would take up class time with stories and barely go over the stuff on the tests.

He has a very distinct personality, in the school i feel as though you either love him or you hate him. I got in many arguments with him and almost always left my lab feeling more drained, frustrated, and upset then when i arrived. I know a lot of the teachers feel the same way about him and know how students feel.

Gouwens hates Siri. Be prepared to not be allowed to use your phone because Gouwens has a hatred for technology. Also be prepared to bring up ChatGPT in the presence of Gouwens, as he hates creativity of such tools. Gouwens does love sitting around in lab, so be prepared to get no knowledge from him. Wouldn't recommend if you want to learn material

Professor Gouwens wishes he has the presence similar to David Goggins. Unfortunately he will only be David Gouwens as he lacks the experience and ability to teach while imposing his "standard" of expecting everyone to know everything for a freshman course. Expect a lot of bite with nothing to back it up.
Terrible professor. Homework is repetitive and pointless. Boring in lecture. Very abusive communication style. Would not recommend.

This professor ran the lab section of the class and didn't know the material at all. The TAs ran the lab while he just sat there in the front. To make it worse, the TAs had differing instructions that made the class confusing. I had TAs ruining my gain and breaking machines, worksheets badly explained, and forced to use ancient equipment. Terrible!

Teaching is not the word to describe the work Professor Gouwens does. Instead, he sits around and watches you fail, then yells at you for not knowing material. Unfortunately, will not each the material, so it becomes a never-ending loop of failure and yelling. Fortunately got a slightly better grade after talking with the head of the program.

One of the most boring classes to take but he makes it fun with stories. Not too terrible class if you understand the content by studying. Don't think too hard over the quiz and exam questions as he formats them to start making you familiar with FAA written questions. Would take this class again for the professor and how to start taking exams.

Took this class semester and actually really enjoyed it. Honestly the content is really boring, but Gouwens made the class enjoyable and I still remember a lot from it. There aren't many homework assignments and your grade depends on the effort you put in. Pay attention, take notes, and study a bit and you will do well. Tells entertaining stories.

AMAZING professor. Tough on the outside, warm and squishy on the inside—basically a sarcasm-wrapped teddy bear. LOVES ducks as he has a jeep. Legend has it he once calmly graded 50 terrible lab procedures with a smile on his face and a duck video playing in the background.  Most well tempered person you will EVER meet. MAKES LAB BEARABLE.

Genuinely one of my favorite professors I have had. His classes are definitely a little challenging, but they actually prepare you for future classes and a career after college. He doesn't assign busy work without a purpose or make you do homework just to do it. A good professor who makes hard topics bearable and actually cares about his students.

Got Diarrhea in lecture at one point and cancelled class the rest of the week, passed with flying colors because Gouwens didn't realize I kept putting laxatives in his coffee so he kept cancelling class. Besides that every test there were like 3 questions that never made sense, he could be suffering from dementia but the jury is out on that one. -Q

The professor was at first a terrible instructor. Class attendance was mandatory and very test-heavy. At one point, I was on the border of failing the course. But then the professor pulled me aside and helped me figure out what grades I needed to pass. After that point, he and I always went over a topic so I could understand it better.

Professor Gouwens is a character of unparalleled variety, from stealing one of my pets, to doing backflips across the lecture hall to try and wake up a student. There was one time that he yelled so loud at a sleeping student that he set off the fire alarms in the lecture hall and we had to evacuate. Then there was another time he waterboarded me.

Professor Gouwens was a great professor. He was a tough grader on the homework assignments, but explained why I got the points off when I asked. He was willing to sit down with me and help with test prep. I would love to have him again as my professor
Taught the material very clearly and had a sense of humor. Not a difficult grader. Not super high workload.

I think he was a pretty god prof, he made a really boring and annoying topic more bearable by making jokes and having fun with it, he posts his slides and makes the class very easy to pass if you just do the work and follow the instructions. chill guy, can be a little annoying but i would take him again.

This was a lecture class so of course he did some lecturing but really chill. He brought his dog into class and if we made a paper airplane that made it across the lecture hall, we did not have to take the final exam. Wish i could have him for every class.

Gouwens has showed some improvement from comments left by students from previous semesters. His lectures can be a cluttered mess at times and confusing to follow. On top of that, he may also go on long tangents about his own personal life. The tests are simple if you look over the slides beforehand.

Not great. Makes attendance mandatory, then spends half the class either telling his same 4 life stories about when he "worked in the industry" every class, or talks about sports. He will also make fun of students for longer than the joke is funny, making the whole room uncomfortable. His exams are multiple choice, but the wording can throw you.

It was a nice change of pace to take a purdue math course that wasn't torture. She recognizes what it's like to be a student and doesn't give out an insane amount of homework or make exams terribly difficult. Would definitely take another course with her.

Amazing professor for linear algebra who genuinely cares about her students. Weights homework heavy and creates practice problem sets that are very similar to exam questions. Only minor critique is that she writes fast during lectures and it can be tough to keep up with (5-6 pages of notes per class). Responds to emails fast too. Take if possible!

Great professor; very knowledgeable with great lectures, very approachable outside of class with fast email response times and lots of office hours throughout the week. She makes great practice problem study guides with worked out solutions before each exam, curated to what actual test questions might look like.
big fan; always replied quickly to emails and practice tests were always very useful for actual tests

Super helpful, great at explaining concepts, genuinely wants you to succeed. Holds insane amount of office hours and will hold an office hour pretty much the entire day of an exam right up until the hour before it. Posts lecture recordings online.

Dr. Polak is one of the best professors I've ever had, and although attendance isn't mandatory, her lectures make it worth going. She prepares study test material that are very similar to the exam. For those in the future: REVIEW HER EXAM REVIEW LECTURES! She is patient and very understanding in explaining any confusing concepts in office hours. :)

Raechel is a wonderful professor who is far and away the best lecturer for MA266. Lectures focus heavily on the conceptual explanation of the topic at hand and, while entirely handwritten still give you all the knowledge you need to do the homework's. She is also very helpful over email, a fast grader, and willing to fight with the worse professors
So kind and genuinely cares about her students success.
Great lectures and creates very useful study guides for exams. Couldn't recommend more.

The math department is largely intense, overwhelming, and inconsistent. However, Prof Polak is the eye of the hurricane. I hope and pray this prof gets the recognition she deserves and knows how much her students appreciate her dedication to every students success. This prof inspires me. If you can, refresh Unitime for this prof!

Polak is the BEST math prof that this university has. I have never had a prof so caring about students. Answers questions thoroughly, cares abt students mental health, gracious to every student. Prof Polak has office hours throughout the week and will kindly explain 1+1=2 if the student needs it. All profs must attend her class and learn from her.

Honestly, an excellent prof. She seems to really care about the success of the students in her class. If you have any issues, she is also very reasonable. Her exams are also some of the fairest exams that I have taken in a Purdue math class.

Arguably ONE of the best professors I have had at Purdue thus far. Her lectures are amazing and her homework, quizzes, and exam aligns with what she covers in lecture. She is very respected and genuienly cares about her students. I went to her office hours because I didn't understand something and she took her time to explain it.

One of the the absolute best professors. Practice quizzes and exams are harder than the actual ones to prep you & are designed to test understanding of concepts NOT your algebra. If you show you know the process, you will get most of the points (saved me a lot of pts). Lectures have motivation, derivations, and lots of examples. Highly recommend!

Amazing professor. She is THE professor to get for any Purdue math class that she teaches. Super easy to understand lectures, and gives fantastic practice quizzes/exams that help deepen learning and perform better on the real quizzes/exams! Fantastic professor, one of my favorites at Purdue. 
TAKE HER CLASS IF YOU CAN GET INTO IT!!!!

Thorough lectures with lots of example homework problems sprinkled in which makes the homework easier to do. Genuinely seems to care about her students and not making the course harder than it needs to be. Use the practice quizzes and exams she sends cause they're actually worthwhile.

Genuinely one of the first professors to truly care about her students and understand that they are human. I got really sick and missed a week of classes and she was so kind and accommodating. She went out of her way without me having to ask for anything. Once I did the wrong homework by accident and she extended it. Amazing person, great lectures.

MA 266 is not an easy course, but Professor Polak makes her lectures simple to follow and understand. She will answer any questions you have, and spends the perfect amount of time complementing theory with examples. She is also very understanding of circumstances and willing to negotiate homework due dates. Lecture notes and recordings are posted.

I have Professor Polak for MA262, and she is by far one of the best professors I have had. She is beyond caring and always responds to emails very quickly. She understands situations out of the student's control and is always accommodating. Her lectures are easy to follow since she breaks down the content quite well. She is also just so sweet.
Very reasonable

Best professor I had last semester. She teaches very clearly and provides more than enough resources and tips to succeed. My major has nothing to do with calc, but her class made me consider being a mathematician. I feel bad for anyone who didn't have her.
Excellent professor and does a good job explaining pretty difficult material. Lots of resources are available to prepare for exams/quizzes.

Explains the topics well and provides many examples. Has a quiz almost every week and 4 exams throughout the semester. She provides resources to help prepare for exams, and is willing to meet in person. Also provides opportunities to get bonus points. Attendance isn't mandatory and she posts all lecture videos, guides, and notes on Brightspace.

Pretty solid professor. Calc is going to be inherently hard for most non engineering majors but she does a solid job at making it less hard. Only critique is she sometimes goes a little fast through her lectures but that may just be me. Class is hard but that has mostly nothing to do with her teaching abilities and more to do with the material.
She makes me sad that I won't be taking more classes that she teaches (this was my only required math class). She is amazing. By far the most caring professor I've ever had.

One of the most uncaring professors I've had so far at Purdue. There is SO much busywork that really doesn't aid your learning at all, it's more reasons for her to find something small and particular to deduct points from. Avoid.

If you can please DON'T take this class! This class requires tons of work, random small points are taken off, and the professor sends an insane amount of announcements. Aside from one useful project, the majority of the assignments were busywork and seemed inconsiderate of students' overall workload.

Prof Bailey is not as bad as these reviews say, but still not easy. She was nice to both me and my groups in emails. The worst part of the class is that she is pretty harsh grading the big group projects, so be sure to do well on the other things to try and offset that. Read the instructions well because she WILL deduct for the smallest things.

Honestly it was nowhere near as bad as the other reviews have said. As long as you write something competent that hits the word count you'll get a perfect on individual assignments. She also grades within 24 hours and is one of the only professors I've had that gives personalized feedback for every single assignment.

I was scared of taking this class with her because of the ratings but she was awesome. My group had a problem with the company we were supposed to work with and she understood everything. I emailed her often to ask her some doubts I had and she responded quickly. Just try to send emails to her in a professional way and you will be fine.
Hands down the worst instructor I have had in 3 years at Purdue. Blunt, lacks compassion, and just plain mean. Bad, bad, bad.

She grades quickly & rubrics are (mostly) clear, but her disengagement is apparent. She requires work with outside companies for a project and you are penalized if unable to secure a partnership with a local business/organization. Grades shouldn't be dependent on anyone besides the students. My A+ was earned in spite of, not because of her.

Bailey uploads a document weekly that contains the week's assignments, but this , the individual project rubrics, and the syllabus often contradict each other. She's a tough grader & knocks points off if emails are "unprofessional". One nice thing I will say is she grades quickly. I recommend taking this class with literally any other professor.

Would assign work with an insanely quick turnaround. She never handed out rubrics and the actual courage page was very difficult to follow. I absolutely hated this class and any time I emailed her for clarification she was extremely rude. I would tell everyone who is scheduled in her class to try to get into another section.
I was scared to take this class because of these ratings, but it really wasn't that bad. Her rubrics are extremely clear. Make sure you finish things before the deadlines.
Horrible class would not recommend just take any other class.

Professor Bailey is pretty strict with her grading, but she posts the rubrics before the assignments are due. It's not like you don't know what you need to do to get the grade you want. The group projects are kind of annoying especially if you pick a bad group but they're not terrible. Professor Bailey seems like a reasonable person.

THE S***isht professor in the entire university. She thinks she is so righteous and doesn't allow for makeup that is seconds late. Just take a other similar class. English class is not hard but she will do it to make you suffer.

Rachel does not exhibit an ounce of altruism throughout any of her interactions with students. Her demeanor towards the course, expectations, and people create a discouraging, non-engaging learning environment. Her assignments are disorganized and It does not seem she is passionate about teaching.

There is a fast turnaround with projects so you have to keep up with the content, but the hard part is that she grades very harshly and takes off numerous points for small errors that another ENGL 421 professor wouldn't blink an eye at.  Furthermore, her emails are rude and she doesn't maintain the etiquette that she expects in an email to her.

Incredibly inconsiderate, and often times rude. She grades extremely hard and is never accommodating, not to mention she is extremely difficult to get ahold of. I really wish I had looked into her before I decided to take this class, as it was an elective. I thought I would be interested in her class, but she has completely ruined it for me.
This is the worst class I've ever taken at Purdue. She's very harsh at grading and also rude in her emails. Does not give any partial credit even for the simplest mistakes. Do not take it!
Do not take again. So confusing and almost impossible to reach via email. Uses a Gmail account so the University can't track what she's saying to students. DO NOT TAKE.

Poor course organization and assignment descriptions. Extremely rude and very poor response time on emails. Uses her gmail account so the University can't monitor her ill-mannered responses or lack-there-of. Relatively easy course made difficult by how much she sucks.

Very poor professor. Rude in emails. Not very helpful. Emailed all day trying to get a very small question answered and she never gave me an answer and was extremely rude in doing so. Avoid at all costs. Very easy class. Very poor professor.

Never take this instructor's course. Not only is she a harsh grader, but she is incredibly rude and will fail you if you simply don't use proper email etiquette. She will tell you your papers are written beautifully and then still give you a B. If you have to take 420, avoid her at all costs.

Don't take this lecturer  she is a tough grader and gives so many assignment in a week. Her replies in email was so rude and rush. Be aware of the group project. She is so unorganized in every details regarding the projects and assignments.
She made the requirement to get an A a 93.5% instead of a 92.5%.

she is an unorganized teacher. She creates online lecture videos that never truly line up with her lecture slides that she provides us for notes. there is either slides in the lectures slides that never get discussed in the video or vice versus. If you email her she will not respond, so if you have a question you have to talk to her in person.

If you are trying to take this class online or in person, just prepare to have no free time. There are homework assignments, Labs, Data Checkpoint projects, Quizzes, and 2 Exams. Oftentimes, it felt as if Christina Munson was against her online students and not encouraging them. She is constantly fear-mongering to her students as well.

DONT take the flipped class. Her notes are very unorganized and dont teach you anything. The lectures are fine but theyre just review questions for attendence and sometimes she doesnt do them so you just sitting there and listen to her repeat herself without getting credit for being there. She cancels class a lot but she forgets to tell you a lot.

Munson treats college students as though they are middle schoolers. You'd be lucky to actually learn something from lecture--labs are the only ok resource. The exams are not given any partial credit, so get ready to balk at that score. There's a huge group project that you get assigned early on, be careful of your group. DON'T DO THE FLIP SECTION.

I would not recommend this class to anyone. I cried so much over this semester due to this specific class and professor. The professor seems to hate her students and want them to fail. I can't tell whats worse, her or the website. Both are unbearable.

She is as useless as they come. Online lectures are repetitive and hard to decipher useful information. Group assignments are lengthy and tiring. Labs are the best way to learn information. Lots of extra credit on homework which is very useful. Exams do not match homework, labs, data project assignments, or in-class quizzes. SPSS can burn.
Dr. Munson does not care about her students. I highly do not recommend taking her class if you want to build a relationship with your professor. Taking her class is my biggest regret.

She's iterated on the course every semester since taking it over, and I found this semester's flipped section to be super palatable. Lectures are short but informative, and much of the work can be completed on your own time without a ton of effort. In lab: read the manual! An A is readily achievable +/- a decent group for the project.

I have mixed thoughts about Munson and STAT 301. I really thought the class was fairly organized, but hated this "made in China" SPSS that they made us use. It often ran into bugs and problems. I also didn't like the peer reviewed parts of the class. Munson from emailing her appeared to be passive aggressive and lacked understanding of situations

For those taking the flipped section of STAT 301, make sure to attend class. While attendance is not mandatory, it will likely make a difference on what letter grade you receive. Dr. Munson provides excellent example problems, provides chances to ask questions, and will often tell you what will be on the exam.

It was a struggle having to take her flipped class. Her video lectures are severely unorganized (redundant material, poor slideshows, low resolution,). And god her TAs were a trainwreck. They lecture n whispering voice, they mostly didn't have a clue of what they were doing, and it took them decades to grade (classes ended and still missing grades)

If you're not a statistics major, I will not recommend it. DO NOT TAKE THE FLIPPED CLASS. The class is very 'lab' heavy with one lab per week. You use SPSS Statistics program that is hard to navigate and the concept they use are quite complicated and hard to understand. Not much help from the instructor or the TA.

This professor reads off their slides and gives tons of examples on how to do problems but if you have questions or happen to miss a step that she wrote down, good luck asking questions. She is extremely condescending and rude when you bring issues to her attention. Its almost like she only wants 20% of the class to pass the class.

Dr Munson almost wants you to do bad. She is never clear in her instructions. She is condescending & rude when you email to ask questions for help. Let me mention when she recommended I get help from a TA and I did, the TA told me the work was good and I still somehow did poorly. She also blamed me for that not her TAs.

I personally really liked Dr Munson, she is good professor and explains every concept very well. The only downside of this class is the labs which can be time consuming. There is extra credit for the homeworks if you complete them 24 hrs before they're due. The exams are very similar to the practice exams she provides as study material.

She's a great professor. The class is extremely easy if you do the basic things such as lab and homework. I never studied and got high A's on every exam because I just paid attention in class. She does a lot of practice problems which is nice. She doesn't post her filled lectures though, so make sure to attend class.

This class is 100% doable if you put in the time and effort. Labs and homework can be a drag because of SPSS, but they're never too difficult. Exams are easy if you really pay attention to the past/previous exams, and make a good reference sheet. This class is also extremely useful to have if you want to pursue a research lab at Purdue.
Lack of compromise

This class is very manageable. She does go a little fast, but she posts videos of the lectures later and you can rewatch the lecture if you had trouble keeping up in class. The practice exams she provides are exactly like the real exams, so make sure you do them, they really help!

Tobias is a great guy. Lectures are amazing and also funny. Go to class and participate, your grade depends on it, but the TA in charge of attendance doesn't seem to remember that. Overall, the class isn't hard; just a lot of work. Most things are completion based. Exams are tough but not a big part of your grade.

i got above a 93 on every single test for this class, but still got a B+ in the class bc my participation was a 78. GO TO CLASS, especially for his class. the thing is even if you try to talk in his class he just shuts you down and lowk makes you feel bad for saying anything in the first place. content might seem like common sense, its not.

Professor Dennerlein required a lot of participation points and was pretty harsh when grading the participation points. His Brightspace is extremely unorganized, and it is difficult to find instructions, rubrics, and assignments. His class requires a lot of reading and homework. The group project is very involved and was poorly explained.

Tobias is the goat. He does require participation in class but he is great at leading discussions and making class interesting. The group project is a bit of an annoyance but he is pretty organized in letting you know what is due when. He is super nice and passionate about the material. Overall, a good guy.

Did not know how to use Brightspace, so I did not know what my grade was until the last day. Lectures are useless for exams as they are based on the textbook chapters, which he does not talk about in class whatsoever. Avoid this professor at all costs. However, if you do have him, do all of the extra credit assignments and you'll be fine.

The course was extremely unorganized. Be prepared for your grade in the course to not be available the entire time. I had to ask multiple times what my grade was and was given very arbitrary answers. There are 3 exams that cover the textbook material. However very little of the textbook is discussed in class. The course is hard to navigate.

Terrible communication, never know what is due or what is supposed to be on it. Learned less in this class than any class I have ever taken. Professor mandates you take his surveys to get a good grade. Also mandates you get other people to take his survey (which takes over an hour) to get a good grade. Avoid this guy at all costs.

Extra credit carries for the class. The first exam was a bit surprising, but once you realize that OBHR exams are mainly based on the textbooks, it's easier to do well. Participation points were annoying and I got an F on that. He also offered tons of extra credit, which saved me, so take advantage of all the extra points you can get.

I had a lot of fun in Tobias' class! Very discussion-based class, so I feel like I got to know everyone in the class very well. On the first day of class, he encouraged us not to take notes in class, and to try and learn from listening to discussions instead. Exams & projects can be a lot of work however, but participate in class and you'll be ok.

He's pretty good if you reach out to him and talk with him. The course was difficult though and the other ratings here are fair in terms of his exams but my experience with him was that he was a pretty good guy and fair to everyone.

A very confusing class. I was super excited to take his class, but I soon wanted to drop out. He doesn't give clear constructions, but claims that he's very organized because he's "German." We would answer surveys and tell him that he was not organized, and he wouldn't change a thing, even though he claimed that he "took them seriously." 0/10.

Highly do not recommend him. You are expected to talk 3 times during the hour however that is not feasible as there are 50 people per class. Tests are not based off class so make sure you heavily read and understand the chapters to do well on exams. He is a very harsh grader. He also doesn't communication his expectations until after the first exam

Avoid at all costs. Does not care at all. For the exams, study every bit of information in the chapters. I always made quizlets that ranged from 200-400 terms and got low B's. You basically need to talk 3 times each class in a 90-person lecture hall to get full participation. He does not convey his expectations so your grade will suffer.

What he covers in class will not be on the exam. When he asked us for feedback on the first exam (60% average) he said "If you want me to cover what's on the exam go to community college." He also switches seats every day for "networking". You also have to do surveys (for his research) that take 45 minutes. AVOID THIS PROF LIKE THE PLAGUE.

Dude doesn't teach anything on the exams. Nothing you learn about in class matters. Not only do you have to participate, but you get graded on how good your question is. Average of the first exam was 60%, and he said it was better than he wanted it. Also doesn't release your grades but your exams so you have no clue how you're doing in the class.
Does not cover exam material in lectures. Grades participation extremely unfairly (class average was 26% and makes up 20% of your total grade).

This guy was great if you are a chemistry major. I am not. I read these reviews and thought omg this sounds so good i love chemistry and this guy likes to go on rambly tangents about his interests, i'll learn so much! then my other classes got bad and this was an elective. don't skip lecture but if this is your hardest class you'll be fine.
Very nice professor, a little quirky here and there. Lectures are interesting. Test heavy class!
Quirky dude.

He's a very nice person! He is always willing to meet to talk about assignments, but get ready for the reading and participation in class. He also talks a lot in class and expects you to answer his questions. He creates interesting discussions, though!
Class was fun, easy, the group assignments were engaging, very understanding if you have problems. If you are taking SCLA102 just take ewing hes the goat

Ewing is a good prof. He does require a lot of reading, however the books vary in content, so if you don't like one you will have another you may like more. He requires us to do group projects where we receive individual and group marks. Also will talk a lot at times, but he also expects you to contribute as well (worth 15-20% I forget exactly).
