show up and take notes or you will fail. the reviews saying he was their personal hell are slightly dramatic as he makes it easy to pass the class. Only 100/200 points are needed for a C, if you cannot get that then maybe you should pick a different academic route
he was lovely
This professor does not care about his students and genuinely tries to trick them. I've never had a class question my self-worth, damage my mental health, and question whether I should be at Purdue.
Impossible to succeed. Super harsh grader, grades fully opinion based, exams are curved down a bunch.
avoid if you value sanity

COM 204 is a challenging course, but really, if you go to class, follow the rubric and his grading scale closely, and consult Dr. Boyd and his TA's for help, you will succeed. Dr. Boyd is honest in his grading and his words, and wants his students to grow; however, a level of commitment and consistency is expected for this class.
Incredibly tough grader. He uses a 5-point scale for grading and if you meet all requirements on the rubric, you get a 3. Good luck, it's rough.

While the class was okay I found myself losing some sanity over it. Every assignment is graded out of five. While the rubric is clear, it is very objective and opinion based. Major papers are graded on the five-point scale and then multiplied by 6 to get your grade out of 30. Same for the exams. DO NOT use Grammarly; he considers it AI.
I did NOT like his class, it made me question my sanity, and I felt stupid. GO TO CLASS he doesn't put the slides online
Really bad, my mental health went down the toilet during my time in his class. Super nitpicky
grading scale sucks and too hard of a grader made me lk cry every day
Class is rlly hard and grading is REALLY hard
Very friendly professor, you can tell he respects his students. He is a very tough grader though. It is hard to get an A on the speeches but he gives awesome feedback.

This class was difficult, but if you put in genuine effort you can get a good grade! Go to office hours on both major papers! I got a poor grade on the first paper, but still managed to get an A in the class, so don't give up. Don't ever skip lectures, no excuses. He does not post anything online! This being said, his class will teach you a lot.

This class is ridiculously nitpicky. I understand the need to teach students the importance of clarity when it comes to writing, but the way each assignment is graded makes it nearly impossible to succeed. Every assignment is graded out of 5 and, oftentimes, the grading is subjective under the guise of an objective, "clear rubric".

I recommend taking thorough notes during lectures--though attendance isn't mandatory you won't pass if you don't go. There are two major papers, and I suggest starting early to have your TA review them before the final submission. The class is interesting, but the grading is tough, and you must put in the effort to earn a good grade.

Prof Boyd does a great job of explaining material in lectures and is very clear about expectations. Help was made readily available for major papers, you were able to submit a draft for feedback, and exam review sessions were offered. If you attend lectures, take half-decent notes, and follow instructions you should have no issues getting a B
GO TO LECTURE, go to office hours when doing papers. exams online.

While his class made me question my intelligence and wonder if he even wants his students to succeed in his class, he does give somewhat entertaining lectures. I would recommend letting him give you feedback on papers, and ALWAYS going to lecture as he doesn't upload his slideshows. Every assignment is graded on a 5 pt scale, so put in effort!

Oh boy, this class is HARD. You absolutely have to show up to every lecture. He will throw in pop quizzes or in class activities. He is understanding, but he will not listen to excuses. Read every word on the syllabus and assignments. Talk to him about every assignment and go to office hours. You will succeed in this class, but start strong!

Dr Boyd was a great professor, however I really disliked the course, as someone in fye, it was difficult for me to write at the high level he expected but through the course of the semester I learned a lot. Attending lectures is the most important part of this class because he does not record or post notes.

It was very clear that he wanted you to succeed. At the start of the class, there are a ton of reading assignments paired with a quiz and a lot of them are loooong, so make sure to start them as soon as possible and keep up to date with them. In class, you have to participate in small impromptu speaking prompts but they're very low pressure.
literally awful grading system. hated this class made me want to cry every day.

Attendance isn't required but if you miss a class, you WILL be behind. This class was challenging and demanding. If you are a pre-comm major and this is one of your only comm classes, you will likely have more time to work on the assignments. If you're taking it in another major with heavy work, it's hard to manage. Be prepared!

Boyd and his TAs have very high expectations. You will be expected to do a lot of work, and really challenge your brain. At the same time, he is an amazing guy, very smart, engaging, tells funny stories, and is always willing to help. Dont skip class, and PLEASE go to office hours. It is a necessity.
Easily one of the best professors and lecturers I've ever had. Communicates well, knows his content, and is very caring towards his students.
Great professor with a lot of life and personality. I would recommend him and would definitely have him again.

Best professor I ever had. He made CS381 pretty easy. He explains things intuitively, so if you hate formal proofs, take his class. Homeworks and exams were easy, and he gave great advice and prepared me for any technical interview.

Focuses a lot on theory and proofs, rather than ensuring students understand material, even after repeated requests for more examples. Adds an undue amount of convolution and ambiguity for the sake of 'academic integrity'. Learned much more from external sources than from class materials.

Prof. Atallah cares a lot about his students and makes a constant effort to ensure he is helping them as much as he can, using class time to focus on particularly confusing concepts. He often goes off on tangents, but even these end up being very funny stories and helpful life advice. Don't miss the chance to take a class with him.
Atallah is one of the best professors at Purdue, if he is teaching a class, it is in your best interest to have him as your professor.
Great Professor!
Awesome professor. Make sure to know the slides inside and out, and you will do well. Don't take notes, just listen and read the slides.

*EXCELLENT* professor. great teller of stories and REALLY-REALLY knows his stuff. however, his lecture style is mostly verbal. not very much written on the overhead. you have to be very astute at note-taking to take down what he says
You can tell he cares a lot about teaching this class.
Very good at explaining, and easy to understand.  Keeps lectures interesting, though has a slight tendency to get side-tracked.
Probably the best professor I've ever had. He does an excellent job of explaining material in a manner that is easy to understand. He keeps lectures interesting, enjoyable, and educational.
LOL
Great instructor!

He was the best prof I've ever had.  Made himself available to students and would explain subjects in multiple ways.  I'm more interested in algorithms than i ever was before.  He gets the teaching awards for a reason!  Generally, the students who didn't like him just didn't seem to want to put the effort into a hard class. Its college not HS!

Despite what other students have said about this course, the professor obviously cares about the course material and is ready to teach it to those who want to learn it. He uses a very hands-on approach, and expects participation, which makes sense in a class like Algorithms. As a non-major, the course gave me a new respect for Computer Science.

As already mentioned, constantly gets sidetracked and tends to talk about papers he's read and written, problems he's worked on, etc. way too much.  Relatively amusing guy and fairly friendly, a poor instructor.  Biggest complaint: he doesn't prepare notes in advance and really makes those who attend class feel like they've wasted their time.

Atallah's exams and homeworks were extremely difficult, but he was extremely helpful outside of class. He was willing to re-explain topics in different ways, and even scheduled additional office hours for anyone who asked. That alone displayed an interest in teaching and a concern that students learn the material that most professors cannot match

One of the best professor I have ever known. I come to love Computer Science because of him. He thinks outside the box. He makes hard problem become easy, by the way he explains, and somehow I see a light in my mind, somehow after he explains, I can totally understand. Plus, my interest in solving problem increases, because of him.

Gets side tracked very easily and expects students to go beyond anything learned in lecture to complete the homeworks.  He may be a very intelligent man but he needs to work on the fact that he's teaching undergrads without any phd's! try to avoid this guy.
He get's angry when you ask him questions that are "trivial" to him but obviously not to us since you're asking him. His exams are balls hard.

Makes everything very difficult to understand.  Thinks everything is 'trivial.'  To him, it is - he wrote a book.  He teaches you a bag of tricks instead of how to solve and learn algorithms.  Does not know how to explain.  Homeworks are extremely hard and graded unfairly.  Tries to make you come up with a new algorithm based on what you learn.
Lectures are interesting but in no way prepare you for homeworks and exams.

makes the material interesting, and presents it in a very clear manner.  He approaches algorithms in a very interesting way.  Instead of just explaining the algorithm and how it works, he goes beyond and attempts to explain the thought process that lead to development of it.  One of the best professor's I've had.
He likes to tell stories a lot, and will change the topic of lecture to suit the students needs.  He's just an awesome prof.

Great lecturer and understanding of students. Very different from other CS profs at Purdue and by far my personal favorite. CS381 is a hard course, but Kent makes it more reasonable. Definitely recommend taking it with him.

The entirety of the course content is on a PDF file that is 700+ pages, which is very inconvenient and not user friendly. If you don't understand the lectures, your only other option is to read through the very dense lengthy passages of text on that unfortunate PDF, which is overall pretty unhelpful and wordy. Overall a very difficult course.

Generous curve. I did perfect on homeworks and exactly average on exams and ended with A-. Super friendly and helpful and accessible outside class. Really cares about you. Best prof I've had at Purdue. Very knowledgable and inspiring.

This 3-credit course is actually a 9-credit course in disguise: Probability and Random Processes, Algebraic Graph Theory, and Algorithm Design. Everything is so intertwined that it makes the material much harder to master. 
While the instructor is patient and passionate about teaching, the course pacing is simply indigestible.

Professor Quanrud is a great lecturer, and the class was enjoyable. There were a few hiccups (errors in the textbook, self-grading policy, etc.) but overall the course was well worth it. Would recommend taking a class with him in the future.

He teaches well and breaks down the concepts into smaller chunks, but everything else in this class is poor. He has his own textbook, which is riddled with math errors (good luck studying). Both HWs and Exams use only problems from the textbook. There is no diversity in assessing you. You have to grade your own HW, which the TA evaluates!?!
Great professor with engaging lectures that always build from basic principles to help build intuition and make clear many complex ideas.
By far the best professor (and class) I have had at Purdue. Kent expects students to put in some effort, but it's definitely worth it!
Best professor I had during my time here

Look at the trend in Kent's reviews, undergrads complain bc his class is hard, and grad students unanimously love him. The reality is he teaches a hard topic, but he actually makes it intuitive. Beyond that, he's one of the most abundantly caring and available CS professors I had at Purdue, he's the kind of person who it is impossible to dislike

The professor has written a book which can be used to understand the concepts better. He also paints an intuitive picture which makes the topics easy to understand. The course is homework heavy and would recommend solving as it helps with the exams.
AMAZING GUY AND DIFFICULT COURSE, but you will love it :)
Hardest class I've ever taken but amazing and super kind professor.
Well organized materials and lectures. A little bit heavy, but you'll overcome it, if you try enough.

Amazing lecturer and very passionate about the topics he teaches. Concepts can be hard but he is always happy to help, and course policies (drops, group work, etc) help blunt the difficulty. There are a lot of topics, and this is probably the hardest class I have taken. Even then, I had a lot of fun taking the course and learning the content.
Kent is smart, creative, organized and knowledgeable. It is a delight to be in his classroom.
Kent is just awesome. Even though there are lot of homework, I enjoyed the course very much.

His teaching skill is amazing. The lecture is very clear, does not any prior knowledge and also a lot of fun. I believe even those who hate this subject would get to love it once they attend his class.

Kent makes algorithms so much more interesting than other professors, in part with the colorful slides and commentary, and just by talking about intuition rather than all theory. I came in expecting to scrape by with barely a B but this wound up being my favorite class this semester. Purdue would be better if every professor had Kent's personality
He's amazing. You just need to be patient to realize his awesome.

Couldn't imagine passing this class without Kent. He's encouraging & motivational beyond any other professor here, & he breaks things down into actual intuition rather than memorizing patterns. Exams are always padded with free giveaway questions, & that's before he even curves. 580 is supposed to be 1 of the hardest classes here, but not with Kent

I have a love/hate relationship with the actual class content, bc it's pretty hard... But I love Kent. He frames problems in a motivating light and teaches through intuition rather than regurgitating the rigorous proofs found in the textbook. I honestly wouldn't want to take this class from any other professor after seeing how painless he makes it
I really like Kent as a teacher, his lectures are amazing. His approach to algorithms is very good, bit different from traditional algorithms courses. highly recommended to take this course.

I really like Kent as a teacher, but this class is insanely hard. His policies are really weird and you can take advantage of them a little, but that doesn't change the fact that it's still really hard. His lectures are great, really colorful and well-explained, but the nature of this class requires luck and creativity, so studying more won't help.

381 is overall a difficult class by default. But he gives decent practice materials like practice problems for graphs and dp that where similar to exam problems. Overall gives a fair curve. Be ready to put a decent amount of effort if you want a good grade.
Dr. Jacovides makes quite dense ToK readings intuitive and accessible in lecture. He is very approachable and genuinely enjoys engaging with students during class discussions.

Had to drop the class because his lectures were so painful. He mumbles and rants for an hour and if you don't answer his questions exactly how he wants it, he berates you. Daily assignments must be typed and printed, which is super inconvenient. Don't try and take this class without another PHIL course first or you will be totally lost like me.
I would reccomend meeting with him to go over essays.

Lectures can be very incoherent, but he gives wonderfully organized handouts for each one. He can be extremely uncertain and self-doubting: the signs of a good philosopher, but a frustrating teacher. Good guy, can be very funny. Still, sometimes tends to obscure more than clarify the material.

Nice guy and a solid professor. He does tend to ramble a bit in class, but that's true of all Philosophy professors, and Jacovides does try really hard to encourage student input/discussions. The course itself was interesting and pretty easy. Skim the readings, go to most of the classes, and pick paper topics you're interested in. You'll do fine.

Jacovides is a good lecturer. At times he can be a bit verbose. Be sure to ask a lot of questions. If you don't ask he'll assume you know it. Grade is based on 3 papers. Interesting and worth taking overall.

Prof. Jacovides is one of the most boring professors I've had for any class. I can sit there for the entire lecture, listening to him with my full attention, but at the end of it, I can't remember a single sentence he told us. However, the class is only graded on four assignments, and they are all pretty easy.

I enjoyed this course. Prof Jacovides is a good lecturer and makes it interesting and funny. My TA was also really great. There's a few big assignments/ papers, but he makes the class very manageable. Attendance is mandatory in order to turn in homework, but I did not mind going to class. He also gives out handouts for notes. Recommend the class!

Jacovides cares about his students and is upfront about the difficulty of the material. He provides lecture notes which are crucial for writing the papers. No exams or final, but the three 5 page papers are very heavily weighted. Picking the best topics and having a genuine interest in the material are critical for success. He is a fair grader.

I thought Dr. Jacovides was one of my better philosophy professors. No exams, I thought he was fair grading the papers, and he did ask for some in class discussion. It was a little dry (history of philosophy always is), but he got his point across pretty well and I always got a chuckle or two out of classes.

The only stimuli in this class is the prof pacing in front of the class while raising his arms a lot. He goes off on a dozen tangents each lecture making it hard to figure out what he is trying to say. Doesn't really hold many people's attention, and doesn't foster any class discussion on the topics. Upside: easy course to get through.
Personally, very interesting guy, that's what you what in your philosophy professor
Yes, Jacovides courses are difficult, but if you don't take it personally, you can learn a lot from him. He, more than anyone else, made me the philosophical writer I am today.

Dr. Jacovides has a difficult job: teaching logic & reason to people who have never had their world-views significantly challenged. He is patient & kind even to those angrily explaining that their deity is THE right answer & no other ideas should be discussed. For me, this low grade led to richer understanding & many higher grades going forward.

At first I was not crazy about Dr. Jacovides; he seemed to have that supercilious attitude that others are suggesting. But honestly, he isn't. He isn't outspoken or warming so it seems like this at first. He is a good professor in that he is incredibly helpful AND kind outside of class. He actually does care about his students. He's funny too.

Very knowledgable. Wry sense of humor...but: he mumbles, and he stalks back and forth which is rather distracting. He tends toward arcane phil. terminology and is not at all clear or precise. Class interaction is low becuase he appears to harbor a supercilious attitude toward students. His grading is fair but his expectations are unrealisitc.

He gives out handouts/notes so you dont have to take any. He doesnt give quizzes over reading like other professors. Attendance is not mandatory. The exams are take-home exams. I dont think he could make the class easier. I never bought the assigned reading, and you don't need it. Everything you need to know you can learn online.

Worst professor I've ever had. Very hard to understanding, scatter-brained. Does not respond when you say you need help. Doesn't take other people's perspectives into account and think he's always right. When he is not.

Great professor. not interested in history of ancient philosophy before taking the class, but loved the class. Taking another class with him because he's a great teacher. Interesting and clear about the course material.
Very challenging class, but Jacovides was great.  If you're not into straight lecture/ discussion then philosophy in general might not be for you. He knew his stuff and was very approachable.
He's rambles on about nothing for hours, he means well, a nice guy. Harsh grader, and has unrealistic expectations for entry level philosophy students.
Also had Jacovides for 503 and 432.  He's great at clearly explaining strange and wacky concepts, and his jokes are pretty good.  Choco-Leibniz.

He's getting a bad rap so far.  Not knowing where the old stuff ends? He says when done "That was review" and passes out new notes, so I don't see the difficult. Very eloquent speaker. Material hard but interesting.  If you truly enjoy PHL and are willing to study=good prof. You want a blow-off class?=someone else.  Gladly helps outside of class.

He always does a review of the previous class sesssion - hard to tell where the review ends and new stuff begins.  Reading text is not necessary to do the papers.  Funny at times.  Feels like purgatory.  Stickler for points - would not change grade for 1/2 pt.
No Comments

Dr. Brite makes a class on a niche topic interesting, and gives assignments that encourage students to explore the campus. She assigns weekly readings that she annotates to help with participation in the lecture section. As long as you show up, participate, and make an effort on the assignments, you'll be fine

while i didn't love the topic of this section, i was able to engage with the material enough to find it intriguing. many topics required abstract thinking (no concrete answers). she is very caring and likes to hear different ideas and thoughts during class.
Her teaching is very abstract

This is by far the most uninteresting honors topic I have heard of. If Supermodern is your thing, go for it, but for me it was so boring. If you want to participate it is so easy to bs and act like you are educated in the field. Nice teacher, just the topic is not for me. Easy 100% though if you remotely try.

Dr. Brite was great. Her class was engaging and very organized. She encouraged everyone to share their ideas in class and was always open to anyone's interpretation of the content. She grades based on participation and completion, so just make sure you turn in everything that is required and you'll be fine.

Dr. Brite is an absolutely wonderful person and professor. It is clear that she is so passionate about her work, and she is great at instilling that passion in her students. She encourages everyone to participate and truly listens to what you contribute. As long as you listen and speak up if you have something to say, you will succeed in her class!

Professor Brite is very knowledgeable in her field, but the class was hard to get through because I am not interested in the topic of water problems. She is kind and an easy grader. There were a few readings and a group presentation at the end that you had to work on outside of class. It has to be 50 minutes long, so hopefully you get a good group.

I had a great experience being in Elizabeth Brite's class. You can tell that she is passionate about the subjects that she teaches and is also passionate about Purdue's Honors college and its interdisciplinary pillar. Despite being an engineer, I found the subject that Brite was teaching to be very relevant to my future career.
One of the best professors on campus. Take advantage of her class to discuss topics about the environment, history, and more.

Dr. Brite is a fairly easy grader, and the entirety of the class is weekly group discussions and presentations. Group presentations may take some time out of class, but they aren't difficult to manage, and her class is bright and fun.

Dr. Brite generally is very engaging and insightful about the topics that she teaches. She is very kind and always open to discuss the course materials and debate topics in the course. I did find that some lectures were boring or lacked any meaningful content, but otherwise the material was interesting.

Dr. Brite is very nice and is obviously very passionate about the topic she teaches. I took her HONR19901 foods class. The class was very easy and all work was done in groups. There was some work to be done outside of class, but it was minimal. I found the class to be very boring but an easy A.
Definitely a good combination of a professor that is tons of fun and makes you think. Loves being collaborative, and very passionate about what she discusses in lecture!

I took the "Food" section which was split into, an online lecture and an in-person workday. Lectures were interesting and she has real-world food research. Some participation during lecture and she was quite friendly and encouraging. The homework is mostly readings and team projects. Not too difficult and a fun class!

This class was just working with a group of people to read papers and make video reflections. Super easy, and Dr. Brite gives really good feedback on the topics. There wasn't a lot of work, and she was very engaging. Would definitely recommend her honors sections.

I had Dr. Brite for the "Food" section of HONR 19901, and although there is a fair amount of reading that isn't too interesting, the class is an easy A as long as you show up and complete the easy assignments. Dr. Brite clearly cares about this topic and her students, and brightens up an otherwise pretty dry subject.

Professor Brite made the class so fun and interesting. You can tell she is passionate about her field and its infectious. She even met with me individually so I could ask her questions about her career. Great teacher and loved her class!

Professor Brite is the nicest professor I had this semester by far. She is so passionate about the courses that she teaches and that really shows. There is minimal homework in her class and some people got by without doing any work as it was all group-based. This class is an easy A and requires minimal time.
Great professor for Honors seminar.

Prof. Brite is so amazing. She is really passionate about the topic she teaches and has a lot of experience in the world about these topics. Highly enjoyed the class and the discussion about the topic with people from all different backgrounds. Would highly suggest taking any class from her.

Dr. Brite is a great teacher. Took her food class, which was very insightful and full of fun group projects which didn't have a strict structure. Dr. Brite is also very knowledgeable about her subject and loves to discuss it with her students.

Her water section was mainly based on group papers, which were very simple and could be knocked out in a couple of days. Lectures can get a bit boring but aren't extremely important in terms of grading as long as you show up.

Water seminar: Great Professor who prepares many interesting lectures. My only big problem is that all the recitations were group projects, which was frustrating. Always willing to carry on a conversation about her topic.

Dr. Brite is really nice, engaging, and smart. She really knows her material and provides interesting lectures. She enjoys discussion and class participation. I would encourage taking classes with Dr. Brite.

Her lectures are amazing and very engaging since she hand writes her notes, and it's really easy to grasp the material, the second professor didn't do as good compared to her. Makes you really excited to learn chem.
Handwritten notes rather than reading off slides, and she is generally friendly.

Loved her as my professor. You could tell that she was engaged with the course content by the way she lectured and answered questions. I found it super helpful that she projected hand written notes as she lectured, rather than just teaching from premade slides. Her lecturing pace was perfect.

My number 1 thing with her: she writes out all of her notes. This is insanely helpful. Thermodynamics, kinetics, and equilibrium are all math heavy concepts, and her drawing everything out makes it so much more digestible. Very few profs seem to be putting in the effort to write out all of their notes. Her speaking abilities are strong.

Professor Li is so good. I loved her and I'm hoping she teaches other classes because she made me really excited to come to lecture. She's very personable and easy to watch. Highly recommend to anyone who struggles with coming to lecture because you'll want to come with her lecturing.

Dr. Li is an amazing lecturer overall, during the first half of the semester of CHM 116 I had a perfect 100 percent in the class as she covers almost everything you need to know for homework and exams. I would still supplement her lectures by reading the textbook, as she does not use too many examples and skims over easier topics.

Professor Li is a great professor. She writes everything down as she teaches instead of reading off of slides. This helps you understand exactly what is important. She also does examples by hand which is helpful too. Overall, great professor. If you are taking 116, try to get professor Li.

If you're in CHM 116, go to Dr. Li's lectures and office hours! She delivers simple, and straight to the point lectures without going off on tangents or overcomplicating information. First exam was really rough, but if you go to her office hours, she'll give you study tips/strategies for how to do better in the course. Overall,very nice and caring.

Great at teaching, seems to care about student's success, great handwriting and writes lecture notes. She explains topics really clearly and her examples are helpful when doing the homework. One of my favorite professors so far.
She is a pretty good lecturer. She lectures at a great pace to take notes. Will require a weekly PARTE class which is basically mandatory TA office hours with ~300 people. Not a fan.
Best handwriting in the game. Clear explanations on what is being lectured and does a good job of reading the chat during broadcasted lectures. Glad to have her for CHM116

Dr. Li is fantastic. Best lecturer for CHM116! If you simply go to lecture, right down everything she explains/writes in her lecture notes, you should be prepared for most of the things on the exam. It's hard to get an A, but if you pay attention in lecture you'll definitely have a chance. No extra cred and exams are about 60% of the grade!

Her lectures were excellent. She wrote everything out live (no powerpoints). There were lots of examples. She oversimplified things sometimes, which hurt me on the tests. Still, the best gen chem teacher I had.
Li was a great teacher! She explained things really well and gave mathematical examples as well as demonstrations. She teaches at a really good pace also, and she's super sweet and funny!
Great professor, Helpful in office hours! Wasn't the best student and had to work hard, but I did well considering how I struggled with CHEM 115

Dr. Li was a good lecturer. She explained all the topic in depth. In my opinion, I thought she dumbed chemistry down a little too much, considering how hard the exams were, but I would take a dumbed down version than a too technical one. However, she walked us through a lot of examples, which was helpful.
Professor Li explains things very clearly! She has no accent, writes legibly, and covers all the material from the homework/exams. I really enjoyed havin her, because she made a hard class seem easy.

Teaches calc 3 at UGA Super great guy and pretty good teacher. Old school (used a chalkboard all semester) but his teaching is good nonetheless and he will stop to explain anything that you have questions on. Practice in class was similar to exam and quizzes and homework was harder if anything so it makes good practice. Had a good experience.

Calc 3 teacher at UGA not Purdue. Tests and quizzes are exactly like the practice problems done in class. The lectures are very in depth and easy to follow as well, with practice problems for each section. I would definitely take him if you get the opportunity too. Super nice guy too that cares about students.

Teaches calc 3 at UGA. I think his lectures are awesome and he is great at teaching us to understand the concept and the applications. Attendance not mandatory and he always answers questions super in-depth. Def take him!

Good professor. just hard to understand. the class is reverse taught so you have to teach yourself the material for the whole semester, and you only go to class to take quizzes or to ask questions about the Inclass work. yet attendance is mandatory
Homework was somewhat annoying but the course is easy if you understand the theory of linear algebra.
His lectures were very helpful in helping understand the content, albeit a bit slow. I especially liked his exam reviews, he showed a lot of shortcuts to solve practice problems and the like.

Prof. Zhang is one of the better Math professors I've had at Purdue. I tried not attending his classes for a while and just learning from the textbook (which I did for Calc 1, 2, and 3), but his course content is more from his own lectures than the textbook, so I had to start attending his classes. The lectures are very straightforward
Dr Oguri's 590 taught a lot of application which is sorely missing in the rest of the controls department.

Took Professor Oguri's Applied Control in Astronautics. His notes are well structured and he always makes time for questions. The homeworks are lengthy but they're often very similar to the notes and he offers lots of extra credit. On top of that the grading is very fair. He's a very intelligent guy and well qualified to be teaching the course.

Oguri does not explain things well at all. I think you'll love him if you're already at his level, but goof luck if you struggle to understand some things. There's extra credit, which is nice, but the homeworks are either super short or insanely long, and not much in between. His lectures can definitely be a bit hard to follow most of the time.

Had Professor Oguri for 440, made me appreciate Spacecraft Dynamics a lot more! Homeworks tend to be a bit long, but if you watch his lectures and take notes, you should be fine given that homework is weighted more than the midterm and final. Cares about his students a lot, and gives a lot of extra credit to 440 students only.

Dr.Oguri has excellent lectures that do a great job of breaking down complex topics and making them easy to understand. While the homework is very long, doing them feels rewarding since they are worth 70% of your grade and also include opportunities for extra credit. I would recommend taking 440 with him if you are able.

Nicely and clearly presented complex topics. Class is graded 70% by homework, 15% by midterm, and 15% by final exam. The homeworks are biweekly and quite lengthy, but still fair. They also include ample extra credit (for 440 undergrad students only) that can make so that you don't even need to attend the final exam.
Very caring professor, makes an effort to connect with students and is easily accessible outside of class.

Professor Samuel was the best professor I've had all year.  He was passionate about his students success and made time for students that struggled in topics. We would always have fun in that class and even if a project was really difficult, I was able to understand after going to office hours.

Professor Samuel is a great EPICS team advisor, he is always very clear with course expectations and deadlines. He is excellent with making sure the students are in charge of the direction of the project, but he is also very available for help and questions. He has a lot of expertise and is a great resource for working on the project.

I loved this class and professor! The class has easy online exams and 3 easy assignments. There are in class activities but they are not bad and 5-10mins max. You can tell the professor cares about the topics a lot and is very informed on it. Lectures are well structured and easy to understand. Easily accessible and an easy grader.

Upadhyay is a great professor and very knowledgeable about the subject she teaches. I really enjoyed her class and her assignments. However, the writing assignments, while they do help aid the course objectives, are graded pretty harshly and are very time-consuming. Every week, a writing assignment is due; beware, this class takes a lot of work.

She very clearly cares about the subject and makes the material easy to learn and understand. Two homework assignments were easy and enjoyable. Tests were pretty much straight from the study guide. Would def take again.
Nothing special; very boring lectures and has an attendance grade. Claims readings are required but def not. Pretty easy exams and easy course. Hw harder than exams.

Professor Maienbrook is an amazing instructor! Her class is not overly difficult because she genuinely cares about her students' success. She often offers a curve and provides plenty of extra credit opportunities. Additionally, she gives out study guides and past exams, which are incredibly helpful for test preparation.
Is an excellent professor and is receptive to new ideas and discussions from her students. Would take her again if I have an opportunity to do so.

Maienbrook is really good at breaking down concepts in a way that makes them easy for everyone to understand. You can tell she genuinely cares about what she is teaching. The class does have a fair amount of homework, but it is not overwhelming since its only twice a week, which is less than most classes.
Very knowledgeable and explains concepts clearly.
Overall, the class served as a huge benefit to my education as well as my work ethic. Tara clearly cares about her students' comprehension and opinions, so feel free to speak up.
Interesting lectures, cancels class pretty often, and grading is pretty chill.

Maienbrook is a great in two ways---
1. Structured: makes it clear what you need to do by when, what to expect during exams, and slides are pretty much all you need to know (I would suggest reading textbook to master understanding/if you're struggling).
2. Fun: actually makes accounting sound interesting, lectures are engaging, and so on.
She really explains the topics very well in class and is overall an amazing teacher and gives extra credit on all exams as well.

Professor Maienbrook is AMAZING, she gave so much extra credit bumpimg my 90% final exam to above a 100%. She knew so much about accounting but broke it down into simple terms and was very understanding. Also the exams are multiple choice making them very doable.

Maienbrook is clearly knowledgeable about what she teaches. The class is well-structured, and there are plenty of extra-credit opportunities. She is also very approachable and offers flexible office hours. If you show up to class and actually do the homework, you're bound for success.

Maienbrook was the most competent professor I had this semester. Her course is well-organized, and her exams are straightforward. Watch her highlight videos for a good pre-class gloss. There are only 2 weekly assignments - both of which prepare you adequately for the exams. She genuinely cares. S/O to her TAs this semester (Nathan and Joseph) too!

Best MGMT200 professor. She is incredibly knowledgable in her field and her lectures make that obvious. Conveys the information of the class very clearly, keeps lectures engaging, and is always open to taking questions. Provides opportunities for easy extra points, just show up to class. Exams are incredibly fair, final is not cumulative.

Amazing professor, teaches well and is very knowledgable about the subject. Has worked out a great system for lectures, homework, testing, and participation. The course feels very well organized and the content isn't hard to learn as long as you do the homework.

She is a great professor and her class, though test heavy, is fairly easy. Her lectures are engaging, and lowest homework & review are dropped. There are three tests that make up 60% of the grade in total (15, 20, 25) but the last is the final exam, studying should get you at least a B on each. Extra credit can be obtained and applied to each test.

She is the goat. She explains topics very well and helps 1 on 1 very well too, whether after class or office hours. She is very good at explaining topics and if you are confused she answers questions and breaks it down well.

Amazing professor who really cares about the students. Class is somewhat difficult, especially if you fall behind, so be sure to stay on top of everything. Pay attention in class and make sure you actually know how to do the homework, and you'll do pretty okay.
Maienbrook is an amazing professor. She makes a somewhat difficult course way more approachable. Great professor!!!

Terra was an amazing lecturer and would prepare us well for exams. I left the semester with a very good understanding of basic accounting principles as well as how they relate to more complex topics. She is super approachable and definitely made time for students if they were struggling. I miss having her as a professor!!!

She was overall a great prof! Very approachable. MGMT200 is also quite easy content-wise, but she is very adjustable. A decent amount of credit is given for attendance, so please make sure to show up to class.

Maienbrook is a great accounting professor and seems very knowledgeable in her field. She is super approachable and nice, and her lectures are never too boring. There is tons of extra credit in this class, so just showing up will give you an easy A.
In class lectures are good and get out early! IClicker questions very similar to exam. She's a wonderful person as well. Lots of extra credit opportunities.
Incredible, incredible, incredible , cares about her students so much and is the sweetest person you'll ever meet. Do everything you can to take her class
Very easy class if you read her lecture slides and go to class. She's very good at explaining things as well

she was a pretty mid prof tbh. seemed nice enough, read powerpoint slides, but was helpful to like go over basically exactly what was on the homework. extremely structured class, 2 online homeworks a week, iclicker points for attendance. i didnt rly go to class and it was pretty ok. boring af tho. midterm 2 is randomly hard so be ready.

Dr. Toms is an amazing prof. His lectures are pretty funny, and he explains the content quite well in lectures. I also think his homework and exams for this class are both very fair. Only downside is he doesn't upload notes, but he does reference which topics to look at and accepts a variety of proof styles. Take any class you can with him.

Solid lecturer, but can go too fast at times and the class information is tough. Something this professor could do better is repeating important information. Stories were funny, and made the lectures better.
very engaging, very clear explanations. humorous guy.
Good lectures, notes were easy to take.
One of the greatest professors of all time. His lectures were so fun to be in when he would make jokes. His notes and teaching style was also very clear for the students to understand.
Greatest of all time

Probably the funniest professor at Purdue. Makes every lecture enjoyable and explains every concept extremely well. He was very accessible to talk to after class and always stayed to answer questions. Midterm and final were very fair and extremely comparable to the practice exams. Weekly homeworks were easy and he gives great feedback on them.

Doesn't waste any time. Gives you exactly what you need for exams. He wasn't even my prof and I went to all his lectures anyway outside of the first week. He's also hilarious in a kind of out of pocket way, which is awesome.
Single most goated maths professor on campus. Absolutely hilarious.

Toms is a great professor, one of the best for Calc 3. He's very knowledgeable and keeps lectures interesting with the occasional joke and story. He also teaches well.
Definitely would recommend taking Toms.

His lectures were excellent and he explained the content very clearly in a way that I could understand it well. He is pretty funny too and makes a lot of jokes! I highly recommend going to his lectures, whether he is your professor or not. A lot of people who had different professors came to his lectures anyway.
This + Chenflix

All the Ma261 profs were bad so everyone seeks refuge at Tom's lectures. He is organized and great at going through relevant examples. His lectures are almost always full on any given day. Seriously, this guy wasnt my prof and I went to all his lectures from day 1. He tells fun stories as an intermission and keeps us all engaged. Funny & smart
Professor Toms is the best! He's very funny and explains relatively difficult concepts in a very clear manner.

Prof. Toms is definitely the best Calc 3 professor! I like how he already has the notes ready before the class, so he never runs out of time during the lecture (unlike some other professors' classes I went to). He also makes a lot of jokes, so the classes are rarely boring.
Toms was a great professor who was able to teach effectively while also adding a couple of stories to break up the class. 100% recommend attending lectures.

Professor Toms is the best lecturer for calculus I've ever had. He breaks down concepts in an understandable way and is always very open to questions. He also makes funny jokes in lectures and doesn't take anything too seriously. I strongly recommend him if you're taking MA 261 (even if you don't technically have his lecture time go to him anyway).
Only thing that could make him better would be if he was right handed
Great professor with a great sense of humor.

Extremely good at teaching. His office hours are somewhat infrequent (you can go to other lecturers' office hours though), but his lectures are top-tier. Definitely recommend 100%. This is a difficult course, but he explains concepts very well and even makes jokes sometimes.
Very caring and actually has a personality and sense of humor.
Very enjoyable lecturer, teaches well and is interesting too.
despite the content being kind of complex. it is pretty easy to score in the class due to a lot of it being homeworks and quizzes that are pretty easy. Exams were also not too hard.

MA 261 is fairly easy if you were good at AP calculus or calc I and II. Professor Toms explains very clearly and goes methodically in the syllabus. If you catch up with all his lectures (and maybe chenflix), you're good to go with the concepts. Also he is hilarious.

I wasn't assigned Toms as my lecturer but I heard about how great of a professor he was so I started attending. From my first midterm score of 52%, I went up to 84% on the second midterm score after starting to attend his lectures. He is prepared and clear...notes are clean and comprehensive (prewritten which he explains)...also really funny.

She explains everything very clearly in lecture and walks through problem-solving step by step. Pacing is good in her lectures. The lectures lean towards the boring side, but she explains everything clearly and completely, if you lock in you will get a lot out of it. Caring person as well, approachable and always willing to answer questions.

Schmidt is pretty good. She's pretty boring but she explains things super well and the pace ensures anyone paying even half attention will do well. iClickers are annoying and she does a ton of them, but kind and makes things simple. HWs are a huge pain, but its ok.

Dr. Schmidt is very knowledgeble and well-spoken, the only thing I could say is sometimes its easy to fall asleep in her lectures. I would reccomend going to Miller lectures instead since she uses IClicker questions that are usually on the exams.

I was fighting for my life to stay awake the second I stepped into her lectures. Her lectures are incredibly boring and move very slowly. She simplifies her lectures a little too much sometimes which makes them hard to sit through. I would sit on my phone for half of her lecture and miss out on nothing.

Schmidt is great at teaching. I'm slower learner, and her pace felt perfect. Her slides are helpful, neat, and easy to read. She goes by the textbook. There were just too much stuff to do for this class: pre/post rec quiz, rec worksheet, & 30 questions HW. The tests are really tricky, please be careful. Lock in for the iClicker questions.

Good lecturer, frequently connected conceptual topics to practice problems. She moved a bit slowly and had not much energy which made it difficult to follow at times but was never a significant problem. Sometimes did not include skills that were needed for homework, but did for all the exam material. Make sure to show up for I-Clicker.

the homework was always so time consuming and i dreaded doing it.
the lectures were so boring and consisted of solving exercises and explaining from slides.
the prof referenced the textbook a lot. accent was never a problem.
a few words of wisdom: iclicker, attendance, BOILER EXAMSSSSSSSSS.

Very caring and well-spoken, however, the class is not the best. Everything is geared toward helping you become a chemist, however, ~50% of us do not plan to become chemists just because we are chem majors. Stopped going 2 lectures in and got a 100% in the class. If you want to be a chemist this class will help you tons. If not, skip.

She was fine. Not super good or bad. Easy to understand and the information she gave was usually applicable to the tests. Not a super interesting lecturer and I would not have lost much by simply reading her slides myself, but attendance is mandatory.

I don't understand the low ratings. Schmidt is very "by the book," but so are the exams, so I think that is a good thing. She does a good job at actually teaching the content, going through the learning objectives, and answering questions.  She is caring and makes herself accessible to students. I didn't find her hard to understand at all.

Overall just a completely average professor. Everything is just average. The slides, notes, lectures, everything. Honestly contemplated not going to lectures more than 70% of the time with how uninteresting they were, but attendance is 4% of your grade. She had much better handwriting and stayed on topic compared to Simpson though so that's a plus.

She's respectable and fairly easy to follow. However, she goes way too fast in the notes. Instead of releasing class early, she could give us an extra 30 seconds to write slides down. Clicker questions were way easier than the ones the other prof assigned (prof switched mid semester). So I did not learn as much from the iClicker in comparison.

Her lectures came straight from the textbook and learning objectives, which was nice when studying for exams. I wish she would go over the math just a bit more, but it wasn't bad overall. She is always ready to answer questions out of class and is willing to stay past her office hours times to answer questions if you or someone else had a lot.
she gives a lot of practice problems but that's the most i ever got out of her lectures. read the textbook and self-study

I would say Dr. Schmidt is good and would recommend. Sometimes she can be a bit difficult to understand, but her slides and notes are very clear and the information is pulled right from the textbook. She's funny in class and does plenty of example problems. After class she answers questions and is accessible during office hours.
She was very difficult to understand, and her lectures and notes were not explained thoroughly so you were left confused.
Difficult to understand

Doesn't get too in depth in lectures but does lots of practice problems. She would go through slides quickly and almost always got out of class ten minutes early which was very nice. You will likely have to learn the concepts or why things happen outside of class but working through the problems helps with exam prep.
Dr. Schmidt is very kind and lectures well. I found her easy to understand and helpful.

They claim the quizzes were on what was taught in lecture, but I regularly attended and did not know nearly any answer to the quizzes. She also. was very difficult to understand and did not teach it, but rather do the examples. I do appreciate how she gave us our notes though.

I think she is a pretty decent professor, not outstanding, but not bad in any way. My one gripe is that she skips a lot of slides, but she also works a ton of practice problems which helps. As far as I know I don't think you can really go wrong with CHM 115 professors.

I had Dr. Schmidt for chm 111,she is very helpful if you express to her that you want help and you are struggling as well as that she has very detailed notes in her lecture slides. As the class and how she grades things, the quizzes were worth a lot of points. I didn't like how she expected the students to learn everything by themselves.

This class has an extreme lack of communication and the lectures are near impossible to learn from. They are structured like an SI session? instead of teaching the material, its "here you try!" and then students are given messy "notes" to try and learn from? 
Additionally, enjoy paying hundreds for the multiple websites/programs this course uses.

She's very nice, does a lot of practice problems for hw/exams, makes good exam review lecture sessions, and tends to prefer to cover topics specifically on the exam at the request of students. In terms of lecture quality, they're not great but she cares and tries to gauge the audience to see what topics need to be emphasized. Overall a safe choice.

Dr. Gudrun Schmidt is a nice woman, but her teaching style is very scattered and disorganized. The lecture material wasn't presented clearly, and I had a very hard time staying engaged with lecture. Many times she would just have us do practice problems in lecture, which wasn't very helpful since she hadn't taught us the material yet.

The way this professor lectured was not necessarily helpful. She shows a problem, briefly explains the concepts used, and shows the long solution (either typed out, which is a lot to look at, or sloppily written) for a brief moment, not even allowing students to process any information. Additionally, no lectures are recorded.

The lectures were fine. They weren't the highest quality, but I could still follow. I feel like Professor Rebello gets a bad rap and is much better than most people think. Take advantage of the extra credit opportunities. Lastly, don't be afraid of office hours. I found it helpful and would recommend if you are struggling at all.

This professor is really nice, and if you attend their office hours, they can provide feedback.
At lectures, be prepared that the professor won't finish the lectures. If this is your first physics class. I suggest you start learning physics on your own time, cause it will get very confusing fast. Homework and problem quizzes are super hard.
Terrible lecturer, important content was often given after quizzes. Overall, heavy assistance needed

The professor will teach material in her lecture that was on your midterm a week or two weeks ago. She assigns weekly quizzes every MONDAY on material that you should master by the END OF THE WEEK. You learn more doing homework than you ever will going to the lectures.
Very Boring  and not useful lectures

Professor Rebello is a very nice professor. She moves kind of quickly, and she tends to read off the slide. Other than that, she seems as though she really cares about her students and making sure they succeed. As long as you do your work, you shouldn't have to worry about passing.

She is an okay lecturer, basically just reads the slides. Just go to enough lectures to get all the points for the category. Be sure to get every point possible in all the non-exam categories and do all extra credit. Grind the practice exams before each exam. Average around a high B- in exams and getting an A is not too hard.

I took PHYS 172D so we didn't interact directly. However, the course instructions had me rent a lab kit for 3 months, so I did so, assuming that, you know, they knew what they were talking about, but we continued to use the lab kit for the full 16 weeks and I had to pay a late return fee. Errors in lab intros sometimes made them hard to understand.

Prof. Rebello single handily carried me through PHYS 172. In the beginning, I panicked and actually thought I was going to fail the class. The first couple weeks are super intimidating. I was quick to learn that Rebello is super welcoming and emails back quickly. She is willing to do just about anything for you as long as you ask!

She doesnt have a heavy foreign accent so you can understand what she is saying. She answers questions in lecture as long as they are quick and helpful. She goes in depth with the derivation of equations. I understand physics mechanics so much more than I thought possible. I couldnt have asked for a better professor.

Rebello does a fantastic job of teaching and is really helpful in her office hours. She truly does her best to help students succeed. Even though it is a difficult class, you have the resources available for you to pass if you use them.
Absolutely no time spent teaching, more time spent reading off slides.

Class averages on tests were absolutely horrible (like a 57% on midterm exam 2). There are NO curves, instead they offer a decent amount of extra credit. Content-wise, it is very similar to AP Physics C. Lectures are useless, never watched a single one, and would learn more doing 5 minutes of research on google than an hour in lecture.

I don't think Professor Rebello responded to a single email of mine throughout the entire semester. She only reads off the slides and doesn't explain things clearly at all. The requirements for the class are insane and it is a lot of work. TAKE ADVANTAGE OF EVERY EXTRA CREDIT OPPORTUNITY YOU HAVE. Seriously, extra credit is what saved my grade.

Lectures are a waste of time. She takes several minutes going over an example problem but doesn't even give the answer at the end. There's a lot of concepts in the lecture but its all written in formulas so its hard to understand what the variables actually mean and how it relates to the real world. Homework is 10x harder than lecture problems.

Rate of change of momentum is force, just say F  ma for god's sake. Her online lectures weren't great at all and required a lot of extra research outside them. Could've read the textbook (and I recommend you do) but I was too lazy. Disliked her teaching method of going over deriving equations without explaining what was going on.
How difficult is it to read from a powerpoint? Seeing how little she actually understands about physics has made me confident that I can do whatever I want in life.

As someone who had physics experience prior to taking this class, I can confidently say that Carina is not a good professor, she reads everything off of powerpoints (while still somehow making mistakes), and doesnt clarify the principles very well. If you have her, good luck.

Her lectures aren't meant to be hilarious; it just seems like she's prepared less for the class than the actual students do. Frequent mathematical mistakes made during the lecture, not to mention the number of pauses taken to read what's on the screen properly. The class isn't bad, its just that she ruined it for me.

The class is really dull especially when it's online. That being said it's an easy A if you put in the work. The lectures are a little incoherent and I feel like only half of what's on the lecture is actually covered on the exams. As long as you do the homework and practice exams I think the class is more than fair.

It seems like people liked her in person but she is not the best online teacher. I couldn't understand a thing in her lectures. If you put in the work its not hard to get a good grade in this class through because of the massive percent of your grades that comes from assignments outside your exams and the tons of extra credit.

this was the most annoying and difficult class of FYE. I don't think the concepts were bad, but the lectures were filled with errors and hard to listen to. In addition to that, she took advantage of online learning. There was supposed to be 100 minutes of lectures but it'd usually take 6 hours a week. Everything about this class was bad except EC

She totally took advantage of online learning during the COVID pandemic and assigned more homework than ever. Almost every day you have stuff due, and you have more due on weekends. To top it off, she scheduled an exam the Monday of our Thanksgiving Break Week, when we hadn't had any breaks that year. I live 8 hours away and had to cancel plans.

I don't know how class was with Prof. Rebello before the virus, but online classes with her suck. The only thing she does is read off a PowerPoint, to which she doesn't even do THAT right (reads a problem and solves for the wrong thing), there is also little to no communication. Class is confusing. Demos fail a lot. Bad for international students
Really great teacher who has easy and straightforward exams, he is very patient, caring and considerate of his students. They won't know this is my alt account right?
If time had somehow rewinded back to January when we first had to take biochem with Casey Krusemark, I would kMs.

Each lecture covers 30-40 slides with exams that honestly made want to give up. Dr. Krusemark is a nice guy but one of the worst professors I've had when it comes to teaching relevant material. Studying Krusemark's material doesn't do you any good regardless of the amount of time you put in. The only reason this course was achievable was Dr. Hudmon

You can study for 8 hours a day for two weeks before every exam and still not get above 65%. Every exam felt like it covered the entirety of every biochemistry topic to the very smallest details. In truth, I could have studied more but you will never be able to learn everything because anything could be on that exam. MCMP208 will crush your dreams.

Very difficult class, and very rough lectures. Material is presented to you but not necessarily taught. Slides contain so much information, makes it nearly impossible to learn all of it before each exam, even if you study daily. Class needs to either have more exams, or figure out a better way to teach. Ninja Nerd saved my grade in this class, GL.

There is just so much content that he does not know how to teach. I feel like I learn nothing from his teaching, and it's absurb he is the course coordinator. This class has the possibility to not be so hard, but feels nearly impossible to learn with this professor.

It's wild to think he's the course coordinator when he is the reason students don't pass this class. For a course coordinator, he says a lot of "I think" and "maybe"s like he doesn't know what he's talking about (which is true, he doesn't). He's also the type to start the semester with a speech about how everyone can get As and why he won't curve

All I will say is that his score on here is an understatement, and he has one of the worst RateMyProfessors I've ever seen. Just read through the other reviews, and you'll get a pretty good idea of this man and the WONDERFUL time you'll have in his class

There is way too much information crammed into the slides. The textbook seems more concise compared to Krusemark's own lecture slides which is absurd. The TA help sessions are better to learn from in comparison to the actual lectures. The horrendous exam scores should be a wake up call to change something up. He's not mean, just hard to learn from.

Krusemark's ability to teach is a little difficult; he also fails in answering students' questions properly in lecture. His exams are worse; he picks out some of the most random topics from lecture slides and puts them into his exams. He doesn't even release his exam average and I'm convinced it's because it's so bad. He's not concise.
He can not teach and the exam averages are like a 40%
he is not very concise which makes things difficult to understand, lectures are based on the smallest detail in a slide and there are many many slides you have to memorize
tests are unfairly difficult and does not align well with lectures
you have to memorize 11 lectures, and he'll ask a question about a minor detail in one of them. the average for the exams are so low. he seems confused in lecture

lectures do not align with exam, seems confused all the time in lecture, exam averages are very low, does not provide review sessions or any helpful tips, won't tell you average of the exam, goes on about unnecessary topics, rambles, the curve doesn't account for how low the test grades are

beginning of the semester, my heart rate would go up by how mad i was to be in the same room as him. at the end, i started to hysterically laugh in lecture cause i couldn't believe he was real. this class with krusemark drove me to insanity and it will for you too.

Krusemark makes tests out of so much cursory material that is not connected to each other that I came out of this class feeling like I was trying to prepare for fun facts on tests instead of actually learning key concepts of biochemistry.

Cool person, just not someone that I would want to ever write my tests or lectures again.

This is the kind of class where you celebrate getting anything above 50%, purely because his exams are impossible to prepare for. Krusemark throws so much material at you in a 30+ slideshow presentation and will choose the smallest details to write exam questions over. The only time I knew peace in this class was when Hudmon was the main lecturer.

krusemark pulls questions out of his butt. no matter how much you study, you will not pass any exam of his. this class has made me question my sanity and whether im fit for pharmacy. do not recommend taking a class of his

If you value your sanity, please take this godforsaken class anywhere else. "Unlike most sports games, you can all win this class!" (quoted directly from his first day analogies), and then proceeds to have exam averages in the low fifties. I am a victim of this man's poor ability to teach and will have nightmares about his exams.
It was so bad, I want to give you a zero, but that's not possible, so I give you a one.

if assembling a wall of text and blurting it in the form of a "powerpoint presentation" is considered teaching then yeah Krusemark is an amazing teacher. Add to that the fact the man can't do public speaking about his own topic for his life and his exams ask about the smallest sub bullet point on exactly one PPT slide and it's a recipe for failure
Way too much information on lecture slides, exam questions are very specific and tedious. Very bad.

Dr. Krusemark should not be teaching this class. Copying and pasting textbook material onto lecture slides is not helpful at all. In fact, all of his lecture slides have confusing material, abbreviations, and images. He always question himself about what could be the "right" answer and never conveys what he wants us to learn in lecture.

Dr. Krusemark has to be the worst professor out the pre-pharmacy curriculum. He could never give straightforward answers during exam review sessions and wouldn't know the answers to his own questions. Slideshows are an absolute eye sore and information dump, and somehow everything is important given how hyperspecific and hard his exams are.

He is very nice and you can tell he is very passionate about what he does. Sometimes his lectures can be a little boring but as long as you take notes and do the reading, you will succeed in the class
Very interesting class. I really enjoyed the professor too. A few midterms and quizzes as well as an in person essay at the end. Highly recommend
Absolutely despised this class. Do not take it if you have a choice! By far the worst class I have ver been in, professor sucks and so does the structure of the class

Lectures can be a bit bland, it's just material but he does a good job of explaining content. I'd highly recommend going to lecture, there are no videos posted and it's easier than reading the slides from home. Read the textbook too!! I found that to be a great resource.

Very intimidating at first but super friendly and funny professor. Lectures can get boring but he is always very excited to answer questions and have discussions. The readings are super dry but all the exams and quizzes are 3 open-ended questions straight off the reading guides. So long as you read the slides and books, you'll be fine

He's a pretty enthusiastic Professor who really enjoys talking about the texts covered in SCLA 101. As long as you do the reading and answer the prompt you're guaranteed at least a B. Purely graded on how well you answer the prompt and not on writing skill.
Prof. De La Fuente is an amazing lecturing and is very nice. If you you read the material and pay attention to key themes you will succeed.

I recommend taking his classes to anyone! You can tell he truly cares about his students and teaching. He's always enthusiastic to answer questions and discuss topics and makes lessons fun and interesting. As long as you show up and read the books, you will do fine.
What a great professor! Makes each student feel valued, and does a great job explaining material. Gives a lot of great ideas for the very few writing assignments he gives. Please take this class.
A really good teacher, and the class was very interesting.

He is a wonderful Professor. Very kind and strives to make sure you understand the point he is trying to make. Loved this class and this Professor. If you attend and participate, you should get an A. Would definitely take another of his classes.

There is a lot of reading to do for this class, but do it because class consists of a quiz and discussion! Points are given for participation in discussions, so doing the reading is a must. You can tell he is passionate and knowledgeable of the reading materials! He is very respectable and understanding, and guides class discussions very well.

Take 211 if you have an interest in soccer or what to expand your knowledge of how soccer and world history fit together. The prof knows what he's talking about. If you understand his overarching concepts and ideas of the lectures, you'll do fine on the exams, which are all essays.

He only graded us out of 4 tests, but the tests were based almost 90% of his lecture. Only two questions in total were not covered in lecture.   He covers the readings during the lecture very well. His lectures are long and boring, but you can usually use him as an audio book and focus on something else.

My favorite professor that I've had yet. We do read a lot but other than that only one presentation for homework all semester. Tries to put humor into lectures which keeps them entertaining. He is a very good lecturer and genuinely believes in his students and their abilities as long as you try in the class you'll be fine and learn a lot.
The lectures can be a bit boring, but he really does try to make them interesting. Overall, he's a very lovely guy who is always willing to help his students whenever they need it.

Lectures can be VERY boring. Nice Professor. His accent takes some time to get used to. Take this class if you LOVE history. Don't take it if you don't like learning history and reading a lot. Sleeping in class for one minute  miss a decade of history. Wash your face or drink some caffeine, you'll need it in class.

AMAZING! He is such and open-minded guy who loves his job, and made history lectures as interesting as he could. It is a lecture heavy class with essay exams, so you never knew exactly what to study. This being said, grading was fair as long as there was some thought and intelligence in your writing. Take his classes just so you can meet him!

Loved this instructor. Funny, engaging, clear, and makes you think about history in a whole new way. Reading assignments weren't long, but reading the textbook was not required to pass tests (except for the sources part). Multiple-choice quizzes, essay-based exams (both of which were not difficult). Overall, wonderful class. Would take again.
Loved this professor. I hate history but he made it interesting. His accent isn't hard to get through. He's a great guy! I strongly recommend him, especially if history is not your strong suit.

He is from Argentina and has an accent so sometimes it's hard to understand.  Most of the time he explains as clearly as possible and is very helpful this more than makes up for difficulties with the accent.  He is very passionate about history and you can't help but feel a little bit too because of him. Questions on the test are broad.
His lectures are informative, interesting, and eloquent. As a professor he is very helpful and funny. One of a kind teacher. Definitely recommend.

Most stuffs are easy and fun other than the quiz, but quizzes take up to 20%. So it looks like an easy A, but if you really want an A, do the reading, carefully! The professor is full of passion since he is a huge fan of soccer and barca, and don't worry if you are not, the lecture is AMAZING, and he is hilarious.

So I took Survey of Global History (HIST 105) and he led this class. He put up lecture slides for each class. He requires reading from the books but usually the slides cover what would be on the test and quizzes (about 10 quizzes per semester). No homework but you should seriously read the required book to understand what you're learning in depth.
He is a nice guy who is interested in the material. If you go to class and pay attention you should be able to get a good grade. If you like history you will enjoy the class.

Very concise lectures with great pacing. She clearly wants her students to succeed, and she gives a lot of helpful examples in class that make the homework and the exams much easier to complete. I would highly recommend taking her section if you want to excel in this course.

Very concise lectures with great pacing. She also gives a lot of examples that make both the exams and the homework much easier (my goat), and she clearly wants her students to succeed. Overall, I would highly recommend taking her section for diff eq if you want to excel in this course.

Explains everything super well in lectures and answers students' questions perfectly. Has amazing pacing in lecture, and does a great job review material before exams. Probably the best math prof. I have had a Purdue.

Dr. Poghotanyan is a great math professor. For previous classes I would ChenFlix, but she is just at or above Chen's level. She goes through lectures at a good pace but makes sure you understand the concepts she is covering. She leaves time for questions and I have a good understanding of differential equations thanks to her. Highly recommend her.
She is very nice and always make sure to answer your questions during lecture. Goes over a lot of material during lecture so make sure to show up.

Dr. Poghotanyan is far from a bad option for this course, but given a choice I'd choose a prof who provides more exam prep material and is proactive with helping students prep for exams. Lectures are fine. She is a caring and nice person. Accent is negligible. Overall, an okay professor, but I'd choose a different one for this course if possible.

Great lectures! Lots of examples that help on the hws. Exams were reasonable and there are lots of past exams that prepare you well. She tries to block out time to review content, so that is nice. Great prof!
Great lectures with lots of examples, which is super helpful for homeworks and exam prep. However, this is Purdue math so exams are very heavily weighted (final alone was 36%)

Dr. Poghotanyan goes over plenty of practice examples in class for HW and exam content. Lectures were helpful, and her style of teaching was usually understandable. She answers student questions well and is approachable. Overall, I'd say the course material itself isn't terrible as long as you use BoilerExams.

Good lectures, enthusiastic about the material, and grades exam free response questions more leniently than other professors (from my own and others experiences). I thought lecture was valuable, but supplemented with the textbook. As always with Purdue math, do lots of past exams and you'll be fine. Dif eq is very formulaic

Poghotanyan was professional. MA 266 is a difficult class with fairly boring content, but she delivered clear, informative lectures. Sometimes lectures were long and boring, but that's simply the case with any math class. I wish more things were posted in the brightspace, but that's my only complaint. I'd recommend taking her class.

Professor Poghotanyan was one of the best Math professors I had so far at Purdue. She explains the contents very well and covers most of homework and exam problems in her lectures. She also was very kind to solve doubts that might appear at the end of the class. She grades fairly as well and she loves when her students participate on her class.

266 was probably my least favorite math class at Purdue, but no fault to Poghotanyan. She is a solid lecturer, but don't expect her to help you after exams if you don't show up to lecture or OH (not that I did). I personally found the lectures a little slow, but she does cover most cases for homework/exam problems.

This was my favorite math class I've taken at Purdue because of Dr. Poghotanyan. Her lectures are concise and straight to the point and does a lot of examples in class that are similar/same as the homework. She grades her section(s) exams, so if she tells you to format the answers a specific way, do it! She helps students before/after class.

Professor Poghotanyan is one of the best professors in the math department. I would highly recommend taking her class in the morning section. She physically writes out all of her notes instead of just scrolling through prewritten notes. 266 was the best math course I have taken at Purdue. She is a little quiet but consistent and great.

Prof Poghotanyan was one of the worst professors I've ever had in the math department. There was minimal effort to teach this course and zero communication at all from the professor. I don't think she cares to teach. Other professors would create study guides for their students. Definitely would not recommend it unless you are ready to self-teach.

Typical Purdue math exam and homework structure, but she is a fair grader on the free response and gives amazing lecture. She explain the content in a way that is easy to understand and is approachable for questions.
very good professor, one of the best at purdue so far, able to teach effectively and open for questions. A must for 266
