Prof. Gayane is very organised during her lectures. She is only few of the set profesors who have been taking 266 for years now (purdue math tends to have several new grad lecturers each semester for 266 who can be hit or miss). Keeps a good pace during semester and responds quickly via mail. Overall, a good choice for 266.
Professor Poghotanyan's lectures are well organized, and she covers the content very thoroughly. If you're going to take MA 266, then I highly recommend her.

Good professor. Tells you what you need to know for hw/tests straight forward in lecture. Can be done remote at any time(minus exams). Lecture notes online, though recommended to go in person to ask questions. Handwritten hw prepares you well for exams.

She's a wonderful teacher. The lectures are super clear and she welcomes you to interrupt midway with a question if you fall behind at any point. The lecture notes (which are always posted online right after class)match the homework, so I rarely get stuck.

Dr. Poghotanyan has been my favorite Purdue math professor so far! Everything in class was explained clearly and well, with notes written in a notebook with DocCam. So, you can very easily follow along in your own notes. There were great examples in every lecture that reflected homework problems well. Office hours were very helpful, take advantage.

Great professor. The class is fairly easy as long as you show up to the lectures given in person. She goes over examples every meetings that help a lot with the homework given out. She does a great job preparing you for the test which are do able as long as you know the basic material.

Pretty run of the mill for a Purdue math instructor, has an accent but is understandable, makes written homework easy by doing examples in class that are directly taken from those problems. Take practice exams and you'll be fine on the actual ones.

Professor Boersma is awesome. She makes an effort to foster a community in the class. You will become a much better speaker by the end of the class. As long as you participate and put effort into the assignments, you will do well. One of the most fun classes I've taken at this school.
She gave good feedback on my assignments and understood us as students. Also was very welcoming and everyone in my class liked her.

She made this class so fun by fostering a very nice community within the class. It was my favorite one this semester! Very presentation based, and you become way more comfortable presenting in front of the class by the end with feedback from her and your classmates. Just follow her instructions, and you'll be all set.

She is a very good COM teacher. She makes everything that we learn very relatable. The class focuses more on presentations with feedback from your peers. The class is pretty laid-back as long as you do what is required of you. She makes the class very fun!

Cari runs an easy yet fair class. as long as you do the quizzes and prepare a little for presentation you can get a A. The class atmosphere is lively, and I looked forward to going to class every day.
Funny, talkative, relatable. Cari inspired a culture of community within the class and made it one I looked forward to each week! Highly recommend.

(Actually took COM217) She's a lively woman. She keeps the class engaging and pretty simple. She makes the course more presentation-oriented than other classes but that's a strength for me instead of having to write more papers. As long as you participate and play nice with her games then she's great!
An awesome teacher!

She was a great teacher.  She really cares about all her students and wants to see them succeed.  She makes the class fun and engaging.  If you need anything, just reach out to her, and I am sure she would be very accommodating.  It should be an easy A if you put effort into presentations and do decent on the quizzes (online).
Grading is kinda random honestly.

Her class is really laid back and a lot of fun if you get a good group of classmates.  She doesn't grade too hard and really encourages all the students, letting everyone know what they've improved on each presentation.  As long as you put in a little bit of effort and do the extra credit, it's an easy A.

Com 114 is already a pretty easy class. I had Boersma for the honors section of Com 114 and it was definitely my easiest class. I've heard other professors grade more easily, but I still did well on all my presentations (A average). For context, I usually started the presentations the day before.

Very much a chill and easy professor as long as you do what you are supposed to and take her feedback on your speeches. Our class often got distracted by outside conversation, which made it very fun and laidback. So crack a joke or two with your classmates and you'll have fun in this class.

I was struggling very much my freshman year of class and Cari was absolutely the best help and was the most understanding teacher I have met. Not only did she help me with all my class needs but also with my outside mental needs when I felt like I had no one else.

She is an awesome professor. She cares about her students outside of the classroom and doesn't grade too hard on the speeches. If you do your work you will get an A in the class. She is also very funny and has good stories.

An amazing professor! she really seems to care about her students. Always gave good feedback on speeches and told me what i needed to do in order to improve. As long as you put in effort and show improvement you most likely will get a good grade. Each week you have a couple of assignments and then a lecture quiz and a chapter reading quiz.

I originally did not have my speech schedule included from my course requests, so I just signed up for Boersma's class out of the blue. I'm glad that I did. She is a wonderful professor who was understanding and her made expectations very clear. If you do the assignments and use her feedback for your speeches, you will get the grade you want.

Dr. Crain responds to emails well, gives good feedback, and clearly cares about his students. His lectures are a bit boring, but he is very understanding and has a lax attendance policy (5 free skips, easy to excuse absences). Definitely recommend Dr. Crain.

Dr Crain is the goat, grades pretty easy as long as you look like you care about your presentation, you get up to 5 absences, he is very accommodating with missing things and a very understanding guy in general.

Lot of work for what is a required course that most students don't care about. He gets this though and is extremely lenient with grading and time requirements and at least makes lectures worth showing up for.

Dr. Crain is the best lecturer I've had at Purdue and the best teacher I've ever had! He is very caring and genuinely cares about his students learning the materials properly. He offers a ton of extra credit and grades very fair. He provides lots of feedback on his grading so it is easy to learn and improve. I wish I could have him for every class.

Dr. Crain is one of the best professors I have ever had at Purdue. He is provides great feedback on all speeches and has a genuine care for his students. He can be a tough grader at times, but gives plenty of opportunity for extra credit at the end of the semester!

Dr. Crain was wonderful. I was very nervous about taking this course, but he made it very easy and clear. He truly does care about his students doing well. He will work with you and your schedule, even asking students their availabilities before assigning presentation dates. If you're taking COM 217, please choose Dr. Crain if you can!

Dr. Crain is great! As a com major in a science presentation class, I thought that I would struggle, but it is very similar to COM 114 (I'm pretty sure they use the same textbook too).Though the textbook was necessary for the quizzes, they don't won't ruin your overall grade. I averaged 3/5 on most of them and still ended the class with an A.

Professor Crain is such a kind professor. Giving presentations can be stressful, but he does a great job at creating a classroom environment where everyone is comfortable with each other. He gives great feedback on how to improve as a writer or speaker and is an easy grader. If you hate public speaking, he's the professor for you.
Grades a bit harshly. Lectures are boring but he has good presentation skills. Attendance policy is painful. Seems to care about students.

Crain is a wonderful prof. He clearly cares for all of his students and is just a nice guy to be around. The curriculum is already planned (not by him), but he does a good job of working through it. If you have to take COM 217, take it with crain. Also very clear about what he expects from you in each presentation.

Very chill professor, COM is a pretty easy A with him. Just follow his instructions and you will do fine. Some of the assignments were a bit pointless, but they're not hard and give you free points. He also offers 30 points of extra credit. For us, the class was hybrid online and he was accommodating due to that. Overall great person and professor.

Dr. Crain is such a nice man. I was weary about having an 8:30 3 days a week my last semester at Purdue, but this man made it so much better. He cares and really seems passionate about what he teaches. I'm not a fan of public speaking or communications classes, but he made my learning experience better.

Dr. Crain is genuinely one of the nicest people I've ever met. The man has the patience of a saint and his class is never boring. The class is set up in a very organized format and I feel like an improved public speaker and author as a result. Plus, he has a really cute dog and will share pictures if you ask.
He puts in a lot of work into preparing the lectures and you can tell he really wants his students to succeed.
The best professor! Appreciate!!

Professor Crain was a great professor. He had a clear grading rubric for every assignment, taught us everything we needed to know to get an A, and gave good feedback. Everything was clear cut in his class. His lectures aren't boring and he keeps everything upbeat. He treated everyone respectfully even if they didn't do the same. Overall great guy.

I have not finished taking the class (ECE 49595NLP) but he just announced 3 exams all within the span of 5 days. The first one is scheduled for 14 hours after his announcement. The syllabus stated there would be no exams at all. It is week 12/16. We are still in the middle of doing an assignment and a term project. Please don't take this professor.

One of the worst professors and courses. The class materials are outdated and notes are unreadable. There does exist a course recording but you can barely hear anything. Often threaten to fail you if you do not satisfy some requirements.

- Course is a Joke 
- Project got canceled 5 days before deadline
caused by mislead and miscommunications

Knowledgeable professor but awful with regards to structuring and teaching the course. Makes assignments for his own convenience and assumes all students come in with experience for the course that is not explained or taught in class. Does not show up to office hours.
Does, in fact, manufactures "A"s. Super funny guy.
His lecture is very interesting.

His knowledge is immense, and lectures are great. He puts in a lot of effort into teaching, and making the syllabus the way it is. The examples and the problem sets are designed to give you an understanding and appreciation of how real world AI systems work. I also appreciated his passion for history in the field of AI/CS. Extremely bright man.

Great professor! Even though he only handed out 3 of the problem sets throughout the semester, he was still very open to questions and took time in getting everyone on the same page. Active participation is highly encouraged in the class.

More or less, the only thing you get out of the course is comfort with Scheme. The assignments are somewhat tough because you are not comfortable with scheme. Or else, it is like an introductory course. He spends a lot of time teaching scheme and in talking about historical perspectives. He goes through the AI part too quickly. The grading is easy.
He spent most of the semester teaching scheme. He never really got around to actually teaching AI. The class was easy, but a waste of time if you actually want to learn something.

so..he's a funny guy. a bit of a show off, but still an EXTREMELY knowledgeable guy. knows his stuff and how to explain it easily very well. his problem sets are tricky, but you will be able to ask for help and he does extend them on occasion.
Great professor!! Cares about students. Very helpful. I had an amazing time taking ECE 473 with him.
Throughly enjoyed the class. Learnt a lot.
Throughly enjoyed the class. I learned a lot! Every PM should take this class.
Fun assignments, no exams. She sends email with assignments and deadlines each week which I think is very nice and helpful. Many extra credit opprtunites.

Prof. Aggrawal is by far the best professor I have had in my Dtech program. She gives ample redo opportunities and provides very detailed feedback. You can tell she cares about your learning and less about grades. Make sure you cite AI use properly though or you will get a 0. The course had more work than expected but highly recommend!

She seems like a nice person who cares about the subject, but she makes this course WAY too challenging for a 2-credit 200-level course. The amount of content she goes over and the specifics she expects you to know is a bit insane for what the course is laid out to be. Over 60% of the course is exams too, and the homeworks aren't helpful imo.
Just memorize past tests and thought questions, homework/quizzes are graded by ta's and if you get a good one they will grade easy, but if not then you are out of luck.

Worst class I've ever taken. The exams require you to memorize specific examples for almost every question, and there is an unreasonable amount of content. She plays loud videos during the in-class quizzes and it takes forever for things to be graded. The rubrics are also very unclear and the homeworks are extremely tedious.

Tough grader and more content than you can imagine; but she genuinely cares for her students and the subject and is very approachable outside of class. She values true learning and is a great mentor if you go to her office hours. Edit: nvm she ghosted me lol

Lectures are super fast and overload you with information (40+ slides per lecture). There are only 3 exams in this course with no drops, including FRQs and MCQs. Absolutely do the thought questions, they will help you SO much. Be prepared to memorize everything, including examples. Homework takes a few hours, with research and citations required.

This class is very memorization-heavy, and it often feels like the professor prioritizes memorizing a large number of organism examples over truly understanding the material. I attended almost every lecture, but even though they are recorded, they are very dense and difficult to get through. Good Luck

The content isn't the most difficult, but the sheer volume is unsuitable for 2 credits. Zielinski is nice if you get to know her, but the grading is extremely strict so be precise. Would not recommend taking at the same time as BIOL241/CHM339. Create an Anki deck for the thought questions and you should be good.

Prof Z is a wonderful professor if you actually take the time to get to know her after class. The lectures are engaging and she always makes an effort to make them exciting. I really loved her class and the exams were easy if you pay attention on what to study and put effort into the course.
The most unorganized class I have ever taken. Inconsistent grading. She will also play videos of birds at max volume while you are taking your quiz.

This professor seeks a bell curve in her class grades and it shows through in the test performance and teaching quality. She does seem to otherwise be a great person, however I did not find her teaching effective and she mainly read off the slides. Review sessions were okay, but did not help actual test prep. Unfortunately was required for major.

If you do not have to take this class, DON'T. it is an unnecessary amount of work for a 200 level 2 credit class. the exams are impossible and hard, 80% of the class wouldn't even finish the exams. The answers they are looking for are so specific that you don't even know what they want. IT IS NOT WORTH THE TIME AND ENERGY

This semester was a rollercoaster, with unclear grading criteria, confusing rubrics, and inconsistent grading on alll assignments. While it is totally fair (albeit frustrating) to be asked to provide very specific in-class examples, it was not fun to have stuff in the rubric that was not in the prompt. Poor teaching, from otherwise nice person.

This was one of the most frustrating courses I have taken. The workload felt excessive for a 2-credit class, and the expectations were not clearly communicated. Exams focused heavily on memorizing specific species, graphs, and wording rather than understanding. The teaching style did not help me learn the material effectively.

This class gets hate only because of the amount of content and work that goes into it. To succeed, you need to be making flashcards and memorizing all the in class examples at least a week ahead of exams. But Dr. Z is a great lecturer, and she is a valuable resource. Go to her office hours, email her when you need help, she wants you to do well.

Very specific question wording and required examples. The extra credit questions are nice but only if you can get them correct. The grading can be inconsistent, and while most questions are fair based on the slides, they definitely feel like curveballs can trip you up easily—so read the question several times, carefully.
Exam Advice: Write fast. Write small. Write a lot. And mostly importantly, write the right answer.

Avoid this woman at all costs. This class is the worst. I take notes, I practice, I do all the things asked and this class is still impossible. The homeworks are painstakingly long and extremley writing heavy, and the grading is niche. There are 450+ slides of content per exam and she flies through it in class. Way too specific for intro level.

do what you can to take w/ humphrey in the spring. she makes exams unnecessarily difficult for a 100 level intro bio class (none of the averages were > 60%) & they feel almost impossible to study for bc every question feels like a riddle. her lecture slides are just paragraphs of text and she goes so quickly you don't have a chance to read them.
Impossible exams

She seems like a nice person but her quizzes and tests are insanely hard. The answer choices on the test feel like they are always made to trick you. Her tests basically require you to remember all the tiniest details from her lecs that are filled with the most insane amount of material. A lot of her lec slides have a lot of random small facts too.

Second worst class ever. Made way harder than other professors that teach this, does not care, only cares about her research, does not screen her TA's for communication skills, her PowerPoints are word slop, unclear about goals of class/lectures, goes on tangent, terrible presenter. Bad tests, quizzes, homework, different than in class material.

Despite being a wonderful person, Dr. Zelenski is one of the most evil professors at Purdue. Every year she receives the same feedback about her exams, and a very small fraction even passes these exams to begin with (the most common grade for an exam is a D), but the exams never change. She makes them impossible with pride. Save yourself early.

It's clear Dr. Zielinski cares about her students. I overheard her asking people about programs they had applied for & celebrating their accomplishments. However, she can't teach. I didn't expect it to be interesting but it was mind numbing. The lecture slides have too much info and are unclear what's actually important. I didn't read the textbook

She's a very nice person who cares deeply about what she teaches. I feel that a lot of the bad ratings are more aimed at the course rather than her teaching. With that being said, I would agree that the course needs to be raised to 3 credit hours or have a reduction in content.

Avoid this class at all costs. For a 2-credit, 200-level course, the amount of extra content and depth she covers is insane. And quite frankly, evil. Her lectures are pointlessly long and packed with irrelevant details/examples that show up on exams, which make up most of the grade. It's no surprise so many students drop the course or fail out. Run
Not sure why the rating are so low. Prof. Jin is SO CARING. One of the only professors on campus that put in the extra effort to help out students, even outside office hours.
For the sake of future students, she needs a new job.

Teacher has a remarkably difficult time speaking english for having lived in the united states for 20 some years. She moves through content very quickly and often struggles to finish all of the slides before class ends.

The class itself wasn't too bad, but the lectures are a little hard to understand, especially when there's a lot of information crammed into one slide. Attendance is also required for every lecture, so you can only miss two lectures.
Must watch videos outside of lectures to stay afloat. Hard to understand her. Final was pretty easy.

The lectures are boring, but the best way to learn the topics are through the homework anyway. My only critique is that the labs are unnecessarily complicated. Most of the time the homework is just that weeks lab with a few extra steps.

I have a lots of programming experience and am a CS major so CS 176 was pretty manageable. If you do not have programming experience, this class is pretty hard and its good to learn some python before hand. The lectures are pretty useful and she pretty much tells you everything you need to know for the exam.

Worst professor I have ever had. Her slides contain several typos and misinformation. I brought her slides to the TA because they weren't making sense and the TA agreed that they were completely wrong. She shows up late for lectures and will yell the whole time. She will scold students for leaving for any reason including using the bathroom.

If you cannot understand a standard Chinese accent (for whatever reason), don't take this class. If you are normal, she is not a bad lecturer remotely. If you choose to pay attention, you will learn everything that you need to. Stop blaming your failures on a lecturer.

This professor is absolutely terrible. Reads directly off the lecture slides, and if anyone asks a question, her response is to simply re-read the material louder. I learned more from watching python tutorials on YouTube than I did from her. She commits academic terrorism...do not take!!!

This 100-level Python class was supposed to be "beginner-friendly," but Xiaojin Liu will personally escort you to the ninth circle of coding hell. I have never been so utterly confused by a class in my academic career. Lectures are mandatory. Xiaojin Liu performs academic terrorism. It's 50 minutes of her reading slide answers aloud. Avoid Liu.

This is easily the worst class I have ever had the misfortune of taking. The "teacher", if you can call her that, genuinely seems to hate her own students. How you could yell at the mic every single day at 9:30 am for no good reason is simply beyond me. The lectures and labs tend to be more gibberish useless busywork than the slightest useful task.

The only problem that I had with her is that i dont always understand her language. However, I think her slides explain stuff well. In general, this course exams are not typical coding questions. You NEED to study past exams and study the textbook because a lot of material comes from there.

The professor teaches at a nice pace and uses lots of examples. Attendance is mandatory no matter the instructor you have. She barely has an accent (especially compared to other professors), and you can easily get a good grade if you review slides and participate in labs.
Gave me a migraine each lecture, is obnoxious and reads off script and asks unintelligent questions that they seem to believe helps students learn but only serve to confuse the lecture hall

Honestly shes not as shi d as people say but her lectures really are not very great. Shes not understandable, she has an accent but its pretty clear honestly its just she only reads off the slides and it can be pretty boring.

Ok this is my first prof review because I have to put some respect on Xiaojin's name. I have literally 0 c experience and I found her lectures very understandable. Her accent is really not bad at all and I have a really hard time with accents usually. Her lectures are actually engaging and I find myself not even looking at the clock. Great Prof

The worst professor I've ever had no competition. She yells at the mic and gives me a headache. I dread going to her lecture every time I do. Also has a very thick Chinese accent and really hard to understand.
Is there a way to program her so her teaching isn't bot mode
Inflexible and imposes assigned seating for lectures which are mandatory.

Terrible prof, attendance is mandatory even though it doesn't help reinforce material. Simply reads off slides and her way of trying to answer questions is simply reading the material louder. Very unclear lab and HW prompts and the autograder doesn't make it any easier. Don't take her class if you don't have to.

The worst professor I've had. Her lectures are mandatory, which i can understand because if they weren't no one would show up. You would get better results by spending the semester watching python coding tutorials on YouTube.

Easily the worst professor I've ever had the misfortune of having. Lectures slides, quizzes, homeworks, and even exams are always riddled with typos and mistakes, the TA's are all useless, and the professor is barely coherent, yells into the mic at 8 in the morning, shows up late, and poorly communicates important information. Avoid if possible.

I had no coding experience before this class and it was so hard to go through can't believe i clutched an A but my god presentations, practice exam and even the final exam are riddled with mistakes. Attendance is mandatory and having her in the morning screaming into the mic is just a recipe for a headache

Do not take from her if you can. She has you going to every lecture and uses iclicker, which would not be a problem if she actually taught the material. The powerpoints that she has are full of typos and do not actually explain how the code works. The exams were difficult but I would say she was very transparent with what is going to be on the exam

Get ready for a lot of homework and readings that don't really help. The class was tough, but you'll be fine if you look and memorize old exams. Dr Chang has got to be one of the SWEETEST people ever. If you are every struggling, reach out to him and he will do his best to help you.

He really cares about your success in the class, and the letter grade is less important to him than your learning and care for the course. If you show that you care and you make an effort, he is very lenient and helps you with your grades. Amazing professor. 10/10.

Despite the difficulty of the class, Dr. Chang is a very good professor. He has help sessions where he goes through homework questions, and I highly recommend attending. The practice exams are also similar to the actual exams. The only thing worth mentioning is that his notes by themselves may not make sense, so you will need to rewatch lectures.
do not take this course. I MEAN DO NOT. Exams are too hard, first exam avergae was a 64% with no accomidation.. like whattttt.

He's good and you can tell he cares until after Exam 2 and then everything changes. First two exams were alright, very similar to practice exams, and then the amount of content dumped for the final is mindboggling its 20 different subtopics with different examples every single lecture and he expects us to memorize/know everything

Dr. Chang is an amazing professor, and a great resource. The bad rep for this class comes solely from the fact that genetics itself is a tough subject. Use the practice exams heavily as review, go to office hours, and redo the variate homework's. If you put in the work, you'll be fine. The course is straightforward, just requires you to lock in.

I LOVED Dr Chang. There's not enough space to say how much I enjoyed his class. He's funny and he cares about his students. 15/10 would recommend. He answered all of my emails very quickly and posted more practice problems when requested. The class is hard but it's fair. He gives a practice exam and honestly using that as a study guide helps a lot.

This professor is very caring about the well-being and success of the students taking his class. You need to consistently pay attention in lecture and do the homeworks with the purpose of learning. The quizzes and exams are very difficult, but consistent with past years. I love Dr. Chang, but the class itself is just more difficult than most.

Exams are hard but mirror past exams. Lectures are dry but Chang cares for and makes time for his students. Attendance not mandatory in any capacity. Homework reasonable. One annoying thing is he starts a class from the second half of the previous lecture and then finishes with the first half of the lecture he is supposed to be on.

Love love love Dr. Chang; class is not easy, but his lectures and resources make it manageable. Every exam is slightly different--that first one is *significantly* different, which requires some adjustment to study strategies--but none are insurmountably hard. Complete HW intentionally, attend review, and study the small details; you'll get there!

Dr. Chang goes out of his way to offer office hours late at night. He helps you work through the homework during office hours, which was honestly more helpful than learning in lectures. Exams were very difficult, but overall, if you understand the homework and go to office hours, you will do relatively well.
He is fine. He is accommodating and knows what he is talking about but really he is just a mid genetics professor that I am fine not taking again.

Dr. Chang is an amazing professor. You can tell he cares about the material that he is teaching. Although exams are hard, they are mostly fair with a few random difficult questions. Other than that, if you take the time to either go to lecture or watch the recorded lectures, and you do all of the online homework, you can do well in this class.

He's probably one of the most reasonable professors at Purdue. He is extremely nice and will go the extra mile to explain concepts that you don't quite understands. If you utilize his availability your sure to preform well.

He is the nicest guy ever and is obviously passionate and knowledgeable about the subject, but he is a terrible teacher. I think he has a hard time really understanding the fact that this stuff is brand new to us. Additionally, those exams were just brutal. He is super helpful 1on1 when you can ask specific questions but lectures are incoherent.

I say tough grader in the sense that the class average is pretty low. I liked him as a person and teacher, but I wouldn't say you could get away without studying. Best advice: watch the notes outside of class so you can pause and come exam time go through the practice exam and make sure you know all the terms and why answers are right/wrong.

Chang is a very cool professor who is very accessible outside of lecture hours. The class was a trainwreck curriculum-wise, but he himself is an amazing person. Be sure to review exams and review lectures because it will be confusing (common trend for bio at Purdue). Be sure to reach out if you need to and definitely find a study group.

Dr. Chang class is very informative, all of his information is very relevant to further exams (MCAT GRE), I like when he explained blendr experiment. Overall challenging, but do practice exams and study and do homework and review lectures and you will get A. All exam material is from lecture, quizzes are kinda hard tho ngl, but great guy, mad cute.

Chang is a straight forward professor that highlights what's important for exams. There is lots of homework but they keep you on track. To do well on this class, review provided practice exams & find other ones on the internet. Redo homework, find a study group and really focus on understanding the lecture notes (not from the textbook). Enjoy.

He's a really funny guy and welcomes questions during lectures. If you need anything, email him and he will respond pretty quickly. This class is extremely difficult and has a lot of material, so read the textbook and use the old exams provided to study

Dr. Chang has been the best professor I've ever had while at Purdue, he wants his students to succeed as much as possible in BIOL 241. If you're struggling in the class, ASK FOR HELP AND EMAIL HIM! I can't emphasize this enough, Dr. Chang responded to all my emails during the spring semester and he also responded in less than 24 hours.

Dr. Chang was nice and I really wanted to like him, but clear expectations were not set for the class. The only way to succeed is to go online and find more old exams to use. If he decides to make the exam like the practice one he gave, you'll do fine. If he makes it way different, the whole class will suffer. Overall an inconsistent mixed bag.

Dr. Chang is a great professor. His lectures were very engaging although fast paced. He was always available outside of class to discuss homework or lectures. It was clear he wanted the students to succeed despite the difficulty of the course. Ask for help if you don't understand the concepts and it will help a lot for getting a good grade.

If you want a very nice professor, he's your guy. If you want a professor who tests on every single tiny detail he teaches, he is also your guy. Each exam covered ~10 50 slide powerpoints. You will have questions on a table in the bottom left corner of slide 167 lol. Exam heavy course that makes it hard to excel. He will try and help you if you ask

Funny lectures/nice person. But i hated the class. Didn't understand lectures unless watched in slow speed online. Main way to succeed is by doing old practice exams or memorizing every lecture slide in detail. Tests and quizzes are hard but hw's are very easy. Curves class well. He's always open to answer any questions.

Sunkula's lectures are actually very helpful in my opinion. He often uses visuals and graphs to help you actually understand the math you are doing. He also gives a 5 min break every lecture about 2/3 through which is very helpful for actually remaining attentive. The exams are hard but MA261 is a hard class and he gives very helpful study guides.
I thought his lectures were helpeful, as he spent a lot of time working through practice problems that were similar to the exams. He also provided a lot of good study materials.

I personally did not think he was a very effective lecturer. He works practice problems halfway and does not explain how they actually work. He gives plenty of resources to study for tests, but his teaching style was not my favorite.
Overall very good teacher. Lectures are good and works plenty of example problems. Gives alot of resources to prepare for exams. Would 100% recommend.
Very good professor who gives lots of resources to prepare for exams

Great professor. Lectures were very organized and clear. Homework was also reasonable. Exams were fair as long as you did the homework and understood the concepts. Really made me enjoy the content of the class!
He is a good professor. Not too slow, not too fast. His abundance of examples during lectures is really useful during homework.
Lectures aren't all that bad, but the examples he does are so much easier than the tests and homework. It's really the exams and quizzes that will get you.

Firstly, his lectures are amazing - they are really easy to understand, and everything on the test were (mostly) mentioned in the revision lectures. He is also very accessible outside of class (office hours and feast with faculty) and is very friendly. The only thing bad (about this course overall) is that you have to pay to do homework)

Amazing lecturer. His slides are very helpful, and he consistently uploads lecture recordings and materials. He explains concepts clearly and makes sure students understand the material. Calc 1 is manageable with regular studying. Attendance is not mandatory, and I would recommend this professor.

Dr. Sunkula is a very fair and kind individual.  He does his best to support students inside and outside of the course, often hosting extra office hours during the weeks before exams.  However, the exams require quite a bit of studying, and the weekly recitation quizzes are quite difficult.  Also, the class was at 7:30, which made it suck.
Barely passed the class but I definitely could not have passed it without his help. He is easily accessible and cares about his students success

I took 16200I. met once a week power hour and recitation. small group presentation in lecture (super easy) shares exam writing with regular 16200 professor. posts great lecture videos, lectures are basically just doing practice problems

Professor is very good at his job in general but there are lots of homework and they are significantly harder than the exams, which also require considerable preparation. The textbook is also expensive for a single semester of usage. He's very caring but be prepared for a fair amount of commitment

He is very good explaining, and always takes his time for people to ask questions. His explanations are  well paced and he shows genuine cocern and care for his students. Some of the questions of some HW's are kinda hard, but nothing other than that. His exams are very easy. I would definetely recommend him :)

Professor is great but the course is not. He teaches very basic examples, the homework is much difficult and the exams he prepares are insanely difficult. Your grade depends on Quizzes, 3 Mids, 1 Final and just 15% on the homework. Thinking about 'A' is impossible due to this as no one can get 90+ on every quiz, and exam.
Awesome prof. Highly recommend!

He lectures very well in my opinion but this was not my first linear algebra course. I felt like he really went out of his way to give us as many free points on homeworks as he could. Grading took a while but the professor was very easy to get in touch with on Piazza. One of the best classes/profs I've taken at purdue.

Professor Sunkula is a great professor. He is a nice guy who is great at explaining things but can be a bit quiet at times. I personally didn't like the flipped nature of the course and would deter anyone from taking the "Impact" or "Flipped" version of math classes if you are a person who learns well by example and practice.

he was a great lecturer and was very good at explaining everything. the actual structure of the course is not the best, with most of your grade coming from 2 question quizzes that are very difficult to get 100% on, however, that is not his fault. definitely would be okay with having him again!

Good professor. To me it sometimes felt like he went over the "easy" things in lots of detail while glazing over the "harder" elements. Overall, a good lecturer, answers questions well. The recitation quizzes are pretty important, so make sure you do okay on them and prep for them.

Sunkula is good if you are able to pick up on material easy. He records and uploads his lectures onto his website as well as providing his lecture notes afterwards. He can be a little dull but his explanations are in depth and quality.

Wonderful Professor for Math! He genuinely made me love what I was learning and tried to show visual illustrations of what he was trying to teach us. Get used to the exam format quickly by doing the past exams on the archive as each exam is worth a big portion of your grade. He is very patient and always answers questions students ask.

If you have taken Calc 1 this class will be easy. 16100 is the 5 day a week calc which basically means you get an extra recitation with a 10min quiz at the end, if your TA is chill it will be free points. Ended with a 94 and they curved an A down to a 84 anyway so its not too bad. Use exam archive to study and it will essentially be the exact test.
He was your average lecturer. He was pretty clear, and always posted lectures. Responded to emails quick. He was not exceptional, but not bad in any way.

She herself is really cool and clearly knows a lot about what she is teaching. TA's are a different story, as they often messed up in review sessions, so I'd recommend skipping them. She made biochem really interesting and lowkey fun so just go to her lectures and you'll be ok.

Make sure you put lots of detail into the homework, in the beginning i didn't and this caused me to lose points, also towards the harder part of the class she went super fast and went super slow on the easy stuff so i would reccomend going over that part with various metabolic pathways.

She taught the content for the first two exams at a reasonable pace, but the metabolism units are very rushed and can catch you off guard. The study guides are helpful, but they are a bit vague compared to the questions on the exams. There were also mistakes on some of the practice exam keys. Overall, pretty mid.

Dr Lyon wasn't my favorite professor, but she wasn't bad by any means. She had study guides for each exam & aside from the final they were pretty accurate/helpful. She was very nice & lecture was easy to follow. My main complaint is she took awhile on the stuff that was mainly review of gen chem & then rushed through the hard stuff like metabolism

This is probably my favorite class I have taken at Purdue and part of that was Dr. Lyon. Her lectures were fun and easy to follow. I felt the content was easy to grasp and plenty of help was given with review sessions. I would take again if I could.
Highly relevant to the MCAT. The course is becoming reasonable year by year. Highly recommend it for any pre-health majors preparing for the bar exam.

I really liked her. I wish I would have gone to her office hours more. The exams seem reasonable and I feel like I learned the material well. I would recommend her to everyone. Lots of cool information, but lots of boring stuff (like buffers). Overall, I really liked the class and her! I would say take it!

Dr. Lyon is one of my favorite professors at Purdue. Her lectures were very interesting, and she was extremely helpful during office hours. It is clear she cares about her students.  Additionally, this class taught me a lot of material that is relevant to the MCAT for both biology and biochem. I would highly recommend CHM 339 with Dr. Lyon.

Dr.Lyon is very caring/positive, but her class is the worst it made me cry sm :( Her homeworks/quizzes were graded harshly but she did offer some extra credit. Make sure you review EVERY slide before an exam and take the practice test. Always write your exams in pen bc you can def get points back! She curves well, went from C- to C+.

Hard but fair. Homework, quizzes, and exams are all difficult, but not so difficult that it feels unfair. Lots of memorization. Lectures are info dense but helpful. Final exam was brutal. Gave some extra credit near the end of the semester.

Dr. Lyon teaches in a way that is very accessible. Her lectures are dense with information but interesting to listen to. My complaint is with the grading. The exams and homework are almost all free response and allow little room for error, so you have to memorize everything in her PowerPoints. It's a difficult class but you learn a lot.

This seems to be a hot take, but I enjoyed Dr. Lyon. She could improve by providing more practice problems and preparation for the exams. She tends to only give out one previous exam in preparation for upcoming exams, which really sucks. She does give out study guides that tend to be nearly all inclusive to the material, though.
The homework are very much hard and she teaches only be reading the PowerPoints. The exams are all free response with very little multiple choice. She also gives very little practice problems.
Very hard class. A lot of memorization. Should have exams more multiple choice heavy than basically all open response. Could have done better given more practice problems.

Dr. Lyon is an amazing lecturer who keeps the material interesting & uses real world examples. Sadly, the homework and tests are crazy. She said in her last office hour that "we the graders only skim for key words". Use her exact words on everything & write as much as possible. She will test you on all the tiny details, so memorize them.

Dr. Lyon is awesome!!! Yes homework and exams are graded harshly but her office hours are a must. She is always willing to help you and is open to all questions (even if they aren't directly about her course). Material is tough and a lot to learn so requires quite a bit of studying

Prof Lyon is a great teacher. She knows what she is talking about and her lectures are clear and easy to understand. BUT, the way homework and exams are graded is horrible. The graders are so nitpicky about everything and it's hard to get points back if you didn't exactly write down what they wanted.

Professor Lyon, not reasonable at all. Her and her TA's grade every assignment extremely hard. I wrote at least a paragraph for each question on quizzes and homework to make sure to get full points. Lyon is not to considerate to students and expects way too much from them. Worst chem professor I ever had, she makes me want to transfer to IU.

This is a hard class that requires a lot of outside work and memorization. Dr. Lyon's lectures are okay, but she goes pretty fast since she has to cover a lot of material. The grading on homework and exams doesn't seem to be standardized. The quizzes are difficult and require nitpicky details from lectures, and the exams cover a broad range of info

The amount of material students are expected to memorize for quizzes/exams is EXTREMELY unreasonable. The TA's are really tough graders, and it's like they want to take points off your homework. The class is not curved, so you really need to do well on the exams, which Prof. Lyon makes nearly impossible with her expectations from students.
Her Biochem 339 class has been the worst class that I've taken so far in college. It is wholly and completely unfair in the amount of information you need to know for quizzes and exams.

Honestly, the most difficult part of this class is the unnecessarily robust memorization. Dr. Lyon is an alright lecturer, and the content is organized well, but if you don't prepare for quizzes it will screw you over (they're all memorization). TAs are ridiculously hard graders for some reason, so you generally have to be perfect.

Biochem is challenging, but if you put in the time to understand the material and form connections between what is being taught, it makes it so much more manageable. You don't need the textbook to do well in this course. Just stay on top of the homework and study for exams and quizzes. Lyon is super kind, always open to questions, and is fair.

Professor Datchev is extremely experienced in mathematical analysis, and his teaching reflects that as well. He doesn't seem to need any notes at all for lectures; he kinda just yolos it. Yet he never fails to explain difficult concepts and proofs clearly and thoroughly. He is really sweet and patient during office hours. :)
Amazing professor. Clearly very passionate about what he is teaching and cares about students understanding the nuance behind the concepts he explains.
Absolutely amazing professor. Best by far I have had the pleasure of learning from at Purdue. Professor Datchev, if you are reading this, never change.
Class is difficult, but he's good at explaining things and has a good energy about him
Best math professor at purdue, I took MA425 for his class. He explained everything clearly, and he always stops in his class and asks question to make sure student understand the lecture content.

I spent a significant amount of time in the 5 hours of weekly office hours he offered! I found it interesting that he combined content organization from several textbooks, and I really enjoyed the material. His exams were near copies of the review.

Great at explaining complex topics in an intuitive and easy-to-understand way. Homeworks and exams aren't easy, but certainly aren't unfair either. I would enthusiastically enroll in another one of his classes given the opportunity.

Datchev is an excellent professor with great examples and comprehensive proves. The Homework is a little bit hard but it is acceptable. The feeling in class is so great cause everyone is focusing on class with lots of deep questions. The only imperfection is his handwriting is not good enough. Have to say, he is the best Professor I have ever met
Makes Real Analysis very digestible and easy to understand. Homework is challenging yet not impossible. Good balance of work. Very personable outside of class.

Tough class but Dr. Datchev teaches well; he's always available to help and will go off course to make sure you understand the basics before he goes back to solving whatever prob he was on. Exams are very fair--close to the practice exams and HWs. Sometimes he goes a bit too fast in class so make sure to stop him from time to time. Solid choice.

Kiril is the smartest professor I've ever had at Purdue. He is enthusiastic not only about math but anything related to sciences as well. He made the homework problems all by himself, which is usually frustratingly hard so make sure go to his office hours. I am so thankful that I had him this semester!

No getting around it, this is a rough class. As a lecturer, Kiril ranged from being comprehensive and well-paced to visibly confused. Occasionally he'd stop working his own problems part-way through because they really were too advanced. Outside of class, his failed to answer questions clearly and exams were brutal. Get a good tutor.
I must admit this was the first math class that I really enjoyed attending (trust me I have attended a lot of them). Prof. Datchev was keen on making sure that you really understood the concepts.

The difficulty is more like a 2.5. Dr. Datchev is very caring and sincere. He is willing to help you after class and explain things in a way that make sense. His exams are very fair and model closely the practice exams he hands out in class. If he is teaching a class you are taking, I would choose him.

Very organised and interesting lecturer. One of the best I've had at Purdue, and sets weekly difficult homeworks. If you do the homeworks you'll be more than prepared for the exams, but be aware he does make them hard (but curves appropriately). Make use of his office hours, he's very friendly and helpful if you ask.
Best professor I've ever met at Purdue. He's not only smart and nice, but also excellent at teaching. Learned a lot from him!

She did not even know how to do one of the homework assignments when I went to office hours. She did not explain topics very well during lectures, and the practice exams were nothing like the actual exams, and we had 6 each. She never taught us the code and grade the hw assignements harshly and just overall do not take.

STAT350 is an intro class and was taught harder than ME CLASSES. She somehow made the class harder than calc 3 and diff eq. It was a free for all and also not a single thing in her class helped. She was unprepared daily and I understand it's her 1st year teaching but she needs to teach something she can understand. Please avoid.
Can't understand a word she says, class itself wasn't too bad but you pretty much have to teach yourself.

Do not expect her to actually teach you anything. You will have to learn everything on your own and just pray that you pass the class even though the exam averages were never an actual passing grade. She also refused to curve the class and basically blamed it all on us. There also aren't any other instructors teaching this class in Indy currently.

Unless you took a statistics course previously, I wouldn't recommend taking STAT350 with this professor. She is unable to explain things well and it's hard to understand what she is saying. Also she does answer questions but her knowledge is pretty limited. I had to self study in order to pass the class.

Lectures are extremely hard to follow and the professor gives very unhelpful answers to student's questions. Midterm averages were 48% and 57%. She took no accountability and blamed it on the students wile making it very clear there will be no curve. Is rude to students at times over class concerns and doesn't give good help to prepare for exams.

Does not prepare you for exams. Lectures are very sporadic and hard (almost impossible) to follow along. At the end of lecture, slides are often extremely rushed. With all the negativity said, she still cares for you as a student and wants you to do well. She is just new to teaching and the class may be too in depth.

first midterm the class average was 48.25% and the most common was around 45% most of the class did not do well, for the second midterm the average was 57.98% and the most common grade was 58.75% a minor improvement. She blamed students for the terrible average on the first exam, was entirely unhelpful in preparing for exams and is rude to students
Blames class averages on exams of 40-50% on all students, saying time should be used more wisely.  No accountability taken by professor, no curves
So caring, sweet, and funny. Can't say enough great things about her.
Megi is extremely kind, caring, and understanding. Her teaching style makes it easier to learn and understand Spanish. I highly recommend having her as a professor!
Super good experience, Megi was nice, caring and really made an effort to get to know every student. She is super understanding about assignments and tests. Amazing professor!

Oh my goodness I could not speak more highly of Megi. She is genuinely the best Spanish professor I've ever had. She truly cares about her students, and is willing to set up meetings outside of class if you're struggling. She also grades extremely generously. You would not regret taking Spanish 301 with her.

LOVE MEGI. If you will be in her class, then you are in good hands. She is super flexible and compassionate. She values the uniqueness of each of her students. Participation is important in this course, but Megi makes it super comfortable to speak in class. Made lots of friends in this class.
Megi was amazing and everyone in my class agreed that she was the best professor ever. She cared for every student in that classroom and I truly miss her so much!
I loved this class and Megi! She is so sweet and the class was so fun! Plus no hw! Honestly, my favorite class so far at Purdue and Megi is one of my favorite professors.

Very text heavy course and be perpared to print out every reading for class since she doesnt allow laptops or phones majority of the time. She holds great discussions over the text and homework which helps you better grasp the topic. She has a great attittude and loves to use philo texts in class.

Solidly good prof, lots of small assignments with short responses, quizzes, discussion posts, attendance, 1 exam, and 1 paper so its easy to do well. Very discussion-based class and she cold calls if she knows your name. Content is very interesting and she presents many concepts clearly through readings. Quick to respond to emails and understanding

Had Dr. Kane for SCLA 102, all our readings were centered around the idea of work and labor. Super interesting and helped students understand how the course reading materials were applicable. Participation is encouraged and class discussions were very interesting. She wants you to succeed which makes the class easy as long as you put in some effort

Dr. Kane is a really great instructor. The class is very discussion heavy and participation in discussion is crucial for your success in the class. The readings are sooo long but most of them are still interesting. Her attendance policy is very strict though, so even if you go to the doctor, dont expect to still get the points for the day.

There's a reading for every class, then in class you discuss it. She chose interesting readings for the most part, and facilitated discussion well. 3 presentations are the majority of the grade, but if you're a decent public speaker, they're easy As.

Attendance comprised of 3 "random checks" throughout the semester, which led to people gambling on which days to attend. Lectures are UNBEARABLY boring, especially since they are extended lectures, 1h15m instead of the normal 50m. Exams are all online but with several grammatical errors, making it difficult to get good scores.

Sun is so nice, truly, however his class is extremely boring. I love him as a person, but the lecture is unbearable and the quizzes and exams are tough. Make sure you go to class... you may be the only one there, though.

370 is cryptography and Sun shows great knowledge in this field in class. Crypto requires high-levels of math to understand fully but Sun understands students in this class don't have the complete math knowledge yet for this class and therefore does a great job at slowly explaining basic theory in crypto without the math. Tough class but doable.
Very caring and is willing to talk to students.
Very nice and organized

Professor Lark is amazing and really cares about how well her students do in this class. Everything is really clearly laid out at the beginning of the semester, and it's very clear what you need to do to earn full points. As long as you show up to class and complete the easy assignments, you'll get an A.

Such a fun class! Lectures were engaging and prof is passionate/knowledgable on the topic! Quizzes every week, but super easy if you came to class. Exams were even better, a subset if qs from previous quizzes. Jazz concert requirement made got me to try new events at purdue so I actually enjoyed it! Highly recommend this class!

Super fun and interesting class which teaches a lot about jazz history and its importance. The class is pretty easy with open note quizzes and exams where you can study the exact questions that will be on it. Going to the jazz concerts is a little annoying, but it's a great experience.
Excellent professor with a real passion for literature and philosophy. Also really interesting to talk to outside of class.

He's nice but I dont like English. To be honest it felt more like we were supposed to think about "important" stuff rather than grammar, the Theseus myth was cool though and I liked the group project more than i thought i would.

Not a difficult class and participation graded heavily. He gets students to talk more than in most classes. Cool if you like asking philosophical or deep questions. The myth stuff I didn't really like but he made it relevant in an engaging way.
Not a bad or extremely rigorous class but lectures are tedious, which is the majority of the class. Also, he checks for annotations of your reading inconsistently, which is very frustrating.
he really helped me at a difficult personal time

Solid class. The pass/fail grading system is clear and straightforward. It really eased my anxiety about any individual assignment since you can retake whatever you fail. Really encourages learning and supports student development without stressing too much about any particular error. Focuses on the big picture.

His grading system is extremely frustrating and unclear. The pass/fail structure offers little meaningful feedback and makes it hard to know what is expected to succeed. Standards feel arbitrary, and effort does not seem to correlate with outcomes. The grading approach creates unnecessary stress and undermines learning rather than supporting it.

Prof. Renfro deeply cares about his students and wants them to learn as much as possible. The class is fun if you like talking about big ideas and reading books. He's super into critical thinking, and he accommodates you if you have a bad day. He's really nice in office hours, and there's no way you're gonna fail the class if you show up.

If you want to learn and not just go through the motions of a class this is perfect. There are a lot of learning oppurtunities and the professor makes sure that the students take away important information. Professor Renfro teached in an engaging way and I believe that this was the most I have ever learned from an english class.
He is da GOAT.

The pass-fail system put a lot of stress on students which I felt was not helpful to learning. Every graded assignment has to meet all aspects of the criteria, every single one which gives students an anxious feeling and not conducive. Too many things to get an A in this class as well. Every assignment is handwritten.

Dr. Renfro is a wonderful professor. He's passionate and give students the space to speak and debate freely, whilst honing their humanities skills. His grading system is straightforward, working on a pass-fail system. Do the readings, don't procrastinate, and you'll be fine. No technology is used in this class--be ready to handwrite everything.

The class has a weird pass-fail grading structure that makes every assignment stressful. Way too many assignments required for an A that have nothing to do with the course material. Every assignment must also be handwritten, and expectations for each assignment are weird. Nice professor, but the structure of the class isn't conducive to learning.

Dr Renfro is an amazing professor who cares about his students and wants them to grow as people and students.  The class can seem like lots of work at first, but if you stick with it and work hard you will be glad that you did.

Give this man a medal! He loves his students, knows that work is work and isnt always easy, and still makes it fun and asks super provocative questions. Take Dr. Renfro if you actually want to learn and grow in SCLA 101, but not necessarily if you just want an easy A.

I enjoyed the discussions; they were quite thought-provoking. The lectures were engaging, and the conversation videos were a great experience. He clearly wants his students to think critically. There aren't many real assignments, no final, one big research project, two in-class essays, and a journal with 10 entries minimum.

Dr. Renfro laid out three goals at the beginning of the course and he ensured that most, if not all, students met them. I call that success. Plus, it was super fun and interesting going to class and wondering what he would ask us.

Dr. Renfro's rmp is crazy skewed. He's just a nerd who loves literature, it's not that deep. The course was an intro so of course, it was extensive. The class was reading-heavy, but his insight was interesting and provocative. He's invested in his student's success; he even curved the midterm 6%. There's only 4 assignments for a C. He rocks. :)

Dr. Renfro might be one of the greatest teacher's I've had, he challenges you to think critically about everything. His class definitely wasn't a walk in the park though.

Come prepared and participate every day, that's really all it takes. I can't even count how many second chances he's given people, he just wants them to get a good grade.

Dr. Renfro's grading style is unnecessarily complicated, and he gives far too much work for 3 credit hours. That said, I actually did enjoy the discussions when people actually read; they were quite thought-provoking. The lectures were engaging, and the conversation videos were a great experience. He clearly wants his students to think critically.

Prof. Renfro is really caring but pushes you to do your best. If you talk with him in office hours, he's the most supportive professor. He tries to get everyone to talk and is really into Socratic seminar-style discussion, which can sometimes lose some basic info, but that's sort of the point of SCLA 101, to read and come to your own conclusions.
Genuinely cares about his students and seems tough on the outside but is super nice. Asks super provocative questions and is really good at getting students to talk in class.
most interesting class i've had at purdue and i'm a junior, freshman don't get how unique this bro is. just show up and do the work and readings and its easy
Nice guy but badly structured course. You're required to attend events outside of class and you have to hand write every assignment and essay.

Really cool guy! would love to take another class from him again, a lot of homework and reading so be prepared for that. Research project is fun. I find he is a forgiving grader for small mistakes as long as you don't make to many. Lots of discussion, he clearly loves his students and wants them to think critically. Definitely take his class!

Very excited professor and makes lectures fun but very difficult to work with. NO PHONES, MUST HAVE Physical Remote Iclicker, and must attend every lecture/lab. Covers material well but you will have to work for your grade and follow all the rules to her classroom.

Dr. Zuelly's classes are easy classes as long as you do the right things. Go to the lectures, go to the labs, do the assignments. Studying for her exams isn't bad. Do all the right things, and you also don't take a final. Would recommend.

Zuelly is super funny and incredibly knowledgable about the topics she covers. She communicates clearly and makes the content more engaging. She might seem intimidating at first but she is very nice. She uses iclicker to take attendance, but the post lecture quizzes are not hard at all. Just do the work and the content will be easy to understand.

Dr. Zuelly is an excellent professor whose passion makes labs both informative and fun. Her policies may seem strict, but are fair and help you become a better student. Even if not required, I'd highly recommend her class to any Animal Science undergraduate student. 10/10 would take again in a heartbeat!

Llectures are great and super organized, very easy to follow. All the information is given to you that is needed for exams and quizzes. However, she made the whole class buy a physical iclicker instead of using the app on our phones. This was a bit ridiculous because they were pretty pricey at $50, and that could have been avoided withe the app.
Class was pretty easy and lecures were really easy to follow along. The professor was just not very nice, sometimes rude.
Not a good person

Just go to class!! Attendance is very important but it definitely pays off bc she makes it very clear what you need to know for the exams. If you pay attention and put the time into studying with the resources she provides, you'll be perfectly fine!

This class was pretty easy but not very lax on any missing attendance. Attendance is required and there are NO drops or points that you can earn back for missing class. Zuelly is very funny, and I really enjoyed her class, but you really cannot miss much before your grade starts going down.

Fun and chill, but unorganized with unclear or last-minute group project requirements. But, she is an easy grader and lets the class be fun. Not the best for in depth learning but a laid back professor.

Dr. Zuelly is an amazing professor, and I highly recommend taking her classes. Attendance is mandatory, but if you don't have any absences, you are able to opt out of the final exam! At the end of each lecture and lab there is a mini quiz, and if you paid attention during class, you will be fine!

Professor Zuelly's class had clear expectations and was not difficult. It was nice to not have final exams with 100% attendance throughout the semester as well. I was not a fan of her as a person. She has a lot of grace for herself and her own mistakes (which happen a lot) but is very strict on her students. Class is easy, but she's not kind.
attendance mandatory

You don't have to attend class, but she does an iClicker for attendance as a grade. If you have perfect attendance you don't have to take the final. Exams are heavy on attendance quiz questions and lectures.
Super respectful of your beliefs/morals. Lectures were great and she explained everything very clearly.

Absolutely amazing! She explains concepts very well and breaks it down so even if you aren't good at ochem or bio, you will understand what she is teaching. Very chill and if you do all of the hw you get a chance to waive the final.

I loved having Dr. Zuelly in ANSC 255. She outlines her expectation of the class in the very first lecture, so there are no surprises in the class. Make sure to take good notes over the lectures for exams. Labs are interesting and relevant to the lecture material we cover in class. Overall, an amazing professor.

I highly recommend taking a course with Dr. Zuelly, the material is never too difficult and is very easy to understand if you pay attention to the lecture quizzes and lecture material. The course is made up of online discussions, lecture quizzes, group lab assignments, and individual quizzes. The exams are easy but you must study for them.

Such a fun class - the labs are the best part! I learned a lot and Dr. Zuelly is a wonderful professor. If you had perfect attendance you didn't have to take the final at the end of the semester, which is a plus.

Dr Zuelly is an amazing professor! she does a great job lecturing, and posts recordings of her lectures so you could go back and rewatch the lecture if needed. She also has great labs and we were doing something different every week. My only negative is her grading scale, Her grading scale is changed so a C+ is an 83.9%

Difficult class with lots of information for one semester. Dr. Zuelly is an alright professor but can easily be annoyed by small things. She will get upset if her attendance rates drop. Very strict phone rules, lose 25 points if she sees you on a phone. Labs are interesting, make sure to have old clothing you don't mind getting a little blood on.
Dr. Zuelley's class is tough. She gives lectures and lab quizzes so be prepared to know a lot of material. In-person lectures aren't required since she records everything.

I loved having Dr. Zuelly in ANSC 255! She's such a good teacher and is very thorough in explaining. The exams will cover everything she teaches per section, but as long as you take the notes and study (quizlet is the best for this) then you will do just fine. If you have any confusion or questions, she will definitely answer them well!

I'm so glad I took this class with Dr. Zuelly! She is very knowledgeable and fun to be around. You will learn a lot and feel confident about the skills you learn. Exams aren't hard if you study, and she has EC on them. There are quizzes every lecture and lab, but they are easy. The final is optional if you have attended every class and lab.

Very amicable and respects all students, always there for questions and office hours. Put in the effort and succeed, or fall behind and struggle. Content can be confusing at times, and often labs felt like an excessive workload. (WAY too many packets) However, you'll be surprised at how much you know about soils at the end of semester!
He was a great professor, lectures were interesting and the class overall is structured very well, exams were pretty easy and he provided practice exams that were pretty similar.
Activities outside of class are very useful and help a lot with learning the content. Exams and quizzes were pretty easy and there is final exemption if you do certain things.

This class is very well organized; you know what weekly requirements you must meet. You'll need to find your own time to finish the labs. This class has a lot of work; you must fully understand the concepts to succeed on the exams. Practice exams and TAs are extremely helpful. You'll be surprised by how much you know at the end of the course!

Shams and all the TAs for the course were great, but the course itself is insanely difficult. The weekly labs are good because you can do them anytime but the amount of time it takes is a LOT. And the content itself is a lot less intuitive than the first few weeks make you believe. Get ready to study, this course is very difficult!!

tough course. shams is very kind, but the amount of homework is actually ridiculous and unreasonable for one course. the homework is not even a lot of points considering the amount of time it takes to learn. students are expected to teach themselves most of the material, which is difficult

Course organization and communication could have been better. Lectures mainly followed the slides, and some material on exams was not emphasized clearly in class. Grades were also posted later than expected.

Very disorganized course with poor communication throughout the semester. Questions often went unanswered, lectures were difficult to follow and mostly slide reading, and exams did not reflect what was clearly covered in class and on the assignments . Grades are posted very late. Class was empty most of the time

Very disorganized course with poor communication throughout the semester. Questions often went unanswered, lectures were difficult to follow and mostly slide reading, class was empty, and exams did not reflect what was clearly covered in class. Grades were also posted very late.

Professor does not use email to communicate w/ students, instead uses a google sheet that has a Q&A tab, where questions were never answered. Exam material was way beyond what was covered in class and no materials were given to prepare for the exam. Professor also simply reads from slides during lectures.
It is extremely hard to accurately prepare for exams, professor doesn't accept emails and created a spreadsheet as an alternative for people to ask questions, which also got completely ignored.
This professor gives the hardest exams with no practice at all. The practice questions are nothing like the exam. Please avoid.
Very hard to understand. Does not even accept emails for questions. Too much weightage on the final exam.

Worst prof I've had at Purdue, the class was very unorganized, and we were tested on concepts far beyond what was covered in class. She's hard to understand and just reads off slides that don't help with the few exams. The McGraw Hill homework is tedious and has little application in the real world (discount rates are not objectively knowable)

Very hard to understand in lectures. Homework assignments did not cover the same content that was covered on exams. Your overall class grade was not available to you throughout the whole semester until final grades were submitted. The worst professor I've ever had.
Prof. Bindra shares amazing insights on Reactor systems while teaching fundamental thermalhydraulic topics.
Comprehensive course in Thermalhydraulics/Heat transfer. I took ME version as well but I learnt lot more in this course than that.
Learnt a lot in this course.
He is fair in his grading process but exams ( particularly the first) are hard.
I thoroughly enjoyed this course. Dr. Bindra is phenomenal teacher, he not covers basic fluid mechanics fundamentals but also shows examples relevant to nuclear power systems.

Professor Bindra did not prepare me well to understand fluid mechanics which is a fundamental class for an engineering. His lectures were all over the place. Prof Bindra was difficult to contact with and did not utilize TAs. His exams were nothing like anything in class and through 4 exams his averages never got above a 50%.

He openly admits to giving exams that do not contain content he has taught the class. He said he would've done good so we are just dumb. We had a 27% average on the exam so he spent the entire next lecture bragging how smart he is and how they told him he was the only man for the job so if we're doing bad it's because we aren't trying hard enough.

Dr. Goforth was absolutely amazing. He made the material we were learning interesting and actually fun to learn. He is so passionate about his field and it was a real pleasure to be one of his students.

Dr. Goforth is one of the most inspirational professors I have ever had.  He has impacted my career path enormously and I can never thank him enough.  I am glad that he has such a passion for teaching and that I have gotten the privilege of being a student of his.
Decent at explaining concepts, she's no chen. Course was reasonable in it's difficulty, but students geniunely were not doing that well. Very generous curve at the end too.

She literally just reads off of her computer and copies down the notes on her iPad. She does not bother to explaining what she is writing down, and all the students in the class get lost because of how fast she is writing and scrolling without explaining.
Dr Hood is a fantastic lecturer. She presents ideas in a very natural way. Probably the best math lecturer I have ever had.

Much better experience than the non honors Calc 3 would be. Concepts are well taught. Attendance is mandatory, there is a decent amount of homework (albeit it is a five credit class), and there is a quiz during recitations but these boost class grades. Partial Credit. No curves but grades are deserved. Fun class and teacher if you engage.

I found her lectures easy to understand and she did a great job answering questions. Homework is very reasonable and her attendance policy is flexible. Unlike MA261 exams, all test questions are free response and either conceptual or computational. She awards partial credit, and I found her grading generous.

Prof. Hood was a decent lecturer. Hotseat questions kept you paying attention. MA 261 is known to be difficult but if you study the past exam archive you will be fine. Strangely our final exam had questions without correct answers (mcq), but it was curved significantly.

(For context, I'm a math major) 
She couldn't really explain the more difficult topics all too well; had to come to her office hours a lot to get those down. But she's very accessible and makes time for her students. Lectures were easy to understand, and were almost always explained intuitively and with some sort of proof. Exam was relatively easy.
Amazing professor for Honors Calc 3. Lectures are very organized, and she covers everything in a way that makes it understandable.

I loved the way that she explained the topics in class. She demonstrated how to go about difficult Calculus I problems in a logical, clear manner. She was also very kind to students, and was willing to advocate for those who could not hear due to unrelated noise. Overall, great instructor.

The class itself is hard, but I don't think that has to do with Dr. Hood's abilities. She does a good enough job during lectures, and is a pleasant teacher. Most of MA 161 really depends on your TA's abilities to explain things
Dr. Hood explained topics clearly, and her clicker questions and review were good preparation for exams. Lectures were engaging, and she was very helpful in office hours.

Overall lectures by her are average, and you don't really have to attend. Class itself is tough and sometimes it can feel like she hasn't taught you enough to do some of the homework/exam problems but honestly MA 161 depends a lot on your recitation TA rather than the lecture professor
