Overall quite a good professor. Very approachable in and outside of lecture, takes a lot of questions and answers them in a satisfying way. The exams are difficult, conceptually pretty dense, but this is a Purdue math thing in general.

Hello freshman, here's what you need to know for Hood's MA161.

1. She is organized. Know what you will be doing in lecture. Check Brightspace page weekly.

2. Watch Chenflix/Organic Chem Tutor before class. Please, for your sake, do this.

3. BoilerExams/Purdue Past Exam Archive to review for tests.

4. Have a good TA, and go to office hours.

Dr. Hood is a great prof for MA266. Notes are promptly posted to Brightspace, and lectures have plenty of examples and use iclicker practice questions which are helpful for review. Her section had very high averages on both midterms (high 80s). Some practice materials had a few typos but not much of an issue.

Least favorite math teacher ever. Have never been more confused and she could not answer questions during lecture or office hours correctly. Final exam average was 52% and standard deviation was a 38%.
Dr. Hood is an average lecturer, no standout qualities in her teaching. She seems kind enough but there were times the material was not taught in the clearest manner.

Professor Hood is not bad by any means, as her lectures are pretty average at best. I understood most of it but was often confused, way more so than in calc 2. The homework were very lengthy, and sometimes had no help if I was completely lost. The midterms were very fair but the final was incredibly scuffed, but that might've not been her fault.

She is a very solid, caring calc professor. She frequently asks for student input and utilizes it. The only issue I had was that she sometimes struggled to explain why a certain theorem works. This made it so understanding why certain things work was tough. That being said, she's a great choice and she'll keep improving as she gains experience.
Please just use chenflix. Her lectures are messy/unorganized and seem almost rushed. However, the exams she writes are on the easier side. Don't really know how people are giving her fives.

This class was my second time having Dr. Hood, and I would take every math class with her if I could. Her lectures are very clear, and she provides notes that match them. She also takes time to answer everyone's questions in detail. People's biggest complaint about her is the exams, but it's just like any other Purdue math or engineering course.

Dr. Hood was the best calc professor I've had. The fall 2023 final was a hot mess, which was unfortunate because I went from an A+ to a B+. Regardless, Dr. Hoods lectures were amazing, with many opportunities to ask for questions/clarification. I also liked Dr. Hood's dedication to her students by providing more office hours before tests

Dr. Hood is a fantastic professor. Lectures, office hours/study sessions before exams are very helpful and you can tell she really cares about her students. Fall 2023 final had numerous issues (questions without correct answers), but she responded well to student concerns and handled the situation as best as she could. Best Calc 3 prof by far.
The lectures were clear.  Dr. Hood really cares about the students. She is one of the best calc professors at Purdue.
I would only go to the lectures for review material before exams, but she answers questions both during and out of class well.
The pre-labs took so much more time than they were worth, and for multiple labs I kept losing points without any feedback. She would literally kick you out if she didn't like your drawing.

Genuinely one of the most rigid professors I've seen. In my situation lab flow allegedly would “never not auto save” on a makeup lab I did and had proof the work was completed. only found out when it was graded 3 weeks later.  Lab manuals are so poorly written (tell you to toss something out then want a pic of it)Overall a dumpster fire of a course

Basically the same as what everyone else here is saying, there's just no logic to the grading at all. The TA I had seemed as confused as I was about what she expected as I was, and I got kicked out of the lab a couple times due to unclear diagrams (automatic 0/20 in the gradebook). The grade you get is basically just luck and usually a b- or lower.

Genuinely I hope that no one ever has to go through the displeasure of talking to this woman. She gave me less than 24hrs notice to meet, when I told her I was busy she proceeded to say If you can't make it for any of the time slots available, I will just pull your schedules and set a time.  Please try again.

Very unclear about what needs to be included in pre-lab, will kick you out of the lab (worth 20 of 220 of total points btw) without warning if she doesn't approve of your drawings. Impossible to pass unless you go to the TAs for help, who give more useful information than she does. I wish her the best during course evaluations.
Kicks students out of lab if she decides their drawing is not good enough. Extremely unhelpful and the course has lackluster grading policies.

Provides no clear instruction on chem labs and is not willing to help or listen to students concerns. Will kick students out of lab over trivial reasons such as not including highly specific and generally irrelevant details on lab procedures. Seems as if looking for an excuse to prevent learning of students. Do not recommend by any means.
Very condescending, good luck with her and hope you never have to speak with her one on one
Her labs are disorganized and she kicks people out of lab before going over lab expectations.

Gives absolutely little to minimal guidance on pre-lab procedure, will decide if students can participate in lab with no prior warning, no care for how students perform, emphasis only on proper dress in lab
Runs her department not caring about the students at all. Takes away points for legitimately no reason.
She was thorough in her videos and explained in high depth how to use the elements of the labs. Be prepared to work for comprehension.

Professor Conklin was a great professor for the CHM 11520 lab. The lab section of this course wasn't very difficult, and often helped with understanding the CHM 11510 lecture material. I definitely recommend watching the weekly pre-lab videos, it will make everything easier in lab. Great explanations in the videos.

I'd like to mention something about her program under the tag of Academic Dishonesty. Let's say you know some material on an assignment, you tried hard to learn that criteria, right?...wrong. You worked hard on that assignment, but AI said it wasn't your work, no matter how much you plead to get them to understand it wasn't, she won't budge.
never again. 129 warrior

Dr Conklin is by far the most unconcerned, and careless course coordinator I have ever seen. First off all her labs are written terribly, very hard to understand and are unclear of direction. Second of all she is extremely rude, and any problem that might happen she does not care, and is so strict. Almost like she enjoyed to see me do bad.

Transferred my junior year, wore leggings (my fault for not reading syllabus) told me I had 15 min to change, so I did and made it back in 10 min, still kicked me out of lab. Also just rude, and the TA I had was not helpful. Overall lab was easy but she made it miserable.
Very rude, for a person with a high level title, instead of implementing knowledge she wants to implement control.
Her "drawing" workflow wastes an excessive amount of time without adding any real value.

I think some of the reviews about her personality are too harsh, she definitely commands respect, which is admirable. Some male professors with similar personalities would never get these kinds of reviews. Genuinely loves chemistry, and made class interesting to me. Her teaching style seemed to work well for me. Office hours were helpful.
Did not stay on schedule with lectures. Liked that she gave examples, but was disrespectful towards students

I found her way of explaining topics to be very helpful. While there are some time management issues, all content gets reviewed fairly well. Especially, if you attend the pre-exam review session and listen to lectures.
felt like I survived war when this class was over

This class lives up to the expectation of a 5 credit. She tends to use a lot of scientific terms that get confusing, and is definitely intimidating but you can tell she commands respect, which is a quality I think should not go unnoticed as a women in stem. Don't skip lectures and go to office hours.

Don't listen to the her old reviews, Conklin is an engaging, caring, and extremely helpful professor!! She gives so much good advice and provides countless resources, and you will be just fine if you take advantage of them. Sometimes she doesn't get through all of her lecture slides as planned, but her clear care for the class 100% makes up for it.
Finals were pretty straightforward and basically what we covered in class. Chatbot project was fun but I wished we had more time.

Professor Ying is a nice person and tries to make the class enjoyable. However, this two-credit course turned into a huge headache. The midterm and final are worth a large portion of your grade and feel like a lot for the credit hours. The TAs rarely knew what was going on, and the assignments felt pointless.

This class is not like any other and you cant just expect to pass without doing the work. Really will need to learn the key concepts, apply and think about them to do well in the labs and exams. But it's not too bad if you paid attention in class and in class exercises helped.

You get out what you put in. Show up to lecture and recitation and you should get an A. Midterm and final were kind of hard, but just study and you'll be fine. Labs were all graded fairly and I got at least a 90% on all of them. Big project at the end of semester makes up most of grade, just pick your lab partner wisely and you'll be fine.
Covers lots of topics, that's the nature of AI, it is constantly growing and evolving. Exam wasn't too bad if you paid attention in class. She will give hints on what to study.
Either you love it or you hate it. Important to show up to class.

Professor Ying was understanding and provided outside help if someone wanted it. Although the lectures are long, she takes her time explain the concepts for the class which was helpful. Overall, class was not that hard and labs aren't graded that harshly. But the instructions were very vague and sometimes confusing.
It wasn't too bad. She is nice and helps us out when we need it. Bonus points for in class exercises
She was easy on us for the finals!
Finals was pretty easy, 30 MCQ and basically everything covered during lectures. You'll do fine if you paid attention in class and participated.
She offered us a choice of harder exam with cheat sheet or keep it the same as midterm. Class voted for no cheat sheet - I actually prefer to have a harder exam with cheat sheet
Just finished studying for the exam, feeling good about it. In class discussions and exercises surely helped. Fingers crossed for a good final grade.

This class is genuinely so bad. Anytime my recitation has asked questions about the exam or some of the material the T/A's respond the same, "I'm not sure" and shows a total disconnect between them both. Half of the midterm was things never covered in lectures as well.
Fun lecture. You can easily fail the class if you don't show up and do the work though
Awesome teacher, very funny, and truly wants her students to succeed.
chatbot in 3 weeks.
Easy Class.
Dr. Ying is nice and cares about our success. Goes the extra mile to help us out.
3 weeks to build a chatbot.
Memes and in class games. Chatbot project, 5 labs, midterm and finals. Fun class.
Second half of the course is way easier
Too much work

5 minutes demos were rushed. I guess it's hard to get everyone organized especially with technical issues and people running overtime - glad to have the opportunity to present our chatbot and also to evaluate another teams work. Not bad, but wished we had more time
It was chaotic with everyone presenting during labs. Technical issues and people running overtime. It was good to see other people's work and have the opportunity to evaluate another team.

Wished our demo was longer and less rushed. It was a fun project to build our own chatbot regardless. Second half of the course was way easier with less technical stuff and more business applications. Professor tries to make the class interesting by including games and in class discussions. It's a good intro class.
If you have the option do not take this class online. It's difficult to remember about it. Also because its online he tends to grade even harsher.

It was a simple and very good class. The only thing is in the recording you can hear his mouth noises which were very off putting. It made me no want to listen to the lectures because it was nasty. If that's not a problem for you then you'll have a great time. Besides that he is really helpful. If you have questions he emails back fairly quick.

The course I took was ancient western medicine and I had to WORK for my B. Very tough grader and values tons of detail. Tests were all made up of 10 short answer questions that had to be answered in 100 words minimum. This course took years off of my life.

If you are interested in Classical Epics, he taught an amazing class. Sync online class, with 10  500 word min papers a semester). He is the reason I'm now minoring in classical studies. I full intend to take pretty much any class he teaches. Highly recommend if you are interested in the topic. If you're not interested in the topic, don't take it.

When I took CLCS 235 I was excited. I had a deep love for Greek mythology and could not wait to learn more. Unfortunately, I was let down during this course. This professor lacked professionalism within his discussion posts and within his grading. Get ready for mindless reading and argumentative discussions as well. I do not recommend this class.

i took two classes with prof dickson (greek religion and roman religion). classes are pretty easy to pass and get a good grade in. the workload is very manageable. he used a textbook but the readings were optional. he's really fun and really nice and very knowledgeable about the classics 100% recommend taking a class with him
Course wasn't difficult, super interesting lectures. Inspired me to minor in Classics

This class was fine nothing special. The exams are kinda difficult if you did not watch the lectures. His essays are pretty self explanatory, kinda rough if you don't know much about greek mythology. Professor answers emails almost instantaneously and gives extensions for certain reasons. The class consists of 7 or 8 quizzes, 4 essays, and 3 tests.

I surprisingly loved this class despite it being my third choice of a CLCS class. Professor Dickson is extremely knowledgeable about pretty much everything, and I always found his lectures to be extremely informative. The essays aren't graded super easily, so make sure to put in some effort and prove that you understand the subject matter.

Despite being online and during Maymester, the material isn't too overwhelming if you watch 3-4 mini-lectures a day. His lectures are also quite funny and not too boring. The grade is composed of quizzes, tests, and discussion posts. You probably need to take notes as his review slides aren't detailed enough for the quizzes/tests.
Not recommend

Taught the material in an engaging and interesting way despite being online. Had all of the lectures posted by the start of the semester with weekly lecture quizzes to keep you on track that were easy points if you've watched the videos. Prompts were clear and gave you more than enough to write about compared to the minimum counts.

This is the BEST prof I've ever had. He's engaging with material, responds timely to emails, and his classes are always accessible. Online lectures are pre-recorded, weekly quizzes but they're easy and he provides study sources, he has a decent sense of humor too. If you're a disabled student, you're going to love this professor.

So the mistake I made was underestimating this class because many people said that he was easy - DON'T. I did pretty bad on the tests (there were only 3, and were worth 60% of my grade), so I didn't get the A I wanted. However, the teacher and the class is really interesting and I don't regret taking this class. Really recommend it, do the work lol

Gives really engaging lectures that are easy to pay attention to. He has a great storytelling style that makes the material easy to remember. Only grades are exams and quizzes, but theyre fairly easy if you pay attention.

If you ask him for accessibility to the full lecture slides, he will probably give them to you. I didn't take too many notes during lecture because if you listen attentively, you will most likely absorb the important stuff. Be sure to review past quiz questions before the exam.

He has very engaging and interesting lectures and keeps the class interesting. He is very passionate and knowledgeable about the subject, and is accessible outside of class. As someone who only took this class for the culture requirement, I'm actually glad I took it and might take some of his other classes too.
Not a good proffesor when it comes to lectures. Tends to drone on, skips through slides too quickly, powerpoints are not helpful for notes

The dude's tests are exactly what he goes over during class and what are on his online practice tests.  No surprises, no stress.  Studied for all the tests the day of and aced them.  Brilliant teacher who can really hook you into his storytelling narratives during lecture.  Nothing negative about the man.

By far one of the best professors at Purdue. He wants you to learn, not simply absorb and then regurgitate the information. He is INCREDIBLY intelligent, and knows pretty much everything about anything.

I can't give you the perspective of an average student, since the subject matter is of interest to me. So if you dislike mythology, then stay away. I found this professor to be a good speaker. He even made a few jokes from time to time. All in all, I found his class enjoyable and would recommend it for anyone who has an interest in this subject.
Amazing Professor, funny, engaging, and a passionate scholar.
He is funny, engaging, and always willing to help. Both CLCS 233 and 235 were easy as long as you paid attention in lecture. I never even opened my book.

Lecture is mandatory, and pretty boring. You don't need to buy the books, you can find summaries of everything you need on his power points or through google. His tests are hard, but he gives you a study guide so that helps.
Great class! Great professor! Very helpful, lectures stick to exam material. Overall great experience. :)
Exams are honestly pretty hard, but Dr. Klose evidently wants her students to succeed!
Exams are honestly pretty hard, but Dr. Klose evidently cares about her students and wants them to succeed!
Exams are honestly challenging, but it is very clear that Dr. Klose wants her students to succeed!
She doesn't know how to teach She's a great person but she doesn't know how to teach and she just skims through the slides that's all. And the grading its too harsh

Although Dr. Klose is a very nice person, this has been one of the worst experiences I've had with a professor. Grading is very harsh, as you are expected to write even the most basic steps or equations, which you would most of the time not even know you're supposed to write. Homework is a logistical nightmare. No reasonable decisions made either.

Im conflicted about this review because she is such a nice and sweet person but her lectrues are extremly hard to follow and the grading is such a mess but overall she is a good professor that wants you to do well.

This professor isn't just a "tough grader". ECON 340 is essentially a math class, but you can complete all of your math perfectly and receive a C if your work can't "teach another student how to do the work as well". You're supposed to write a textbook for every assignment, and feedback is vague or nonexistent. TAs were bad, too. Avoid if you can.
so specific, super unfair grading, lectures make no sense and she confuses left from right stuff like that, taught myself better on my own than going to class.

Dr. Klose is lecture-heavy. Her entire class is composed of going over material in a pretty bland way. She's unclear on how much work to show during problem sets and exams. Exams are pretty tight on time.

I had Dr.Klose for Game Theory (ECON 451) and she was fine. I was worried with the bad reviews, but most of the content was not hard to follow. Guessing that most of the reviews are from an earlier period. The exams were difficult, but we were told what to study. This course was more difficult than most but not the hardest I have ever taken.
She needs to take a step back and really consider that teaching is maybe not something she is capable of. Also none of us believe those positive reviews those do not seem genuine.

Poorly designed course. She lets the curve do all the work. You will pass the class as long as you aren't the very bottom. Her grading is the most unreasonable and inconsistent I've ever seen out of all my years of undergrad, graduate and even high school. This is the first and only time I've cried because of class. Avoid at all costs if possible.

Bad lectures, I would ask for help with something, and she seemingly wouldn't read the full question before responding with a nonhelpful answer. Prepare to teach yourself. I repeatedly had to go and protest against her grading mistakes she made in grading to give me my correct score. I should not have to review the professors grading ever.

Don't believe what people say here. She does give lengthy assignments, but she is extremely helpful and will guide you. The hw is necessary and working through the problem sets will help you learn and retain the material. In the end you really feel like you learned something. If you take accountability you will do great.
Super tough grader. She will deduct full points for the tiniest of errors. Also the lectures are awful. Pretty much have to teach yourself

Buckle up for loads of hw. Tests are pretty similar to the problem sets but they're only 45 minutes long. Not a lot of guidance but she does offer help sessions. She cancels them if specific questions aren't sent to her in advance.
Bettina was terrible at explaining things. Without a doubt, the worst economics professor I have ever had.
Lectures and textbook will not prepare you for the homework and tests. Awful class, made me regret my masters at Purdue.

Unless absolutely necessary do not take any course with Dr. Klose. The lectures are very brief, you are expected a lot more details on the tests and homework. The textbook is also not helpful, but she refuses to use a better textbook.

Exams were very similar to homework and sample papers, so if you could do the assignments, the exams would be a breeze. Responds to questions on the Brightspace discussions boards very promptly. Grading was very lenient as well, as some assignments are graded for completion instead of correctness.

There is a lot of homework that is difficult and takes a lot of time, however, the exams are very similar to the homework's, as well as the old exam provided. She also gives extra credit assignments that are worth a lot of points to help your grade.

Dr. Klose is great. Yes, her class is extremely homework heavy and the homework is quite difficult but if you work hard enough on them, preparing for the exams is a piece of cake. Dr. Klose is also extremely approachable and helpful and responds very quickly on the Discussion board. Also, her exams are very very similar to the hw and sample exams.

I am a good student. I have a 4.0 GPA. I have taken calc 3 and linear algebra... I am not afraid of hard classes. I remember looking at the first problem set in Dr. Klose's class and having absolutely no idea what to do at all. She simply does not teach the content. Take this class with someone else if you want to succeed or learn.

One of the worst professors in Microeconomics. Homework is nothing like the lecture, test is nothing like the homework. The textbook is extremely brief, you will get more content from the lecture than the text so dont even bother. She does curve the grades though (no one will make it if she didnt)

The homework is never like the lectures, there is always a curveball that she never shows the class how to do. You have to participate online with other students to collaborate and find answers, and this just makes the process even worse, it is like a Facebook of questions... Her tests are about as hard as her homework, and you'll always fail.

This class is ridiculous. Avoid it if you are just looking for a class to fulfill a credit. Its supposed to be an elective class but it ends up being a class you do too much work for. If this class ever changes back to a traditional exam instead of written exam I would recommend it. It is also one of those classes where its a lot of work for an A.

Great professor, boring class. 2 TAs to grade all of the work and they grade very harsh. You are expected to read a lot of different articles and sections from the text book that could appear on the exams.

Lectures were monotone and unengaging, making it hard to stay focused. Grading standards were unclear, workload was heavy, and grading was very slow. Work is often graded by TAs who provide little feedback. He himself also grades based on how much he likes the students. 
very bad professor, must be going through a divorce

Expect unclear grading standards and limited transparency throughout the term. Assignments required significant effort, but grading and feedback were slow and often arrived near the end, leaving little chance to improve. 
instructor also shows clear bias against students he does not like

Very intelligent professor! Really knows how to explain certain topics and cares a lot about student wellbeing. Plenty of grace for grading periods and ample extra credit opportunities. Avoid using AI, it's frustrates the TA/professor grading. The assignments aren't that plentiful/hard. :)

He's a great professor, very accessible outside of class and leninent with grading. Overall, an interesting course. There is a lot of reading -- but it's a political science class so that is to be expected. I would recommend to take it if you need to take a POL course or are interested in it!
Incredibly smart professor. He holds his students to a high standard, but the material is very intresting. If you do the readings and pay attention in class you will be okay.

This class is a reading heavy class, so if you don't like to read I don't recommend taking this class. Dr. Reed is very knowledgeable within this topic but I feel like if you're not interested in this course it's hard to understand. Overall, an average class if you need to fill a credit.
Dr. Kurtz is a good professor but very monotone and it can be hard to stay focused during his lectures. This class is easy enough and graded by very few assignments.

The class was asynchronous and easy to follow. However, I could barely get through the lectures because they were SO dull, and he had a monotonous voice. TAs graded everything and were the main points of contact. The quizzes weren't bad, the midterm was meh, and we could exempt ourselves from the final depending on our grade.

It was all online. There was a lot of reading which could be difficult to keep up with but there is always the option not to do it. The TA's were pretty tough graders, but Dr. Kurtz was available for virutal office hours and was a very caring person.

I took POL 423 with Dr. Kurtz last semester and this class was basically a repeat with very little original lecture content and assignments. That said, he clearly cared about the material and made an effort to check in on us every week. Grading seemed to be TA-dependent but was fair in my experience. As an online class, participation was optional.

Dr. Kurtz does a great job of caring for his students but be prepared to put the effort in because this is not a "give me" class. Be prepared for the Reading Quizes and take notes on the readings. The papers are not terrible if you put work into them and pay attention to lecture. Overall, very interesting class.

He is an ok professor. The thing I'm not a big fan of about him is he takes forever to get to the actual lecture. One day it took almost 20 minutes, he was just talking about changes to the syllabus and scheduling stuff. He also repeats a lot of the same phrases. Not a hard class as long as you do the work.

Lectures are dry and I struggle to pay attention during class. Moves very slowly through slides and you end up learning very little. Given the amount of reading you never cover all of the readings, only small parts (even though readings can be long). I would not take again personally, class is boring and I haven't learned much
Dr. Kurtz's lectures were not particularly captivating, but his grading criteria was very clear and I was graded very fairly. The class is easy to keep up with and the workload is nothing bad.
Class was easy if you did the readings and did well on quizzes. You can opt-out of the final if you do well enough beforehand. The lectures were boring but he cares a lot about students.
Eh... not remarkable one way or another. Lectures are kind of dull.

*review for POL 323*
Kurtz was interesting to say the least. Pretty easy grader in my opinion if you aren't incompetent and don't know how to format an annotated bibliography. be prepared to put a lot of time into the final project. I stopped doing the readings (he assigns A LOT) bc he covers everything in his lectures & quizzes are open note

Lectures were a complete waste of time, 400 level class but acts like he's teaching to elementary school students. Everything covered was very introductory despite there being prereqs that were supposed to prepare you for this class. Did not learn anything new.
Really good guy, and he cares a lot about his students. If you go up to him and tell him any issues he's super willing to help out. Overall just wants the best for his students.

If you take notes and refer back to them, you will do good on the quizzes. Only two writing assignments if you keep between a B+ and A and opt out of the final. I found the lectures boring at times, but not to his fault. Nice guy, willing to extend things if you have a valid reason. Very fair policies and grading, overall easy and enjoyable class.

Dr. Kurtz was my favorite professor this semester. He doesn't make his projects/quizzes/exams crazy hard, he is a really fair grader, the projects actually line up with the course. He does assign a lot of readings though. He doesn't shy away from talking about controversial topics like racism, because they are a reality in politics. Overall great!

Professor Kurtz really showed how much he cared about his students throughout the course. Reading was not necessary, but important topics from articles were on quizzes. Lectures were more important, and questions always came from things discussed in lecture. Overall, I really enjoyed the class and the professor. Definitely recommend.

Kurtz was a pleasant guy but his grading on tests is inconsistent and he doesnt outline what he wants specifically enough for you to get a good grade unless you give more information than youre asked for.

He did a good job teaching ODEs. His lectures have a few mistakes here and there, but his teaching is very clear and thorough, and you can tell he cares about his students and enjoys teaching the course. I enjoy that he puts a little personality into his lectures

Very nice guy, as a non-math major, I think sometimes he's too smart for me in a way that he goes into things way deeper than necessary, but if that helps you, then it's a plus. He is a very nice guy and cares a lot about your success, and answers questions very quickly. Attendance isn't mandatory, but he also records all lectures.
Great professor!

Steve Bell is without a doubt the best math professor I have had during my time here in Purdue. He is an artist with the subject and is a really good lecturer. The material is tough, however he is absolutely 100% open to explaining them further in his office hours. Would absolutely recommend to anyone to take his class in a heartbeat is possible.

Professor Bell is one of the best professors I've had. He cares intensely about his students. Not to mention he may be one of the smartest people I've ever met and is a very accomplished mathematician. His passion for problem-solving and beautiful solutions has made me enjoy my major even more. His class is a must-take.
This professor made classes interesting and engaging. Course was enjoyable and all the topics were described well making everything feel a lot easier.

I do believe Professor Bell handled the online nature of this class and the entire Covid climate perfectly. He was kind, thoughtful, and above all compassionate. There were clear and concise instructions throughout the semester and very fair exams. Have not had a better experience with an online class or Purdue math.

Bell is a great teacher that cares about his students' well being. He makes very hard concepts seem quite simple. My only complaint is that his lectures have little mistakes peppered lightly throughout the lecture, but the main ideas are always concise.
Professor Bell is the best ODEs professor at Purdue - nuff said

Bell is a great professor and he obviously loves math and is genuinely excited to be teaching calculus. I would 100% take him again because I learned a lot from him. If you do all the homework, do well on the recitation quizzes, and study a lot for the exams you'll do fine. Also this class has a pretty big curve which is nice.

Dr. Bell's MA161 class was the worst experience of my life and made my first semester at Purdue miserable. He hardly teaches the material on the hw/exams and spends most of the time talking about the history of math, which no one cares about. The exams are ridiculously hard. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS IF YOU HAVE NO PRIOR CALCULUS EDUCATION.
Bell's a good lecturer who takes time out of class to answer questions students have. He does get distracted easily, but it's not a problem overall.
Steve Bell is a great professor of Calculus. He makes sure everything is relatively easy to understand and wants you to succeed.

For MA161, Bell has provided online links to his lecture notes as well as his recorded Boilercast videos. Anyone that has bell as a professor will have his website and is accessible to you to live stream his lectures as they happen. Introduces most lectures with a review of last time, and ends with a look ahead at what's next. Fantastic intro to PU
Great, funny guy that really tries to ensure that you understand the material before he moves on. Study for the tests though or you'll beg for death.
Great professor that makes math interesting and lecture fun. Makes a lot of jokes and thoroughly teaches the material and makes sure you understand it before moving to the next topic.
I had a different instructor last semester on MA527. They are both good. I have say MA528 is much tougher than MA527. Don't take it unless you need it !

Bell really likes the subject and makes it as interesting as it could be.  The textbook (Kryzeig) is big, expensive, and not one you can just read and work independently (fairly worthless).  Took it through distance, Bell went way above and beyond to have convenient office hours and answer questions on "Piazza".  Bell is a good teacher, liked him.

Prof Bell is clearly passionate about the subject and this translates to an effective teaching method. He is process driven and succinct. Material is not too tough depending on experience, but you'll probably have to work for a good grade.

He's a good guy but he really tends to talk around subject matters. He seems more concerned with teaching the history of the how certain equations and laws came to be rather than actually teaching how to apply them. It's still interesting but regardless it doesn't help you learn the material very well from lectures. Had to practically teach myself.
Comical with his remarks. He knows what he is teaching and does a really good job at it.
An absolutely outstanding professor, made this course highly enjoyable and far more interesting than your average calculus 2 class.
Awesome guy, brilliant, highly recommend

Professor Bell is terrific. The material is hard if you've never had it before but he's always willing to answer questions. Puts the notes online too which helps if you fall behind (he writes kind of fast). Overall a great professor, would definitely recommend him.
Took the course via distance learning.  Overall Prof. Bell was very helpful and understanding.  Subject matter was difficult at times but I didn't exaclty put a lot of effort into it either.

Dr. Hauske is a very kind and fun professor. He genuinely loves what he teaches, and it shows. Just do the reading and go to class to do well. Assignments were fairly easy as well, along with a lot of extra credit offered.

Professor Hauske was a great professor. If you take his class you will love it. The only thing is: he is a tough grader on papers, and you have to read all of the instructions. If you don't, don't be suprised if you  fail an assignment. Also he has some semester long assignments, try to do those early, because a lot of stuff piles at the end.
Grades heavy but doesn't tell you what he wants. Weird essay prompts. Kinda rude.

Great professor! Always looked forward to his class. He's funny, friendly, and easy to approach. The lectures are super interesting if you get into literature and deep topics. If you have a good class. it makes it even more fun. Writing isn't bad but there are 3 big papers, and you're expected to read a lot (though he doesn't care if you don't).

Wonderful professor. He is genuinely excited about the content he teaches and you can tell. While he gives more assignments than some of the other SCLA 101 profs he is a fair grader and gives lots of extra credit opportunities (which helps if you suck at his quizzes like I do).

Professor Hauske does an excellent job at engaging the class and I never found any of his 50 minute lectures boring. There are three main writing assignments throughout the semester with a few easy reading quizzes peppered in between. You also need to do one 5 minute presentation.

He provides ALOT of extra credit opportunities so don't worry alot!

Most of the class time were spent discussing with peers. Prof.Hauske host a positive environment for students to share their thoughts on books, short stories, and films freely. The assignments weren't really hard to do as long as you are willing to put effort in. He was helpful during office hours and gave great feedbacks on papers.

I loved his SCLA 101 course. A lot of reading was involved, with a moderate amount of writing. He does not help you write better, and I wouldn't expect to get As on papers unless you're already good at writing. Discussions were always fun and he was an understanding/forgiving professor.
Professor Hauske does provide a lot of extra credit opportunities, so it's hard to not get an A. I am not interested in Liberal Arts at all, but this class was funnier than I expected!

Professor who is passionate about what he does. Lots of extra credit assignments. Most of class is discussion. Issue is that he doesn't really explain what he wants in essays, and we never do writing activities where we can actually improve our writing. My writing skills have not improved in this class. Other than that it's a fun class

This class is basically useless. If you are good at writing take this class, but if you want to improve your ability to read or write stay away from this professor. Prof Hauske does not teach you anything. Everything is about reading, nothing about essays but he expects you to compose essays at a high level. He grades very harshly.

Professor Hauske was my favorite professor last semester. SCLA 101 was very reading heavy, but it is not impossible to get a good grade in the course if you don't read every chapter of every book. Reading quizzes are hard to do without reading, but they aren't worth a heavy grade and there is a bunch of extra credit. He is very nice and cares a lot
Easy class
Very interesting topics! Complete the work on time and you'll get an easy A!

This was the easiest course I ever took. There were weekly quizzes, weekly discussion posts, and a final exam. The quizzes were all easy and directly based on the textbook. My only complaints were that the due dates were confusing and I had to buy a $95 textbook.

This was by far the most easiest class I have ever taken. Most of the times I forget I enrolled in this course. Your grades are dependent of quizzes, discussion posts and responding to 2 people. I never used the textbook, however, theres bonus points for buying it. Again, really easy class. Keep in mind, the prof and the TA barely answer your email

The professor and the TA do not care about you. They never answer any questions in the discussion designed for Q/A. This course is also poorly structured with due dates being wrong and confusing. Grading criteria is also blurry. One of the worst class I have taken in my life.

This was one of the easiest classes I have taken so far. It was an online course. Each week consisted of a short essay submitted via blackboard and then feedback to two other classmates. Super easy! There were 8 quizzes. These were a little difficult and I think some questions on the quizzes were never in the reading. Extra credit helps!

Lots of homework, group discussion is graded based on your English level and your grammar, makes this class like a English course, except his English is too poor to be a English teacher. and the topic he gave us were posted 2 years ago. For circuit he will only give you one day to finish, if you miss one section of circuit, you lose all credits.
Easiest class ever. Only a 8 week only class for 3 credit hours. Take EAPS 120 online if possible. No exams and quizzes are online open book.

Prof. Howarter is very disorganized and slow to provide grades/feedback. When he lectures, I get the impression he is seeing the lecture slides for the first time, even though he has taught this course before. Fortunately, he is a lenient grader and gives plenty of time for assignments. He is also very friendly and approachable.

Very chill professor. Talks about his passions with materials and engineering frequently. Just come in and listen and you'll do fine. Extremely simple assignments that take little effort. It's a good way to do what the course says - (get an) intro to materials engineering, but it would've been nice to know a little more about the field of MSE.

Incredibly easy class, but I learned absolutely nothing! Professor Howarter was so unorganized and would cancel class 20 minutes before it actually started. Class felt like a waste of time to even attend.
Couldn't care less! Worst of the worst! A non-sense of a professor
I would rather take a class in an actual department with an actual professor. Pathetic class and professor in a non-existent department
Shady professor in a shady department! Never take him!
Professor Howarter is very caring towards his students and a very easy going professor. Talk to him if you are not doing well in the class, he is ready to help you by any means.
WE ARE DONE LIKE YESTERDAY'S DINNER
Amazing professor and person. He enjoys what he does and it shows!

LOVE CRAIG! He is the HK dad! He starts class off with music and dad jokes! He loves what he teaches and is good at it. He actually makes me want to go to class, and enjoy it! Craig is very caring and supportive of all of his students. He strives to have a personal relationship with everyone. If you have a chance to take a class with him, DO IT!!

Professor Voll is an incredible professor. All of his students adore him and he is very personable to everyone. For example, before every class he plays music and says hello to every student who walks through the door, he usually knows them by name. He always has a smile on his face. I will be taking another class with him, he is amazing.
Professor Voll is a wonderful person.  This is my favorite class.  He gives out candy, and the class is actually entertaining.  Please take his class.

Dr. Tan was one of my favorite professors at Purdue! She genuinely cares and was always a light during difficult times. I took 4 classes with her and wish I could have taken more because I genuinely learned a lot from both her + the discussions the class is designed around. If you get the chance, would recommend!

Dr. Tan is an incredible professor who is very educated in her field. Her class is easy: although it requires you to participate in group discussions and projects, they are not difficult whatsoever. The only thing students may find "difficult" is to keep up with the weekly readings. Take all of her classes! She's amazing!

One of the best professors I have had here at Purdue. This class specifically was not a lot of work and was more about group discussions. You still have to do the work but she is very lenient about due dates. I would definitely recommend this class and professor!

Great EPICS professor who cares a lot about the students and the EPICS projects. He's always willing to help and looks out for students' well being, which lots of other professors don't do, and he often gives great feedback for presentations.
Very sweet and caring professor.

Andrew is the best! I've had him as my EPICS professor for 3 semesters, going on 4 in the spring. He genuinely cares about his students, wants them to succeed, and will support them in anyway that he can. He is very serious about class participation though, and doesn't like when students aren't actively doing work during lab. Chill, funny, good guy

Honestly, one of the best IE professors at Purdue. This class does require a lot of work as there is a new project every week but he genuinely tries to make everything easy by giving cohesive instructions on how to get started. Grading was easy and its very hard to get a bad grade in this class unless you miss projects.

Prof. Martinez is honestly one of the best professors I've had. He is super nice during office hours. Like, genuinely cares if you're stuck and will take the time to help you debug models, animations, or even bigger project ideas. You can tell he's passionate and really wants students to learn, not just get a grade.
IE-370 was my favorite course this semester. Prof. Martinez made the classes fun and helped me a lot during office hours. He is a very nice guy

I honestly don't understand how anyone can think Prof. Martinez's exams are hard. He's extremely clear about what's important, gives practice exams, and even provides flashcards to help you prepare. This semester, none of our midterm or final exam averages dropped below 70%! Easily one of the most organized and caring professors I've had at Purdue.

Prof. Martinez overall is a teacher than wants you to learn the content properly and do well. His exams are generally fair, but he made a final exam irrelevant to the general course with a Final Exam average between 30-40% due to the midterm averages being 85%. I've heard that this isn't the first time he's done this so I'd be aware for the future.

Prof. Martinez makes IE-370 feel less like a requirement and more like a class you want to be in. His passion for teaching shows in every lecture. Assignments are fun, exams are fair. Just go to class and stay engaged. Amazing professor.

IE-370 with Prof. Martinez was a highlight of my senior year. He's not only knowledgeable but also a gifted communicator. You can tell he genuinely wants you to do well. His exams are fair, and he tells you exactly what to focus on. If you pay attention in class, you'll be more than ready. Take him if you get the chance!

One of the best classes I've taken at Purdue. The practice exams and flashcards made prepping for the final so much easier. The lectures are engaging, and he often includes real-world examples and fun anecdotes that make complex concepts stick. If you attend class and listen to what he emphasizes, getting an A is very doable.

This course lacks supporting resources and is heavily lecture based. No study guide or practice exams were provided, even though other sections received them. The professor doesn't respond to emails, and I had to take the final without any idea of what to expect.

One of the best professors I've had at Purdue! He makes even the most complicated topics feel approachable and is always happy to answer questions. The class is hands-on and packed with cool projects (especially if you're into tech and design). Be ready to put in the work because there's a new assignment every week, but it's worth it.

A great class! Professor Ramses does a great job keeping lectures fun and interesting. The course goes over a lot of 3d modeling techniques and animations in a short time frame so make sure you have some time to work the 3d modeling. There is one assignment due by the end of each week and cover something different. Overall a great tech elective!

Professor Martinez runs a tough but rewarding class. The work can be challenging, but he explains concepts clearly and is always willing to help. Using resources like Unstuck study alongside the course materials really helped me succeed.

I failed this class 3 times, once under dr. martinez but frankly I think he is an extremely fair professor. He explains things very clearly but he's a no-nonsense guy. He wont make any special accommodations for you but he treats everyone equally. Definitely go to office hours foe help. Also he does great reviews before exams to help you prepare.
Take 370 with him if you want to learn the material. Not that hard to get an A if you just show up and pay attention consistently.
Professor Martinez is the best professor I have had at Purdue. He explains topics in ways everyone can understand. There is nothing bad I can say about professor Martinez.

This has hands down been my favorite class at Purdue so far. Martinez is a tough, but engaging professor, and really pushes you to have a deep understanding of the content. Exams are tough, projects are time consuming, but if you are interested in manufacturing, 100% take this class. The time and challenge is well worth it.

It's ridiculous. The homework was unhelpful (10 min quiz, 5 multiple choice with no feedback/partial credit), the exams were difficult and tedious, and you could ask the TAs for help but they were condescending and rude. I am retaking this course right now with another professor and it is so much better!

Professor Martinez is the most helpful professor I've ever had. He's always willing to go the extra mile to help me or my friends. The review sessions that Professor Martinez offered really helped me understand the material better, so I did not find the course difficult.
Extremely tough and specific grading in exams, does not curve. Classes are interesting but not worth getting a bad grade when it is an easy A in fall.

This has not been my best semester and I did terrible in my first midterm. Prof. Martinez was very supportive and helped me get back on my feet by giving me many tips to study and answering all my questions. I ended up getting a good grade on the second midterm and the final exam.

Professor Martinez has amazing lectures and will teach the material effectively. The grading can be difficult but if you go to class and listen, he does actually state his expectations, and follows through on them.

Dr. Martinez was always available during office hours to help me with anything I need. He is also very understanding about personal problems and always makes time to listen. I learned a lot this semeter thanks to him.

Martinez is the most helpful professor I've ever had. He's always willing to go the extra mile to help his students. The review sessions that Prof. Martinez offered really helped me understand the material better and do well on the final exam.

The review sessions Prof. Martinez offered were so helpful! They really prepared me for exams. He gave us tips to memorize tough concepts that really worked. Also, Prof. Martinez is the fairest grader I've ever had, I went to see my exam with him and he took the time to understand my work and gave me back the points that the TAs took for no reason.

Professor Martinez is one of the most caring and helpful professors I have had. Although he is a strict grader, he makes class very interesting and is more than willing to help clarify concepts. Piqued my interest in manufacturing, and would definitely recommend taking this class with him, it might not be an easy A but you will remember the content
He was an okay lecturer and had some cool demonstrations. I found that the best way to learn was just to read the textbook and go over the standards from there. It's a lot more specific to the exam.

Fun and interactive professor, but strayed from necessary topics, did not effectively cover all material, and ran out of time frequently. Left a lot of skills for me to figure out on my own via homework and Organic Chemistry Tutor. Would recommend finding a different professor if available, but not the worst option.
He cares about what he teaches but, didn't always go over everything we needed

I disagree with people who say he's too monotone. He's less energetic than the professor who teaches the second half of this course, but I like his calm lecture style and he explains the material clearly. He's organized and we cover all the material in class so you don't have to read the textbook. I highly recommend him or Ms. Miller for CHM 115.

I had Nash for the first half of the semester, and although it was difficult to keep up with the slides and writing notes in lecture, he covered everything needed for the exams and I didn't open the textbook once. He starts each class with rock music and does interesting in-class experiments, it is absolutely worth it to do the tedious note-taking.
i fell asleep every lecture

Dr. Nash was okay overall. His lectures are monotone and boring sometimes but he covers practice problems and concepts and explains decently. The class isn't hard and the grade drops are helpful but make sure you understand the material and do the homework to prepare for exams.

Chem 116 as a whole is terribly unorganized, so be prepared to have no idea how you're doing for the entire course. He seems chill as a professor and definitely a very nice guy, but his lectures are so monotone and boring I almost fell asleep several times.

His style might work for some people, but it just didn't work for me. He seems chill and his demonstrations were good, but he is just not a good teacher. Lectures consist of him rambling on and on in a monotone voice, with no indication of which information is actually important. Spends too much time on theory and does almost no practice problems.

Nash is a chill teacher, couple of demos through the semester. He moves super fast through material though so we were ahead of the exam schedule by quite a bit. Good teacher though, would take him again.

I do think his voice is a little monotone but overall he speaks clearly and I am usually able to understand what he's talking about. Gives lots of example problems. Sometimes content on homework is ahead of when we cover it in lecture. Not accessible outside of class - one office hour a week and it was during the beginning of my lab for the class.

personally didn't like his lecture style. very mumbled + slow but did have the occasional demonstration. Often skipped showing the work/explaining practice problems in lecture, and would speed past slides he thought we should know. prefer prof Miller and RK
He was a very good professor who explained things with many real life examples and helped me grasp chemistry better.

I couldn't understand anything he taught or lectured about. I tried so hard but couldn't I would recommend the other CHM professor cause they are much easier to understand. He is like train and does not stop at all. He is a nice man it just couldn't chemistry with him.

Dr. Nash was just okay. You could tell he was way smarter than the class and didn't want to be there. Definitely learned a lot more from my TA and just doing the assignments. He's pretty funny at least.

Dr. Nash was a funny guy, but he was really bad at teaching chemistry. I had to go to the chemistry lectures taught by the other professors in addition to his to understand what was happening. He didn't teach the 400 check rule to his class because he "didn't like it." He also didn't teach how to calculate E° cell the way it was shown on the exam.
seems like a funny guy. mostly just reads off the slides. the labs are the most annoying part about this class. homework and exams aren't bad if you can understand the course content.

Funny guy but boring and uneventful lectures. He's insanely monotone. Brags about his pool and car which is kinda funny. Overall, the class is carried by Boilerexams and recorded lectures. If you must, I'd suggest attending/watching Dr. Schmidt's lectures as you aren't required to go to your assigned section.

Overall, this course can be very difficult, but if you ask for help and go to TA office hours, you can succeed. In the spring semester at least, each week they give you exam problems on Friday instead of lecture, and these are super helpful for exam prep.

The lectures were so boring for me that this was the only lecture I've ever taken where I didn't sit front row because I didn't want to be disrespectful and be obviously falling asleep from boredom. Class is easy either way and it's embarrassing that I got an A- (I bombed the final). Dr. Nash's voice is more monotone than Siri for me.

From start to end, Dr. Nash's lectures were chill and knowledge-packed. The vibe/environment was chill and the presentations were organized and understandable. Additionally, the demos were enjoyable and the overall flow, in terms of syllabus content, was smooth sailing.
Professor Nash's lectures are engaging, but he tends to skip example problems. Luckily, Unstuck AI and the free textbook were helpful for practice and staying on top of the material.

Professor Nash while being a decent lecturer was extremely boring and more importantly, would focus on the parts of the topics which didn't even come in exams. Exams were weird and focused on numerical questions for theoretical topics and theoretical questions for numerical topics. Even the TAs couldn't figure out what he was doing with the exams

Nash's lectures were straightforward and clear. They do feel a little slow and monotonous sometimes, but the examples and his explanations helped me understand chemistry way more compared to when I was taking CHM115 last semester. Some humor is sprinkled throughout lectures and he is always open to helping. Cool dude.

I took PYSCH 12000 Online, relatively easy course with plenty of drops. There are videos posted for the week with small quizzes built into them--you can retake as many times as needed. Discussions are the only assignments with a hard deadline. She had time for her online students on top of in person students.

Teaches well, great professor but WAY TOO MUCH coursework in general. Classes 5x a week, weekly assessments, weekly readings + weekly required discussion prompts. The contents of the assessments themselves aren't hard at all but the sheer amount is overwhelming

Nice lady and meant well, but made absolutely everything 10x more difficult than it needed to be. I had many friends in the in-person section of Psych 120, and they didn't have half the work all semester that Erin assigned in the WEEK that I spent in the course. I had to drop it was so horrible. Take the in-person section if you can. Avoid this one

Dr. Sparks was amazing! Highly recommend taking Psych 120 with Dr. Sparks. The class was very engaging and fun! Coursework is very manageable and straightforward. Does not take very much effort to succeed.

Dr. Ward's class was very engaging and thought-provoking. The class format is super flexible, so students can attend in person or synchronously online. Attendance is taken, but some are dropped. Exams are online and open note. I definitely recommend her class to anyone.

Great professor. I took PSY 120 online, and her lectures were captivating and funny. She had a great attitude, and if you participate in the discussion posts and watch the lectures, you'll get an easy A. She makes psychology interesting!

Dr. Ward was by far one of my favorite professors here. I took her PSY120 online class and I was stressed at first that I wouldn't be able to understand her concepts and do so much reading with an online class, but the class was pretty easy. There's a lot of "busy" work and readings, but her lecture videos are super helpful and easy to understand.

Dr. Ward is very passionate about her job and gives you all of the tools to succeed. The online course is homework heavy, but it is all doable. I will warn though that the individual SONA research credits that are required outside of class can be hard to complete (but that is not her fault).
PSY120 online can be time consuming, but it's super easy and interesting. Her videos were great and she explained things very well. All exams were open note and open internet.

Class was very well structured and there were so many opportunities to earn extra credit. All tests/exams were open book and open note and none of the assignments were graded harshly so its very easy to just focus on actually learning the material without being stressed.
Super easy and fun! As long as you do the work you will absolutely get an A. I took this class online, but Dr. Sparks seems like an amazing teacher, I would have loved to take in person.
GOAT Psych Professor. Easy class and W lectures. As long as you have like 2 brain cells to just pay attention to whatever she says tests should be light work.

This class had SO MUCH EXTRA CREDIT. She also has an asynchronous option but I went to class most days. Professor Ward is a great lecturer and explained the course material well. You should at least be able to get an A in this course!

She is truly amazing. You may feel a little bit flooded with the homework of this course, but believe me, her recorded lecture are GREAT. She makes me love psychology and wants to learn more about it. Her videos are hilarious and she makes sure you don't get bored by the content. She makes me want to take an in-person class so bad.

A queen. I have her online and her class is so doable and she makes it so fun and easy. There should be zero reason you do not do well in her class. She is great! She really isnt that easy to reach outside the classroom, but I haven't really needed to for important things. I recommend this class with her, online. Easy A.

Ludicrous amount of work for a 120 level 3 credit hour. 40+ pages of reading, 5 25 min videos with quizzes, online forum discussions, 50+ question assessments every week. This class takes as much effort as 5-6 credits.

Erin is a great teacher who is very passionate about what she does, but the workload for the credit hours is downright awful

Prof Ward is one of the most caring and easy going professors I have had at Purdue. This class is made up of weekly smartbooks, weekly quizzes on mcgrawhill, weekly discussion posts, and 3 exams that follow her slides and study guide. She takes attendance for each class but also has so many opportunities to earn easy extra credit. Very easy A.

I'm sorry but her voice was so annoying that I could no longer show up to her classes and I had to read through the slides on my own. Like everything she said made me cringe so much that I wanted to drop the class but I needed it for credit so I had no choice but to stay in her class. She also talks way too much about unimportant things

Psy 120 online was comically easy. Dr. Ward explains the content very well and as long as you read the book along with listening to her lectures you will ace every exam and assessment. I never studied for this class once.
Fun class

Amazing lectures, best class ive ever taken! She is really a master in psychology because you can see it from her passion and understanding of her field. Fun fact: she would incorporate tv shows such as the Office and Big Bang Theory to make her points! Doesnt get any better than that!!!!

I really enjoyed having Prof. Ward. Although the class was fully online, her lectures were engaging and fun to watch. Homework was very straightforward and the book is helpful. Quizzes and tests are straight from the book and her lectures, so doing well isn't difficult. Her study guides are the key to success in this class.
Dr Sparks is THE best professor a student could ever ask for!

A really caring professor, I didn't really put much effort in the class but I still got a pretty good grade in the class. I recommend going with her for psy 120 if she is available. There is a bunch of homework and quizzes weekly alongside videos but it was manageable and as long as you did a little bit of work every day you can do really well.

Best Professor ever. Very passionate about what she teaches. Although the class has a lot of assignments, its worth it because Dr. Sparks makes them seem so fun. You can easily ace the class if you complete her study guides. Also, she incorporates amazing shows like the office and big bang theory to her lectures. Doesnt get any better than that.

I really enjoyed my time in Cliff's MGMT 254 class. I would definitely recommend taking MGMT 254 with Cliff, as you learn so much in a fun way. Tests are 80% of your grade, and they are online, and 20% is participation, which is just going to class and doing the "project" in the first 5 minutes of class.

Genuinely cares for the learning of students with a mix of good humor and clear communication. You read textbook / watch lecture videos before class then discuss the material in class. There are short quizzes at the start of class to check if you read/watched the material. Would 100% recommended taking with Fisher.
Great classes and very interesting/interactive and useful. This guys the goat
Lectures are both entertaining and insightful. The content you learn from this class is both applicable to your future job and personal life.

Cliff was probably my favorite professor at Purdue. Really helpful in and out of class. Definitely take 529 if you are planning to go to law school because I learned so much about law school and the legal profession that I would not have known

Cliff is a great professor and is very generous with the curves for each exam. Attend (almost) all classes because he gives 2 questions in the beginning of class for attendance (but all graded if you at least did it, not necessarily the right answer). For his exams, mainly watch his lecture videos and take notes, and read the book.

Great guy. 80% of MGMT254 was exams and the other 20% was attendance. He will pick on you in class if you don't have your name tag up. If you're on his good side, he leaves you alone and enjoys asking you for help when doing class assignments. I got an average of 67% on all exams but the curves are GIANT because many people fail. Great guy overall.

Prof. Fisher was great. Loved coming to his class. He curves his exams a lot because they are so difficult and because of that I expected a higher grade but until the final grades came out I didnt understand how heavily he enforces the participation grade so make sure you go to class.

Cliff is a crazy & passionate. To me, I think he's intimidating b/c he cold calls & if you aren't on his good side, he'll lowkey pick on you the whole semester--so be on his good side. Show up to class b/c lectures are heavy in material and the stuff he talks abt will be on the exams. Exams are difficult but there aren't any assignments, so do well
Cliff was great! Highly recommend taking a class taught by him, he's an extremely great lecturer and definitely makes coming to a 7:30am class worth it.
by far my favorite professor that i have had. great lecturer, hilarious, smart, and engaging.  absolutely loved this class.

Cliff has a great instructor and teacher! He teaches in a very unique way that does not include PowerPoints or lectures. The class consists of 3 exams and a research paper that is 25% each. The class requires a lot of reading, but it is a class you should take if you plan to attend law school.
I love this man, he is so nice and clearly loves his area of work. Lectures are nice, go to them, class is an easy a if you pay attention and take notes.

Fisher is an amazing teacher! Your grade is mostly tests, with quizzes, and 1-2 assignments. Everything is open note so take good notes on lecture videos and during class. Go to class- things discussed in class will be on the exam and the quizzes will take place here. The class is very interactive so come prepared.
TAKE HIS CLASS. Class is pure discussion but its never boring. If you do the reading, watch the lectures, and actually try (emphasis on actually), you will have so much fun with this course.

Professor Fisher not only as a professor but as a person values the idea of wisdom very highly, which he states as "taking knowledge and turning it into something useful." He highly encourages involved students to take their learning seriously and his tests greatly follow this idea. This class is not an easy A, you will have to actually learn here.
Amazing lectures, clear grading criteria, gives good feedback.

Cliff is an experienced and fun lecturer, I learned a lot from his class. Encouraged to watch lecture videos before coming to class as there might be pop quizzes. Exams are tricky but you can prepare better by reading and researching the articles he gave, and asking him questions about the article before exam.

Fisher is a very interactive professor and this is not a traditional course. Be prepared to participate in class almost every session. There are four exams, and in-class quizzes throughout the semester that are worth 20% (Other exams are worth 20% each). Take good notes on the lecture videos as this is a "flipped" course, and videos are first.

Great lecturer, must pay attention in class to really understand everything. He tries to make law into a more udnerstanding topic. Everyone in my section just loved him. he will answer any question you have after class, and emails back.

Cliff is a really great professor. He made a sometimes confusing subject very interesting and more understandable with his use of everyday life examples. This class was easily my favorite class I have taken so far!
Read the articles and youll do fine

I LOVED Prof. Fisher's lectures. They were always entertaining and I looked forward to listening to them. I took the 4 week asynchronous summer class, and we were grade consisted of 4 exams. The exams tended to be tough, but I found that reading the chapters and listening to the lectures helped. Overall, I really enjoy this class and Prof. Fisher!

He is such a loving professor. He only takes 4 exams 25% each. No homework, papers, or projects. He also curves. A textbook is required but honestly just watching the lectures is enough. He explains complex law concepts with great (and funny) examples which makes his lectures easy to follow.
Do NOT take this class!!!

Professor Li clearly knows what he is talking about in reference to the course content but is not very good at actually teaching the information. In addition to bad lectures, the difficulty of the exams was increased out of no where halfway through the semester for no reason.

Poorly designed class. Ratcheted up the difficulty for no good reason other than to see students fail. 10 labs + 2 practicals + 4 exams makes for an incredibly time consuming class. Verilog is not taught at all, his lectures are quite difficult to understand and he tends to ramble. The labs are extremely long, but not difficult conceptually.
Not much i can say, very bad lecture (he tries his best but not enough), I dont understand him at all. It makes such a fun class boring and not fun anymore.

I really dislike junfei li, he isnt clear at all never explains things well and I dislike how messy his class is. its so demotivating being in his class because I just cannot understand a single thing he talks about. It makes me not want to go to class and find it a waste of time if Im just going to watch lecture videos from another lecturer.
He just doesn't speak english

Professor Li cares a lot about the course content and is good at explaining concepts when students are confused. However, the course was a bit disorganized at times (homework posted late, quiz in syllabus wasn't scheduled until very late in the semester). Overall, he's a good professor and makes getting an A very doable. He also gives extra credit.

His cadence and abrupt pauses can sometimes make him hard to understand, but he explains concepts extremely well. He strives to help students and is fair with practicals and exams. The slides are self-sufficient, but the transistor chapter of the textbook is helpful if you haven't taken 2k1. Some self-learning of SystemVerilog's syntax is helpful.

Dr Li is an engaging and decent professor with his lectures being pretty informative. That being said, the difficulty of the exams and lab vagueness can definitely make ECE270 a nightmare. All in all, be prepared to teach yourself and practice SystemVerilog hard for the lab practicals and really understand the concepts for each module.

The difficulty level of this course has visibly increased, especially in terms of the exam difficulty. Moreover, the content covered in class is very poor, and it requires a lot of time for self-study.
The exams were tough and no curve was given
ts pmo gang
I honestly loved every minute in Junfei Li's lectures. He brought a unique perspective to the material that I wouldn't have gotten just from reading the textbook, and was so much fun in class.

Professor Li is an amazing instructor and I learned a ton from this class, probably more than any other 200-level ECE class I've taken. The homework assignments can be pretty tough but they do help you learn the material well. The class is also generously curved, so even though I was worried about not getting an A I ended with an A+.
Professor Li is a poor lecturer, but you don't have any other choice if you plan on graduating in ECE. So suck it up

I don't like Prof. Li's lectures. But he is a supportive guy if you go to his office hours. Exams are not very hard either. Choose the lab sections on Friday so you have more days to complete the labs.

Professor Li has been the funniest & best professor I've had during my time at Purdue. His lecture style is to briefly explain concepts first and then jump into lots of informative examples. Initially, some people might incorrectly discount him because of his accent; however, once you get used to the accent you'll realize how amazing he is.

Dr. Li is a very enthusiastic lecturer and clearly is passionate about digital design. He gets the whole lecture hall to answer questions (and occasionally, count in binary). Some of the slides were a little disorganized and occasionally lectures went on tangents, but overall a good experience. Exams were fair and resembled the HW.

His lectures are super funny and he gets the whole classroom answering questions- even in the huge lecture hall hes in. he can be difficult to understand sometimes but there are slides online and videos from previous years. his TAs are also super nice

Professor Li has a great understanding of the content he teaches, he struggles to imbue his students with the same knowledge as him. He was very energetic and it was fun to watch him lecture. His rating would have been a 4/5 if not for ECELabs, which made the workload almost unbearable in a class where so much work is already required.

Prof. Li knows how to work a room! Feel like at times he rushed through material, but he made each lecture interesting and engaging. Labs, homeworks, and exams were also very reasonable. Definitely one of the easier ECE classes, but would 100% take it with him again.

Do not get me wrong, the class is very interesting and is very laid out for you to do well and the professor himself is really adamant on making sure his students do well and is very beneficial during office hours. However, the main issue comes during his lectures due to what seems unorganized slides + his accent can be a little rough to understand
Great guy. Not a very good lecturer. Lectures don't stay focused and topics aren't explained well.

Prof. Li is awesome. ECE 270 has fairly simple lectures with fairly difficult labs, but he will answer all your questions if you have them, delaying lectures however long as needed. Understanding him with an accent + a mic that easily peaks can be difficult at times, but you get used to it. He actually cares about students learning. Love this guy.

Really great guy, makes learning some pretty tough topics relatively easy. Lectures are a little tough to understand, but that was mostly his mic. You can tell he really cares about his students. Breath of fresh air in a really tough semester.

TAs were also great.

Junfei Li was one of the most kind and caring professors I have ever had. He was often available outside of class hours helping students in the help room and providing insight to problems on piazza. He is clearly very knowledgeable, passionate, and cares deeply about his students success and understanding.

Dr. Laskin is kind, funny, and genuinely cares about her students' learning. She and the TAs are always willing to help and emphasize true understanding. Although the course felt unorganized and experimental at times with labs and new exams, her dedication and support made the experience fair overall.

course is designed in complex way while it doesnt have to be this confusing. Course is boring, slide and hw is enough. Lab makes you angry. You don't get the answer and you get punished 30% of lab grade for "inaccuracy" yet different machine in lab give you different result on same sample. 
worst class taken and greatest workload in a single class
The most terrible class I've taking at Purdue. Like everyone else said, don't take this class if you don't have to.

I chose this class for my chemistry minor, which was the worst decision. I am in engineering, and I have never had to do so much work for a class. The lectures are also extremely boring, and the lab reports are actually ridiculous. The final project takes forever. Everything is horribly designed, and I wouldn't wish this class on my worst enemies.

You can tell that she is enthusiastic about the material but the class is poorly constructed and the TAs are less than helpful. Good luck when you get to Solver. No one knows how it works including Laskin. If you have a question about how to do anything they'll tell you to just do it. Overly paranoid about AI use. The whole course needs overhauled.
I would rather crap in my hands and clap for an entire power. If you ever have a question in this class she will tell you to google it by the way.
