Easily one of the worst classes overall. Every assignment is extremely tedious with unnecessary restrictions and minimal instructions. Students expected to have and demonstrate skills with no training on those skills in software that is older than the students using it. This class should be redone or taken out of curriculum immediately.

I will never understand why analytical chemistry courses in particular are so poorly constructed. The TAs and professors can not seem to structure a class that is not rife with foreseeable errors. Material is terribly tedious and seems overly obsessed with proving that it is in fact useful, which I doubt.

This course is hands down the worst chemistry class I've ever taken. The grading criteria are completely unclear. The workload is overwhelming—I spend hours every week on pointless assignments. It's unbearably tedious. Unless it's a required course, don't take it. This class will kill your GPA.

Mid prof, sloppy notes, boring lectures. Do the Parte and study early to do well on exams. This class is unreasonably brutal, but that isn't up to Dr. Laskin. Dr. Laskin does her job well, but doesn't really try to exceed. I prefer Dr. Li overall. She also defiantly dislikes when people leave after iclicker, even tho she says she doesn't care.

Mind-numbing course that will make you hate analytical chemistry. Laskin herself is a fine lecturer, but the setup of the course is absolutely abysmal. The homework is simply bothersome and not helpful, while the lab reports and exams are graded with such pickiness. Hopefully this isn't a required course for you.

THE TAs are literally awful. My TA literally treated us according to her mood. The labs are very bad too. Equipment faulty and we were penalized points without proper explanation. The professor is nice but the TAs need to do better. They made me hate analytical chemistry although I used to love mass spectrometer (I doubt it now.)
She was a fine professor. Nothing terrible or amazing. She was available to ask questions to. Her explanations were pretty clear. I did better in CHM 116 than 115.

All content is easy. If you can do the dizzying amount of mind-numbing busy work, you'll do well. Weekly homeworks ~3 hr/week, Weekly Lab + reports ~12 - 36 hr/week, Take-home exams ~20-80 hr/exam. Content-difficulty of a 2 credit, 100 level course with 6-7 credits worth of straight busywork.

The TAs don't understand the labs and exams so I don't know how we are supposed to do them accurately. Dr. Laskin is nice but the class really poorly designed. Lab equipment faulty and you can't tell how accurate your results are but they grade on accuracy. Impossible to get an A unless you're extremely lucky. Worst course I've taken in my life.

Absolutely no quality control in lab. Multiple experiments didn't work and we were punished harshly for the grades thatoutside of our control. If you don't mention one arbitrary thing in each section of the lab report that isn't told to us, you get 0 credit for the section.
Awful course, the most difficult course I have ever taken in my whole life. I spent 20 hours per week on this course, I think I got depression because of this course

Professor Laskin is extremely knowledgeable in her field and tends to have pretty decent lectures overall, that being said sometimes she can be a little difficult to understand due to her accent. Expect to also read the textbook outside of class to supplement her lectures as at times she spends too little time on certain topics.

Professor Laskin explains concepts in chemistry very well. It is evident that she cares about her students (often holding review sessions for exams when no other professor would) and she has a passion for chemistry. The labs in CHEM116 can be a pain (mostly the lab reports), but Laskin ties in lab ideas in lecture to help us out. Great professor!
There are many writing assignments in this course, they are frustrating and difficult and everyone got a low score. Do not take this course.

Overall a challenging class, definitely geared towards more chem major students (which I am not) which can make things a little hard at times but really just sink as much time as you can in and you'll do ok. Having a good lab group is also really important, being paired with low-effort people can really ruin your grade for the labs.

Dr. Laskin is nice but her class is unbearable. The instructors are inaccessible and when you ask a question they say "what do you think?" and "we cannot answer because it is in the course" We aren't allowed to talk to each other and an unreasonable amount of work is required for only 4 credits, as if we have nothing else going on in our lives.

You have to work on the labs, papers, and hw for 15+ hrs a week and if you ask for any help, the prof and TAs will tell you "I don't know" because the "cheating" policy is inane (they'll hide students' Qs on the online forum). They also don't know how to use solver, doesn't teach you it, and then marks points off if you do not get it exactly right.
Dr. Laskin is a cool professor and she really cares about whether you understand the topic. This course can be very challenging but she and the TA team are very willing to answer any questions.

Professor McKinney's teaching style is poor. Fast paced, reads off the slides, all work is done before class and briefly shown. Not much reasoning or conceptual conversations within class. Questions are are answered with no explanation and jargon heavy. Exams are arbitrarily over the top difficult, and don't reflect anything.

McKinney explains course content and practice problems clearly during lecture. His exams were more difficult than Al Othman's but they were reasonable (similar in difficulty to textbook problems). However, the final had a few mistakes (there was no answer on two MCQ questions), but he gave full points back for both questions.

His midterm 3 was designed to deceive you and induce mid-test anxiety so you ended up performing worse and no longer able to solve questions you once could with a clear mind. Glossing over small details when studying lead to failing entire questions. The final was better but still had several tricks. Cutoffs were only lowered by 1%.

His exams were actually insane. Run. Had Al-Othman and halfway thru McKinney replaced her and he singlehandedly brought everybody's averages down. A 45% average on exam three is DIABOLICAL after a Bish average on the first two exams. Idk how he decides what to put on these tests but they're not indicative of any of the material we get. Hated him!!!

Feel he was put in a tough spot based on the current 2k1 situation , but he teaches like its his part-time job. Barely responds ,or answers questions extremely vaguely and his exam average of when he came in was a 40 compared to high 80s in the first two- maybe thats a course related complaint but not sure.

Always just reads through his powerpoints in class without providing much examples. Doesn't feel like he cares about our grade, when I go talk to him during office hours, it simply feels like he does not want to help.

In complete honesty, he is very neglectful, has low effort, and has no compassion for teaching. I struggle to find a worse example. Said to me in person that there are too many people in the class for him to give a damn about a particular student. Like me.

He doesn't give two shits about people's struggles. I asked him to fix my exam grade since I had the right answers written down squarely on the paper with work to support, but the bubbled choice didn't reflect that. Thus, he refused. His reply was that 1000 people are taking this course, and he doesn't care if you fail or not. His exam avg: 40
Bro has curved his classes down before
Very nice guy, willing to help if you need help. He cares that you know the material and makes him self available.

I feel like Professor McKinney had a lot of potential in his lectures and he knew the content well, but honestly he would click through the content too fast. His best lecture was when he finally did the lecture without a slideshow and had audience interaction. Overall, pretty decent choice and would be great if he lectured like his last lecture.

2k1 is a hard class, but doable. Exams are difficult, but if you go to lecture and pay attention the class is passable. Make sure to put in extra time outside of just doing the homework to make sure you understand. McKinney's lectures are mostly helpful, but sometimes he answers too many questions and breezes through explanations but otherwise good

Somewhat unengaging lectures; though if you ask questions, answers are good and helpful. Responds to emails quickly and is a fairly chill professor. Lecture attendance in Fall 2024 wasn't mandatory; lecture slides were posted on Brightspace as well (other professors lectures were there too).

He definitely knew what he was talking about and was knowledgeable in what he was teaching. I do think some of the exam material was more so meant to trick us rather than testing us on what we did in homework and in lecture. Lecture notes are definitely a bit messy with some inconsistency in notations. I gave him a 2 because he really was ok.

One of my favorite profs so far. Knows a lot and is very open to questions. He makes everything super clear, be prepared to work hard though to understand the material (those who don't end up complaining the most).

Professor McKinney cares a lot about his students during lecture. He will stop and answer every question anyone has even if it makes the lecture go over time. Sometimes, this leads to us having to cover content outside of class. The 2K1 exams for Fall '24 were really easy, difficult, and mostly difficult, in that order.

Knows his stuff but isn't too great at presenting it on slides which makes it hard to follow. Sure you can say the slides are fine or alright at best but I would never say they were ever good or great. He's good with answering questions. Exams seemed to have tricks in them rather than testing what we actually did in lecture and homework.

Good lecturer, content and slides are well organized and well structured, explains concepts well and answers questions. My rating stems from the exams not being created in good faith, 8 multiple choice options, every question felt like a trick question. Exam 2 have an average in the 50s AFTER giving partial credit and removing bad questions.

Prof. McKinney is a good instructor for 2k1. Slides are well-organized and easy to follow. He does take many questions during class which sometimes disrupts the flow of the lecture, but not a big issue overall. Very helpful in office hours. Exams were difficult (typical for 2k1), but they are the same for all sections.

He may seem like he would be a good professor to take 2k1 with based on his reviews. DO NOT BELIEVE THEM!! He had failed over half of the class the semester I took 2k1. It was so bad that he was taken off of the course the following semester. His lectures are confusing to connect to the homework problems and the exams he wrote were excessively hard
almost everyone FAILED

I was easily able to schedule a meeting with him when I was worried about my exam performance and I felt like he gave me good feedback that drastically improved my next exam score. My final grade is more of a testament to 2k1's difficulty rather than his ability to teach, as I feel like he was very good at explaining all of the concepts.

Good lecturer with deep understanding of the topic, kind, and thoughtfully answers questions. Semester was curved up mostly because he made the second exam too hard, but told us he would account for it. In my opinion, answers too many questions in lecture which doesn't always let him get through all of the material.

Jason McKinney loves what he does and wants his students to be successful. His experience makes him an amazing guide into the world of the music industry and his personality is great too. He's funny, charismatic, and great at what he does. 10/10 would recommend.
Very friendly, down to earth, helpful.  Genuinely tries to help students.  HOT!   Exams were a bit difficult.

Adding my perspective from "the outside" as I took this as part of the Finance minor but my major is outside of Daniels. Many of the reviews here are too harsh imo, if you attend class and pay attention you'll be okay. He definitely talks in very technical/corporate language (good for learning imo also). Friday zoom is also great.

I'm not going to lie this dude is not as bad as people say, it's a very hard class in terms of the material is a lot and people give him a bad wrap for that. Does teach at a very high level and can sometimes go very fast during lectures. But cares about his students and is a nice guy

When you consider Craig Brown used to teach Advanced Corporate Finance, then you'll understand why this course is absolute hell. Pretty sure he just recycled the material from a master's program course. Craig is absolutely brilliant but I'm not sure he has any idea how to teach.

Stay away from this prof. Lectures are very dry, mostly him reading off slides. Sometimes comes off as rude/annoyed when you ask questions. Participation makes up 20% of the grade (which seems good but then you realize the rest % is exams) but he doesn't keep track? Told me he "just remembers" which doesn't seem fair or realistic.

Stay away. Craig is not a very good lecturer and a majority of the points come from the midterms and final. He also has the tendency to be somewhat rude in class and does not make the environment very inclusive for learing. The cases were kinda fun.
Craig is literally a king. Exams are def hard though. Zoom class on Fridays which was a plus. Can see why people may find him intimidating but he's actually super nice.
He does not go over the topics thoroughly as he expects you to know the material without ever teaching it. Not approachable and his lessons are not engaging.

The rest of the reviews are very telling for Professor Brown. I am suprised Purdue admin hasnt seen this and looked into his teaching style. Basically, he teaches by going at 2x speed through things he expects that you know to teach complex topics. He does not blend the theory and math very well together and makes things very difficult. dont. take.

Professor Brown is extremely disorganized. He puts material on exams that we never learned, and the study guides are useless. No answer keys are posted, and lectures aren't tied to textbook chapters. Homework isn't reopened, so there's no way to review or improve. You'll end up teaching yourself everything just to keep up. Would not recommend.

lectures are very confusing and hard to follow and do not aid you properly for the exams that he writes.  I genuinely feel like I understood the content when studying for an exam but would give questions on the exam that are nothing to do with what we learned. Brightspace page has no information. Cold calls sometimes and is rude and unapproachable

One of the less enjoyable courses I've taken at Purdue. He does not seem to be trying to help when asked, as previous review mentioned, refuses to show clear grading standard, refuses to give homework and test answers, practice paper is pretty much irrelevant from actual test. Allows cheat sheet, has a take home final that is in the form of essay.

Tough grader, exams are based solely on his lectures, exams does not reflect the practice exam he gives out. Participation is 20%, but when asked how he keeps track of that, he stated "I have a good memory". Monotone lecturer but definetly knows his stuff.

Professor Brown does very little to actually help you succeed in his class. I am a student who loves a challenging course when it is because the material is difficult; however, Professor Brown refuses to upload homework answers or exam question answers and does not leave notes from his PPTX. Would NOT recommend to anyone.

As long as you have the attention span of a 10 year old, you'll do fine. Most of what was on his exam he reiterated in class multiple times, you just have to pay attention. He's also quite funny, and isn't mean when he cold calls and you're wrong.

He does not know how to explain topics and expects you to know everything he is talking about. His exams have nothing to do with the study questions he gives you and overall does not prepare you for the exams.

Do not take this professor if you can help it at all. By far the worst professor I have had in my academic career. He makes the exams near impossible to finish in time and does not even show up for the exams on time. He gets mad when people don't understand a topic he just introduced and is not helpful when trying to get more help after class.

He is knowledgable in the subject but cant teach it at all.  He makes class very unpleasant to attend, for the first test review session he kept saying "oh man since everyone says the same answer I guess its too easy and I should make the exam harder." nobody knew what was happening and we were all struggling.  He is just very difficult to approach
lectures were very hard to follow and the exams were impossible. Homework is on Chegg though

Lectures are very confusing with the content being confusing and then becoming more confusing the more he talks. 2 midterms 15% each and a final case 30%. Very tough grader and very unhelpful when going to him for help. You will not know your grades until after the class is completed. Overall very terrible class with a very terrible professor.

Without a doubt the worst professor at Purdue. He does not explain topics clearly and assumes that you know everything already. He gives two exams which are only a few questions that will be marked wrong even if they are right just because you didn't write it how he wanted it. Participation grade is 'his opinion' on what you should get. Avoid this.

The lectures do not prepare you whatsoever for the exams and he is very unreliable in explaining good detailed explanations to your questions. Final is take-home and worth 30% of your grade. Expect 8 pages of work to earn you a D.

The wrong textbook is in the book store, so you either have to order the McGraw Hill textbook or use the online version. Not a huge deal but kind of annoying. He is not very clear. His participation grade is ambiguous. He jumps right in assuming you will know where you are at. Overall, not a good experience.

Do not take his class, even you think you are the top students. Just don't try!! Do Not Try!!!!!! I got full credit on the first exam. After that, he covered all the grade and I received a B at the end. I didn't even know how was my second exam and final report. Terrible lecture and rarely replies email.

Would not recommend at all. The grading criteria is unclear, and questions are also not worded clearly. Exam reviews and study guides are not useful, as the exam prep sessions & material will be completely different from the actual exams.
DO NOT.... UNDER ANY CIRCUMSTANCE.... TAKE HIS CLASS. Lectures are too complicates and exams are next to impossible.

I loved Dr. Hart's personality! However, assignments consistently opened up late, and due dates were very hard to keep up with. The case studies are nearly impossible, but aren't graded too terribly hard. Attendance is not mandatory, and skimming the video lectures is more than enough to keep up with the course.

Dr. Hart is great and willing to work with students! She makes biochem fun to learn and has a great personality. While her lectures don't include many practice problems, I found that looking up questions or using my MCAT question bank helped reinforce course content. The only thing I can critique was the TAs opening up assignments late.

Dr. Hart is a fantastic lecturer. Sometimes, though, it seems like her homework and quizzes (but not exams) were out of date? Or at least not updated to reflect the material she covers in class. Sometimes it wasn't in the textbook either. Just small inconveniences like that are the only issues with this class.
Exams are online, so if your wifi goes out and you fail, it doesn't matter how much you know the material or studied. In class, the professor is fun, though.
Prof. Hart is a fun and kind lecturer, but the majority of the course can be done online. It is a relatively easy A if you keep up with the assignments (3-5 Brightspace quizzes/week).

Very unorganized. Was late on posting the exams and assignments. She is a nice person, not a great teacher. She talks like she is on 2x speed and does not have the best lecture slides. There was a lot of info on the exams that were never talked about in class. The exams are extremely wording and give you no time to process the question.

Dr. Hart was my favorite professor this semester. While her lectures were not mandatory, they were always fun and interesting. Lectures were mostly helpful for case study sessions, as Dr. Hart and the TAs were available to help. She is often difficult to reach over email, but if you ask a question in person or follow-up, she is very willing to help

Definitely a good professor, but I think the class is challenging. The advice I would give is to read the textbook and know the content before coming to class, and be engaged. Incrementally studying will ensure that studying for the exams is not a chore. Exams are not a joke and are synthesis-based with some route memorization.

She often asks random questions in quizzes that are not covered in class or her lecture slides. you can entirely understand biochem at a 1st semester level and still lose sevral points. cannot switch between questions on her quizzes/exams either, so you can't manage your time(why??). also unclear/ incorrectly graded quiz questions. nice ladyatleast

Dr. Hart is a very understanding professor. She gives plenty of exam prep and extra credit opportunities. The only issue I had was that her lectures relied heavily on examples, and it was hard to figure out the general trends that we were expected to know for exams and quizzes.

Dr. Hart breezes through lectures, so do readings before class!! The exams had recommended time limits on the questions and did not let you go back which made them tricky. Even if you knew the content the real-world applications made it difficult to prepare for. Overall OK class, just wish she slowed down and elaborated on important things.

Professor Hart is a wonderful woman and very passionate about biochemistry. Unfortunately, her exams were extremely difficult. I did not fail this class only because she gave a good amount of extra credit towards the end of the semester. The exams were long and in paragraphs and you are given less than a minute for each question to read and answer.

Dr. Hart gives amazing and interesting lectures and she really cares about her students. Some of the exam questions can be tricky but imo very doable. Mostly they require you to think analytically about biochemistry and sometimes require consulting outside sources. Nothing compared to engineering exams but you cannot get by on pure memorization

As a person, phenomenal. interesting lectures and a great speaker. Prepare to be assessed over things never discussed and have NO TIME to do so. Very long, wordy questions that cant even hardly be read in the amount of time given. its an unfair assessment of knowledge with such little time given. "goal is to learn while taking the exam"

Dr. Hart provided plenty of extra help on the brightspace page. However, she was extremely hard to get ahold of. I took this class over the summer and I emailed her more than 3 times and she never responded. I had to send the email to the TA to get an answer.

Nice prof. Generous grading scale with a functionally optional final. There's a decent amount of work each week, but nothing excessive. Quiz and homework questions sometimes feel nitpicky or poorly worded. Case studies took months to be graded, despite numerous promises from the TAs. If you've done cell bio/genetics, this class is 85% review.

Dr. Hart seems like a down to earth person, but her class is unorganized and had a numerous amount of tech issues which caused confusions with due dates. The material that she would go over in class is completely useless for the actual exams that you take and tend to be fairly difficult.

Solid professor, and passionate about Biochemistry. Dr. Hart is super down to earth and easy to access outside of class. This class can be a lot of work!  2 homeworks and quizzes each week. Exams are online/open note, but can be hard! You will still need to study  in advanced for them! Definitely learned a lot, great MCAT and other classes prep!

Dr. Hart is one of the coolest professors I've ever had. However, this course has lots of workload with up to 2 study guides, pre-quiz, and post-quiz due each week. Exams are open-note but somewhat difficult. It's very easy to let the work pile-up in so make sure you stay on top of it.

I took the online summer course.  Expect to cover 2-3 chapters per week.  Each chapter has a pre-quiz, a study guide, and a post-quiz.  I highly recommend watching the lecture videos (on 1.25 or 1.5 speed) and filling out the study guide as you go.  Exams are tough, but open note.  You may be asked to discuss your exam afterwards, so be prepared!

Will never take this course again even I got a C. My 3.95 GPA was totally ruined by this course. Can't understand lecture cause professor spoke too fast. A lot of homeworks and a lot of materials for a week. Homework and post quizzes are outside range of the knowledge talked about in class. Homework was graded very strictly.  Extremely hard exam.

I thought that this class was super easy and a great introduction to biochem for the MCAT. The lectures are all online, the office hours were always on a new interesting topic (optional). The exams are take home with an emphasis on applying knowledge to new concepts. The only bad thing is that exams and hw take long to grade.

Extremely unorganized class (no information on when homework, quizzes, exams and final exam were to be released/due until hours before). Professor did not have office hours, and some TA's wouldn't show up to their office hours.
Completely inaccessible via email. 
Grading occurred very slowly.
Tested on material that was never discussed or taught

TBH I thought this class was cake. Exams are open notes and resources and she encourages you to work with others, as the problems require understanding and application of concepts, not regurgitation. Was lecture heavy over summer w/ 3 50+ slide powerpoints a week. No A+ given either, which is annoying.

Genuinely speaking, you will be hard pressed to find a professor who is more down to earth and understanding than Dr. Hart! Not only does she treat students like adults who have real world problems, but she also has such a truthful passion for teaching students about biochemistry. She will help with problems and doesn't judge you for getting lost.
It feels like it's never good enough for her.
She's too opinioniated when grading, doesn't grade fair.

Has very unrealistic expectations for an 8 week course. Her grading and comments almost seem AI generated. She dances around what you actually need to work on and truly grades too harshly for an entry level english class.
Dr. Bawa is literally difficult. She gets happy undergrading your work.
Does not give feedback on assignments

Dr. Bawa gives an unrealistic amount of work weekly. Her course organization is severely lacking. I am frequently left searching for content in obscure folders. I feel exhausted and frustrated trying to navigate her disorderly blackboard site before I even start the assignment. She supplies minimal support and assignments are unclear in direction.

I liked the other comment on here about her being passive aggressive. Tries to belittle you, not very professional to do so. Hard grader and not an effective communicator. I have never rated a prof before because most are good and I am a good student but this was trash. Please switch to dif section at Purdue it will save you time and worry

She's actually not that bad until you have a bad idea, which could be anything if you ask for an explanation she vaguely explains what you did wrong then if you ask what she means she fires back with an answer and some form of passive-aggressive comment "let me dumb this down for you" and "it's self-evident, don't you think?" Avoid if possible.

I took this as an online class for Ivy Tech, and for the most part, the teacher is absent, she doesn't respond to emails, and will give 0's with no other explaination that its "late" for assignments that are literally 5 minutes overdue. I understand taking off points, but a 0 on a 5 page paper is absurd.

I'm not surprised that she already has a low score. Please Please unless you are an English major avoid her! She takes points off left and right. I worked with the tutors who approved of my paper and she basically told me to scrap it because it was so bad. This is my first time evaluating a professor because I have NEVER had an issue. AVOID HER!!!!

I took her On-line class at Ivy-tech. It's the worst class I've ever taken. Useless PowerPoints, No commends for drafts and stupid grading. For an assignment I worked for several days, only because I submitted one or two minutes late, she didn't even read it and just give me an zero. I learned nothing from her, change your section if you got her.
Just two words: she's horrible.

This was a frustrating experience! She doe nothing to create a positive learning environment. Does not provide corrections on assignments but seems to take of points like it is a sport.  She depended heavily on peer reviews and discussion boards but offered no feedback other than MLA/APA assignments which are easy to grade. Avoid her classes!

Have Technical Writing online. Have never received the poor discussion board grades before. With this class, I have yet to have a good grade on them. Points are taken away, yet never told what was wrong with the posts or assignments. If I don't know what is wrong, how can I be learning? Can't correct mistakes if I don't know what the mistakes are.

i am taking her class at ivy tech, and ivy tech told us to buy a book package for like 80$, i did...and a week before class, she emailed everyone saying to buy totally different materials...and i could not return the book package that i had already bought.....thanks lady. :( just now starting her class, but its note a very good start in my opinion.

She is a HORRIBLE professor. She doesn't help you when you ask for it, and she's very bias when shes grading your papers. I called her out many times about her lack of help and she reflected that in my grades. I will suggest to all of my friends at Purdue to not take her class because she will screw them over if they speak their mind.
i never complain about teachers but she is the worst teacher i have ever had. its all busy work end of story

Ignore some of the negative comments here from other students; they probably didn't try that hard in class. Papia is approachable and really cares about the subject. If you do too, you will do fine. I got an A. I had to work for it, but what else do you expect?
I had her online at Ivy Tech and she is pretty fair.  There was not a lot of feedback but she is consistant.  I got an A without a ton of effort.

She doesn't teach at all.  Her projects are from the English department, and when you see them for the first time, she is seeing them for the first time, so don't bother asking for clarification on anything as she doesn't know anything more than you do.

Makes the semester a drag. Compared my workload with others taking ENGL 420 and it was ridiculous! Don't expect to get very much credit for your work. She will take points like crazy! Always talks about being concise, but I never completely understand what's going on. Switch sections and get someone else!

She LOVES to pat herself on the back. Refuses to give anything over a 96%..and this is on papers that should be published. Accuses students of forgery based on the number of times they saved a Word do****ent.
She's getting an unfair wrap. I got A's on all the assignments and got a 98 in the class.  Just go to class, and if you don't get it, ask questions until you do, thats all I did.

She is no good.  She dooesn't explain anything.  Her feedback sucks.  She won't give you an "A" on a good paper because there is "always for improvement."  If you get her as a teacher, switch sections
No Comments

She really care about her students and made a great effort to help them learn. However, her exams were a little too simple and policies regarding grading were extremely lenient. This is beneficial for your grade, but I'm not sure if it helps in the long run. Her slides were decent and her explanations were okay. She provided a lot of resources.

Super sweet professor that genuinely cares about her students and made ECE an approachable and intuitive subject. A shame that she had to leave due to her safety concerns and complications in the middle of the Fall 2025 semester, but from what I've seen, she is absolutely your best prof in ECE2K1 no question.
Professor Maryam is fantastic. Loving, caring, kind, funny, helpful and all the great things in the world.
She is the sweetest lady ever, and while she was here made the class awesome.
Class was very fun. Prof is very nice, I hope she stays at Purdue to help future students.
Awesome! Taught me a lot

Good professor, ensures you understand the material. She makes reasonable exams and her office hours are great help right before the exam. This sem she also had 5% extra credit for each midterm which helped.

Best professor at Purdue! She goes above and beyond to provide extra material and practice. She explains topics really well and truly cares about her students. She would spend late nights hand grading every exam by herself, over 1000 exams within 2 days, to make sure students got their grades back quick.

If you go into her class with an open mind, you'll love her. She knows what she's teaching and is by far the best professor for 2k1. Even if you don't get in her section, go to her lectures and office hours. She genuinely wants to help students.

Lectures were pretty good and she focuses on breaking down concepts into plain english rather than getting lost in technical jargon, which I appreciate. You'll never miss lecture because it's hilarious.
A bit disorganized but so many office hours and extra resources, she really cares. (even if a bit too easy)
Funny and cares deeply about students.

Professor Al-Othman is phenomenal. Her lectures are amazing, and she puts in an absurd amount of work outside of class to create extra resources so her students can succeed. Taking any other professor besides Al-Othman for 2k1 is probably the worst mistake you can make.

Professor Al-Othman is a very transparent and compassionate teacher. She encourages her students to learn and makes an otherwise daunting course much more manageable. Doing poorly in this class with her as your lecturer will never be because of how she teaches or a lack of resources; it will be because you don't put in the effort. 10/10 prof
A little crazy but a very good teacher

Dr. Al-Othman is THE GOAT. She made a class like 2k1 (anciently known as living hell) an actual fun class with lots of opportunities for growth. She also very clearly cares A LOT about her students and that is shown in her teaching.

Outstanding professor. It is very easy to meet with her outside of class and discuss coursework. Does well to keep students engaged. She also provides lots of extra notes that prove useful for future courses and related topics.
One of the best professors at Purdue, cares alot about student learning and is constantly making efforts above and beyond most other professors to help students learn.

Despite 2K1 being a very difficult class Prof Al-Othman does an amazing job at introducing material, keeps lectures interactive and entertaining, and it is very clear that she cares about each of her students and wants them to succeed. Highly recommend taking this profs section for 2K1.

Othman was a great professor. I can probably bet everything I own in saying that she definitely cares more than you think about this class. She did every possible thing you can think and ask as your professor. She made study guides, practice problems, and made learning 2k1 enjoyable. Now that she is course coordinator, people will enjoy the class.

Dr. Othman is a great professor she is caring and wants all of them to succeed. Her txtbk and resources she provided were really useful throughout the course and preparing you for exams. One tip I would give is don't get lazy after exam 1. Exam 2 is usually the hardest. Studying more could mean a difference between a C and B for your exam 2 grade.

Professor Al-Othman truly cares about the success of her students. She always worked to ensure we were as prepared as possible, and put lots of work into us outside of the classroom. She provides many resources and brings motivation to her lectures every day.

She truly gives every single resource you could possibly need to succeed in 2K1, and wants everyone to do well.
This class is incredibly difficult (exams ≈ 80%). But if you put energy into attending lectures consistently, do the homework, and go to office hours, she will ensure the material taught connects with what you need to succeed in 2K1.

The professor was very kind and thoughtful. She tried several new methods you help students. The grading scale was awful, they removed the +/- from the scales. This meant that someone who was above average for every exam could still be given the exact same score as someone who would not have passed in past semesters.

She is one of the most caring professors I have every seen. She is willing to do anything to help her students. She is very good at explaining complicated topics in a digestible way. Her textbook (which she provides a PDF of for free!) is also helpful at explaining concepts. She is absolutely hilarious, especially if you like darker humor.

Genuinely the kindest professor I've ever had. 2k1 is a hard class in general, but Dr. Al-Othman is your best bet for getting through. Loves the material, wants her students to succeed, and will go above and beyond with study materials/office hours/outside of lecture help. Rearrange your schedule to have her as your lecturer and recitation leader.

She is a very brilliant and hilarious professor that cares deeply about her students knowledge and growth.  Don't use AI or play games in her class.  Don't cheat, she is very strict on grading and has no tolerance for us taking the easy way out.  One of the best professors in Strategy and teaches material that will be used in industry.

Professor Chiu makes lectures very fun and interactive. She gets students who do not normally participate to raise their hands and gives lots of opportunity for extra credit. Her exams are more difficult than they should be but your grade will be curved in the end. She makes class enjoyable but grading is harsh.
The professor is a total pass from my side, very biased, sometimes says inappropriate things in class. very strict.
Very diffivult to underdtand what she is teaching or even saying

Prof. Chiu genuinely cared about my success and made the class engaging and relatable. She connected with students through fun, real-world discussions. Exams were challenging, but if you show up and pay attention, it will be one of the most enjoyable classes in your schedule.

Really good professor overall, keeps class interesting and funny. One of the most interesting classes in Daniels. Lots of extra credit opportunities too. She wants to see her students succeed. Case studies are really enjoyable to read too.

Her lectures were interesting. she does place a heavy emphasis on participating during class, but it made me understand the material better. She has a lot of extra credit opportunities and curves the exams, although they were difficult. She asks people to share what they think about international topics but was never looking for the "right" answer

worst professors I have ever had. she gives no context in her assignments so when you don't answer it the way she wants you to you're wrong. majority of my responses were correct, but she refused to give me the credit i deserved because I didn't answer it the way she would. avoid her at all costs. she is on a power trip.

While she is knowledgeable in her subject, some class slides included strong political references or a strange sexual analogy that felt out of place in an academic setting and made some students uncomfortable. She asks students their personal beliefs about political topics on the spot in front of everyone. Might not bother everyone, but not for me
Very unejoyable lectures and extremely degrading to students who don't answer in the way she wants
Slow grader didn't get grades few months after assignment were due.

Participation heavy, but extremely enjoyable class. Super funny lectures and I can tell she has fun and genuinely cares about the students. Lots of opportunities for extra credit and you can be as creative as you want. Grades can be a bit harsh but she's more than willing to curve -1st exam averages were low and she immediately curved them. Nice!
Mandatory attendence but overall she's a funny and professional professor.
worst

Professor Chiu might be one of the greatest profs I've had. She has such a quick and sharp wit, and can really make you think about the way you perceive the world. Her grading is tough, but if you get a good grasp on the material you should be okay. Prof Chiu is a solid 11/10 would take again. (You do have to participate, but she makes it easy)

Great professor. She is funny, makes you think, and the class is p easy. Assignments do req you to take them seriously & correct. 8 cases, you can do a max of 5, 4 highest scores. Exams are easy & she allows a note sheet. Participation is on a curve, but it's not as bad as it sounds. If you take her class seriously-ish, EASY A. (~1 hr/wk + classes)

Probably one of the WORST professor I had in Purdue. She doesn't care about her students at all and her participation grade is extremely biased. Her exams are almost impossible since some of the questions are not related to the class content. Avoid her at all costs.

Such a great professor; I wish I was able to take her other classes! She is very clear on her lecture material and is willing to work with students. Funny lectures! You can tell she really cares about her students and wants them to succeed!

Professor Chiu is one of the best professors I've had. As long as you listen and take good notes, you should be able to do well in her class. Also, her class relies a lot on class participation. Hence, make sure you participate well as it will decide your grade. She makes lectures engaging and fun. She is also hilarious. She knows how to teach well

Review for MGMT 459
Professor Chiu is an awful professor, first B ever from straight A student. Her grading is abysmal, she gives out low grades and refused to elaborate on why. Exam grading is blatantly incorrect, after proving an answer was correct, she refused to give points back despite agreeing. Participation grade is biased and flawed.

One of my favorite professor at Purdue. Lectures are worth attending. She is able to make every lecture interesting and relatable. The concepts studied in her class helps when working in the industry. She is really good at explaining difficult concepts and always prepared to teach. Exams are difficult but doable as long as you work hard.

One of my favorite professors at Purdue! Had her for 352 and 459. She is a very interesting person with lots of cool stories and experiences that she shares and makes relevant to the material. She is funny and ensures lectures are not too boring. There is a lot of homework, but it is all relevant to the course, and doesn't seem like busy work.

Wan-Ting is an amazing prof. She made lectures fun everyday. Discussion and participation in class are a large part of your grade. The case studies are fast paced throughout the semester and the exams are challenging. You have to truly understand the course concepts in order to pass the exams. She truly cares that we understand the material.

Prof. Chiu is so funny and knowledgeable.. Lots of in class discussions, group projects, and a couple papers. Class material is not too difficult, but she's a very tough grader. However, if you go to her office hours or email her, she explains things so well and replies fast. Not an easy A but overall good class + great prof!

Best professor I've had at Purdue. Very funny professor, actually made you want to go to class. Materials not easy, but she made them interesting and fun to learn. Loved the cases she assigned and they actually helped relate classes to real world. Not an easy A+ esp if you missed lots of classes, but most of my friends got A+. Take her!

Marsh is extremely passionate about Native American History and wants to inject that passion into her students. She does not give out that much homework, just a written assignment once a week, a book quiz, and a written midterm and final. It was very easy to manage the class, and it is very interesting and thought-provoking.
Do not attempt to skip classes because you will be lost without her. She focuses heavily on Native American stories so I unexpectedly learned quite a bit. She enthusiastically helped everyone.
Like another person said, she teaches mostly native american history vs overall American history. Lectures are long and useless.
Dr. Marsh is great! She really cares about what she teaches and it shows. However... get. ready. to. read. I know that's part of any history course but truly, wow... So much reading!

The course material was very interesting to me. The reading is required and has pop quizzes over it (very easy), but even without the quizzes the book was worth reading. Lectured on Mondays and Wednesdays and then had some image or primary source analysis on Friday.
She gives terrible feedback and isn't much help when formulating your paper. Still I guess she was a fair grader.

Not only was there no helpful feedback given, points were docked for not meeting the exact word limit. The class material was really interesting, but there were no lectures or points, you had to read all the material yourself and write the essays.

Dr. Marsh is incredibly helpful as long as you are willing to reach out! She is somewhat of a tough grader but not unreasonable for an upper-level history course at  Purdue. If you do the work and put in effort you will do well in her class.

Took as first semester senior. Prof Marsh is actually a funny and kind professor the class itself just kind of stinks. Its very dry and you just listen to her talk the entire hour and you almost fall asleep or just lose interest. Youre required to be there on certain days for group discussions: counts for points. And you have two essays. Not bad

This class is poorly taught, especially for an online class. There is no criteria for the papers you write, and she grades them very harshly, leaving rude and unhelpful comments. The only way I got full credit on the homework was to write about 800 words per question. I liked the material of this class because it was interesting, but she ruined it.

Above this section to write is a group of words and phrases that best describe this professor. It is lacking inconsistent feedback, does not provide specific examples for critiques because those are two things she does really well. I avoided doing the work b/c you never know what will pass and what wont, even if youre writing is consistent.

Grading was inconsistent and often delayed; her comments weren't very specific or helpful. Sources assigned for each learning guide were interesting and relevant. The class isn't difficult, but the sheer amount of writing and the tough grading can get pretty tedious at times.

Each week consisted of a chapter of reading and then five essay questions of 250 words. Although there was no grading criteria, if you answered the question fully and used some quotes, you'll get a good grade. No tests, but a midterm and final paper that are made up of two essays that are 750-850 words. Overall an easy A if you do the work

Literally the only professor here at Purdue that I have really had to work to impress and obtain a good grade on assignments. Her grading is intense but it makes it a better challenge, I wouldn't take this class if you're looking for an easy A. However, I would take it if you are wanting a little more challenge out of college classes.

The only material we are given is a very bland book and an even more bland documentary series which we must use to answer 4 learning guide questions every two weeks(one due the day after Thanksgiving). It's two book chapters and a nearly 2 hour documentary per LG. Grading is inconsistent and it is hard to contact her over email. Would not recommend

Took this class online.  Thought it would be interesting, and it was.  However the grading was very inconstant.  It seemed like the graders would give you 100% randomly, and then every once in a while they would rip you apart.  VERY slow at getting back papers.  Still waiting on a paper I wrote at the start of October.  Its almost December.

For the quizzes you really have to do the reading and remember the details. There are only 3 papers for the whole semester so don't wait until the night before, you will regret it. Go to her for help with your paper, she'll give you resources and likes to see you use them.

The lectures were very boring, and although she was teaching American history up to 1877, she made most of the lactures about native americans, because that is her specialty. TA's did most of the grading, and I did not find it hard to get an A.

Fantastic professor and very interesting subject matter. If you attend class, actually do the work (including reading, note taking, writing) you will do well. Anyone saying that she "grades hard" is probably because they did not put enough effort into the class!

She gives interesting lectures, but her grading is the hardest I've had. Her notes to you on your papers aren't helpful, they come off with a very "are you really this dumb" type of vibe.  It's frustrating because I get A's or B's on other papers, but never hers.

Dr. Marsh is one of the best professors I've ever had at Purdue, she is tough but fun and really knows her stuff. I wish I had found her prior to my senior year! Finally a professor who wants you to learn something useful to being a historian, like research, in a library! Gasp!
Sarcastic and hilarious. Was a bit tough on the exams, but overall was a good history class!

Professor Pandey was really great; I learned a lot from his lectures and found them to be very easy to follow, and he always took time to answer questions. The exams were also very fair and not hard. He was also really nice which is great!
Doesn't put anything online which is annoying. Doesn't force attendance though which is cool, and the tests will have mostly fair questions
He is an amazing professor. He comes to each class prepared and every lecture is very engaging. He is very friendly and approachable. I looked forward to every lecture.

Professor Pandey is (likely) the best professor you will ever have. He's super passionate about math and teaching and makes his classes really interactive and even fun to attend. His exams aren't hard, but don't be fooled, you will walk away with a very deep understanding of the material if you follow along his lectures and put effort into the hw.

Professor Pandey is one of the best math professors I have had. I'm not that much of a math person, but Pandey helped me understand Linear Algebra. He taught the subject and used examples in class so you could see how to use what you're learning. The exams were extremely fair and followed a similar format to the questions asked in class.

Pandey will take time to ENSURE that you understand, but you have to let him know whenever you are confused. Syllabus moves at a very manageable pace. You MUST study the hw, notes, and ATTEND to get A's on the exams (very doable for anyone), but if you have a good understanding, you are at least getting a passing grade. No trick questions on exams.

Professor Pandey is perhaps the greatest math professor I've ever had. Attending the lectures has truly been so helpful, as they're very interactive and well-structured. The homework (out of the textbook) is definitely helpful, and reviewing HW and notes is definitely a must to do well on the exams.
Absolutely amazing teacher. Extremely caring towards students and passionate in what he does.
ABSOLUTE GOAT.
Very good class
He is very good at teaching the idea behind a concept. He is also funny sometimes

Easily the best math professor I've had at Purdue. Takes the time to explain topics and does lots of examples to help you understand the content. Comprehensive review before exams that prepare you well. Always responds to emails and has office hours to help when needed, and doesn't shame you for it. TAKE MATH WITH PANDEY!!!

Professor Pandey is a great, thoughtful lecturer. He wrote everything out alongside us so tempo was not too rushed. Every class began with definitions and content, then 2-4 practice problems that he went over. There was never a concept on HW or exams that Pandey did not cover clearly. No attendance required, but go to his class!

Prof. Pandey a great math professor. Explains concepts very well and also encourages discussion about problems in lecture and participation.
His homework and tests are pretty fair. Only thing I wish was different is that MCQs/True-False questions are worth too many points.
Otherwise, great prof. Take his class if you can.

One of the best Purdue math professors. He is super clear in everything he says, and explains everything extremely thoroughly, even going into simple proofs of the concepts that we are learning. Exams are fair, and he takes feedback from the students after each one as well, and adjusts accordingly, however questions' weights could be adjusted.

really good professor. teaches slowly and clearly. his exams kinda annoyed me because the short answer/mult choice were 10 pts each out of 100 so if u get 2 mult choice wrong ur alr down to a B-. no extra credit. hes prob one of the best profs ive had here tho he teaches rly well, i just wasnt v good at it.

Great lectures, very clear and articulate when it came to explaining the content. Encouraged participation and emphasized an intutive understanding of the material as opposed to just memorizing certain topics.

Professor Pandey was the best math professor I have had at Purdue, he was the perfect mixture of entertaining and informative. He made complex abstract problems simple and helped greatly during office hours. I would recommend him to anyone.

I think Mr. Pandey is probably one of the best math teachers I've ever had. He brings passion to his lectures and honestly creates an environment that is open to questions. He is great at explaining confusing topics and is more than happy to help any one student full grasp a concept. Honestly blessed to have such a great instructor

Pandey is a very clear lecturer that breaks concepts down very well. It is clear through his teaching that he is incredible passionate and knowledgeable about math. However, it is expected for students to be very proficient in any prior math classes and questions on anything of that sort is often answered pretty dismissively.

Vaibhav is one of my favorite math profs at Purdue so far. His lectures are very bright, and he makes a point of drawing students into the discussion. He gives plenty of examples to show how theorems work and often gives students time to try the examples themselves before going through them himself. Very knowledgeable and passionate about math.

I had him for CHM 115 as the second lecturer and he was awful. Would never get through the material on time because he would talk about unrelated stuff for most of the class. He'd have to spend half the class catching up before talking about new material. Avoid at all costs

Dr. Wei, while knowledgeable, went very fast during lectures, did not offer examples, and wrote exams that were very different from what was discussed in class. Dr. Wei was unwilling to work with students and blamed all students in the class for the poor exam averages. The situation was so bad that he got replaced by another professor.

Worst professor I've ever had. Like others have said his exam scores were below 50% for the first two exams. His attitude towards students is degrading. He wont answer questions in class, and blames students for performing poorly on exams. At one point he told the class to "shut up". Do not take anything with him. He ended up being replaced.

Worst Chem Lecturer. His exam averages were all 40%, he never curved anything, and the other professor had an average of 80%. The TAs didn't even know what was going on. No one knew what was going on, even him. There is barely anything to raise your grade after you tank an exam. He is like actually horrible.

This guy is hands down the worst professor at purdue university. I would say he is the worst professor at any  university. We had two exams under this guy the averages was a 42.5 and a 45 percent. So many students complained they fired him from teaching out lecture and we got a new teacher. DO NOT TAKE A class with this guy NO matter what

The class average for exams was 50%, and he believed it was the student's fault. Even if you followed all of the suggestions of the class (office hours, book problems, recitation), the class was still impossible. He was replaced by a different professor mid semester because of the poor test scores and the failure to work with students.

This was Dr. Wei's first semester in this course, but he assumed that we knew a lot more than we did coming into it. First 2 exam averages were really low & required an insane amount of studying. Book problems aren't for a grade, but he assigns many & claims it is one of the only ways to do good on an exam. Hope he does better in the future.

Alex Wei is by far the worst prof I have ever had at Purdue. I took 256 with him and no only did he refuse to work with the other professor on lectures or exams, but he was a horrible lecturer and is rude to students. Our class avg was 50% while the other lecturer had an 80% and he blamed our failing on us. Even TAs thought our exams were harder.

Professor Wei has been one of my favorite professors this semester. He is super passionate about chemistry and it shows! In my opinion, he was too forgiving and gave lots of extra credit to help the students. As for the bad reviews... it might be because chemistry can be hard for some people but it is not the professor's fault.

His exams are difficult and if he writes a question wrong, he won't admit to his mistake and give credit for that question. His exams also aren't curved even if the class average is really low. His lectures are boring and really difficult to focus on or understand. Don't take CHM 111 with him if you don't have to.
The exams he writes are so hard. In lecture he skims over a lot and then tests us over the deeper level stuff. Do not take this unless you have to.
Just do not take it. Prof Wei knows about teaching and he made a lot of mistakes during lecture.

The lack of preparation for this class, since it was the first time he taught it, would be forgivable but his attitude was NOT. He expected too much and while no one was graded harshly he gave terrible feedback and would not hear opinions that were not his own. The criteria for the course was ignored by the third week making the final grade murky.
Best Chemistry professor at Purdue. If you ask for one on one help, he will gladly provide it, and really help you grasp the material. He is a chemistry person who can also teach, which is rare.
a legend in his own mind

Professor taught a very rigorous Spectroscopy course.  Course material was straight-forward and clear, and was walked through very carefully.  Quizzes and tests were difficult and fair (either right out of homework or what was talked about in class.)  I hated the idea of timed quizzes, though.
He's so awesome...too bad he'll be in England this Fall...
He is amazing, I love him.
I suppose he's a good professor, but his voice sort of fades out at the end of every sentence he says.
Dr. Wei is the best!
Goatmez!!!
Good professor. Take 2k2 with him if you can.

he's super nice and seems to actually care about us. his lectures were good. it took a bit to understand his handwriting, but he records the lectures so if you miss anything you can always go back. his office hours and review sessions are awesome. i wish i could take more classes with him.
As someone who scraped through 2k1, can confirm Gomez is the guy to take this class with. Definitely recommend the textbook and extra problems book to supplement but otherwise great professor!

LUIS THE GOAT. 

Awesome lecturer. Super caring and always willing to help. Very reasonable exams, you can see that he genuinely cares about your learning. Also keeps the monkeys he experiments on happy (allegedly).

Even tho 2k2 is to be a hard class, Gomez makes it manageable as well as does his best to make sure his students are not only learning well but prepares us best for his exams. Attendance is really beneficial bc his class slides are the best resource to study on top of past exams. I did worse in 2k1 then I did in 2k2 and that is bc of him.
Makes an otherwise difficult class much easier.

Take my word for it, Gomez is the guy for this course. He is an amazing lecturer and gives good information about what is relevant for your professional life vs exam material. Class is hard, but Gomez is very engaging and the guy you want. Gomez? nah, GOATmez.
Best of the worst. Difficult to follow in lecture, doesn't offer a lot of solutions if you're struggling in the class, and the class itself is awful. Good guy but not a great professor.

If you are going to take 2k2, prof Gomez is the guy. Although this class is tough, he tries to record every lecture and he will do exam preview before each exam. One time he forgets to record the class, and he will do the class again just for recording. Amazing person and great lecturer.

Really good lecturer, was able to really simplify a lot of really hard topics. However, 2k2 is just a really hard class so be prepared to put in a ton of work. 90+% of your grade in this class is determined by 3 midterms + a final
A+ professor

Prof. Gomez is a fantastic lecturer and a better person. His lectures are concise and comprehensive. He is always available outside of class. The difficult rating is due to bad course management leading to very difficult exams, but this does not reflect much on Prof. Gomez as a lecturer. I highly recommend anyone to get in his section to pass.

Professor Gomez's recitations were the only thing that saved me in this class. I did pretty badly on the first exam   but pulled back entirely due to Professor Gomez's amazing teaching. The difficulty rating of 5 is definitely not due to Prof. Gomez, but due to the difficulty of ECE 2k2 itself.

This was my second time in 2k2, and I probably would not have passed if it was not for how helpful and caring Professor Gomez was. He is extremely helpful in breaking down the complicated concepts of the course, and helping us prepare for exams in recitation and office hours. I would super highly recommend that you take him over Capano or Makin.

The lectures are very easy to understand. The notes are clear and the professor is helpful inside and outside the class. The projects were also valuable and he assisted in those projects as well. Homework is also beneficial.

In ECE 20002 where the class wasn't run smoothly, Professor Gomez came in clutch. This was his first semester teaching at Purdue, and he did outstanding. The class was run on only tests grades, and the given homeworks for practice were horrendous, so the only way I passed this class was Gomez putting in work to make his own reviews and practices.

I don't understand how anyone could rate this professor above a 1. I get this is a difficult course, but blaming students after the class averaged a 44 on an exam is unacceptable. There have been serious complaints made to the department each year he's taught this course, and encouraging students to discuss Christianity with him is inappropriate.

The lectures don't match the homework, the homework doesn't match the exams. The exams are impossible and written by a TA who sleeps in class. Blamed the bad averages on the students instead of taking accountability. Can't pass exams no matter how much you study. Prof. can be demeaning and inappropriately pushes his religious beliefs on students.

He's not that bad of a lecturer when you get used to how he teaches. However, that took a month and the content can quickly get out of control fast because he writes quick. Before doing HW, rewrite all notes in your own words and it will help you tremendously. This class sucks and the TA's don't make it any better, but you just gotta keep going.

To whoever said Ming gives extra credit, just know it is only your class that got a surplus of bonus points due to that horrid exam; extra credit is the bane of his existence. Ming should not teach undergrads, as he's very demeaning when you ask questions or attempt to do better in class (despite exhausting office hours and going to lecture).

Overall, the class is not impossible; it can be done, with the help of God, and with extra credit, you might pass. The lectures are fast and heavy, so I suggested going but only paying attention somewhat. I found that reading through the lectures later on and looking through the book were much more helpful than trying to keep up with his pace.

Ngai's a fine professor, lectures were understandable and study materials were given though the exam averages were a bit low. (Also, a couple times, he talked about his faith and how he used to be skeptical, encouraging people to talk to him about Christianity. It was a little uncomfortable, but I'm not gonna fault him for a few awkward moments.)
The exams can be really bad but dont be too worried because he gives a ton of extra credit. If you need help, go to office hours. He is so funny and really encourages questions in his class.

An interesting professor who doesn't bother to stop teaching new material to teach old material where students could understand former concepts. Goes too fast paced in notes with no regard for students writing down the material or even grasping it.

Does not care/humiliates students asking questions. Exam 2 average was 44% and students received same advice from exam 1. Head TA writes exams difficult on purpose which other graduate TAs disapprove of. No curve provided. Rushes through content in lecture, does not answer questions. Academic advisors had to audit course after students complained.

Extremely Rude to students, Rushes through lecture, clearly does not care about students nor their mental wellbeing. Class averaged a score of lower 60s for an exam to which Ngai responded "I don't understand, this is easy you should've gotten higher than this"

The (rare) times we go over easy topics he slows down but any other material that is difficult is incredibly sped up to the point where you need outside resources to understand any of the things going on. You can have full understanding of lecture topics, homework, lab, etc. and still get a bad grade because the exams are made so difficult.

Bro truly couldn't give a gaf about you. He has failing exam averages and he just says that YOU could be doing more to do well rather than reevaluating his own teaching methods. He should not be teaching an orgo class designed for CHEM MAJORS if a good chunk of people are currently failing.

Ming acts caring in class, but destroys you on every exam then proceeds to gaslight you to show up to lecture and office hours. Could use more Christian values when writing exams. The TAs convince you that you don't belong studying chemistry, and refuse to help or explain material. If you're a chemistry major, life gets better after this class.

Exams in the class are extremely specific and I felt like studying did not help. They also make up a large percentage of your grade so doing well is important. Lecture is unhelpful he skips through the slides fast and gets sidetracked often

The professor was amazing and seemed very passionate about this class. However, the exams, in my opinion, were very hard. I highly recommend memorizing key vocabulary words which he does emphasize during class.
Cares alot for his students. Lectures alot, but is very animated and makes them more enjoyable than some other professors.

Williams is a caring and knowledgeable professor. Attendance is mandatory, and he asks for a lot of participation (he doesn't hesitate to call people out), but lectures usually get out super early. The only assignments are one homework per week. Exams are online, but don't underestimate them because they are difficult and are worth a lot of points.
Lectures are required but very engaging. He will call on you even if you try to avoid it. Homeworks are graded for completion and exams are short and online. Great guy, easy class.
Great professor. Attendance is for points, but lectures are easy. Online exams and connect assignments. You have to participate or he will call on you.

Most of the time the lecture would be let out extremely early which was always great. Towards the end of the semester though I could realize how that didn't necessarily help me as we didn't cover lots within the lecture, reading will be needed along with going through the notes on your own.
Dr. Williams is a pretty easy professor. Class is not hard, and the assignments are easy just 1 per week on McGraw Hill. Exams are meh.
Easy A, he was funny

This class felt like a random survey more than a real class. Although participation matters, at least half of every lecture is the prof. mulling over who to choose/torment when they aren't paying attention. It just makes an odd dynamic, he likes to talk with people rather than really focus on content.

Professor Williams takes a different approach to teaching. He constantly wants audience participation rather than him just lecturing. It's an interactive class although be prepared to be called out if you appear to not be paying attention. He likes to call on students even if they don't know the answer.

Exams and attendance are the majority of grades. He is an okay lecturer but the lectures really are not useful because you have to learn all of the material before class to do the assignment. No absences allowed or you will get penalized. At least all exams are open note and book so you can do fine even if you don't know much.

AAE203 and AAE204 are very poorly run; TAs are not held accountable for how they grade (AAE203 final had some students getting >30% back on regrade requests) and exams often contain mistakes that could have been caught by having TAs test the exams beforehand. Changes made to courses differing from the syllabus very late in the semester in AAE204.

He's okay at teaching. He's the head for AAE 203, 204, and 352 and he likes making the tests very hard then telling everyone that they're not smart when everyone does poorly. You can study 100 hours and still not do well on his exams because he'll put something on there you've never seen in your life.
One of the best lecturers I've experienced. Clearly understands the content extremely well and is able to answer all questions that people have and encourages people to ask them.

He is clear, cares about learning, and explains concepts well. But the course was overly difficult for a class on beam bending. First exam had unfamiliar setups and too many calcs, no one finished, so he added 20%. The second was more fair but too long; average was failing, 20% extra credit assignment was offered. The fair final allowed me to pass

Prof. Agyei is a decent lecturer, however, he presents the content in an incredibly bland way making this class unbearably boring. His exams are often needlessly tricky and have problems that test knowledge not directly discussed in class. Attendance is also required which sucks because listening to the boring lectures at 9:00 am is insufferable.

Professor Agyei is very passionate about the material and it does help in learning it. The only issue is the material is kinda boring so his passion can only take you so far. Lectures were very helpful. I think exams were a little too hard imo based on the practice exams and homework, but office hours would definitely equip you to succeed.

Prof Agyei makes a really easy class really hard. I had to teach myself the lecture content in order to do the homework, because by mid-semester he was 2 weeks behind in lecture. Gives negative points for incorrect multiple choice answers on the exams. Claimed the class wouldn't be curved but then made the final so hard that he had to curve by 5%.

man he is so boring I couldn't take it. And required attendance. He was pretty clear in explaining though. But hw and exams were just strangely tough or a stretch past lecture content. He was much better than the other professor of the semester though. I felt bad bc no one laughed at his jokes, no one paid attention.
Very caring professor who is good at answering questions and will thoroughly explain concepts. Lectures can get long and slow but that is partially the course itself.

Professor Agyei is one of my favorite professors I have had. He is always passionate about the content at hand and does a great job throughout lectures to answer questions the class has. He always leaves time at the end of class for students to come ask individual questions. He will even crack jokes every now and then.

Tried a flipped classroom, which ultimately went poorly and was reverted. Moved slowly through simple concepts and usually didn't complete said lesson by the end of lecture. Seemed to think any students' poor performance was his personal responsibility. Overall not a bad professor; just struggled to figure out a good format for his first class.

Intense and can be overwhelming, but he teaches so thoroughly and gives so much material that you are very prepared, even if intimidated or scared of the sheer volume of material covered in depth. Ultimately his exams and HWs are extremely fair. Very available and helpful. Amazing professor overall. I was apprehensive but glad I took this class.

Took the micromechanics of materials course. The concepts of the course are difficult, but are well explained in the lectures and case studies provided on Brightspace. Test questions were sometimes confusing and did not very closely reflect ideas from the lectures, but were graded fairly and offered partial credit for correct theory application.

Dr. Siegmund does an excellent job facilitating this course. Lectures are highly organized voice-overs of ppt slides. Dr. Siegmund is very responsive on piazza/discussion posts. Course content is well-paced for working professionals. Overall, one of the best teachers I've had at Purdue in 10 graduate courses taken.

Very well organized. So far the best course I've taken in any university. If you are working and studying, this is one of the best courses, as it allows you to self-pace, yet follow a schedule. The material is pretty dense, but he knows it very well and is reasonable with his expectations.

Awesome organization for online students. His "lectures" were powerpoints with voiceovers, separated into smaller chunks by week to help you digest the information better. Exams were open note, which was very helpful since I found exam questions to be similar to homework. Very accessible outside of class via Piazza.

Lectures are structured really well. The content is precise and to the point. A little theoretical to my liking (most problems we solved, were by hand - not a lot of programming), but it is advertised as such, so I can't complain. 

Tests are relatively easy (as long as you don't get tricked by the wordings). White paper writing is equally fun. 5/5

The professor was extremely helpful throughout the semester via Piazza discussion board as well as email. Do the homework and quizzes! He's a fair grader and class was really well organized. Tests were difficult but reasonable and doable.

Great professor and great class. The material is pretty dense, but he knows it very well and is reasonable with his expectations. Doesn't give many practice problems, but if you understand the ones he does give (HW and case studies) you'll be well prepared for the exams.

Prof. Siegmund is a knowledgeable professor and cares about the subject. His lectures were a bit long with tons of slides and often containing non-relevant material. Homework assignments were pretty short compared to other 500 level ME courses. Tests were short and tough. Pedantic grader on paper format but not content.
One of the best ME professors I ever had. He is always trying to make links of stuff he is teaching and the real world. He is very enthusiastic and always very prepared for class.

By far the WORST professor I have ever had in my life. The class cake. I think I payed attention for 20 minutes in the whole class combined. Just copy his notes on a crib sheet. He is rude towards questions, is incompetent when it comes to technology, has the poorest communication skills ever, and gives mean pop quizzes. DONT TAKE A CLASS WITH HIM!

In my four years at Purdue, he is the WORST professor I've had. I completely agree with the other comments. DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS! He should NOT be teaching. Main issues: LANGUAGE BARRIER- both ways, rude towards questions, lectures are him reading his slides, dismisses class late EVERY DAY, and pop quizzes that make no sense.

Read the other reviews about this guy. The ABSOLUTE WORST PROFESSOR I have ever had. Does not prepare for lecture at all, uses PowerPoints from 2001, gives in-class quizzes that make no sense, is difficult to understand, his handwriting is chicken scratch, responds rudely to questions, etc. I don't know how this man is employed. Avoid at all costs!

I was looking forward to taking a manufacturing class since I am trying to narrow down my career choices and I have never been so dissapointed in a professor. I have had some painfully bad professors but never one so bad that I felt the need to write reviews. DO NOT TAKE GARYS CLASS. I could write essays about how bad he was but I only got 350 char

His class was very straight forward with reading the materials and answering questions. He is understanding when a deadline is missed and he gives another chance. The midterm, final was take home with no quizes. Easy A class.
I would give 0 if the website have this choice, worst professor I had ever met in my life. Did he ever prepare his class? He just read the notes for all the class

I've seen dumpsters with better organization than this class. Easy because the homework hasn't changed in 1/4 century,  but Gary gets offended when people don't attend class and retaliates by declaring random quizzes. Exam questions occasionally don't even relate to things we've learned in class. All this is balanced by an easy grade scale though.
