Worst professor of my life! Class was decently easy until he decided to not allow us the use of crib sheets. The time continuum literally stops in that class because it is so boring. He is the quintessential  useless professor in any university. And he gets angry because people dont go to class. People dont go to class because it is a waste of time
Boring. Class is decently easy if you do the homework and attempt to pay attention.

I really loved this class. Professor Cooper is knowledgeable and passionate about the subjects; I didn't find a single lecture boring. With that being said, attending the lectures is important for doing well on the exams. Expectations for each assignment were clear. There are very few assignments though.

So. Much. Reading.
For a class primarily for engineers / other STEM majors, Dr. Cooper does not hold back on the daily assigned readings. Between 40 - 80 pages of dense, confusing, BORING text. The pop quizzes are pretty tough. He basically expects you to understand/fully comprehend the text before its even discussed in class.

Dr. Cooper is very passionate about his field and teaches in an interactive way, making his lectures great. He answers questions well and is fair in evaluating student work. His History of the Nuclear Age course is well organized, but with only 6 assignments (including exams) and no curve, get ready to write some near perfect essays.

Dr. Cooper really pushed the students in this course to produce intellectually rigorous papers, and while the readings were really dense they were also really interesting and intentionally chosen. He was very engaging in lecture. Would recommend for students who want to take a writing heavy and critical thinking course.

Very organized and has a sense of humor. However, he does not seem to be very caring about his students and can be a bit egotistical. One time I reached out with a suggestion and he laughed at what I said in a disrespectful way, even though I said something thoughtful. He also seems to grade based on his mood and how much he likes the student.

Doesn't have any care for students.
Asks some questions in a perfunctory manner for the sake of asking.
Course has a lot of reading for a speech-related class.
Readings would never be read outside of class (boring, depressing). 
Grading rubric is nonspecific.
He literally can grade you according to his feeling that day or about you, which he does.

Dr. Cooper is a fantastic speaker and structures his classes really thoughtfully. He has a real passion for what he teaches and genuinely wants everyone in his class to learn and do well. He's also very easy to communicate with and willing to work with you through any issues that you might have in or outside of class.

One of my favorite professors. Interesting lectures. Readings for class are really long but they're in place for homework. You don't have to do the readings to do well in the class. Definitely not an easy history class though requires some studying. Heavily based on writing assignments, but you don't have to be an excellent writer to excel.

Class is graded on a couple papers (weights differing), a quiz, midterm, and final. Latter 3 are all straightforward if you've been taking good notes and study up a bit, writing well on papers is more of an art, so use OH. Good professor but hope he gets away from lecture heavy class as it's a bit difficult to study for quizzes/exams.
Lectures are sometimes hard to understand but the notes he has on Brightspace are usually good enough for the homework and exams. One of the easier IE courses.
I recommend Dr. Lee. His class was pretty boring since it was mostly him drawing Arena on paper. However, the language is useful and the class wasn't too difficult.

The professor is very clear in what he wants. Homework and exams well definied before hand and although he has an accent, he has very good lectures with lots of examples. This was his first semester teaching PM and it was a good experience.

Prof. Lee is a lighthearted professor with a pretty decent understanding of Simulation Theory. This course under Prof. Lee talks only about the use of Arena for Simulation. Therefore, this course only teaches you the use of Arena for Simulation Modelling. The course has very less homework, and the course is well paced with a lot of good examples.

He is a nice professor. Easy grader and takes in a lot of efforts to teach. He sometimes finds it difficult to communicate, however, you can still understand the material. One of the best courses i took in IE
He is easy-going. He cannot speak English very fluently, but not bad.
Thumbs up!

Really good professor and you can tell he cares for the learning of his students. The lectures are very relevant. Big plus is that there are no exams or quizzes. However don't let that fool you as the class has a beefy workload at times with the group project, labs, solo project, and other assignments. If you take make sure you have time to devote.

Prof. Davis's lectures are incredible and there are a lot of connections he makes to the real world. He cares a lot about his students. However, the grading for the course is very slow given the amount of workload. Feedback is not given on time and grades have not been posted for anything, despite the end of the semester approaching.
One of the best professors I've had at Purdue. Highly recommend taking any class offered by him

ECE 461 with professor Davis might be the best class I've taken at Purdue. His class is structured in a way that really help you get the feel for what an actual Software Engineering role might look like in industry and his lectures are just plain fun to attend.

No doubt at all, the best classroom experience I've ever had at Purdue. Dr. Davis does not teach in a "traditional" manner. He teaches his students in a result-oriented manner that actually preps you for the real industry/academia. He cares for his students, goes above and beyond to make sure that they make the most out of the course. Full points!

Davis was a pretty interesting professor for software engineering. He brought in several engaging speakers and has a lot of good experience and ideas to share. Classwork felt a little heavy at times and there wasn't a good way to rate groupmates who were behind on the project.

Coco may be considered a hard professor for art and design kids, but as an engineer, she is a great professor to have. Not only do you learn a ton, but it also is an easy and fun class. Just make sure you show up to class.

CoCo stands out as one of the best professors I've had during my time at Purdue. Her talent is evident, and what sets her apart is her genuine concern for our academic well-being and future career. Every assignment she gives us is well thought-out and truly makes sense.

Coco offers great industry insights and career feedback but struggles with clear communication. Grading is harsh, with unclear expectations and minimal feedback. Policies like project resubmissions were revoked without explanation. While speed projects were insightful, their scope left little portfolio-worthy work, affecting the experience.

The course should definitely add more content on role of AI in product management - one guest lecture isn't enough. At times it becomes too theoretical especially the exams. Though the TA created homework assignments on topics not exactly covered in the lectures; he was very lenient in grading them which made things easier.

The course lacked structure and suffered from major scope creep. The TA graded inconsistently without a rubric and played favorites, creating an unfair environment. Despite the professor's industry experience, I would not recommend or retake this course in its current form.

The course does not require python knowledge, having no experience made it hard to follow with his teaching style. Lectures are slide based with limited explanation and zero engagement, often walks into class late. Students with Python and stats experience will likely do fine, if you have not taken one or both I highly recommend changing sections.
He kind of reads off the slides and isn't that interesting of a lecturer, but the class is easy anyway so it doesn't matter too much.

Meh professor. Class is poorly structured with very limited resources to study for exams. Final grade is very dependent on exam scores. Exams always have errors in them. For a seemingly easy course, they made it difficult.

Extremely full of ego and very arrogant manner of him. Absolutely full of himself, often belittling students and disrespecting them, one of the worst professors I met in the MSE department. I would never want to meet a professor, nor individual like him again.

Marinero is an amazing professor who truly cares about students' learning. He's passionate about what he teaches, and it shows. His exams are fair and actually test your understanding. One of the most genuine and admirable professors I've had.

It is very clear Dr. Tremblay is passionate about what she teaches. However, I think requiring students to attend class through iClicker polls is not really necessary. Other than that, I think she was alright. Just not great at making what she teaches sound interesting.

I didn't do well on the midterm exams. Luckily the final exam took place online. She encouraged students to go to her lecture in person but there are synchronized recording meetings on Brightspace and all you need is to answer the hot seat questions. She only gave one assignment per week and the answers could be found on Quizlet

Class is super easy and laid back until you take the exams, which look super simple and make sense, but you can never get a good grade no matter what you do. The recitations are annoying but doable. Besides that, Professor Humphrey is very kind.
good and gooder

Dr. Humphrey is actually the GOAT. Even though he posts them afterwards, you should absolutely still go to his lectures. He does a really good job of explaining a lot of things quickly and will pause at the end of each slide to make sure that people can keep up with the notes.
Very chill guy, lectures don't count for attendance and all quizzes/exams are online.
Great professor highly recommend

The greatest professor I have had so far at Purdue. Easily accessible outside of class and very helpful. He breaks down the content into small chunks that make you understand biology and not simply rely on rote memorization. Unlike a lot of other profs, he doesn't move through the slides at a million miles an hour and lets you absorb the content.
Amazing Professor. He is highly kind and understanding! The course overall is very manageable and interesting. Kindest professor I have had.
Amazing. Just amazing. He posts the slides AND video recordings of the lecture.

Dr Humphrey is a phenomenal professor. He ensures that his students actually understand the material and can take notes on the lectures. His lectures are entertaining and are posted online. Exams are also all online and usually easier than the quizzes. Homework is for completion.

Humphrey was amazing! He is a great lecturer and posts the blank and filled in copies of all of the notes. The homework is pretty easy and you get to continue trying on it until you get a 100%. The tests are also much easier than the quizzes, so if you do well on the quizzes (the TAs go over everything right before) you will do well on the exams.

Lectures always covered the content presented in lecture exams, and he always tried to make the topics more interesting and relevant. Dr. Humphrey is very knowledgeable and always has an answer for questions in lecture. Exams were fair as long as you paid attention to his examples. Recitation quizzes and lab exams were the most challenging.

I never went to a single lecture or watched the recordings online. Labs and homework are easy 100%. Recitation quizzes were a little challenging at times, but the recitation discussions were not bad since work was split between a group. The majority of points definitely come from the exams. I never interacted with Humphrey. Easy class overall!

Dr. Humphrey cares about his students and is very willing to help. He makes lectures interesting by providing real life examples. Lecture exams were straightforward since they reflected what was on his notes. Lab exams are more challenging but doable as long as you review the handouts on OneNote. I would definitely take again!

Humphrey is a very sweet and passionate guy. Lectures are not attendance mandatory, but labs and recitations are mostly mandatory. He posts all of his lectures online and the lecture exams are also online (and only over lecture material). Lab exams are more difficult but online as well and doable. Honors contract is very easy and manageable.
very good, learned a lot

Professor Humphrey did a great job, with clear lecture slides and helpful outside resources, like his review sessions and posted practice problems. Unfortunately, the recitations have changed, and some questions on the quizzes are factually impossible to answer with the information taught in class. It's still possible to do well, but be warned.

Easily one of the best professors I have had! He is so organized and his class is nearly impossible to fail if you just do the work. I literally got an A and never went to lecture my first semester. The lecture exams are easy if you study. The lab exams are rough but if you do bad you will definitely still pass the class so don't stress. Love him!

Very caring and sympathetic. He's a great resource to students and is very approachable. Lectures are very engaging and straight forward. Gives students ample time to write down notes during lectures. No complaints.
Genuinely the best professor I've had so far. Very caring and understanding. You know he's good when as an engineering student I didn't sleep during his class and actually paid attention and learned🐐
LOVE him. Always recommend his classes

One of the best professors I have ever had! He simplifies complex topics and makes is realllly easy to understand. He cares a lot about each of his students and makes himself easily accessible outside of class.

His lectures are very easy to understand. The only grades he submitted were the homework (you get two weeks to complete it and three tries), iClicker (participation/attendance credit), and the exams (done online).

Best thing was recorded lectures so definitely utilize them- I skipped the class for like a solid month whoops but going to labs and doing the homework really helped with everything- this is such an easy class so no stress.
A nice and friendly professor who clearly enjoys what he does. His lectures are both entertaining and easy to follow, and he is always willing to answer students' questions.

Dr. Humphrey is my favorite professor at Purdue! He explains materials so clearly and really cares about students' learning. BIOL231 started with content that I thought was too simple for an upper level biology course, but the content became more interesting as the course moved forward. He did a great job with the course under the circumstances.

He cannot lecture whatsoever, his final exam average was a 38%. He just mumbled the entire lecture and you can barely comprehend what he is saying and each lecture is incredibly dense in material. The exams are also just obscure trivia, you'd have to memorize everything on the very dense slides to get those points. Don't take any class with him
After having exam averages in the high 30's he decided to have absolutely curve at all. not even 1%

Seems like it's been a while since he's taught this. Lectures are bad, his slides are dense, he misspeaks, is hard to understand, and contradicts himself. Labs aren't helpful and weren't graded till the last two weeks. Exams are disorganized with no good prep material (either too easy or random content), which is why our final median was 35%.

This was the most unorganized class I have had the displeasure of taking at Purdue. The first two homeworks required 2 revisions before it could be completed, most labs had issues with either the instructions or sample code, and labs were often graded incorrectly. I had a D on lab 3 which was finally corrected to an A after several weeks.

If there are seven layers of hell, this class with him would be the eighth. Prof. Li does seem knowledgable about the topic (comp arch.) but can't communicate any of it well AT ALL. He just reads off the slides (often confusing) and his exams are more like obscure trivia. I'd rather delay my graduation by a year than take 250 with him.

This man simply can not lecture. He mumbles A LOT. The lecture slides are heavy with content and he practically reads from them, all while mumbling, and and proceeds to go on tangents. His labs are very poorly timed. By the end of the year, he wasn't done grading half of the labs, making determining your grade impossible. Just pray.

This guy might be worse than turkstra. He just read off slides in lecture and therefore you dont learn anything from lecture. Labs felt so disconnected from class content. Exams would ask about random lines from slides in lecture. Wouldnt respond to most eddiscussion posts. Almost no prep for exams so you go into them pretty much blind.

Great professor. It was the first semester he taught the course when I took it with him, but his slides were really well made - he wrote down the terminal commands we needed, detailed explanations, and nice diagrams. He answered most of the questions asked on Ed Discussion clearly.
Awesome professor 👍

Lectures were not very engaging, but sometimes helpful for understanding broader concepts. Prof. Li clearly knows the material, but is not the best at explaining it. The best way to do well is dedicating time to the projects, since that is what the exams are based on, as well as the majority of your grade. Not a very difficult class.

The project is time-consuming, spending nearly 60 hours for each project from Project 2 to Project 5. I recommend using C++ to write the project. I think the professor focuses too much time on parsing. I would like to learn more about optimization, garbage collection, instruction dependency, etc. Overall, this course is challenging.

Doesn't mention a lot of the details of the project, only covers the general features and expect you to figure out everything else by yourself. Doesn't reply ed discussions promptly. Should cover more details on project.

From project 2 to 5, all of them are about 2000 lines of code. And what he taught in class can't help you in project. Spend 8 hours per day on one project and still can't finish it. And project 5 is in dead week. Although you don't have a final exam, you have to take midterm 2 and a in class test. This course is the worst cs course I've ever had.

Awful class made worse by poor instruction. The lectures and projects were different and all of the projects build upon each other so if you do poorly on project 2, you are likely screwed for the rest of the semester. Took this last semester and would not reccomend

Prof. Li is honestly undeservingly underrated on RMP. Parts of his lectures where he explains algorithms could use some reworking, but his lectures ultimately do contain all of the content necessary to understand course material. Assignments in CS 502 are very fair and effective in teaching course materials, and Li is very kind and helpful overall.

it's a mixed bag. code projects are very long and demanding... more time consuming than i would prefer even for a phd level course. the lectures are fine, the theory is all fine, the homeworks are fine, and the exams are fine. my issue is the code projects - and other 502 prof's use the same project, but it's too much especially for one week each

Lectures are really clear to the point - he makes an abundant effort to provide them synchronously online & with recordings for asynchronous students. He gives very frequent homework and take-home quizzes, so you can't just fall asleep until midterm week, but the questions are all fair, & doing them always prepares you for the next assignment/test
The absolute worst professor I've encountered, doesnt know how to teach at all. Just plays a recorded presentation during live classes. Gives a lot of hw and the final project is torture.

He's a kind and lenient professor, but not a great one. You can tell that he tries to help the students by giving makeup opportunities, an easy project, and conducting review lectures. However, he does not teach the basics of C and students often had to look online/Campuswire to do well in the homework/projects.

The lectures were mediocre. Due to vague hw assignments, you'd have to browse Campuswire for hours and consult numerous TAs to understand the hw requirements. However, Li did make the class pretty easy as there were makeup hw assignments, no exams, a relatively easy project, and a generous curve.

Professor Li is truly an intelligent man and knows quite a bit, BUT I'm not sure how he was even approved to teach. He reads practically word for word off of the slides which are confusing. They are incoherent with the incredibly difficult homework. He knows the information but cannot teach whatsoever.

Professor Li is not the greatest professor I've had. The class was on the difficult side, with many fresh topics not touched upon enough. I'm giving 5 because he deserves it for he and his TA staff's support on campuswire and dealing with the mob mentality that has arisen. The project and hw was also generous in its grading and was (mostly) easy.

Coming in from CS 180, Im so disappointed in Purdues computer science department for allowing this professor to teach. C is a hard language, and Ive had only the internet as a resource during this class. The lectures are useless. Most of the TAs dont help at all. I cant count the number of times Ive had a mental breakdown over this class.

One thing that students often get wrong about this course is that they think that they are going to learn how to program in C. Once you rid yourself of that false assumption, this course is probably one of the best offered at Purdue.
Professor Li is one of the best professor I have ever had!!!!!!!! I understand every word that he says!

Be prepared for the exams. Even though they are online, they are much more challenging than the lecture material. The lab exams are especially difficult. I recommend taking detailed notes and attending office hours to stay on track. He is a great professor and really cares about biology

Dr Humphrey is super sweet, and it's obvious he loves teaching! The class was pretty easy as far as following along in lectures, homework, and lab. The lecture exams were slightly challenging but doable, as only material from the lecture was covered. Lab exams were harder and made less sense, but reading over the lab manuals usually helped.
There was just a lot of exams, and some of the exam questions were never covered in lecture or in lab. The lab and lectures like never lined up. Otherwise, he's a really good prof.

Okay yeah Sean is a cool professor but be prepared to fail his exams cause he makes then 10x harder than any biology exam should be. There is no point in making them so confusing even if you study so hard for them.

One of my favorite professors. Makes lectures interesting and doesn't talk in a monotone. His lecture exams are straightforward and there isn't any material on there that isn't discussed in lectures. The lab exams are harder and feel like we didn't learn the material. Overall, he is a great professor and would recommend taking his class.
Exams are all online and straightforward. Willing to help you whenever needed.

Best prof in the world he cares so much about biology and about the success of his students. He lectures at the perfect pace and he is always striving to make sure everyone enjoys lecture. For BIOL110 lecture exams are pretty easy, lab exams are a bit harder but very doable and all are online. Humphrey is so awesome and makes you love bio
It is easy to see how passionate this professor is about teaching biology. He is amazing at lectures, always timing things perfectly, and encouraging students to engage.

Very funny. Make sure to study for the quizzes!! Don't push them aside. Sometimes the lectures are completely different than the exam but for the most part i recommend taking a class with Sean Humphrey!

Dr. Humphrey is a great professor! He is so funny during lectures and always wants his students to succeed. Be prepared to read assigned textbook sections before class and use Quizlet to practice concepts. Recitation quizzes are terrible; be prepared.
Awesome professor!

Chill guy. Exams, homeworks, labs, recitations. Easy class if you take notes and attend class. Exams and quizzes online. Don't bother cheating, or only relying on cheating, he teaches so great. At least attend his lectures and pay attention. If not, then what are you doing in university? You have a great prof, take advantage of it.
Professor Humphrey posted practice exams for exams, had helpful iclicker lecture questions that reflected potential exam questions and easy homework (3 attempts).

Best professor at Purdue. He cares so much about students and takes time out of his day to help after or before class. Sean Humphrey's lectures were enjoyable and lecture exams were easy (and online). Beware of the lab exams they were much more difficult.
he is the kindes most flexible proff at purdue. is very personable and a great lecturer

Dr. Humphrey is a caring, kind, considerate professor. He is clear and communicates. His lectures are clear, but his lab exams are difficult. Pay attention to the labs and review the focus questions before lab exams.

He really cares about his students and actually wants them to succeed. Recitation and labs are mandatory but lecture really is not. Although, I would recommend going to lectures because he teaches the material very well. Overall best bio professor by far.
Prof. Humphrey was great! But generally speaking, the course itself is also fairly easy.
online exams, labs and recitation mandatory but lecture was not

He is literally the best bio professor. 100% would take this class again. He actually takes the time to look around and make sure everyone is ready before he changes slides. He gives more time to take notes when needed as well. Before every lecture exam there is always an optional review sessions during normal class time
It's between Humphrey and Chen for most popular professor at Purdue. Great guy who clearly cares about his students. Should easily get an A if you actually put in effort. Would definitely suggest.

Bill Nye would live this guy. Phenomenal educator, clearly explains topics and makes sure class has time to understand and take notes. Lectures and notes are posted online after lecture. Passionate and patient. Exams over lectures are made by him and are easy and online. The only difficult part of this class is the lab exams, which are not by him.

Professor Humphrey was an awesome lecturer and tried to make the class fun at any turn, he seems to be very passionate about the topic. It's very easy to pass this class; tests are online, HW is essentially just participation, and labs are fairly easy. Just pay attention a little bit and you'll be fine!

Dr. Humphrey makes coming to lecture very enjoyable! Despite not being a massive fan of the subject, I found myself very engaged while in class. He is always willing to let students catch up while taking notes and ensures you understand the material. Lecture exams are incredibly easy, but the lab practicals will take more effort and studying.

Overall, great professor! Well organized class, sets clear guidelines for how to do well. Lecture exams are never hard, literally taken from the notes in class, so as long as you look over those you should do well. Lectures themselves are clear, easy to follow, and fun to attend. Would 10/10 recommend!
Clear, Concise, Perfect!

I LOVE DR.STARR!! She's one of the sweetest professors that I've ever met and she truly cares about her students understanding the content! Her and her TA's do a phenomenal job of helping you as long as you reach out to them (especially in office hours). She gives tricky exams, but she gives many resources to prepare for them.

Dr. Starr is a great professor! I had her for both CHM 125 & CHM 126! She makes her class challenging but doable. Her tests are reasonable and are always related to class material with very few curve balls if any at all. Moreover, she gives plenty of extra credit for students to earn! Going to class isn't required, but counts as extra credit!

Dr Starr is amazing! The class is challenging but very doable. She explains the concepts well and is very helpful during office hours. Labs and homework were fairly easy, and exams reflected the material. There are a lot of opportunities for extra credit, go to class and read the instructions on your exam!! Overall, a very fun and engaging class.

Dr. Starr is great. Go to lectures, it will improve your grade. There is no curve for any of her classes but the extra credit helps. If you study you can probably get over 100 on every exam. Study for the final unlike I did!

I love Dr. Starr. She genuinely loves what she does and teaches with passion. Use her office hours whenever you can because they are really helpful. She will help you with any confusion one-on-one. She gives extra credit just for participating in class. I loved 125.

Dr. Starr is one of the kindest professors ever. Her office hours are really helpful, and she offers to meet with students individually after exams. Her expectations are reasonable and fair. She makes class at 8:30 fun.
Dr. Starr is one

This professor's grading criteria is my biggest issue. The amount of homework was definitely manageable, but I did not like that the homework was due the lecture after finishing the homework material, I would much rather prefer the homework have constant due dates (Ex. Every Friday). I did not like that the lab reports were due 3 days after lab.

Dr. Starr is a great professor who is very passionate about chemistry and made lectures engaging. I looked forward to attending every class even at 8:30am. I strongly recommend this class for first year chemistry majors since it is a great class to build foundational knowledge.

Dr. Starr is such a kind person who truly wants her students to succeed. I had her for the whole year and she made freshman chemistry fun and helped make it less intimidating. She has very high expectations, but she gives plenty of resources to help and she offers to meet with students individually. You should definitely go to her office hours.
She was absolutely amazing, so intelligent and so kind. Tests were fair and she provided a lot of study materials and was always so helpful and approachable!
Actually an amazing professor. Class is easy besides for exams but office hours are so welcoming and a great resource!!

Dr. starr is a super caring and kind person, she really cares about her students, and she prioritizes  making meaningful relationships with her students. She have a very strong grasp of the material and has never not been able to answer a question. she gives a good amount of partial credit on the exams and all the exams are very fair. 10/10!

She is accessible outside class, offers helpful advice, and uses a clear grading rubric. Tricky exam questions are flagged in lectures. Attendance, though at 8:30 AM, earns bonus points. Exams aren't curved; most content is covered in lectures, with key lab and recitation topics revisited in the three midterms or final exam.

Class is pretty easy if you have taken AP chem if not, it might be a little harder, but there are so many opportunities to have lectures explained in smaller settings. Overall, she is amazing and absolutely love as a professor.

I had Dr. Starr over the summer and she was great. Even though the class was online, she still had regular times to meet with us and was very clear about what would be on exams. She was kind and supportive and I'm a little sad I couldn't take a course with her in person. She's probably the best professor I've had at Purdue.

CHM 126 has a good amount of assignments, but it also has many opportunities for extra credits. The exams this year were also exceptionally easy, being mostly short answer questions with points for work. Dr. Starr was a good lecturer but I did not go to class much because she went a little too slow. Her notes are great, very concise and neat.
Dr. Starr was a great professor. She really cares about her students. Her lecture example problems reflect what she puts on her exam. Her lectures can be fast paced, but she is easy to follow.

I loved Dr. Starr! She has high expectations of her students. The exams were very fair since most of the questions followed her in-class examples. I really like how Dr. Starr is passionate, down-to-earth, and very energetic; even at 8:30 am. She is super helpful and friendly in lectures and office hours. By far my favorite prof at Purdue!

Dr. Starr is one of my favorite instructors I have ever had. It is very clear that she cares a lot about her students and she makes an effort to connect with us. She's a very good lecturer and while she does go quickly through her slides, she is not difficult to follow. If you show up to class and put effort in, you should have no issue succeeding.

Dr. Starr is super accessible and incredibly kind and energetic. It is not hard to do well as the exam questions are almost always formatted exactly as the examples during class. She effectively prepares you for exams and assignments and is very fair with giving partial credit on exams. Great professor for chem majors to build base knowledge with.
She does a good job at keeping up the pace in lectures because this is a very fast-paced course. She's fairly new, but it isn't that obvious!

Fast talker with dense lectures in order to get through all of the CHM 129 content. Get ready to ask for help if you don't have a strong chemistry background - it's easier if you sit near the front of lectures or go to her problem solving sessions - but she's very approachable and very friendly.

Dr Starr talks VERY fast and it is sometimes hard to keep up with notes. It taught me how to take notes efficiently. The class is really homework heavy, but that forces you to study. Dr Starr was really helpful and friendly when I had questions. It's obvious she enjoys what she does. She provides a lot of in class practice problems that help a LOT.
The class isn't super easy, but calculus isn't easy. He is very kind and does what he can to teach you the topics. He makes the exams as easy as he can but you need to study a lot outside of class.

Great teaching style! He does practice problems all class, which makes the homework and exams so much easier. He is also great at answering questions and checking in with the class to make sure everyone is following along. His lectures aren't mandatory, but he does quizzes, so you'd have to go to class to know when they are.
Super amazing in and out of class, there were periodical in class quizzes about once a week. Homeworks were much more difficult than the exams.

He is an amazing guy. I struggled heavily with the first exam, but started going to him for office hours. The remaining exams I got perfects on. He is very understanding and will give you everything you need to pass the class. Do not be afraid of him at all. Also he has really cute dogs and loves to talk about them!
Very good at explaining things & really encourages asking questions. Lots of help if you have questions on homework and good with email. Quizzes were open person and very simple!

He is one of the few professors that actually care about teaching the material, and has always been very helpful during his office hours. Brings in his dogs as well so please let him know if you are scared of them or allergic. The only problem is that his tests are weighted pretty high

Dave Norris is a fantastic professor. He clearly explains the material well with expertise and enthusiasm. Lectures are optional but there are quizzes at the very end. Grading criteria is very straightforward but exam heavy. He makes Calc II feel like a breeze. Study and put in the time and you will come out satisfied. Also brings in his dogs!

Absolutely goated Calc 2 professor. Took the class in Fall 2024. He explained everything very clearly and was easy to follow in the lecture. Easy open-note lecture quizzes which really help your grade. Answered every question perfectly in office hours, and had great exam prep. Very entertaining guy too.

Amazing professor who knows what he's teaching. Clearly states assignments, when everything's due, and what you need to know to do well. You can tell he wants you to succeed in this class. Also very helpful if you have any questions, and lectures are thorough.

Dave is by far the best professor that I have had at Purdue. I did bad on my first two exams because I did not properly study. I then started going to his office hours and he was so helpful. I got a 100 on the last two exams. He is also super relatable and is always down to have a good chat. Also he loves Oreos.

Dave Norris has shown time and time again that he is the best math teacher Purdue has to offer. Maybe even the greatest math teacher of all time. I do not like math, but he had me wanting to go to lecture every single time. This and other reviews show the impact Dave has on expanding peoples' minds in math and beyond.

Amazing professor. All open note quizzes. All 3 exams were easy, and if you don't understand any concepts, his office hours are very helpful as well. Actively asked if students had questions and was willing to share key things we had to know for the exam.

Mr. Norris is the best Calc 2 teacher! He's chill, funny, organized & explains concepts clearly, ensuring we get notes before moving on. He explains every topic, holds exam review classes & posts his class notes on Brightspace. Achieve HW is clear. Partner/open-note quizzes. He answers all Qs & goes over every topic in OFFICE HOURS. 10/10 teacher!

Truly an amazing and caring professor. He is extremely personable and cares a lot about his students. He is also very funny during lectures. He always asks if anyone has any questions and is willing to explain anything to students. The class itself wasn't hard. The exams were easy and all multiple choice. Some easy in-class open-note quizzes.

Norris is a really chill guy, and shows a true interest in both the class and students. Occasionally makes mistakes when doing problems, but actively invites students to correct and discuss the material with him. Encourages students to learn and meet with him outside of class, and generally creates a great atmosphere in the classroom.
The best Professor ever He cares about his students. There is so much I could say about him that is positive there is nothing negative I can even think about Dave.

Dave is super helpful, I met with him for office hours almost every day after I bombed my first exam and I dont think I could have passed if it wasnt for him. He really cares about his students and wants us to succeed.

He's incredibly patient and caring about his students. Quizzes are open-book and encourage you to learn more. Very thorough in his method of teaching and makes sure we understand each concept well. Be sure to attend all of his lectures if you can.

Possibly one of the best instructors in the Math Department and one of the few professors at Purdue who can actually teach the material. This instructor is passionate about having his students understand difficult calculus concepts and teaches in a way that makes sense. You would be considered very lucky to be able to enroll in his section.

Dave is honestly one of the best professors I have had at Purdue. He works really hard to be a resource for his students and will go out of his way to help a student understand. He is always willing to help, even if you're not in his course anymore.
MA 158 material is easy, and Dave makes it easier.
Dave is the best math instructor that I have had at Purdue. Go to his office hours! He takes the time to explain the problems to you and works at your pace. He truly cares about his students!

Dave was extremely helpful and is very intelligent when it comes to finding tricks to help you on exams. I truly do not believe I would have done as well as I did in pre-calc without him. He goes fast during lectures but allows you to print out notes which helps a ton. He still helps me in the math department when other professors arent available.
If you ask the professor questions, he will definitely answer them. Although the homework is quite frequent, you can easily get good grades if you work hard enough.

Professor Norris makes many resources available such as sending a PDF of the notes before each class, and holds helpful office hours, making sure to take care of those students. Dave is also a funny laid back guy who doesn't take anything too serious. There is a lot of homework, but Dave helps students understand concepts if they make the effort.

He did a great job teaching in the online format. There was a lecture for every lesson, which I cant say for the rest of my profs this semester. He continued to teach and didnt take the easy out like many profs this semester who just link articles and post quizzes. Helpful also
the course is just exam heavy and made tough. The project is really interesting.

Pretty easy course overall (compared to other 3xx CS courses). Lectures are pretty boring, but the content is not difficult. Exams are very similar to past exams. If you do the homework and learn the content, the lab exam is free.

The content of CS348 is not difficult, however, he made the exams as difficult as possible. The course is now extremely exam heavy, with the addition of the lab exam. The exams felt like they were purposefully set up for you to fail and test whatever is not covered by the homework and practice exams. Doing the homwork and practice did little help

.databases is a bit dry but very useful and he definitely tries to introduce more industry usable ideas in class which makes it worth attending. demanding on deadlines and slightly repetitive lectures. Reasonable exams with lot of prep material. Lot of homework though

Lectures can be long, though I have always felt that it is more efficient to read the slides in my own time. Exams are average diff. Homework can be in weird formats and sometimes not so cohesive. I had him for CS440 and CS473. Hisham does a lot at Purdue in addition to these classes, so he can be busy.
Not that hard. But definitely annoying. Finish your homework on your own and try to solve all the problems they provide in PSO, you will be good in the exams.

He is a very respectable CS professor. The content was moderately difficult but taught well, the homeworks and exams felt fair (at least to me) and there was a decent amount of extra credit. 348 isn't the hardest course, but I felt like he taught us enough to be useful without ever feeling helpless.

The exams were fairly similar to the practice exams, although you have to write code with a pencil which can feel completely different to any of the homeworks. The professor was somewhat forgiving and gave extra credit opportunities. There were online i-clicker activities during the lectures (we didn't have to buy one)

348 has certainly become much more difficult. The exams are extremely challenging. Similar to the practice exams, but just at a much higher degree of difficulty. There was a nice curve, but the class does not follow the reputation it has of being an easy elective

Prof Benotman was a nice professor. His lectures can be boring, but he is teaching databases so I can't blame him. He was accessible outside of classes, and willing to adjust deadlines as needed. Exams were open-note, and he released a practice exam in advance. The project was basically a fullstack CRUD app, so super easy if you have experience.

I don't know it's just this semester, but it was obvious that we're like... not his priority class. He clearly was putting more effort into teaching CS348, which isn't a bad thing but... whyd he make it so obvious? Anyways, it was okay. He posted the practice exam 2 days before the exam on a Sunday night. Lol

His lectures are pretty good, but sometimes hard to pay attention to. He seems like a responsive professor, willing to answer questions and change deadlines to things when needed. The homework and exams are hard, but the project isn't bad if you don't procrastinate.

Poor lectures and lack of engagement. It seems like he didn't read the slides beforehand. The material was interesting, just a bad lecturer. Assignments were extremely tedious but not difficult. Attendance counts. Project was interesting. I would avoid if possible, lectures alone make the class extremely tedious and sluggish.
The amount of time you have to spend in this class is reasonable. Grading is fair. Dr. Benotman makes the lectures very intuitive and easy to understand.

I took his CS348, and he is one of the best professor among other CS courses professors. He explained well during the lecture and also provided recordings every time after the class for students to review. He is also very helpful in his OH and on Campuswire.

Benotman is not the best at lecturing. He is very knowledgeable about the information. There are many homeworks and they do take a while. The exams are very easy because they are open note. Any of his classes are easy because of this (ex. 471).

Professor Benotman is an overall good professor. He has fair expectations for his class. Was also helpful after class when we asked him about homework specifications. Very flexible when I needed to make up an exam or accidentally forgot to turn in a part of the homework. Curved by a lot and got an A when I expected a B or B+.

Professor Benotman is an overall good professor. He has fair expectations for his class. Was also helpful after class when we asked him about homework specifications. Flexible when I needed to make up an exam or accidentally forgot to turn in a part of the homework. Curved by a lot and got an A when I expected a B or B+.
Good professor in general. Slow but helpful lectures. There's little need to read the textbook. Sometimes the homework grading can seem arbitrary, but overall they are very useful.

Benotman is a fairly good professor. He can be a little dry in lecture and he doesn't really follow his class schedule a lot (he gave one in the beginning of the semester, but it wasn't updated with changes). However, he does seem to care about your learning and is very approachable. He is a safe choice overall.

Nice Professor, Friendly person. I have done a project with him. He is very welcome to students during his office hour and even out of his office hour. But he is somehow not that good at explaining. but mostly, he is a very good professor.
Good quality lectures and assignments. The course covered a good selection of topics.

He was one of the most caring and helpful professors I have had, especially throughout the duration of the pandemic and online setting. He made material that was dry seem interesting and easy to understand through the use of many examples.

If you watch the lectures, youll do well in the class. Goes over all the homework questions during the lectures and is very available to help, and very understanding. Great professor and made the databases class very enjoyable.
He's very understanding throughout the pandemic. Homeworks can be somewhat challenging at times but nothing unreasonable.

She is AMAZING. She cares for her students, is insanely funny and it very lenient with deadlines. she goes out of her way to make the class enjoyable and tries her best to get to know her entire class. She does her best to give her entire class an A and she is extremely helpful. Her classes are the break I needed from my hectic schedule.
She is a great and understanding professor.

She is a great professor overall. Attendence was not required but strongly recommended. My one issue was that a few people used ChatGPT when they were not supposed to on the first online exam; we all got punished for it on the second exam by making it in person and handwritten. Otherwise, she is a great teacher and a very nice person.
Take this professor if you have the chance.
Amazing professor. She cares about her students and wants the best for us. Highly recommend her for any of the courses she teaches.

Amazing professor! She really facilitates the discussion and allows you to learn from different perspectives. The class itself has only written papers, which are easy if you come to class. The work was very manageable! Loved it.
Time was wasted, I didn't learn anything

Professor Parrish was the greatest professor ever. We were only graded on 3 papers and an oral presentation, but she understands that you have other classes. She is an easy grader, and you should talk to her if you encounter any stresses. Definitely take a course with Professor Parrish.
Prof Parrish was wonderful! She made the class super fun!!! She is super approachable and is a great resource to students. Love her way of teaching. Best prof ever.

Professor Parrish is an Amazing professor, one of the best I've ever had! She is really laid back and makes the assignments in class as fun as possible for her students.
Papers are really easy, you just have to have read the book to successfully write them.
very good!

Professor Parrish is a very chill person and is very approachable. She is available as a resource to students. The papers in this class were very easy, you just have to pay attention in class and do the homework assignments to get an A. Overall, no complaints.

I took PHIL270, I really enjoyed it. I think it is one of my favorite classes I have taken and part of that is definitely because she was a very good professor. You can tell she cares about her students and wants to make the course manageable. Extra credit is offered but you need to remember the only actual grades are the three short essay exams.

Her class was very easy, with three presentations in total. You can choose the topic. You don't have to read anything in her class since she'll explain the book to you. If you talked in your presentation, then you will receive an A in the final grade.

Dr. Parrish is a great professor to take SCLA with. She is very lax with deadlines and doesn't mind if you turn in assignments late. If you have tough STEM classes to get through, Dr. Parrish's class can be a nice break.
Best english professor! Super helpful and caring.
Very knowledgeable about the material and genuinely cares about students. Make a conscious effort to get in her class. You will not regret it. Very few assignments.

Professor Parrish is the best professor I've ever had. She's always willing to work with her students. And knows that we have other classes so doesn't assign and overwhelming amount of reading or homework. Anyone who gets her in the future is lucky and should enjoy every second of it.

I took PHIL 270 with her and she is easily my favorite professor at Purdue. She is an excellent professor who truly cares about her students. Her lectures keep you engaged and the textbook is actually interesting. There were three short answer exams and 6% of extra credits. Answers have to be detailed but if you do the readings, you'll do great.

Professor Parrish is one of the kindest professors I've ever had. This class is graded by very little things, including 10 hours of volunteering outside of class, a food drive class project, and an ending reflection essay. There were a couple opportunities for extra credit and I took advantage of those, since I didn't attend class often.

She offered a lot of extra credit. She was clear in what she expected in her papers. She also is in general, a very kind and likeable professor. If you are going to take SCLA101, choose her. She's definitely the best professor for that course.

Great Professor. Give an effort to pay attention to key themes in books and a good grade is not problem. She gives extra times on essays and takes time to read through thesis statements individually. Not much more a student can ask for.
Parrish was great! She explained each assignment thoroughly, and would give extra time if needed! I did take this class online so Im not sure how her in person class would be.

She is an absolutely amazing professor and makes the required English courses actually enjoyable. She explains the readings really well and gives a super light workload. I would definitely recommend taking her class if you have the chance, she is super approachable and understanding!

Shes the most understanding and easy-going professor Ive ever had. She doesnt grade harsh and cares about her students. Shes REALLY passionate about what she teaches. Get her section if you can. Youll love her.

This is the most disorganized professor I've ever had. The due dates are unclear as the syllabus and Brightspace show different dates. There are multiple closed-note quizzes a week with Lockdown Browser. She also speaks English half the time. Take this class with Sánchez-Llama if you can.

This was, without competition, the worst class I have ever taken. Do anything in your power to avoid her class. I would like to say that she cared about us students learning, but I am not even sure that that is true. I am fluent in Spanish, and became her personal dictionary during lecture- it was downright disappointing. SAVE YOURSELF DO NOT TAKE
Genuinely terrible. I do not know how she still works at Purdue. Only talks about Cuba.

It's a yapathon but she's the only one running. Don't be fooled by the grade I received, this course is very difficult. She gives a ton of additional work that takes forever to finish and gets upset when you don't know everything during class. The class is doable, but if possible, just try to take this with a different professor.

This class was a nightmare. She goes off on tangents for the entire clas. All of the important reading and actually helpful information is done outside of class. Her grading is contradictory. Also, assignments will show 3 different due dates. The class is very poorly organized and due dates will jump out at you out of no where. Good luck soldier.

I don't know how I could condense this review into 350 characters. Please do NOT take this class. Extremely disorganized, marks you off for things that you "didn't include" in your paper that WERE FULLY THERE, to the point where I had to highlight all the requirements so that she could see them. Terribly harsh grader, unclear expectations.
Very difficult professor. Tough grader. Extremely disorganized and contradictory. Find another professor, do not waste a penny on this class.  You're wasting your time.

I had high hopes entering this class. Prof Stinchcomb came across very friendly and accommodating, but things changed drastically a few weeks into the semester. Due dates and expectations for assignments were never clear and instead of taking responsibility, she blames the students. I would advise anyone to not take a class by her.

This has been my worst experience with a spanish professor at Purdue and it's not even close. She is so unorganized and then pushes her lack of preparedness on to her students. She expects more hours of work in a week than she probably puts into planning the semester. Our entire class is clueless as to what is going on. Avoid at all costs.

Although this was a really difficult class for me, Profesora Stinchcomb was by far the most caring professor I've ever had. I reached out to her when I was struggling with the class and she was MORE than helpful and accommodating. She's incredibly understanding and has a genuine care for her students' wellbeing and success, even beyond her class.

She goes on rants that have nothing to do with the class at all for the whole hour and 15. She also grades very harshly and expects 20+ hours a week dedicated outside of this class, which is ridiculous. She knows a lot about the topics, but she does a terrible job teaching it to us. All in all, do not take this class cause its added stress.

I see a lot of people giving Dr. Stinchcomb very unfair ratings. While the class does have harder quizzes, they are not impossible. She also gives many makeups, especially if she sees the class did not do well. If you go to office hours she is very helpful. Effort is key to this class. Some of you want things handed to you and thats very telling.

I had the misfortune of being placed in Professor Stinchcomb's SPAN 241 class last fall (2021), and let me start by saying that it was a terrible experience. Stinchcomb is extremely unorganized, both on Brightspace and in the classroom. She is a harsh grader, poor at explaining assignments, and accused me of false actions several times. Very bad!

This was easily the worst class I have ever taken in my life. Prof. Stinchcomb repeatedly showed up to class unprepared, and demonstrated an inability to lead a classroom effectively. The level of quality of her teaching did not reflect the resources she provided to us whatsoever, and I felt almost hopeless as her student.

The most disorganized instructor I have ever interacted with. The Brightspace page was horrendous and very clearly published without being proofread first -- this is also evident in her emails. Also provided incorrect information frequently -- there were times that I was not even a bit convinced she knew what she was talking about.

One of the worst professors I had at Purdue. Saying a lot because I studied engineering. She was unnecessarily callous and strict but was also unclear in her expectations, making the class extra stressful and unenjoyable. Be warned that this "Latin American Culture" class is entirely focused on Cuba.

Her syllabus is very unorganized, and this class takes more of my time than my science-based classes. She is abrasive and difficult to approach about questions. Her exams and quizzes are hard as the material is unorganized and composed of many different sources. There is little time for speaking in this class, as she lectures the whole hour.

Most ppl complain abt Prof. Stinchcomb's hw load bc we usually turn in @ least 1 paper/week. What people are forgetting to mention is that most of them are for a completion grade & require 2-4 paragraphs. Also, yes quizzes are hard but only 10% of the grade. She even gives make up work opportunities. We get to watch movies & shes always smiling :)

She's easily the worst professor I've had at Purdue. I've never been in such a disorganized class. She changes the syllabus multiple times per week because she cant keep her assignment due dates straight, and it's by far the most confusing syllabus I've ever had. We never know when things are due, and she gives conflicting information regularly.

She is enthusiastic, but her lectures do not seem to follow any real structure.  While she seems knowledgeable of the material, she is not the best at explaining it. She does NOT FOLLOW  the syllabus (frustrating). Her quizzes are impossible not matter how much you read and prepare. Tests are manageable. Participation is crucial!
EXTREMELY UNORGANIZED
Dr. Stinchcomb is a very nice professor.  Her exams are undoubtably hard, but she's a really cool person and will help you out as much as she can.  Definitely my favorite Spanish class at Purdue.

Incredibly difficult class when taught by her. Online quizzes were close to impossible to get good grades on no matter how much you read beforehand. Gradings (quizzes, exams, compositions) were all unfair and her teaching seemed more of her trying to gain respectmake a name for herself than actually teaching students to learn. Would not recommend

She might be knowledgeable on the subject, I learned very little from her in the course. All assignments felt more of an exercise of her power in a desperate attempt to establish respect than as a learning tool. Excessive homework and assignments were unreasonable and physically impossible to complete by nature of the sheer volume of readings to do
Poor professor, extremely difficult class not because of material but because of inconsistency and lack of preparedness for class.

Very entertaining and effective lectures, gives premade note sheets that you fill out with key terms/ideas. Homeworks were started in a collaborative session and not overly difficult. Exams are fair and cover material on the homeworks for the most part. Showing up to lectures will help a lot with doing well in this class. Great professor.

Dr. Troy is the GOAT. Only professor I've ever showed up to non-mandatory lectures for. Dude is engaging, structures his course well, and makes things very easy to understand. Every professor should take some notes from him
Best professor in the online graduate course options for civil engineers. He is helpful and willing to work with you. Awesome professor.

Cary is an absolutely phenomenal professor. Lectures are very good and thorough. Coourse's content is interesting. The one thing I don't like is the hw grading. I believe TAs graded them and sometimes they take off points even when the qs don't ask. The project is very time consuming so be prepared for that, and pick a good partner.

Professor Troy is funny and kind. His lectures are fast, so make sure to pay attention. His Friday lectures are just time to work on the homework and he provides the answers: you still get graded on work. His exams are fair, and he is just a really good professor. I actually cared about learning the material and found it interesting because of him.

Hilarious/amazing educator. Chill environment, connects and engages every student. Class Slack is super helpful. I never felt the need to read the textbook & you don't need it for hw. Go to office hours and Fri class (working hour) to finish the HW. Records all lectures. 3 exams + the final. HIGHLY recommend Troy; favorite professor I've ever had.

This is hands down the best class I have taken at Purdue. Troy's dedication to his student's learning is clear, and he makes class fun and interactive. He gave out fill-in-the-blank packets every lecture which were extremely helpful. The Friday class was a mandatory office hour to work on your homework with other students. The exams were very fair!

Troy is the bar that all professors should be striving for. Dense yet informative and manageable lectures (posted after class so you can review). Fridays are power hours to work on hw, super helpful. He also posts mini lectures and example videos every week to help on quizzes and homework. Take 340 with him if possible. "Best. Class. Ever. Period."
Best Professor

Awesome and fun classes. Prof. Troy interacts with the students a lot which creates a good learning environment. There is a short quiz per lecture and usually one homework per week. Both are ok to do if you have been in class but the homework can take some time. Other grades are two exams and a group project for which you will get prepared well.

Excellent class. I had in-person lectures and if you go you will pass. There are 3 short weekly quizzes and a weekly homework which can be a lot, but none of it is extremely difficult. Exams are straightforward and lectures are engaging, informative, and use "gapped notes".

Troy is an excellent professor and took a class that I was not particularly interested in (hydraulics) and made it one of my favorite classes I've taken. The exams are very straightforward and the homework answers are posted in class so you know if you're on the right track. Go to lectures and you will succeed.

Yes he is very funny and nice but in general for me this class was very painful. He was very bad about posting notes online and he never posted lecture slides. His exams were online which was nice but they were 3 hours long and it seems like most people needed the full 3 hours. Taking those were awful. His exams even required googling stuff

Professor Troy is the type of person all professors should strive to be. He makes lectures entertaining and relevant to quizzes, homework, and tests. He has taken this whole COVID-19 fiasco and rolled with the punches. Not only are his lectures short and to the point, he goes out of his way to make students laugh. He's the best teacher I've had.

Probably the best professor I've had at Purdue. His lectures are very easy to follow and relate directly to the homework which relates directly to the exam. He doesn't try to trick you. He is very helpful during office hours as well. I really enjoyed going to his class.

He is very straightforward when it comes to exam reviews. The handouts are helpful to keep track of what topics are important. He makes the lectures interesting with videos, music or jokes to keep morale up.

This guy is literally amazing. He's hilarious in class and genuinely cares about his students learning the material. Exams are fair and predictable.   I'm looking forward to taking more classes with him in the future.

Cary is the best prof. at least for Hydraulic. I used to take Hydro before but I dropped the course at last minute. The other professor made thing difficult even  it's just a simple concept. The other prof regards all the students are genius but Cary regards all are stupid guys. I'm not clever and I need professor to explain things in a easy way.

A lot of students like him and his lectures. I thought he was ok, but he did make hydro pretty interesting. Homework assignments can be very difficult and there are 3 midterms for this class. Average class, decent lecturer.
Great chap.

Professor Troy is the best professor I've had at Purdue. He teaches the material in a very straight-forward manner so that it is easy to understand. His exams are very straight-forward so if you actually do the work, getting a good grade is guaranteed. He is very helpful and is always available for extra help.

He keeps the material interesting with his unique sense of humor.  He tries his best to help students out and gives us more than enough opportunities to ask questions or go to his office hours.  Definitely one of the best CE professors at Purdue.

Troy was my favorite CE professor. He has this very mellow and down to earth personality to him that he mixes with great humor to keep your attention in class. CE 340 is not an easy class but he uses great handouts and lectures to help you understand the topics. Do your homework/study for exams and you'll get a god grade. He raps on the last day.

Professor Troy is one professor you should take a course with at Purdue.  He is incredibly funny, helpful, and uses lectures to fully explain the material.  The course is tough, but made so much better with Professor Troy.
He is a very fun professor who loves to teach. His lectures are engaging and takes time to ensure that concepts are understood by the class.
amazing lecturer and a nice guy. will definitely miss his enthusiasm. HWs are based off of the worksheets. the exams are very manageable too, especially if you've taken some stats before.

There is an online section, but I recommend coming to lecture. It's clear he cares about what he's teaching and came to class lighthearted and smiling. Good pacing. Lecture video, with all code and notes posted on Brightspace. Content was not too challenging, and he explained everything well. Great professor!

The best professor ever. Lectures are very well paced and he's a very understanding person. Always makes time to answer questions and is very open about office hours. Exams are fair, tons of resources!
i love keaton he's so wonderful and he cares so much about his student's success. exams were fair and pretty easy, honestly just watch the lectures and try and you'll be fine

Professor Keaton was a pretty chill guy, and only gave one very fair homework per week. The exams were actually pretty easy, and he gave good study material for them. I wouldn't describe his teaching style as life-changing, but he was a very solid professor and would recommend for 355.

Amazing professor, maybe the best one I've ever had. He clearly has a passion for teaching and it's infectious. He comes up with many creative examples to illustrate his points, which is very helpful. Easy exams and one of the rare STEM classes to actually respect your free time. Don't skip his lectures or you'll be missing out.

Super capable, nice guy. Very friendly, clear grader. Make sure to try hard on the homework cause that's what cost me my A in the class. Homework is graded really thoroughly and well though, and his feedback is super helpful for studying for the midterm/final. Overall super nice guy, one of my favorite professors I've had.

Absolutely love the guy. I'll be taking 506 next year as my stats selective just because he teaches it. Gives amazing in depth feedback for each individual homework question, so you can use homework to study for midterms/final. Course is clearly organized and dude is genuinely a great teacher. 10/10.
Wonderful
Very good at lecturing and explaining the course content in an easy to understand way.
Entertaining class and attendance was optional. Not too many homework assignments and he was great at teaching R. Always willing to help and very approachable.

Dr. Keaton is a passionate professor who clearly loves his job. While attendance is not mandatory (during the pandemic so video lessons were made available), I still went to each of the in-person lectures. He really took the time to expand upon the material and give supplemental examples, solidifying our learning.
His lectures are boring and he grades harshly on homework.
I took this class with the impression of him being a nice guy. His lectures were good but very boring, easy to get distracted. His tests were hard. Outside of class he wasnt really a nice guy.

Dr Keaton is a wonderful professor and is very clearly passionate about the course he teaches, which is quite easy if you pay attention. The online lectures are entertaining and he encourages the class to be interactive. The provided worksheets help a ton for homework and midterms, and the end-of-semester project is a great prep tool for the final.

Keaton was super kind this semester with everything being offered online. He provided worksheets and solutions to help practice lecture concepts. These worksheets were very helpful to reference for homework and exams.
Good professor and the course was not too difficult as long as you make sure you pay attention.

I took 355 and loved him so much that I took 506. He doesn't do plus/minus grading so make sure you work hard from the beginning to get that A. He explains everything really well and is ALWAYS available to help. Go to his office hours!! He's kind and sits down with you to help you understand the homeworks/material.

Really nice person and he explains concepts very well. He is easy to talk to and does his best to make it interesting when the subject matter can be dry at some points. He has a lot of office hours and is more than willing to find times to meet outside the class. Overall cares a lot about his students.

Tim was one of the best professors Ive had at Purdue. He truly cares about his students and it is easy to see how passionate he is about the material he covers. Some of the questions on his homework assignments may be tough, but he is always willing to help during office hours no matter how many questions I had.
He's a friendly guy.

Prof. Miller was a decent lecturer. Exams were sometimes tricky. Lectures were engaging and he explained topics well. Some classes were just group problem solving to prep for exams. It is clear he knows mathematics beyond class exercises and teaches it like so. I recommend you follow along with MIT OCW videos to conceptually understand the topics.
He goes really fast but I do think he did a good job explaining things. He's funny sometimes too, 265 is just a hard class. Utilize his office hours

Everyone I've talked to who had him loved him. He explains every topic clearly, and will always clarify/reexplain things if asked. He's a bit dyslexic and gets lost occasionally but it rarely interferes with your ability to understand where he's going with things. Practice tests are almost identical to the actual tests too, which is always good.

Prof. Miller's topology course is a perfect introduction for students learning this subject. In lectures, he can always provide straight and concise answers to our questions and illustrate the main logic of each lecture clearly. He posts written notes and corresponding sections from the textbook in his website. He is also accessible after class.

Great guy I took his class last semester and he is pretty funny. His lectures don't really line up with the textbook at all, so going to class is in your best interest. Love all the quirky sayings he sprinkles in to every lecture

Miller is very much a math guy, so if you're a math lover you'll probably like him more. He has a pretty standard lecture style and is light on homework. Definitely go to office hours (he's pretty nice) if you're confused about anything though, you do not want to fall behind in this class. He always has old tests and extra credit, which is nice.

The professor is lost, the textbook is lost, pretty much nothing can help you out apart from the extremely scarce practice problems it gives you. Huge time investment needed to succeed partially in this class.

I'll be the devil's advocate and say I had a great experience with Miller. I love math (especially theory and proofs), and although lots of people might get confused as a result of his tangents, I find them funny and educational. He's a nice guy, but only take him if you're good at math.

Pure Math major here, his first class was completely random and really intimidates you, just wants to get over with the syllabus and doesn't teach anything else related to that afterwards. If you have any other professors you can go, do that. You will be fine after really reading the book at least 7 hours a week and keep on practicing the material.

it's very hard for students to follow his lecture, because he jump from here to there without detail. In additon to that, he did not give his lecture following the textbook. There is no syllabus, not lecture, note on the brightspace. The worst lecture I have ever seen in Purdue .

This professor emphasizes heavily on abstract principles in MA265, which is only a small fraction of the overall final administered by the math department. The abstract questions are very difficult, and exams consist of mostly abstract questions. The finals were comparatively much easier than the exams the professor gave. Hard to follow lectures.

He tries to teach you the material but fails very badly. He gets lost very quickly and skips all over the topic, jumps between topics, his exams are very hard and neither his lectures nor the homework will help you with them.

He is one of the worst professors I have ever had, he is very messy in terms of teaching, neither his homework nor the past exams help you with his exams. You basically do not understand his lectures since he is very bad at note-taking and all over the place. He is a good person, but switch his class if you can.

Yea, he can get lost in lectures sometimes so make sure to actually follow. He answers questions pretty well, though. Down-to-earth guy. Provides lots of study material (including a practice test) for each exam, and always gives extra credit questions on tests. Pretty fun class as far as math classes go.

Miller is a pretty solid professor. He's pretty scatterbrained but I really feel like I know Linear Algebra and he's also very good at preparing you to do well. His office hours are extremely helpful and he's all around a great guy. You need to go to class to do well but if you do you'll be rewarded. Consistent test difficulty.

Miller is one of the worst professors I've ever had. I did poorly in his course because he jumped everywhere in lectures and when I went to his office to speak to him, he went in circles with everything he said. It was a horrible, stressful experience. With him, this is a pure theory course with him, so stat majors specifically, stay FAR away!!!

Dr. Miller is a very nice guy who will go far out of his way to help you understand the content. He is always willing to take questions in class and clear any confusion on before he moves on. His test are also nice, and he clearly outlines what to expect from them and always adds about 20 points of extra credit to each one.

Not a great lecturer. Lacks confidence big time. The homeworks can be tough and you have no idea how others are doing compared to you so that can be annoying. However, tests are very fair. Read the textbook and you'll be able to get by.

Mr. Miller is a super nice guy that definitely cares about his students.  He is a relatively new professor and he can be a little ambiguous in class, but if you ask him to repeat a process he absolutely will.  Linear is definitely one of the toughest math classes I have ever taken, but I would say that Mr. Miller's tests are pretty fair.

Was very unclear in lectures, lacked confidence, and put me to sleep nearly every day. I felt like he was guessing at everything he was saying and it made it very hard to understand what we were supposed to be doing.
The worst instructor I had ever had... don't take his class
Avoid. Way too much work for 1 credit and grading feels very subjective. She even promised extra credit for peer reviews but never followed through. Completely unfair and a waste of time.
I would not recommend.

Honestly, a pretty fair professor. It is a decent amount of work for a one-credit class, but you have the option of taking it another sem for 3 credits. People also complain a lot about the quizzes, but if you read the textbook even the day of and make sure to write all the definitions down and read the rubric in the FRQ part, you will be fine.
Learned nothing

To get this started, Professor Putman is not a bad professor. The main reason for my rating would be the fact she is trying to shove so much information in a 1 credit class- which I somehow had more homework in than any of my 3 credit classes.

she is such a sweetheart and loves what she does but she gives so much work for 255. the quizzes are so odd its like a specific sentence from a specific part of the textbook and the entire quiz is on that, super unfair grading.

She is such an amazing professor. She grades very fairly and makes sure that any questions you have get answered. She genuinely cares and wants to see people succeed. Talking to her is extremely easy and as long as you put in the effort and do the work she will make sure that you get a decent grade. Read the textbook and take notes!
sorry i love you

She gave good lectures. She was engaging and connected stories to concepts, which helped deepen understanding. However, her grading practices were unfair, as she often strayed from the rubric, grading based on her subjective assessment rather than what students were doing, and she is difficult to talk to, as she tends to shut individuals down.

Professor Putman ALWAYS brings the energy to class and it makes it a lot of fun even during a 7:30am. She does a great job incorporating stories into her classroom which helps the lecture material soar in engagement and help legal concepts stick.
