Cara is, in many ways, an awesome person and professor; she cares for students and does not make you feel left out. She is always willing to meet with students to help them prepare for the weekly quizzes this course requires, but make sure you know your stuff, especially by reading the textbook.
the most harsh grader ever.

Professor Putman is extremely focused on helping students succeed in MGMT 254! Her quizzes are extremely difficult; however, if you got to office hours she will always go through answers with you and help you prepare for the future! 

Please make sure to go to class!!! If you do the lectures are full of stories that make the concepts jump out!

The quizzes are straight out of the textbook, and you won't do well if you only attend lecture. You have to read and watch all of the videos if you want to pass. Good opportunities for extra credit and easy points through participation.
attendance is marked, but you can miss a couple of classes. Class is straight forward.

Prof. Putman is an extremely kind and genuine individual who really cares about the work she does. On top of that she provides many good real world examples of the content and helps to really drive home the impact that law has in business. I really enjoyed her class!

Honestly, I think she was one of the best professors I've had at Purdue. I'm looking into TA-ing for her next year because I absolutely love the material she teaches, as well as her overall teaching style. Highly Recommend.

Cara is probably one of the most passionate professors I know. You can tell she puts a lot of effort into her lectures, and makes sure that each student understands the topic and whatever she is covering that day. She has great examples and tries to compare it to the real world as possible. It wasn't a lot of work; weekly quizzes and open note exam

At first Professor Putman was not my favorite. She's super bubbly and I'm not. I often see bubbly people as fake, but she's extremely genuine. She cares about her students and really tries to help her students retain the material.

Professor Putman is by far one of the best professors at Krannert. She ensures students understand the content and takes the time to connect with her students. I can't wait to take more of her classes in the future.

Prof Putman is a great professor to have. She makes her courses engaging and open to students interest. She has a fun way of teaching classes and does a good job catering to everyone. Would take again

Professor Loeb is clearly very passionate about what he speaks and lectures about. You can tell he cares about students' success, and he is the perfect professor for freshmen that are trying to get accustomed to college life.
The GOAT. Great guy. Lots of reading and in-class discussions, but he does his best to promote inclusivity/comfort.

Great professor and person. Eases you into the college class experience. One of the more generous graders and gives plenty of valuable feedback. Very understanding person too, and keeps us wanting to come to class.
Great lecturer, cares about students. Fairly easy to do well in the class.

Excellent professor with good lectures. Attendance is taken at random with one drop. Definitely a great class and counts for gen-ed credit. Tests aren't hard, and he provides a study guide that tells you what will be on the test.

Really chill and funny professor. Attendance is random (can miss one). Five short papers and three exams is all ur graded on. He gives great review sheets, and YouTube helped a lot. Didn't pay much attention and still did fine. Lectures are long (1h15m), but it's a laidback, interesting elective. Star Wars day best day

Great professor. He cares about the class and shows interest while lecturing. Probably the only class here that I retain the information I'm taught. Gives clear guidelines on assignments and exams so that you do well in class. Attendance is taken for a grade, but the class is interesting enough that it's not a problem even as a non-history major.

The semester is still not over, but this class is so much fun. Absolutely fantastic lectures and discussion 1-on-1, copious dad jokes, and interesting subject materials. Easy assignments and VERY clear guidelines for exams and assignments. Generally an amazing lecturer and a great person too. Knowledgeable and friendly. A great professor! 10/10
Great lectures, clear and understandable, along with enticing
bro gives amazing lectures. so much fun. absolutely love this class.
This professor is very kind and interesting content if you are open minded. He is easy to reach and invested in his student's success. Engaging classes. Texts are a bit heavy. Tough grader.

He gives out a studyguide for the exams which is very helpful. So helpful infact that I found that I did not need to pay attention in class at all. I found that simply going back through his posted slides and filling out the study guide helped me to get the highest grade in the class on the first exam and a 94 on the final. He is funny and great.

Loeb was a phenomenal professor who is clearly passionate about his classes, cats, and Star Trek. Besides his dad jokes, his lectures were truly interesting and engaging. The first one or two assignments can be a struggle, but his guidelines are decently clear and he is always ready to help when asked. I would recommend one of his courses to anyone

Dr. Loeb was an engaging lecturer and made complex historical content fun to learn. The assignments for HIST 30605 were not difficult, and expectations were clearly outlined for each. Dr. Loeb was extremely kind and answered questions fast and effectively. He was consistently willing to help students and truly seemed to care about their wellbeing.

Prof. Loeb was a fun and easy going teacher. For the semester we did have to read four books for the class, but he did select books that were easy and entertaining. The projects and assignments were simple and he provided examples of what they should look like. Also, he allowed us to ask for extensions on any assignments if we needed extra time.

She's not a good lecturer; she mostly reads off of slides but doesn't even seem to fully understand much of what she talks about. She tries to make jokes a lot but they come off as very awkward. Most of the time, you'll just be watching videos and doing handouts during class like in middle school. All of the assignments are really easy though.
Took ANTH 100 with her but couldn't select it. No quizzes or exams, super easy to get an A if you just do the homework. Homework is short but some assignments are kind of annoying.

Very nice person, but lacks at lecturing well. I didn't really understand more than half the lectures, and the info was random. Super easy class to pass though, and allows for creative freedom. ANTH230

I found the lectures super boring, and it felt like she was unprepared for most of our classes. However, she was a very lenient grader: 30% of your grade is based on attendance/completion, the quizzes are open-everything and you get 2 tries, and there are a lot of opportunities for extra credit. Some of the out-of-class assignments were pretty fun.

Really boring lectures but also a really easy class. Weekly ~150 word discussion posts, takehome quizzes, essays, and in class worksheets make up most of your grade. very lenient grader so its very easy to get an A with minimal effort. super accessible outside of class and a genuinely good person.

She's very knowledgable, passionate, and easy to approach. Assignments are not weighted heavily so as long as you pay attention and follow rubrics, it's easy to have a 100%. Genuinely a fascinating course, I would definitely recommend her section of it.

Nice professor but the class was pretty boring, however it was still easy. Graded on participation, 3 papers, and a few quizzes. Quizzes are easy and you get 2 tries. Papers are only 500 words and also pretty easy. Eklund is a good professor and manages to make the boring lectures a little more bearable.

She's incredibly easy to communicate with outside of class, I emailed her twice and she got back to me immediately and gave a lot of extra credit too. Lectures were a bit dull but she's an easy grader and the work doesn't rely on the lectures a ton.

I feel bad for Professor Eklund as she tries to be funny and relatable but most people aren't engaged. Would note that she acted very weird during the sex/gender unit, seemingly very uncomfortable with saying names of genitals in front of a bunch of adults. Not sure what that was. Otherwise very light and easy class, also interesting.
Really good professor, pretty easy grader. Emails with agenda every day, very organized.

She seemed nice, but the lectures were dull and dry. Having this class in the morning made it difficult to stay awake. Additionally, majority of the grade in the class relied on papers due throughout the semester. The grading criteria is very clear though so as long as you follow the rubrics closely you should be fine.

Professor Eklund was a very passionate teacher for this course (even when the class wasn't), and the lectures were clear, focused, and relevant. She provided a lot of flexibility for assignments and papers, and was always available to provide helpful feedback.
She grades very easily and is extremely accommodating to absences. She also wants us to engage in class and I thought the material was interesting. Its a great intro class to anthropology.

The class itself is interesting and I got a good internship related as I am interested, however this prof is actually the worst I have ever had. No clear grading criteria for final project, a lot of things to know for the exam and she ends up asking 25% of the full material. Also, I feel like she treats people with accents differently for real.

She is a good person but her lectures might be one of the most boring lectures I have ever had so far. She basically reads off of slides and adds couple extra information once in a while. I mean at that point, just learn by yourself. Exams are multiple choice and in-person, so no partial credits.

Her lectures are pretty straightforward, she pretty much reads off the slides and adds some additional info every once in a while. Most people started skipping class a couple weeks in and did fine. The tests can be difficult if you don't study much because you need to be able to apply concepts instead of just knowing definitions. Nice person
Ridiculously hard given it being an entry level class. Exams are difficult and sometimes do not relate to the course material.

Prof. Malone was a solid professor. The class was interesting, and the lectures covered everything needed for the exams. That being said, the lectures were a bit boring to sit through, but as long as you pay attention and take notes, you'll be fine. The class also isn't graded with many assignments, but the few assignments aren't terribly hard.

Dr. Malone's class is pretty easy. The homework takes maybe 15-30 minutes per week. 90% of the questions are covered in the slides. The exams are entirely memorization based. While attendance is not mandatory, I wouls reccomend going to class because she explains things very thoroughly.

Professor Malone is very nice, and she clearly loves her profession, but the lectures are pretty boring; she just reads WORD FOR WORD off the slides. The exams cover insane amounts of material, which makes the study guide extremely long. Overall, the material isn't very difficult; it's just really boring.
Great professor but exam were mainly based off of memorization. I felt like this class was difficult but probably because I have never taken a psychology course before

This was my 2nd class with Dr. Kraft & I have really enjoyed her! She only tests you on material covered on her slides and the study guide she provides. As long as you show up to discussions, do quizzes, and study some, you'll be fine. She made material enjoyable & class discussions were always good. I know she reads these, so shoutout Dr. Kraft!

Very straightforward lecturer and makes the content easy to understand. She doesn't require attendance unless it is a discussion day (once a week). As long as you stay on top of assignments, show up when needed, and study for a few hours before exams using the provided study guide you fill out, you will do well in this class.

Easy if you keep up and keep track of quiz and discussion deadlines. Exams aren't difficult, just memorization. Monotone lecturer and reads off slides, but will give helpful examples of concepts in class, which helps you remember the content. Will not test on material not covered in class. Show up, take notes, and review, and it will be an easy A.
Exams do not relate to the coursework provided. Ok overall

I really enjoyed having Mary Beth as a professor! You can truly tell that she cares about her students and is passionate about the class. This class was helpful in many ways and prepared me well to CODO. Although attendance is mandatory, she drops the lowest three grades. Show up, participate, and you'll do great!

Very caring prof. She serves as an advisor as well for current exploratory studies students and is very invested in the program. Always willing to talk to students about their interests and has clear grading criteria. EDPS 105 is a simple class but has a ton of projects and inventories to get you interested in a future career and different majors.

I LOVE Mary Beth. This class was very enjoyable and so useful in school and life. All of the activities we did were well planned and thought out. Mary Beth was always willing to help and easy to reach. She also would share other info through email that was super helpful. Show up to class and do your work and it'll be an easy A. Totally recommend.

She is very organized and lays out her grading very clearly so you're not surprised by anything. She also grades really easy but she's kinda passive aggressive. Really chill teacher though and if you do you're work really easy

I was in the honors section of the class and I really liked how she taught it. The grading creiteria was always very clear, if you follow the rubric you will be fine. You can tell she cares about her students, I felt comfortable asking her any question and she is very accesible and thorough by email. She always responds quickly.
Passive aggressive and condescending. Not sure if she actually cares.
Not super helpful, sometimes condescending, but the class content is good.

I absolutely hated this class. In my experience, Mary Beth was not flexible with anything. Due to medical reasons, occasionally I had to miss class and let her know as soon as I could ahead of time. However, I still received multiple F's and there was nothing I could do because I wasn't there. She's unreasonable, ridiculous, and the worst.

This class is very easy if you pay attention and complete your assignments. Mary Beth is engaging and you can tell she cares about your success. Her grading criteria is very clear, and you know what is expected of you. You need to bring your Course Pack to class everyday.

explains concepts relatively well and super patient and open to answering questions in class. you can tell he really cares that everyone understands the material. exams are really easy and feels fair. homeworks can take a while sometimes but still feels fair for a 3 credit class. only issue is that he doesn't post lecture notes or recording

Unbiased opinion, best mathematics lecturer among all the 265 Profs. Extremely reachable, deeply understands the topics, and clearly explains everything. If you just show up to class and listen you will be well prepared for exams already. Very open to questions in class and pauses to make sure everyone understands. 100% recommend.
He is the GOAT!
Been a great experience he really cares about his students and is good at lecturing. He goes slow and you can ask questions whenever. Highly reccomend taking MA265 with him

Excellent linear algebra lecturer. Wasn't in his section but attended his lectures because timing and location was convenient (course is synchronized across sections - schedule, HW, and exams are same). He explains all the concepts really well. The pacing of his lectures and balance between concepts and examples is really good in my opinion.
This professor was able to teach us the difficult material in an effective fashion. All around a stud, would have him as my professor again.

Too bad he's only a visiting professor. I hope Purdue hires him because he's the GOAT. He stops for questions and let's students answer questions instead of straight up lecturing the whole time. He also doesn't rush to complete the material like most of the other professors. I got a 100 on both of the midterms and I owe it to his teaching efforts.
I'm sure Dr. Wilson is a great person, but he's just not good at teaching. He straight up reads off his slides and has a monotone. Very boring to listen too.

Very nice guy but after taking a couple of his classes there are areas that lack structure. Overall this professor is accommodating and caring. Very homework and attendance heavy so be ready to put work in. Email responses take some time to get back.

Overall a very easy class. About 3 homework assignments a week, but all were pretty easy. Discussion Board assignments were on a website called PackBack, which uses AI to evaluate your posts which got pretty annoying.
Easy class. Only went to lecture earlier on in the semester.

Great professor for an easy A+. He is clearly passionate on his subject and cares deeply about his students. Professor Wilson and his team of TA's grades everything in a timely manner and are always easily accessible. 


However, it must be said that he speaks in a very monotone voice that can quite literally put someone to sleep.

Dr.Wilson is a great professor and he truly cares about his students. He is passionate about his profession. He does kind of talk in a monotone voice though so sometimes it's easy to tune out of listening to the lecture. He answers emails and makes it a priority to meet with students. He is an easy grader.
Good class, lots of reading and he really focuses on the notes.  Tests are hard if you want to do well and fairly easy for and average grade.  Lots of writing assignments.

Bras is a good guy, but I felt I did not get as much out of the course as I would have liked. Sometimes the grading is confusing, and it takes a long time to get grades back. To be fair, this semester was his first time teaching the class, but he could do better.

This prof is terrible. Makes you feel like he blames & laughs at work for no reason, doesn't listen to explanations of your essay. Ignores emails. Ask about your private but insults you. If you love writing but hate unfair criticism, avoid him. Find a supportive prof instead. DO NOT take the class - it's not worth the emotional toll.

He waited until the last day to put up grades. I asked him multiple times where I stand in the class and he kept saying I was looking really good. He barely responds to emails, and the class does not seem organized enough.
She's super nice, and cares about her student. The way she teach is super gooD. However, there will be many assignments.
If you want A don't take his class. He makes you think you are doing very well but gives you grades that you don't deserve at all.

I liked this professor's way of organizing the class. He let us know when we need to upload assignments and show up for class (we got LOTS of days off). He is super accessible and doesn't care about grammar mistakes but about understanding the material. My only "complaint" was not getting any grades back until two weeks before classes ended.

Horrible prof. Focuses way too much on the grammar instead of actual content. Stay away from him if you are an international student and wants an A. He doesn't like giving As to International students because their native language is not English and should be glad to receive a "B".

Seemed constantly confused, poor lectures, spent about a month doing nothing but lecturing on different kinds of sorting algorithms. She clearly didn't practice her slides and wasted huge amounts of time.
yeah i did not know what was ever happening in this class tbh
Extremely boring lectures. Does not explain concepts well.

Do NOT take if possible. Is not a good lecturer and cannot explain concepts smoothly without losing train of thought. Bad time management across lectures: will spend half one class on a basic example, then rush through examples on the hardest topic of the year. Loses students attention without 4 weeks of course.

Thick Chinese accent makes it hard to understand anything. It honestly feels as if she doesn't know what she is talking about. Homework is a pain, especially writing it in LaTeX which most people haven't used before. CS182 is known for being a dumpster fire of a course so don't feel too bad if you aren't doing as good as you hoped.

There was a big disconnect between what was taught and what was on the homework. The average person without any background in discreet math simply cannot do the homework with just attending the lectures. You need to attend office hours and/or PSOs. Exams were slightly easier than homeworks. Be prepared to self-study a lot.

This professor, and by extension, this class is one of the worst experiences I have had while at Purdue University. To start, this is one of the most disorganized courses I have ever taken in my life. There is a substantial disconnect between what the professor talks about, and the professor doesn't seem to realize other courses exist.

Just read off the slide in lectures. You are expected to know things that are not taught in lecture, with no specific mention of it. Course felt really disorganized. I learned more watching youtube videos. Ended up sleeping through most of the lectures i did attend.

I had selke for 182 when she co-taught with Dey and she was great. She was very easy to approach and was very helpful, willing to take the time to re-explain topics and go over things with students. One of the better CS profs I've had here.

He is very lecture-heavy and not very good at demonstrating how to do problems. If you are someone who learns by watching the problem be solved step by step, you might have to do a little more work for this class. Exams were hard, but he gave plenty of material to study.

I was worried with the bad reviews, but most of the content was not hard to follow. Guessing that most of the reviews are from an earlier period. The exams were difficult, but we were told what to study. This course was more difficult than most but not the hardest I have ever taken.
Disagree with the previous rating. Professor Oh has prior experience and makes reasonable exams
Disagree with the previous rating. Professor Oh no has no prior experience and makes the exams unnecessarily hard.
He knows what he is talking about. Concepts are pretty clear. Although I would say that the class is challenging

I previously took AT-362 with this professor, and my rating was significantly different. This class was well organized, and the lectures/classes were engaging. The attendance policy is a little extreme though and some of the material I felt I did not learn much from.

How well you like this class is really dependent on your group and TA. Kelly is a good lecturer and always looking for feedback on how to improve the course, and he's very accessible outside class. It's really easy to do well in the class even if your fictional company doesn't do well

I'm currently in his AT362 and enjoy it conditionally. Warnings first--directions for course work can be kind of loose at times and can be frustrating when it reflects how you do in the simulation(Always ask your TAs for guidance. They helped a ton). Lots of group work. If you have a competitive spirit(non-grade reflective) you'll have a good time.

Shaun Kelly was a nice guy but the way he teaches AT362 is pretty bad. He constantly changes the rules of the scenarios and tbh the simulation is pretty unrealistic compared to real world standards. Lectures are on point though. 

Worst thing is the amount of busywork required

Tldr this is SATT equivalent of TECH120, avoid this class and Kelly.

I took AT-362 a few semesters ago, which I believe was his first time teaching the class. Things were sort of disorganized, and the grading was tough. However, he did end up being very generous with extra credit so most students did well. He did make an effort to improve the course, so hopefully it is better now. He does still use AI way too much.

The midterm and final time was bad, but he doesn't have much control over it. Otherwise, he is a really good lecturer and explains the concept clearly. He also included some funny examples during lecture, such as the "vs robot chess", where the robot won by going out the box. He also isn't afraid to call out students who talk loudly during lecture.

Very knowledgeable lecturer and responds to emails quickly. Our lecture was at 6pm so they were recorded which is extremely useful when the assignment deadline or exam is coming even if you went to the lecture. The final was a little rough but there was a curve. Make sure to do the extra credit on the homework assignments.

Very good lecturer. He explains concepts pretty well, and most content on the exams and homeworks are taken from these lectures or practice exams. Even if an exam is hard, he curves very generously. A decent chunk of extra credit was also given. Overall it was an easy and fun class.

Awful scheduling in the evening so I never really attended any lectures in person. The lecture slides and videos had examples of how to do various procedures step-by-step, and they were pretty clear. However, the programming homework was kind of  difficult

The best professor I've ever had for all my AI focused computer science courses. He keeps lectures engaging with slides that heavily utilize step-by-step examples and the occasional meme. He gives detailed, in-depth, step by step explanations in Ed Discussion. His exams are difficult, but if you truly pay attention, you shouldn't have an issue.

Jinsuh is probably the 2nd best professor I have had at Purdue, if you don't want to pay attention at all to his lectures and blow this class off, you can still do fine. But I would recommend listening because he is a great teacher and knows this material flawlessly. Homework is light, projects can be tough but if you aren't on track he helps you.

Most unhelpful professor I have ever had. Instead of helping when I missed the first week of class for travel - He told me to drop the class. I stayed and ended up doing just fine. Very concerned about rudimentary policy. Ex: wont answer a question unless you copy your team and/or post it on the discussion board.

Lectures are informative and imo, somewhat interesting. However, he wasn't good at teaching R Studio and it was always a mess bc he went too fast w/o making sure everyone was on the same page. He is condescending and not helpful when it comes to questions. Also find a really solid group for the project bc it'll make or break you life and grade.

This class is fairly easy, however the professor is one of the worst professors I have ever had.The group project that the end is 35% of your grade. My groups google ads account got suspended and he did nothing to help. His lectures are boring, meaningless, and repeats himself a lot. He is also every rude during office hours. I DO NOT recommend him

Jinsuh is a nice guy, for sure. If he is new at teaching, then disregard the following statements. But, I have noticed that he is pretty unorganized at times, and not prepared in the likely event something goes wrong. Be aware that he is very big on teamwork in group projects, and won't answer you if you don't cc your team members.

For a class that is deceivingly difficult at times, he does a good job teaching the concepts at a reasonable level, allowing us to get practice and develop an intuition for the subject. The grading is lenient with opportunity for extra credit, albeit at the expense of a lot of homework. He's passionate about math and it shows, but hope you are too.

I took ma416 in person, so experience might be different from people who took it online version.
But for me, lectures were great, homeworks were helpful and exam was mostly from the materials covered during the lecture. Also he had a lot of supplementary materials.
I would say he's a great professor for this course, recommend him.

Dr. Lancette was strictly ok. His explanations of the material were fine, and most of hte content was doable, though the final exam was quite difficult. The zoom quizzes were bizzare and worth far too much and the course (at least for online students) was poorly managed as the first exam wasn't graded until after we took the final.

Quick on feedback and provides decent materials and lectures.
However, the exams are extremely difficult and sometimes include material that wasn't covered in the lectures.
You will learn a lot, but your gpa will likely to go down.
he's just another one of the professors that reads off of the slides, but he lists past exams so studying for the exams is fine

Gill is sharp, and a decent guy. My problem was the severe discrepancy between material covered in class, homework and the exams. Exam questions seemed to be incredibly difficult compared to anything we practiced with. Since game theory is a niche subject, it was also hard to find samples to practice with. Classes were interesting though.

Gill is a nice man. He tries to make his lectures fun and interesting although students definitely don't appreciate it. Exams are hard but that's because Game Theory is hard. He doesn't give trick questions and provides multiple past exams and practice homework sets to give you an idea of questions that will be asked.

The answers to Problem Set (assignment) and the exam practice are misleading. Only results are shown. No process. However, in the exam grading, TAs are crazy to criticize the details of the process. In addition, TAs are resistant to accept their faults, and David usually agrees with the TAs. In short, TAs in this class are tough graders

He taught the material well and went into depth about the topic. The homework compliments the course well and at the right amount. Nonetheless, exam is the difficult part as there are no partial credits. A visit to the office hour also shows that the professor is not caring.

I thought he was a great teacher who showed he was very informed and passionate of the subject he was teaching. I read the Iliad, Odyssey, and Aeneid in his class and he provided useful and interesting context and commentary for them.

Overall a good professor who is passionate about what he teaches and cares about his students' learning. We had three papers in his class and a couple of other short writing assignments. Sometimes I felt like he was a little harsh with grading, but overall I feel like he was a good professor, and it's not hard to pass the class if you show up.
He was a great professor. As long as you participate in class, once a week, and listen to his requirements for your papers, we wrote three total, you'll get a good grade.
She explained concepts very poorly and largely could not answer questions. She spent all lecture writing long equations instead of actually showing us how to use them or doing examples.

Overall not a bad class if you rely heavily on the textbook for understanding the concepts, but Prof tends to get flustered and abrupt when unable to sufficiently explain certain concepts to students or adequately address questions.

This was Dr. Schultz's first semester at Purdue. I think she did reasonably well. The biggest complaint is that she basically taught word-for-word out of the textbook with few examples or explanations. She was accessible and helpful in office hours and by email.
I loved being in this class, she went at the perfect pace for me and answered questions effectively

her 9:30 class had low attendance and I was in the 12:30. She started giving us extra credit for it. Also attend the review sessions for exams and come prepared with questions because she'll usually hint what is on the exam + ask ur PSO TA's about the exams they'll tell you the difficulty of it sometimes.

She taught first semester, the exam wasn't too bad. Even though attendance isn't mandatory lectures! You WILL fall behind if you dont. She does a really good job of explaining, uses lots of videos and visuals to help people. Also PSOs are not mandatory but they give you the answers at the end and have good explanations.

Dr. Chmielewski is an amazing lecturer. Her lectures are often interactive, and she moves at the perfect pace. She is great at answering questions and creates an inviting environment. The class itself is pretty easy and requires a bit of memorization. Exams are very fair and get graded quickly. Homework is minimal. Overall, great professor.

Great attitude towards organic chemistry and enjoyable lectures. She sawed stuff in half and had us smell different enantiomers. She seems to care about the students and explains concepts well enough that practice and the textbook can clarify a lot of stuff.

I love this professor with my whole heart and wish she taught all my classes. She made chemistry make sense and I'm SO mad they swapped professors halfway through. She's awesome. Throwback to that time she sawed a cooler in half to explain chirality.

Prof Jean has been the best chem professor I have ever had. She teaches at a good speed and provides not packets that are very helpful. Her exams are not too hard or long. The lectures and Psos don't have attendance because she respects students' ability to choose how to spend their time. You don't need to buy the model kit that she asks you to.

Excellent lecturer. Teaches OChem in a very understandable way with lots of opportunities to practice. Prepares you very well for exams and for 256. One of the most passionate science lecturers out there

She melted away my deep-seated fear of organic chemistry and helped me to discover that I'm actually good at it! She makes intimidating concepts look doable and has many ways to help us visualize concepts, such as demonstrating chirality with a saw! I hope future CHM255ers can have her as she is one of the most amazing professors I've ever had
I love her so much. She's such a great teacher that genuinely wants you to learn the content. One of the best professors I have had!

She is a great professor who seems to care about what she is teaching and how her students are doing. Lecture is not required, but if you go, the content makes much more sense. Most of your grade is based on exams, but she does offer some extra credit opportunities.

She made CHM256 enjoyable even though its a tough class! Her lectures were really thorough, and she always made time to answer all of our questions. Most of the grade points come from the midterms and final exam, so make sure that you understand all of the lecture material for those and do the problems in the back of the textbook. Overall, great!

Had a really good experience in CHM256 with her. Her lectures were always really helpful and she has a really good way of explaining things. She was always willing to answer questions and overall just make a tough class very manageable.
Great professor that cares for her students. Definitely need to watch her lectures to succeed in the class.
Shes a good person, she cares. Give her chance and shes got a pretty good joke or two during lecture. Also, she has a few technical difficulties with her IPad but she figures it out.
Chmielewski is awesome. Excellent lecturer who really cares about her students. You cant go wrong with either her or Uyeda, 2 of the best professors in the chem department.

I had her for both orgo 1 and 2. She doesnt do hw but does give problems to do at the back of the book those are very helpful. She knows how to teach Orgo very well. Its A LOT of work but she provides you with all the resources to do a good job. She answers question in lecture too. Notes packets are huge but she tells how to summarize for final

She gives really clear explanations of lecture material and is always open to answer questions. 255 and 256 are graded mostly by exams, but the grading scale is pretty generous and she gives a lot of extra credit so the class isn't too hard.

Professor Chmielewski is an amazing lecturer. I had her for Orgo 1 (the first Organic Chemistry course: CHM 255) and she decoded organic chemistry into something I could understand and follow throughout the course. Orgo became my favorite class because she made it fun and easy to understand. You have to go to lecture because she doesn't record them

Absolutely love Dr Chmielewski! She made organic chemistry fun, relevant and not too hard. Offers multiple extra credit opportunities, some of them very easy. Explains concepts well. Do the back-of-the-chapter problems for extra practice. Get the model kit because sometimes you just need to see the molecule. Plus, you can use it on exams.
She is an amazing professor who cares and wants everyone to do well. She gives you many resources to help. She does not do boiler cast but does hand out note packets every week that you just fill in.

She's a great professor, it's obvious she wants to make sure students understand the material. If you go to every lecture and recitation it is very possible to get a B or A with the extra credits she also gives. My tip: mechanisms seems to give people the most issues draw it again and again with simple and then more complex structures!

As if CHM 255 wasn't hard enough, Dr. Chmielewski was ok at teaching it. Her lectures were interesting, but the subject itself is just difficult. It didn't help that the class was only out of 500 points (4 homeworks & 3 exams) with no curve!

Dr. Chmielewski is awesome. She makes organic chemistry feel simple, and she has so much experience teaching the class that her lectures are well-understood and super organized. There aren't a lot of grades outside of exams, but a lot of extra credit is offered, especially if you go to weekly recitation.

I LOVE THIS PROFESSOR. She makes something as daunting as O Chem seem like a fun challenge. Well structured lectures keep your attention and the note packets she gives out at the beginning of each week are EXTREMELY helpful. Use the packets to study for each test and you will pass without a problem. 255 is very test heavy, but with plenty of EC.

He has a fairly thick accent but it's still pretty easy to understand him. He does a good job at getting through the lecture material but he tends to go on long tangents, sometimes up to 15-20 minutes going over material detail not at all relevant to the course or assignments. The textbook is optional and he takes time to answer student questions.
His accent is quite thick, so it may take some time to get used to understanding what he says. He does provide some amount of examples. Overall, I think Selke or Grama's lectures were better.
Once you understand his accent and take notes the class is pretty easy
i luv pookie bear szpawznky
Professor is an incredible. He really knows his stuff and makes every lecture interesting. Exam is reasonable.

Incredibly hard to follow—mumbling, speaking away from the mic, and rushing through material at an unreasonable pace. Explanations often contradict/mess up the provided content on the slide, and their writing on the projector usually off the screen, making it nearly impossible to keep up or understand. Please go to any other professors lecture.

Extremely revered professor who fits the typical stereotype of a math professor. The extent of his knowledge is so vast that it is probably not suited for an intro class like CS182 but his optional lectures where he goes beyond the lecture material are thoroughly engaging.

When I try to think of stereotypical math professors, he definitely gives off that vibe. I thoroughly enjoyed sitting in his class even though at times he can overcomplicate. I personally love the old-school style he has and even though the course was intense, he explained things well.

Prof Szpankowski is a brilliant man who struggles a bit with conveying what he knows to a freshman audience. His accent makes him difficult to understand at times. He did lots of examples in class, some of which were more easy to follow than others. I liked him because he showed how to prove lots of things, which I enjoy.

He's not a bad person or anything, but as a famous mathematician, I would expect a little bit more refined teaching style. He just read off of Sellke's slides and gave us examples that sometimes helped or just weren't very relevant, wasting time. I didn't feel overly prepared for exams/homeworks but the grading is often pretty generous.

I really enjoy taking CS182 with Prof. Szpankowski. The course is very organized but you should expect to submit 1 homework every week. The exams were very fair and doable if you actually solved the homework yourself.
He is almost impossible to understand and the TAs under him are extremely unreasonable and do not help at all.

Definitely not for everybody but compared to the nightmare of 240 this class is great. The homeworks are a solid amount of work but nothing crushing. I find him to be very good at explaining everything well but I came in with a higher than average background in some of the topics covered. Overall its a class anyone can do well in if you dont slack.

He's okay at best, his lectures are very very boring to watch through and often very difficult to understand. He will go through examples during the lecture that are insanely difficult and then usually say something along the lines of "make sure you understand this". The issue is that he will only go over this one example, then move on.

Fantastic teacher who really cares about his students. However, the class is quite hard and you really have to work for it. Never skip the lectures. The PSO sessions can be worthless, depending on your TA. Go to office hours if you need them.

Szpankowski is not a terrible lecturer, but he's not good either. His accent is understandable, but his lectures get very boring very fast, and you can learn just as much by just reading the class notes and the textbook.
Do not take any class from him if you can. He seems to be a professor that the department keeps on to bring in the research money.
He explains things in a long and convoluted way. I found better information in two sentences on the internet than his hour and fifteen minute lecture.

Very hard to understand his accent, which is muffled by way he positions his microphone, has bad handwriting, pronounces most words incorrectly, does ridiculously long problems in class that will only make you more confused, jumps around the material during lecture, etc. I couldn't follow his lectures, so I read the book, which helped tremendously.

Be prepared  for the worst professor ever. Unannounced quizzes over material not covered in class. Homeworks that are due in 10 days are moved up to due in two days. My turned-in homework was lost! Our midterm was moved up by two days with short notice. Professor mumbles and writes on blackboard, so you have to arrive early for a seat at the front.

simply cannot understand with low volume, not clear with explaining the materials. if one person gets pointed out by talking, he will complain it about for at least three minutes. it happens in mostly 13 of every class.
Hard to understand, has pop quizzes, lecture topic order sometimes doesn't make any sense. He was replaced midway through the semester which is why I ended up with a B, I had a C before he left.
All I can say is if you can understand what is going on during lecture you've already accomplished a miracle.

Horrible teacher. Does not care about this class. You can't understand his horrible accent and it is made worse by the fact he doesn't know how to use a microphone. He doesn't actually teach this course, he more just does really difficult examples that have nothing to do with homeworks during lectures.  Tests are rediculous.
He doesn't care about this class at all.

More on the class, less on him. The workload in this class is insane for a 2 credit course. The homeworks/projects took way too much time and were graded strictly. This class needs to be organized better.

He's argumentative and narrow-minded. He doesn't help/review coursework out before tests/midterms. Before our final he told us we weren't allowed to ask him or any of our TA's questions to help us prepare.

Great professor that obviously knew what he was talking about. Lectures were very important despite not being mandatory. The time spent on homework every week was more than all of my other classes combined but definitely worth it. Midterm is tough and definitely do not put off the final project!

Professor Cory is a professor that clearly cares about his students. The homework assignments are lengthy, usually taking 3+ hours per assignment, but he works through many of the assignments in lecture. Don't put off any projects, especially the final project which easily takes 50+ hours to complete.

I never thought I'd ever find a class with such a workload for 2 credits. Clark is great outside of class. very understanding when it comes to health and personal matters outside of the classroom. But in class, its like a flip switches. If you don't understand it the first time, good luck... and be ready to spend a lot of hours on AutoCAD.

Clark Cory is one of the best professors I had at Purdue. As a civil eng. student, I feel like I have used his material the most of any other class in my internships. He is caring and understanding, down to earth, and teaches fundamentals that you will forever use. The class can be time consuming with assignments, but they truly make a difference.

Has real experience and is a pretty decent lecturer. Not difficult, just make sure you work on the final project well ahead of the deadline. Sometimes instructions are unclear or simply wrong so talk to your TA if you have a question. Midterm is pretty brutal so if you aren't decent at CAD by then that sucks. HW can feel like busy work

This is for CM360. Clark is extremely disrespectful, and will send very unprofessional and unclear emails. One of his emails has read “nope try again” in response to a very lengthy emails asking questions. This is especially unforgiving due to the fact that this class is only in person one day of the week. He also does not use bright space. 0/10

Prof. Cory was so much fun. He posted video demonstrations on how to do the CAD/Revit assignments. Easy-going. Midterm is really hard, simply because you don't have enough time. Labs never last full time. Class only meets at 5:30pm.

Clark gives you everything you need to succeed in this course (recorded lectures, lots of office hours, TAs), all you need to do is put in your end of the work. Don't start final project last minute and you'll be fine. Clark cares for his students and even promotes opportunities for students outside of class like internships and career fairs etc.

Professor Cory was a great lecturer, and his CGT 164 class is easily passable as long as you attend or watch the lectures. He also has a great deal of experience in the construction industry, so don't be afraid to come to him with any questions.
He is awful. 0 help and always rude to students when you don't understand something. Ta's run his class and are the only way to be somewhat successful in his class.

Clark is great. He is kind and compassionate. He really cares about your education, and he wants you to succeed. The course is mainly one big group project the entire semester, so choose your group wisely. Coordination and communication are key.

Clark is a great professor who is super approachable and helpful outside of class. The course itself feels more like a 3 credit class with the amount of work given. Being a complete beginner in the software used in the class, there was a bit of a learning curve, but once again Clark's help outside of class was greatly appreciated.

Would highly recommend taking this class over the summer. Feels very high school esque with the sheer amount of busy work assigned every week. The class is only 2 credit hours, but will most likely be the class you spend the most time working on assignments for. Professor Cory is super approachable and will help you if you need it, though.
Easy to talk to, and open to meet outside office hours. Records all his classes so you do not need to attend. The videos are very helpful.

Clark is kind and really cares about how you do in class. He pushes you to learn the material and do your best, but never be afraid to ask questions if you are confused as he is readily available to answer any questions you might have. The course can be a lot if you don't have any CAD/Revit experience, but go to him if you are having any issues.

While this class is a lot of content for only 2 credits, Clark is amazing both in and out of class. He is available every day in his office - he will help a lot with sketching assignments, AutoCAD, and Revit if you go to office hours. Overall super nice professor and guy.

Professor Cory, more so than any other instructor I've had at Purdue, genuinely seems to care that his students are prepared to hit the ground running when they start their careers. His lectures are dense but informative, and you will have no problem passing the class - even if you have no prior cad experience - if you show up and put the time in.

Great guy. Has a lot of experience in the construction industry and it shows. If you pay attention during lecture, you will know how to do the projects. He also posts recordings of the lectures online for your reference. Weekly quizzes are super easy. Usually responds to emails within 2 hours regardless of time of day.
He was rude and did not really help at times. His lectures were very useful, but the sample videos at times are very confusing.

He is a wonderful professor who is very helpful. If you have any questions you can always go to his office hours and he is happy to help. He also has many connections and will help point you in the right direction. Very nice and the class is not too bad as long as you stay on top of things.
Great Professor

Dr. Cory Clark is an excellent professor who wants the best for his students and it shows from feedback whenever you ask, texting him a question about the project at 2am, or stopping by his office to grab snacks. He has industry experience and many contacts outside of Purdue to help you get in touch with companies. Excellent teacher!

Easy class. Assignments are clear and mostly just replicate what you see. He gives quick instruction on how to do it in class but you only need to go to one of the two lectures for quizzes. He's extremely personable and easy to talk to outside of class for help. Also, he doesn't give out "minus" grades, so a 90% is an A.

I really enjoyed how Professor Urba made her lectures fun and engaging. There are some derivations she goes over you don't need to know, but they're interesting, nonetheless. I also appreciate that the course is structured such that if you don't do well on one exam, it won't completely destroy your grade. Study hard for the exams though.

The annoying thing is that the lectures had iclickers that you had to get right to get points. It was annoying and I felt like during lecture, we spent too much time on proofs. I did not enjoy being in lecture at all.

Professor Urba is great, especially for a challenging course. Her funny, clear metaphors make tough concepts much easier to understand. More test prep would be helpful, but reviewing recitations, iClicker questions, and practice problems is the best way to prepare for the exams.
She is a good lecturer who teaches well and actually understands the material. Her accent is sometimes hard to understand but in general she teaches the course really well.

She teaches using mostly slides, walking us through equations and derivations of other equations from those. She uses some demonstrations and interacts with the class well. She is not overly serious and can be fun.

Maybe I'm slow but the lectures were kind of hard to grasp, especially the derivation heavy ones, so I ended up not paying attention off during the derivation parts since you don't need them for exam (I know, bad mentality). I ended up self studying the entire course myself which is not fun. On the bright side she's energetic and passionate!

Took the professor for PHYS221, not a bad class but understanding the formula sheet goes a long way on the exams. Urba is a very good professor who you can feel cares heavily about the subject and teaches it very well. However, some of the slides are a bit confusing but overall not bad. Uses iClicker for attendance.

Good prof. Sometimes lectures get really heavy into derivations that you may not need but it's still good to see how the equations you will use are derived. Looking at the slides before lectures and writing down some key points is very helpful. Try to as well as you can on the midterms since they are weighted more than the final.

Professor Urba did a great job at teaching this course. While the slides do include derivations, they are very important to build intuition to the real physics at play. She stresses key ideas over memorizing formulae, which is the best way to truly learn the material. Exams were very fair and the course is very well structured. Totally recommend.

Prof. Urba is such a kind lecturer, but at times her lectures are boring and hard to engage with. The exam were extremely difficult, but the content is also just hard to learn through a lecture format. There was about a 2-3% curve, but our exam averages were also pretty low. Lectures are mandatory attendance except for a few absences.

Urba is a great lecturer. She has an accent but it is bearable and understandable. However, her slides are very derivation heavy which can confuse many. The class itself is fairly difficult compared to 172.

Great 241 lecturer. Had a decent number of derivations, but they weren't crucial for the exams. Covered all contents at a good pace with excellent slides posted to BSpace. Know the equations on the sheets and know the homeworks well to succeed on exams

Urba is an excellent lecturer. She has derivation-heavy lectures that often go beyond the scope of the tests. The homework and review packets are better representations of the questions that are in the tests. Tests were often miserable as nearly every single (multiple choice) question would have both the correct answer and the same answer negated.

Urba is very clear in lecture with good explanations. There is iClicker for points every lecture, but it's a small percent of your grade so it's not the end of the world if you miss some. Mid to low exam averages, but fair otherwise.
For phys 221. Switch out of Xie's section and into this one. I was in Xie's section but watched all of Urbas videos. She is the best. Very caring, good at explaining

Prof Urba is a great professor who knows the content very well. Sometimes it can be a bit confusing in lecture, but as long as you do the homeworks and don't cheat yourself it shouldn't be too bad of a class. Make sure to practice homework problems along with the test review guides as the review guides don't always reflect what will be on the test.
Enjoyed her lectures and she was a reasonable professor. Answered questions and gave help to those who asked.
Professor Urba's class was really relaxing to be in. She explained the concepts well and made sure we understoof everything. The class was a breeze with her teaching the concepts.

Professor Urba is a caring professor, yet has a lot to improve in terms of teaching. The overall lecture is very hard to grasp in class, as she goes very fast. The questions in the class are nothing like the questions in the exam. The exams are very hard, and the exam average was only 60% even though attendance is mandatory.

She would often move too quickly and read directly off slides while lecturing. Concepts were hard to understand as a result. I found myself having to study a lot outside of class. In addition, time management was an issue. She would start the (graded) lecture quizzes after the class had already ended- I was lucky my next class was close.

Urba is a great professor who actually cares about students. She explains the material really well, always replies to emails, helps students outside of class, and shows the derivation process. She talks very fast and covers a lot of material, so sometimes I had to go back and watch recordings of lectures.
Prof. Urba is definitely a good choice for PHYS 272. Her lectures are intuitive and easy to understand. She is also very humorous. She is caring to her students.

Professor Urba does a great job at explaining the content in PHYS241, but the lectures are extremely fast. You'll definitely have to spend time outside of class to digest the material. If you need help, Professor Urba is extremely helpful, and she really cares about your understanding of the content.

Urba is a good lecturer, she gauges the class, does a good job conveying content, is very nice and very helpful outside of class. Her main downside is for some slides she just reads formulas off slides for a large chunk of the lecture which puts a lot of people to sleep, but its physics so its fair.
Very helpful outside of class. Lectures were very theory and derivation heavy, but out of lecture resources help understanding. Good at answering questions and even following up outside of class.
Do all the extra credit and your grade should be fine. Not a lot to help u prepare for exams, prof kind of annoying but besides that it's fine

Professor Eagan has extensive experience in accounting, a true expert in his field. He's definitely one of the better lecturers at Purdue. He presents the information in a way that even those not in accounting can understand it. My only criticism is that there are limited resources to help prepare you for the exam, compared to other sections

Professor Eagan keeps students engaged even in late classes, making lectures both fun and informative. He explains tough concepts clearly, sets up timelines that prepare you for exams, and ensures success if you follow them. Accessible office hours and staying after class allowed me to get all possible questions answered. Amazing class!
Avoid taking this professor. They do not provide study guides and do not prepare you well for exams—it is more of a do-it-yourself approach. Other professors offer extra credit and curve grades.

Lectures were pretty boring, but overall a really nice guy and cares about you. Definitely take advantage of his office hours if possible. Class is test heavy and it is import to stay on top of readings and listen in lecture.
Alright tax prof but weirdly obsessed with the one big beautiful bill act

Get ready for a high octane journey through the world of accounting with professor Eagan that'll have you laughing one moment and intrigued the other. Professor Eagan is a great professor who clearly knows what he is talking about and breaks down the concepts for everyone. He is hilarious and wants you to succeed.

Honestly one of the best professors I had at Daniels. JT makes tax fun. He genuinely cares about his students. The tax project was interesting, my only feedback is that I wish the meeting grading was a bit more consistent. He challenges you because he wants you to do better. Overall, I took a lot away from this class. Definitely take him if you can

JT is an engaging professor but felt quite condescending 1 on 1. 504 has a ton of HW assignments, 4 exams, and a giant tax project. The project is difficult if you're not good at learning new softwares quickly, because you are provided virtually no help, at least for part 1. 2 exams were in person, the last 2 were online and all multiple choice.

Great guy, but he's very arrogant. He keeps the class engaging, but you can tell he's doing it because it's his job. His biggest claim throughout the semester was that exams are meant to test understanding, not memorization, which is false. The exams are mainly about memorization. He also claims he doses believe in curving grades.

How Eagan has anything below a 5 is shocking. By far the best professor in the business school. The project is extremely well made. The class can be a lot of work at times but it's all intentional. I've learned more in his class than any other at Purdue.

No bad words about this guy. He is funny, kind, and understanding. The class is an adjustment. I struggled a bit. Eagan was nice to have because he genuinely broke it down into something I could digest. Only class I actually wanted to attend this semester. Lots of HW but it's ALL very helpful for the exams. Study it. You're in good hands with him.

If you have the option to take MGMT200 with Eagan, do it! He makes the lectures engaging, even if they're at 4:30 in the afternoon. Go to his office hours if you want feedback on a test or honestly just want career/future advice. He's super easy to talk to and is always in a good mood!

He's probably my favorite professor. He makes the course and projects enjoyable. Exams are challenging, but extra credit and the final project compensate for low grades. Visiting his office hours is helpful. He gets to know you and helps. The final project can be daunting, but using the time and his resources makes it manageable.

Professor is extremely chill (dissed Bronny James at one point in a lecture) and gives fantastic lectures. Attendance is taken by iClicker but he's very lenient with those points. Met with him 1on1 in his office hours and he was chill af. Course is very well organized; exams are worth an absurd amount of your grade so don't underestimate them.

Very charismatic and loves students—he really made an impact. When I politely asked about a grading mistake, his reply was unexpectedly harsh, which stung since I respected him a lot. But months later, he greeted me warmly in the hallway as we chatted. Kind of a “sour, then sweet” personality, but overall memorable and one of the best at Purdue.
nice and cracks some jokes and teaches good

Dude is pretty intense. He gives out exams way too frequently and assigns literally everything in the textbook. He doesn't really understand that his students have lives sometimes too with this workload. 

However he is an excellent teacher; he boils down concepts very easily and makes it super digestible to his students. He also makes dad jokes.

The professor was really caring and explained the terms you need to know very well. However, there was a lot of homework that you have to get done before a certain time period. For the exams, you just need to watch the videos and do the exercises and you will be fine.

He's awesome! Tax project is fun but a lot of work so start early.
A lot of HW to keep up with. Do all the extra credit!!

Professor Eagan is easily the best professor I've ever had. His lectures are straightforward and go by fast, and he explains everything so well. You have plenty of time to get homework done, and he usually makes Friday lectures online. Exams weren't too long or difficult. As long as you actually pay attention, this class is an easy A or B.

Amazing Professor. Lectures went by fast, and you genuinely learn at his lectures. Homework is an easy A, and he gives you plenty of time to do it on your own schedule, not his. He usually doesn't do lectures on Fridays just for everyone else's sake, and his exams are directly related to homework.
This was a great class. The lectures were enjoyable and went by fast. The work isn't difficult if you do it on time. I have really no interest in accounting but he made it engaging and interesting.

I really loved class with Eagan. His lectures were very straight forward and the iclickers (for points) he did use, made sense. The exams followed EXACTLY the homework and ICE. I really enjoyed class with Eagan and as long as you don't wait until the last minute to do the assignments, this class is easy.

Professor Eagan couldn't have done a better job teaching this course. He always comes to class with tons of energy and always cracks a joke during class. He enjoys getting to know his students in anyway possible. He is an excellent professor who I can count on to give me the best advice in the future. I can't wait to take future classes with him.
For a senior level class, genuinely surprised he's still allowed to teach this after like 7 years. Don't think anyones had a good experience with him, at least for IE486.
Literally the worst professor I have ever had at purdue. Reads off pdfs for lectures without clear guidance on anything.

Professor Caldwell's lectures are very unique in that he has no slides but rather has paragraphs written out in a Word document, and his lectures are more or less reading them and expanding upon them. It makes for somewhat frustrating notetaking but the grad-level class is more of a thoughtful dialogue.
Stay away from this professor at all costs. Cannot teach to save his life.

He just gets word files on the projector and just keeps reading it (tries to joke sometimes- but he's so bad at them), For IE 590 he'll ask the TAs to set a score of 83 and to start deducting/adding points from there. Just a bad professor. Don't ever take a graduate class with him. The grading is so random!. I hurt my GPA so bad with this class.

He is a fair professor as he challenges graduate students to go out and do the extra bit research in order to be prepared for lectures and assignments. Though it can be difficult to understand how things are being graded he is fair. I wish him and his TAs didn't grade as hard for grammar, but overall I learned a lot in regards to systems thinking.

Worst worst Prof ever. Few guidelines for paper while grading extremely harsh. There was no rubric and they just gave out points as they felt like. Projects submitted by companies are very easy and we were asked to do simple tasks again and again and again. To be honest it is really wasting time. Barely learned anything. Avoid him for sure.
I would rather commit suicide than take another class with this professor. This guy is incorrigible and apathetic.

This prof does not teach but rather discusses vague ideas related to statistics. He spends little time in class showing the mechanics behind solving a problem and does not use examples in class. The TA is the only hope. Avoid this professor at all costs.

One of the worst profs I have had at Purdue. I spends the entire lecture reading paragraphs of jibberish and spends no time teaching how to do actual problems. He has yet to do an example and this is a math class.

He is the worst professor I have had in all my four years hear at Purdue. That pretty much sums it up. I've been in the class for over two months now and haven't learned a thing. Classes like his are the reason IE are referred to as "Imaginary Engineers". He has a pleasant enough demeanor but is a completely selfish educator. Avoid if possible.

This is a senior design class and he emphasis on writing skill rather than the real engineering stuff and how the team have help the customer. All the grades is based on deliverable, where huge potion of points are deducted for small spelling mistake while he have typos in his email himself too. He is **** too, so choose a American teammate.
He is not very good at all.  teaches IE but has psych degrees.  not a horrible person, soo much.  well slightly.
Literally the WORST. Complete Ego maniac! Lectures are complete waste, gives no help when requested. Amazing he still continues to teach at purdue!  Complete Waste Do Not Take!

Had heard some negative things going into the class about the professor but as long as you keep an open mind and have a willingness to go to him for help/guidance, the semester shouldn't be too bad.  Can be vague at times but this is intentional because he wants you to go to him for help and not be handed a solution.  keep an open mind
his name said it all, "B.S." Caldwell...

He is very liberal in teaching style, likes to ramble and describe things that have no link to engineering from an engineering aspect. His notes are NOT helpful because they are huge paragraphs of nonsense. It's hard to find out what is the important information from lectures. Lalaine, the TA, however, does a good job of sorting through all his bul

Not to be mean but he is the worst I have ever had and I think most ppl in the class agree pretty much. We learn nothing for real and I don't think he has such credibility and eligibility to teach most our class. I took 330 and 332 twice be4 w/ him and I feel useless infront of others that learn from other prof. I learn nothing compared to them....

The course has been murdered by Prof. Caldwell. There is no sense of order in what he is teaching. The projects and notes have been presented in the vaguest possible manner. Grading is almost random and it seems that there is no structure to the course. He just teaches whatever is on his mind. I hope nobody has to take a course under him.

Caldwell's "alternative" teaching technique is in essence an excuse to do minimal work in terms of teaching and helping students while using students for his research purposes. I learned nothing from him in his 332 class and expect to learn no more in 486.

I wish he'd start teaching for a change.. talks 40 mins of useless information in the most vague manner possible. . lecture notes on a slide again are pointless.. does not look at the course from the students point of view.. i think he knows he isnt a good teacher and thus most of the grade is left for projects etc .. LEARN ON YOUR OWN POLICY

Without exaggerating, the worst prof I've Ever Had.  During an hour lecture, he would spend only 5 minutes teaching vague formulas that didn't apply, and the rest on "housekeeping" (maintaining his own WebCT account).  Offered little help, and often belittle student's who approach him with questions. You'll have to learn everything on your own!
Odd
He tries to elaborate his idea through discussions with his students, without the intention of helping students. Selfish teachear
No Comments
She would accuse people of somewhat obscure forms of plagiarism, and then turn out to be wrong about it.

Although this course has a very high workload, the professor was very good with reminders and giving strong feedback. I highly recommend NOT procrastinating with the writing assignments, as much as I love to procrastinate, because you will fall behind and struggle. She was by far one of my favorite professors!

I learned a lot through this class. You have to be willing to work and it is not an easy A. She cares a lot about her students and will know everyone by their name. She wants her students to do well and if you try enough, participate in discussions and follow her instructions you will get good grades. She is super supportive.
Attendance policy is absolutely ridiculous. Decent feedback. Stay on top of heavy workload or your GPA will tank.

Dr. Rosell was an amazing professor, in my opinion. You can tell she genuinely cares about her students. She welcomes participation and open discussion in class. While she does grade toughly on essays, she allows you to turn in a draft for her feedback before the due date, in which she tells you everything you should alter within your paper.

Great lectures, it's clear she wants her students to succeed. Starting each project can be confusing since the styles are generally new, but if you ask questions, Dr. Rosell will always help point you in the right direction to succeed. Not exactly an easy A, but if you do the work and implement the feedback thoughtfully, you will be fine.

She's definitely a tough grader, but she makes that clear from the very beginning. To succeed in this course, you need to be ready for a significant amount of reading and writing. This is not a class to take if you're looking for an easy GPA boost.

She truly engages with students, but her class isn't for those who just want to sit back. All assignments have a clear rubric but asking questions is key to a good grade. While her grading can be tough, she's approachable and helpful.
ENGL108 has frequent deadlines and isn't easy, but you'll learn a lot if you put in the effort (not a GPA booster).
Very tough grader

Dr. Rosell is a good prof. But she definitely is a tough grader. ENGL10800 by Dr. Rosell is NOT one of those English courses which you go to the classroom and get an A. But it's not the end of the world, put in hard work and you will be fine. A recommendation would be to actively ask for feedback on your work and REVISE YOUR FIRST DRAFTS.

People dump on Dr Rosell so much and going in I was so wary of how she would be as a professor Her papers are sometimes really hard to write and it can be exhausting but she is good about giving extensions and her big projects are easy to put together so long as you do all of the work in her class As long as you aren't lazy and do the work its easy

I loved Dr. Rosell. Class was a good amount of work but none of it is very hard at all. She gives amazing feedback on papers so getting As on all the big projects is very easy because she literally tells you what to do. She is funny, understanding, compassionate, and will work with you so you get good grades. Makes class as easy as possible.
DO NOT TAKE THIS PROF IF YOU WANT YOUR GPA TO BE GOOD. She's a very tough grader. Do not take her class.

Took this class during the Early Start program, crunched into five weeks. A lot of homework and sleepless nights, but Dr. Rosell's lectures are something I always looked forward to. She made the homework bearable and was kind enough to extend deadlines regularly. Our class was amazing and we were almost like our own friend group.

Dr. Rosell is a great professor.  She was able to maintain my focus throughout her lectures and always gave very useful feedback on the assignments.  I'm an engineering and pre-med student so I wasn't looking forward to taking an English class, but Dr. Rosell was able to make it not only doable but enjoyable as well.  Also, her cats are cute.

I absolutely would recommend Dr. Rosell. As a STEM major I was dreading taking my freshman comp class, but Dr. Rosell made it so enjoyable. You must be prepared to write as it is a composition class, but Dr. Rosell provides great feedback on your first draft, which is not graded, so that you can make the necessary revisions to get a good grade.

She is an amazing professor really wants you to grow but makes this class unnecessarily difficult. I don't regret taking her class as much as my GPA does but make a choice of learning or a good GPA. She is a tough grader, she will grant extensions if you ask in advance but participate in her classes, and you will do better at the course.

Avoid this professor! She's a nice person but her class structure is awful! Her absence policy is atrocious. Your grade is weighted by quizzes instead of the 8 page essays she requires you to do. The quizzes are the worst. She'll ask questions like the color of a character's clothes in a scene. She also assigned 20 full books in one semester.
Professor is responsible, but grade strictly, a lot of work, get ready to spend your whole semester on it
watch out the date of issuing grades and compare it with that of these comments. Sick
The grading scheme is not clear. Don't take it

One of the best English teachers at Purdue in my opinion. Prof. Rosell has a very clear grading criteria, extremely helpful feedback, and she really wants you to improve as a writer. Sometimes the class demands can get heavy, especially around the final drafts. Still, if you have the option, choose her class.

Rosell was the best professor I had my first semester here at Purdue. She makes class engaging and makes her lectures very enjoyable. She incredibly nice, down to earth, and very understanding. She also wants the best from each student and goes out of her way to give fantastic feedback on every essay.

DO NOT take this course. He is the toughest grader in Purdue. I did not take reviews seriously before taking this course but after taking it I realized that every bad thing here is true. Do yourself a favor and learn probability on your own.

He is the toughest grader I have seen in any course. He will deduct marks for the smallest of reasons. 2 midterms are each 1 hr long, which is absolutely not enough for the extremely difficult problems. DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASSES. You will only get good grades if you already know the material well and want to build on your prior knowledge.
So hard to understand and he graded extremely hard.
Anirban is a lovely soul with moxey and passion for probability. He is beacon to our society. May the spirits guide him into your lives as you will surely benefit from his overseeking wisdom.

Toughest course I've taken at Purdue. 1 HW due every week, 3 exams in total (not having been a Stats major, it was very demanding). Anirban is a great lecturer & tough grader, took time to explain basics in the initial classes. I def learned a lot. Make sure to attend all lectures and use TA office hours; He gave us cookies in each exam

He's a smart person but constantly goes off the road. The lectures roam around and you won't be able do do the homeworks or quizzes solely based on his lectures, that means, you need to already have A LOT of background knowledge of the materials covered. Tough grader.
Exams and homework are very difficult. He has extremely high expectations of the students. He is knowledgeable. But, he is not good at teaching and understanding students' needs.

Bob, as he goes by, is respected and well-known in statistics circles and he definitely knows his stuff. Sometimes that means he makes the material too challenging or theoretical though. Be sure you understand concepts and theorems for the exams, and he loves people who go to his office hours. His cartoons and jokes are in a class of their own.

If you like a prof who knows what he's talking about Dasgupta is your way to go. That being said, his exams have nothing to do with what he teaches in class and they're incredibly difficult. His handouts help you understand the material well but it still won't help you with the exams.

Amazing Statistician but terrible professor. I do not recommend taking this class. He makes you hate probability. He is the most difficult grader. Even office hours are not useful as he cannot understand your question. His teaching is all over the place and he gets distracted from the topics very easily. Take any other professor, just not him.

Bob is great. I just wish he had a better webpage, I find it difficult to learn when I don't know where to access information about the class. The material itself is pretty abstract, but his explanations are good. Some complain of an accent, however I've never had issues understanding him. Bonus: he is an artist.

Hes extremely old school and does not know how to use the internet. His addiction to diet coke and love for William Feller has made him a spoof. Extremely tough grader and its hard being a weekend warrior in his class as you wont have easy access to any solutions.
To be honest, it took me 2 weeks to understand his accent and writing also. Very strict on time. If you a 3 mins late, give up the lecture, cause he will argue:)
Worst class ever.
