Dr. Bob was an excellent teacher and definitely helped clear up the information I didn't learn in STAT 416 (from Anastasia... run if you get her!).  His jokes, although sometimes lame, were refreshing, and he was always very helpful in office hours, and he really wants his students to succeed.
He is by far the worst teacher I have ever had in my entire life. He thinks he is funny. Let me tell you Professor, you are not.

An extremely unhelpful, biased and a prejudiced person. He has some sort of inferiority complex, since he talks about his culture and his people all the time. His jokes are as lame as he is. He should seriously improve on his jokes, stop sniffing in the class and correct his accent. Instead of wasting time on his lame ass jokes he should teach.

I love this man.  He was the reason I got up for school every morning.  In fact, I too wish I could take his class again.  If only I had the forsight to have failed this class so I would have a reason to come back into the realm of Anirban Das Gupta.

I LOVE DASGUPTA!! He has GREAT odd mannerisms and I'D GIVE MY LEFT LEG TO BE IN HIS CLASS AGAIN! His chronic sniffing is beautiful and it kept me alert during the grueling stat power hour. He has the voice of an angel!!
Locked in his culture.  Doesn't seem to know how to help us.  Doesn't have time for students who struggle and definitely doesn't understand WHY stat can be difficult.  Has odd mannerisms.
You can tell the professor is passionate about the subject, and that made the class much more enjoyable. I learned a lot without feeling bored or overwhelmed.
She really cares about students and is understanding. She lectures well and is not too tough
I quite enjoyed this class. Content was very interesting, assignments challenging enough, group projects fun. Will take all her classes if I can.

Did not enjoy her class. All assignments felt pointless and not clear on instruction. We had 3 group essays that were 2 pages which was hard to slit up and write appropriate responses. Final was an essay that was pretty hard to understand and felt pointless. Was going to have a lockdown browser midterm, but she turned off camera at last minute.
Great class, highly recommend!
Lectures were really interesting and i always learned something useful. she clearly cares about students and I appreciated her being understanding when things came up.
Good class, easy, and well taught.
Her lectures are super engaging and she explains things well. Assignments are tough and require you to pay attention in class. But the quizzes and midterm are pretty easy.

She's knowledgeable about the content. However, lots of group papers. The page limits are not enough for what she is asking most of the time. I wish she would allow another page or have less requirements. Syllabus was kinda all over. Midterm was very easy. Take notes during lectures!

This class leans pretty heavily into psych, which is fine if you're interested in that. Monica is passionate and knowledgeable about the topic but her class is definitely lecture heavy so showing up is necessary if you want to do well.

Honestly such a nice change from the usual textbook classes. She doesn't teach straight from a textbook like other profs. Lectures are easy to follow. There are a lot of group assignments, but I enjoyed them for the most part. Grading is pretty fair. It's hard not to do well in this class

I really enjoyed this class! the material was interesting and felt really applicable to real world situations. This really made it way more engaging than other classes I've taken. I found her lectures well-organized and easy to follow, she explained concepts in a way that actually made sense.

Dr. Gamez-Djokic came late or barely on time to class nearly every lecture. The syllabus was not consistent with her course schedule and she would forget about our quizzes or case studies. There needed to have been more grades in the class, since much of the material was dependent on group work.

While I wish I had positive things to say about this professor, unfortunately, that is not the case. This class is more than just unorganized, it truly might be one of the most messy classes I have ever had to sit through and experience. While Monica herself I am sure is a lovely, and nice individual, that does not shine through in her teaching.

She's a nice person, but her lectures don't really go into depth and they're very repetitive. You get graded on like 5 things during the class and she grades them all very harshly and the feedback looks like it was generated by ChatGPT (genuinely). She's always 5 minutes late to class too, but so was I, so maybe that was a good thing.

Monica is obviously very knowledgeable about her areas of teaching, but her boring and repetitive lectures (which are the entire course) make it difficult to pay attention and be interested in what she's saying. She seems like a nice and intelligent person, but her classes are monotonous.

Her class wouldn't be hard if she wasn't just a tough grader. She just doesn't grade fairly and she isn't clear with what she is grading. If your group ghosts you and you do all of the work, she will still give them full credit!!!!!!!
typically comes to class 3-5 minutes late, always seems unprepared in lecture (tech issues and stumbling through slides), unclear communication on course expectations and technology needs.
Lectures off of slides, does not answer questions, unclear grading criteria, slow grader

Amazing lecturer, maybe best in cs. Passion shines through and tries to teach in a manner that is more simple, to help students grasp difficult concepts. It's unfortunate the material itself in this class is extremely difficult and exams are tough, but he grades them generously, and this class has one of THE MOST INSANE CURVES I HAVE EVER SEEN!
tough exams(we do not expect you to solve all of the problems) but great lecturer. this guy is most of why i think cryptography is interesting
Proof heavy, but doable. Going to the office hour with Albert was a HUGE help. Going to lectures is recommended.
Albert Yu is the best TA ever! crypto is hard as balls but albert makes it better

Really enjoyed Maji's class; he is one of the best lecturers I have ever encountered and really cares about students. Do wish some things were mananged better in the course (e.g., assignments grading could have been more timely) but otherwise this class was the highlight of my semester. Looking forward to taking more classes with Maji if possible.

Professor Maji teaches you how to think like a cryptographer. He breaks down complicated crypto concepts into small math puzzles that everyone can understand, instead of hammering you with a bunch of abstract math and algorithms. His lectures feel more like a treasure hunt, I've never been so excited to be back for the next lecture. 100% recommend.

I took CS 355 with Professor Maji and he is an amazing lecturer! His class is very interactive and he takes the time to make sure everyone understands the material before moving on. CS 355 with him will be very difficult for you if you don't have strong math skills, as the assignments are very proof heavy. However, the curve is very generous.

Maji is very passionate about what he teaches, and I learnt so much from the lectures. BUT this class has homework way more difficult than what is taught, you are going to spend hours puzzling a problem and still not get it. Yet I have learnt so much from this class and would take a class by Prof Maji again.

Professor Maji is extremely passionate about cryptography and tries to teach students how to think. This means that he does not show students how to solve specific problems, but instead teaches students how to approach problems. His class is very proof heavy. Although the class was difficult, I don't regret taking his course.

Maji focuses entirely on the math, and his homeworks have a lot of proofs, but they often are really helpful in guiding you to the right answer. He's great at explaining the "why" and "how" of cryptography but doesn't explain history much. It was a hard class with the biggest curve I have ever seen.

CS355 is one of the hardest CS courses at Purdue. The lectures were very good, but then the homework was a lot more difficult than what was presented in class. It is very easy to pass this class as the cutoffs for a C are super low, but it is not curved as much from the top.
So glad won't have to see him again ever. Has been teaching the class for the last 4 semesters. Purdue better have someone else teach CS 355.
Please don't take 355 with him. Makes the class unnecessarily mathy and hard for no reason. He teaches well and you can pass the class too but he can be very inconsiderate and stubborn at times.
His class is clearly not 'Introduction' to Cryptography. Unnecessarily incorporated too much math and algorithms in Crypto. Maybe he should look at how others teach this class.
Best instructor in Purdue. Very nice and caring.

Maji is a good Professor in general, but what I did not like about him is that in the lecture he will describe to you what 1+1 is equal to, but in the homework, he will ask you to find the integration without describing how. His exams are very tough also, the average is always around 50.

Maji provided one of the best academic experiences I've ever had with Intro to Cryptography. His course was very tough, but I learned a lot and my problem solving skills have significantly improved after taking this course. Every Maji lecture was always very exciting and I loved coming to class. Definitely recommend taking a class with him.

Great enthusiastic professor that loves what he teaches and easy to talk to. Incredibly tough on grading. Partial credit doesn't really mean partial credit. Talk to him and not only you will you be more enlightended but working on the material given will be less like swimming in a tsunami but will be more like having a boat in it.

Her exam questions were very poorly worded and unreasonable. Even the TAs admitted that they were confusing and difficult. She did not do a good job of preparing students for the tests and the practice questions she gave out were never similar to what showed up on exams. Overall, a bad instructor and very unhelpful

Lecture content does not translate to exams. Professor is not good at explaining how to solve problems. Unreasonable grading on exams. Horribly written exam problems. She gives out "practice problems" that are nothing like the problems that show up on the tests. I wish I had Thomson for this class.
Extremely hard exams compared to homework/lecture

The most frustrating class I've ever taken. The level of difficulty of lecture examples vs. exam questions is not comparable. She gives everyone a C. Would not recommend AT ALL if you can avoid having her for this class.

Prof Liu was the better of the two professors this semester. She worked out practice problems and explained the thought process behind her steps. Her lectures were clear. She seemed to genuinely care that students understood the content, reintroducing it in a new way if a student misunderstood. She gets a bad rap because the course is difficult.

Very test heavy class, so make sure you can do the practice exams. Writes hard tests but usually clear test problems. Review the material and know how to approach problems in ways specified in class. Good office hours and clear lectures. Overall a fair professor.

Dr. Liu was a VERY difficult test writer. I took CHE205 with two professors, and when exams were handed out, you could ALWAYS tell which problem was written by Dr. Liu because it was unreasonably difficult. She does not explain topics very well in class and in-class questions seem like a chore for her.

I took with Professor Liu & Morgan. Professor Liu had extremely clear lectures, and always walked through examples that were extremely relevant to what we would be graded on. She is always accessible outside of class via email, and is also extremely helpful during office hours. She shows genuine care for her students and wants them to succeed.

For some reason, Professor Liu takes pride in having low exam averages. The overall concept of the class is not difficult (CHE205), since I was able to get an A after I retook it with another professor. But for some reason, Liu and Morgan love to confuse students. Liu is also very condescending when you ask her questions in office hours. 0/10.

Awful exams that are impossible to understand
Attempt to fail everyone

Prof. Liu gives extremely hard tests, often with averages between 50-60%, much lower than most other CHE 205 professors. These tests tend to be graded quite harshly as well. She is quite a mediocre lecturer, so conceptual questions should be directed toward TAs instead.

not the worst lecturer i have ever had but not the best. in office hours she was pretty condescending when answering my questions. the material in this class did not come easily to me, but when she would answer my questions she answered them in a know-it-all way which made me feel dumb so i would leave office hours still confused and frustrated

"Teacher" does not lecture or do anything specific to help kids out. Holds office hours to do homework problems and thats it. Have to teach the entire class to yourself through videos and textbook. Worst teacher I've ever had.

Professor Liu is pretty good. During the online semester there werent as many lectures as usual which was less than ideal but the videos of hers we did have were very detailed. Theres a lot of office hours and theyre recorded so others can watch which is also helpful. Shes very willing to work with students and is a super nice professor.

Professor Liu is very respectful and good at answering questions. She cares about student feedback and is friendly. That said, this was her first time teaching this class and it was online during COVID. There were no lectures, only office hours and brief recordings of the previous professor teaching.

I thought the test questions were not the best reflection on what was covered in class, and I wish they were altered beforehand instead of having to give back points for questions that she "discovered" were tricky.

Knox is a great professor! The class is relatively easy if you go to class and study well for the exams. Participation matters, my class was very quiet, and it made the class incredibly boring (no fault of the professor) so if you want to get the full experience... TALK. There are usually participation activities randomly for attendance.
She concisely explained topics during lecture. Homeworks and exams were pretty easy and didn't require much effort.  She gave out candies during our last exam too.

100000% recommend taking this course! This professor is very compassionate and kind. She is clear on her expectations but is very graceful about deadlines. This course was not only for credits but honestly helped me with my life.

I'm looking forward to taking 201 with her. Her class is so fun and she always implements mindfulness in her class which is so nice to look forward to 2x a week. I hope anyone reading this considers taking HDFS 201 with her. It is a super easy class but I've found some of the units super useful in every day life.

This review is for HDFS 390, a mindfulness and compassion class and it is THE class to take. You learn practices and research regarding mindfulness and compassion. Very engaging and she encourages you to really just become a better human and sets a great example. You get out what you put into this course and it can be life changing.

Dr. Knox is an immensely kind professor that delivers concise, informative lectures. All of the graded material aside from attendance originates from the textbook, and exams are both fair and accessible. Don't love the required attendance but understand the necessity. She's evidently invested in both her students' wellness and success!

This is the kindest professor i know! She cares deeply about her students & makes lectures fun and engaging with music and games. This is a discussion heavy class, if you dont want it to move slowly make sure to take part in the group discussion because it overall makes the class better! Love Dr. Knox I am taking more of her classes in the spring!
Very caring and nice Professor. Expects you to do your work but understands life happens and will work with you if things get in the way of school. One of the best Professors I have ever had.

Professor Knox is so kind and caring and creates such a safe space for her students! There's in-class participation so you can't miss many lectures but the homework, papers, and exams (open note) are very straightforward. She's super flexible with due dates too. We watched music videos and did ice breakers for fun, loved this class and prof!

SHE IS SO SWEET! She always lets you know when things are due, and even then, there is typically always a "2 week grace period," which is basically an extension on all the due dates. She tells you in advance before talking about triggering concepts in class. Only took 2 exams (online + no camera) and wrote two essays (3 paragraphs). LOVE HER!!!

I would quite literally die for Professor Knox One of the most inclusive and kind professors cares about all of her students and is so lenient when it comes to due dates and assignments She creates such a safe environment for topics discussed in class. homework is easy, two exams and two papers a semester graded very fairly. I love this woman.

Dr. Knox is genuinely the sweetest, most genuine professor I'v had this far. Class is super easy! Weekly knowledge checks on Mcgraw Hill, midterm and final exam (all open book, open note). She gives a 1-2 week grace period for basically all of her assignments, which is really helpful. Attendance taken on Hotseat - lowest 3 get dropped.

The class gave me a solid understanding of how modern systems work from the ground up. Professor Zhang made everything click with engaging lectures and real-world examples that made the tough stuff easier to grasp. I really appreciated the professor's enthusiasm and support throughout the course.

Professor Zhang made the course both engaging and informative. The lectures were clear and well-organized, breaking down complex topics with real-world examples. The labs were hands-on and helped solidify the concepts, and he was always approachable for guidance. Highly recommend!

Horrible at communicating during lectures and often gets lost when explaining the textbook's source code. The labs have no test cases and is up to the student to "test", but if you do not have a perfect understanding of what is supposed to happen you will fail. What you're supposed to implement in the labs are vague and at most two sentences.

Horrible communication and lecturer. He reused another professor's slides and just read off them in lecture. He went MIA until the day before the midterm. After the midterm, only scores were posted and the only way to review your answers was to book an appointment with him. However, his labs are relatively easy and the deadlines are more than fair.

This has been one of the worst classes I've taken at Purdue. The lectures were boring and uninformative, exam preparation was non-existent, and the professor almost never answered student's questions. I was excited about this course, but Professor Zhang made it dull. Most of what I've learned came from studying the book on my own.

I never took any systems course but prof. Yongle had made fall in love to distributed systems. He touches all the contemporary topics on this subject (not that much deep but it is alright, makes it easier for outsiders). The coding assignment is huuuuuge, the test is somewhat tough. But the worst experience came from the TAs. they never responded.

A disaster of a class with Prof. Zhang. Negative communication for days leading up to the midterm. Apparently, the lecture content on the day of the midterm IS COVERED FOR THE EXAM. He doesn't record the lectures, and reads the slides word-for-word. Be prepared to read the textbook on your own to study for exams and complete labs.
hard to understand his accent. Doesn't clarify doubts.
Teaching is  bad, I would suggest students to watch online lectures from university of Washington, instead of wasting time in his lectures.

The lab assignments are relatively challenging but you will be fine if you have some system programming experience. It is extremely important to start working on the lab projects as early as possible as it would take lots of your time if you want to get a full grade.

The class is really good. Professor Zhang really knows how to teach and pushes you forward. This is one of the classes where I really learned something practical. The class is designed to get your hand dirty building DSs. Thanks a lot Professor Zhang!!
A great professor but the class is not worth taking. The project is ridiculously difficult and time consuming and a lot of time is spent in minor coding stuff rather than learning the concept.

I found the content of this class difficult and his exams difficult but Professor Silva is a very kind grader and he curves very generously. He is also very kind and always willing to help. Due to his generous curves and grading I was able to survive his content heavy class.

Semester long group work (mult. assignments) that make up large part of grade, but you can choose teams. I honestly found the class challenging and hard to follow. The professor is very nice and patient, just not really a topic I understood or liked. Lectures were very content heavy which made material hard to absorb. Coding is involved (Rstudio).

Professor Silva grades exams super quickly, his grading curve is very nice as i received an A+ with a 96% in the class (check boilerclasses as well), his lectures get into theory but the exams are practical, so he gives u a sample midterm and final that are very similar to the actual thing. Problem sets and final project are easy and manageable
I found the class boring really really boring

Exams aren't hard. I really didn't prioritize this class when I should have, and I swung a B. Exams aren't horrible, the content isn't hard. Projects are hard, but he grades them kindly. As a professor, he is great. The content is boring but he's so passionate about it that it's entertaining.

Silva is a very kind professor and does a good job of trying to keep students engaged. The class is pretty difficult for undergrads. There are five group projects, plus one final group project. He is always willing to help students, but get ready to work a lot if you want an A.

Pretty great professor. Not sure why the 1/5 ratings. If you do not attend class you will struggle but if you go then you are guaranteed at least a B. He also was willing to answer questions after every class.

Go to class! Lecture is super helpful to understanding the material.  Plus, if you go to class he always starts by asking if there are questions which can help a lot with projects and assignments.

Only big downside is that a lot of the assignments are done in groups, so if you have a bad group, you might be doing a lot of work by yourself.

Dejanir is one of those teachers who can't simplify things. The notes seem like they're made for grad students, not undergrads taking their first investments class. He fails to explain concepts nor teaches how to apply them. The class is extremely disorganized. Literally the worst professor I've at Purdue.
Makes the course harder than it is
Irresponsible :,(

Lectures are unengaging and difficult to sit through. She is impossible to reach outside of class, with no clear office hours and minimal email responses. I tried to contact her over a dozen times about a TA position for CNIT 271 and only received a response twice, one to tell me to reach back out later, another to tell me there's no more space.

Thanks to her I spent my lovely fall break writing lab reports!
She makes you write a paper about her research paper for her thesis!
Suchly a lovely lady :))
She yaps for hours and hours but literally no one understands as well as cares about whatever she's talking about. I would never like to take class with her ever in my life.

Attendance is mandatory (have to sign your name on a sheet) and the lectures aren't interesting at all. Overall she's a decent professor, just uninteresting. Exams & quizzes are worth a massive chunk of your grade which are all taken in person during class. TAs were generous graders which made the class much easier.
She is very nice and will respond to emails/requests for extensions, lectures are a bit boring but the content is not hard. She does take attendance for lectures

Very reasonable grader and accessible outside class. Had a problem with a quiz and emailed her about it, was immediately fixed. Lectures can be a bit dull but show up and take notes and you'll be fine. Takes attendance every lecture. Labs have some challenging parts but TAs are very helpful.

This is her first semester teaching the course so she was still figuring a few things out. She took a lot of feedback and applied it almost instantly. Labs were easy and mostly relevant to the material learned in lecture. Quizzes can be difficult sometimes, but they are not surprises and she will tell you a week in advance if there is a quiz.

I am not the best test-taker which is why I didn't receive the best score, but Zach is so chill and always willing to help and answer questions! I'm surprised I got a good grade! (To some a B is probably bad but I am AWFUL at math) If you are taking statistics I 100% recommend Zach.

He gives a balanced workload, has great and engaging lectures, and is accessible during his office hours, where he will help significantly. He gives pop quizzes but will warn you beforehand, and he does them all on the same day of the week, along with partial credit on exams and quizzes. Individual exams aren't curved but curves your overall grade.

MATH-1511G course (calc) 
Honestly a great professor! He structured the course in a way that that material was easy to comprehend. He works out every step on the board, and answers students questions really well and without judgement!
Homework is online with multiple attempts. Occasional quizzes and its 50/50 if you get warned
Great professor. I am not a math person but he made the subject enjoyable. Accessible outside the classroom and understanding. Definitely, take the class with him. You won't regret it.
This instructor makes difficult concepts make sense, and this is coming from someone who doesn't like math! Definitely take advantage of office hours; it helps immensely. Great instructor!

Dr. Letterhos is such an easy professor. We had ten (at home) quizzes that you could expect to do well on as long as you pay the bare minimum amount of attention in class. Homework assignments were graded for accuracy, but we only got ten homework assignments throughout the entire semester, so you could take your time on them. Exams were easy.

Great professor who really cares about the students' learning. The lectures are well planned and easy to follow. You still have to work fairly hard to get a good grade, but he is always available for help and responds to emails quickly.

Overall his course is fantastic and he proves to be a great teacher. He's very flexible with his students and gives generous feedback. Overall one of the most pleasurable Purdue Math experiences I've had (not that there's many but he's really great).

Dr. Ringenberg is very chill, she'll work with you if you give her advance notice of stuff. Provides plenty of extra credit opportunities to make up for absences, so it's not a big deal if you don't go to class once or twice.
Lectures can get long, but the material is pretty interesting. She will remember you if you answer questions during class.

Had her for CNIT422 (not 371) overall she was a pretty solid professor, nothing significant to remark. She taught ~40% of the lectures while her graduate TA taught the other 60%. You are rewarded *heavily* for in-class participation. Didn't take notes in lecture and just studied the slides before exams and ended with an 89; an A is very achievable.
I made sure to mix 5 hour energy into monster to stay awake in her lectures because of how boring they were.

Dr. Ringenberg is very laid-back and will work with you as long as you are respectful and show up to class. Attendance is mandatory for 371 and there are in-class activities, but there are 4 extra credit assignments and each one has the same weight as one class. Overall very reasonable, and lectures are pretty engaging.

The professor is nice. However, PRC is a 1 credit hour class, and she assigns way too much homework. Weekly, there is a paper (~1000 words), plus a discussion post requiring cited sources. 

The class content is insultingly easy, which is why the amount of work is ridiculous. This class is for working professionals, we don't have time for this.

by far the rudest professor i've ever had. she will insult you personally and ruin your grade just because she can. she never gave any specific feedback and would give entirely unreasonable tasks and then insinuate that you were not trying if you did anything less than 150% all while she would forget everything she was supposed to do. horrible

Professor Okuniewski does a great job of making semi-complex material easy to understand. You will constantly take notes during the class, but she will always stop to answer any questions you may have. If she does not know the answer to your question, she will always make sure to come to the next class with the answer.

Basically the lectures are just you watching her copy her notes onto another another piece of paper. Hopefully you already know the material covered in the class because she will not teach it to you. The material itself isn't super difficult, but with her grading style it becomes way harder than necessary.
Relatively easy class for graduate students. Did a lot of note-taking during classes. Homeworks aren't too time-consuming.
I loved her options for attending lectures which were very flexible, and her lectures were perfectly organized!
So easy! She had online recordings of all lectures so you never HAD to go in person! All tests were open note as well.
lectures were great and she didnt make us buy the textbook!

The professor is an AMAZING lecturer and provides tons of opportunities for extra credits. Though I wouldn't say I'm into art history, I love going to her lectures and just listen. Attendance isn't mandatory and you can probably watch the lectures at your convenience. The textbook is there but isn't necessary to help you succeed in the class.

The math heavy notes are scary at first, but when you get into the concepts the exams are easier to understand. The last half of the course was very interesting and well worth taking the class. Grading is very generous and if you put in the effort of showing up to class and doing the homework, an A is very achievable.

This class does a lot of proving of equations but you don't use many of them on the exams. The exams are more so theory based. She's an excellent person and cares about your learning. If you show up to lecture, understand the use of equations, and the actual economic theory, you'll be fine
Professor Espino is very sweet! she comes to class with lots of energy and cares about everyones learning. This is a tough class, but she is always open to questions and gives several exams..
She's a good prof. The mid-term was pretty hard, but the final was better. Some pretty interesting stuff if you're interested in studying how the banking system affects the overall macroeconomy.
It was a difficult class for sure, I wish there was more practice on the problems. most of the class was math heavy.

Great at explaining stuff, but lectures were a bit boring as he seemed not very interested in the course material. He was very helpful in office hours. I think he's a bit overrated but still pretty good professor. His resources on Chenflix for various math-y things are amazing
Prof. Chen is a solid lecturer with a solid lecture format. The only reason he's not a 5 is because I didn't really get that excited about the class like I have some other math professors.
Simply the best to ever do it, inside and outside the classroom
Simply the best at teaching calculus I have ever found. Very engaging teacher who cares deeply about his students and uploads all his lectures.

Prof Chen IS the Purdue Math department, despite having different professors along the way this man has carried me from MA 162 to MA 303 with his recorded lectures. His explanations and lectures are incredibly thorough and he explains in a way that resonates with many students. His exams can sometimes be tough, but are fair. Thank you Prof Chen!

Chenflix saved my life during math courses at purdue (162, 261, 266), and I've heard his in person lectures are even better. Very good at breaking topics down, explaining how to do them, and why we do them. Very efficient teaching style with not a lot of time being wasted too. Very easy to understand, one of the best professors at purdue.

Since I had the ASYNC lecture format, I decided to just ChenFlix instead and it's been amazing. Lots of flexibility to watch the lectures whenever you want, and Chen's explanations are great at providing a conceptual understanding of Calc 3. Ended up acing both midterms and doing alright on the final.

Professor Chen is a great lecturer, not only for his ability to explain mathematical concepts in a clear way, but also his commitment to making his lectures accessible to other students. He does a great job at giving his students a strong intuition about how we model/solve differential equations. This intuition is easily applied to other courses.

unlike many Chenflix users here, I had the fortune of taking Dr. Chen's class in-person. he does explain and simplify complex concepts really really well, focusing only on the topics we would actually want to know for an exam. and although the exams were a bit harder than i expected, the grading was pretty lenient.

Awesome Professor! No complaints.

Best professor I have ever had, I can't say enough nice things about her. Dr. Steinhour treats every student like they have the potential to excel in his course. Very kind professor. The class is very interesting and he always makes sure students have what they need to succeed in the class. I would really recommend this course!

Super nice, super awesome. Great teacher and great person. PLEASE attend lectures as he does not post them online. Exams aren't too bad but do take notes and pay attention. He is very passionate and helpful.

He is the best! If you have him you'd be lucky! He was always so understanding and would be easily accessible when I needed help. He replies to emails very quick. Take advantage of resources on campus including professor and TA office hours, the Chem Resource Room in Wetherill, SI session, and free tutoring on campus. Good Luck!

Dr. Hardwood is amazing. She is so accessible outside of class and she wants you to succeed. She knows the topics that are hard and makes an effort to help students with those. I found lecture to be helpful even though it wasn't mandatory. If you make a little effort you will be rewarded in her class.

Dr. Harwood is a great professor in my opinion. Coming into this class, I have a background of chemistry classes, so I found this class easy. My peers who did not have chemistry experience still found it easy to understand what she is teaching. Dr. Harwood also posted accurate expectations for exams. However, there is a cumulative final :(

Dr. Harwood is deeply caring in a way that is not seen often with professors who have more than 300 students in each lecture. She is funny, kind, but most importantly fair. She reflects on how her teaching has set a student up for success or failure, and will give you opportunities to gain points if she feels that the grading was unfair.

so this professor is fine. but a lot of the times she trips over her own words and recently her lectures have been useless and did not provde any examples. if there were examples they would be really simple and did not correlate to the hw much. 111 was easy if you took chem in high school, it is pretty much a review. but this class ight 6/10 ya dig

I am so bad at science so I was nervous for this class but Dr. Harwood really helped me understand the material. There is a lot of busy work and homework for this class, which kind of sucks, but it really does help you on the exams. The class is very well organized.

Lecture is not mandatory but you get extra credit if you go, there are exams in the class but if you study for them you will do okay. She is really helpful and does her best to make sure everyone does well in the class.

Dr. Harwood really cares about her students' success. She constantly has TAs walking around the lecture hall making sure that the students understand coursework and homework questions. She is a great professor, and I would recommend this class.
Great professor who genuinely wants to help her students and teaches good lectures
Professor Harwood explains things very well and helped me to understand every topic we covered.

Dr. Harwood is so incredible. She is down to earth and genuinely wants to help her students. She always made herself available to students. She had student hours instead of office hours because she would work almost one-on-one with students on homework or other projects. She is also super funny. Exams only make up 40% of your grade.
She explains the material very well in class and answers all questions in an easy to understand way. Very enthusiastic about teaching. Material was easy if you go to lectures and may attention.
Very nice Professor

The material moves pretty fast at times which can make it hard to learn the language if you have a lot of stuff in other classes. Prof. Conner is passionate about Latin, though, which helps the class feel engaged. There's a ton of homework (4 assignments per week) which sucks, however it's graded on participation. Good class but can be a slog.

Prof. Conner is amazing. I very rarely rate professors, but I felt compelled to rate him. He LOVES Latin, which makes learning much more fun. Additionally, he's a very nice guy. Super understanding and easy to talk to. I recommend going to his extracurricular meetings (various classics related gatherings). They're a lot of fun.

Dr. Conner is by far my favorite professor thus far. He is extremely knowledgeable and intelligent and makes learning Latin an incredible experience. He is funny yet determined when it comes to his lectures. I cannot recommend his class enough.

Took him for Latin 101 and 102, he was an amazing teacher who cares about the success of all his students. There is a crazy amount of homework, but in return the grading is quite easy. I would recommend his classes to anyone.
Great professor, extremely knowledgeable and caring.

I would 100% take his class!! During class he goes over the important parts of the homework so you don't actually need to read! He explained things in a good way for students and is very passionate about what he does. Overall such a sweet guy!

Prof. Conner is the only English or communications teacher whose class I have enjoyed taking. The assigned readings are easy, and he sets you up greatly for your papers. His feedback is incredibly helpful and makes the lectures fun. Frequent extra credit opportunity.

Great teacher, very passionate. The assigned reading is not too overwhelming. Attendance and participation is mandatory, but he allows for 3 absences per semester and there are weekly extra credit opportunities. Gives great feedback on papers and is not a hard grader. I'm happy that I was able to get another class with him in my schedule.

Prof Conner marks hard, but gives constructive feedback. He has required reading for each class, which can vary, and during midterms can feel very overwhelming. He has mandatory attendance (3 excused absences) but being there is not enough. You must ask questions or make comments based off the text to get points that affect your final grade.

Professor Conner is extremely down to Earth and caring. He is punctual and very lenient when it comes to grading and is willing to work with you if you are falling behind. Overall if you want to take Latin, his course is extremely well-paced and accommodating.

I took this class for SCLA 101 and it was a good class. Attendance is mandatory, however, there are 3 attendance drops which is extremely nice. The lectures can be a bit boring at times, but there were some interesting topics

I had Dr. Conner a few years ago but I am coming back to comment on how much of a difference he made in my college career. I am now a language teacher and he showed me what a good language teacher is supposed to be. He is understanding, accommodating, and his class is engaging. It is clear that he loves Latin and teaches it with passion.
Really good professor who cares for his students. He makes the class fun.
good professor

A really easy grader that makes sure that everyone is engaged within class and has the opportunity to ask questions. Makes discussions very engaging and gets to know the students. My only complaint would be the excessive amount of reading, but the assignments are reasonable.

Normally, I am wary of English classes, but I really enjoyed this course. You are graded on 3 papers, 1 oral, weekly participation, and blog posts. The blog posts are easy and can usually be answered by the previous class. He is a very easy grader, and as long as you complete everything, it is an easy A.

I took this class because one of my friends took it last semester and said he is a great teacher. However, I did not think so. I emailed him several times to ask him questions. He did not reply to me what so ever. I went to his office hours, he is there the whole time. I waited for about 20 minutes and left. Participation is important in this class

Super nice teacher. I came into the class thinking i'd hate it and that it was a dumb gen ed but it was actually fun and helped me get more perspective on life. He's also super chill and funny and is very thorough with instructions. Makes sure no one is left behind or confused!

Daniel Conner is INCREDIBLE! I've taken several courses with him now, and he's been nothing short of a delight. He's funny, passionate about what he teaches, his lectures are easy to follow, he genuinely cares about his students, accommodates those who ask, is easily approachable, and loves questions. I've learned so much from him! Highly recommend

Professor Conner is my favorite professor at Purdue! He knows so much about Classics and is such a great lecturer. He really cares about how well we do in his classes, and actually makes me look forward to seeing the comments he made on my essays. I really hope I get to take another class with him.
Professor Conner is a great professor. He is really passionate about the material and gives a lot of great feedback on essays. He is very personable and friendly.
Enjoyed lectures

Daniel is hilarious and truly makes his class enjoyable. He clearly loves what he teaches and that enthusiasm is contagious. His lectures are easy to follow and he often makes modern references or encourages us to see how what we're learning in class can be tied to the real world. 10/10 would take another class he taught.

Conner has been my favorite prof so far. He's really nice and funny, and turns boring PPTs into really great lectures. I like how much he cares about what he's teaching, he's so passionate about it that even if someone hated classics/greek mythology, they would still find his lectures interesting.I really hope I get to take another class he teaches
I love his lecutures on Greek mythology! He is so funny and makes pop culture references and helps us understand the material well!

While she sounds pretty monotone when she speaks, her lectures are still interesting and well organized (especially if you are an anth major). She's a lenient grader for this class and prioritizes actually coming to the lectures since it's an introductory course. You need to go to lab to pass, but the assignments aren't difficult.

For a 3 credit class this was the easiest thing ever. Her lectures are very monotone and not the most engaging, but it was easy to zone out or do her homework during it and then get the attendance credit. There are no tests or quizzes, only online homework due every week. The labs were very interesting, and the whole class honestly felt fake.

This is probably the easiest class you can ever take in college. It is genuinely impossible to get below a 98 unless you just skip every class. No tests, exams, assignments are graded by participation, and on the homework you can answer as many questions as you want until you have a 100.
Very easy class with good teacher, would take again.

overall not a bad class. lecture attendance is mandatory but the lectures tell you everything you need to know. most of the class is just inquisitives and the lab recitation. only thing that irks me is the way she says potentially.

She is so amazing!!! ANTH 204 was recommended to me because it is a science that is easy to follow, and it is great if you just need the credit. Dr. Veile makes it easy to get her students to achieve.
Easiest class and professor you'll ever take. Great GPA boast. Used her lectures as a study hall. There's 1 group project but it's pretty easy.

Overall she was a very good professor. The material was interesting, but lectures were lengthy and sometimes she was very boring to listen to. The textbook is mandatory because the weekly assessments have questions related to the reading.

Lectures can feel a bit lengthy, but the material is interesting. Often loses her train of thought but generally speaks knowledgeably. Sometimes falls behind on grading but is not a harsh grader. Sometimes jumps between topics or does not emphasize a key point enough, but generally explains material well.

Awesome professor! Everything is very clearly laid out in the syllabus, and it's easy to contact her. Many chances for extra credit. Class was an easy science requirement for my major! Plus she really cares about what she's teaching and makes lectures interesting. Would recommend to everyone.

Assignments that I did in September did not get graded until the end of November, so it is hard to gauge your grade. Her lectures are vague and hard to follow, and her grading scale is brutally steep. It is nearly impossible to succeed in this class, avoid this teacher and class at all costs.
Dr. Veile seemed like a great teacher, but it was hard to connect with her over the online course. She was super easy to get in contact with, and her lectures were pretty manageable.
I loved this class! I really enjoyed the material covered and she did a great job explaining everything.
Took her for ANTH 204 and she was amazing. Didnt beat around the push and was clear and concise during her lectures. Very interesting and intelligent lecturer. Adapted very well to COVID.

Dr. Veile was the most amazing professor I have had to date. She is extremely knowledgeable about the topics she discusses. Her lecture can get a bit boring especially if it is over something most people do not enjoy however she always tries to keep the conversation going.

I just finished a class with Dr. Veile and I want to take more. We had some of the best discussions I've had since starting Purdue. She knows a lot about anthropology and biology which is a cool combination. She is relaxed in the classroom and supports all her students. Tests are hard but she grades easy, lots of chances to get extra credit too.

I liked this class much more than I thought I would! The professor is really busy and kind of distracted but so smart and helpful and understanding. You talk about controversial things like sex vs gender and racism and human aggression but its from a scientific and culturally sensitive perspective. we had great discussions too.

If you need to a UC science credit, I highly recommend you to take her ANTH 204. It's a free A. She gives out study guides before exams, and those study guides are basically the exam. Also, it doesn't even matter if you fail those exams because she provides tons of extra credit opportunities. I can feel her passion in her lecture. Amazing professor

I recommend this professor and course. She's really organized and up-to-date on the newest science findings. She talks about science history and tells funny stories about the scientists behind the discoveries, which makes class enjoyable. Each chapter has an online quiz that help you prepare for tests, tests are fair and straightforward.

I absolutely loved her! My favorite professor I've had at Purdue. She has had real experience in South America and she loves what she teaches. I would recommend her to anyone that has a general interest in Anthropology.

This is a great intro course. I am a comm major and was dreading the science requirement but professor Veile made it fun. You learn so much about genetics and primates and fossils but its fun and seems relevant to real life. She also has a lot of cool real life experiences as an anthropologist in the field.

Dr. Veile is extremely knowledgeable and her lectures are entertaining. Walks you through challenging material, like all the different fossil species, and always willing to help students out. Great intro course.

Exams and quizzes are fair based on course material. Rewards students with many extra credit opportunities. Lab is kind of a joke but easy points too. Lots of reading if you want to do well on exams but its not difficult to understand.

Fair teacher, gave good feedback, and gave points back on exams when she worded questions poorly. Just go to class, pay attention, and you'll be fine. Book is irrelevant. Lectures are boring, but she's extremely knowledgeable and one of the most fair professors I've ever had.
Corless makes this class super easy. Light homeworks and exams. Super chill guy too, Irish accent is on point

I have never seen such a level of apathy from professors or TAs
- Posting of incorrect/incomplete homework solutions
- Homework solutions were incorrectly labeled or sometimes forgotten
- Piazza posts go unanswered or get unbelievably unhelpful responses
- Mostly easy homework with a few impossible problems
- Online notes can be useless at times
Generally just doesn't care

Took 564 with Professor Corless, which wasn't too bad overall. Notes packet can help get you to complete most of the HW given, otherwise, go to lecture if you want to learn in more detail. He can be a bit slow and quiet during lectures, but an overall good and caring professor.
Prof. Corless is AAE's gem. Super friendly and chill. His classes are easy but you will still learn the material well. He is also very direct and helpful when answering questions during office hours.

Corless is an absolute blast. His courses are rather easy. At times, he can get a bit boring, but he's got a really good sense of humor. He can also be a bit awkward at times, but he's really nice and helpful.

If you want to learn from lectures, you'll have to sit near the front and pay attention. He can seem a bit dull at first, but once you get to know his personality he's actually pretty funny. That said, he doesn't always cover everything that's on the homeworks in a lot of detail. Also, AAE 564 is basically the same class as 421, so don't take both.
Seems like he cares about the course even less than students. HWs are lengthy but tests are exactly like the practice exams provided.
I took AAE 564 with him. His lectures are a bit boring, but course was interesting.
It's all stuff you know until the end. I stopped going to class. All homeworks are the same as prev. semesters, tests are 30 minutes or less. Very good professor, but also very easy.

I still remember his jokes like- "You must be knowing PID controllers since your Kindergartens." And I still wonder why only 2-3 people understood and laughed at his humor. He is very student friendly and helps one a lot. His textbook is a bit unclear but then his classes are a better answer to that.
Classes are a little dull but has his own text that can be helpful.  Class covers many of the basics and is mostly a review of other aero classes.  Exams are very much like past exams.
He's soft spoken and a little dull at time, but if you listen up and pay attention, you'll learn the material and get some good laughs from the subtle humor he throws in at times.

He has a really unique theory that all you need to understand the material is a few basic concepts. It works, and I felt like I learned a lot off of just a few simple equations. His lectures were pretty dull at times, but overall not a half bad prof. Exams are very easy if you study HW problems.

I took AAE 421, 564, and 666 with this prof. His theory is you only need to remember the basics to truly understand material, so his class was somewhat easy, but you will still walk away with a good understanding of the material.
Pretty good lecture notes that he gives to you.  You don't "need" the book but he always suggest helpful ones.
The best professor.
Good use of examples to illustrate points, however you do have to take good notes and not simply rely on the provided course book (PDF)

Generally one of the better professors in the department.  He is a soft speaker, but his dry wit is fairly entertaining.  Most importantly, he does care about how well you understand material if you show that YOU do too.

Dull lectures with occasional subtle humor thrown in. The material is not hard but I always found myself having trouble finishing the homeworks just because they had little glitches I couldn't fix. Endlessly frustrating.
Good prof, have to pay attention to get subtle humor
No Comments
No Comments

Professor Ganser is a great lecturer and makes even the most basic content interesting to listen to. If you show up and pay attention, you should have no problem in the class. Exams are also open note which makes them a lot easier.
Good lectures, attendance is for credit but because all of the other assignments and exams are open note it's easy to get a A. Very interesting content also.
Great lecturer. The summer final was open note and fairly easy. Good intro to sociology class, and the exam prep questions were cool. Overall very light hearted lecturer and a fun class.
Attendance is mandatory which is a little annoying, and the lectures are pretty boring. The entire class is open notes, so it's a free A.

LOVE HER.
Her videos were so easy to understand and she is so kind. She allowed 10/18 discussion posts to be dropped and one quiz. Material was easy to comprehend. If you need help at all she's there for you

I took this course over the summer during Early Start as an incoming freshman. I really enjoyed her class! She was very understanding and cooperative with students if they had to be absent or had questions. She is very passionate about her material and sets you up for success! She is very friendly, approachable, and personable!

Second class I took with her and she is really nice. Makes the content really easy, especially for SOC 100. You get to drop a bunch of discussion assignments so it's a lot of opportunity to increase your grade.
2 or 3 assignments a week, with two online tests, which were both open book. Speeds through lectures sometimes, but the slides are posted that night so you can review anything you missed.

She made it hard for people to not skip class, changed the rules a few times throughout the semester. Her lectures are great & genuinely interesting especially for those who likes sociology. A great introductory class. You'll have to do a lot of discussion boards but as long as you make an effort and follow the criteria you should be good.

Professor Ganser is so sweet. She loves when the class participates and is very good at responding to emails. Her attendance is based on iClicker, which she requires every class. The exams are relatively easy and open-note. She also goes over the study guide if you come to the review sessions. She really sets the class up for success.

Very easy class all the assignments were open book open note. There's a textbook but it's not needed to pass the class. About half of the assignments were dropped. Lectures were good sometimes there were irrelevant examples. She got upset with students leaving early and with iClicker. She's understanding and wants students to care as much as her.

Professor Ganser is a nice professor, but her lectures are so boring, especially for non-majors. All lecture slides are posted, but attendance is taken w/ iClicker. Assignments are 2 discussion boards (25 discussion boards and 10 drops) and a quiz each week. The only exams are a midterm and a final. You will get an A as long as you do your work.
I love Brittany
Very passionate teacher. Homeworks weren't bad and I believe you had infinite attempts if I remember correctly. Tests were also doable.

Prof Ganser was very fair and passionate, required only 10 discussion boards and 10 attendance credits, and cared about student input. Exams were online but open note; I'd still recommend taking notes and trying, though, because there's only 2 exams the whole semester. Really low maintenance, easy class.
amazing professor

It's an introductory Soc course, so the material isn't too interesting imo, but Prof. Ganser made the class great. Lectures came right out of the textbook (which has an online PDF), both the midterm and final were online, asych, and open-note. She gives lot's of extra credit oppurtunities and attendance is taken with iclicker.

Ganser was a great teacher who avidly enjoyed teaching sociology. Also provided lots of extra credit opportunities and allowed for many attendance and discussion board drops, which helped when I was busier with other tougher classes. Liked the fact that the exams were online and open-book so I could concentrate more studying on my core classes.

Class and prof were fine. Reads off of powerpoints but takes attendance on iclicker, attendance should not be mandatory in my opinion. Discussion posts every week that don't have anything to do with material. Exams were super easy though
baaaaa - tom shih probably cuz he's the goat

Previously took undergraduate fluids with shih. Didn't care much for his teaching style but decided to give him a second chance on this course. He is definitely much better when it comes to teaching this course. He is very helpful during office hours. There are few Homeworks but they are very had. Exams are open note and don't hold much weight.

In my opinion, he was horrific! He was terrible at teaching, slides were eh. they kinda had the hw solutions in the slides though. Just go to TA office hours and everything will be good, not his hours. He had a nice curve where if you get a higher score on the final, all your previous exams get bumped up. ask abt it.
I thought Professor Shih was an amazing professor that genuinely cared for the well-being and success of his students. He was very accommodating and helpful in class and especially in office hours.
Great professor and even better person. 10/10 would take one of his classes again.

Prof Shih runs one mess of a class.  Terrible lecturer, slides are super busy and confusing, and homework problems often have solutions that are unclear unless you attend office hours. Cancels office hours a lot.

Despite all of this, hes probably my favorite AAE faculty member.  Super nice guy.
Great Lectures and Very Knowledgeable

Shih definitely cares about his students. The class is definitely difficult, but if you understand the HWs you can succeed in the exams. The lecture notes are clear and concise. Shih doesn't care about plugging in numbers or simplifying algebra in the exams, which is nice.

Tom Shih is very knowledgeable in CFD and has amazing slideshows, but they often contain some mistakes that he catches and sometimes he gets lost in thought. But overall he explains concepts pretty well, and makes himself available to help in office hours. Homework is difficult but doable, and is usually only every other week.

Shih is passionate but he needs some serious help with teaching. He rarely explained concepts clearly and even told the class to forget the way the textbook did it because it was wrong. Homework problems were made up from scratch and he purposefully made up exam problems to be things we had never seen. Amazing guy, definitely not a great teacher.

Professor Shih is an awesome human being. Genuinely cares about his students and wants to develop them into great engineers. Cares more about building intuition and conceptual understanding than solving simple math problems. Alight course, great professor.

Professor Shih is a fantastic mentor, engineer, and professor. He cares that we learn the material, and he makes a conscious effort to make sure we understand the concepts. He's usually available outside of class, and his lectures are helpful as well. Overall, Prof. Shih's class is highly valuable for an aspiring aerospace engineer.

HW: missing variables, unclear directions, incomplete soln keys, grades at end of sem, takes points away if you don't calculate something he never asked for. Goes off-topic. Lecture slides are dense and confusing. Exam answers are symbolic, not numerical. Exams/PExams w/o solns. Freq absent from O-Hours. You can tell he puts absolutely no effort.

Spring 2021. Professor Shih gives a decent lecture and very accessible outside of class. The course and the material he teaches are hard. Get ready for self-study and use the office hour for homework. Last having Hans in class sucks.

The class was completely unorganized. Professor Shih would notice mistakes in his notes (which were very complicated) as he was lecturing, and sometimes he could not follow the material he was trying to teach. It was hard to follow his lectures, which made homework assignments very hard. Shih is a very nice, smart man, but not a very good lecturer.

Tom Shih is clearly respected as a leading figure in the aerospace field for his research in CFD. However, in this CFD class, he is incapable of teaching. The lectures have an incredible amount of errors and it seems like he has never seen the material in his life. It is impossible to follow and the entire class clearly struggled because of it.

Taking Fluid Mechanics online in Spring 2021. Worst class experience I have ever had. He is very nice and smart, but shouldn't be teaching. Exams are not similar to examples, homeworks are poorly explained and the lecture notes contain a ton of errors. It's impossible to learn anything. Other students have said 333 is easy with other professors.

take anyone but him for this class his requirements are not clear whatsoever and most of the assignments that we do in class has nothing to do with marketing management. he does not lecture properly and the exam is built on very niche things where he says that there maybe multiple good answers but we have to pick the best one AVOID HIM AT ALL COSTS

I created a RateMyProfessor account because I'm shocked at these low scores. I agree that attendance was highly graded and that he took a long time to grade things.  However, he was usually clear that with grades we would all be okay after the curve(even with the class doing poorly on the pop quiz). Overall, he was a nice guy and a good professor.

Pretty harsh with grading, and there's lots of assignments that he never gets around to finishing so they just linger on Brightspace ungraded. You have to purchase access to this business simulation website for 60 bomboclat dollars and run trials with a team. He's chill most of the time but also incredibly unenthusiastic at presenting/lecturing.

Very disorganized and difficult to learn from. Lectures are mostly random case studies with sloppy explanations and no slides or structured material to follow. It feels like you're teaching yourself most of the time. For a 7:30am class especially, the lack of organization makes it a waste of time. Would not recommend
Dry and boring is all I have to say. Ended with a 91.8 and got curved down to a B+ and he just says grades don't matter in the real world. Avoid if possible

Disorganized, unclear, and poorly run. Grades posted so late you never know where you stand. Midterm was 13 MCQ with 8 answers each in 20 min and felt designed to trick, not test. Teaching is outsourced to student debates and the syllabus totally ignored. Final project timing is unrealistic. Professor lacks basic course management skills. Avoid.
Sad Sad Sad, the worst professor at Purdue.

Every class feels more like a waste of my time. Very off script and has us doing random assignments that are not applicable to the class outline from Purdue. His grading is subjective and just based on ranking your group against others, and there is very little guidance besides trying to figure out what he may be interested in. Avoid.
I too would like to state that the one star reviews keep getting deleted. sus...

Dr. Nowak is an awful professor. Do not take this class. He grades unfairly if you disagree with him, and missing more than two 7:30 AM classes drops you a letter grade. Most students scored under 40% on the midterm, and you must write a paper to get the curve. I would also like to say that 1-star reviews keep getting deleted- avoid at all costs.

His grading system is inconsistent and highly opinionated. Quizzes are completely subjective and there is nothing you can do to effectively prepare for them. Class averages are almost always below a 50%, and he continually blames students for his historically low-class performance.

Dr. Nowak is disorganized and doesn't know how to prepare or educate students. He also goes off-script, ignoring the course outline in the Purdue catalog for what this course is described as. He makes snide remarks to people who don't agree with his viewpoints, which are opinionated and HIGHLY bleed into his grading style (which is never linear).

No syllabus, no lectures. You're intended to grasp random concepts from different Harvard business cases that groups present on but that he never lectures on. Very unclear what concepts we're supposed to be learning. Class average on our 13-question midterm was below a 50%. Grading is slow. Said he was a "type-B" professor, meaning disorganized.
Awful. No practise questions for the midterm exam.

Dan is a good prof. Extremely strict about AI and attendance (miss more than 3 and you drop a letter grade for every absence). There are a LOT of group projects and discussion posts in this class, but if you just put effort into it he grades nicely. Also, one surprise pop quiz that the entire class failed lol

Professor Nowak says that he has experience teaching but honestly, I'm not sure that he does. His course is disorganized and he doesn't have a clear grading criteria or a clear schedule for the course. It felt like he was making everything up on the fly and nothing was planned ahead. Lots of group work and some projects were not relevant.

I liked Prof. Novak, he had a very large turn around from the beginning to the end. In the beginning you could tell he was a bit overwhelmed by being a new professor at a new school, but by the end he was comfortable. He's a nice guy, fair grader, and gives everyone equal opportunities. If you don't like group work, you won't like the class

dr. pappas is a really nice guy and a good lecturer. the thing that sucks is that the exams really are not over lecture content so you have to memorize > 300 multiple choice questions in order to do well. lab was not as bad as others make it out to be, just make sure you get a good partner.

He's a good professor! Lecture exams were fine-- use the brain dumps and Quizzizz he provides to study, those questions are exactly what is on the real thing. No attendance for lecture. The lab section of the course is pretty difficult. Just be forewarned-- long & confusing labs, and HARD lab exams!
he is super funny in lecture and really knows his content. The exams are based on memorizing test questions, which aren't that hard.
he is so funny and good at lecturing, it is so easy to pay attention in class, he is very generous with mcq's on exams and is very knowledgable when people ask questions

The lecture portion of this course was actually quite easy. Focus on memorizing the bank of test questions he has for each lecture and explaining the learning objectives for the exams. He explains the lecture content quite well. I found the lab portion of the course to be much more difficult.

It's hard to respect a professor who creates his exams from a series of 400+ MC questions you need to memorize for each exam that probably hasn't been changed since 2022. He comes off kind but does not show effort in preparation of the course.

Dr. Pappas is an ineffective instructor. Success in this course requires extensive memorization of lecture quizzes rather than learning from lecture, which is often confusing and unhelpful. Exams consistently contained errors, and concerns were met with delayed or dismissive responses instead of meaningful support. Don't take BIOL221 w/ Dr. Pappas.
The dude loves smoking meats and loves cheese. Class is hard.

A fine professor, nothing special, but information is clear, though sometimes lecture can be info-dense. But, micro is already very difficult so not all him. Make sure to study like crazy; everything is extremely easy to study for if you give yourself time to do so. Lab is honestly more difficult to do well in than lecture, lab exams are impossible

Grading isn't harsh. Did not appreciate the occasional political comments. Do not take the BIOL439 lab with Dr. Pappas unless you are very passionate about micro. Otherwise, you'll probably finish the semester with a DISinterest in micro. It may fulfill 3 lab requirements, but it's not worth it without heaps of passion. C: I crashed out mid sem.

Dr. Pappas is a hilarious and passionate professor, but be warned, this class is very content heavy. The exam questions are pulled from a pool of 400 multiple choice questions, with additional short answers. To perform well, memorization of these multiple choice questions is key. No exam drops or curves, so you have to be on top of every exam.

Class doesn't promote meaningful retainment of information, as exams are created from a pool of ~350 questions, excluding the cumulative final. Instead, memorization of the right letter answer is the best way to complete the class as there is simply too much material. Exams worth most of the points. Avoid if you actually want to learn microbiology.

Professor Pappas outside of class seems chill, but the class itself (Eukaryotic Genetics) was a mess. We didn't actually learn anything in lecture and just went over homework questions every lecture. There were no provided old exams to study as well. You just go to lecture to go over homework and you can't skip since there's attendance.

10/10 awful experience. I have never witnessed a class more disorganized and purposeful in being a weed out. The syllabus states that 1/3 of students will not pass with a C! If you like memorizing over 500 MCQ for Exams worth almost all of your grade and participating in chaotic labs with no structure then this is the class for you. Avoid!

This is the WORST class you will EVER take at Purdue. He even says on the first day that it is a weed-out class. This class is insanely difficult. He is trying to weed everyone out of their majors. I would HIGHLY recommend taking this class at a Purdue extension OR Ivy Tech. Don't waste your time and energy taking this class here.

I took the 8-week summer version of this course. 2 lecture exams and 2 lab exams, each of which covered a decent amount of content without feeling overwhelming. Plenty of extra credit built in. By studying question bank and lab manual, it was easy to learn and feel well-prepared. Pappas is an engaging lecturer and a funny guy; he clearly cares!

Overall, nice guy and cares about his students. However, this class is pretty difficult in regards to both lecture and lab. Make sure you pay attention to the MCQs he posts to review for the lecture exams. His lectures can be entertaining, but lecture quizzes are a thing and can be challenging. As long as you're on top of it all, you'll be fine.

EOC is a great professor who makes it as easy as possible for you to pass his class while still having interesting lectures and helping you learn. However, his lectures can be really disorganized and he likes to go off on tangents (he then forgets what he was talking about originally).

Dr. EOC has entertaining lectures but can sometimes go on extended tangents that are hard to follow. This class is not hard by any means but can be time consuming with the long readings, quizzes, and exams. Quizzes and exams are straight out of the book and rarely include lecture material. All are online with multiple attempts which is delightful.
Really great professor, kind and does a lot of demos that are entertaining. He has the occasional movie, read and go to class and it should be very fun and pretty easy to do well.

Dr. EOC cares a lot about his students and is very fair in terms of grading. This class was very book-heavy in terms of what was tested, and participation is a large portion of the overall class grade. He is very knowledgeable and enjoys this subject, which makes the lectures more enjoyable.

This professor is incredibly knowledgeable in this subject and is a great lecturer. The professor made sure to encourage the class to participate and was always good about answering questions. The course itself was fun and very enjoyable. The professor did in-class demonstrations and allowed students to participate in them. Very educational!!!

Dont hesitate to take ANTH 201! A fair amount of the grade is from class attendance, but the lectures were very engaging and included demos, films, and discussion. You shouldnt be stressed about your grade if you do all of the assignments and attend class.

I greatly enjoyed ANTH 201 and am currently taking ANTH 313. While he focuses a great deal on the readings, the quizzes and tests are online, open book, and can be retaken. Lectures are aided with fun demonstrations, videos, and a good sense of humor. Participation is vital - attendance is not enforced, but attend class because it's fun.

ANTH201 with professor Erik is very instructive while also being a lot of fun. There is a nice balance between the simple and interesting lectures (w/ demonstrations) and the rich text that accompanies them. The course gives students a nice intro to archaeology and provides a new perspective to human prehistory with fairly straightforward quizzes.
A good class if you like to watch movies and want to balance your coursework with some less heavy material.
Laid back and funny. Lots of hands on demonstrations with volunteers.

Dr. Otarola-Castillo is a solid choice for an easy-A-type class. He doesn't do the traditional "lecture" thing; he hosts a casual classroom. Because of this, it's hard to figure out what we need to know for exams. That being said, he is a FAIR grader and is FLEXIBLE with students -- which makes up for the lack of structure. Not too accessible tho.

knowledgeable. Prefers an "open" style to his class: few formal lectures, notes, papers. Lenient attendance policy, but you won't get the info you need from just the readings cuz they're so dense. He's fair & flexible, but his exams are very detailed and he doesn't provide much direction in class to know what to take notes on/to remember for exam.

He is a soft spoken and nice guy. Usually helpful in office hours but sometimes can be a bit unapproachable. He is also not the greatest at answering questions but he def tries his best. Lectures are pretty chill, overall decent guy

Prof. Joshi's lectures felt like I was [residing in chair] at home watching a YouTube video of the course (kind of boring). Examples typically relevant to homework. Didn't answer questions super well. A bit hard to reach for getting issues fixed (3 weeks of me trying, even in person). Middle of the road, not bad guy.

Dr. Joshi does his best to make up for the deficits of AAE204. He's a great professor, but often struggles to answer questions in a straightforward manner in class. AAE204 itself had many issues this semester, including a lack of clarity for project criteria, regrade requests, etc. Dr. Joshi did his best to handle questions, but often fell short.
Dr. Joshi is a nice guy who clearly cares about his students, but he's not the most engaging lecturer. AAE 204 is also just not a well run course at all.

Dr. Joshi is a pretty nice guy and is clearly a very smart person, however his lecture style can be quite slow and boring. He  is a pretty responsive guy and will try to answer your questions quickly.

Joshi is a kind and caring professor who's helpful during office hours. That said, his lectures often feel unorganized and don't clearly cover homework or exams. He means well, but the lectures can be boring and hard to follow.

Prof Joshi is a great guy who is  passionate about structures and acoustics. Really cares about his students, very open to questions outside of class. Gives in-depth, comprehensive answers to homework questions and makes connections to industry. Very soft spoken, it is recommended to sit up front. Lectures match the assignments very well.

Usually don't care enough to do this, but the ratings for Joshi are just a bit harsh imo; dude showed up, gave the content that matched the homework perfectly and was always free after class to ask conceptual questions and homework help.  Clearly wasn't doing a cartwheel into class to talk about structures but was passionate about it if you asked.
Pankaj is such a nice fella but his lectures are boring and he's always playing catch up on course material. Exams were mad long for no reason this semester which resulted in pretty low averages.

Dr. Joshi is very soft spoken, so I often find it hard to pay attention in lectures because of how monotonous he is. Most of the time he doesn't understand what a student is asking when they ask a question. Also, somehow we ended up three weeks behind the syllabus and will now be going over new material during dead week, which is kinda nasty.

Joshi is a nice enough guy, but he thoroughly lacks any sort of passion for what he teaches. Concepts are poorly explained and he can be at times be unapproachable. I would strongly suggest that you get a different professor, unless you enjoy listening to someone explain something with the enthuse and intrigue of watching paint dry.
Great professor if you can stand the occasional quirkiness

Professor Canino is an awesome professor who really cares about his students and their learning! Engaging lectures, although occasionally he can be a bit moody. DON'T, however, let this fool you into thinking the class is easy. You are given everything you need to succeed, but exams really test you, both computationally and theoretically.

Canino is a great professor. He cares for his students and their learning and is passionate about the content. He uses fill in the blank notes which is hard without a tablet. He also moves quickly through or skips derivations, but still provides them to work through on your own. The homework and exams are fair and relate directly to the lectures.
