I had him for his first semester teaching at Purdue. Still one of the best

Absolutely legendary goated individual. Canino made me like AAE again for the first time since coming to Purdue, he actually cares about students' learning and well being, and is a generally great guy. He is the only prof I felt welcomed to come to office hours for. Turns out that made me go a lot more and actually learn a lot more! Overall, 10/10

Canino is a great professor who makes lectures enjoyable and is very accessible outside of class. This is a very difficult course but he did a good job explaining the material and giving clarification on homeworks and projects if needed.

The best professor I have had at purdue by far. Actually cares about his students, relates to them and makes it feel easy to asks questions in class. If he doesn't understand something, will research and get back to you. I actually enjoyed his lectures so much that I would forget to check the time and be surprised when class ended

Not much to say other than Prof. Canino is truly amazing. One of the best professors I've had in my experience at Purdue. Genuinely cares about students and their success; lectures are very enjoyable and usually fly by; generous grader (for exams). Highly, highly recommend.

Very funny, takes time to explain things to students, is not afraid to nerd out with students, and also a GOAT overall. GOD BLESS JAMIE CANINO. GOD BLESS JAMIE CANINO. GOD BLESS JAMIE CANINO. GOD BLESS JAMIE CANINO. GOD BLESS JAMIE CANINO. GOD BLESS JAMIE CANINO. GOD BLESS JAMIE CANINO. GOD BLESS JAMIE CANINO. GOD BLESS JAMIE CANINO.

A very good professor. It can be a difficult class, but he explains things very well, and answers questions well. I didn't understand shocks AT ALL in 339, but after one lecture from Canino everything made more sense. Kind and funny professor that answers questions (even the stupid ones). Cannot recommend enough for aero.
Good lecturer, very responsive outside of class. Goes above and beyond to help the students, and is willing to push deadlines if needed. Absolutely would take again.
Lowk looks like the pillsbury doughboy as in he looks very huggable and cute. Super nice dude too. Also very soothing voice.

Was not a good professor. Acts like he is caring and kind but will penalize grad students for working. Homeworks were 5+ hours and he didnt explain in lectures. He is a printing guy playing space. Run. RUN. RUN!!!

AAE 340 used a terribly difficult class. Prof Cunningham is improving it so that the material is learned thoroughly without making the content impossible. Lectures are very dry and not worth much, but homework is engaging and kind of fun. Exams are a time crunch but are graded generously. It is obvious that Prof Cunningham cares about his students.

Cunningham is the GOAT. I had him for 203, 251, and now 450. Each time he made learning the material fun and, by extension, easy. He has unc status. He's that guy who is always looking out for you, but will challenge you in ways that make you grow. 10/10 professor and 10/10 person.

(shoutout Talaria and the entire Despicable Me franchise)

This is the quintessential Cunningham course. Very clear lecture style, first half of the course is a breath coverage of the spacecraft subsystems which can be a little dry if you are already familiar with them. Second half of the course is much more interesting coverage with failure analysis and operations procedure. Also very joyful person!!

Thomas Cunningham is who you want all your AAE professors to be like. Great notes, charming guy, and he makes a somewhat boring class (203) pretty interesting. Occasionally gives hints for HW and gives great tips for exams.

This class is chill, but man the grading is kinda petty, and it's a lot of MATLAB assignments so be prepared. It's nice that it's group work tho. You will survive the class, and I would say "Tom Cat" is a good choice.
I took this class over the summer with Cunningham and he was a good lecturer. Pretty clear with the content, good lectures, good exams, he's a very nice guy. I would say I recommend him.
Great Lectures

Awesome professor; super knowledgeable about anything aerospace! This course is insufferably tedious and the homework rubric can be frustrating, but Cunningham is forgiving on any projects he grades. There is plenty of extra credit (up to 4%) to make up for bad grades. Prof. Cunningham is quite amiable and makes this challenging course bearable.

Professor Sánchez-Llama was incredible. He really takes the time to make sure the class understands what he's talking about. If you are taking SPAN 241, I would highly recommend taking his class. The exams are super easy because they're all memorization. I maybe studied 1-3 hours for each and never got lower than a 98. He's also a caring person!

Dr. Sanchez-Llama is a great professor who enjoys what he teaches. He's very helpful. Unlike some other Spanish classes, he fully teaches the class in Spanish with very little English involved. This actually helped me be more confident in speaking Spanish and sharing in class. Class is very reading-heavy, and he is a lenient grader.

His accent is tough to adjust to at first if you have never had any exposure to it, though easy to adjust to. If you do not understand a word, he will write it on the board in English for you. Make sure to do the reading and homework before class. Sweetest prof, very helpful. Can tell he wants the best for his students. Loves to discuss and engage.

This professor doesn't even know how to work a powerpoint, so you spend all of class reading out of the textbook. He also doesn't seem to understand us when we speak to him (in both spanish and english), so communicating with him about anything is impossible. I have had much better spanish departments professors and he disappointed me.

This class feels like I am taking it in Spain and not Indiana like it is for Spanish native speakers which was weird and fun. If you do not have much experience with Spanish from Spain, it will be hard to understand him at first but it was fine for me. There is no homework except for the readings and he grades the very few quizzes very nicely.
Great
best spanish teacher ever!! and adorable

Great professor! One tip: read the readings before coming to class.  You'll get more out of the class if you do.  He's incredibly nice. Best part is that he won't speak English in class as99.9% in Spanish.  He spoke maybe 5 words in English in the two semesters I had with him.  He writes English words on the board if he thinks we didn't understand.

First day of class, my mind was blown. He talked with an accent I was not used to. By the end of the third week or so, I was perfectly fine understanding him and really enjoyed the class. He is so good at giving summaries, you do not have to actually read the entire piece of literature. But I do recommend skimming it to get the general gist.

Prof. Cooper is one of my favorite professors at Purdue. He has a good sense of humor and his lectures are really interesting. You're expected to show up to lecture to do these small attendance assignments (they take like 30 seconds). You will have to do the assigned readings so that you can complete four essays which make up a lot of the grade.

Professor Cooper is a great instructor with a sense of humor, and the class won't siphon your lifeforce. I am not an Anthropology major, and I enjoyed my time with this course. Would recommend going to class, participating in polls, and take some notes. Participate if you want to pass but it isn't too demanding and the material is interesting.

A very kind and caring professor who cares about the subject he is teaching and the people he is teaching it to. This class is not very hard and is super interesting, 4 essays in total with a weekly Packback assignment and weekly film quiz. The final is online and open note.

The greatest professor at Purdue. Dr. Cooper truly loves archaeology and cares about his students. The class is very light and even if you do happen to miss a deadline he is extremely forgiving. A great professor and a great man.

The best professor I met at Purdue, treated his students like real people with respect and dignity. Love his class, 4 essays in total with Packback questions every week. Finish all that you'll get an A. It is not mandatory to come to class if you wish to take something easy.

As long as you get the few assignments you do have done on time you will likely pass with an A or a B. Textbooks are required but I do not know anyone who actually read them much. You can access the free online version so you do not have to buy them.

Professor Cooper is hands down my favorite prof Ive encountered here at Purdue. Hes an incredibly genuine guy who you can tell actually cares about us students. Its refreshing being treated like a human being and not a robot. Such a nice man.

Professor Cooper really cares about the topics he teaches. Anthropology is central to this man and if you are looking for an interesting class that's an easy A if you do the work, take this class. No real exams, just quizzes that are not very toughly graded. Maybe a couple hours of work a week with no required lectures.

He's very vague with grading criteria, but he is more than willing to answer questions and chat anthropology topics. There are lengthy weekly readings that you'll need to do for the weekly quizzes and discussion. It's a straightforward class with no exams. Do the work, and you'll get an A.

professor mohammadi is a nice guy who obviously cares a lot about his students. his lecture slides and hw/exam questions can be a bit confusing and conflicting. most of your grade comes from two midterms and the final which sucks but he does give extra credit for attendance/iclicker. if you just pay attention and do the homework you will be fine

This professor was great and really cared about what he was teaching. Lectures were interesting and attendance was extra credit. Grades were based only on exams and homeworks. All exam questions were based on homework questions and iclicker questions. Overall easy if you pay a little bit of attention.

This course was graded based on about 3 exams and 3 homeworks total. No projects/participation/quiz. Students in the class had to correct the professor on errors made in the content that was presented in lectures. There were many errors on the homework and even the exams! hard to learn when the content was taught wrong 35% of the time.

The class is not very well organized. There are several times where the exact same question is repeated, word for word with the same answers, on homework and on an exam. Although not his fault, he is difficult to understand sometimes. This would not be much of a problem, but he prefers to teach by speaking and relies on that instead of Powerpoint

I am enjoying this class so far. The iclicker questions are key, so make sure you take pictures of them. He is a good lecturer and explains the concepts pretty well. Occasionally the iclicker questions will be tricky (so be careful there because they are worth a lot), but other than that, I'd recommend this class.

He seemed fine at first but as the semester went on, he became just a lot to handle. He gave iclicker questions meant to trick us which means you dont get your participation points for the day (10%) of your grade. He made exams and quizzes harder than the homeworks, and overall just has a poor teaching style, I loved genetics before this class.

I really loved having Dr. Mohammadi as my professor. He is genuinely caring and kind. He is from Iran, so there is a bit of a language barrier just due to his accent. Other than that, his English is outstanding. Genetics actually is one of my favorite classes I've taken.

She makes so many errors on her end and when you email her about them she refuses to fix them. The only redeeming thing about this class is the content is interesting from the notes. However, don't expect an easy A because of her errors and mistakes and a refusal to fix them.
She's alright as a professor. Her lectures are sometimes hard to understand but are generally informative. I honestly think you should avoid online courses, they're just way harder to learn in.

I hate to say this, but the language barrier is really a problem. It is hours worth of recorded lectures and she is impossible to understand. There are sometimes captions, but they often blocked content. The quizzes are impossible because, as much as you study, they aren't grammatically correct. The final is peer reviewed, due during dead week.

I took FS 363 and she was the nicest professor I have ever had. She always made the concepts easy to understand, and whenever I had any questions she always answered them clearly and thoroughly. The assignments were a bit long but she is a very kind and fair grader to all, and she'll specify any missing details. I would love to have her again.

I took the online food science class and it was awful. I could not understand a word she said in her videos and not all of them had captions or they had captions they you couldn't turn off so it blocked text on the slide. My saving grace was the transcripts for some of the videos so I copied them into a word because I couldn't understand Dr. Cho.

Very straightforward course with minimal work -- just a few homework assignments, five quizzes (you need to watch the lecture videos themselves to get the answers so make sure to take good notes), and a final group project. Dr. Cho is very nice and accessible and cares about her students. Lecture videos can get boring, but content is easy.

She cares about her students and is a great professor. Work consists mainly of quizzes, assignments, and a final project. Her lectures are recorded and at times hard to understand but overall the class is fine (the quizzes aren't easy though).
one of the goats of AAE. Nothing much needs to be said except that she's goated. 514 is mostly just a review of 334

I took AAE 514 with Professor Bane, not 334. Generally speaking she was great, very friendly and helpful whenever people needed homework help. She organizes the note slides into topics, so you can sometimes be scrolling through 120+ slides to find what you need and can't ctrl+f because all the equations have subscripts. Other than that, loved it!

Lectures explain why things happen and why eqns work as opposed to just reading the textbook. Connects class material to the real world. Answers emails and HW questions quickly. Helpful during office hours. Class materials and website are very neatly organized so as to facilitate self-learning. She's everything you could want in an instructor.

She is just amazing! So nice and wants you to succeed. It was only a lab prep lecture but she planned all of the labs and helped out if someone needed something. Overall great person and even better teacher!

This class is extremely difficult and he overcomplicates key concepts. The daily quizzes were rough, just because of the complicated wording. He paces around the room lecturing. He is obviously passionate about philosophy but his instruction of it is so hard to understand and isnt suitable for a 100 level course. Hes a great guy though. avoidifucan

Super complicated quizzes every class (the wording is bizzare), kind of just paces around the room the entire time. 300-400 word reflections every week, and grades them extremely harshly. Overcomplicates explaining all the theorys. Not a bad guy, just a class id stay away from.
I had to withdrawal from this course it was so bad. He paces the room screaming in lecture. I went to his office hours on numerous occasions and he was no help.

Everything is clearly written by AI, and the professor does not seem to know what is on the things that she gives us when asked about it. Exam questions are extremely vague, yet the professor wants a specific answer. There were some improvements made for the second exam, but by that time it had already affected our grades.

Doesn't admit when she makes mistakes on grading even when shown proof.   Unorganized.  Test questions are extremely vague.  Doesn't follow syllabus.  Ordered so few supplies that had to work in groups of 7-8.  Would not recommend.

Nice person but... Very unorganized. She does not order enough supplies for lab. Syllabus has different due dates for assignments. Starts lecture late (still making slides). No exam prep available. Does not give out data sheets until days after lab is completed. Lab instructions unclear, comes in and changes things mid lab.
10/10 best teacher ever so great best teacher
very skibidi sigma
No Comments

Shuyi is by far my favorite professor at Purdue. The way he outlined MA266 allowed me to really grasp the information super well. Along with this he is just an overall great person and I would totally take a class with him again!

His lectures weren't bad but often he didn't have enough time to finish the difficult problems. I really appreciated him posting unfilled notes before the lectures as they helped me stay more organized. Exams were very difficult, but it was expected that the exam are hard.
Very unhelpful. When asked questions, answers with sarcasm. Good thing the class is easy.

Dr. Weng is by far the best professor I have ever had at Purdue. His lectures are incredibly engaging, and it is abundantly clear that he truly enjoys his work. I can tell that he is a professor not only focused on research, but also deeply committed to making a meaningful impact on undergraduate students. I would 100% take any class taught by him.

He's nice and funny, and he's a good teacher. However he's not great at making tests. I would go through all the study materials, and half the questions in the exams are phrased so awkwardly and unsimilar to study materials, which made them way harder. Plus, the average on the final was a a fifty and the curve was five percent

Calc 2 is a hard class but Shuyi is the goat. His teaching skill is on the level of the esteemed Chen from Chenflix - he manages to introduce all of the weird calc 2 topics in a way you can understand, which is not an easy task. Also his enthusiasm will make you actually want to show up to lecture even though he doesn't take attendance.

Homework due 3x a week. Calc 2 in general is hard at Purdue since exam grades are determined purely by multiple choice.  I watched every lecture but really your success depends on how much time you put into doing practice problems until you understand calc 2 itself.

All year he was a great professor, however the final was brutal and he only gave a 4% curve for Calc 2. I would say using previous years' final exams is not very helpful since he write his differently than other professors have in the past.
Shuyi's lectures are pretty good. Course relies a lot on tests with three midterms and a final and he doesn't make them easy. Review salons and quizzes for exams.
Don't know much about him, used Chenflix and did well in the class. Fall25 final was a joke tho, 54% class average with a SD of 12

DO NOT USE THE RMP SCORE TO JUDGE HOW HARD THE CLASS IS. This class is unreasonably hard, sometimes making me question if they want to teach the material or your reading skills. Lectures are pretty ok. Recitation barely helps, and quizzes suck. Past exams don't reflect difficulty of exams. Read lecture notes carefully and use hw to study. good luck

This class was the bane of my existence. So much homework, recitation preparation for quizzes, and obviously exams. The exams were extremely difficult and no amount of studying really helped me. Boilerexams helps to an extent.

Shuyi is a great professor. His lectures were amazing and very well organized. The recitations weren't the best and did not help me. The homework takes up a lot of time and there are three a week. Be sure to really study for the third midterm and especially the final.

Shuyi is great at teaching, but sometimes gives dismissive responses. The salons are not helpful, the quizzes are hard, and all tests and final are incredibly hard. The Math Resource Room is underrated, so please go and do not fall behind. There are 3 HW/week, and they took hours for me to finish.

Good professor, but recitations had difficult assignments with Salons and Quizzes that made up a significant portion of your grade. Everything in this class was difficult, HW was not super helpful. You are better off studying for exams with old exams or practice problems. Watching OGT and chenflix carried me for the exams.

he has incredibly clear, well-organized and methodical lectures that i found wanting to attend despite not being mandatory. focused on the fundamental concepts which improved my understanding. super approachable and gives tons of help to get you through the course. tests are tough as they always are, they're doable if you practice well.

Shuyi is a phenomenal math professor by all standards. However, his approach to teaching Math is more derivation-oriented, so if you're someone who benefits more from practicing exam difficulty questions, Shuyi's classes may seem a tad drawn out. Personally, I love Shuyi's approach since it helps me understand the concepts from the ground up.

For context, I already had a 5 in Calc BC in hs.

For every unit besides series, I feel he taught it extremely well and concisely. He derives everything before giving you the formula to connect the pieces in your head. His notes are very organized, too.

His tests are extremely difficult and concept-heavy, so don't fall behind. Stay consistent!

Shuyi is a very tolerant professor and does his job very well. He does not hold back on exams. Know what you're getting into. Definitely go to lecture and GO TO HIS EXAM REVIEW SESSIONS. Lots of people skip them to review on their own time. He picks specific question types that will be on the exam.
Pretty good professor. Class was difficult but he helped make it better. Some topics aren't gone into enough in lecture due to time constraints.
Go to lectures or watch chenflix and you'll pass, but be careful on those exams, exam 3 was already bad but final came in like a nuke.

Unfortunately, his lectures were outright bad.
Oftentimes his lectures just turn confusing, and would often just make it harder to understand in general. The material itself is presented good, but his explanations did not do it for many of us.
(Watch out for the final exam!)

The class was fair enough that if you kept up with assignments and recitations you would (most likely) pass even with subpar exam scores. He's a good professor, and his exams are usually difficult but not impossible. However, you should watch out for the final. He moves like Von when cooking up the final. Just slaughters the students & their grades
lectures are engaging and you can tell he cares! class is hard just because its a hard class, he doesnt make it harder than it needs to be.

Shuyi truly is very knowledgeable and is quite fun to listen to talk. He always does a lot of problems in class step by step, which is super helpful in understanding. When it comes to sequences and series, I truly believe that no professor can teach that unit perfectly, so I still struggled quite a lot in that regard.

The professor is highly confusing, and the structure of the course is poor. I would not recommend it to anyone, especially if you have no linguistic background. Expectations frequently changed, and overall, this class made no sense.

I've taken Ling321 and I have learned more in this class than any other one. She can be direct but she isn't trying to be mean or rude! She's just from a culture where the style of communication is direct and honest, even though it did sting at first. Don't take what she says personally. She means well and cares about her students. Ask questions!

I'm aware this is an unpopular opinion, but I did truly love this class. Advice to future students: you cannot take it personally when you're corrected. You have to come into this class willing to put in effort and be ok with being told you're wrong. Prof was also understanding and expressed genuine concern when I missed class due to health issues!

It's hard to describe the experience that was this class with Benedicto. Tons of busy work, most of it to be done with your group. She doesn't teach much in class but expects you to somehow know everything during in-class activities. The most disorganized course I've taken -- the first few weeks were the worst, and no one knew what was going on.

Going in, I knew over the years she was regarded as the worst professor in the linguistics department. Unfortunately, that is absolutely a fact. The class is awfully structured. All we do is get scolded for "disappointing" work, and we have to deal with her constantly changing expectations. She expects our lives to revolve solely around her class.

Professor never grades and is extremely passive aggressive and condescending. She is very knowledgeable about the subject and clearly only teaches so she can research. She has the passion but not the teaching capabilities. her class isn't hard but the workload is plenty of busywork and she isn't clear on what she wants for an assignment.

The content is interesting, but she makes it sound very complicated. She has us do group projects, but berates us when we don't do it exactly how she wants (without providing rubrics or examples). Makes attendance optional then asks me why I skip. She teaches us like we are confused high school students. Weekly reflections are just busy work.

Prof Benedicto has been my least favorite professor at Purdue by quite a bit. I really wanted to learn the material but she made it so difficult and would make pretty rude comments at times. I would avoid her class at all costs.

I dropped the class and decided to graduate without my linguistics major because of this professor and class. Her instructions and expectations were unclear, yet she was condescending when there were misunderstandings. I learned more from the linguistics classes I took that were taught in my second language than this one.

This class was rough. If you are not a linguistics major, I would avoid at all costs. If you are thinking about minoring in linguistics, I recommend you dont ONLY because of this class- it is not worth it. Many people dropped the class due to mental health reasons. The professor is condescending and will never answer questions.

Content of the class was not hard but she made it hard. The course page was a mess to navigate, she gave few examples, and she did not explain anything in a concise manner. Often classes will feel drawn out and unproductive. People drop the class due to her manners.

To be honest their is more that I just can't summarize well but avoid if possible
tumbs down
never grades anything
never grades anything, makes up assignments on the spot, does not have a clear schedule set, her slides make no sense, very passive aggressive

shes a refreshing prof. Most profs are like robots but she has a clear personality. but sometimes if you argue a point, she will blatantly disagree with you and it sounds pretty harsh. 
The class was difficult but you interact with your group more than anything so if you dont like her you dont have to really talk to her.
Shes a late grader though.

I had professor Benedicto several years ago. Every time I get hit up for donations from my alma mater, professor Benedictos employment is the reason that I do not donate. Most of my gripes echo the reviews below. Her behavior is unprofessional, her grading is inconsistent, and she doesnt even want to be on the classroom. Let her stick to research

I came into this class eager to learn. Sadly, Elena didn't come ready to teach. She is passionate about linguistics, but she is NOT passionate about teaching. Students are not her priority, and this is apparent by HER MANY absences from classes during the semester. Looking back at the reviews, it has been this way for over a decade. Step it up, EB.

As a graduate student who's had more than one class with Elena, I do NOT recommend her classes. Very low quality, very confusing explanations, little discipline, being late to class, leaving for days for conferences in other countries, not grading your assignments on time, etc., are the features of this professor!

There aren't enough characters allowed here to express my frustrations with Elena. As I type this, it is Friday of finals week and we just received our final report assignment 2 days prior. 3/31 grades have been entered, and after confirming we weren't behind before she left for SPAIN, she sent emails with numbered "missing essays" 2. DAYS. AGO.

Yes, while she does leave the country for research purposes, the students and the class that students paid for should be at the top of her priorities.  This is not the case at all.  She didn't even bother to check her e-mails while away, when there was a problem with the materials that she was supposed to have delivered to us. flat out apathetic.

Elena is actually a wonderful teacher for any student who has a desire to make the most out of his education. It's true that she leaves the country but only in order to conduct research. She is readily available for consultation and, being a teacher, she is more than willing to help a student having trouble.  Basically, if you try, you'll succeed.

Elena Benedicto is a wonderful teacher. She explains things very well and is very helpful. She makes you think... Put some effort,  guys, linguistics is not easy! Learning is not only fun, you need to work as well.

Undoubtedly the worst teacher I've ever had.  Quite possibly, the worst teacher I've ever seen.  Unless you need her class to graduate this semester, don't take her class.  If you can't avoid taking her class, start drinking.  A lot.
Simply awful.
Possibly my favorite professor at Purdue, you can tell he really likes Power Electronics and cares about the learning of the students in lecture. Very funny guy as well.

Dr. Pekarek is an effective lecturer, but the pace of the course is very fast. If you fall behind, it's difficult to catch back up and there is a huge amount of electromagnetic theory that you need to know before taking the course. For mechanical engineers, I wouldn't recommend it unless you have serious experience with motor theory and matrix math
Pekarek teaches well & classes are enjoyable. He has high standards, exams, projects and quizzes were challenging but there was a curve. Focus on learning the material & not the grade is my advice.
The LeBron James of the Purdue ECE department.

Hardest ENGR course Ive ever taken. He is funny and knowledgeable in terms of deriving the equations during classes, however that means a lot for the students to crank through themselves. Almost no examples are gone through in classes, making it almost impossible to do the HW without going to the TA. Exams tests you in a different way than the HW.

Steve was an amazing professor. I didn't do the best in this class due to some outside medical reasons, but he went out of his way to be compassionate. The material was very interesting and his lectures can get boring (as it's a lot to cover), but he makes it a point to learn every name and keep it engaging. Seriously one of my favorite ECE classes
Dr. Pekarek is a great lecturer and easy to follow. His office hours are super helpful for the projects.
Really enjoyed taking this class. Is funny during lectures. Ask questions if you don't understand. Very helpful outside of class. Exams are not too tough.

Pekarek is one of the most enjoyable professors to have. He teaches the material well and cares most about students learning it. He's lenient when he needs to be. His lectures are engaging and cover the material well. Pekarek learns every students' name and tries be approachable.
A great teacher who is unique in his own method/personality

Very kind and helpful. Explains concepts in great detail and makes sure everyone understands it. Very accessible and Helpful outside class as well. Always willing to clarify student's doubts. Exams are conceptual and test your understanding. He is one of the best professors I ever had during my engineering.

Prof. Pekarek genuinely cares about his students. He does a great job of clearly explaining tough concepts and adjusting based on student feedback. His assignments and exams are fair if you show up and do your work. He was very willing to meet with students outside class and help you out if you're struggling.

Great class, he makes you learn the concepts very well. Has a great rapport with the students, makes some nice jokes to ensure that the class isn't very serious.  Exams are concept-based, need to know the concepts well to score a good grade. Definitely recommend this class!

Can be quite confusing during the lecture, but really helpful during office hour. If you don't understand, GO and ASK him. He is very very patient and helpful outside of the class. Class itself is okay.

Textbook is not good, but is written by him, so you're in a corner there. Very good teacher, great lecturer, funny. Helpful during office hours. Class is challenging, homework was time consuming. Exams unpredictable.
Spectacular professor!

This class was weird. He was funny, which made the class tolerable, but I didn't learn much. He covers a lot of stuff but doesn't exactly say how any of it's relevant, which is important in a class like this. Still, it's an easy-A, so it's not a total loss I guess.

One of the best professors I have had at Purdue. He is always available for help and makes sure his students learn. He will always give extra chances to bring your grade up if he feels that the whole class needs it.

Prof. Pekarek is the best professor i have had at Purdue. He is really interested in what he is teaching and wants to help students understand and like the subject like he does. He presented the material with an appropriate depth, but was always willing to share more of his knowledge.
He is very good and very nice professor. He is so modest and he treats students like his friends..

Definately not easy... at least for ECE 610. Tests will actually make you think instead of regurgitating notes. Having said that, you will not learn nearly as much from any other professor as you will from Prof P. One of my top 5 professors across about 170 credit hours total in my student career.
He is an awesome professor and clears all doubts.
Best lecturer ever had!
I took the class when it was still ECE495I. He's great at explanations, I could do the Fourier Transform after him going through it twice. I'd have him as a professor any day :)
Pros: explains things well, humorous, is available if you need help.  Cons: course is hard, exams are hard. speaks very very slow.

Can say this is the best professor I've had so far at Purdue. Homeworks are long but every professor uses the same homework. His lectures are amazing, he explains very well and gives hints for exam questions. His exam and quiz prep lectures are also very effective. He takes questions and cares about his students. Much better than Pam Porterfield.

Professor Wolanski is very kind. Although material can sometimes be difficult, he understands that and makes extra time for those topics. He is very understanding and cares about his students. Prepare and go to class, it's much more difficult to try and learn the material on your own.

Loved this professor. The class was very organized, and he is a straightforward lecturer. He genuinely cares about your success and is very helpful during office hours. I've recommended him to all my friends. He's a great professor, especially if you have PTSD from Pam, because he'll remind you that statistics isn't the problem she was.
Good teacher

My GOAT. Professor Wolanski was amazing. I actually did better here than in MGMT 30301 (which is basically AP Stats). He's very available in office hours, quick with emails, funny, and genuinely cares about Purdue (he's from Indiana). His engaging style encourages collaboration and discussion, so you're basically studying while learning.

Got an A in this class and a C in 30301. The content in this class was harder, but Wolanski makes it easier to understand. He is very caring and really wants you to do good. Go to his office hours and get help on homework it makes the class a lot easier. Do not wait to do the homework last minute it takes a lot of time. He is an amazing professor!
Inconsistent policy enforcement. If you're having a tough semester and end up with him, good luck.
Go to class and you will understand the material. He allows 1 regular sized note sheet each exam. The exams are very similar to the practice exams. He is a great professor.

I hate stats so I dreaded this class but Prof Wolanski made up for it. He is very into what he teaches and wants to see his students succeed. He knows that this class is a pathway course for a lot of classes and thats the only reason students take it so he tries to make everything as painless as possible.

Professor Wolanski is one of the best professors I have had at Krannert. I struggle a ton with Stats and he really helped break down the material and made it a lot easier when studying. He really takes feedback to heart and is really great. Overall, one of the best professors and deserves an award.

If you go to lectures and office hours, there is no reason you should do bad in this class. Professor Wolanski is great, he really cares about how you do in the class and that shows in office hours. Lecture material is exactly like the homeworks, quizzes, and exams. Goodluck.
If you like yourself, then you ask for this guy. Cares about the material and each student in his class.

He knows what he is doing, and he tries his best to make it as easy as it can be while not simplifying too much. Homework takes a while, but they're just once weekly. Exams are OK, 15 multiple choice questions don't sound bad, but you have to know the material, just like every other class. Not a class to stress about, which I liked.

He is such an amazing prof. He really cares about every single student he has. He makes sure that he know everyone's name and gets to know them. I would say go to office hours because homework can be challenging. Another piece of advice I would give is to make sure you to to lecture because he goes over everything that you need to know.

He really cares about his students and is very helpful when you have questions. Attendance is taken at random, but I recommend showing up every class because the lectures really help you with assignments and exams.
Really good prof. 305 is a lot easier than 30301. There was no surprised on exams and he provides enough practice material. Listens to what students give feedback on
Great teacher, good response time, good lecturer, nice guy, no complaints

Overall very good professor. Tough grader on the homeworks, but provides lots of help in office hours. Exam review is so helpful as he asks the class what THEY want help on. There is random attendance, but go to class anyway as the in-class examples are super helpful

Wolanski clearly cares about his students and the material. I struggled in the stats class required before this class but Wolanski makes the material very understandable and he is patient when explaining. On top of that, he just seems like an all around sweet and caring guy. Hoping he continues to teach this class for other students!

Professor Wolanski is great! One of the better professors I've had in my time at Purdue. Very organized, and communicates his expectations very well which is much appreciated. Fantastic lectures allow him to break down complicated concepts in a very effective way. He also has consistent office hours which are very helpful.

He is a great professor I took him his first semester @ Purdue and he paid attention to the students wants/needs very well. Anything that you asked him to change he would. The only problem I had was that he gave us a practice exam that was nothing like the exam meaning that everyone studied the practice exam failed, but he listened and changed.

Just make sure to get 100% on the homework by going to his office hours and asking questions. Study for the exams and quizzes and you'll have no problem getting an A. If you don't understand something, don't dwell on it and go straight to office hours to understand. He always responds to emails and is helping students.

Professor Wolanski is a bright professor who clearly has a passion for the content and his job. He is very accessible via office hours and email. He is willing to be flexible on homework grades (a large portion of the final grade) within reason. Get ahead on homeworks and use office hours to help and you will do fine. Very approachable and caring.

Really engaging lectures and you can tell he really cares about what he's teaching. While the reading and subject matter can be dense and time intensive at times, the few graded essays and the project are graded leniently. Would totally recommend for his positive energy and if you're not as inclined towards the subject.

Great prof. He knows what he is teaching and has great discussions. Did not have to try much in his class and feel like he emphasizes learning and discussing more than worrying about what grades you get in the class.

This professor really is the goat. Class is pretty fun especially with the discussion group thingy, you really only have to participate (pretty easy unless you miss/sleep in class like me), three essays and in class writing and that's a free A. He is a super easy grader too, as you can see from my grade and my level of writing. 10/10 professor

His lectures are engaging and make literature exciting. He fosters open discussions, where everyone's voice is heard. He brings enthusiasm to every book, making them exciting. He genuinely cares about his students' success. He encourages a discussion based environment which leads to a better understanding of the reading at hand. I highly recommend!

He is a kind and understanding professor who keeps class discussions engaging. The course includes regular readings and a few essays. You do not need to participate much in discussions to get an A, and overall, the class is very manageable.
Professor Riesbeck is easily one of my favorite professors here at Purdue. Great guy, easy to talk to, makes class fun, and a very chill grader. Would 100% recommend taking his class.

He's a fantastic professor. Cares a lot about his students and is very funny. Relatively easy grader and is always very willing to help. He turns otherwise boring books into engaging and interesting discussions. Just a couple writing assignments through the semester and pre-class readings. 10/10 professor, take this class with him if you can.
Amazing professor who really cares about what he teaches. Turns the class into an active group discussion on topics from the reading. As long as you come to class and participate, you'll be fine.

I've heard about many horrible profs for SCLA 101, and he is definitely NOT one of them. 

Makes both lectures and assignments engaging and fun.
He was awesome! Very interesting and engaging… and not too harsh of a grader.

I'm an engineering major and hate reading, but this was by far my favorite freshman-year class. He's genuinely so thoughtful and made philosophers interesting. Try participating and don't use your phone and you'll get an A.

Attending class and participating results in doing just fine in this course. He allows you to miss class if needed, but only a reasonable amount of times. He is looking for you to participate don't have electronics out and I promise you are set. He is hands down the best SCLA 101 professor Purdue has!

This class was very easy but was fun. Almost every class was an open discussion style over whatever the assigned readings were. The reading material was tedious at times but there were almost no assignments for the class, and the ones that were posted were extremely easy. Lots of extra credit was also offered. Would definitely recommend.
The class is fairly easy since Dr. Riesbeck is not a tough grader and the homework is just readings. Class discussions can be fun and Dr. Riesbeck tries to make them engaging.

This is the most engaging English class I have ever taken.  Some of the stuff we read I had read before, but never cared about until his class.  Other professors would have made the topics drab and the reading a slog, but the way he guides conversations makes you want to do the reading and participate.
Great professor! He made bleak topics seem super interesting and did a good job at making the class really fun and interactive.

One of the best professors I've had here. His class was probably the first time I've actually enjoyed a writing/english class. Class participation is necessary, but it's very easy to participate. He's also a very lenient grader which is nice. I'd highly recommend his class, he's really funny and makes class very interesting and engaging
Wonderful professor.

Prof. Riesbeck is an amazing prof. He is passionate about the class & students success. As long as you have a good understanding of the readings and try and participate in class, you will do great. Attendance is mandatory and no tech is allowed, but you also don't need tech in class. Overall great prof. Highly recommend taking SCLA 101 w Riesbeck.
Really cares for his students . Gives great feedback. Really personable and down to earth . Made SCLA 101 fun
Prof. Riesbeck is very personable as well as a very lenient teacher. He is passionate about what he teaches and grades fairly.

This class is mostly about group discussions so there's not a lot of outside work other then weekly reflections and 3 papers. Professor Riesbeck obviously cares about connecting with his students which makes the class more personable and interesting. Overall and amazing professor and I would definitely take this class again.

Great Professor who cares a lot about his students. He made SCLA 101 super interesting and actually fun. He does a great job at breaking philosophy up and explaining it. I would love to have this professor again and sit in on his lectures.

Absolutely great professor who cares about his students, funny lectures, encourages students to do well, available during office hours a lot. Easy grader, actually made SCLA feel interesting and not like just another English class. I love this professor and would take any class he teaches. 10/10

Professor Han is decently good at teaching, gotta give her that. But what makes her not so great is her harshness. It's like she doesn't care about her students, is very blunt and doesn't try too much to be understanding to any extent really.
Strict grader, difficult to understand. Does not respond to emails, but notes are posted and can be followed in class.
Just sitting in lecture stresses me out.

Han is a pretty good lecturer to have for dynamics which tends to be a more difficult class. She talks pretty fast through the content and equation derivations and spends a good amount of time working through example problems. She can be quite blunt and straight forward at times which may rub people the wrong way but can teach content well
Poor attitude, extremely petty and rude. Disinterested in helping students. Avoid for any class if possible.
She is kinda petty and condescending sometimes, but other than that, she teaches well.

Professor Han is what I can best  describe as the pettiest professor I've had. While she's very smart, informed, and you can approach her for questions, she is very critical over small things. She gets upset if people converse with one another, stops teaching and even refused to give extra credit because one person left class early.

For grading there is no good reference for where your getting individual points from(lab manual only helps so much). They take points off for absolutely anything and everything. They are very particular about things that are not important or constructive to the material being learned. Grading is inconsistent. Hope she improves but rn 32301 is buns.

ME32301: Thinks she's better than everyone else. Refuses to provide feedback and then grades reports poorly. Felt worse about myself leaving her class each week. Reported full lab groups to the university for plagiarism with no forewarning, because she felt their executive summary was too similar to the lab manual we had to purchase for the class.

For 1-credit ME32301, there was no clear grading rubric on the projects. When we asked for rubric, she did not want to give the grading rubric in advance. As some mentioned earlier, she can teach, but she needs to fix her attitude. Very stubborn in a bad way. Lectures are boring. Schedule of assignments was great though and gave us to vote on it.
She does not care about students and shows no respect to your personal situation at all. I also do not think she has any ideas about what she was teaching.

Professor Han did a pretty good job in teaching statics. The combination of theory and examples helped give a good overview of the content. Professor Han would give good tips, and I felt the explanations got better as we got further into the semester. I would have liked a bit more theory, but you'll be fine if you show up and listen.
Lectures were never very informative. The class didn't feel set up to succeed, seems like a good person, but not a very good lecturer compared to others for ME 274

I was not a fan of Tina. She includes light-hearted example problems in addition to the from-the-book lecture problems, but her lectures consist of so little theory (only examples) that the relevance of any single problem is limited only by how well you are able to generalize its concepts to other homework or exam problems.

Tina is a much more casual professor when compared with most (since she technically isn't one). Dynamics is by far the hardest class I've taken so far as an ME, so I don't really blame her for me being confused from time to time. Our class used slack meaning I could message her any time and usually got a response in less than 30 min.

Not very good at explaining material. I would frequently read through the book before class to understand what was going on, then leave class more confused than when I got there. I only kept going to class because of pop quizzes. She also didn't respond to questions very well, she seemed to get upset when people didn't understand something.

Although from what I understand Econ 370 is a difficult class. However, Professor Xiang made it clear and explained the concepts thoroughly. As an alumni and looking back I can say without a doubt that Professor Xiang was the best professor I had throughout my college career. Make sure you attend his lectures and will get your desired grade.

Professor Xiang was great. He gave very engaging lectures and did a great job of relating theoretical models to real world situations. If you study the homework and the quizzes you will do well on the exams.
I dont know how you will use this information for a job.

Very intelligent man, best Econ prof I have had so far. He relates every model to real world scenarios and I took a lot of information away from this class. He can be quite funny too. Tests are all T/F questions, and they are very reflective of homework and quiz material.
His lectures are like stories. Very easy to pay attention the entire time. Every concept just sticks if you pay attention well enough. I dont even think taking notes is fully necessary.

Very good professor and extremely understanding to any issues that may arise. Some problems on the exams are worded a bit funny, but other than that it is a great class and I would definitely recommend!
He can struggle sometimes with English, but it is fairly easy to understand him. Homework and quizzes are really straightforward and easy. Not to much work.

Very clear in his explanation of all material.  Always willing to help students who are struggling and need additional help.  Tests are very straight forward and fair.  I'd highly recommend any class with Dr. Xiang.
Solid professor.  Relatively interesting lectures.  Straightforward tests.
Completely useless. Reteaching econ 370, but poorly. Tests are almost incomprehensible because of his struggles with English.

His lectures are very boring, he teaches one thing and tests us on other material. Bad teaching and bad manners. Disrespectful to the students, doesn't help at all, contradicts himself most of the time. His arrogance is very obvious. Unfortunately, most of the professors in Econ dept @ Purdue fit this description.
Not bad
The material is boring, no need for textbook. Lecture note is enough for the midterm.
yap, I agree. No need for the textbook in this class and save money.
You can take as many notes as possible from this class, you don't need to purchase textbook in this class. The midterm only come from his note, his class is quite boring.
You learn something from his class, but the class atmosphere is really dry. He writes everyting on the board and the midterm will be basically cover from the note.

This was one of the most interesting and fun lectures I have had in my (adimitedely not that long) career here at Purdue. I liked the lectures enough to make an account to write this. Professor was engaging, always open to questions and very dynamic interacting with us in lecture. Amazing instructor, beautiful topic, just overall good times.

This class was genuinely one of the hardest classes I have taken here at Purdue, probably the hardest undergrad math classes imo. However, this professor genuinely made the experience much more manageable. Exams were ROUGH, and he was upfront about it from the get go tbh. Just study as you do your homework and go to office hours, Youll be fine

Quite possibly the greatest professor I've ever had at this school. One of the best lecturers I've ever come across. Make no mistake though, this is a brutally difficult course. The content is manageable, but the pace is extremely quick and the exams are quite difficult. Well worth it (IMO), I've learned so much in such a short amount of time.

Awesome guy, impeccable lecture notes. Really caring in office hours. Has a strong accent but still understandable. Manageable homework loads. However, exams are really difficult (maybe complex analysis is just difficult in general). Skippable final which is a big plus. Honestly on the better end of math professors at Purdue.

Some of the best math lectures I have experienced at this school. He explains content at a good pace, simplifies definitions when necessary, takes questions, and gives warm-up and in-class problems. He is also very open during office hours and will take time to explain anything one-on-one.

One of the best math professors I've had so far at Purdue. His lectures are very well organized and engaging, starting with warm up problems before going to the content. I also found his office hours to be very helpful.
I felt unprepared for exams too.

Probably the best professor I've ever had. He is a lovely, happy, and upbeat guy who is very good at explaining and connecting concepts. In the past I've been more fond of computational math classes but he made me find a passion for theory based math. If you have the chance, take his class.
nice, and helpful

Sasha is one of the best professors I've ever had. I've struggled a lot with Purdue math, and I struggled in MA 265, but he was very good. He's very passionate in a way that made me more interested in the material than I otherwise would have been. He's also very attentive to students in his class and makes sure to involve students in the lecture.

He's extremely caring for his students and explains everything very clearly. He's willing to repeat the same concept however many times it takes to get the point across to the student in office hours. I didn't think I'd like Linear Algebra, especially having taken it before at purdue with another professor, but he changed my mind.
Spends a good amount of time going over grammar. He's very friendly and approachable, and overall an excellent teacher.
Gives really good feedback. Helpful and easy to approach.

Rathmann is easier to understand when we talks in German than in English :) The class is predominately in German. Most of the class was sitting and reading through the textbook. We completed textbook activities in class as well as for homework. Tests and quizzes were easy to pass with some basic studying. Really good feedback outside of class!

I really enjoyed this class, although the professor is very strict when it comes to grammar mistakes. However, he is very helpful when giving resources to students that struggle with grammar. We had to write two papers. If you wanted to, you could go back and correct the first version (by looking at the notes he made) and get a higher grade.
Super nice and quite funny!  I made sure that I have him as a professor next semester too because I like having him as a professor
Really clear and easy to understand. Tells you exactly what to study for the exams and the final is usually just a project. Nice guy!
Really good professor. He is really nice and understanding.
Not very clear on course objectives. Knows exactly what he is talking about, but is very vague with respect to what he expects of his students.

Absolutely wonderful professor for linear. Truly cares about all her students, is very kind to talk to during office hours, and homework is very manageable. Lectures were great as well, she stepped through quite difficult topics providing intuition and examples throughout. Would 100% recommend.

Professor Delaney did an excellent job teaching MA366. Her lectures were extremely effective at explaining challenging content. Provided helpful example problems through her lectures to reinforce concepts taught. Extremely approachable outside of class for help in office hours. Exams were straightforward with no surprises. Highly recommend.
Good professor
Lectures are recorded and not required. Tests were graded very generously. HW wasn't that bad. The lab section for 366 (which is unrelated to prof) wasn't very good but is tolerable.

I took Ordinary Differential Equations with Professor Delaney, she was a great professor! Her lectures always explained the concept well and included excellent examples, which were great preparation for exams. She wrote extremely fair exams and if you have an understanding of the concepts being tested and practice enough, you will breeze through
COM 251- Professor Jha was incredibly relatable and funny. The lectures were super insightful and often hilarious, especially towards the end of the semester.

Ms Dyuti, is a tough grader in everything that can be graded. The exams frame could be better, can have more MCQ and less essays. Be prepared to do 25 MCQ and at least 750 words of essay in 50 minutes, and of course, hand-written essays. There are also individual essays of 1500 words and group essay of 1000 words and presentations.

Dyuti Jha has very harsh conditions when grading, especially exams. She does not communicate her expectations for any of the assignments and has issues communicating with the class in general. She is also very slow with grading assignments.

Terrible, her grading criteria is extremely unclear and changes constantly. We have to remind her to send us a study guide and it doesn't even reflect what is actually on the exam. She takes way too long to grade things, it's currently the end of week 15 and we still dont have a grade for an essay from the first half of the class.

Prof. Delworth is such an amazing professor. I have never been good at math, so I was weary of how this course would go, but the content was very easy to understand. Delworth and the TAs are very accessible and make sure to give entertaining and informative lectures so that the material is super easy to follow.

Nice guy, but lecture style is very much just going through work super fast. Tests make up 70% of the final grade so if you aren't a good test taker, good luck. It was taught as a review class almost with comments being made like “if you never saw this in high school good luck learning it now” but for me it was the first time learning all of this.

Didn't have him as a professor but attended his office hours and classes instead of my original professor. Math has always been very difficult for me but he explained things so well. Such a funny, engaging guy. Worth the 7:30 lectures!!!

Dr. Delworth is literally the funniest human alive and I swear this is the first useful math class I have ever taken. The content is pretty easy to follow and actually applies to real life. I got really sick the day of the exam, and he worked with me to make it up. Overall 10/10 professor.
Love him, his humor, his classes, and his dog. We're all going to miss you!
HIlarious and so fun. Made math relevant to real world issues
Attendance is mandatory, but if you show up and do the basic homework you will have no trouble getting an A.

I love this man so much. His class is so easy, and I felt like I was actually learning useful information. My TA wasn't the greatest, but I was always able to get all of the homework done in class. Honestly the best math class I've taken, and definitely the easiest way to get my last math credit out of the way.

Delworth was a great prof. Weekly homeworks aren't bad and the review guide before the exam is identical to the actual exam, just with different numbers. Attendance matters! You get three free absences and then 1% of your grade for every class that you miss after that, so don't miss. Lectures are fun and engaging so there's no point in skipping.

Delworth was a 10/10! as a non math-savvy person, MA155 was great and actually enjoyable. the assignments each week were easy to keep up with, simple, and applicable. the course was very straightforward and extremely easy to follow and pass with flying colors . delworth is a great guy, and his jokes and uplifting personality made my day every time!
Great teacher who will help you preform well on test. The best math professor you will ever have. Also very funny

I have always struggled in math and have always disliked the subject due to how much I struggled. Delworth is the first instructor to actually help me understand the subject, and made me want to come to Calc 1 every morning. I recommend him highly, and if you can handle a 7:30 am, Delworth will be the best professor for you.

I have always hated math. And Delworth said on the first day "this class is for you then". The first time in my life I ever had an A in math. He makes it understandable and fun. Best professor if you need to get a quick math credit, easy course!

Love Delworth! Struggled a lot to wake up for the 7:30 am calc class but his personality made it so worth it to show up every day. He has a lot of phrases that he says which I find hilarious, and you can tell he truly cares about his students. You should definitely have lunch with him at feasting with faculty--great way to get to know him

I was skeptical of the reviews, but Delworth really does make the 7:30 am class worth it! Never once regretted my choice to take this section. There is a random quiz once a week, so attendance is semi required. He explains things well and doesn't overcomplicate them, and he's pretty funny.

I've always struggled with math throughout high school, but his explanations and willingness to answer ANY student questions was amazing, no matter how basic. He makes the 7:30am class time worth it. Tim is very funny, which also helps with the early class time and hard content. Do yourself a favor and take his section, you will not regret it.

Having Delworth as a professor is probably one of the main reasons I was actually able to pass math. This class is super test heavy, with 80% of your grade being based off of exams, but you can drop the lowest midterm. He is always available outside of class and is super funny in class which made the 7:30 am worth it. He always posts test reviews.

Mr. Delworth is a gem of a math teacher! He teaches at a great pace and finds relevant real world scenarios to help with understanding. He gives a practice exam for each unit which has the same questions (diff numbers) as the exams which is awesome. He really emphasizes understanding which is rare for a math teacher. A lot depends on your TA though

Delworth makes calculus tolerable, even at 7:30 am. His engaging lectures and funny personality make the tough material digestible. Use unstuck study alongside the free textbook to excel in this class.

This professor is one of the best math teachers I have ever had. His lectures are clear and you can tell he wants you to succeed in the class. He is respectful and understanding of students. If you go to lecture you will do fine on the test and quizzes. He is amazing as a person who was very worried about calc and not a super huge math person.

I got a D first semester calc with a different prof. Delworth just makes it all make sense. Totally recommend. 7:30am is a hard class to get too but if it means you get a good grade and have a good prof it is worth it.
Biggest regret is not having him for MA158, only passed MA16010 because his lectures are super engaging, take his 7:30!

Delworth is hands down the best teacher I have ever had. I had him as well for precalc and I took a 7:30am class just to have him again. His jokes are really funny and his notes help retain the material.  Everybody should take 158 and 16010 with Delworth even at 7:30am.  Also (x+h)^2 does not equal x^2+h^2!

Delworth is the best math teacher I have ever had. His 7:30 lecture is tough to wake up for, but worth it. If you struggle with math and want a chance at passing Calculus, you have to take Delworth's class. Even for a 7:30 am class he is always funny and keeps the class engaged. He also encourages office hours, so getting help is very easy.

If you have the chance to take 16010 with Delworth, TAKE IT. The 7:30 AM lecture is brutal but he makes it tolerable. His classes are engaging and he is really funny, and is more than willing to help you out. He tells you exactly what you need to know. Example problems he does in class are the same as homework and tests. TAKE HIS CLASS.
His class has been an enlightening journey, thanks to his deep understanding of the subject and his ability to inspire a love for mathematics in his students.
The class has been an enlightening journey, thanks to his deep understanding of the subject and his ability to inspire a love for mathematics in his students.
Really cares, will do great lengths to help students who are struggling to the point of setting up zoom calls to explain concepts before exams.

Lectures are pretty slow-paced but he is very nice and cares a lot about students to the extent of knowing names and such. The final focuses a lot on content after the last homework covered in lecture so be prepared for that. Very lenient grading too. I highly recommend him if you want to have an easy probability experience.

The first two midterms are very easy, and Dr. Glubokov is a really lenient grader. To be honest, the final exam was kinda unexpected and difficult for me. He assigns fair amounts of homework; he responds pretty fast to emails. He was pretty helpful with any questions I had and was really sweet and caring, so be sure to go to his office hours!

Great guy, but not an amazing lecturer. The class was very slow (we didn't even get to a few things listed in the course description, like moments and CLT) but very easy. Kind of funny that he bots his rating.
Best professor ever. Made 67 joke during lecture.

Thank our lord and savior Jesus amen that Andrey Glubokov entered my life. Prior to this experience, I was lost and depressed. However, when I finally understood the fundamentals of probability, I was saved. 

"They say getting an A is for the barbaric. Getting an F is where those with class lie."
--Matthew 192 (The Quran)
Very fair grader and a great personality. Lectures can be slightly hard to follow but overall very solid professor.

A person who did not attend the class and received mediocre grades blamed the instructor. I was on almost all lectures and it helped me very much to pass the course. It was a lot of work but it worth it. The instructor was awesome answering the questions during his office time. He has an accent, but it doesn't matter.
Botting RateMyProfessor reviews by a professor is a new low. Look up his name on reddit for actual opinions.
Good class overall
Class was superpopular and very very crowded, big minus. It was also cold+

Tough class, very lecture and exam heavy, but exam partial credit was pretty generous. Make sure to write down all that you can think of. Homework is participation, and a key will be provided, but no additional practice problems were given. Attendance is not required, but lectures are not recorded, and I would definitely show up to this class.
Class was superpopular and very very crowded, big minus. It was also cold

Dr. G  is one of my favorite professors. His class is very interest-based and open to further exploration. I contracted his class for honors credits, and he was very knowledgeable, enthusiastic, and easy to work with. If you like to be involved, you'll love his class!
Outstanding class performance!
Intense and brutal class but helpful professor, very optimistic. I do not know anyqone who ddn't succeed
Design of E is very useful class
Very easy class to take with this Professor.
It was a relatively well organized class. Professor acted optimally,no complains
Pretty good class
Tests were hard
The office hours are very useful and final is pretty doable

Some really like him. To me, he is OK. For independent learners I would not recommend this prof. 

Grading is arbitrary and can be (very) generous or harsh. Tests are based on in-class problems. Final was not the best. Supposedly cumulative but heavily lob-sided to last chapter. No curve. Difficult to reach for any appeals after grades are posted.
A very enjoyable person and fun class
Best math professor I have had at Purdue. Always able to answer questions and made lectures very funny and entertaining. Highly recommend.

Had Prof. Vercleyen for MA 262. Can't count the amount of times that I have almost died of laughter in that class. Awesome, funny examples during lecture, and very easy to follow teaching style. Love the effort he puts in to make sure everyone participates and keeps up with the material during class.

Professor Vercleyen is great at making difficult subjects much easier to understand. He also has a sense of humor that make his lectures much more interesting than you would expect from a math course. Overall, he is an amazing professor who wants you to succeed and you can tell.

Dr. Vercleyen is the best professor I have had at Purdue so far. He somehow makes his lectures (non-mandatory) so digestible and entertaining. I had Dr. Yeung at the beginning, and could not follow his lectures, so I attended one of Dr. Vercleyen's and immediately felt the difference. Dude is the goat

Dr. Vercleyen is the best professor I have had at Purdue so far. I had him for MA 262, and he deserves a five-star rating. He goes through everything thoroughly and engagingly in his lectures. Attendance isn't mandatory, but I still went to every lecture I could.

Attendance was never mandatory, but our entire class stayed consistent with going to lectures the entire semester if that tells you anything. He's just a passionate professor and prepares you to perform on exams well. He also wipes the chalk board clean in a very satisfying pattern every single lecture which I really enjoyed watching all year

Prof Vercleyen is truly a hidden gem of the Purdue math department. I know multiple students who had other professors but started coming to his lectures to understand the content better. He kept lectures engaging and explained everything in-depth.

Gert is honestly the best professor I have had. He genuinely cares so much and works so hard to make lectures worth while for the students. I feel like i have a decent understanding after leaving his class and look forward to going. He wants to see all his students succeed and it shows in his teaching and enthusiasm for what he does.

Prof V. is one of the best math professors I have ever had, rivaling even Purdue's esteemed Dr. Chen. He teaches clearly, taking extra time on problems the students are struggling with while not falling behind the course content. I felt I didn't have to put as much time into studying because I understood the content immediately in class. 10/10.

Prof. Gert is the best professor I have had for math here at Purdue. He makes his lectures so funny and enjoyable and genuinely makes me want to show up to class everyday. He taught me the content so much better and deeper than any other math professor I have had. Definitely one of my favorite professors I have had at Purdue yet.

Lectures are not mandatory but are very informative and helpful. Professor is easy to understand, funny, and puts effort into lecture by writing with chalk instead of flipping through slides. I usually struggle to pay attention in lectures, but I could always rely on Gert to keep me locked in. It was a pleasure to attend his lecture.

Professor Vercleyen puts a lot of thought into his lectures and genuinely wants his students to learn and understand the course material. He takes the time to answer students' questions during lectures and also offers plenty of resources (lecture notes, office hours, exam tips). His class is the most engaging and enjoyable class I've taken so far.
Best lecturer

He is one of the best math professors in the Purdue Math department. Really tries to make the lectures engaging and you can tell he enjoys the subjects he is teaching. Makes harder subjects easier to understand.

Prof. Vercleyen's lectures were great. A couple times in the semester he did a fun lecture that deviated from the usual. I believe his sections often scored higher that the average across all sections.

Professor Vercleyen is a great professor who is clearly passionate about what he teaches. He is funny and his lectures are always very engaging and enjoyable. 100% recommend taking his class as he will set you up for success on exams and in the course overall. He explains the material extremely well.

Professor Vercleyen was a great professor, but an even better teacher. He made the lectures fun and engaging, using creative real world examples that made the subject feel like he was talking about more than just numbers. Moreover, he was incredibly helpful at office hours! I would highly recommend professor to any student!

This is an amazing professor who cares a lot about making the course relevant and engaging for his students. He is always willing to go the extra mile to make sure you are capable of succeeding in the course. Attending his lectures will majorly help your understanding of the material, and if nothing else, you're guaranteed to get a good laugh.

Prof. Vercleyen is engaging, kind, and the perfect math professor. He explains problems both theoretically and practically, and consistently stops to ensure all students are following. He teaches concepts in a fun way, like writing code to analyze the motion of a falling nut in "Ice Age." I highly recommend taking MA 266 with Vercleyen.

Being Purdue math, the course is difficult. Professor Vercleyen was an excellent professor who gave comprehensive and engaging lectures. He's very good at going over problems that will be in the exams.

Professor Vercleyen is the best math Professor I've ever had. 
He did an amazing job of explaining topics clearly in a way that was engaging.
He was funny, he was sweet, and every lecture was worth the time.
Teaching style is amazing, always is prepared for class. Uses a chalk board so it is easy to keep up with his lectures writing notes.
Clearly very passionate about mathematics and works hard to make his lectures engaging. Also, he grades much faster than all the other professors.

Prof. Vergleyen is one of the best math professors I've had. His clear, engaging lectures make complex topics relatable with fun, real-world problems. Attendance isn't mandatory, but his class was so enjoyable I never missed a lecture. He is truly an exceptional educator who makes math exciting; you can tell he really enjoys teaching!

Great professor. I think the most important thing is to actually learn and retain information through college, while the grades don't necessarily matter as much. That's what I say at least to cope through ECE, but Professor Brinton has been a blast this semester. If I could take classes with only him, I would. He cares for every student's success.

Brinton is a top 3 ECE professor at Purdue (and he's not 2 or 3). His lectures are fun to attend, he communicates with the class often and openly, and evidently cares about the students' learning. This class is challenging, but I have no doubts it would be harder without Prof Brinton
amazing guy! deffo recommend!

Professor Brinton teaches ECE302 really well! He explains concepts very clearly, answers questions without judgement, and keeps the class engaged. He's also funny, which makes the conceptual difficulty of 302 easier to tackle.

Professor Brinton was a great lecturer and came to class with the same energy every day. You can tell he cares about the students and the grades that he is giving out to them, he doesn't want anyone to fail. He makes himself more than available to talk to and always replies to posts on Piazza if needed. He also played guitar for the final

I really liked Prof. Brinton, and I would definitely recommend taking 301 with him. However, our final is very hard, and we will see if there is going to be a curve. I wish there is more problems to practice with.

I was low key dreading taking this class initially, but Professor Brinton actually made me super interested in the topic. He teaches well and genuinely seems to care about us. His handwriting is neat, his notes are organized, and his lectures are easy to follow. I think he's the best professor I've ever had.

Prof. Brinton gives me hope for ECE. His clear lectures, well-designed assignments, and humor made learning easy. You'll have no issue acing the course if you pay attention and do the homework. 

Aside from easily being one of the best teachers at Purdue, Brinton is a stand-up guy who cares about his students and seems like a lot of fun at parties.

I am so glad I took ECE 301 with Prof. Brinton, wouldn't have had it any other way. This class is super math heavy and took me way out of my comfort zone as a BME but the amazing lectures, hilarious dad jokes, and brilliant lecture write-ups are what drove my eventual success in this class. Best Prof ever! Take his class, you won't regret it!

Great lecturer that is passionate about the topics he teaches. He takes the time to make sure you understand the why and the how behind the new information you are introduced to. Work load is what you would expect from a graduate ECE course, put in the effort as with any other class and you will be fine.

Great professor and a cool guy. You will need to watch the lectures as the very least, because his notes are unintelligible without context. Fairly easy class, with a focus on probabilistic analysis of communications. Allows you almost 100% exclusive control over the choice for your group paper and presentation topic.
