Great Professor. I had him for 301 and he always sets you up for success. Lecture and notes are easy to understand, he's always happy to answer questions, and the homeworks/practice material prepare you well for exams.

Professor Brinton did an excellent job of breaking down complicated concepts that ECE 30100 has, and explaining them in a way that is clear, concise, and not excessive. He does not put in unnecessarily complicated explanations, and if you pay attention to lectures, you will have no trouble with the exams and homework.

ECE 301 is inherently challenging: complex content & computationally heavy problems that require sophisticated mathematical thinking. Prof. Brinton did a phenomenal job of clearly explaining the material in an engaging way. I am truly inspired by and respect his expertise in this field and in teaching. The exams were fair and he's really funny!

I really enjoyed 301 with Prof. Brinton. His lectures make the difficult material much easier to understand. I don't think I would have enjoyed 301 if I took it with anyone else. He is also extremely helpful and approachable outside class. One of the best professors I have had at Purdue

They restructured 301 this semester and did a really good job of it. Homeworks were a little challenging, but you'd end up doing really well on the exams if you really understood them. Lectures are also amazing, and Dr. Brinton is just a hilarious guy in general. If you understand lectures and hw, you're set.

Fall 2022 is Brinton's first time teaching ECE 301. Considering that, he did a great job. His lectures are relatively straightforward and is very approachable/helpful. There is quite some homework, which is difficult, but lots of partial credit as well. Exams are like HW, but also chance for individual curves on each exam.

Overall, amazing professor and did a great job both in class and out of class making it clear as to what needed to be done in order to succeed in the class. Exams were similar to the practice exams and allowed you to actually feel confident. I have never had a professor or teacher that has cared this much about his students and is a stand-up person

Professor Brinton honestly is a really good person. I loved attending his lectures and always made me feel welcome in the classroom. He is a super understanding person and takes that into account when doing grades. In one exam, someone interrupted us everyone the end and Prof. Brinton gave everyone a couple extra points to compensate for it.
Great teacher, great class, actually cares about his students

Prof. Brinton is a diamond in the rough. He also leads advanced research at his own startup AI company, so he knows what he's talking about in lecture. Tests are somewhat difficult. Homework isn't too bad, especially with TA help. But Brinton made himself very accessible during a mental health crisis, which is what I really respect most about him.

Professor Brinton was a great professor. This semester we functioned as an online class due to COVID but he still made himself available through recitations and requested meeting. The course was ran very well, with a manageable work load, clear grading criteria, and ample office hours. I would definitely recommend taking 20875 with Prof. Brinton.

Professor Brinton is one of the best at Purdue. He's really approachable, knowledgeable, and caring. He truly wants you to succeed and will work extensively towards that end. All you really need to do well in class is a good attention span, as a lot of the lectures are very dense, and a solid work ethic.

Dr. Brinton is a great professor. He genuinely loves teaching and takes great pride in the course (which is extremely well-run). He is quick to respond and happy to go into further detail than what is covered in class. For 20875, I'd read up on basic matrix/vector operations since they pop up a number of times in class.

He's one of the best professors we have at Purdue. Down to earth and very transparent about anything related to class. You can always email him and expect him to reply back with a full response to your question. If you have the option, please take him.

Very clear lectures. Course is content heavy and has lots of work, but the content isn't difficult. Exam can be challenging but is reasonable and not difficult. The only hard thing about this course is the amount of ODE techniques and formulas to memorize.

He is a average lecturer, going through the material rather robotically. Makes everything we do very procedural feels more like a methods class than a purely mathematical class. Does not explain connections to other areas of math very well.
Very knowledgeable professor. His lectures are clear and easy to understand. He makes sure to explains parts where students may easily get confused. He also has nice handwriting.

Professor Dongs lectures are unfortunately very boring even if you are mildly interested in the subject. Lots of tedious homework every week and no drops so have to do every single assignment except for the week before finals. However, he is fair and the midterms are open notes. If you have past experience with Calculus, the course should go ok.

First of all, the course is badly structured. There's a lot of homework, with one due during spring break. Homeworks are not graded before the midterms. While the midterms are fair, the points weightage for each question is not stated before. He doesn't share his lectures or notes, even if you had to miss class for a valid reason.

Does not upload lecture videos or notes. Very hesitant about sharing them after the end of his in-person lectures too. Has homework due every 5-6 days, regardless of breaks or exams. Had a homework due during spring break. Had HW due 2 days before and after the exam too. 2 midterms and 1 final. Exams are fair though. Most boring lectures ever.
Well organized lecturing style. Uploaded lecture notes, and homework/test solutions. Consistent office hours and response to emails. Solid Professor.

Maybe due to the nature of the course, he did a horrible job of explaining concepts and most people stopped going to classes (Right after we started to have in-person classes after covid...) Worst of all, he moved homework delivery dates around without properly notifying the class via email. He was usually cold and a bit rude, but exams were fine.

Truly the worst professor I have ever had in my career. He rushed through lectures and never stopped to explain anything. If you missed something, tough luck because there was no blackboard or anything. I barely could understand him or his writing, and he explained the concepts so poorly that almost the entire class stopped showing up/taking notes.

Professor Dong is knowledgeable in the course materials and explain them well. Lectures are fast-paced, so stay focused. Exams are fair as long as you understand the lectures and review them. Numbers in exams are pretty nice, but they test your overall understanding. Homework is a pain, MATLAB or other coding tools recommended. I enjoy the class.

Extremely knowledgeable! Has a great understanding of the underlying concepts of the subject, and does a very good job of explaining them to the class. The exams are just right, with an ideal mix of problems with all levels of difficulty and complete coverage of subject material being taught.
nice guy but cannot lecture for his life unfortunately. also did not post ANY content regarding the 150 question final exam except for time and location. curves on exams are awesome though
legit just reads off lecture slides and is very boring. Exams can be easy if study and labs are a breeze however would not want to take agian. Had to do learning all myself.

Lab for this class is easy as long as you utilize the TAs when you have questions. Hacker, on the other hand, is not a great teacher and will often go on tangents or ramble about topics unrelated to the class. He provides no study guides and holds exams at very inconvenient times for students. Not a bad guy, just not a good professor.

Professor Hacker was one of the weakest professors I've had at Purdue. Cannot for the life of him take control of his classroom and put together a solid, interesting lecture. Exams and labs were supremely easy, however, which didn't make the class too bad. (Until the 150-question final exam, which was ruthless)
I could listen to this man go on tangents for hours. The actual lecture material is okay.

Actually a horrible professor. Cannot take constructive criticisms. Will purposely make exams right after breaks. Exam 2 for this class was the Monday after Thanksgiving. He is very condescending towards students

Will huff and puff and make a fuss about trying to take a look at your exam. He is bad at answering questions during class time. And has a bad attitude overall. 0/10 would give negative rating if I could

Horrible teaching style. Will read off of the slide decks that he did not create for all of 25% of the lecture time then will open a Linux terminal and do random commands for the rest of class. Cannot speak up when lecturing, he will mumble constantly. He will engage in side conversations not pertaining to the information. Exams are poorly written.
he puts his exams after breaks on purpose
Super nice!

Prof. Holloway has a solid lecture structure. My only issue with the structure was that the multiple choice type questions were never really practiced in class. She really knows the content. Very quick to respond to emails and regrades. I actually feel like she reads my regrades too, which is always a plus. Clear speaker, I recommend for thermo.

Very approachable. She will go through the concepts for the first half of lecture and then do an in depth example or two for the last half. Never went into too much detail on the concepts to the point of making you confused. Midterm averages were around the low 80's so the exams were definitely not too bad.

Dr. Holloway is really friendly and approachable outside of class to clarify lecture concepts. Her lectures are also pretty solid and tell you the stuff you need to know for exams, and her worked examples are super useful for doing the HW. She's also willing to give back a lot of points on HW.
Professor Holloway is one of my favorite professors at Purdue. She explains the material well and provides many examples in class. She is also a fair grader.

I honestly think her lectures were super helpful because they were orientated more towards worked out examples. She understood that a decent section of the test was concept based, but she chose more to focus on reviewing for the written problems on the exam.
She was an average professor. She explained material well. Logical and pretty easy to follow her examples. Quizzes every Friday which reflected the material (only day you are required to show up).

I work my butt off in Thermo more than any other class, and I bomb my exams. I am an A student, and my friends with different professors score significantly higher exam grades than me. I feel like it has to do with Holloway... Lectures are poorly structured, flies through content, she likes to say "this is review" when it isn't. Take 7:30 Naik

Beth may have a degree in Engineering Education, but she does not know how to clearly explain the topics. Is sarcastic and confusing in class and moves way too quickly, no regard for students understanding of the topic. Is confused why she has the second lowest average on exams.

Her first year teaching ME200, Professor Holloway shows that she truly cares for her students. She can get little flustered while lecturing but presents the information clearly. You'll need to do a lot of work outside of class to get an A.

If you have even a tangential interest in aerodynamics, Professor Schneider's lectures are an absolute blast. He's the only AAE professor I've had who talks about real world project issues like funding/timeline/interest. He's fun, knows an incredible amount, and is honest about things he doesn't know. Homework/exams are hard but not in a cheap way.

I took this class online, and the only delivery method was an audio recording alongside his class notes from 1999. I think it's a cruel joke that Purdue allows this class to be delivered in such a way. Schneider is obviously an expert but expects perfection and will laugh at your problems. I recommend avoiding this professor.

if you didn't do everything EXACTLY like he and his TA want you will lose tons of points on both homework and exams. Homeworks have nothing to do with lectures and lectures have barely anything to do with the exams.

Had him for Hypersonic Aerothermo.  Almost didn't take the class because of bad reviews both on here and from students.  SO glad I did!  Learned a ton. Relates concepts back to real projects. Yes the class is hard but he really knows what he is talking about and can explain it well.  One of the best professors I have had.

The title of "hypersonics" in any course immediately means something is cool.  It's too bad this professor's notes consisted of scrawled equations and incoherent ramblings.  Even his grad student got no better than a 60% on an exam!  The other students got an average of 25%, and there weren't many dummies at this level!  Terrible professor.
BAD!!!!
Not at all helpful.  Everyone is on a need to know basis, and you need to know more than he thinks you do.  Keep away

Wanna have a rainforest's worth of handouts and be baffled by lectures?  Then prof Schneider is perfect.  He thinks everyone else is stupid and you can't speak 2 words of a speech before he interrupts you.

I think Dr. Route did a great job presenting. I could always tell that he was very interested in what he was talking about and wanted us to be. The experiments were always cool. I started the course not looking forward to it, but he made the class interesting. So just keep it up.

I actually really enjoyed Dr. Route's lectures. You can tell he likes his teaching, and answers questions with class and grace. I like his demonstrations, as they're very easy to follow in class and help me understand the content better. He also takes the time to explain every practice problem, and also explains why the other answers are incorrect.

I took physics in high school and that definitely helped with this course's content, as they are essentially the same thing. Prof. Route is a somewhat dull guy but is knowledgeable about the content so office hours may serve you better than the lectures. Grading is pretty lenient for a STEM course as you can drop your lowest exam.

Taking physics in high school definitely helped with this course (as the content is really similar). The grading is pretty lenient for a STEM course and you can drop an exam. Prof. Route did an okay job during lectures, he is knowledgeable but really dull. The lectures just can't keep people engaged and he makes bad jokes. He's an okay option.
Dr. Route is great at explaining new concepts in a way that is easy for us to understand, helping aid the necessary intuition when learning physics.

Ngl he is fine when it comes to teaching the course its just a little boring. Also, when he would do an example question he would just stop his work and then randomly show the answer instead of actually walking through the WHOLE process of the problem. What I did was watch another professor's lectures before every exam and that seemed to help!
Loved this Route's lectures, seemed very excited to teach.

I bet route is a great human but he's not a great teacher.I was confused after every lecture.I even asked him questions and he would give me some insane answer that was not remotely related to what I asked.He's smart, he's just not cut out to teach a beginner physics course.Most of my rage is fueled towards the course coordinator

The slides are course coordinated so it's the same across PHYS220 sections. He is a good lecturer and explains things well. A bit dull, but he walks through example problems step-by-step which is helpful. Loves to answer student's questions about Physics. Just an average professor.
Very good lecturer. I've never felt bored in class and as long as you pay attention and try even remotely, the exams are very easy and you should do well.

He seems like a very nice guy, but the lectures just aren't the most engaging.  There's a few cool demos and he does take time to break down equations for example problems.  I know he doesn't determine the content of this course, so as a professor he's fine, but the material just kinda sucks.

If you're wanting to question your intelligence this is the course for you! Lecture material will leave you guessing and homework takes literal hours. Lab was not bad at all but you'll really have to put in the work to get a good grade in this course. Good luck.
GOAT. Great at explaining stuff, and super funny. Occasional Minecraft-related problems on homeworks and even exam. Partial credit was given generously on exams. Loved this class!
Challenging exams for sure but can't fault prof chynoweth, you can tell he really cared about the content and our understanding of it.

I have no idea how Prof. Chynoweth makes all the info is this class so digestible when we moved so fast. Really clear lecture style with great slide deck. Exams were really reasonable. Somewhat humorous kinda. Loved the class.
bro has a tiki bar in his house. just for that he gets a 5

Lectures were quite exhausting, but otherwise substantive. The way she assigned partners for the semester project was a bit awkward; my partner ghosted me entirely and I had to do the entire thing, but got a 100 on it, so at least the grading was fair.

Content is super easy but like all of the other reviews say, the lectures are pointless. Everything is in the slides so as long as you go over them before the exam, you're fine. pretty hard to sit through the whole power hour when there isn't anything engaging about the lectures, and I feel like psych isn't hard to make exciting

Attendance is not mandatory, and this is her area of research so she's very knowledgable on it.The power-hour lecture is really hard to sit through I'll admit, but attendance isn't mandatory so I didn't go a lot of the time.
This class is super manageable. The professor is knowledgable and gives clear grading criteria.

Just really, really boring. She doesn't do a bad job preparing us for the exams, but the role she plays in it is meaningless. She reads through the slides in class and adds nothing of value. Having the slides (which you do get) is just as valuable. She doesn't seem passionate about the class or students, but just there to do her job (reading).

Her lectures are as engaging and enjoyable as watching paint dry... I genuinely hated this course because she was the professor. The lectures are so dry and she sounds like she hates her life. On a good note, her exam content comes right from her lectures. She makes exam study guides to help. Glad to never have her again!!!!
Not a good professor, lectures have a lot of information, but instead of teaching, she just reads from her slides. No help in preparing for exams, so good luck studying if your not good at that.

I honestly thought the other reviews were over exaggerating...they were not. It's my 2nd day in her class and I'm already finding myself fighting to stay awake due to how monotone her voice is and how much it irritates my soul. The only thing she does is read off the slides and talk for an hour and 15 minutes. I wouldn't suggest taking this class!

She is OK. She is approachable and often includes fun pop references in her lecture slides, but lectures are difficult to sit through because her speech has a sleep-inducing quality and she mostly reads off slides. Her tests are doable with a moderate amount of self-study, thus easy to get an A, but beware of her monotonous lectures.

Her lectures are annoying, she speaks with a bored cali girl accent, and her slides don't match the "study guide" she gives for the exam (which is just 200 arbitrary sentences and terms), so very often material you aren't expecting appears on the exams. She teaches the terms and definitions but not how to apply them, and the exam is on application.

literally so monotone i fell asleep in every class. she CANNOT teach, be ready to do a lot of self studying outside classes. the only good thing i can say is that psy 200 was well structured, so getting an A is easy bc the exams are easy and the attendance quizzes/tafmeds/coglabs are alright too. but you really gotta put in the work outside class.

She does take attendance but drops around half of the attendance points so you don't have to attend classes for half the semester. The video quizzes aren't bad and exams are fine if you review the video quiz notes. Also, the APA paper is pretty easy and outlined exactly how to write it, plus you're given feedback multiple times.

People are being too harsh. Dry lecturer, but expectations are clear and attendance policy is super lenient. Amount of assignments can be intimidating, but everything is spelled out for you ahead of time so it's super straightforward.

The class is honestly really easy. Some of the exams were a little difficult. Essay is easy, you get a lot of instruction. You barely have to go to class which is really nice especially because she has the most monotoned voice I've ever heard. It's honestly hard to listen to her.
She is not the most interesting lecturer. Just info dumps the subject matter at you in the most monotone voice imaginable. But she is really sweet and nice. Very accommodating.

She gives many quizzes and exams and also uses iclicker. I really do like her and feel like she does not deserve the hate. I actually understood what she was teaching and ended up doing better in her class. I do wish she would add more excitement in her voice when going over the lectures.

I honestly do not understand the hate she gets. She is one of the nicest professors at Purdue. She provides so much support for all of the assignments and her rubrics are very clear. She provides examples and answers for almost all of the assignments. There are two video quizzes per week, but you get to drop 7/8 at the end of the semester.

All of you saying that she gets too much hate are suck ups. This class was horrible. Half the time she can't figure out how to use the software right and she's actually the most boring person to listen to of all time. I've legitimately never wanted to spontaneously pass away as much as I do every single time I go to this class.

Had her for PSY 203. Amazing professor. She is very flexible when it comes to assignments, deadlines, and attendance, and has very has engaging lectures. She is very helpful and provides tons of resources for the research paper and exams.

Professor Swisher is incredibly knowledgeable about the content she teaches, however, I don't think teaching is her forte. Many of the lectures were incredibly monotone and hard to follow. The tests were also very difficult for a psychology class, with my class having an average of ~75%. Much of her content was very number heavy as well.

I have had Dr. Swisher for three semesters in a row and each time it was clear that she really cares about the topics she teaches as well as her students. Her expertise is primarily in behavior change methods so she could answer any and all questions. Always willing to meet with you after or outside of class to clear up any confusion.

I took the class online over the summer and it easy. I didn't find her as boring as the other reviews indicate. Despite being crammed into a month the class was easy to keep up with. I spent ~4 hours a day on it but didn't do anything for it over the weekend. Psych is not a topic that excites me but she managed to make it relatively interesting.

She gets more hate than deserved, I wasn't a fan of the class just not because of her personality. Her class was structured differently, a lot of the lessons were assigned as video quizzes leaving lectures to be review and application questions. The exams are in person and there is a final paper to write, the paper was easier than most.

Really nice professor, had time to meet with everyone for their project. No exams, just open note quizzes. Final project was a lot of work but not impossible and she lets you do it individually or as a group. The rubrics are always super clear and detailed.

Swisher's lectures are long and monotone, but informative. You are required to go to 10/22 lectures and complete iClicker for points. You do not have to go to the labs, as there are online alternatives. If you use her study materials and lectures for exams, you'll be completely fine (got 98,100,98). Honestly did not do much for this class
Rude and condescending, standards seem to change on a dime.
Love this professor! Will take another class. She is awesome IF you work hard!!
Difficult to understand and communicate with.

Prof. Mitchell is a great scholar but not a great teacher. She's obviously very knowledgeable in what she teaches and the lectures tend to be pretty interesting, but she isn't very good at answering questions or giving feedback. Her TA (when I took the class) was honestly more helpful. She also treats this level 100 course like a level 300+ course.
Nice enough prof, and class is interesting, but take another history class for your gen-ed. tons of work, and harsh grading

Professor Mitchell is obviously very well educated, but her course is not very well organized. This class was really interesting to me and I enjoyed most of the assignments but grading criteria and lectures are very vague and can be hard to navigate. TA office hours were very helpful in understanding grading criteria.

Her lectures were vague, disorganized, and often not related to the class work. Her grading was incredibly strict and she provided little feedback on how to improve. She seemed to be looking for specific things or ideas in our writing, yet failed to communicate what they were.

Unclear in her speaking AND her assignment requirements. Wants you to read every week, expected, but if you don't understand the text to the letter in your report, you will be penalized. Gives poor feedback, has lectures with almost NO substance, and contradicts herself often on her rubrics. Not a bad person, just a very bad teacher/professor.

This class is terrible. For a course with no prerequisites, I expected an easy, engaging history class, but the assignments barely relate to the lectures and the due dates are disorganized. The grader expects citation and background knowledge at a graduate level, which is unreasonable.

Professor Mitchell is very passionate and loves her subject however her assignments are difficult! Grading criteria is very confusing and she doesn't answer questions very well. I found the Geary book online so save yourself some money and look up the textbooks beforehand. I found myself dreading all assignments. VERY VERY WRITING HEAVY.

Silvia Mitchell really cares and has passion for this class, and it really shows. However, the class is really unorganized and often times you will be doing assignments and readings that have almost nothing to do with the lectures for the week. She also doesn't answer questions very well, she's quick to cut off your question and answer it wrong.

Taking this class for fun a free elective. Always have loved history and thought it would be a fun and easy class. Was I wrong. most strict, hardcore grader I have ever met/ Lectures are mandatory but pointless as she just shows a picture of some random map and drones on and on and on about some randomness that has nothing to do with the hw.
Not the best with assistance
I don't like her cuz u have to give very specific answers. Means including every single detail and if ur not. Then you are just wrong. And she doesn't talk with her students respectful enough.

Not one of my favorite professors. She does a good job lecturing but can be very short/rude with students when asked questions. Her class was easy and you can do well without attending lecture as long as you read the book.

Worst teacher/professor I have ever had during my time here on Earth. No disrespect to her accent, but I cannot understand half of the words she says and half of the time she just goes on and on about nothing. Her powerpoints are horrendous and are just pictures of maps that she rambles on about for 25 minutes before switching the slide. FADE HER!!

Professor Mitchell is obviously an expert in her field and an amazing resource for the historical community. However, her teaching style is nothing short of abysmal. She constantly goes off on unrelated tangents while simultaneously grilling you to answer questions in the same manner she would. Great for research help; avoid her classes.
She is not afraid to dig into you, which can sometimes be helpful. Other times it is not. She often does not know what you are asking, and classes are filled with silence.

Dr. Mitchell did a reasonable job in organizing the material for this online course, however, assignment dates would be changed because they were not opened at the correct time so scheduling was difficult. This intro-level history course was graded much harsher than any upper-level history course I have taken at Purdue.
Rude and not willing to help students that ask for it

I had the displeasure of having Professor Mitchell for SCLA 101. I'm not pursuing the Cornerstone certificate she strongly advised out of fear of having her or anyone like her. She was always reminding us that she is a historian in a WRITING class where all we talked about was the Italian Renaissance all semester. Worst class I have taken so far

Professor Mitchell assumes that everyone taking HIST103 has a substantial background in medieval history. She jumps right in without explaining anything. She's a tough grader because she looks for very specific answers, and if you don't have them you will be wrong (even on opinion questions). Lectures go on forever and are very confusing.

Not even through the first class I was shot down for everything I said. If you dont explain it EXACTLY how she wants it, even if youre right... youre wrong in her mind. I dropped out of the classes not even 30 seconds after.

Silvia is a below average teacher. When asking questions, she has one specific answer in mind. If you cannot relate to her then you are wrong. She does a poor job of understanding that not all students have the same historical background. She also just tells you to format the paper correctly, but doesn't say what the "correct" way is.

This is my third semester here at Purdue, and I haven't even gotten a grade yet in her class, but I can say without a doubt that Mrs. Mitchell is the absolute WORST professor that I have had since being here! Literally anything and everything about her as well as her teaching style is negative, I can't think of a single positive thing to say.

Calc 1 shouldn't be a weed out class. Concepts itself were easy however exams were not. On the final exam she sent a very passive agressive email about how she provided everyone with exam material. However, 300+ practice questions is a lot to go through if only 12/300 were going to be on the final exam. Wish grades weren't so test heavy.

For how hard this class is she made it simple and easy to understand. She really cares about her students and is willing to help anyone. If you want to get a good grade in her class just go to the lecture, do your homework and STUDY. IF you don't study for her test you will fail!

She was the GOAT, she cares about her students and made me feel supported through the course. I am not good at math by any means but she gives you all the resources you need. There is a lot of homework but it's great practice for the exams. She also gives practice exam questions from the past exams. If you practice, you will be fine.
Explains things well.

Loved Delgado! Took MA16010 during the summer. Had around an hour of homework w/ unlimited attempts and AI help. She cared for all students and tried to answer all questions as clearly as possible. I recommend taking class during the summer as I'm not sure what class is like during regular semester.
Our class really got to know Prof. Delgado and we all enjoyed having her as a professor.

Professor Delgado did an amazing job teaching this course over the summer. If you don't put in the work to succeed, you won't do well. everything is straightforward, so if you truly know the material, you will do well in the class. She explains things thoroughly, and if you ask questions, the professor tries her best to explain things step by step!

This class is not as hard as everybody saying it is. People who do bad in this class probably just don't study hard enough or don't do the practice problems assigned right before the exam. I did all practice problems for midterm one and just got a B while working a fulltime job and doing the class online. It's an easy class if you just put in work.

I only had her as course coordinator, but I think the course is designed for you to succeed if you use all resources. Review questions ARE SIMILAR TO THE EXAM, SOMETIMES IDENTICAL, use them. Asked for her help and she was really accommodating. Quizzes weekly, don't forget to study for them. Workload may seem heavy, but helps prepare for exams.

no quizzes in the online course. that was cool. extra credit definitely helped. the online math book was helpful towards the start of the semester and gradually worsened. there were entire hw assignments at the end where you couldn't find any of the question topics in the book. exams are okay. just do the practice exams and you're good.

You will probably have a TA teaching your section, not this professor, so make sure you end up with a good one otherwise the class will get confusing fast. Homework after every lecture with 3 drops. Weekly quizzes, but they are easy. Don't use their review questions, use BoilerExams because the questions are more accurate and are sometimes reused.

I wouldn't underestimate this class. The tests are made with questions to make you second-guess yourself. I got 100 on every quiz and then struggled on the exams. I would say whatever time you usually study or the time you think you need to study for double it. Make sure to check the professor rating on here before picking the class.

I took this class in five weeks so my experience may be significantly different. She would provide us with notes and walk us through them during the lecture. She made sure her office hours were during a time all students could attend. She provided us with over 60 exam prep questions. I would say the questions were similar to what was on the exam.

Does not help when students ask for help via email, generally directs students to "rewatch lecture video" or "check notes", the class does not allow 2 line calculators for seemingly no other reason than to annoy students, and the exams give no credit for work.

This version of applied calculus at Purdue is extremely easy to follow for someone who didn't do well in math in high school.The notes given in class are exactly like the homework, the hw and notes are exactly like the practice exams, and the practice exams are exactly like the exams. You just need to put time into understanding the material given.

Cannot understand professor, does not explain concepts well, even harder to understand and not many resources that help students. Lectures do not go over anything for the homework and the exams are hard. When exams are such a big part of the grade I would never take this professor again in my life.

She's a very intelligent person but is pretty hands-off with the class. It mostly depends on your background in math and your TA but I enjoyed the class because it was mostly a refresher. Her exams are pretty straight forward and you won't get caught off guard by new topics. Office hours and Piazza are both incredibly useful and there'll be help

Professor is a typical "math-wiz", "I expect you all to be math-wizzes like me", notes and lectures are for the most part, absolutely terrible and useless. Typical, "gives you extremely easy and simple examples on the notes", the homework and tests show a much different face. People who give her high-ratings are very likely math wizzes.

There is really no teaching involved in IMPACT. I heard she recently got promoted to "senior lecturer" with a hefty raise. I am not convinced that she deserves it. Most of her sections are taught by wonderful TAs and hard-working limited-term lecturers. They deserve more credit.

Nice lady, but the exams are horrible. I study by going through homework, working through practice exams, past exams, watching YouTube videos, etc. what Is on the exam is sooo much harder than what is taught.. its like they want us to fail. The exams are not a good representation of what you know, but exams are basically your grade, so its screwy

Has an accent, though it's not always an impediment to understanding what she is saying. Her ability to explain difficult concepts is an entirely different story, though. She has video lectures, but they are useless. She is never in her office. Hard to understand why she gets raise while excellent Graduate TAs (who teach much better) aren't.

Highly recommend going to all of her office hours, even if she is not your lecturer because you have a graduate student or whoever. I had to retake the class and going to her office hours was far more helpful than any tutor I've ever had. She is also one of the most caring professors I've ever had, she wants to help and cares about her students.

I had Professor Delgado for Impact. Since I've taken Calculus before this course, I was able to get by pretty easily. However, if you've never taken it before, you'll have to put in the effort to do well. As long as you complete the homework in a timely manner outside of class and put in the effort to learn the material you'll be fine!

The whole structure of this class is awful. I received an 88 on the last exam and the exam average was a 65% and there was no curve. She messed up several of the exams and had to throw out questions. Calculus is a very big class for Purdue and I'm ashamed to know that this lady is the face of the class.

Overall, this class is challenging, exams are short and pretty challenging. However, Professor Delgado really cares about her students. My experience with her has been nothing but good. If you go to office hours and ask for help she will set you straight. Success in this class is up to whether or not you go and ask for help when you need it or not.

Do not let a poor professor rating fool you; people do not like Tech120, not Professor Farrington. While the class itself can feel like required busy work, he is aware of this and does everything in his power to make assignments as easy and free as possible.

Gives off strict vibes at first but is definitely not too strict. Lots of homework (before class and in class assignments for every meeting) but grading is very lenient and work is more so busy work than difficult. Try to choose a good group for the 3rd project, it is many weeks long. Do a good enough job on your assignments and you will get an A.

General consensus on this guy is right - gives off bad vibes but is a decent guy and can for sure be rude. The class has a lot of work that isn't hard per se, but some assignments will take you hours. You do three projects one assigned by where you sit first class, one by major, and one by group choice and your group can make or break this class.

Shawn Farrington gets a bad rap. He 100% gives off a bad vibe, but the man himself is actually really nice. The Tech 120 class has a lot of Homework but it's all just mind-numbing busy work. So on one hand, it's super easy, but on the other hand it's really annoying and repetitive. Get ready for group projects.
Rude, lazy, and boring. I hated this class for every minute I had to spend on it. He made that somehow worse.
He is inconsiderate!!

Honestly one on one he is a nice guy and means good. His aura tho in the classroom is just not it. A lot of work in this class and will take a good amount of time outside of the classroom to complete. If you do not have a good group for the projects its really wack. Shawn just has a weird aura around him where you cannot tell if he is nice or not

Shawn would be an amazing motivational speaker. He is not explicitly clear about directions, or expectations. Even if the course work is easy you get the feeling you have to sink hours and hours into it. I recommend not doing that because even if you put in the minimum amount of effort you will probably be fine.

Agree with the general sentiment on here that he belongs in a high school classroom. I do have some sympathy for him as it seems impossible to teach this class the way it was intended and also retain any respect from your students. He will answer your questions, but sometimes vaguely and in a condescending manner. Grading is somewhat subjective.

Shawn is a terrible college teacher and belongs in a high school classroom. He lacks a sense of reality and has lectured kids for showing up late or leaving early (even when they have finished the class assignment). The entirety of the coursework is busy work. It's a waste of time class for freshman. Shawn also lies and exaggerates things.

Professor Farrington makes assignments easy to follow and complete, just make sure to look at the rubric because each assignment has one. The class requires work outside of class a few times a week at least in the honors section, and there are some reading assignments. There are 3 projects throughout the year, each one similar to the last.

Shawn is a great guy, but this course feels exceptionally useless. He does his best to make it fun and engaging, but its just not a class many care about. The group projects can be annoying, as a decent amount of work is required and your group probably won't be perfect. However, Shawn is very caring, nice, and is always there to ask questions.

Should be a high school teacher. Too much work and high expectations on dumb projects. I wouldn't have taken the class if it was not a major required. Not a bad guy, he's actually pretty nice, but the course is pretty stupid.

Shawn is not a nice guy. He is very short and rude with you. The first day he said he would not answer our questions but rather ask us questions to answer our questions. Like as an instructor you should help me out. Tech 120 is a waste of time as their is so much unnecessary homework.The grading is so hard and meticulous.

All the homework is busywork, but for some reason he thinks it is stuff that will actually help us in the future. The whole class is very useless and everyone just makes stuff up and completes the assignments last minute. The grading is also annoying, but there are enough grades so that I got an A in the class.

This is a high school class, but with lots of homework and vague rubrics which will get you points off for things you did not even know were required. When the professor is giving criticism for your work, he will get his point across that your idea is terrible but provides no assistance afterwards.

This class is literally a high school class. Complete waste of time for all parties involved. Talks way too much and only talks about the homework (which, by the way, is basically all the work in class). This is the only class I wish wasn't required because is it just abysmal.
Only speaks about the prework you were assigned. The class itself is so intuitive that you shouldn't need a professor, so surely anyone could teach it right? Nope.
What a great guy, truly a fixer of things
I look forward to tech 120 everyday. He even fixed the TV today!
He fixed our monitor in class today... #imafixerofthings
What an awesome teacher. #imafixerofthings
Hes a swell fello
I love him with all my heart

Shawn is okay at lecturing. He knows what he's talking about. TECH120 has a lot of work (that apparently has recently been cut down.) The course moves fast. Shawn can give you help, but only if you ask him. Friendly if you reach him outside of class.
She's a good lecturer, and is very supportive and willing to help with anything in class. ENGR 132 is just an annoying course
Very nice teacher. Don't be afraid to talk to her or answer her questions.
She's very nice, very understanding. Hate 132, pointless class.

As long as you show up and do the work you will pass. Beware of the first assignment. I received a D and was convinced I would fail the class, but it got easier after the first assignment. No complaints about Prof. Ferdoush. Gives plenty of class time for projects as well.

She is very clear with her grading, and was open to giving certain extensions if your reason is valid. The class was fun, and the group projects were relatively easy to handle. The amount of studio time is great. The GTA is okay, but a little pushy at times.

Firstly, I'd like to say that the reviews on here are far from reality. Professor Ferdoush is an AMAZING and CARING and LENIENT professor. That gives SO MANY BREAKS and genuinely wants to help the student succeed.

If you are looking for a prof for ENGR 131, (and probably 132; I haven't taken it yet,) do NOT hesitate; pick FERDOUSH!!

Ferdoush is a fine professor. The class itself is just absolutely useless since it's basically just a bunch of busy excel work. Some TAs can grade quite harshly, and the GTA was also annoying, often more unnecessarily strict than Ferdoush herself since she was often open to letting groups leave early.
Nice professor, GTA is devil sent, TA's not helpful. Over all class wasn't too bad.

This is by far one of the most horrible and useless classes in terms of content. The TAs grade stuff really harshly for no reason and there is a lot of useless busywork. But the professor is good and fair, she gave extra credit, lets us leave early, and is very understanding and empathetic.

The class is horrific, but that is not Prof Ferdoush's fault at all. She answers emails fast and is great at answering your questions in class. While the lectures can be boring at times, she always has a smile and seems genuinely happy to be there. The class is super easy but you may get cursed with a lazy group.

ENGR131 is unclear and disorganized, and Ferdoush doesn't help. She often seems unprepared, arrives late, struggles with her own material, and gives confusing instructions. Though she cares about students, her course was a blind leading the blind situation due to her unclear expectations and frustrating teaching mistakes.

Decent lecturer for a poorly designed class. Taught MATLAB concepts well enough to be successful on every project or assignment. Almost everything else is set up by College of Engr and graded by the TAs, makes grading criteria really unclear and structure pretty poor. Ferdoush is caring and supportive and available for help though

The class overall is structured very strangely and they don't give any guidance for the TI kits or for the first project. I think Ferdoush is a good professor overall and is usually pretty helpful if our group has questions.

Decent professor, really bad class. A whole class dedicated to Excel and learning to "do engineering." I got a decent group but I've heard many don't. A bunch of pointless busy work. At the beginning of the semester, they also grade quite harshly (seemed to get more lenient later). Professor didn't make this bad class any worse, so would recommend.
She is ok teaching.

This review is for ENGR 132. Overall, average class. There were times when I would listen to lectures and times when I would just focus on other tasks. Some assignment and exam questions felt more difficult than what it should be. Most of the time, info on the lectures would not really help you in these scenarios.
Professor Ferdoush was very helpful through out the semester.
Professor is confused on what she is talking about
Ferdoush was a very laid back and chill professor. Let us leave early after exams too. She didn't change my life but she made a bad class bearable.

ENGR 131 has many design flaws, especially the Brightspace page which is quite hard to navigate. The grading from the TAs is very inconsistent so sometimes your grade just depends on which TA you get. You can do regrade requests though. Professor Ferdoush does care but is ill-equipped to do much about the situation. She doesn't worsen it though.

Professor was not helpful throughout the semester, brightspace was super messy and non-intuitive. Tests were decently easy but grading is still very tough if you were to get a grade near the boundaries.

Engineering 131 is a poorly organized and planned class to begin with, but Ferdoush's soft spoken and often confusing lectures and lesson plans did not improve much. She did seem to genuinely care about the students but did not seem to have the experience or expertise to help them to succeed. The TA's were also not even remotely equipped.

This class itself is not set up great, which is not Professor Ferdoush's fault. However, the grading from the TAs are often inconsistent which can make submitting regrade requests frustrating. There are also three group projects which are essentially busy work, and if you get a bad team then the work in this class goes way over the two credit hours

Not necessarily her fault, but the class is a complete trainwreck. Unqualified and useless TA's, largely irrelevant lectures. Very heavy course load for a two credit class with often unclear directions. Grading is extremely tough and even more inconsistent. Exams and assignments were very dumb and you'd lose so many points for the smallest things.

Incredibly disorganized class. The TAs don't know absolutely anything, and this professor knows even less. It's very hard to pay attention in class. The professor changes instructions and never explicitly confirms it through Brightspace even though absolutely every update is in Brightspace.Don't take her, the other professors are way better.

With other professors, I could see this being a boring class, but Dr. Zulli was able to engage the class and make it interesting. There are 3 big tests and assignments, but they are all pretty chill especially since this is a 100-level course. I recommend taking a class with Dr. Zulli no matter what it is.

Dr Zulli was the best professor I have ever had. She inspired me to add a minor in Communications. Attendance is mandatory, but she makes class fun making you actually wan to attend. Her lectures are engaging and teach you a lot. She had 3 writing assignments and 3 tests. Very doable class that I really enjoyed taking!

Her lectures were so engaging, and she made them so fun and personable to listen to. She encourages the class to participate. You have to talk to others every day in this class, and she is a tough grader for her writing assignments. Also, studying and reading are important to get a good grade on the tests.

A true helicopter mom, cares far more about the class running in accordance with her arbitrary unnecessary rules than she does about any student's learning. I suggest only taking a class with prof Zulli if it's required.
really enjoyed this course, Dr. Zulli is so kind and made the material a lot of fun to learn

I enjoyed this class so much - actually decided to minor in Communication 100% because of Dr. Zulli. Her lectures are interactive and interesting, and the material is genuinely interesting to think about. Overall, this was a great course and I hope you have the chance to take it with her.
Tough grader.

I really enjoyed Dr. Zulli's lectures, she makes every class interesting despite what material she's covering. She is a fair grader and makes lectures fun. Definitely go to class, there are random participation activities and it's much easier to do in class than to do the homework.

Genuinely one of the best professors I've had at Purdue. Zulli cares about her students, has clear and relevant lectures, and while it isn't a challenging class, I've walked away learning a lot! Can't recommend Dr. Zulli enough.

Dr. Zulli was an excellent professor. This class was very useful and she made it very interesting. There are a few major papers that are worth a lot of points, but they aren't terribly hard. If you have questions go to her office hours and ask. The three exams are easy if you take good notes in class and do the assigned reading! She's great!

The teacher is great, however her TA was not. The class is easy, but contains a lot of assignments. There are lots of essays and other activities to do. She makes learning fun and the class is very interesting.
Exams online, participation activities in class for grade but she posts them online for homework for those who miss class but will make the activity way harder.

I LOVE her. She is an amazing professor and makes the class so fun. You need to show up to class and pay attention. Her exams aren't easy, but they are not overly difficult. A great professor who cares about her students and a great class.

I loved COM 253 with Dr. Zulli. The class always went by so fast, even though it was a power hour. If you go to class consistently, you will never have homework outside of the 2 papers because she does homework as in-class activities. Exams are online and open note. Great class, very informative.

Dr. Zulli is amazing. I was unsure if Public Relations was right for me before her course, and she gave me all of the tools I needed to make a confident decision about my major. She gave excellent feedback and was always very accessible. I wish I could have taken a full-length course with her rather than a Maymester!

The first class I had with her was a power hour, but she's the kind of professor that you don't mind listening to for that long. She can make boring info easy to listen to, doesn't ask for too many assignments, and grades tough but very fair. Also a fantastic human.

Excellent professor! I looked forward to class every week. Her lectures and slides were very dense and high-leveled but with her charisma and sense of humor made them fun and engaging. Loved how she would prompt class discussion, she graded hard but gave great feedback and rewards improvement, interesting assignments, all around awesome class.

Honestly, she is one of my favorite professors. Though, her discussion papers are no joke, and highly suggest starting them as soon as she assigns them. You'll thank me later. If you have a problem or circumstance in life or in class, talk to her if you need something. She is really caring about her students and wants the best for you always.
Dr. Zulli is one of the best professors I have had at Purdue. I would definitely recommend.

As a student in a technical major, Dr. Z really helped me to fulfill my com selective requirement. She was a great lecturer who made "going to class" enjoyable. I learned a lot from her lectures and even more from her feedback. She was very accessible and quick to respond with guidance. I would 10/10 recommend and would take another class of hers.
The class wasn't terribly difficult. Her exams were more in-depth than I expected, but after the first one it got easier. All of the assignments outside of the exams were fairly easy as well.
Did not like her one bit

She is by far one of the best professors at Purdue! You can tell she really enjoys what she is talking about and makes every lecture interesting. As long as you go to class, pay attention, and study the lecture slides you should get an A!

Prof. Makin does a great job teaching a very difficult course. He does a great job making lectures interactive even with such a large lecture size and teaching you things you don't understand in OH (I definitely recommend going to them), if you listen closely in lecture you will catch many stray jokes lol) make sure to apply yourself to succeed!

I did poorly on the first exam, went to professor to see how I could improve and learn from my mistakes. He told me that if he was my 2k1 professor he would have failed me, said he will fail me out of 2k2 and then he told me to drop out of college. My friend witnessed this. I dropped the class, took it with another prof - got an A easily.

Prof Makin is a knowledgeable but tough ECE professor. His exams are challenging, and learning just from lectures isn't enough imo. I had to attend office hours regularly and do homework with the TAs. However, he genuinely wants students to succeed and does his best to answer questions. It's a hard degree and this class was a needed wakeup call.

there are some students who love him, unfortunately i was not lucky enough to be one of them. online lectures and the other professor's review sessions made more sense to me. his in class quizzes were also harder and the other section. he is not super helpful outside of class. the textbook was a life saver

Underrated professor. Given that 2k2 is one of the most difficult ECE classes, he does a great job of going through the material in a comprehensible way. Also very helpful in office hours, and post-exam is willing to go over your entire exam with you. Will always take feedback from students during or post lectures and adjust accordingly.

One of the best professors I've had the privilege of learning under. Make no mistake; the course content is not necessarily easy. But Makin makes lectures feel important to attend, is very lighthearted, and conveys the content well. Exams will be difficult but it is a rewarding course and Makin is very invested in your success.

One of the best professors at Purdue ECE. Teaches the material very well and makes it intuitive. Approachable and patient; willing to answer questions outside class. If you can, take 2k2 with Professor Makin (Fall 24 - Prof Makin's section had the best averages across all the exams).

Highly recommended for anyone working in machine learning. The course is intensive with hard biweekly homeworks and content-heavy lectures. However, exams are relatively easier, course project manageable and grading lenient. Dr. Makin is hilarious, genuinely cares about students' learning, flexible about deadlines and accessible outside classes.

Feel like Makin gets a bad rap. 2k2 is a brutal class and takes a ton of outside effort to do well. Makin is willing to help you understand material, but you need to constantly put in effort to learn material/go to class. The downfalls of this course should be chalked up to the head instructor for the course and ECE administration.

Disappointed for a graduated class at Purdue. Feedback to help fellow grad students. The course title is somewhat misleading for those expecting current content. Older material and lacks depth, and hand-wavy instruction complicates understanding. Outdated homework with no discussions/solutions/sufficient office hours.
He's one of those professors that are so smart to the point that he can't explain things at a learning level. Everything is intuition based with him, and he doesn't listen much to his students.

Had him last semester. Class design was online lectures, and then in-person recitations at another time. Very inconvenient and time-consuming. And more, his lectures were very scatterbrained and very unhelpful. Class average on the final was ridiculously 38%. Serious overhaul is needed to both motivate this professor and make the class reasonable.

Professor Makin was one of the best professors I have ever had. He is very personable and easy to approach about problems. He explained things very well in both lectures and recitations and was always willing to help outside of class. You need to have a deep understanding of the material to do well on the exams, which were difficult but manageable.

Prof Makin is a good guy at heart but he is a terrible instructor. At least for 20002. He doesnt know what he is doing. Makes a lot of mistakes. His video lectures are not understandable and doesnt give any notes. Exams are terrible usually averaging around 40%. Avoid him at all costs

I think Makin is generally an average professor. There were a couple comments he made throughout the year that sort of implied that people who didn't try hard enough deserved their low grade, which I understand, but like ouch. The content being taught was difficult, but I do think he tried to help us all understand it.
Bad lectures. Very insensitive to students. Ngl I genuinely dislike him on a personal level, which is very rare for me, so props for him.

The course was very dense, but you learn a lot. Classes are packed with information, and often went overtime. Prof. Makin can answer questions well, and makes sure to read up on questions that he doesn't immediately have complete answers for. Class is fluid in that topics that are more confusing can lead to more in-class discussion. Overall good!
Class is entirely graded on test with final being 40% of your grade

Lectures can feel pretty slow and boring, especially when they are typically early in the morning. The lab, however, is pretty enjoyable as projects can be done in large groups. Most lab projects do not take the full lab time slot, and you are encouraged to ask questions while messing with projects.

Very lecture heavy, sometimes boring but I thought he was nice and he was honest. Had some interesting stories about his time in the service.  His quizzes could sometimes be confusingly worded, but the exams seemed to be better. Quizzes & exams are take home, online. Essentially your entire grade is attendance + quizzes and exams.
He focuses on lectures and the homework can add up if not prepared. Overall loves his work but can talk a lot. Not an issue for me as I listen.

I may be biased, but he is one of my favorite teachers in SATT. He is very caring, quite knowledgeable, and is honest when he doesn't know the answer to something. Lecture can be not the most interesting thing in the world, but lab is definitely more fun and he is always happy to help.

His lectures are not the most interesting but he makes it better with his stories and funny remarks. He cares very deeply about what he is teaching and is very fun to talk to in lab. Lecture is boring but the lab is fun. He's also kind and understanding.

Good professor. I don't understand why most people don't like him. He's pretty laid back on attendance and giving grades. If you mess up, you get the grade that you deserve, and if you do good you get a better grade.
He reads off of pre-posted slides for 1hr 15mins, and his "homework" (quizzes) are confusingly worded. He has great stories about his time in the industry, but many do not pertain to the course.

Lectures are boring and the 8:30 time is painful, but not his fault. Reads off slides uploaded to brightspace, quizzes are easy if you know where to look, but the exams are harder. Exams are take-home and online. Attendance is mandatory bc of the FAA requirement for A&P students, which is sad bc I'm management and don't really need the class.

Classes are lecture-heavy, most likely to put you to sleep. Occasionally, there is interesting content and stories about his military past. Homework and quizzes are straightforward multiple-choice. Exams are online multiple choice with the questions being pulled straight from quizzes.

He's alright. However the course structure has so many problems combined with his teaching style. He reads off the slides but you can basically learn everything off them pretty easy. But there is the FAA mandatory attendance policy which makes this class a pain in the ass at 8:30 in the morning.
Quizzes are worded poorly, lectures will put you to sleep.

His lectures would put you to sleep and attendance was mandatory. He would read off pre uploaded slides for a hour and 15 min straight. His quizzes are very confusing, questions would sometimes have  2 correct answers and would only give credit for one. His exams are very hard and gives no study guide or any direction of what to study.

I would not recommend. His lectures put you to sleep and you really do not need to pay attention at all because the lecture slides are posted. If you know anything about Aviation then the first month of class will be boring and repetitave and overall he does not understand email etiquette and is kind of sassy.

Also took AT 116 with him. My highlight during his classes is when he talked about interesting events in aviation history before each lecture. He also refers to his air force service. That was entertaining, but the lectures themselves are very boring and he mostly reads off the slides. Class itself is easy and labs are fun.
Very good Professor for 207. If you finish the assignments and homeworks on time then you can get an A on the class. Lectures are Interesting and labs too.
7:30 class and boring lectures where he just reads off slides. Unorganized class on top of that. Great guy outside of academia.
AT207 was a very easy class. The labs actually help with the exams and the class is overall pretty fun.
Tests are in class, but they were open note this year. 3 tests and 3 quizzes for the semester. But he gives extra credit. Easy class but all lectures.

AT106 was a breeze, come to class take notes, all quizzes and tests were open note, and were word for word the notes. Fall 2021 was his first semester teaching at Purdue, so he is still finding his footing. The topics were vaguely interesting, and a passing aviation knowledge gives you an A. He is willing to answer any questions and is very nice.
Painfully boring class. Not sure what we were learning most of the time and still can't tell you the point of the class.
Would not recommend unless you want to fall asleep in class!
Do not take this class or professor unless you have to. Way too much work for a 200 level class and her lectures and readings are painful.
The lectures were painfully boring and the assignments were unclear. The material we learned loosely connected to the projects and once again it felt very disorganized.

Mind-numbing. Most of the class is collaborative. Grades tougher on reading reflections. Attendance counts for points, participation in class not mandatory. She thinks she's better than everyone because she has a PhD, she even called us illiterate the first day of class. Soooo much reading.

by far one of the most passionate professors i've seen. she really loves what she does as an anthropologist. teaches things very well, fosters a great classroom environment, and it's easy to get an A in the class if you just do your part in the group project. had one open-book exam and it was fairly easy as well, nothing to be worried about.

The professor is nice and she really respects her students. Attendance is 15% of your grade and lectures are pretty boring. The TA's are a little picky and tough graders when it comes to the weekly readings but easy to complete. Make sure to pick a good group since there is a final group presentation and paper that are worth a lot of points.

Attendance is worth 15% of your grade, but attending it is borderline painful. It's boring, uninteresting, and a waste of time. We had 4 major assignments: an essay, a midterm, a group project, and a group essay. All 4 were based on a road that holds almost no significance to life on campus and had nothing to do with what we learned in class.
You have to read a lot for this class and the group work is a pain because she didn't give enough time but was open to change things in the future. She really respects her stufents.

She was not in class for so long yet still takes attendance, and they still use the attendance sheet for such a big class. At the beginning of the semester, the TA didn't tell us where to sign after the class if we didn't get the sheet. Students just pass it around the whole room. Group projects are hard and long (25-30 pages). Don't take it.
She is a tough grader and doesn't want to communicate with her students

I HATE THIS CLASS.
the professor was sick throughout the whole semester for about one month (I know this is not technically her fault but I'm still mad af)!!! The group projects weigh a lot of points, and for a class of over 100 ppl she still uses paper attendance sheets. Grading is super slow, lot of reading before mid-term. JUST RUN.

This class is all busywork and she expects essentially grad-student level work from students which is pretty ridiculous considering the class is just an elective for a majority of the class. She also wasn't in class for 1/2 the semester but still took attendance for points. Her and her TA also don't respond to emails! Don't take.

Dr. Nyssa is hands down my favorite professor I've ever had. Her Technology and Culture class was extremely interesting and I enjoyed every lecture she gave. The assignments correlated not only with the curriculum but with current events ongoing in society. She has a real heart for her students and her passion for teaching transmits to her students

I literally love him! He is super accessible outside of class and actually cares about his students. He wants you to succeed in this class. The outline of his grading is super clear and will give you some leniency. I found that his exams and quizzes are a bit more difficult than the other professors', but going to his office hours will help a lot
Dan truly is the man.

Dan truly takes the edge off a class as difficult as Organic Chemistry, and it's very clear in lectures and outside of class how much he cares about teaching. He approaches the class with an attitude of wanting us to learn Orgo, rather than wanting the course to be an obstacle to our future goals or career path, and it shows.
Amazing, amazing, amazing man. He makes the material make so much sense during lecture and he is so accessible outside the classroom. I would love for him to teach all my pharm classes.
Awesome professor, definitely puts student learning first. Wanted to get to know us outside of class time, which was pretty cool. Made lectures very clear, easy to understand, and fun.

Dr. Flaherty really cares about your learning. He is very accessible during office hours and is able to explain things in a way that makes sense. He has helped me with advice about graduate school as well! Overall, an amazing professor and I learned a lot in his class. He will also give partial credit on test.

Dan is the type of professor that excites you to go to class and be more eager to learn organic chemistry. He genuinely cares about his students and wants them all to succeed. A breath of fresh air in the Purdue community, had a wonderful experience in his class. 10/10 for sure.
Dan is the man.
He is a good professor and he is very motivated about organic chemistry and he is also very passionate about teaching. Love him, maybe his exams can be a little more challenging.

Dr. Flaherty is easily one of the best professors I have ever had. This class is definitely a challenge, but it is worth it. He is basically a dad and really wants all his students to succeed. If you're ever struggling, just go to office hours. He is more than willing to help any student who needs it.
The best, sad he retired
So sad that I was in his last semester of teaching. The guy was the best I've ever had.

Professor Malone demonstrates obvious care through his teaching. He emphasizes discussion in his class. The exams are fairly easy, as he pulls questions from a study guide and his lectures. Speeches and presentations are fairly graded. Prepare to rent a couple of books.

Professor Malone is very kind and cares about his students. His class is relatively easy but I found it to still be informative. The books we read were interesting and I never was bored during his lectures. This class is heavy on participation, but there are also tests/assignments. I would recommend taking a class with Professor Malone!
Easily my favorite professor I've had at Purdue. Makes class interesting and engaging. Would 100% take again, highly recommend. Malone is the best!
I love him. Is a cool person all around

I would highly recommend taking SCLA-102 with Dr. Malone. He is by far the best prof. I've had this semester. You read 3 to 4 books up until November and discuss them and then give two speeches, one on a personal topic and the other on a persuasive topic. Heavy on participation, but there's a lot of opportunities to speak up. Easy class, really fun

Professor Malone was an amazing professor for SCLA 102. I thoroughly enjoyed the class and learned a lot about the lecture topics, and he tried his best(and succeeded) to make funny jokes and appeal to us. For the presentation part of the course, we have a lot of freedom for the topics you choose to present and he gives great constructive feedback!
One of my favorite teachers I have had in my scholastic career. You will enjoy this class!
Most enjoyable class I have taken at Purdue. Malones lectures are always easy to follow and I enjoyed the discussions at the end of every class, I felt everyone was able to be themselves.
She isn't a good professor, doesn't teach very well. Don't like her at all
Professor does not care about the class. She does not give you feedback. I took this course when she was doing a masters program somewhere else. She didnt care.

She is very disorganized. She is not very good at teaching but but she cares about students. She gave A LOT of extra credit for students to succeed. The content is very heavy, and doesnt explain very well. The quality of her online lectures werent good also. Very nice person but not very good at teaching.

It's true, she truly is simply unorganized. Often came to class with some type of game plan but often got visibly frustrated when things went off of it in the slightest. However, her intentions as a teacher are pure and she does help out a lot during office hours. Attendance technically isn't mandatory, but random attendance checks are a thing.
I was completely comfortable with this course structure. There was a good balance of background and applicability in the statistical information presented.

She's not very organized in class. She gets increasingly frustrated as lecture goes on because we haven't learned it immediately. It feels as though she blames us for her shortcomings. Also, she started taking attendance because she was upset that nobody was attending lecture. Homework is difficult, lots of typos in it and lecture notes.

I had her for her first semester at Purdue. I will admit that she seemed unorganized at times and changed the course a few times in the middle of the semester. Aside from that, she is a good professor who wants students to learn. Offered a lot of extra credit and graded homework on completion. Exams were tough but not impossible.

It is hard to follow her classes, as she doesn't teach anything in class. She gives papers to read and we have to read everything ourselves.   It would be better if she explains some concepts.  She doesn't grade anything instead does peer evaluation. Better to take an online course than getting into frustration after taking this class.

I enjoyed this class. Lecture is a fun, collaborative environment heavily steered by class participation. Grading is a little tougher than you may expect from a humanities class at Purdue but it's fair and the feedback is thorough.
English king von

Prof. Davis' teachings never follow any straightforward path. He often spends tens of minutes on tangents. Attendance is not taken, participation is sometimes tracked (seemingly randomly).

He is a very nice person, and he genuinely cares about his students. He gives good feedback on your performance.

The class is graded by "attendance and participation" points, but he doesn't take attendance and only grades you based on how much you speak in class. On top of that, half of the class is typically used for speeches or him talking about random experiences in his past so there is not much time to get those participation points. Tough grader as well.

Huge part of our grade was participation but he inconsistently marks you down for it. Rambles about unrelated things and turns the class into a philosophy class. He also has a condescending tone and looks down on you if he doesn't agree with what you say. Constantly praises his structure of the class when there is no clear structure to begin with.

He spends most classes talking, not allowing many students to participate even though it is a large part of the grade. He is very full of himself, and grades very hard for an intro level class. If you get him you should switch out, as other professors in this course are much better.

Overall a fine lecturer. He previously was an upper level Philo professor at UNC and as a result believes that he should throw the book at every undergrad attempting to pass his class. He does not provide rubrics for work or feedback other than scribbles that even he admits are "needing translation". I would strongly urge avoiding his class.

I'll note it's his first year teaching at Purdue. We were graded on few assignments and only one rubric was posted. Grades were not posted until mid December. I feel as if my speech improvement was not recognized or reflected in my grades. Each class felt like a battle against to get my participation points because he calls on the same students.

Don't take his courses, or switch professors. He has no consistency in his grading, and used to be a high level philosophy professor at his old school so he's incredible difficult and a tough grader. He heavily encourages office hours, but he low-key deducts your grades if you don't go.
The lab for AT208 can be super fun, but the lectures are a bit of a pain to sit through sometimes. Exams and homework are pretty fair, and she provides study guides, which is nice.

She really cares about her students, as long as you complete the labs on time and keep up on homework you will be fine. She keeps lectures interesting with real world examples, and offers extra lab time to anyone who needs it at the end of the semester.

Her lecture material is not super engaging but she tries to make it interesting by passing around real examples, quite helpful to keep you locked in. She cares about what she's teaching and is understanding and reasonable. Good in lab and you can tell she really knows what she is talking about. Tests are hard but manageable.

she does not care about this class at all. she frequently cancels classes and I'm 90% certain she doesn't even look at the assignments she grades. She brags about her industry experience despite the fact she's had very little and often teaches wrong information. She will not correct her mistakes

The professor is horrible about communicating to students when classes are canceled. I've had multiple occasions where I commute 30 minutes to class, just to see that she canceled lecture or moved it to webex 30 minutes before class starts. Beware of going to class just to see an email saying that class is canceled after you have already arrived.
Professor Habig is great. I would love to have her as a professor again.

Professor Habig was a great professor that really cared about her students. She made personal connections and made sure to check in with students on their progress often. The class did demand a lot, but she presented it in a way that was approachable and offered solutions. Labs were fun!

She's a really great professor. I think that she does a great job demonstrating how to do the projects for the lab and her textbook for lecture is super helpful. She clearly loves what she does and I would absolutely take her class again.
