Salgado is the best geotechnical professor in the nation. He teaches the fall semester so make sure to take it then as it is clearly the better time for geotech. I felt well prepared going into Geotech 2 and it would have been a dream if he taught that as well
Hands down the best geotechnical professor.
Inspirational and caring
He is an awesome Prof ! Fully prepared for class and the projects are useful. I can totally see how I can use what I learn from the class in my future job!

Salgado knows his stuff, but doesn't explain it particularly well. He spent far too long on some concepts and not nearly enough on others. He had us use the book he wrote, which was not very good. I design foundations in my job on occasion and I don't use anything from this class. I would only suggest taking it if you want to be a Geotechnical Eng.

Decent lecturer, but nothing exciting. His homeworks can be very challenging. But his exams and lab assignments are very easy if you utilize your crib sheets well. Makes you understand things on a conceptual level. Looking back, it was a good class.
I have him right now and he definitely seems like a smart guy who knows his stuff, but he's not a very good lecturer at all.

do not take this class. crib sheet was nice for exams but so much information made exams feel impossible, no matter how far i started studying in advance. probably the hardest class ive taken at purdue. go to class because there are iclickers for grade every time and start right when class starts
spiritual cell-level hypnotist.

Lecture goes fast. Pay attention to both what he says and writes. Homework is tricky (Weekly ~25 problems upwards of 70 total parts). There is supplemental reading but isn't "required". Exam was VERY computationally heavy (similar to the homework), but the multiple choice nature of it makes it relatively nicer. 1 crib sheet allowed on the exam.
If you are forced to take it, I'm sorry. If not, RUN!!!

This is the worst professor in my life. He with strong accent speaks so fast, it's impossible to understand. Once a weekly assignment had nearly 100 questions. At exam, I almost laughed, if you were to actually compute the calculation problems step by step, it would take 40+ minutes, as if deriving everything from ancient times to the AI era.

He was kind, and cared a lot about every student. There was an online platform used for homework/textbook/exams, and he reviewed the material from the lesson in class. He planned interactive games to keep students involved. Did have a few group projects throughout the semester, but they were pretty easy.

I took 201 with him, but it's not listed. He was the best teacher I have ever had here. He is so caring towards his students, and he wanted the best for all of us. We used an online platform for homework/textbook lessons, and would review the material in class the day after it was due. There were some "group projects", but were not difficult.

An ABSOLUTE sweetheart, so kind and really did care about the students. He was not always super clear about what was wanted, but very approachable for questions. PLEASE take this instructor, we played a ton of learning games and he always kept it fun. I LOVE YOU PARRA SANTIAGO!!!
Absolutely loved this professor; could not say enough nice things about him. If you ask for help he will absolutely give it. I would love to have him again. Highly recommend!

Santiago Parra is a super fun professor! Santiago makes sure to keep class fun and energetic, we frequently played games and had an overall fun classroom atmosphere. I only took Spanish because it was required and hated my last few semesters. But I learned way more and had more fun this semester thanks to Santiago, and my grades reflect that too.

Santiago cares about his students and you can tell. If you ask for help, he will do as much as he can to help you. He always tries to include something fun in class even if the material is boring. If you have him, you're lucky! He was always funny and overall a 10/10!
I love Santiago. Hes awesome. Will always help you with anything if you dont understand.

Some pros, some cons. Participation is graded subjectively, and students who don't speak up may struggle. The exams had multiple answers that seemed correct, making some feel like 50/50 guesses. The short-answer section was graded more strictly than implied during the review session. I would not recommend.

Some pros, some cons. Participation is graded subjectively, and students who don't speak up may struggle. The exams had multiple answers that seemed correct, making some feel like 50/50 guesses, possibly to meet curve requirements. The short-answer section was graded more strictly than implied during the review session. I would not recommend.
Professor Klapper's course is highly enjoyable. Paying attention in class is rewarding as he conveys the material very well through real life examples.

The GOAT. Such a fun professor. Made strategic management super interesting.Added so much personality to his lectures, so class was always lots of fun. Most of your grade is attendance, participation, and weekly quizzes. Show up to class, participate, and understand the material and you'll do just fine.
A chill professor who wants his students to succeed. The material was very interesting, and his exams were very fair. Read the case studies to do well on the quizzes. Funny professor, too.

cares about his students & creates a learning environment where we want to participate. He does not lecture at us but incorporates discussion. His lectures are interesting and relatable to current times. He picks interesting case studies and will bring in chocolate for interaction! One of my favorite professors at Purdue!

He is a great, charismatic, professor, truly cares for your education. There are groups for case studies and a project. Read the case studies to do well on the quizzes. Attendance isn't technically required, but weekly quizzes and participation makes it. Review the slides and textbook to do well on quizzes and tests. Pretty fun class overall.
He is such a nice guy and really cares. The class was very disorganized and was very frustrating.

One of the most brilliant and kind professors I've had at Purdue so far. Infinitely accessible outside of class, extremely helpful lectures and office hours, cool and relevant assignments/projects (no exams), and in general super friendly and very knowledgeable. He will push you to learn but in a good way. Take his class - I loved it. 10/10

My favorite professor so far! Teaches each topic with passion. He makes difficult concepts manageable. Very accessible and will go through great lengths to ensure that you have the right resources to succeed. His written exam is a fun one to do, although challenging. Learnt a lot from his class! I eventually use a lot of it during my internship.

Will is ridiculously nice and has infinite patience. He's one of those profs whose top priority is students' understanding. He made calculus & stats topics applicable to the real world. Assignments are directly related to class content and he doesn't try to trick you. For SP21, he opted to make the 2 exams into short papers about academic articles.

Can be demeaning when responding to in class questions. Stated how good exams have averages of 60-70 percent and proceeds to consistently have averages below that. Review materials she provides are almost all not helpful. Made this class way harder than it needed to be.

She does a solid job of covering material and working problems, but there were instances where exam questions were written in a confusing way or tested topics in contexts that had not been covered in class. Overall, I would take another one of her classes, but I would go in expecting to know the exams could be non-straightforward.

I did not like how she did not post all her lectures and was not good at answering my questions. I don't know how a proffessor can be so busy when the TA do most of the work. When I asked her format of exam a few days before she says she doesn't know cause she hadn't made it. She is slow at responding to emails and says I asked her last minute.

I had Professor Hebner along with Professor Morgan, both were quite poor lecturers. They both provide very vague lectures that are at best mildly helpful. Professor Hebner once admitted not even looking at the practice exam posted. Her office hours are not very helpful as she will return questions asked with her own questions. Avoid if possible.
He was definitely a tough grader, but he also gives a lot of good feedback. The class is more about the research than the design. Even if you are not in ux make sure you contribute to you group.

Paul is a cool guy. I've heard that this learning studio is harder than most. We had a great TA. If you are UX, make some good UX friends. If you're not UX, PLEASE CONTRIBUTE TO YOUR PROJECTS. Show up to class and get your projects done earlier rather than later.

Paul was an amazing teacher. He was very knowledgable about the world of UX Design and a great professor to look up to. There were 3 main projects for the course, and they are all group work. Groups were assigned but he took peer evaluations into account and let us choose our team for the last project. Would take again!

Paul Thomas isn't nearly as bad as many people make him out to be, at least from my experience. He puts in an effort to make class interesting once in a while, but his lectures can get boring sometimes. The exams aren't worth much and they are just basic definitions. The group project is graded pretty harshly though.

Paul Thomas gets more hate than is deserved. I think most of this is because the content of his class is dull and would be boring no matter who taught it. The worst part are his own weird rules "to raise the standard" or whatever that are annoying and pedantic at times, but at least they are all clearly listed in his lengthy syllabus.

The professor uses a self created grading scale tied to attendance (to be different), which is taken randomly often on days students are less likely to attend (e.g., before Thanksgiving break). He IS passive aggressive!! The class itself is easy and feels more like a freshman course than a senior one. He tries to make it more important than it is.
Good luck for the rest of the major cuz the profs only get worse from here.
This "professor" assigns data entry as homework in a 400-level class. His classes offer about the same level of insight as an AI-generated LinkedIn chart.

The professor is funny but sometimes comes off as a little snarky if you read him wrong. As for the class, the grading scale is a bit ridiculous, with a 93% being the threshold for an A. The exams for the course have a high impact on the final grade, as well as the group project, which the TAs grade inconsistently with what's taught in the lab.
Whenever he is teaching, it looks like he does not wanna be there. Class is chill and labs are also chill.

Bad professor, hates his job and does not want to be there. Is very snarky and doesn't feel like answering students questions, semester long group project that is graded harshly and the trophy is awarded at random with no rhyme or reason to who gets it, would avoid at all costs.

Prof Thomas is too funny. Some days, he's super mean-spirited and passive-aggressive, while other days, he randomly starts rapping the lesson. Overall, class is not too hard, but the fact that a 93 is a B is insane. Be aware, the group project is graded pretty harshly. The exams are not too bad, just memorize the terms and acronyms in the textbook.

Paul Thomas was not a bad lecturer per se but his passive aggressive tone towards students and abnormal grading scale (94 being an A, and 93 being a B) is simply diabolical. If it weren't for the semester long group project, the class could warrant the grading scale but the project alone could drop you a grade level. No office hours too.

In what class is the cutoff for an A a 94? In what class do you get a fraudulent, passive-aggressive lecturer? Doesn't sound like he's enthusiastic about teaching. Maybe him leaving industry after only 3 years wasn't his decision I don't know! I cheer him on 100% to go back into industry and out of our curriculum. Avoid at all costs!

I did not have a good semester with Paul Thomas. He was constantly passive aggressive, even to the students that tried their hardest to participate in his boring lectures. As the year went on, I was getting a feeling that he hates his job. For the content taught in this class, a 93 should not be a B. All in all, a horrible professor.
We see the fake ratemyprofessor reviews and profiles that you're making paul.

What did past students do to Paul that makes him so passive aggressive and one of the worst graders I've ever had?? Dude cared more about going viral on insta reels and bad raps than actually teaching the material. 93% is a B, are you kidding me? Grades like this will be the most important class you'll take but teaches like he doesn't even care.

I have never seen a situation where you can get a 93.4 and end up with a B. He is passive-aggressive and acts as if everyone around him is incompetent. He'll gloss over lecture slides, acting as if they are unimportant, but they will be on the exam, which was moved to in-person because someone from an unrelated class cheated.

To get a A is a 94, I'd recommend taking this course over the summer. He has students who suck him off in class to get benefits no one else has. He seems like he hates his job and grades like its an electrical engineering class when its a class I could teach with my eyes closed. I have no clue how he got a PHD degree.
B=93 :) If u get 93 before exam 2, u can skip it cuz 84=93=B

It's supposed to be an easy class, and it was before, but I have no idea what he has experienced to make the course so terrible now. Nonsense grading, very tough grader, 93% and B, and you only get attendance 3 times a semester, which adds up to 5%, meaning you lose an A directly after you didn't arrive before class. Second midterm is in-person!!!!

I think I can teach this course, as I just read the slides, do attendance, give class activities, and hold exams. I don't know how he got a Ph.D. degree. I cannot imagine that the grading rubric is given by a normal human-being, as 93.49 is a B.

Comes to class with a pessimistic attitude. Vague instructions on the assignments. They don't foster an environment where I want to be engaged. They do the bare minimum in presenting course materials, and give exams that don't include half of what was presented. A 93 is considered a B, when the course's difficulty does not warrant that.

Not the worst professor I've had when it comes to lecturing, but that's the only thing he's got going for him. Have a question for him? Expect him to roll his eyes at you, ignore your email, or just belittle you. His grading system is pretty stupid, and he just never acts like he wants to be there.

93% = B.
Yeah, nope.

Did not have the best experience with Prof. Thomas. He is super passive aggressive when you talk to him and seems to hate his job. There were two times I got docked a lot of points on coursework for small stuff and his graders aren't helpful. The class is manageable, but make sure to READ THE DIRECTIONS for assignments to avoid dealing with him.

Paul needs to get out of teaching, he clearly hates his job and blames the students for it. This class shouldn't be a required credit in the first place. He has no idea how to manage a large class and will blame students for his crappy system for getting attendance.
Dude just needs to quit his job since all he does is complain. He has a massive ego, lil bro thinks he should be the president of the world and everyone else is incompetent.

Very nice professor who wants you to succeed. The class consists of 4 major assignments, 2 of which are in a group. Every day, you will receive a score out of 10 for participation and attendance. I think raising your hands 3-4 times will get you 10/10. 95% of the class is just discussing the text assigned for that day, and it can get pretty boring.

don't be fooled by other comments. the people saying anything bad about her must just hate their lives. she was nothing but positive, supportive, and gave thorough feedback whenever you asked her to. i hate purdue lectures, but her's were actually thought-provoking and genuinely interesting. she is kind, funny, and isn't even a harsh grader.
your grade depends on your group. That is the gist of Prof Frketich.

Overall, this class is taught by the sweetest kindest most joyful professor in this god forsaken, freezing, featureless, hell hole. I have read some of the other reviews and yes, it is hard to get an A in a college course. But overall, she's pretty generous when it comes to grading especially compared to any class with math in it.
Very cool and passionate about what she teaches.

Nice person. She bases so much of work on group work when it should be more individual, and is extremely unwilling to change on certain things. poor feedback, doesn't really help you get a good grade. I could teach this class a LOT better than her.

The class itself is not bad and many of my friends have taken the same class with different professors. One thing that I didn't like was that she was very unwavering in her grading. Many friends who deserved A's ended up with A-'s and B+'s. One assignment can majorly screw up your overall grade. My advice is doing the extra credit and participating
she gives a horrible final that no one i know is able to finish

I don't appreciate that like so much of your grade is group work, and she tends to be kind of unmoving on certain things. Would not recommend, if it was just individual assignments would have an A, not an A- in the class.
Grading seems random and accompanying comments are not clear.

Professor Frketich shows genuine interest in her students' lives and well-being, and by the second week she had learned everyone's names. She leaves great feedback on assignments, will help you in-class, and wants to see you succeed! So grateful I took this course with her!
She's a harsh grader for some and an easy grader for others. These other ratings are capping.

Do not take a class with her. She is extremely unfair when it comes to assignment grading and your grade for each class is determined if she decides to call on you or not in a room full of students all trying to get their participation points for the day. Overall awful teacher would not recommend.

Dr. Frketich is very kind, understanding, and takes joy in what she teaches. Her assignments are minimal work (if you're moderately okay with essays) and usually pretty interactive if they're not essays (although I had to do a group essay). Attendance is mandatory, but she makes an effort to get to know her students and is generous with grading.
love her. great person to discuss with, very empathetic and kind

I took her class as a requirement for FYE and I've got to say she made her classes one of the most enjoyable parts of my semester. Almost everything is participation, we had philosophical readings and just had to read one book. Most projects were in teams and she gave us plenty of time and resources to prepare. Take her class, you won't regret it.

Great prof and super easy to reach outside of class. A couple group papers+project but they're very easy if you have a good group. She doesn't like tech in her class and you have to print things out for class (I spent most of my printing credits on her class alone) but overall very chill and easy

Professor Frketich is very kind and dedicated to her students! She makes the effort to learn everyone's name and is ready to offer help to anyone who asks. As an engineering student who often tends to gravitate away from the humanities, her class was by far the best English course I've ever taken.

Professor Frketich is an amazing professor! All readings bar one whole book you read are provided to you through PDFs. If you show up, do the readings, and talk once or twice a class you'll get an A. 3 papers througout the semester, 2 group, 1 solo. She gives great feedback and grades fairly.
She is very easy to communicate with and the work is not difficult.
Professor Frketich is a chill teacher, she helps and is cool
Great teacher
Highly motivated professor, really fun classes!

I think that she is one of the nicest people at Purdue. She cares for her classes and is super creative in her teaching style. She is a very fair and understanding professor who will be open to communication on anything related to the course. Get ready for field trips and maybe a play performance.

Frketich is a unique teacher, she seems creative. 20% of your grade is attendance AND participation. I feel she's a pretty good teacher because she likes to include the students when making rubrics, the class does not feel like a 50 minute lecture. Only downside is that she may call on you to read aloud or answer a question. A couple field trips.

Super chill professor, and the class is structured in a way that allows you to work at your own pace, wherever and whenever you want. Only annoying part was the 2 500+ word discussion posts we had to write, but honestly, the rest of the class is so chill I don't mind it.
The lectures are generally good and easy to follow. There are four online exams, with one exam dropped. The homework is long but manageable as long as you don't leave it all until the last minute.

Dr. Moss is very passionate about this course and allows for you to engage as much (or as little) as you want in this course and still perform as you desire. For anyone who is going to take this class not knowing what they are getting into, trust me its easier than you think.

Dr. Moss is honestly amazing. I had taken AP Macro in HS, and so much of her content was review. The homework is due twice in the semester, and every exam is open book open note with two attempts. It is VERY hard to do bad in her class, and she works with every student if you ever have any questions.

Probably easier than any class I took in high school tbh. Only went to lecture like for the first month and was just fine and the professor cancelled class frequently anyway. Did not even have to take the 4th exam or do the last group discussion and still finished with an A.
didn't go to a single lecture after the first month and i still got an A+. best class ever. everything is open book
Believe me, guys, if you need this for a credit, take this class. Trust me.

Dr. Moss truly cares about student learning and is passionate about what she teaches. She has many ways to explain topics and provides a lot of practice and in-lecture questions to go over concepts. She hosts Q&As before every exam and answers the most pressing questions. Lectures are long but she keeps them interesting and uses real-world examples

Dr. Moss is very kind and was extremely nice and conversational since the first day of class. You can tell she actually cares about her students and her subjects she teaches. I would 100 percent take a class with her again.

Love her! Her lectures are always very engaging and she genuinely seems to care about her student's success. I took this as an online class, and I love the flexibility that is built into the course with the due dates.

She overall just seems very personable and is always happy to answer questions or explain something further.

Take this class!

Very organized, smart, overall amazing professor. Made things incredibly easy to understand and always took the time to hear back from students and made sure they were also understanding. Exams were really easy , two tries per exam, and all exams were online. Great class

Dr. Moss is one of the easiest and chillest professors I've had. She makes the lectures very engaging and easy to understand. Exams are online, and the HW is fairly easy. The lowest exam is dropped as well. I'd happily take this class again.

Genuinely the easiest, nicest professor I've ever taken. I could have sat on my toilet and taken this class and still gotten an A+. If you are even thinking about taking a class from Ms. Moss, do it, it will be the least work and most happy you will ever be.
In my opinion, one of the best professors at Purdue, she explains things thoroughly and make sure you're never behind and she makes people participate in classes, and make example of everything

Took as an online class so I was able to go at my own pace, but I could really tell that she cares for her students thru the lecture videos and also with announcements. Be ready to lock in especially if you slack off for some weeks, but she was always responsive to my emails at a great time, so everything works out with her :)
A great professor who genuinely cares about her students. She is remarkably quick in responding to emails and is always ready to help. Highly recommend taking her course.
Dr. Moss is awesome and she does an excellent job at teaching. I would recommend taking this class if you get the opportunity.
Wonderful professor! She always tries to help her students during lectures and office hours, and I respect that. The course itself is pretty easy too. Definitely recommend.

Dr. Moss made class interesting and understandable. There is an online section of this class too. There are two major homework deadlines, so I recommend to try and keep on pace with the guided syllabus. Review sessions are helpful before exams too! She is the best professor for this course.

This class is so manageable, and it helps that Dr. Moss was an amazing professor. Online open book exams and homework is doable. Dr. Moss is SUPER personable and makes lectures really entertaining, even though they are 75 minutes, which can get long. This class will not be one of your stressful ones, and I often enjoyed the energy in the lecture.

Great Professor, she explained the entire course on the Syllabus very clearly which is all you need to follow for AGEC 217. The Learning Curves, Homework, and Tests are very doable and are meant for your learning. I enjoyed the Distance Learning Style of the course as I could go at my own pace and made it a stress free class.
Amazing professor!

It's only our first lecture and I really love this professor. She is so enjoyable to listen to and that makes learning so much more easy and unstressful. It is like a breath of fresh air compared to my science professors. (I am a science major) I may update this at the end of the year but so far this feels like a break from the dreadful usual.
The class was really easy, all the work is online and straightforward. Wasn't a big fan of the the instructor but I read the textbook and it was fine.
Cares a lot about her students. Responds to emails quickly. Always answers questions. Two weekly homework assignments

All tests/quizzes open ended with partial credit; but very easy to mess up. Only complaints she makes the class sound easier than it is on the first day and sometimes does not explain context well/will just skip to the math. Her math is very good and clear though and she overall is a very good and caring professor. She makes it very fun/inclusive.

Professor Ray in an exceptional professor, she is good with explaining concepts and if you have questions she is always willing to help out during office hours. MA 373 is a challenging course but with practice and repetition the content is manageable. Always go to lecture, as some have said, notes are not posted online.

Professor Ray is one of the best professors at Purdue. Not only does she create notes for students to copy during class, she is also easily accessible outside of class if there are any questions. The exams are challenging, but she does her best to prepare us.

Professor Ray is the best professor I have had at Purdue. Her lectures are engaging, and she takes the time to get to know every student. The content is hard, but she explains it in a way to simplify it and make it more clear. Very accessible by email, generally responds that night. Homework heavy, but always there to help.

Amazing professor! She truly cares about the wellbeing/success of each student and knows everyone by name. The material can be difficult, but a strong background in MA373 is helpful and attending lectures is necessary. She want students to understand the material and is always willing to answer questions. I wish I could take more classes with her!

Professor Ray is the best. This class is an absolute grind with all the assignments but that is what you should expect taking a 4 credit math/stat class. The class is difficult but without professor ray teaching, it would be even more difficult. Could not recommend enough.

She always brings a smile to lecture and her classes are light-hearted. Her lecture material is not posted anywhere so get someone's phone number if you are going to miss. The class is decently challenging, but her class website and the platform the homework is on are good at explaining problems. So nice with emails and one on one meetings.

Completely disregard that last comment that's simply untrue. Professor Ray is a wonderful teacher in the Actuarial Science department. I didnt do as well as I wanted to on the 373 midterms, but I did well on the final to get UEC credit. I could not have done it without her help in office hours, where she was more than happy to reexplain topics.

She unfortunately is the only professor for this, but this class was awful. Probably top 5 hardest class at Purdue and she is so test heavy for UEC and she makes the midterm and final as difficult as possible. Do not recommend but you're forced with her anyways. Do not recommend. Most overrated professor at Purdue.

Most overrated and glazed professor; Certainly not a 1/5 but 5/5 don't think so. Had her twice and both times it's been a nightmare as a student who works full-time because she does not grant extensions and isn't reliable whatsoever. It's so frustrating working an 8-hour shift on an 18-credit course load only to be told no when I get home late.

Sally is salt of the Earth. She is truly one of the most caring instructors you will find. She is so approachable and wants to get to know all of us. Very easy to ask questions and responds quickly. She truly cares about our success and loves the material. Very big Purdue fan too! Funny and just plain awesome!

Sally Ray is one of, if not, the best professor at Purdue. She is very caring and is very accessible outside of class. If you are unable to make it to her office hours, she will try and setup someone other time to meet, in-person or on zoom.

The class itself is interesting and the material builds on itself a lot. Get the basics down well.

10/10

Sally is probably the best prof i've had. Notes are not posted online so go to lectures for sure. 2 hw assignments per week, 2 midterms and 1 final, tests are fair. lots of additional help as well with office hours and homework help sessions and when i couldnt make it to either of those sally would have 1 on 1 zooms with me. love her.
I love Sally! She is very caring and makes her lectures fun and engaging! The homeworks were fine as long as you went to the lectures. The final was extremely hard, but there was a big curve.

Genuinely the best professor I have ever had, the material is tough but having someone like Professor Ray helps a ton, goes above and beyond in both helping students understand the material and being an extremely nice person. Also, a very fair grader who gives extra credit opportunities.

This was one of my hardest classes, but she recognized that it was a complicated topic and went out of her way to help us in any way needed. She doesn't take attendance, but you have to go to lectures because they aren't recorded. It's alright, though, because the pace is quick, and the class flies by because it is always a lighthearted environment

Dr. Ray is a gem. Absolutely loved taking STAT 472 with her. She is always well prepared for class, teaches extremely well and creates a great learning environment for students to ask questions. Exams are extremely fair. Never had a better professor in any class before.
Great professor!

Lecture and homework were great ways of practicing the new concepts and I could tell that Professor Ray was trying her best to prepare us for the final and ultimately the actuarial exam. She is easily one of my favorite professors I've ever had since it was clear that she cared about us, both in and out of the classroom. Very approachable.
Her lectures were easy to follow and she made time for her students during office hours.  Would take again!

She is a great teacher and definitely cares. MA 373 is a tough class because of the amount of hw and tests we have but as long as you attend class and listen to the teacher (and go to office hours!!) you will be fine.

absolutely LOVE professor sally. there's several times i send late emails (like 11pm) about homeworks and she replied with in 5-10 mins. she tries her very best to help you with anything in the class, also she remembers everyone's name and something about them which is just so impressive!
One of the kindest professors I've had. She let me make up two quizzes outside of class

My favorite professor at Purdue! Her lectures are very clear, and she is always willing to help clarify anything you don't understand. Homework is manageable and she gives plenty of practice problems for exam prep. The exams/quizzes are very reasonable and she doesn't try to trick you. Very nice and funny professor! Cannot recommend enough:)

Incredibly easy class, but made interested through lectures that connected concepts to practical applications. Sometimes course materials or lectures felt a bit messy, but often those tangents turn into great life and career advice given by the professor, just as if not more valuable than the actual course content. Hope to take more of his classes.

Prof. Liu's lectures are very helpful. The course is math-heavy with lots of proofs and derivations, but Prof. Liu goes through all the concepts needed to solve the homework. He does a great job at keeping the mood light during the lectures.

Good professor for the course, is generally clear in explaining concepts. Do not underestimate the class or take the adjusted grade cutoffs for granted because the exams can be extremely difficult. Do the assigned practice problems and past exams or you will not pass. The quiz and attendance scores could be what ends up saving you.
V strict prof, does not have any leniency. Good lectures but gets carried away at times. Course was unnecessarily tough.
Class was fairly easy, exams were fair and extra credit was offered. Decent lecturer, but you will not learn if you don't do the optional problems.

Professor Liu does a good job with lecturing, and is very clear about what will be tested in the class, and prepares students well for exams if they pay attention in class and do the practice problems. Class is reasonable workload wise. Goes into depth about the topic of Probability.

He makes it difficult to ask questions. When you ask a question, he asks you another question. Instead of explaining the question, he might ask you to rewatch the lecture. Also, he only covers the new materials almost at the end of class.

Professor seems to be a nice person and tries his best to explain the material but it doesn't seem to translate well into his teaching. He covers all the theory but rarely puts it into practice in examples. Tests have proof based questions that are incredibly hard with no guidance in lectures or office hours. Final project is long and tedious.

He is a very nice and understanding person. But his classes are very math theory-heavy with little application and hard to keep up notes. Homework and test have sections that are barely cover in class (like application) or hard math concept questions, but generous time windows. He had good reviews, it may be the online component of this semester.
The professor's notes aren't organized and all over the place. He covers basic topics in class but tests on extremely complex proofs in the homework and exams.

The lectures are just long rambles where he just reads aloud what he writes but the homework and exams are intensive problems that are not covered in class. Because he doesn't cover any examples or proofs in class. Only covers theory in class. Seems like a good natured professor but horrible at teaching.

Professor teaches some basic things in class. Homework is on something else that is complex. Zero examples done in class. Exams are as horrible as homework if not worse. Maybe it is because of online instruction but don't take this specific class with this professor. Bad experience all round.
Homework doesn't corelate to what he teaches. Very basic lectures but extremely hard homeworks

Great professor. Taught the class well and was easy even before COVID. Very accessible, funny, nice, and reasonable guy. Would definitely take again! Attendance not MANDATORY, but occasional Hotseat pop quizzes. 1 point for completion, 2 for accuracy.

Challenging course, but well taught! Professor Liu did a good job providing helpful examples and using the book sparingly. Kept classes light with humor, was very strict on no late-work policy. Very understanding throughout COVID-19 stuff.
The best professor in IE. His lectures are very clear and can let you learn a lot.
Very good professor.

Professor Liu is amazing at teaching. Uploads all the lecture slides and gives a clear idea about what will be on the test. Don't be happy with the low grade cutoff because exams will be very difficult.

Good professor. He teaches LP from the PoV of a Math Major. Although this becomes too much at times and his exams test proofs and understanding of theorems very closely, this will help you form a better understanding of the subject and retain it longer.
He made the class a breeze. Great lectures, very clear about what would be on the exams, gave formula sheets, allowed for lots of extra credit. Invested in your success. Also pretty funny in lecture.
Amazing Professor. Really like his teaching style.
His lecture is clear and interesting. I took it last semester with Hunter,and  that is terrible. Definitely choose him instead of hunter or you will be hunted.

He is a great professor and explains concepts clearly. If you go to class and pay attention to the examples he uses, that is all you need to get an A in the class. He doesn't use a textbook and his slides are vague but that's because he encourages more participation during lectures with his examples. I waited a semester for him and it was worth it!

Prof Liu really cares about his teaching. The course is tough, but he explains the material clearly, and also helps a lot during office hours. This course was a degree requirement for me, but he really stoked interested in the subject and I would take another course with him.

Pretty clear due to his examples, so don't skip classes. Do homework in advance and you'll probably get full credit. Exams are a lot like practice exams. He is very generous with the grades. Overall, a great professor with an impressive background (if you care to read it). I'm hoping I can take another class with him.

The day I submitted my last assignment in this class was the greatest day of my life. Brownstein will give you no feedback, vague instructions, and gives you practically no time to finish assignments. Great lecturer, but only posts them once in a blue moon and expects you to learn the content yourself by reading articles.

Subjective grading. Gave no real feedback. Tests/quizzes had little to do with the given materials. Online course but only uploaded outside sources to view, rarely any videos of him lecturing. 0/10 would not recommend.

Dr. Brownstein was great when I had him in the Spring of 2025. It was clear that he cared about the material as well as our individual success in the course. The quizzes are hard as they are free response but everything you need to know comes directly from lecture or from the book. I recommend buying the book.

I knew it was going to be a good class when he jokingly referenced his RMP score while talking about problems with survey responses and statistical bias. I was quite intimidated by POL 413, but he really puts in an effort to ensure everyone is on the same page. To be clear, if you don't turn up to class you are screwed, but come on.

Everything in the class is all writing, so subjective grading. He gives no feedback, and when I went to office hours to get feedback on an exam, he made me feel wrong for trying to improve. Grading takes forever, and the syllabus does not list what assignments are due when, so it's hard to plan for things. Would not recommend taking this professor.
Be prepared to take quizzes every other class. These assessments may cover a substantial amount of material, including notes and concepts introduced during the same class period.

This professor's class is entire quiz and exam based, with very little weight placed on attendance and no homework. This may sound nice at first, but material on exams and quizzes are very unrelated to what is covered in class. It feels like a vast majority of questions asked is stuff he mentioned in passing, or trivia.

For a 100 level course, it was extremely difficult. The only things graded are quizzes, exams, and attendance. Can be overly critical towards students. If someone answers his question with a wrong or different opinion he becomes weirdly hostile.
Everytime i get in my car to drive to his class, i always hope i don't make it there. Never again.

Miss a class and say goodbye to a quiz score. Nothing put in place to help you catch up without going through obscene obstacles. If this professor would just post the slides or videos or something this class wouldn't be nearly as irritating. Also, why can't he just pass back the quizzes? He supposedly makes them new each time so why not return them

Says so much nothing in so many words. This class is useless, maybe that's his fault maybe not, either way, would recommend avoiding completely. Look up “Testing Theories of American Politics: Elites, Interest Groups, and Average Citizens." There is nothing close to this perspective offered in this class, even though this study is years old.

No feedback given without scheduling time, grades don't tell you what you get wrong without scheduling. Talks about irrelevant material during class and then grades quizzes based off of wordiness. Kind professor, but in terms of helping students do well, without wasting more personal time, you are getting nowhere.
Terrible. Crazy that there are professors like this at such a prestigious institution.

Everything a professor should strive NOT to be. Super condescending and scornful, always looking to call out students for the most minor things. Quiz and exam questions consist of minor facts he briefly mentions in lecture that do not relate to key course objectives. Super rude, and does not have a personality. Embarrassment to Purdue honestly.

AVOID AVOID AVOID. He was extremely condescending and quite frankly just mean to students in class, claiming they were doing things they definitely weren't (talking/cheating) and being more of a distraction himself than the students causing issues. The quizzes/tests most of the time felt like it was over things we didn't even discuss. Just no.

Brownstein was super condescending the entire semester. He is incredibly arrogant and mean towards his students and looks to call people out in front of the whole lecture. Guy makes his quizzes and tests 10x more complicated and difficult than it needs to be. Tries to act smart, but is an airhead. The TA was an absolute moron as well. Avoid him.

Avoid. Creates such a tense learning enviornment and is always looking to call out students for not paying attention. Has no personality whatsoever and lectures feel like an interrogation. Quizzes are graded harshly and the averages are always in the 60s. Told the class “your quiz grades are bad so pay attention. Arrogant and overbearing

Dr. Brownstein is a very good lecturer and his presentations are very easy to follow. The readings were based on the lectures so if you decide not to do the readings you will be fine. In total 6 quizzes and 3 exams, but one from each gets dropped. He gives many extra credit opportunities and overall the class is very easy and enjoyable.

I did not buy/read the textbook, even though it was technically mandatory (and he does reference it a lot in class); however, I still got an A+ in this class. He posts pretty much everything you need to know in the slides and it's pretty intuitive to guess the stuff you don't know.

Dr. Brownstein made this class straightforward as long as you were persistent. Plenty of extra credit to make up for poor midterm/quiz performance. Attendance is crucial and you could miss out on extra credit if you skip class. I would also recommend studying a bit for his midterms and quizzes. They were fair, but not a walk in the park per se.

A great class to fill up a gen-ed requirement. Textbooks: free and hardly used. Prof great at teaching the class: participation points about every other lecture. Hardest part of the class, quizzes: about every week, free-response questions about PointPoint content and current political news. Midterms: multiple-choice scantrons, not too difficult.
Great class! Professor Brownstein really cares about his classes and students

8 week POL101 online class was easy enough as long as you do the lots of work. The tests were all 8-10 one-page essays that you had multiple days to work on at your own pace. He gave great feedback on those and the other assignments to help you move towards where he wanted you to go. LOTS of quizzes and discussions and a few short video responses.

Very frustrating, the tests are impossible to pass. There is a large group project and quizzes every day which are not only stupid, they bring your grade down. Overall, this class was a waste of my time and the professor was annoying the entire semester.

He's a great professor, lectures are interesting and fun. Go to class, there's daily 5 point quizzes; the lectures cover a large portion of test content. There's one big paper over the semester but he offers so much feedback and office hours that it isn't bad. Good at discussing from political science perspective but sometimes shows left bias.

Amazing professor, you could tell he really cares about his students succeeding and is just generally a really nice guy. Coursework is manageable, and he responds to emails seriously (in a good way!).
very good teacher

Really nice Professor makes the lectures very enjoyable, be prepared for a pop quiz as he has 6 reading quizzes in the semester that are on random days. Very easy to understand your grade as the rubric is clear and tells you what you did right and wrong, his office hours are also very helpful.

Pretty chill guy, he listens to student feedback and modifies how he teaches the class based on it. There's a lot of reading, and quizzes are all pop quizzes, which is annoying, but grading is fair. The class can be a bit boring at times. Major assignments include a midterm and final speech, plus a final essay.
Lots of reading.
Very good quality teacher. You can tell he cares about his students and wants them to succeed. The class itself is not hard as long as you put in some effort.

Absolutely amazing professor! I would 100% recommend taking SCLA 102 with him. He makes the classes super fun and engaging. Great sense of humor! Also has plenty of extra credit chances throughout the semester. Take him if you can!
don't graduate without taking one of his classes.

Honestly a super chill dude. Very lenient with papers and speech grading. As long as you go to class, read the readings before quizzes and participate you should get an A. He usually told us beforehand if we're gonna have a quiz that following day so a little skim of the text you should be fine. 10/10 recommend for this course.

FANTASTIC PROFESSOR!! One of the best professors I've ever had. Professor Mollison is absolutely hilarious, and the class is very clearly structured. You get to learn about really interesting topics and get a bunch of new perspectives. He's an amazing professor!!

One of the most engaging professors that I've had- super qualified in speech/debate. He approaches class topics and lectures with a brilliantly witty sense of humor. Access to multiple texts is needed, but the readings are manageable. Attendance matters, but he can account for personal needs. You will truly become a better speaker from class.

He is great! This class can be quite challenging and involves a ton of reading, but he gives overviews before every discussion so that everyone has a base-level understanding. He is extremely funny and lenient when it comes to extensions or questions. I've heard he grades hard on speeches but I seemed to slip by that! Made a boring topic bearable

Great teacher. Loves to crack jokes. He says he's a tough grader, but from what I saw, very few people got below high 80s on major assignments. Lots of extra credit available. Gives extensions. Dr. Mollison does everything he can to help students, so reach out to him if you need help, and he will kindly give you advice.
Very funny professor

Great teacher who gives solid lectures and is pretty entertaining. Grades a bit hard on the speeches but makes it clear what he expects from you. Lots of economic texts are used in this class but he makes sure to provide material from both sides of the aisle (capitalism vs communism). Main problem is how much attendance matters.
This class was super fun and engaging! Dr. Mollison made SCLA 102 very engaging, and I thoroughly enjoyed being in his class. Just do the quizzes and discuss in class and you'll be fine.

This was such a fun class with him. He's extremely funny and chill. No boring, stuffy lectures. His grading criteria is clear and fair. He is willing to work with you if you need something. 10/10 class.

Amazing professor. He loves to joke and make class fun. Go to class and do your best on his little quizzes and just prepare for speeches. He is very understanding and the most personal prof I've ever had. I'll see him in the halls and he still remembers me and makes an effort to say hello. You wont be bored in his class with work and socializing.
Really knowledgeable, super funny, makes students get involved in class discussions and topics and interested in the readings

Great professor! The assigned books are interesting; they can be a bit dense but he explains the concepts really well and makes them understandable. He grades hard on speeches, but also provides really good feedback. Gives a lot of extra credit opportunities if you're willing to put in the work to do them.

Really cool guy! High standards for speeches which he reflects in grading but is made up for in other categories. I genuinely looked forward to his class. Explains complex topics simply and well. Books for the class are alright if you're into them.

As a professor, James is amazing. He provides all the materials for the lectures, and has a rather fair grading policy. Show up to class, be involved and don't forget to engage with James, as he is super friendly and approachable.

Overall wonderful professor. Clearly a smart guy. Enjoys big topics in philosophy than the course provided and may stray a bit off topic. Easy class but don't skimp on the midterm or final projects. He grades fairly. Perhaps a bit sassy/arrogant at times but it's all in good fun and to be expected in philosophers. Participate in class!

Taking this class was genuinely a nightmare, it is the same material as 502 and the professor offers no office hours. This class is adapted from EPFL CS-420 ADVANCED Compiler Construction, which recommends taking their CS-320 (Computer language processing with LLVM), a COMPILERS course, BEFORE taking it and I can see why. 20+ hours on projects.

Clear & moderately paced lectures. Interesting projects. My workload was ~9 hours per 2-week project (YMMV by a lot). Rompf declines to provide study resources other than a (conversational) review session, but exams were of reasonable difficulty/content.
Good Luck passing this class. Do your best that is all I can say for now.
Tough class but worth it if you want to learn and is interested in compilers.

Do not do take the course under him. Yes, the assignments are tough and lengthy but I still managed to get a 100% in them. But the exams are unreasonably long and do not curve. Pray for nice classmates who start assignments early and post issues on Piazza. He's a great lecturer but acts as if his savings will be emptied if he gives a nice grade.

He knows the material and answers questions ONLY IN CLASS. He would not respond to an email or give OH. He would be 5 minutes late to every lecture. The TA, Songlin Jia, is also useless; he would not answer questions in Piazza or email. If you go to his OH, he would say, "The answer code has...". He cannot answer any real/difficult questions.

He warned there will be a lot of efforts for this class. But this is too much. Literally every projects consumes 30-40 hours. Literally every project requires effort for compiler projects in cs 252. I am currently taking with other cs courses. But this course is taking too much time from me that I cannot focus on other course well.

7 projects and each of them may take 20~40 hrs if you don't have background knowledge and want to get a good grade. No extensions for projects. One minute late submission will be given 0. And they will not reply to your argument. Sometimes their testing will modify the code that you are not expected to change.

He is actually proud of giving unrealistic amounts of weekly/bi-weekly lab, and assuming his students to work on the labs like a full-time job. Avoid at all costs, unless you really have nothing else to do this semester.

Rompf is a decent lecturer, but his slides aren't great. Because of this, I was consistently working on projects until the deadline. Everyone did awful on the midterm as well and we all walked into the final ready to fail the class since it was worth 30% of our grade. Luckily the curve came through but this class was AWFUL for my mental health.

Professor does little more than read off slides he adapted from another professor during class. Purposefully uses a language that has very little documentation and project starter code is so heavily obfuscated by little tricks that can be accomplished by the language (Scala) instead of making it easier to understand a tad bit longer.

Avoid Rompf for CS352. This class is a grad class, not an undergrad (exact same material except undergrad has office hours). The material is interesting, but he's not a good lecturer (in his words - he's not there to teach, you're here to learn). There is way too much material for each exam. He's hard to reach outside of class about any concerns.

I've learnt more from this course than I have from any other CS course at Purdue. 7 Projects + 2 Exams, most having extra credit. Lectures are great with Tiark, but I do have trouble understanding Oliver (the other lecturer). Projects are difficult and will require time. But TA's and Prof aren't responsive on Piazza, and projects can be vague.

The entitled students on here need to calm it down. Tiark tells students on the very first day of class that his class isn't like a normal class. He treats everyone like adults instead of holding their hands and delicately guiding them so they can get a taste of the real world. Definitely a difficult class, but one of the best I've ever taken.

Never see Professor Tiark in person, all his lecture is taught by his PhD student. A very unclear cutoff settings on the final grade. He never mentions "extra credit" is also being counted as a denominator and thus get a 93/100 cannot get an A but need to get 98/110.

The most disrespectful professor I have ever seen. I am appalled at the way he openly makes judgments about students on Piazza. After a student asked a very fair question on piazza anonymously, he exposed them by name, and made some very demeaning comments about their "moral ground and selfishness". Extremely ashamed to have a professor like this

Professor Tiark is the first professor I've ever seen behave in such a demeaning manner. On Piazza he outed a student who wanted to remain anonymous who respectfully expressed valid frustration about very unclear and last-minute grading, and basically told the student he needed to check his privilege and get a better moral compass. Unprofessional.

I have taken CS 354, CS 355, CS 426, ect ect and NONE compare even close to the amount of time I had to put into this course. I think I saw the professor twice (I went to every lecture), and even then he didn't teach, he was just there for a proctor. His "extra credit" opportunities are actually mandatory and are counted into your final grade. :(

The TAs for this class know absolutely nothing. They never answered questions, and if they actually respond, it has nothing to do with the question you asked. You never get a break from the projects, and they're all pointless anyways. On day one they'll tell you "you have been warned." Unless you're passionate, change your concentration. not worth.

This class is very project heavy and the theory feels under-emphasized. Despite the projects being grad level and me being burned out by the 4th of 7, this course has been good. A lot of project steps are given in the slides so its not impossible. Prof Rompf is a good lecturer and his response to moving online was extremely fair.

All projects in CS 352 are directly taken from CS 502, which is a graduate level course for CS students. Not only he doesn't bother to change the content, the course material pages are 90% identical including the theme, slides, project names etc. There is NOTHING valuable to learn from the lectures except code that you will get from skeleton code.

Gave 3 exams (including the final). Not given enough time for any of them, not even the 2 HOUR final. No practice exams given. Iclickers in class graded on accuracy (several questions where about 50% of the class was marked incorrect). Term project worth only 6% of our grade (not equivalent to the time it takes). His Brightspace is very unorganized
Prof. Cai always makes course content easy to understand and will provides a lots of examples. The homework and exams are straightforward with very clear grading criteria.
Very good professor

His lectures make difficult materials seem like common sense. He is easy to talk to and will do as much as he could to prepare you for the exams. Also, he keeps you awake and paying attention. I truly wish he was my second dad.
Very kind professor and general in grading. lecture materials are information-rich, you can learn a lot if you pay attention on class and review the notes after.

I CAN'T UNDERSTAND OTHER'S COMMENTS. OBVIOUSLY, HE IS BEING UNDERESTIMATED. HE IS EXTREMELY KIND AND RESPECTFUL. ALSO, CLASS ITSELF IS VERY USEFUL AND HAS LOTS OF THINGS THAT CAN INSPIRE YOU. MOREOVER, HIS GRADING IS RELATIVELY GENEROUS AND CLEAR. YOU CAN'T MISS HIS CLASSES.
Horrible professor. Not complicated material, but prepare for headaches. Teaching style is extremely confusing, and his lectures are useless/notes are disorganized.

Not much to learn from this course.   Professor has weird grading systems where 5% is reserved for class participation. If he doesn't like you he'll give you a 0 making it impossible to get an A. I averaged 96% on the exams and projects, but got an A minus.  However, it is a very easy course if you just want the credit.
This is very important course, I strongly recommend to take this course as Construction students. Nice guy and very helpful and easy grade.
Terrible lectures, and really confusing. Tests are unfair. Releases PowerPoint slides with important material blacked out.
Awful! Rude to students and confusing teaching style. His exams are very lengthy and he sets his students up for failure.

This class and lectures were super boring. It was super easy though. If you took 201 it's basically the same class. All the assignments were opened at the very beginning of the semester, and I finished in like two days. IClicker is graded but it's not location based so you can do it anywhere

super easy class. attendance online mandatory through iclicker. do it in bed just dont forget because it adds up, exams and assignments are easy but tedious. you have like 3 quizzes and an assignment one day than like another quiz another day. no project, no final, just exams. good lecturer, GREAT T.A (Julia Gindorf)! so kind and understanding!

Professor Elenbaas is an amazing professor. She genuinely cares about her students and teaching the content. She is super sweet and caring. There is frequent homework for this course, but it is easy. All exams are open-book online. The course content is also just entertaining to learn, so work for this class is not a hassle. Would highly recommend

Laura was super nice and friendly. Takes iClicker for attendence but the class was easiest I've ever taken. MyVirtual, modules and different assignments online but so easy. Exams were open note and online. Super easy concepts. Stopped going to class, completed all modules and assignments 8 weeks in so basically finished the class early. So easy.
Great professor. Very easy to get an A in this class. Professor Elenbaas is very clear and informative. Homework takes little time and exams are open note.

It's really difficult not to get an A in this class. Participation is through Iclicker, but you can log in from home and do it that way. Exams are online and open-book, so they're not bad at all. Just do your homework and look at the lecture slides, and you'll be fine

This class is hard not to get an A in. Professor is clear and informative in lectures. Lots of little assignments (take no time to do and very easy) and a couple of online exams that are open note. Prof. Elenbaas is super caring too, and worked with me when I was having some problems and was so sweet about it. Definitely a good prof and class.
All exams are online and nonproctored so you get to do them on your own time during the day. It is very easy to get an A if you do the reading, homework, and the in class participation.

She is very nice and the class is so easy. Homework takes almost no time and tests are online and not proctored. I did find the lectures to be very surface level. Attendance is tracked through hotseat questions throughout lecture, but you can do them from home if you watch the app for the questions to pop up since you don't have to get them right.

Very chill. All midterms and the final exam were online (not proctored). The lectures were slightly shallow, only covered some general stuff. Exams had lots of content assigned in the textbook, lots of information but pretty easy to understand and memorise. Attendance was checked via hotseat participation, 5 pop up questions per lecture on average.

He is an okay professor. He created slides with AI and sometimes they had errors in them so just beware of that. Participation mattered but it was done online so class sometimes had less than half attendance. Seemed to care about what he was teaching and invested in students learning.

Felt like an average college class. The lectures were a bit boring but it's important to come and take notes for exams. My grade was made up of two exams, two papers, two presentations, and attendance. Nothing's too heavily weighted though and the grading's fairly lenient. Overall Professor Yang is a nice guy and his class wasn't too bad.
very great teacher, and great class. I would definitely recommend this class! Yang is very passion about his teaching and it shows. He deserves more credit and love!

a great professor, but the class itself is very boring but that is just my opinion! The lectures consist of note taking the whole time with discussions at the beginning of the class or the end (not always). Only homework assigned are readings, and the group project. Not much points.
Professor Yang didn't give a comprehensive introduction to American religion in this course and it feels like he doesn't focus on this field.

Professor Yang is a great instructor. His curriculum is reasonable, and he pays much attention to what students have learned rather than grades. Of course, his office hours are also very helpful. Extra credits are super helpful as well.
Awesome teacher! You should definitely take his class.
Very nice professor.  If you have a hard time adapting to accents this isn't the class for you.  I didn't have a problem though.

I enjoyed this class even though I wasn't super interested in the material. Dr. Krok is great. She's super friendly and helpful. The class was pretty easy. There were 4 exams and a weekly reading quiz, both on lockdown browser. They're pretty easy if you do the readings and pay attention in class.
Shes a caring mom vibe and definitley want to take again!

Prof Krok was really nice, and the class was very well organized. Her lectures were interesting and very helpful for homeworks and labs. All quizzes and exams were open book and pretty straightfoward. No complaints from me!

As a psych major, surrounded by mostly slhs majors who already have experience with most of the content and basic concepts, I felt pretty lost compared to others. I felt like my classmates who were slhs majors completed the projects with more ease. In the course, you work with language samples from children and analyze them. TA graded very kindly.

I really did not like this class. Lots of busy work and I hate group projects. Dr. Krok is also kind of rude and inconsiderate. However, she is a good lecturer and I learned a lot from this class. The workshops were very helpful.

Professor Krok is a wonderful professor who works hard to make sure her students feel involved in their learning. She accepts feedback with grace and works hard to make sure her course is fair and worth tuition. People within the SLHS major and outside of it had a good experience and as long as you put in the work you will do well.
Don't buy the textbook it is absolutely not necessary for the course.

Workload was absolutely unreasonable. He was flakey and late on opening tests but still made us turn things in on time. Required all these extra steps just to do the assignment. Randomly gave grades with no clear criteria.
One of the best professors I have had in my academic career. Is very passionate and knowledgeable, but also one of the most humble people I know. It was a pleasure having him as my professor.
Unstructured, unorganised, and weird.

Useless feedback, irrelevant rubrics, meaningless lectures, and bizarre emails are some things present in his classes. I almost failed until I explained how work should be graded, but then I was lectured for pointing something out and made fun of. He's only valuable for his research as the teaching is horrid. Don't take him even if there's no chce
The most helpful class I've taken. Highly recommend!
What a great course!  He is extremely nice and helpful.
This class was very stimulating. It was one of the best classes I have ever taken.

I had him for two classes, but it seems he has lost his spark for teaching. In 480 the grading scale also cut off at a 93/83/73. So, missing two points on a project got you a C for that assignment. I would just say going through and clearly reading everything is important.

The grading scale for this class is ridiculous considering how much of your grade is dependent on one semester long group project with assigned group members. A 94 is an A, and a 93.4 is a B, that should speak for itself. However, not a difficult class I just wish the grading scale made sense.

The class is straight forward. If you do each part of the assignments you are tasked to do you will get an A. If you read the textbook chapters assigned and attend the lectures you will get good exam grades. If you don't submit assignments on time or do not attend lectures(even occasionally) you will not get an A.

Paul Thomas is very good professor. Lectures are clear and well organized and not dry whatsoever.

1) Lectures, tests, quizzes are based off of the textbook. Read the textbook, pass the class.

2) Ask your TA to verify one of your ERD/ diagrams is right so that you don't end up making serveral incorrect diagrams/ERDs  (from a person w/ 99% on labs)
Paul is one of the best professors here at Purdue. You will definitely like this class, its straightforward and well organized. Highly recommend.

The exams can be really tough, even if you study. Lots of calculations, and the equation sheet was a bit confusing. Homework was weighted heavily and graded harshly, you'd get a 100% one week but a 60% the next. Final project included insanely large groups (~10 students) which made it difficult to complete and share the workload equally.

Dr. Rojas is a great professor that really cares about her students. The quizzes are easy as long as you go to class. She takes student input for exams, so as long as you study, exams should be easy. You need to put in work on the labs, but the TAs and her work with students if they have any questions. Overall, she's an amazing teacher.

Dr. Rojas is always willing to help her students and really wants them to succeed and understand the content. She knows the class is hard and does a good job breaking up the material and giving you opportunity to do well in the class. 2 in person midterms with an online final depending on how the class does on the first two midterms.
Good professor

Labs are hard, kinda difficult to understand but if you are confused she will explain it. the TA's can be very helpful in this class. Labs are worth a lot of points but partial credit is hard to get. Lectures arent mandatory and are sometimes more confusing that if you just read the slides. 2 exams are worth 30% of the grade. Heavy calculations.

Reads off slides in lectures and expects us to solve hard numerical problems for Eyeclickers and homework. Group projects are pretty challenging, so expect to spend a long time on them as she dosent prep you for them in class. Never been so disappointed in a class where I got an A. Try to take any other prof if you want to learn something.
She was ight. Didn't understand her lectures. Group projects were worth way too much. It sucked.
Claudia is very accessible outside of class, she is very patient in office hours and will always try and help. The group projects were very useful.

First exam ever, and the average was 55%. Homework is really difficult. Her slides are completely useless and consist of definitions at max 
Her lectures are useless. she just reads from slides and expects us to solve numerical problems without actually understanding the content. Exams just feel like everything is out of the syllabus. 
Worst!!!!!.

I think Vlad is a good teacher, especially when it comes to foundational topics in the beginning of the 231 course content. He has a good balance between lecturing conceptual information and walking through example problems in class. Usually these example problems were similar to our homework questions, which was helpful. He's also very personable.

Vlad is a good enough professor. He's very unorganized, but communicates well when you talk to him outside of class. He also teaches the material exactly as it will be on exams and homework, meaning that you are setup well for success as long as you go to class and pay attention.

Vlad is relatively new to teaching a full circuits class, and unfortunately it is quite clear. He often has numerous errors in each slide set, takes an extremely long time to grade both the main class and the lab assignments, and is very unforgiving in his grading.

Vlad is a great professor to talk to and is very sociable, but unfortunately this does not make up for his lack of organizational skills and harsh grading. He will spend hours talking about unrelated material which is nice until you realize he missed 75% of the material. Also, he would grade in extra criteria not in provided rubrics.
Fai was great for human medical genetics! Super straight forward on expectations. Exams and homework were very reasonable. Learned everyone's names in class.

2 Credits. Seems nice, kinda funny. Horrible grader. For example: I once used the word "needed" instead of "necessary" and for a point off. I contested it (to no avail). If you do not have to take it, DO NOT. If you've ever taken BIOL286, this is its henchman (2 credits and a pain in the 🫏).

Cares about his students learning and is mindful that this is a 2 credit course and we have other courses to worry about. Has a sense of humor, quick to respond to emails, VERY smart, and makes an effort to learn students names. Exams were a little harder than I expected (all online and avg was 90ish, I usually got 70-80 w lots of studying)

wonderful lecturer. exams are not overly difficult if you read as directed and attend/watch the lectures (which are available as recordings), and he drops 1/4. graded on very few items, but generally not a super difficult class, despite being pretty heavy on material.
save your grade and take it onlineeee

The class includes a few assignments, which are very exam-heavy. You get 4 exams and 3 other assignments in the overall class, so make sure you get 60 and above to pass the class with a B or C.I would say take the class online. It is not worth your grade. No study guide, all exams are very lecture-heavy and book-heavy.

Dr. William's class was very fun, and the lectures were really interesting. As long as you did the reading and took notes, you got a good grade in the class. He's very helpful and wants to see all of his students succeed.
he gives great lectures. attendance wasn't mandatory but i always went anyway. just read the textbook and study the terms and this class should be a breeze.
Chill, guy, be prepared to use the textbook A LOT.

He is very good at explaining confusing topics in clear and concise ways. He is able to break things down and make it easy for everyone to understand. Some of his lectures he would predominantly just read off of his slides but these were the small minority of lectures. It always felt like he wanted us to learn the material well.

He has a great teaching personality and knows how to keep students engaged. He posted all the lectures online, so if I missed a class, I could rewatch the lecture. As long as you pay attention to what he says during lecture, it's a super easy class. I'd 100% retake a class with him.

William's lectures are super interesting! They can be information heavy and drag on (especially in the twice-a-week section), but his personality is engaging, and he'll often ask for student participation/examples. He loves answering questions and loves talking to students. Exams are fine, but you will def need to study both his notes and the book

Overall he's a great guy, and while his lectures can get a bit dense/stale at times, he breaks it up by actually having a personality and being funny in otherwise boring topics. Cares about his students and is always happy to answer questions
He is very kind and keeps lectures interesting. The content can get monotonous at points, but I think his personality makes it fun.

I enjoyed this class and thought he was a good lecturer! Despite no tracked attendance I very rarely missed class. Sometimes his exams had some tricky questions but were overall very fair. I thought they were pretty harsh in terms of grading the paper but that did not stop me from doing well. I loved the class and would take it again!

Dr. William is an AMAZING professor. His lectures are always super memorable and he genuinely loves teaching his students. PSY120 is only graded on exams and few assignments, so make sure to put time into the class. Going to lectures and paying attention will honestly get you at least a B because of how easy he makes it to learn.

super engaging, amazing professor. for a 300 level class it's very easy to get into and get an A in. i had to do 2 discussion posts, 3 exams (4 in total, lowest one dropped), and 1 research paper. that's literally it. for the research paper, he gave us very clear rubrics/criteria. get ur ta's help for the paper!!!!
Drinks out of water bottle in the microphone too frequently

i love him. lectures are engaging and hes funny but not mandatory. Four ~650 word discussion boards throughout the sem but aren't too bad-you can easily get max score on all of them if u js put in at least some thought into them. 4 exams but one is dropped. exams are prob the most challenging part of the course but arent extremely bad, just study

Only thing from this class I learned was learned helplessness. I studied hard for exams and still did poorly. My friends all did well though and he does care about his students. The class is just tough.
Very clear and easy class structure. I did my SONA early and did well on the first 3 tests, so I was done with this class before Thanksgiving Break

dr. william is the best professor. his lectures are so good i have literally walked to them super sick just because the class is so much fun and sometimes i forget i'm actually learning. his lectures are fun, easy to follow along, and straightforward. not mandatory attendance. exams and easy discussion boards. what else do you need? excellent guy.
He's great!

Dr. William is my favorite professor I've had by far. He is engaging, informative, and shows passion about his field. There is only one paper due (2.5-3 pages). The information you MUST know in the slides and textbook are either bolded or italicized, so it's pretty easy to pick out what you need to know for exams. There is no cumulative final.

Dr.William is such an energetic professor who clearly cares about teaching students. He keeps the lectures interesting and engages with the class. Please do not skip the lectures; either attend them in person or watch them online. I promise it is not worth retaking a class.
Really funny and makes lectures engaging. Does like to yap.

I loved psy 120 he is a great lecturer! Although there is a lot of slides, he is very funny and entertaining and uses good examples to help you understand the context, I highly recommend taking it, easy A.

You don't need to come to lecture, but would highly recommend it; he's genuinely a joy to listen to. Both lecture and reading are fair game for exams. One paper is assigned, but it's fairly easy. Provides the textbook for free in online format. You'll learn a decent amount about him through lectures, which makes the big class feel more personal.
This class is very independently taught but she will gladly help you with work if needed and is quite knowledgeable of MATLAB

Prof Wible is one of the kindest and most understanding professors I've had. The course itself is challenging, but she does her best to support students and explain material clearly. She was very accommodating when I started feeling sick during class and allowed me to leave without any attendance penalty, which I really appreciated.
Wible is the best professor. This class is just simply tedious. It is not her fault and she teaches well. She often releases the class early, so talk and get to know her.
She is nice and sweet. The class was very easy. To ask questions, find the TA that grades your group and ask. The GTA also answers questions well because GTA knows the rubric.
