the goat. lets u out of class super early. gives breaks throughout the class. super sweet, energetic, and caring!

Prof Wible was very engaging in her lectures and was always in a good mood and seemed excited to teach. Even though the content of 132 is pretty boring at times, she is one of the better professors as she's very knowledgeable about matlab and will spend the time to help you figure out your mistakes in class.
She didn't like me. The class is very easy.
I liked this professor but at times when I asked her a question she didn't really answer the question.

She was nice but if you have any grading issues the GTA will be incharge of it. Out of all the lectures for ENGR 131 and 132 she was very nice and knew what was going on which is helpful because the TAs usually dont know and the grading is super random and tricky on some things. Overall really nice but the class itself is annoying.
Class itself is a complete joke but I cant blame Prof. Wible for that. Shes very kind towards her students and puts in effort to make sure you know whats going on.
Extremely kind and caring for students and their needs. Great professor

She is super caring and understanding, if you go talk to her she helps you out most of the time, and if she can't, its usually not for a lack of trying. She is super outgoing and loves to talk, but gives out exemptions in her assignments and is a super chill grader.

Extremely caring and kind person, she is always involved helping students and groups and really goes the extra mile to make a connection with the class. ENGR131 and 132 are difficult classes to coordinate but Professor Wible does an excellent job, and made this class somewhat enjoyable.

Wible is a very kind and friendly professor. She is very approachable and always willing to help with issues. Overall her class was not very difficult.

Definitely recommend professor Wible for Engineering 132. the class is mostly lead by the head ta. she is a sweet lady who cares about her students. the class was easy consists of group projects that are worth a lot of points. the exams were straight forward and easy. if you go over her slides you should be fine. The kindest and sweetest person ever.

Great professor, easy and helpful class.

This was a great class. I liked the format of the projects. I absolutely loved this class. He is such a good professor and I really liked his teaching style. I really enjoyed him and his class. I must admit that I am not skilled in coding and I had a below-average coding skill at the beginning of the class. I worked pretty hard to get through this course. Glotzbach runs this class asynchronous online. Instructional videos are not very helpful and he does not explain what he is doing. Does not respond to emails. His idea of teaching PHP is providing videos of what to do and that is it. No explanation of what we're doing is given. Attendance is mandatory and yet we don't do anything that we can't do at home on our own. A lot of the time his code doesn't work, or links he provides don't work.

We are barely taught to. They say to find and read books found online, I cannot access Safari (which they recommend using). He wasn't very efficient with his grading. He never responded to my emails (at least to me). On multiple occasions he wouldn't be at his office hours. At the end of the semester I found that I didn't really learn anything from his class. I did not enjoy this class whatsoever.
Enjoyed his class. I'd recommend him. He genuinely cares about students.
He truly wants students to learn.
CGT 141 was pretty easy. Some of the labs were a little difficult. If you did poorly in this class it was probably your fault. Tests weren't too bad.

Good professor, got kind of bored in lecture at times. But it's honestly not the greatest subject to lecture about. It's an applied science. Class was easy. I just wish the projects weren't as much based solidly on requirements (# of pages) but more on technical skill.
Great teacher.  Knows his stuff, has vast amounts of experience with what he teachers and is always willing to help.

Easy class.  Glotzbach makes the subject matter easy to understand and provides a ton of resources to help you out of class.  Overall a great professor just do not ever go to his office he is kind of a dick outside of class.  Go see the TA's you will get more out of it.

Good class, easy labs, walked away feeling like you definitely learned something. I had no problem with my TA, didn't have much interaction with Ron himself. He posts outlines online, so get them before hand and take them to class.

he know's his html

Great Prof... just needs to lay off the coffee a little bit =P

Doesn't care about her students. Doesn't give us all of the materials, doesn't teach, doesn't know her own content, doesn't answer questions, impossible to reach outside of class. Content itself is not hard but you have to teach yourself everything because she will do the absolute bare minimum.

Lectures are just reading off of slides that are not well put together.  Practice exams and actual exams have many errors and are nothing like the quizzes in recitations. The labs range from incredibly easy to impossibly difficult. TA's are not allowed to answer any questions in lab.
Lecture slides are poorly made

Doesn't teach very well and lectures are hard to understand

Only uses letter grades. Lectures don't help, I learned everything on my own even though I've never coded in my life before. This has been the worst instructor I have ever had in college. Doesn't listen to her students and can't get through her own lectures without getting lost. This has been the worst professor I have ever had in my whole college career. full stop. don't think about emailing her, she doesn't reply. the TA's go and check every lab for AI (hint: theres no way to actually check they just guess), don't they have something better to do? and she doesn't actually know how to teach. just spews nonsense for 2hrs

Originally, I would have agreed with the ratings. However, I feel like she geniunely listens to her students. I took this class with prior experience in coding (I had AP credit) and I feel like if I hadn't I would've failed. She breezes through basic concepts and puts way too much emphasis on the niche stuff, especially on exams. She is pretty much unreachable outside of class.

If you have Jessica, you will be better off taking ANY other class. She is so rude and bad at teaching it's not even funny. The tests and projects are so dang difficult itll make you want to use AI, but DONT. You get in huge trouble if the system flags you for AI even if you didnt even use it.

bad experience

this dude straight up vibin tho

Lecture is half beneficial - follows textbook, Posts practice exams that are easier than exam, Class before exam is so helpful (MAKES EXAM SO MUCH EASIER), very helpful with final group project, Homework every week ( USE COMPUTER - this will save so much of your time), they grade 1-2 problems of the homework (that takes you hours each week)

super casual (to a fault) about everything.  his lectures feel heavily improvised and include tons of derivations that do not help with hw or exams, almost like he is trying to show off how excellent he is at math.  pray I do not end up with him for any classes going forward.

In order to get what he is saying in lecture you have to had read the book. He just goes straight to it at light speed. Hardly ever helps besides some help in office hours. All his exams are extremely long, is like he does not want you to finish it.

Funny teacher that made me actually want to go to class. Professor K lectured in a way that a TA talks to their students. He would only lecture the students that sat up front and debated math theory with him. He's old school, and lectures from the slides.

He did a great job teaching the class. Very funny and made the 7.30 class interesting.

Great one-on-one. Had the chance to see him explain something to one of his MA 166 students. Professor K cares so much about his students. The course is mostly dictated by the course coordinator but he will still try to help as much as he can. Made linear algebra into a joy. Lectures are good. He is a fun person so I always wanted to go to lecture even though it was not mandatory. Provides good examples for review. This is an amazing professor who teaches calculus I extremely well. I had Prof K in MA 265 during spring 2025 semester. He was great, funny and very caring. He was very helpful to me in understanding the material. If you go to his office hours he will take the time to explain the material to you.

**I had Prof. K for linear algebra, MA265**

 He gets really funny about halfway into the semester and drops some insane lore. He's nice in class, but rude in office hours... I got eyes rolled at me when I went to ask for exam help, so never went back again. So overall, great in classroom, not good one-on-one.
Prof K is very helpful. He took time outside of the class to help me with understanding the material better. He is very friendly and easy to work with.

He was a great teacher. Always willing to help. If you attend his office hours he will take as much as time explaining the material until you understand.

MA 158, not 160! Prof. K is a very funny guy and is willing to help you out. Sometimes frustrating to ask for help because he will do a problem halfway and then say you can figure out the rest. Made coming to lecture at 7:30 in the morning worth it! He is the best Math instructor I ever had. He is funny and keep the class enjoyable. If you came to class everyday and reach out to him when you have questions, you will do very well in the class.
Prof K is super nice and willing to help you understand the concepts. The class is overall pretty easy. Prof K can tend to not finish all the examples in class which can sometimes lead to confusion.

Prof K. is s great guy. He goes above and beyond to give you everything you need to succeed, and always makes himself available if you have any questions at all. Always comes into class with a great attitude and tries his best to make MA160 fun.

I loved MA1610 with Professor K! He is super accessible and really wants to see you do well. Professor had a tendency to not fully complete problems/leave them half down and claim we knew how to do the rest. I normally skip lectures for most classes, but these lectures actually taught me stuff which is surprising for Purdue. I would really recommend taking dr xu's ma266 lectures if you want to be able to learn nearly everything just in class.
Dr. Xu if one of my favorite math profs ive ever had. She might be the new chen of purdue. She is an excellent lecturer, if you have her you are in really good hands. 10/10

super clear notes and very organized
lectures were easy to understand and clearly communicated
Great lectures and really helps make sure everyone understands the material and posts great notes to brightspace. The content is hard and challenging but ping makes it easier
Good professor. I didnt go to lecture cause im ferda. But the 3 times i went she seemed chill
Prof. Xu is a good lecturer with detailed notes that she posts for every lecture, which is very helpful. The course content is difficult and time-consuming, but Prof. Xu makes it more reasonable.

Amazing professor with great notes. I was not actually in her section but went to her lectures instead of my actual prof.

If Ping Xu has a million fans, then I am one of them. If Ping Xu has ten fans, then I am one of them. If Ping Xu has only one fan then that is me. If Ping Xu has no fans, then that means I am no longer on earth. If the world is against Ping Xu, then I am against the world.

Anyways Ping Xu is the goat. Great written notes, wish I had her for 266.

Ping Xu was excellent at communicating and explaining linear algebra-based content. I had her for MA266 and MA265. I chose to take her for MA265 because I enjoyed her teaching style from MA266. She spent about half of the lecture on examples and half of the lecture on theory, which helped when taking the exams. This helped for taking exams.

I really liked Ping Xu, she was really bright and energetic. She was great at preparing students for the exam, like she would have very clear study guides with examples in the textbook. Fair homework and exams.

Professor Xu is a great lecturer. Attendance was never required but I always went because I thought she was great. The class sucked in general, but that is nothing on Professor Xu. She did an excellent job at explaining the more abstract concepts in Linear Algebra. Lectures are full of examples similar to those found on exams, and her notes are very organized and thorough. Highly recommend her for MA 265.
Her notes are golden!! very helpful and kind, but sometimes talks too fast

Dr. Xu taught excellently. She graded our exams in time to have the scores out on the next day, and shared excellent, organized study materials. Her homework schedule was forgiving as well, with homework sets due the evening of the subsequent lecture. She encourages asking questions, and walks through everything in a easy-to-understand manner.

Professor Xu's lectures are engaging and thorough. The concepts are explained very clearly and the notes are well organized, making them great study tools. Lots of in-class examples are provided and very closely match the types of questions asked on the exams. I highly recommend her as a professor.
Ping xu was very organized and always helped her students when needed. Probably one of the best math professors at Purdue.

I like Professor Xu's lectures. She gives a lot of example problems in her lectures. Additionally, she is very quick to respond to any emails you send. Finally, she creates a very nice study guide for her students before the exams.

Won't disclose anything about exam grades, how much curves will be, etc. Won't curve in-person sections separately from online sections (despite allowing online sections to essentially cheat on every exam with no timer and no confirmation of proctors being legitimate). Really bad communication with students (piazza was closed before final scores).

Do not take her summer classes.

1) Sections not curved separately.

2) Lectures don't have helpful examples. 

3) quizzes and lectures aren't well lined-up. e.g. Quiz 5 on "4.1 material" was actually on 4.2 material. 

4) student responses on piazza are better than lectures lol. 

Fall/Spring probably a lot better.

Very good lectures, sometimes they were too theory heavy but you'll eventually figure out what actually matters. Ping also responds super fast and you receive your exam grades back within a couple days. I had Prof Xu during Spring 2020 semester. She is super nice and great to talk with. Her lectures are very clear and easy to follow. The exams she makes are very fair and definitely help prepare you for the whole course final.

Her tests are a lot more reasonable than the other MA 26500 professors because she adds multiple choice and short answer questions. Very thought-provoking class that thankfully is graded lightly. Lectures can move pretty quick but they're interesting. The workload is really light and you can tell he really cares about his students.

Chill and funny dude. Lots of small but easy assignments and discussions. Unit projects were chill, really enjoyed my time.
Prof. Ballotti is very passionate, very knowledgeable, and very engaging as a professor. I got more out of his class than I thought I would. Would love to take more classes with him.

Prof Dean is very passionate about the subject, and I enjoyed this class more than I thought I would. I learned a lot, and his lectures are very detailed and engaging. Great professor doing his best to teach this class! Very grateful for the time I got to spend with him
Great professor, very passionate and cares about his students. Would take more classes by him, nice guy.

Dr. Ballotti is a very passionate lecturer. He is a great advocate for science literacy! Overall, very easy class and you become more scientifically literate and aware!

One of the best professors Ive had a Purdue. His passion and knowledge on what hes teaching made the class really enjoyable and informative. Even with the entirely online format, the course was well organized and engaging.

One of my favorite professors at Purdue. His lectures were very engaging and I really appreciated how knowledgeable and passionate he was about the subjects. Online classes can be kind of a drag but he really did his best to make it work and I felt that the content/ workload/ expectations for the course were very organized and manageable.
He cares a lot about what he teaches, cheerful old man! Get ready to read a lot.

He's incredibly passionate about what he teaches. His class might be tad difficult, but he posts very comprehensive study guides, so if you study at least a little and actually focus on what he says in lectures (incredibly dense, but just pay attention and be ready to take notes) you'll do fine.
Taught the subject well and was clearly passionate about it. Graded fairly, and although there was a significant amount of work it was not difficult.

With Ballotti you either really like him or you really dont. Personally I think hes great. Hes a big believer in the education before the grade and supporting students where needed. Yes he does go down rabbitholes at time, but only because hes really passionate and knowledgeable about his field. Hes also great with the dad jokes.

Dean was a terrible teacher, nice guy but he can't teach worth a darn. We were assigned to our lab groups so good luck if you get a bad group. All points are based on your lab partner's success. He's the most knowledgeable professor in his field I have ever had. he was horrible. always talked about things we didn't need to know. tests were ridiculous... he would give us F's if you did bad on 2 questions out of alot. he is rude and this class is pointless

Fall 2025. He is passionate for the topics and students, and a super caring professor! Honestly, a real cool guy. Went to his office hours and he really cares about his students.

He's a nice person and all, but lecture slides provide 0 information about the course content. No recorded lectures. Talks super fast making it hard to take notes on everything. Little tough but overall good expierence

Best history Professor I ever had. I honestly love his class. Pretty heavy tests though but he does put a 32% curve on them so it's worth it. Go to the review sessions because that can make a big change for your grade. BTW his initials are O.T.

He helps with the history club and puts on a lot of fun events for the club.

Probably my facorite proffessor ever. He's easy to understand and his HIS 123 is a blast. He's extremely kind to everyone and is an amazing person. Grades fairly and actually helps you learn. I genuinely enjoy his classes and hope to have more of him.
not at all good, route learning
lectures were a nightmare

Overall the professor is good, but you cannot miss any lectures. The slides are messy and do not contain any helpful information. Discussed politics for the whole class once.

The course is hard because there is so much content (Survey of global history) and his slides could be better, but he is a good professor; very knowledgeable, enjoyable to listen to, and wants you to succeed.  Go to the review sessions and study before the tests.

Hard to take notes from as his powerpoints tend to not have any bullets or notes on it and lectures are not recorded online so it's an inconvenience if you miss class, especially considering that all the tests are based solely on the smallest things he says in the lecture. The tests are like trivia games that pick random small things he said from lectures that you probably missed or couldn't write down because he was rambling so fast. Messy lectures. No study guide. The course is graded on tests which cover lecture material, which is purely verbal with no slideshows, no transcripts. Hard to hear at points, talks fast. He speaks very fast in class and the lectures are not recorded. If you miss anything he says, you are done for because he doesnt upload any notes or lecture content. His lectures are just him rambling with no clear emphasis on what is important. Slides show no information outside of pronunciations and maps. He also provides no study material, so it is impossible to predict what material will be tested.

Hes a pretty chil guy, but crams a lot of info in each lecture. attendance is not mandatory, but you're cooked if you dont pay attention to every second. every test and exam is curved, which is amazing.

He talks the entire lecture, even during his examples. Try to listen for his tips as the rest of his work is already in the notes--he just re-does them on the board. Exams are tough but there is partial.

Lectures can get a little boring, but he is clear and good at explaining. He cares about his students, and if you attend class/ office hours, he is extremely helpful. 80% of the class stopped attending after the first few weeks as his lectures are pretty much pointless to attend and doesn't help you a bit for the exams. Exams are nothing like class or HW problems and he is the harshest grader I have ever come across. Avoid at any cost.

The class average for exams are around 45%, so either all of us are idiots or he doesn't know how to teach and makes the class a lot more difficult than it needs to be. You are pretty much on your own in his class, and harshest grader I have ever seen.
His exams are incredibly hard and do not relate to the homework. Also very concept heavy and does not complete problems. Will leave you in the air on how to complete a problem

Took the course with a different professor that was a train wreck and should have withdrawn. Took again with Bobet and got A. If you care, do your work, attend class, visit him during office hrs with questions, this class is very passable.

Talks too fast and doesn't provide decent notes online. He'll say a lot of if's and conditions that are really important but won't write them down. If you miss what he said, didn't understand him, or God forbid miss a class, then tough luck.
His class is helpful, but the class material and homework don't help too much for the exam since the exam is hard

Really nice and very intelligent. Sometimes goes through lectures too fast. Tests were open note/book, but still very hard. TA's office hours were extremely helpful, as he would walk though the entire homework assignment with you. I would've struggled a lot without the TA.

Exams were tough. He really wants students to understand the material. His explanation in step by step problem solving is clear, but homework were almost impossible. His office hour was helpful to solve HW
I thought he did a relatively good job of teaching the course content. Lecture helped me to understand, and the examples were especially helpful. It is a very hard class though.

Absolutely useless as a teacher. Calls students out for not paying attention in a lecture hall of 200+. Really enjoyed the lectures, try to pick a good partner for the groupwork, communication is important on those. Weekly quizzes weren't bad. Never used the textbook and felt like what was talked about in class was enough.
lots of things to do, homework/quiz pretty much every week. but I liked it.

Great prof and lots of fun exercises. course project REQUIRES the use of AI for solving hard strategic problems. you literally work on major corporate decision problems from the current news. cool stuff I doubt I'd learn elsewhere. you are not allowed to use AI for the writeup, and if you do he WILL find out.

This is a course you take to learn something probably no one will teach you. Professor Sariri is an inspiration and he puts massive amount of effort in course design to make it useful. It's a tough course though and takes a lot of time.

This class and professor are not easy. This class will challenge your thinking and the professor will also challenge your thought. This class is not easy, be prepared to work and think hard every class.

Professor Sariri treats this like a masters level course. He expects a lot out of his students and is a harsh grader. It seems like he's still figuring out how to teach the content, and the class felt all over the place.

This is not an easy course but if you are willing to put in the work you'll get a lot out of it. Amir tries to get students to participate in class. His lecture slides aren't copied from a textbook, so lecture is great. Take my word for it and choose a different professor if you can. This guy's expectation is that of master students and doesn't care about how you feel about grades.

Participation matters, the most boring lectures I have experienced for a while which are totally unnecessary for the exams, the exams and the weekly quizzes come only from the book.
The project is tough, but doable. I would never take this class from him because its boring and he doesn't teach a n y t h i n g.

Not a super hard class. Find quizlets to help you study for the weekly proctored quizzes. As a person, he can be kind of rude but I found it challenging and forced me to learn. The group project seems hard but actually turns our to be beneficial for learning. This class doesnt require much of you but when it does you should put in the effort.

Not the greatest prof. Can be very blunt and mean to students when they don't know an answer. Proctored Quizzes every week and Exams are even worse. He's not very good at communicating either. Don't recommend taking this class with him. Should be an A or B but I'm struggling to even pull a C.

Professor Ziviani is an excellent instructor, especially for online students. Professor Ziviani is very knowledgeable and explains theory with real-world examples. Homework is challenging but doable. The course structure can be rough due to changes, and EES is frustrating. However, he listens to student feedback and actively works to improve the course.
Professor Ziviani is a very solid upper level lecturer. He doesn't get lost in theory and frequently grounds his content in reality. Homework assignments take a little while but are reasonable.
Great professor

He is very knowledgeable about thermodynamics and industrial applications. Weekly office hours with TAs are provided, the prof tries to attend if he is not on travel. He is an excellent professor and truly cares for his students.

Professor Ziviani is without a doubt the best professor I have had to date at Purdue in the MSME program. All of the course material, lectures, and references were well put together, and his care for student learning was very evident. He was always reachable for questions. Highly, highly recommended for any class that he teaches.

Get yourself over to one of Ziviani's classes ASAP. This guy is extremely sharp but reigns it in appropriately to get to student's levels and is an anomaly of excellent communication within the engineering field. Final project is hard but grading is holistic and fair. Go to office hours and he will explain things and you will learn from others.

Great professor. Clear schedule, communicates well, and has many office hours. Very open to answering questions and explaining concepts. Lectures were well composed and organized, but can run on a 
 little bit. Tests ranged in difficulty, but were more or less reasonable to material. He's very down to earth and frequently cracked jokes.

You won't learn much in the class. Homework is not mandatory, and final grade is based mainly on exams. The questions he writes are very confusing because the wording isn't clear. He is a boring lecturer. The lectures lack an introduction that explains the purpose of the upcoming concepts, and his tone is boring.

His go to introduction is the weather. Every time...Can you be interesting?

He is available for questions but often will respond condescendingly, and can never admit that his instruction was confusing.

Easy A.

Amazing professor. He is extremely knowledgeable about refrigeration and is able to convey concepts clearly to students. He is very flexible, humorous, and relatable, making this class very comfortable for even students who are not strong in the subject beforehand.

If you had a chance to do a course under this professor then go for it. He is wide knowledge on thermal related fields and he is the boss for HVAC related subjects. You will never be bored to listen to his lecture. The course is designed systematically and contains a lot of content related to the subject. He cares about his students and helps them

Professor Ziviani really cares about how you do in this class. He runs the review lectures before the exam and is accessible at almost any time. He has never said no to giving an extension and always tries to answer questions in the slack chat in a way that actually helps you.
She brought a lot of real world examples and practical assignments. Also, amazing personality! She clearly cares about the class and about her students.

Gibelato clearly cares about the material and students. She is fun and engaging, while also giving very clear expectations that are realistic. I geniunely enjoyed her class (sort of a first for me, I usually don't care much) and honestly wish I could take another class with her.
She was the best professor I had! I loved how sweet and caring she is. Also, the Fridays recap were super helpful.

Professor Gibelato had an extremely upbeat teaching style, no matter the time of the class, which I heavily appreciated. However, at times the class was disorganized, and the professor rarely responds to emails. Once Professor Gibelato adjusts further to the Purdue curriculum, I believe she'll do just fine.

You could do all the work and still receive a bad grade. She was very difficult to work with and did not fairly grade the labs. She's bad with responding to emails, so you will need to go to office hours.

Dr. G has a bubbly personality and makes class very engaging. However, she is slow to respond to emails, and instructions for assignments were unclear. Dr. Gibelato's OBHR 330 class was one of my favorite classes this semester. She makes the class really interactive and fun. Take this class!

I enjoyed having Dr. G for OBHR330. You can tell she genuinely cares about her students and the content she is teaching. She is very upbeat and tries to make class as fun as possible. She is also available for questions outside of class if you are ever cofused. As long as you show up and do the work, you will get an A.

She is an amazing professor all of the semester and says she cares till the end of the class. She made the grade offs to be absolutely BS where you can get a 94% in the class and still get a B. She doesn't make this clear from the start of the class either. Her syllabus was very vague and not even put up till more than halfway through the sem

This class was the easiest class ever. Although the course was the most disorganized class ever, I'm not complaining since it was so easy. Take OBHR330 with her, I've heard other professors are difficult. Exams are easy, labs are easy, and she'll basically give you an A if you ask her nicely.

Professor Gibelato is easily one of my favorite professors I've had. She's so caring and wants all her students to succeed. If you feel you're not understanding certain content, she's so accessible outside of class and is so welcoming. If you show up and do the work, you'll get a good grade.
She prepares you well for the exams and is very caring. Gets grades back quickly and also gives out candy before exams which is nice.
Very Good Professor!
She's chill

Nour was a great professor. She does everything to set up her students for success. GO TO HER REVIEW SESSIONS! They will tell you everything you need to know about the exams. The lectures were extremely helpful, she had slides that explained the content and had example problems. She always made sure to let people ask questions. The professor made difficult concepts easier to understand and had clear slides for each topic. She responds to emails quickly and cares about student success.

Clear lecturer and good slides. Some concepts were harder to grasp (especially towards the end), but she did a good job of going over example problems and preparing us for tests. Graded quickly and was very responsive to emails.

Professor Sedunova is easily one of the best professors I've had in all 4 years at Purdue. She's the goat, really funny, genuinely enjoyed going to lecture and it was easy to follow. Really patient and kind.
Goated professor. Cares a lot about students.

She posts practice exams before every exam and they are very similar. It is always very clear what you're expected to know for every exam. The homework is quite long but not too difficult. Good lecturer.

Amazing professor, if you can take 351 with her, 100% do it. Always willing to help during office hours, very caring, and no attendance credit. The study problems will be a great representation of the exam. Very grateful I had her.

She is a wonderful professor who explains concepts clearly, and she always made sure that we understood parts of the lesson. She posts detailed notes and homework reviews on brightspace too. The homework was reasonable and exams matched the homework and practice problems, so they felt fair and reflected what was practiced.

Prof. Sedunova is amazing! While her lectures and lecture notes may sometimes be hard to understand (as it can be a little bit messy), she assigns and homework and practice exams very similar to the midterms and final and it really helps you understand the content if you do them properly. She also grades very nicely and offers extra help if needed!

Avoid at all costs, worst prof to teach mgmt 411. i would repeat an entire year of college if that meant not taking him. this class wasn't bad at all until the final exam which is 50% of your total grade, 60% if you missed an online exam throughout the semester. He was an okay lecturer but his course was pretty crypto-focused. I didn't feel like he covered the overall subject of investment management very thoroughly. Professor Liu is a decent teacher, the content of this course is pretty hard, and it is up to you to keep up with him and read class materials. Professor Liu is the best professor I have ever had at Purdue! The content could be challenging but he did an outstanding job explaining the materials! The exams were straightforward so never give up!

The most confusing Management class ever. He only reads his slides and make the lectures very boring. The final practice test has no help at all. If you really want to learn the course, just avoid him.

I dont even know what I learned in this class. I have no idea how it relates to investments. He made his lectures way too involved and I never knew what was relevant. His exams are all free response and only 10 questions. The only thing good about the class was that he was an easy grader.
He is lowkey the GOAT
Professor Austin McDonald is an outstanding lecturer. He does a great job putting himself in the students' perspective, making his lectures engaging and easy to follow.
Makes funny jokes during class. Great lectures, very organized, always willing to help outside of class. Overall great professor and person, would heavily recommend!

Great lecturer and easy to stay engaged in class. Actually cares about stutents understanding and teaches the way he would have liked to be taught when he was taking the classes. Also gives out and goes over past exams that are the exact same as the real test. The best!
He Lowkey is the GOAT , he cares about his students , overall he is a G (take his class brodie)

Dr. Norris was fantastic! I took the group communication class which did involve a lot of group work, but she also teaches public communication without that. She was super kind and understanding. Awesome teacher very helpful

Great teacher!

There's 2 halves of this class. You're supposed to build off the first, but Jacko & Friends switched our design so everyone had to rush the back half to present and report on a whole new design. Everyone's presentation had different focus points (Example, Some had a drainage plan, others didn't) but overall you will be graded fairly

A or B is guaranteed as long as your team has the project built together. He deducts a percent from your grade for every class you miss with no drops so make sure to email him if any circumstances occur. We have to present at a place that's about15min drive away from Purdue so you'll need a car/ride. Teamwork is the most important for this class.
Possibly the worst professor ive ever had

I had big expectations for this senior design project but it really is just throw together something presentable and Jacko will let you pass. He did no do enough to guide us and we had little experience so nobody really came up with anything too serious, ready to build. Engineers in the real world do not submit a 25 page essay as a building design.

I think my experience was much different because this class was online, but in general, Prof. Jacko is a very fun and caring guy. Many of his lectures were a bit unnecessary and I wish he had taught more technical material, but this class was easy and expectations were clear.

She is one of the only professors in the Data Visualization or Web Programming majors that actually cares about her students' learning. She teaches you principles of design, ethics, good practices, etc. that is severely lacking in other courses. Only downside is that she's an easy grader. If you make an attempt, you get a 100

Very kind, and answers emails very quick. Not a tough grader at all and willing to let you redo assignments. Lots of extra credit. Sometimes she doesn't understand questions, but other than that best professor I've had!

This class was a snooze fest. Honestly easy A despite some of her quizzes being tricky because she grades rly easy you literally just have to do the work. The hardest part about this class was sitting through the 2 hr lectures at 7:30am while she read directly off the slides in a monotone voice. She does give a lot of extra credit though.

Very well organized lectures and a very nice professor. Sometimes it felt like she did not understand some of the questions asked and would kind of brush it off. Her email response time is one of the best out of all professors. There was a large amount of extra credit given for participation.

Very strict CS course and not beginner-friendly at all. I came in with no coding experience and felt behind the entire class from day one. Exams feel designed to trip you up with none of the above on every question. Lab groups are assigned, and you can't use outside resources or even ask classmates for help. His lectures are insanely boring.
Great professor, has really interesting lectures.

If you have to take CS159, take it with Crum. He is the best for this because of his experience, and he goes very slowly over all the content. The class is not an easy A. So make sure to pay attention and talk to Crum about your problems. Crum is very understanding, and his lectures are great. There is no point in complaining about course material.

I would highly recommend taking Professor Crum for CS 159 as his lectures are very well organized and he explains the content clearly. He cares about his students and makes it easy to meet outside of class. He also writes the tests so what he teaches is most likely on the exam.

The course syllabus and exam content is tough on students. But it is not due to Professor. Crum makes it easy to understand. Even if someone takes a different professor, the finals are going to be the same. There is no point bring frustrated towards Crum when he tries his best.

He is very approachable and understanding, and explains concepts at a pace that makes the material easy to follow. His teaching style helps make the content more manageable if you stay engaged in class. He is also willing to provide help outside of scheduled office hours. I would recommend taking this course with Crum.
Make sure you MEMORIZE ALL THE COURSE STANDARDS.

Horrible teacher. Exams had me on all fours especially with the MCQ as there was always the option for “none of the above”. Lectures are extremely content heavy and Crum teaches until the last second and CRAMS all the content he can. He has the lecture notes packet but never follows it so it gets confusing. Get another teacher🙏🏻🙏🏻

Iclicker is used for lecture and they have a TA seeing if your in assigned lecture seat so you cant cheat the iclicker and is really annoying if you have to get up if u have the 8:30 lecture time. All sections do this. Lecture is pointless, labs and homework are easy but most of ur grade is exams.

Entire class is designed to trick you and waste your time but getting a good grade is very easy. It may be more difficult if you don't have prior coding experience since the lectures are terribly boring, and he explains coding in a confusing way.

Obviously, the exams were awful and designed to trick students. Make sure to take some practice tests so you are prepared for that and pay close attention the the wording of questions. Prof Crum seemed like a nice guy and was a good lecturer. The labs can be awful if you get a bad group. You need the course notebook but not the textbook.

Easily the worst class I have ever taken. Lectures never touched on the difficulty of material on the labs and HW's Exams are ridiculously hard. Assignments are graded very strictly with so many point lost for the smallest mistakes. Class is literally designed to discourage people from asking for help. Very hard to do well without prior experience.

CS159 is not a well organized course. It's supposed to be entry level programming but beginner progammers will stuggle a little in the class. Crum knows his material very well and does a fine job teaching it. Exams are very difficult (use BoilerExams to study) and lab quizzes are impossible if you have lab before your second lecture.

Homework and labs aren't that bad, just start early. Lectures are nothing crazy but Crum explains stuff well and as long as you go and pay attention, you'll get most of the info. Exams have you read over code and find the output as well as answer logic questions which sometimes try and trick you which is slightly annoying. Use BoilerExams as well.
As a professor, he is good, does a good job explaining things and answering questions. As the course coordinator, he's nothing short of awful.

Honestly I learned more from the labs than the lectures, but lectures were free points so I went. Crum is a fine lecturer, and answers questions pretty well, although anytime other than lecture you'll probably have to ask a TA.

Crum teaches CS 159 well, even for those new to programming. The course itself has some tedious HWs and confusingly worded lab quiz questions, but Prof. Crum's lectures cover the content well and he is open to answering students' questions after lecture. He shows many example questions in lecture which is very helpful for HWs/labs.

I think Crum gets a bad rap on here. I think he is really personable and eager to help, and I liked his class. One tip for the course is to make sure you start the homework early and don't wait for the weekend before it's due to start. Also, use boiler exams to study, and make sure you watch the videos.
Crum is a decent professor. His lectures are very boring, but he explains everything clearly. The course itself sucks, and he designed it but if you look at his actual teaching he is pretty good.

CS 159 exams do not test your ability to program in C, they test your ability to interpret intentionally convoluted logic. Crum does little to guide students toward success, but enough practice on boilerexams can make up for testing struggles. His lectures aren't particularly engaging or insightful, but I'd recommend attending for the bonus points.

CS159 aside, Crum is a very good professor. The course itself is pretty meh but there are a lot worse structured classes you could encounter (like chm 116) so its relatively bearable. Crum himself is a good lecturer however, and likely the best you'll get for this class. He knows what he is teaching and aside from not being super engaging he's good

The whole CS 159 course kind of sucks, but Crum is probably your best bet. Lab quizzes are impossible, but failing them isn't too consequential on the final grade. Through the class he appeared to be very indecisive with assignments, and exams, having cancelled the second midterm, only to uncancel it the week before the scheduled midterm date. In addition to this he has poor communication with TAs and students.

Lectures:
The lectures were absolutely pointless, basically just paragraphs taken from the textbook.

Homeworks:
Absolutely pointless, just using applications and never actually solving problems or preparing you.

Tests:
The tests were easy, but just focused mainly on the first 6 lectures.

I would recommend that you never take this class ever

The most disrespectful professor I've ever had. It's clear he knows the subject but mocks people who ask questions. Argued with students weekly because his inflated ego doesn't allow him to take criticism (literally called someone Putin for disagreeing with him). His lecture slides are awful. 

Avoid Gore at all costs. Take Naik instead

Was often rude to students who asked questions or did not know the answer to a question. Grade in course is based 85% on tests. Would often go on tangents that were not a part of coursework. Never worked out example problems fully. When class was about to finish, he would just race to finish the presentation slides by just reading off of them.
Very bad professor with bad slides, and disrespectful towards students. I recommend you take Naik.

One of the rudest professors I have ever had. His lectures and his style of teaching are so bad that I can't even understand what he's talking about most of the time. Every class he makes rude comments at students for asking questions. He is never ready at class start time and often faces technical difficulties logging into the computer.

Professor Gore is clearly passionate about his subject, but he is a pretty crappy teacher. You can tell he's a decent person, but that is overwhelmed by the fact that he tends to snark on students for asking Piazza questions, or any questions.

This person is a deep ultra-violet radiation when it comes to course policy. He changes policy almost every week. He will make sure your life turns out to be extra miserable during his course. No derivations, no useful explanation, and extreme ego. Pointing out his mistakes often gives a reply, "You got such a nice professor!"

His lectures are nothing but with example problems on powerpoint slides, however it won't help you understand what's going on because he seems like he'd rather not show them by his unenthusiastic tone of voice and rushed explanations. He will claim that the key to success is participation but it rarely works and only leads to uneccesary tangents

Gore is quite possibly the worst professor Ive ever seen. When I went to his OH, I made a basic algebra mistake. After I corrected it, he made a big deal out of it even though I understood the problem (he said what if I make that kind of mistake as a professor, what??). He was very incoherent, and asked questions that made no sense to guide me.

Lectures were dull, off topic, and not helpful. He assigned a project with no guidance throughout and seemingly changing requirements every couple weeks and then he didn't even seem to read it for the final grade. I would advise taking this course with anyone else. These issues also seem to be consistent across every year he has taught this.

I highly recommend taking whatever course is being offered by Dr. Gore with someone else. If it's not possible, attend another lecture section. If you're taking 525, good luck. Dr. Gore's lectures fail to convey the material effectively and his feedback for his final project was non-existent throughout the semester. I wish Dr. Naik had taught 525.
Always off topic. doesn't cover all notes in time, so be prepared to read the textbook. The only helpful lectures happened when he was substituted by another professor.
Constantly off topic, rarely gets through any substantial amount of the lecture in class. Uninteresting and irrelevant tangents are frequent.
Gets off topic frequently. Usually only covers about a third of the notes he needs to cover each lecture period.

His teaching methods are so lacking that I eventually have up entirely on lectures and resorted to teaching myself to pass this course. He's pathetic. Also, his grading standards and requirements for the course shift at his whim throughout the semester. He added work to our project after the final exam! Really who does that?

Awful.  Never take a class with Gore if at all possible.  70% of his thermo section failed last year because he's a semi-knowledgeable person who has absolutely no clue how to teach.  Don't waste your time.

Willing to help students but not very good at explaining concepts. Moves way too fast through examples and doesn't seem too concerned about whether or not the students understand the concept. Should focus more energy on effectively teaching instead of trying to be funny.

terrific. smashing. recommended. can be a bit funny in class, and was one of the classes i enjoyed. material is tough, but he's a nice lad. always willing to help students and explains well. DO NOT go 25mins late to class or you get a nice stare

Probably the most caring professor I have ever had, he learned all of our names less than week. Charlie is the definition of inspiring and has helped me build so much confidence in my public speaking skills. I will never forget this class, he clearly loves his job and talking with his students. Love running into him on campus all the time!

Charlie is a great professor! He is so kind and friendly and as he always says, what he wants most is for his students to succeed. I've never had any sort of teacher learn their student's names so quickly, especially considering there were 20 people in the class. And as long as you attend class and submit your assignments, you'll get an A.

Charlie is by far the best teacher I have ever had. Not only does he make an english SPEAKING class fun, but the books he chose for us, the assignments, the videos we watch in class, and his whole demeanor is just so inspirational and interesting and it feels like you're truly learning for the first time. He is someone you will never forget.

It is clear how much Charlie not only loves his job, but loves other people. He showed genuine care for his students and clearly wanted everyone to succeed. My favorite moment was the line of students after our last lecture lining up to give him a hug, or just thank him. If you have to take SCLA102, take it with Charlie.

This is the first time ive really cared about an english/lit class in a long time. Charlie is inspirational and really cares about his students not only in his class, but going forward. Hes the type that will say hi if he sees you 2 years after you had him. Im bad at public speech, but hes a forgiving grader and gave me Bs on all my speeches.
Charlie is an incredible professor with genuine passion not just for what he teaches but for life in general

Professor Charlie has been the most passionate professor I have had at Purdue so far. It is clear that he cares about his work and the impact on students. He does a great job of bringing the real world into the classroom for deeper learning.

I wish that I could select more tags. This man is so kind and incredibly inspirational. I truly have learned so much about myself in his class. Every assignment is intentional and reasonable. I would recommend him to anyone and if you have to take a writing requirement, take him if you can! You won't regret it.
Professor Catalano is the most genuine, caring professor I have ever had. He truly wants the best for all his students, and oriented his course to help us find our own driving motivation.

Charlie has so much care and compassion for his students and wants each one of them to feel valued and gain something from the course, his course content is nuanced and interesting with semester themes that actually make the readings fun, and presentations are outlined very well. Outstanding teacher and amazing person
SCLA 102 entirely depends on your professor and Charlie is the reason I loved this class. He is so kind and truly cares about his students. If you can, take his class

Dr. Catalano is an amazing professor! He makes SCLA 102 fun, and classes have very high attendance due to this. The workload is very fair and many extra credit opportunities are offered throughout the semester. Highly recommend Dr. Catalano for this course.

Charlie was an incredible professor. Within the first week he learned all of the student's names and truly cared about our growth through the semester. All of the assignments had appropriate deadlines and were easy to complete. He helps students through speech anxiety and wants to inspire everyone daily. Books were cheap, and many extra options.

Very kind and caring professor and is very inspirational. His class is accessible, textbooks are very affordable (99 cents on kindle!) and the content is very easy to understand and complete. This class did not take much time out of my week. He is very understanding of anxiety during speeches and makes the room a calming place without judgement.

Charlie has to be hands down one of the best professors I have ever had. He works so hard to make sure his students feel supported, and is always cheering us on. I have never looked forward to going to a class more. One of the most caring professors in the world. I recommend you take a class by Charlie any chance you get!

Literally one of the best teachers let alone professors Ive ever had. We all cried on the last day of class. Super helpful and friendly and cares a lot about his students. His classes are an easy A so try and have fun!
One of the best profs I have met in Purdue!! We love him

One of the most caring professors I'll ever have. Gives instruction and feedback very effectively and makes his assignments actually useful. Makes a personal connection with students and treated us like equals. His class was an amazing change of pace from difficult lecture based classes where the professors don't care. 10/10 would take again.

One of the best professors I had. I was worried when I saw 102 was a speaking class as that is something I hate and actively avoid. However, he was the nicest and most caring person and would encourage you to try your best. His class was the highlight of my day and I wish I could take one of his courses again. A true heart of gold.

Literally the best professor I have ever had and it's his first year teaching. You need to take a class with Charlie. He's amazing and makes you feel welcomed and loved. He's one of those professors that calls students his children and that just goes to say that he really cares about his students. My whole class loved Charlie

Even though he isn't the greatest, he's definitely eons ahead of Krusemark. He knows that bchem is a hard course, and tries his best to teach these difficult pathways and concepts as straightforward as possible. Also very accessible (actually got angry at my class for not getting help from him), so don't be afraid to get help

He is a very nice guy, he is just teaching very difficult material. Super sweet and really really cares about his students. Has plenty and plenty of office hour opportunities and makes himself available to his students. Despite the difficult amount and level of information, I enjoyed his lectures.
He is very funny and cares about his students' success. Biochem is a hard class, but he tries to make sure you understand the concepts and is very accessible outside of class.

lectures are very easy to understand. you will leave lecture feeling like you understand what is going on instead of studying for hours trying to figure out what the bullet points are saying. very concise and engaging

yes you have to put in a lot of work in and study a lot, but the more work you put in, the better results you'll get with his lectures! i learned so much from hudmon and he made me excited about biochem. he cares so much about our learning and explains very well by breaking topics down into digestible materials.

He made this class bearable, his lectures had structure and had enough to keep you focused while not overwhelmed. His tests come directly out of class material and makes efforts to help students out on his free time.

Would take again, wish he taught the majority of class as well.

Dr. Hudmon is the one redeemable factor in this mess of a class. He works hard to make sure we are understanding his material, which can be difficult at times. However, his exams are extremely fair in comparison to Krusemark's and he pulls directly from the slides. Exam review sessions are helpful, given he actually answers questions unlike others.

Dr. Hudmon is amazing and picked up the slack for his co-lecturer Dr. Krusemark. Extremely resourceful and caring about students understanding material. His content is tough, but thats just Biochem, he did all he could to ensure we understood and were successful.

Hudmon seems to really care about his students and explains lecture in way for us to understand. His exam questions come straight from the slides or iClicker questions. He emphasized important information that we needed to know (like for homework or exams). He rarely fell behind in slides and if he ever did, he made extra videos to compensate.

Dr. Hudmon is the only reason this class is bearable. His lectures are much easier to follow and he takes time to ensure we are understanding the material. Exam content is pulled straight from the slides and he will even recycle past exam questions. If he was the course coordinator rather than Krusemark, student performance would be way better.

Dr. Hudmon is SO much better than Dr. Krusemark. Don't be discouraged by the horror that is exam one, once you reach Hudmon's content things become a lot more clear. Dr. Hudmon is extremely knowledgeable and seems to care a lot more about his impact on students, he will tell you the key takeaways you need to know for the exams and future courses.

Was WAY better than Krusemark when teaching MCMP 208. His slides were definitely hard to follow, but if you went to lecture and paid attention/took notes, they made sense. His exam questions were a lot more straightforward and everything was pulled from the slides (as it should be)!

I am the type of student who studies so hard and maintains a 4.0 til my junior year. Don't take this class plz. The professor doesn't know what he is talking about and speed-reads all his slides. There are around 400 slides you need to memorize per exam and a total of 1200 slides for your cumulative final. VERY DETAILED exams too.

It was disheartening to see Hudmon misjudging students' talents. 
What concerned me even more was his lack of support when students faced an abusive environment under other PIs in different labs. His failure to stand up for his students during such distressing situations demonstrates a lack of integrity.

This class is nothing but a train wreck and a professor who refuses to upload answer keys for study guides does nothing but make it worse. I can't express my distain for this class and professor in words.
He is a funny man and cares about students. If you communicate with him, he will always help you. If you have questions, don't hesitate to ask him.

Biochem is one of the hardest classes I have taken. Dr. Hudmon is very nice for students who want to know more and ask questions, go to his office hour and he will explain things clearly to you. The exam is hard and needs a lot of time to study.

Most of the class is spent expressing his opinion. I thought he would get better in a few weeks, but as the class progresses, he is expressing his opinion more and more. The test was also based on his opinion. Mentoring students is the worst part, and he, ignoring the facts, is ending g graduates students' lives again with his opinions.
TNV is a fantastic professor. His lectures are really good, make sure you go.
Expect to commit a full-time job equivalent to rereading and relearning outside of class due to the fast pace.
Project had very unclear guidelines. Practice exams and questions were hardly given.

Very poorly organized course. Lack of coordination among the staff made assignments and expectations confusing. Exams did not align well with the lecture content citing creative thinking but not really true.
this was clearly a very poorly organized class. the professor would be changing submission criteria hours before the projects were due and expecting us to read his mind the entire class.

565 is meant to be a recap of 437 plus very few additional things in terms of material, with the main addition being teaching gem5 which is very important for architecture. Instead gem5 is not taught at all and is expected to be known with zero help and exams are about whether you can guess what “comes to mind” for the professor.

Perhaps the teacher at Purdue that cares most about you learning content. He is as hard as people say, the lab is as time consuming as people say, and oh god his exams. That being said, I could not recommend this class more; it single-handedly got me employed. Show up, put in the work, and it will be your favorite class. Absolute rite of passage.

Lectures are absolutely not optional, no matter what anyone says. You will not do very well if you don't attend them. His lectures are fantastic, but his exams are extremely difficult. That being said, if you take the time to do well in lab, you should do well in the course. The lab is exactly as time consuming as everyone says.

Professor Vijaykumar's passion for the topic clearly shows and he's a great lecturer. Lectures are not mandatory for this class but you are unlikely to do well if you don't attend and pay attention. Exams are tough and place a strong focus on understanding the concepts rather than memorization. Would highly recommend this class to any ECE major!

He is a really good lecturer and knows well of computer architecture. This class is hard but if you need help, Ta and him are very approachable. 
Very good class!! Must Take at purdue

437 is known as a beast for a reason. Vijay is engaging and lively as a professor and prepares you for the labs pretty well and stays ahead of schedule. The class was run very well online, maybe even better than when in person, so that was a plus. Be sure to pick a good partner and stay on top of things. Exams were tough, but that's not a surprise.

Prof. Vijaykumar changed my perspective on what I want to do with my life in one semester. The class is by no means easy, but it is the best class I've taken at Purdue. He's amazing in lectures, helpful if you show efforts, and exams that will bust your balls if you don't have the foundational concepts  nailed down

Amazing professor! I actually got up to take the bus to make it to his 8:30am class every MWF. Lectures made it way easier to get a handle on what was needed to be done in the labs. His explanations are clear and exams can be hard. He will make you think of things you've not explicitly learned about, but know enough to formulate an answer.

BEST professor for this class hands down. If you want to learn and put in the effort then you will get a lot out of this class. Otherwise you will always be at the risk of failing. Do the homework and study concepts for the tests. The tests always have design questions that test how well you can implement a new design.

BEST computer architecture professor at Purdue!! His lectures are so clear, it rarely leaves any room for doubts. You can feel his enthusiasm and energy literally radiating out while he teaches. Takeaway: Never miss lectures! His exams are tough and focus on currentpast research, so it would be wise to  read books, articles, etc in preparation.

Possibly the best computer architecture professor at Purdue. Really engaged the interest in architecture in all students, irrespective of interest. Exams are challenging but makes up for it with his excellent lectures. Definitely a joy to have as a professor!

Possibly my favorite professor at Purdue.  Does an excellent job at teaching 437 and engaging the class.  Promise you won't fall asleep.  He goes through the material fast though.  Exams can sometimes be tough but always fair

Since the class was handed off by Meyer, the organization of 362 has suffered. Lecture Notes are posted after the lecture (despite promises for before). Labs are posted Saturday mornings and are due for some students on Tues morning. Vijaykumar is a decent lecturer (reads from the notes), but needs to get the lab under control.

Was a little apprehensive about taking 495k, but I am so glad I did. Prof. Vijaykumar explains the material incredibly well and makes it so interesting to learn. I really enjoy this class so much. Really the only teacher I like here at Purdue.

Rickert is a very laid back professor. He's extremely helpful and insightful, and his feedback is easy to understand. He does take a little long to get around to students during workshop days, but other than that he did a good job of helping the class out and his lectures clearly improved the performance of everyone in the class.

Professor Rickert was very engaged in the material he taught. Sometimes, his tone and the material became tedious, but he did his best to keep the class engaged. He graded easily on the exams, papers, and homework due in class, so it was an easy A.

Not a great lecturer, but he's fun. If you have good classmates, you'll be fine. Lot's of papers throughout the semester and a lot of reading. He gave the last test 2 days before Thanksgiving which meant I had to delay going home for break. He's a very easy grader, but your grade is determined by 8 papers and 2 tests.

Favorite professor I've had. Super fun and a made the class very interesting. Wish he taught more classes! EVERYTHING is worth 10% of your grade (including tests) and he's a super easy grader so as long as you do everything you're fine. There are group presentations every other week or so as well so just keep that in mind. Great guy!

Laidback and chill, very understanding about deadlines and such. Clear with his expectations with what reviews should look like, and requires weekly reviews to be written. Awful at reading and responding to emails though, better just find him in person if you have questions.
Good professor, but he does have a specific style of writing that he likes.
Very unhelpful.
I really liked this class and the focus it had on different kinds of writing than the standard stuff, like advertising. I learned a lot, and class was always interesting.
Awesome!
Shamefully spiteful to those who don't agree with him or flatter him.
Knocks it out of the park!
No Comments

No Comments
No Comments
yeah whoa what a great guy
ayyyyy they not like us take my boy
Great easy class, great prof, definitely choose in person section. Textbook required. Take with friends!

Took class fall semester of senior year. It was during Covid so the class was virtual for me which kind of stunk but made it crazy easy. You can find answers to any assignment online and even if you do terrible on your wine video you still get full credit. Lectures are interesting just super long. Cheers
A great professor, he was really fun and wine appreciation was a good class. It was asynchronous online so you didn't have to complete anything until finals week if you didn't want to.

Good professor, known mostly for his wine appreciation class. Concentrated too much on the history and background of different wine regions, a lot of theory. Did not teach how to identify flavours or aromas during actual tasting. Still a great course, helpful in real life.

You get 3 different wines, cheese and bread at every class. The lecture is pretty boring though. Bring homework. Good class, but take it pass/fail if you can. Very detailed exam. Great to take with friends!

This professor is pretty much the king of wine. He knows every detail of every piece of wine information possible, and will teach you ALL OF IT. The class wasn't as easy as it seems as you have to know over 700 slides of information per exam. Exams are 80% of your overall grade, along with a wine visit and class serving teams. (20%) Overall not bad

This class is not necessarily an easy A. You have to memorize a lot of inane details for the tests. First half of semester is kind of fun. Second half is insanely boring. Take HW to do during lecture.
Pretty easy grader and super nice lady.
She is very good
She is good

She is a good professor and is very thoughtful about her materials for the semester and her group activities. The rubrics are clear to an extent, I recommend going to office hours if you really wish to do well. My one critique is she does not get back to students via email and is a bit difficult to get a hold of when not in front of her.

Really fun and nice teacher. Her group activities are very creative and probably the most fun English I've ever taken. Only thing that I would say is she's not the best at getting back to emails, but if you talk to her in person everything gets worked out.
Overall very enjoyable class and professor. A lot of reading and the reading is decently tough but hw assignments are easy and in-class assignments are actually fun. Would definitely recommend.

Dr. Wedow is a great professor. Lots of reading with one page reflections due almost every class period, but you can skim and be fine. There were in-class group activites once every 2 weeks. Wrote 2 papers and had 2 group projects. Lectures about the books were boring at times, but not too bad. Overall, I would recommend her as a professor.

her class is easy, and she is more like a high school English teacher in the way that she is more relaxed than others. we only did two actual papers, and they weren't bad. you have one page reading reflections that aren't serious and are more about your thoughts on what you read. she is a kind, calm professor that loves what she does.
The grading is easy
I did the required readings most of the time and I wrote almost all of my papers 2-3 days before the deadline and I was fine. She is a great professor as long as you don't slack off too much.

Difficult class. He is not very helpful outside of class, even during office hour. He would just simply tell you he can't answer your question and go over the slides. He is very unclear about the class content, very difficult to understand the material by the slides.

Professor Diao really just doesn't care about his students. Really didn't learn anything either, he reads straight off the lecture slides. Even though attendance is required I stopped going to lecture because its so dull. He will give you zero sympathy over anything, don't bother asking. Avoid if at all possible.

His lectures are impossible to follow along with, and the only reason I was going was for attendance. I feel as though I didn't learn anything from the class. He doesn't answer questions very well either.
He will give you NO sympathy so don't ask for any. Absolutely no late homework. Also lab attendance is worth 3x more than lecture for some reason

MFET 361-In contention for the most boring and useless class of my life. Reads directly off slides with 0 external input. Strict attendance. Long hw and labs. 0 learning in the class, all assignments are semi-completed fill in the blank, 0 way to know if correct unless he mercifully puts a "Sanity Check" comment for what the output should be (rare)

A strange class to take. The entire course material reads like a work instruction from a shop floor with no intention to teach (go to this button - go to that button - click this to do this). Would recommend staying home and just do your excessively long hw from the lecture slides, which btw are on the level of "where is the insert button in word".

Um.... what was this class even? Professor Diao doesn't care about his students much. He doesn't really understand the tech, and the course material teaches us nothing. You're better off reading the lecture slides, and using them to complete the 50 question homeworks (not joking, 50 questions). It's all rote memory. Lectures are useless.

I think this class has not been too bad when compared to what my friends have said about the previous semester. Definitely if you attend classes and do homework you can understand the content pretty easily. Exams were similar to practice problems and homework. Overall, I would say this class isn't too hard hopefully I get an A.

Maybe other years were different but I really enjoyed learning from Pablo. He is engaged and very knowledgeable. Answers students questions in depth and appreciates his students. Exam 1 had a ridiculous amount of extra credit and was very reflective of the homework assignments which are worth very little towards your grade. Mileage with TA may vary

Make sure to go to class since iClickers are also graded. Exam 1 was mostly theory based and had no numbers. Exam 2 was bit better since it was similar to the HWs. Make sure you remember the details from lecture slides & labs. He does not curve the class btw but he gives many extra credit questions in the exams. You can still pass though.

Does not respond to emails, even important ones, because that's what TA's are for I guess. TAs always had different solutions than each other, so Homework solutions were always wrong. Exam 1 grading was harsh since this is a HEAVY theory based class (no numbers on exam 1). Exam 2 extremely lighter. Lots of extra credit offered during exams.

Impressively awful. No concept of how to keep pace with content, by week 14 he was ~6 weeks behind and a TA took over to speedrun through the rest. wouldve loved to learn about concrete, asphalt or cement in my materials class but not this year. Seems that it will still be on the final. Cool. Labs dont wait for lecture content, good luck with that.

Probably the best pre-pharmacy professor here. She teaches immunology with ease and explicitly states when concepts being taught will be mentioned on exams. She's also so enjoyable to listen to, and there were multiple lectures where she had everyone laughing. Loved Dr. O'Brien downnn, wish she taught the entire class and not just half of it :(

Dr O'Brien is the best professor I've had so far! She is amazing at explaining difficult concepts and very clear on her expectations for this class. Her assignments have a lot of thought behind them and you can tell she truly cares about her students. Her exams are also very fair and a lot of it is what she emphasizes on in class

Dr. O'Brien was the BEST professor that I have had during my time so far at Purdue. Her lectures cover a lot of content in a short period of time, but she makes it clear when concepts mentioned will be on exams, letting you know exactly what to study. She is very knowledgeable and it is clear that she genuinely cares about her students' learning.

She is a very concise lecturer and makes difficult concepts easier to understand. The tests are based directly on the lecture. It's definitely possible to get an A if you memorize the lectures and pay attention in class.

Great lecturer, probably the best in BSPS or pre-pharm. Assignments helpful for studying if you put in effort and don't use AI, and she creates tests that are based entirely off lectures. It is a hard class, but possible to get an A/B if you follow along. Lectures are fine to watch on your own, but don't get behind because it's a lot of content.

Came in to substitute for CHE320 for Professor Thompson and did an excellent job picking up where he left off and following the course material. He did teach us extra things that was a little confusing but not many complaints there. Did not like how he flickered the lights walking into the lecture hall every time.

He clearly understands what he teaches and is willing to format it in many ways to help students further. The content can be hard sometimes but he's good at keeping the class engaged and making the material digestible. He can be a little harsh when disappointed but overall good professor.

The exams are extremely challenging given the time limit. He mentions that he can do them in 10-20 minutes, but that doesn't account for his students who 1. had never seen the material before before this class and 2. didn't write the test so are not immediately sure how to solve the problems. He is very smart.

Really great first time professor. Class is hard however he teaches it in a way that is understandable for us ABEs. Ristroph creates a welcoming environment and tries to keep students entertained even when subject matter is dull.

Despite the difficulty, this class is very reasonable and Ristroph is a fair grader. The homework could be challenging but beneficial. At times, he came off as frustrated with the class which was alarming to some students, however I think he's very intelligent and a great resource for ABE students to have. Having a lab forced collaboration as well.

Had him for both 161 and 162. He was really helpful when I was confused about how to do something for the projects or in class examples, and explained the content pretty well. Highly recommend him as a professor.
You want him instead of Whalen. He is pretty funny, keeps you engaged all 2 hours of the class (very impressive). He is overall a great professor

Dr. Lee is a very engaging speaker and knows how to make his class entertaining. He is also willing to lend a helping hand when its needed. I am happy to share my position that he's a great professor.
Dr. Lee is a great professor for ENGR161. It's a tough class homework/project wise but Dr. Lee makes it somewhat bearable, and he's always willing to clarify any questions he can that students have.
Great professor for 303. Makes difficult topics easy to digest and understand.

Michelle is by far one of the top professors for 303. It's a pretty easy and straightforward course for the first half but then ramps up significantly by the end. Michelle is very accessible outside of class for hw help and exam prep help.

Amazing class, taught by an extremely respectable and highly regarded expert in the field of dependable systems. We had group discussions in class and were encouraged to participate and do our best. Exams are fair (not overly difficult), and the material can be applied to almost any domain. Highly recommend.

The professor's lectures were superb, covering a right mix of foundational material and latest developments in the field of reliable and secure computing. The assignments, though only 3, reinforced the concepts. The professor pushed us to explore new ideas in the course project, which was good. We also got a relevant industry speaker.
One of the best professors I've had at my time at Purdue. Makes content interesting and easy to learn. If you go to lectures you are guaranteed to do well.
Great professor and definitely made the slog that is discrete mathematics more interesting.
Professor is reachable and teaches well. I would take this class again
I really enjoyed this class! All material was taught clearly and the professor really cares about his students!
