All the material is conveyed clearly and I was really happy to learn in this course.
Material is talked about in class and makes sense.
Good class, not too much homework. Would take again

Prof Bagchi was very solid. Good teacher, fair grader, focused on actually learning the content. He was great when it came to talking about specific concepts and teaching about how they interact with each other.

The class content can be difficult, but Professor Bagchi does a great job of explaining the topics and answering questions. Attendance isn't required for every lecture, but participation matters for your grade and going to the lectures is very helpful for understanding the material.

Professor Bagchi was able to make relatively boring topics interesting with his unique comedy style. He would try his best to slip in small little jokes into lectures or invite students to give ideas / do the problems to the best of their ability. I enjoyed taking this class even though I didn't get the material as fast it made sense later.

I noticed that a lot of the way his class is ran is directly through whats taught on the textbook that you must buy. This means that reading it helps a lot and prepares you well for the exams. Participation is important in his class as its grades, but besides that I really enjoyed this class.
Great professor that makes lectures interesting. Takes time to make sure students understand the content, but sometimes expectations for workflow on exams are unclear.

I really liked professor Bagchi's class. You need to participate in order to get participation credit, but besides that the lectures are not mandatory. He is very generous on exams with partial credit. I really recommend this class!

Professor bagchi is a very good professor that cares about his student. The one thing I wish was that there were more resources online and the lecture notes were easier to follow with the class slides.
Takes time to know his students and puts in a lot of effort for his lectures and additional optional recitations. He is a fairly good grader as well and gives a lot of partial credit.
Prof. Bagchi has really good lectures overall, and the exams are fair. He definitely emphasizes in-class and out-of-class participation, so keep that in mind when taking this course!

Professor Bagchi gets a lot of engagement from students in his lectures, making it very important to be immersed in the course material which helps a lot. He makes difficult-to-approach subjects very simple at first before leading up to harder problems.
A very fair class where you get what you put in. Professor Bagchi strives for engaging lectures that definitely help with comprehension.

Professor Bagchi is a fantastic professor and you can tell he's quite passionate while teaching. He is incredibly smart and always attempts to offer help or answer questions whenever possible. The course content is a bit difficult and it is important to read the textbook and understand the HW, or the exams are going to be quite difficult.
Very Good Professor! He definitely made his best efforts in teaching a considerably boring class, discrete math, as entertaining as possible.

In my opinion I would say he's a very average professor. His lectures are nice and he goes over a lot of questions that you will see on exams. However, a lot of the course is very copied from the textbook, and you can learn just the same by reading that. Exams are hard because there's many concepts but if you understand everything it's pretty easy

One of the best professors within the ECE department. He is extremely helpful during lectures and cares about his students' learning. I would highly recommend attending lectures, he tries his best to make dry topics interesting and has done a great job in making me personally more interested in the topics he teaches.

One of my best experiences with an ECE Professor. I have only taken the Discrete Math class with him and although I find the topic not too interesting, he made it feel interesting and I felt motivated to learn. He is a really good teacher I would highly recommend going to his lectures. An amazing professor who cares about his students' learning.
Very well-paced lectures. Exams are not difficult if you study well.

Professor Gulker's lectures are very easy to understand and she uses a lot of anecdotes to make them relatable. It's a very light workload with only 3-4 assignments for the entire semester. The way the midterms are structured also makes it easier to learn content for each one since there are 5 midterms. Overall, I would definitely take this class!
She was alright, took forever to grade work tho and she doesn't give clear feedback on what you did wrong on the assignments. No exam!
The lectures were great. I felt like I was actually learning during her classes. That being said, the tests were difficult. Be prepared to study to learn concepts that are actually applicable.

Enthusiastic lecturer who is clearly knowledgeable and passionate about social psych. Content has a lot more breadth than depth, meaning class structure is modular. Assignments were trivial, participation grade gives free points with generous make-up opportunities, exams essentially test knowledge of vocabulary, textbook useful but not required.

I thought this class was very interesting and informative. She did talk about her kids quite a bit, but she was a very kind grader and it didn't take much for me to get an A. Make sure you pick good groups for the group projects.

Be ready to have the most vague rubric and get back a randomly low grade without any explanation. This class is unnecessary yet Gulker grades so harshly for no reason. She pushed most communication onto the TA and won't deal with you if you ask for help.

I've taken two classes besides this one with Jill, and this was the hardest class I have taken from her. Exams are incredibly hard, and it's best to start the study guide a week before the exam and reread the chapters before exams. Final project was practically rewriting the textbook. Not an easy A,

Truly one of the best classes I've ever taken. She makes class so entertaining and actually motivates me to learn. She does participation, but it's super easy stuff. Most of it was opinion based and as long as you submitted it in class you got full credit.

I liked her lectures. They were engaging and actually taught what was on exams. I didn't touch the textbook once the whole semester. Attendance is mandatory and she uses menti. The exams/quizzes are pretty hard, but if you look over the slides and use the study guide she provides they aren't too bad.

She's a very entertaining lecturer, and makes coming to class well worth the effort. You will need to do the readings to do well on the exams, but the questions are straightforward and rarely anything she hasn't warned you about. I didn't like her assignments that integrated AI, but she provided alternatives that took slightly more effort.
She a pretty good professor, most of her lectures were pretty entertaining and her class wasn't hard at all. It should be an easy A as long as you show up to class.
I enjoyed her lectures. She was funny and made her lectures pretty easy. Only thing I would complain about is her exams were pretty difficult.

Dr. Gulker wasn't supposed to teach this class but took over last minute. I think she's a nice lady, but not a great professor. It's finals week and we still barely have any grades in, and her lectures are honestly pretty boring. A semester long group project that feels useless takes up a chunk of your grade, but other than that it's an easy course

I have taken two of Dr. Gulker's classes; if I am scheduled for a third one, I am switching out. She takes forever to grade things (it is almost finals week, and I still have nothing in the gradebook for MGMT 294500), gives broad guidelines for upcoming assignments, and her grading is very strict. She is a nice person, just a terrible professor.

Prof. Gulker is by far one of the best professors I've had. Although the class is lecture heavy, I genuinely enjoyed going. She is a great lecturer and she makes class fun. Participation points are easy as long as you show up to class. Exams are online and proctored but the lowest exam score is dropped. She has a great attitude with students.
Do not expect to pass this class. She makes things extremely hard for no reason. Even if you try to study or give it your all, you are bound to fail.
I highly recommend Dr. Gulker! She's awesome- her lectures are fun, engaging, and easy to follow. As long as you show up to class, you'll do well.

I'm not a psych major so I took this class as an elective. It ended up being one of my favorite classes. Her lectures are so entertaining and easy to understand. The exams are straightforward. There are very few homework assignments, and the workload is super light. I would recommend this class if you need an elective, or need to get a psych credit

Her behavior overshadowed any positive aspects of the class. She created a hostile learning environment and made it difficult to feel comfortable participating in class by treating students inconsistently and unfairly. Exams are also ridiculous and aren't relevant to the lecture at all. The more classes I take with her, the more I feel belittled.

The exams are a complete guessing game. She gives you a study guide and no matter how hard you study/read/go over notes half of the exam will be something that comes out of nowhere. I have personally gone to every class, read the book, and gone over every study guide and I was barely able to pass an exam👎🏻don't like meeting w TA? Don't take.

Lectures are enjoyable and the professor herself is a very nice person. However, the projects have nothing to do with the course content, are long and tedious, and are graded on a vague yet strict rubric. There are 2 quizzes that are very easy (as long as you attend lectures) but worth very little points compared to the projects. Mixed feelings.

This class was pretty decent and could be funny at times. There were only 2 quizzes, which were made to be pretty easy if you paid attention in class/took good notes. Assignments were long and tedious. She never gave clear feedback or honestly what she wanted to see from the paper, so I would just say follow the rubric as much as you can.

To describe Gulker in one word, I would choose solid. Was this class revolutionary? No. But were there times I laughed or gained some insight, yes. For the prof, her rubrics are a bit wack and can knock off points for things not even mentioned but overall its not that hard. Seems willing to work with students and overall seems invested in the topic

This class was by far my favorite this semester. Lectures were super interesting. I was worried because I completely failed the first quiz, but after learning what to expect from the exams, I figured out how I needed to study to prepare and ended up getting As on the rest of the exams and 1 B. Read the textbook and go to class and you'll be fine!

Dr. Castro is extremely knowledgeable in her respective field. She is the most well organized professor I have ever had, making anatomy easy to grasp. She provides a vast amount of resources that make the difficult concepts included in this class so much more manageable.

Honestly, I've never been that great at Science as I'm more of a Math/Physics nerd. But Castro makes the best slides I've ever seen. Yes, she is slightly hard to understand during lectures, but she gives a large amount of material to study for exams. Instead of a TB, some micromodules are much easier to navigate than paragraphs of info.

Super sweet and willing to answer any questions. Also provides a lot of resources to study for exams on your own on top of office hours/review sessions. Most homework and recitation assignments are easy points. Labs can get annoying and seem pointless, but make sure you know at least enough material to pass quizzes or they'll catch up to you fast.

She's such a sweetheart. Ppl complain about her accent but she has more study material than any not her prof. I've ever seen so you should be okay if you study. Labs are VERY annoying but are not related to Dr. Castro.

Great professor. Does a good job at organizing the course and providing extensive amounts of resources for students to self-study Be sure to get all the easy points like doing the HW, recitation, and lab/pre-lab assignments. Lots of content covered in exams, but def manageable with 3-4 days of studying. No reason this class shouldn't be an A.

Gives so many resources to succeed, had an 8:30 lecture that was empty, but I thought going to lecture was helpful. Use the flashcards, lecture notes, and practice exams to study. Labs were boring and repetitive but did not take that long. 5 exams but the class is not cumulative, so it ends up being not too much information for each exam.

Dr. Castro's lectures can sometimes feel difficult to understand due to the volume of content. However, using the configurable flashcards provided before exams helped solidify the information. She gives a lot of resources to help the info stick. Dr. Castro is a great professor! Lab is interesting, but Lisa Kolo unfortunately controls it.

Watch lecture online, attendance isn't mandatory. Drill the lectures and practice quizzes before exams. No textbook. Use quizlets folders already made online. Lab is super interesting. Assignments are all reasonable and doable.

Very nice and clearly a caring professor. She can be hard to understand at times, but she does her best to make what is a very dense class as manageable as possible. (Practice exams are incredibly helpful and the key to doing well)

Castro is a very nice person, but get ready to study day and night for this class. I'm not a very good test taker and thats what matters a lot in this class. VERY hard to understand her lectures. If you put in time and effort in the class you should be ok, but it was very hard to focus on other classes while taking this one because it is so much.

Professor Castro is hard to understand in lecture, I feel as if the lecture slides are a disorganized mess. There are 5 exams with no exam drops or replacements and quizzes before every lab. I will say there are a lot of extra resources to study for exams but you are forced to do extra material in order to unlock the following week in Brightspace.

This class can be intense, but Dr. Castro explains everything super clearly and always connects concepts in a way that actually sticks. She also gives great, practical tips on how to study and prepare for exams, which honestly makes a huge difference. If you go to office hours or ask questions, she genuinely helps.

Castro's class can be intimidating the first week. However, if you use the material she created for this class specifically, there should be no reason why you should fail the class. She can be hard to understand in lectures, but it doesn't make the content impossible to learn. Put the time in the class and easy A

Dr. Castro is one of the most effective professors I have ever had. Lots claim that she is hard to understand, but I disagree. I have never had a problem understanding her, and even if you do find her pronunciation hard to follow, the words are on the slides. Microlearning modules and Quizlet are the best combo for exams! Easy and fun labs.

Castro is a very nice professor. In class, she includes extra slides that help us students with understanding/memorizing the material. The big three for exams to help with studying: lecture videos, micro learning modules, and past exams/notecards. Overall, she's a good person+ really is trying to help. Just lock in cause it's a lot of information.

I attended almost every single lecture and sit in the front and I still find it near impossible to understand what she is saying every class. Her lectures are also super disorganized at times and I have to take notes before class cause she always makes too many slides. Lots of good resources for exams, which aren't hard. Homework is posted late.
Great Professor!
Dr Castro is nice but I fear I relied and got help from the TAs and SI instructors more than her. She is very kind but her lectures are kind of hard to understand.

Dr. Castro was the perfect professor for the way I learn. She does have an accent, but the more you attend lecture the easier it is to follow along with her. Homework follows the notes nearly exactly and there is SO MUCH review material for exams. She's very easy to contact outside of class and office hours were incredibly helpful.

She's pretty hard to understand but her resources for this class are great. Labs are a lot more tolerable than other classes. Recitation has an unusually large class size but it's not too bad. Just use her resources and you should be able to get an A or at least a high B in the class.

This professor is super hard to understand but that is probably the only negative thing I can say about her and this class. The class is hard but the prof gives TONS of practice problems/flashcard opportunities to learn material. Lab 1000% better than chem or bio lab. Recitation is annoying but doable.

She can be difficult to understand but she posts lecture videos so you can always rewatch those.
The tests are exactly the slides and easy to comprehend and she gives a lot of resources.
Very kind and understanding
Amazing and very smart woman. She has a very strong accent, but she becomes easier to understand the more you attend the lecture. She provides numerous resources for exams. Incredible professor.

Her lectures may be hard to follow but, where she lacks in her lecturing she makes up for it in providing a TON of helpful resources in order to keep students prepared. Homework is well put together and engaging with lecture notes, plenty of helpful study tools as well like notecards and the learning modules. Great job with material out of class.

Difficult to understand. Test scores improved after I quit going to lecture. The best way to succeed and prepare for exams is to use resources provided by Dr. Castro. The study resources are helpful; however, there are too many.
Nice teacher that cares about students. Very good at explaining and going through examples.

If you have the chance to take a class with Prof. Krousgrill, DO IT! I was lucky to have him for ME274 & for ME323, and he is a great teacher. His lecture book is a must-buy and if you show up to class (he does random quizzes but not often) often times you only have to write your name to get credit. His content matches exams, which is appreciated.
Very caring good professor, if you are taking ME274 or 323, take it with Krous!
He was nice and caring.
Krousgrill is a great professor. He teaches at a somewhat slower pace in my opinion, which worked well for me. I had him for ME 274 and ME 323, and I really enjoyed him.
Slow paced lectures but he does a good job of explaining them

Brilliant professor. Always active on the blog and very helpful with homework. The textbook is a very important tool for success in this class because every single example has a worked-out video on the blog.
Way too much homework and an overwhelming amount of irrelevancy

ME 274 is a tough course that CMK gives you a fighting chance to excel. Great use of lecture book examples and live demos, gives clear HW hints, is readily available for OH & blog questions, and applies student course eval comments during the same semester for quizzes and lecture flow. Who cares that he has a stutter, I'm taking him again for 323.

78 homework... yes you heard me. 78 homeworks. You are required to solve 6 every week. In addition, you have pop quizzes . All of these materials do no prepare you for exams. This class is made harder than how a dynamics class should be.
My boy the best professor ever created by god

I also had him for dynamics. He definitely cares a lot about students understanding the problem and the content. He does a great job of taking difficult concepts and simplifying them in class. Just make sure that you stay off your phone and paying attention to the lecture or he will be disappointed.

Nice guy, has alright lectures, and is a fair grader. He's got a bit of a stutter, but it doesn't take away from the overall value of the course. I'm trying to take ME 323 (he wrote the textbook for both that and ME 274). Man's got a v impressive resume, so as long as you can make the lecture, it's worth it.

One of my favorites so far. Genuinely cares about his students, answers questions well, appreciates when people ask conceptual questions. Lecture examples are clear (with opportunity for questions), and match HW and tests well. He will get upset if people are on their phone, especially in the front row. I get why since he puts in a lot of effort.

Chuck is the best professor I have had at Purdue so far. His lectures are highly informative: he teaches you the process of how to approach the problem which can be applied beyond those in lecture. He gives a background of the theory, but his teachings are more practical based. Study the homework for exams.

He was very good and kind, and actually cared for our understanding. But his teaching style was highly conceptual and hard to follow up with. He expected students to already know the material, but was able to connect everything by the end. Exams were harder than homework and challenging. Despite that, very respected professor and human.

Very nice and respectful person. Cares about the students and provides visuals to make students understand better. However, some answers to question are not as clear, and lectures do not prepare you for exams. Also, exams are not fairly graded. Answers to email fast, and explains better in emails and office hours.

Professor Krousgrill is one of the most caring professors I've had. He explained the concepts as he worked through examples and took the time to break down problems in office hours. He tried to accommodate and answer questions whenever he could. Tests weren't impossible you just have to understand the material vs. memorization.
This is the best professor Ive ever had. I failed this class the semester prior and didnt understand anything. I retook the class with Prof Krousgrill and got an A. I cant recommend him enough.

As a senior, he's the best professor I've had. His lecture style is the best. Lots of homework and in-class quizzes. The tests are very similar to the homework. He genuinely cares about your success and learning.

Krousgrill is a fantastic lecturer. He provides clear examples and is very easy to follow and understand as he explains material. He does in-class quizzes and the exams and homework are difficult, but he's very helpful and kind if you ask for it. He speaks with a bit of a stutter, but I don't think this made him difficult to understand.

A lot of homework every other day. The homework problems are complicated and do no represent exam questions. Also, he takes a lot of time to grade and release quizzes. Even exams can take a lot of time to get graded. Not very approachaple.

He's great at explaining things and loves helping students at office hours and even outside of office hours. One time, I needed help and asked if he could meet with me at around 8pm one evening for some help. He happily met with me and made sure that I truly understood the material. He was also very quick to respond to short questions via email.

Krousgrill is one of the best professors I've had in my life so far. His lectures are informative and concise. He'll take concerns very seriously and will always be ready to help students who don't understand certain topics. I got the feeling he really cared that every student succeeds.

This professor is a man who has many contributions to society as well as the world. He is very wise and approachable. The people he taught and taught by them were really lucky. We wish you more health and success.
She is poor at explaining concepts and reads straight off the slides in lectures. She gets a 2 not a 1 because she is very nice and does experiments, but the teaching was not there during lectures.

I actually enjoyed our time together, i wouldn't say she deserves the 1.4 rating and i liked her style. smetimes she didn't explain things the best but she alwasy encouraged questions and gave out candy at the end of class.

really bad at explaining any topic, just reads off slides. don't take her especially if you don't have a physics background, lecture is a waste of time and i only go because of iclicker mandatory attendance

Mainly reads off slides, doesn't clearly elaborate on key concepts, overcomplicates simple ideas, and passes on the responsibility of explaining example problems to fellow students, which creates more confusion. Not having taken HS physics, I have never gotten anything of substance out of Pushkar's lectures.
"If an object is not on a straight path, then the past must be straight" - Yulia
Very good professor who had many different in class experiments and seemed to care about the class this semester idk about previous semester but she was explaining very well now

Might be the worst lecturer I've ever had. Obvious she's just reading off the slides and barely has any knowledge of the content, and gives the most confusing explanations possible. To top it off, the mandatory attendance makes me waste multiple hours a week going to these lectures. Don't take her class.

She mostly reads off the slides, but she asks students to explain their answers. Not that interesting during lectures, but the class is so easy that you don't really need to pay attention. Lowkey you might get more confused afterwards... Grading is standardized so the lecturer doesn't matter.

The only reason I went to her lectures was the graded attendance. She just read off the slides, which didn't even provide all the information needed to be successful in the course. When she is asked a question, she gets another student to explain it for her. She should not be teaching or making jokes. Avoid her if you want to learn.
seems nice but doesnt explain, cant teach
Just reading off the slides and not providing anything important.
Professor uses slides from previous years, doesn't always know what's on them.

Hands down the worst prof/teacher I have ever had. Reads directly off of slides, and answers questions in the most complex way possible. The only way to pass the class is to have good TAs. Practice exams are worthless, and you can forget about partial credit on the free-response problems.

This is the hardest class I've taken at Purdue. On the exams, the multi-choice was made to trick you, but the open responses were easy if you're good at math. The concepts in the class are tricky and confusing, but the professor does her best. She got me very excited for my future classes as she introduced cool phenomena throughout the lectures.

Not sure why she's received such a bad rep. The course is a bit difficult especially for people who haven't taken physics before, but I don't think she does a bad job teaching at all. The examples she provides in lectures help you figure out all of the homework problems. Do not be okay with not understanding something, or you'll fail.

For a university that prides itself in thee quality of education it gives its students, to have Yulia teaching is beyond disrespectful. From her lack of care for the class she is teaching, to her constant mockery of the few students brave enough to ask questions, never have I ever felt so degraded as a student by a professor/ teacher in my life.
Worst professor at Purdue, avoid if you want to learn.
Tried to give her the benefit of the doubt. Lectures are simultaneously useless and mandatory. Do not take this class.

Avoid taking her if you can. My experience was very unpleasant. Granted, I am not an engineering major, so I had quite a bit of trouble grasping the concepts. However, her explanations were very poor and lectures were very boring. If you read the book, there is almost no point in coming to class other than getting clicker points.
Avoid at all costs. All she does is read off the slides and somehow makes topics unnecessarily complicated.

100% the worst professor I've ever had. Very clearly only here for research. It's obvious how little she cares about her students, telling them to "study more" instead of answering their questions. Mandatory lectures consist of her reading plagiarized slides. Constantly slacks off on uploading important study material sometimes as late as exam day.

She's here for her research and is extremely obvious. Lectures are her reading of slides, not knowing the answer to Iclickers many times. When someone participates in class she makes jokes about how they need to study more, which makes students stop caring a few weeks in. Exams are harder than what she teaches us.

she warned the class about how serious plagiarism is, yet she is the one who uses power points slides from University of Maryland. There was an I-clicker question that had the option F as the correct answer, and we all know i-clicker used at Purdue only has 5 buttons a,b,c,d,e. It clearly shows she did not prepare for the lecture .

Professor Pushkar is at Purdue for research, and its pretty easy to tell. She reads off of another professor's slides with a heavy Russian accent, making her very hard to understand. Reading the textbook and going to other lecturers helped me pass this class.

She's a nice lady and I think she kind of knows the material but her lectures are impossible to follow. The slides focus mainly on examples and calculations rather than actually explaining concepts. I would recommend doing ALL the homework for this class as it's a huge chunk of your grade and the tests are insane so you'll need the grade boost.
Lectures had tons of cool demos, super funny lecturer, although does not always explain concepts well. Overall good prof.

As much as I enjoyed his lectures, I didn't get anything out of them. Professor Simpson is a funny guy who is pretty entertaining. He does a lot of experiments to try to help our understanding, most of which are hard to connect to the current topic. He struggles with simplifying topics and his handwriting is almost impossible to read.

Even though Simpson was a funny prof, his lectures were the most unhelpful ever. He kept going off to tangents, spoke too quickly, had bad handwriting which was really hard to read, and moved on too quickly. I wish he would just slow down and taught more calmly. His experiments were interesting. It did not seem to me that he proofread his slides.

He veers from the textbook too far and rarely has time for questions. Sometimes I sat there so confused I couldn't even conjure a question to ask. He goes on tangents a lot. Very funny and great demos, but it seems he doesn't proofread his lectures before conducting them.

He is very knowledgeable and passionate about chemistry, and his lectures are engaging and entertaining. However, for a beginner course, lectures often focus on advanced or theoretical topics rather than core exam material. While the experiments made lectures fun, I found that I had to rely heavily on self-study 0to learn the required content.

He was very nice and funny, but the lectures overall were not good. I felt like there was lots of information on HWs and tests that he didn't provide, and he often did unnecessary demos and talked about random things that did not apply at all. His handwriting was also atrocious. Great person, not a super great lecturer.

It's clear that Simpson is knowledgeable and passionate, but honestly don't expect to learn anything during lecture. His slides are sparse, and he loves talking about extra topics that aren't on exams, and doing demonstrations, which are cool but kind of a waste of time. Would recommend going to office hours/SI sessions to supplement his teaching

The experiments that he did in class were definitely quite fun. He's enthusiastic about what he teaches but often goes on tangents. He barely gets through the material on time and doesn't go in depth, which will impact your understanding of the material. Furthermore, his handwriting is unreadable so don't rely on his notes to work example problems.

You will need to read the textbook to supplement his lectures. Don't rely on him for math concepts because he covers math that is not on the exam more than the math that will be on the exam. His lectures are also entirely his handwritten notes and some diagrams, but his handwriting is illegible. However, he does do demos that keep us engaged.

He's a great guy and I really liked his demos, but I was so lost during his lectures. I feel like he gave us extra information that made his lectures more confusing. I would leave lecture more confused than when I came in. I got a 70% on the first test, on the the second one I got a 85% after watching another prof's lectures and taking her notes.

I think that Prof. Simpson was an amazing lecturer, though he sometimes struggled to simplify down and explain a few topics. He virtually always had a demo in his class, and had me pretty excited for his lectures.
Very passionate about chemistry, funny and amazing demos. Sometimes struggles to explain things in a way that makes sense to college freshmen and horrible handwriting but overall good.
Very passionate about chemistry and has amazing demos. Sometimes struggles to explain concepts in a way that makes sense to college freshmen but overall good.

Prof Simpson is a funny guy and he makes lecture pretty enjoyable. The only issues are that he has bad handwriting and it can be hard to follow the topics he speaks about. Not the easiest lecture, but at least he makes it fun to sit through. Definitely loves his job.

His class for graduate students is hard, but he is extremely accessible outside of class for help on exams. Definitely the hardest class I have had, but he is also the prof that puts the most work into understanding, so it's worth it.

It's clear that Dr. Simpson cares a lot about his students, but I cannot say I enjoyed the format of the class this semester. Lecture videos are taught in a scattered problem-solving fashion, which can make it harder to grasp concepts. Tests are incredibly hard. I passed CHM 115 with an 95% and currently have a 76% after two tests in this class.

Since this was a "flipped" class, Simpson uploads videos for us to watch online. In them, he explains concepts rather simply and doesn't go too fast.  In the recitations he gives us problems like the ones in the videos to solve in groups, with him walking around and helping tables. So as far as you watch the videos and participate, you're golden.

Dr. Simpson is fun in lecture.  He cracks chemistry jokes nonstop and loves doing chemistry demos. He does a fine job of explaining concepts and often makes use of a Surface Pro tablet to illustrate his ideas. I had him for the first half of CHM 116 and he was a solid choice.  His exams were also well-written.
Took me 20 hours to take a take-home exam.  Expects people to not collaborate with each other even though everyone does.  Expects matlab mastery. This guy sucks.

Great teacher.  You can tell he loves his job and he is very fun to be around.  If you understand chemistry then you will love this class.  The only people that I have ever met that didn't like him were people who couldn't grasp simple concepts.
He is very funny and animated. But sometimes seems all over the place and scattered. It is possible to get an A only if you pay attention and work hard.
Very funny. His lectures were always enjoyable. Its kinda wierd that he only taught the first half of the semester though.

I loved taking 116 with Simpson aside from being hilarious I found I was able to learn much from his lectures and enjoyed going to class. His grading policy is liberal though his exams are challenging. I would absolutley take another class with him. Gives cool demos.

Funny lecturer, enjoyed his lectures, did all the homework, all the readings, and attended all classes. Took tests which were insanely difficult given the material covered in class and book. Test averages for his portion of the course was about 50%. This isn't a failure for students to learn, but a failure for the teacher to teach.

The professor provides no help and if he does it is half hearted. He is more concerned with his reserch than helping a student. I hope this rating will help the department to look for ways to improve the course, although I know it will not.

By far Prof. Jahanshahi's best trait is his course organization. Each week covers a topic, each topic gets a homework, each homework corresponds to one exam question. Solid lectures that give you a good base, but most of your learning comes from homework. Extremely generous with extra credit, 1.5% for course eval and 5% for help session attendance.

Not overly difficult with consistent effort. His lectures are a bit confusing and intimidating and he is a very serious character. Homework grading can be frustrating and confusing but I suppose that is more reflective of the TA(s). Just learn how to do all the methods and don't crutch with AI. First unit/exam was pretty much all just 270 material.

Good at teaching. If you had a good understanding of CE270, you will do just fine in this class with Jahanshahi. He explains things in a straightforward manner, and even if you do not get it, he does not make you feel dumb if he has to explain a concept over again. He emphasizes what things you will need to know for the exam.

Really solid professor who clearly cares about teaching and answering student questions during lecture to make sure they actually understand content. Generous amount of extra credit with extra practice sessions offered along with engaging and informative lectures. Exams are quite similar to the large amount of practice problems covered in class.

Prof. Jahanshahi was very clear on what to expect on each of the exams, and how to do well in the class. He provided a lot of examples and made many resources accessible to us. He was very approachable and engaging in lectures, and the amount of students who still came to class even though attendance was optional says a lot.

He is ok. He asks lots of questions in class, but I found it confusing. For example: Someone says the answer is 1. He asks if anyone else thinks it is something else. Someone else would say it was 0 because he would just stare at us until someone spoke. Then he'd spend 10 minutes saying why it would be 0, but the answer would actually be 1.
Great for learning structures, lays it out in a way that is incredibly easy to understand

He is one of the rudest people I have every met. Seems like he has control issues and loves to assert his dominance over college students. He is a little harsh at grading and does an ok job explaining the material but his attitude needs to be checked. Complete hard ass for no reason and yells at students for not understanding the material.

Decent professor. Exam problems are similar to homework questions. Solves a good amount of examples in class with all solutions being posted on Brightspace. He tells you what questions to expect on each exam.
Hes a terrible professors. Homework is 15% of your grade and test are 85% of the class. He yells in class and hes a VERY tough grader

He explains things very well, but the grading of homework and exams is completely unfair. Even following the right procedure, if you make a small error in the beginning of a problem you hardly receive any credit. The averages this semester were high, but unfortunately with everything being online it's likely due to cheating despite zoom proctor

I took this class previously with Ayhan Irfanoglu, and it's like night and day. This professors lectures are really well structured and it's clear what you need to learn in each concept. He does LOTS of practice problems (and actually finishes them). If you keep up with the lectures, this class is easy to do well in.

While it is a tough class it is fair. The professor tells you the whole semester that if you do practice problems out of the book youll do fine on exams and its true. I did terrible on the 2 midterms because I didnt try and I did well enough on the final but I shouldnt have passed. Prof. Jahanshi was just being nice and curved me up to be nice.

Solid professor, no doubt, in fact one of the best I have had at Purdue's civil department. However, your entire course grade depends pretty much on exams and the exams consist of only four problems. Exams are also graded pretty harshly and the class averages are pretty low. If you want to learn, take him, if you want a good grade, skip him.
Fantastic professor. Would highly recommend to anyone. Extremely accessible outside of class, and genuinely cares about his students.

One of the best professors I have had. He does a great job of providing examples that directly applies to the homework and exams. If you attend lecture and do all the homework, you can easily get an A.

He really cares about his students and is accessible during office hours, however, he refuses to post worked examples in his lecture notes, so often you're left with notes that can be confusing and no easy way to clarify. Homework problems are graded VERY harshly, any single mistake drops your score a point, and each problem is only worth 3 points.

He's not a good professor & his teaching method is kinda different than other professors i honestly didn't like hem and keep wondering if i have someone like hem....i may dislike Purdue Civil Engineering. It's not just me but my best friend didn't choose Structure engineering because of him that she may take another class with him & drop the scool

He's not a professor at all, i have seeing so many good professors in Purdue civil engineering department but he's not even close to them it is not just me my whole friends were complaining when we took his class. He maybe be smart but teaching methods is something very different. SKIP the class

I honestly didnt learn anything from him, just keep your smile with him all the time you will be in a good shape. Because he is clearly not someone related to other civil Professors  If you really want to learn something than take static with somebody else.

The material is tough, especially compared to the other professor. Homeworks are extremely difficult without going to office hours or friends to work with. He does care about your grade and will be there to help if you ask. Expect test averages from 50-70%. If you do poor on the first test, don't worry too much. Must do well on final though.

I actually think he's a good professor. The material he teaches is very tough tho. It doesn't help that when i took this class, there was two sections. & the other section was doing stuff way easier than us. But like I said he makes you work. If you chegg all the homework (2-3 a week) you won't do good. Exams are very tough. Know how to do hw
he was actually pretty clear, as long as you read the book, went to office hours, and didn't use chegg you'll be fine. The exams were a little tough, but doable.
Straight up just trying to stop you from succeeding

Cui is a great prof., he is very passionate and explains things thoroughly. However, he goes fast in lecture and has a thick accent so it is hard to understand him at first. The exams and material of 301 is very hard. However, prof. Cui is very inspirational, and works with students to help them understand the material.

Cui's lectures were very fast, but he did a good job explaining things and gave useful examples. Homework and exams had a few more difficult problems but were fair. My main issue was that we didn't have practice exams.

This class was difficult. I always found that I learned nothing during the lectures, so I'd recommend watching the recorded lectures posted on brightspace which are very helpful. Cui is not a bad lecturer, and he seems very kind.
Very tough class
He'll get the key points across but if you actually want to learn and succeed in the class you'll need the textbook and other resources. Paying attention wasn't worth it most of the time.

Cui is a decent lecturer and I would consider him a safe choice if you don't have any good options. He has an accent but its not bad. His lectures are nothing special but he does a lot of practice problems which is nice, and responds via email decently quick, but wont care about any issues or conflicts you have, so expect no leniency.

He's not it. The lectures are the biggest waste of time. I have played an hour of clash royale in every single lecture this year. That being said, I know more about the material than anyone else I've met just because I only learn by myself. This means you only learn how not to do it by listening to professor Meng teach.
Do not take this professor's class

Amazing professor. He both taught the material extremely well and taught to the exams extremely well. Answered questions very well and was overall a good person. Advocated for the students after the average on the first exam was like a 45% instead of blaming them like some other professors.

Dr. Cui can be a little hard to understand sometimes (especially this semester with his gas mask) but I really enjoyed his lectures and he gave a lot of great tips before exams. He did practice problems, discussed a few hidden variations on formulas, and is always willing to help students. The course is tough but Dr. Cui made it bearable
Purdue ECE department is a embarrassment. Im not gonna write an essay but just know they dont care about you at all.

Lectures were mostly examples and problem solving. The professor used different techniques to solve circuits than the online course videos. He would give motivational speeches while we worked out problems in class.
Lectures were not too bad considering I had him at 7:30. Didn't have the same grading scale as other professors in the same course but it worked out in the end
AMAZING professor. Explains everything super super well and makes himself very available with office hours. Gives hints for exams and they too difficult or tricky. He posts lecture notes online.

Very good professor for ECE 201. Has Chinese accent but is perfectly understandable. Tells funny stories and always gives you the easiest ways to solve the problems. Had only one "quiz" that was worth 3 points. Gives extra credit points for volunteering to solve problems in front of the class.

Dr. Cui is exceptionally caring about student performance. ECE 201 is a hard class and often there aren't many good choices for professors. He doesn't have the best handwriting as others note and his accent can be hard to understand, but he is very dedicated to making sure students succeed. Lectures on boilercast and lots of worked exam problems.
Goes through material very quickly, often skipping steps when he is explaining.

Very good professor. Will answer questions well and explain things in lecture. Minor accent. Homework for class occasionally uses problems from textbook. Somewhat short homeworks online are assigned due before each lecture.

He's an amazing professor for this class. He may have a thick accent and pretty bad handwriting, but he is so caring and really goes above and beyond to make sure every student understands. He goes at a good pace, goes over homework solutions and lecture reviews everyday, and he hand writes all of his notes (which I love). Great guy, I recommend!
Very good performance in the class room. Very interesting and shows how things connect in the class. Love this professor.

He likes to write things down instead of using ppt(worst handwriting). Hard to follow his mind. You have to do homework prior to 2 days before next class if you want help with it.( HW due just before the class, classes held on MWF). And his office hour is right after the classes, most of the students couldn't get to it because we have other classes

Awesome and very passionate professor. I took him for both AT103 and 202 this semester. Enjoyed both classes A LOT! AT103 is very easy if you spent any time working on cars growing up, if you have no mechanical background may be harder. AT 202 is more difficult but if you study it is very easy. I never had to study for either classes was ok.

Todd Brewer is an exceptional professor. You show up to the classes and put maybe 2-3 hours of studying per exam and you will get an A. Mostly memorization. He literally will give test questions for free in class if you just pay attention. He tells you point blank at least 25% of the test.
If you show up to hanger tours and pay attention to what he says will be on the test you should be able to get an A in the class

Todd is amazing! Honestly a great guy, and is tailor made to teach this class. He's an A&P mechanic and understands how to teach the material well. He's always happy to answer questions. Not a lot of points available, so exams matter.
Made class interesting and always explained topics well. Gave lots of extra credit and good exam review material.

Passionate professor, ready to help out and work with you whatever the matter is. Labs are a massive part of the course and of your final grade, half of the lab assignments also offer 5% extra credit.

Survey at every class so show up. With that there are 3 quizzes, 3 exams (with extra credit), and 2 homework assignments, along with 1 extra credit assignment. I love the stories he tells about his time working on aircraft and find them to be beneficial. If you study you will be fine, just have to understand the systems and how they work together.

Todd is a great professor and cares about his students and the classes he teaches. The class is almost entirely lectures, tests, and quizzes (3 of each) aside from a few optional hangar tours for extra credit. Class can be boring but attendance is mandatory and he posts the slides online before each class.

Great guy who is very passionate about the all topics. Did not give context for content which made this class more challenging than it should have been. Extra credit at the end saved my grade but there are little assignments other than exams and quizzes so you definitely need to study for them.

He was a very helpful prof, especially since the content itself is challenging, so make sure you take good notes and read through lecture slides again before exams. Todd is good at answering questions in front of everyone, and a great person to talk to one on one. Doesn't give much extra credit though. I would take him for every course if I could!

Todd is such a great guy! He is super approachable for any questions you have. Some of his lectures do get a little bogged down by extra information and stories. There is also a lot of info on the slides, which are in much more detail than what the tests were. There is a little bit of extra credit but it's not much so I wouldn't count on it.
Very easy to understand lectures with lots of interesting stories from his professional life and work.  Good sense of humor and fun to talk to outside of class.

Dude lowkey helped me out when I was getting bullied. That should be enough to determine the quality of a teacher. 

Be prepared tho he is test heavy but he's super knowledgeable and passionate in what he teaches. Be a good student and it's an easy A.
Worse professor, a strict grading scale. Absolutely the worst!!

Todd is always thinking of new ways to teach us instead of reading straight off the lectures. He incorporates videos, analogies, and explanations to try and convey the topic to us. One thing to keep in mind is you should be constantly reviewing as there are three pop quizzes in this class that can be worth a significant amount of your grade.

He is a great person if you have a question one on one, but his lectures are very dense. If you have questions, he will explain well. He only has 3 exams and 3 quizzes, so if you bomb one, you're screwed. However, every exam and quiz has 1-5 bonus points. He has good stories he'll share, and he clearly is passionate about what he teaches.
Chill guy.

Professor Brewer is THE professor you would want in an introductory-level course. Due to his background in aircraft maintenance at Purdue, his lectures became enjoyable while staying practical. Watch out for pop quizzes and take the extra credit. They're fun, you get to check out engines, and are worth a lot. 10/10

Professor Brewer is a very chill guy and did a great job explaining concepts that people struggled with. If you have prior knowledge of engines then this class will require very little studying but be prepared for test questions that are only mentioned in class during lectures. Only downside is that attendance is extremely mandatory.

The class as a whole was not bad, guest lecturers were good and there was not a ton of homework. Weekly reflections are graded by TA's but they grade super strictly, small mistakes cost a lot of points. She is very strict in class (no technology, attendance, etc) and makes this seminar course a lot more stressful than it needs to be.

The Women in Engineering seminar was nothing like I was told it would be. Dr. ZB made the course extremely structured, and the TAs are required to be very strict and reprimand you if you put your head down, check your phone, or leave to use the bathroom. However, I will say that the guest speakers and out-of-class workshops were valuable.

Really fun, entertaining, and informative guest speakers. Zurn-Birkhimer is super kind, but she doesn't tolerate fooling around in class so make sure to pay attention (all devices aren't allowed out during class). She wants all of her students to succeed. Only graded components are attendance, weekly paragraph written reflections, and workshops.
Set reminders for the homework. If you miss 3 classes, it's an automatic fail. TAs grade your work, so not much to say about the professor.

This was a really good class that helped me get acclimated to college. All the speakers that come in to talk are really inspirational and fun to hear from. The professor is tough but fair and goes strictly by the syllabus. I definitely recommend taking this, it's an easy A if you turn stuff in and come to lectures. I recommend!

This class was not difficult the teacher was fine. However I didn't see the use in this class. It was just a bunch of lectures about other peoples journeys, even though none of them had any struggles.What they talk about in the workshops should be what is taught in the lectures. There were weekly reflections which I also thought was a bit much.

She teaches a women in engineering course but is NOT a girls girl. She is backhanded in her answers to student questions, and has basically told the class that being a women in engineering sucks and is going to suck. The home works are tedious and there are no reminders of due dates for them or for the mandatory workshops. Way too hard for 1 cred.

ZB is the instructor for the engr 194 wie seminar, but doesn't grade your reflection assignments. Class lectures are just inspirational for women or to help guide you in your future career. She and the TAs are super nice and grade pretty easy. 1 weekly reflection a week.

I loved this class, although Dr. ZB didn't have a huge role in it. This class helped me pick my engineering major, and I thoroughly enjoyed listening to the guest speakers. There is 1 reflection paper a week, that takes 20 minutes max. Easy class as long as you show up and are willing to listen.

Most guest speakers were interesting, but that's the only positive thing I could think of. The professor and TAs were passive-aggressive, and the grading criteria were messy with no rubric. Ironically, missing small details costs you a letter grade. Assignments were easy but lacked resources. By far my worst freshman class; avoid taking it.

The class itself was pretty good; the guest speakers and TAs were great. However, the professor is not someone I'd take a class with again. She doesn't teach anything and has no grace or kindness with questions. The assignments are pretty easy, but there's a lot, you have to figure them out yourself, and if you forget a few your grade will show it.

The class itself was interesting, I enjoyed hearing from current women in the engineering field. HOWEVER, the professor is not an engineer, and she doesn't care about the women in this course. The way she introduced the CAD and origami was off putting, 'women are not spatially aware because they didn't grow up playing video games or with Legos.'

The class isn't that bad but the professor is horrible. She almost failed me for an absence when I had covid, I had to spend 60 dollars for an uber to an urgent care just to get it approved with ODOS when she told me she had to think about accepting my excused absence. There are only two unexcused absences allowed.

I liked the guest speakers but I didn't like the way the course is constructed. They want you to do CAD which is good but she doesn't teach how to do it. It is all left on you to know how to do it and then she expects you to do it perfectly which of course is very hard. About her M&M program, it is only useful as a senior student, elsewise a waste.

The guest speakers for Women In Engineering seminar were all amazing and entertaining. Everything about this class is fair and doable except for the hours of origami assigned at the end of the semester. I wouldnt let the origami discourage you from taking the class, but I would advise finding someone who likes origami.

I did not enjoy her class at all. It was a 1 credit hour class and we had weekly homework of either a CAD assignment or Origami as well as a reflection paper. I do not recommend this class at all. It took up too much of my time and was not useful. She doesn't teach anything, she just lets the speakers talk. Her TAs are fantastic, but not her.

This class was a run of the mill lecture class for an introduction to Atmospheric Science. The lectures were good and informative and sufficient to pass the quizzes and exams. Just your standard lecture class here.

Professor Zurn-Birkhimer's class really helped me figure out my major. The class consisted of hearing from women in industry. She check attendance so go to class. She also has three papers during the year to write about yourself. She has very clear instructions for them. She makes this a great class to round out a schedule!
She didn't do much in ENGR194 except answer questions and introduce the speaker of the day.

He's so wonderful, extremely charismatic, interesting to listen to, just overall lovely. alsoo, GO TO LECTURE AND LABS, DO NOT SKIP!! He doesn't use any slides during class and he won't post proper notes, so make sure you're actively attending and taking detailed notes, having good notes will save you for exams (entire grade was four exams)

Lots of scary talk about how difficult this professor is, however if you follow lectures and take good notes you will succeed. ANSC 230 is a complete exam based course (every 2/3 week exams). Use the lectures and your notes over the slideshows and book readings. He will never test over material that he did not talk about. Amazing Professor!

Attendance isn't mandatory, but I wouldn't EVER skip. Lectures go by fast and you are only tested on things that he says in lecture, textbook is there if you need any other help or want to. He gives you everything you need for textbook studying, not just reading to read. Go through the processes, it's not just about memorizing.

Dr. Cabot is an amazing professor who cares about the learning of his students. While his lectures and labs are optional to attend, they greatly help to be there, so plan to consistently attend them. While his class is tough, it is a fair class, and with effort and good work put in, getting a good grade is certainly possible.

Dr. Cabot focuses his exams from his lectures and labs, which while optional, are incredibly helpful to attend consistently. His class is tough, but you gain a great understanding of the material, and he is caring and overall a great professor. if you take his class seriously, you can certainly do well.

This course was very challenging, but Professor Cabot did an amazing job teaching it. I never got the textbook and just took detailed in lecture notes which was enough to do well in the course. Professor Cabot was very clear with his expectations. Bi-weekly exams kept the information manageable. There were also hands on labs that I really enjoyed!

AMAZING Professor! He is very smart will give you plenty of resources to succeed. If you do not understand something, just ask! Go to lab AND lecture! A ton of exam questions will be reviewed in both, and he will tell you word-for-word what answers he expects on exams. Lab practicals were the toughest part of this class. Show up and you'll be fine!

Dr. Cabot is an amazing professor. Had him for ANSC 230, and he was great for transitioning into a more science-heavy ANSC course. There are a lot of tests throughout the semester, but Dr. Cabot is great at making sure you are prepared. As long as you take notes and go to lectures, you should be fine in this course.
Dr. Cabot gave a total of 9 exams. 7 lecture exams and 2 lab practicals. As long as you study, you will do great!!

I loved his class; he is a great professor. The only things that goes toward your grade is exams that you have every other week. They are pretty difficult, mostly short answers, and you really need to understand the processes for it. Cabot is always open to questions and really wants you to succeed.
He is amazing teacher who teaches a hard class.  The class is only based on your exam scores and the content on them is difficult. do not write this class off as easy.

Had to do the lectures before class, and then during class, we did discussion questions about whatever material we had to go over. He very rarely taught the lecture material in class. Grades are based mainly on exams. All written exams, no multiple choice.

Professor is very difficult reader and expects students to give word for word definitions from lecture on exams which are all short answer questions. If you can take this class with another professor, I would.

He can talk pretty fast over the topics, but he genuinely wants you to succeed. He is a great professor and is willing to do just about anything to help you pass. He posts a ton of stuff on BlackBoard to help you stay your way through the course.
Awesome class!

Great professor, very enthusiastic about teaching and subject matter. It is difficult material but he seems to want you to truely understand what he is teaching. Tough grader on quizzes, but that will help you overall on the exams. Would definitely take this class again with him or any other class he taught.
he is a great proffesor
I took him in Spring 2025. He is a very kind man; he adds personal anecdotes sometimes to make sure we understand stuff. Definitely rewatch lecture videos and do that cheat sheet!

Rendall is an awesome professor for stats. I knew nothing about stats but his lectures are concise, full of examples, and funny. He manages to work real life examples relating to his family which are both educational and entertaining. Excellent teacher and I strongly recommend him. If you show up to lecture and take notes you'll be fine for tests.
Extremely friendly and approachable. Relates boring topics in stats to real life to make lectures more interesting. Don't need the textbook. Do the homework early for extra credit.

IF you put in the work and ask questions you'll have a blast in his class. IF you act as a spectator in general you'll not have a super experience. Be interactive, ask questions, and put in the work, Rendall will take care of the rest and make a competent intelligent individual. Super! p.s the mean is inferior in every way to the median.

He was funny and seemed to be a nice guy, often didnt know much about assignments we were doing outside of lecture time. Group project lasts basically all semester which sucks and is alot of work. If you do the work and have a fairly decent group you should be fine
Very hard grader with even harder exams

He's a good teacher, he tries to connect the lessons with things applicable to real life. He's nice and happy to answer questions. He is not available outside of class in person because he teaches at the middle and high school but he is available over email. There is two exams and one final which is a group project. The exams were difficult.

He is a very nice person. He would often talk about his life and find a way to apply it to the course. He responds quickly to his emails and answers questions well. If he does not have the answer to a question at the moment, he always gets back to you when he does. There are participation quizzes for each lecture; however, they are fairly easy.
More than of the class dropped his class. Never take this guy again
Waste of money and time
Would not answer your questions, ignore you and tell you to leave the class.
If you ask him to talk with you, he would say to talk to the head of the department. He should not teaching statistics. Don't take him as your professor.

When I asked question during lecture, He laughed at me like I am stupid. I feel disrespected in his class many times. I droped out because he failed me every assienment I have taken. Would Take him again.

Doable course for the most part. Discussion posts are graded leniently. Tests are not made by her, and questions may not exactly align with material, but are not too difficult. Unclear instructions were given for the final project, and her change to an individual work made the project way more intense. Project was graded leniently.

Very sweet professor who wants everyone to succeed. I was in a 7:30 lecture and she would purposefully start a few minutes late to allow a grace period for people getting to class. If you know/follow the syllabus, you should excel in this class. Show up and participate and you'll be fine and learn Spanish along the way.

Professor Blanca is wonderful. She's so funny and Spanish 202 was a really easy course as long as you did the VHL assignments every night. She is a very generous grader as well and she's super funny. The exams are also open note and online, which makes them very easy. Choose Professor Blanca if you're taking Spanish 4! She does take attendance also

Professor Cabrera was overall funny and understanding. She made Spanish fun! Her instruction was a little unclear and her lecture sometimes dragged on but overall the assignments were pretty simple. The class only has a few grades so just make sure to turn everything in.

Professor Cabrera was so much fun in class. There were only 7 of us, so I think the smaller class helped a lot also. She makes lectures fun and can sometimes get off track, but still a great teacher. She takes attendance, so it is important to not miss. Would gladly take again.

Attendance is required and there are technically readings, but you are able to get by by reading summaries before class so you can participate in the discussion. She likes to call on people and will wait for people to talk. No homework except for a discussion post each week. The final group project was pretty big but we had plenty of time.

Blanquita is one of my most favorite foreign language professors in this campus. She's very enthusiastic when teaching and ensures that we learn as much as we can. Being in this class helped me speak more fluently and also learned useful phrases that can be used in Spanish speaking countries. Take this class. You won't be disappointed!

Blanca is the best professor ever, she cares deeply about all of her students and she makes every class fun. Content wise, this class was easy and bettered by accent/ speaking in Spanish. ALSO: Pro-tip for this class, look up the books online/ rent them first because 3 are required and they are all expensive.

Very very nice professor. She is very conversational and the only issue with her teaching is that she gets off topic pretty often. She grades very leniently and as long as you show up you'll get a good grade.

This class was a great class to wake up to. The content was easy because it exactly the same as any spanish 5 ap class you would have taken. She has a great personality, and despite the lack of strict teaching, our class consistently had the highest test averages. I recommend taking this class for a gpa booster, and for a fun break in your day.
One of the easiest classes I've ever taken. If you actually want to learn Spanish, don't take her class. You won't learn anything. However, if you just want to get an A, go for it.
If you have to get through the class anyway, you might as well have a little fun doing it.  Is a very kind grader on the portfolios and in-class assignments.
Good teacher, easy class.
VERY nice professor, very cool and fun. Had lots of fun in class, and she made it very easy.

A great professor who cares about the students' learning. Passionate about his job and teaching history. The only issue with his class was when he created a pop quiz system halfway through the semester and cancelled lectures temporarily because we were unable to answer a question in a Zoom class. Otherwise, would highly recommend.

I took Dr. Wijeyeratne's class as an elective and am very happy with the decision. I looked forward to his lectures because they were very engaging and interesting. It is clear that he cares about what he is teaching and the learning of his students. Dr. Wijeyeratne takes participation seriously so expect to be an active member of class discussions

Had for SCLA 101. Reading expectations were high but doable with effort. Is an entertaining and engaging lecturer which prevent class from feeling dragged. Not a harsh grader for a class with 80% of grade put towards essays.

Very good prof. He does a good job keeping lectures fun, and clearly cares about his students. He's an easy yet fair grader, and gives you lots of time to complete the 4 essays which make up the majority of your grade.

Cannot recommend enough. Picked interesting books and was very knowledgeable. Assignments are fun and not hard, including a rap battle and a presentation of your choice. Chronically funny. Makes you think. I understood why communication classes are important. Made a lot of friends in class. ALL HAIL LORD NIBBLER.

Dr. Wijeyeratne taught not only important aspects of Japanese history but also more obscure stories. There was a lot of information covered in the slides, but his lectures were excellent. I would highly recommend anyone who is interested in history to take this course!

SUCH a good lecturer, genuinely the best I've encountered at this University. His lectures are so interesting. If you like history and are looking for a fun class, this is it for sure. He would always show up 20 minutes before class and take questions from students on anything from geopolitics to sushi and ramen. Can't recommend enough.
Great Guy, super enjoyable class. Wish I had taken his classes sooner. Can't recommend enough!

Very simply put, he is the best instructor I had this semester. If you're lucky enough to have him as your professor, you are in very good hands. If you put in genuine effort and you're interested in the projects, you will have a lot of fun and get an A. Lastly, ALL HAIL LORD NIBBLER!!!

This class was by far my favorite of the semester. I was encouraged to think in complex ways that I had never experienced before. Dr. Wijeyeratne gave me advice on how to improve my speech skills and in the future I will surely use them to my advantage. If I become a professional speaker I owe it all to him. 

ALL HAIL LORD NIBBLER!!

Hands down my favorite class of the semester! He is genuinely so funny and caring, and the material was easy and interesting. I wish I could take Dr. WIjeyeratne's class next semester. Seek out this professor for your SCLA classes AT ALL COSTS, and consider yourself immensely blessed if you are in his class.

ALL HAIL LORD NIBBLER!!

I took this class during the summer, and it was a pretty cool class. The class focuses heavily on East Asia (especially Japan) with technology. Overall, the class was pretty fun and you only have to write a few papers which are graded fair. Overall, pretty great, but there weren't any lectures as it was more self guided. Fits into a busy summer.

Wijeyeratne is a great professor. There was a 2,000-word paper, but it was fairly easy you got to choose the topic that you found interesting. The class is mandatory, so if you want a good grade, make sure that you go to class. The lectures are interesting, informative, and entertaining. The only thing is be prepared to work over spring break.
Awesome professor, lectures are always super interesting and never boring! Attendance and participation are mandatory, but if you do the readings and pay attention the class isn't insanely difficult.

261 was totally solid, she does have a weird grading rubric, but you can go back and resubmit to get credit, and working w/ PepsiCo is cool (even though they aren't very helpful lol). My only gripe is that it's an assigned group project for the whole semester, and mine has been slacking from the start. My only advice is to just start working early!

The assignment's grading rubric is extrme not clear, and the assignment always happened before lecture (you have to finish before learn the knowledge for this homework). When combine these two things, you will find even an AI will did better than you in the assignment. Also the professor will never tell why you lost your points in the feedback.

What a confusing class. Prof David was helpful during office hours but the entire semester-long project and lectures were super confusing from start to finish. She made video projects due the day after we get back from spring break. Communication was awful and grading system is weird, with everything pass-fail. Not hard, but strange - just be aware

Do not take this class. I have been confused the whole semester. Her lectures are dry and not helpful. Very little content and wants you to do a project with Pepsico where she hands out  Word and Excel documents to do it all by yourself basically. Avoid this class. If this is required for your major, good luck.

The structure of this class was not good. She kept students running simulation for almost a month without summarizing the takeaways from the simulation itself. In fact, I did not learn anything from this class at all. So, if you are in the process of considering to take this course, please think wisely.

The farther away from her the better. I didn't learn anything from her. She only reads PowerPoint and does not give grades or standard answers until the end of the term. The mentor is fine. She didn't even check out our group's initial mistakes until our mentor checked them out before the final presentation.  she's ridiculous.

Amy was helpful and had lots of industry knowledge to apply to lectures and the real world.  There were no exams, and the group projects were interesting.  At times her lectures can be a little dry. but overall a fantastic class and extremely relevant!

There is no textbook. Lectures have very little content. Not much more than the powerpoint slides and sometimes the exercises/quizzes require you to know more than what the lectures teach. What was most annoying to me was the inconsistency in grading. Short answer questions would be scored 0% or 100%, even though the question was subjective
Do not expect too much in her lectures. I have taken 2 of her classes and, to be honest, I feel like I could simply just read the slides at home by myself.......

Probably was not her fault but the design of the course, but the experience was not what I expected. She knows very well her stuff and has solid experience in the field, but probably her teaching methods are not the best.

In this class, you work with PepsiCo in a group of four basically the whole semester. I really enjoyed this project, and Dr. David was great. Her lectures were as a whole really helpful and she gave good feedback as we went along on the projects. You can tell she has a passion for supply chain and her students.

He was amazing and cared how students did in his class. He pointed out things from his lectures that would be on the test. Overall, kind man and tests are not bad. Attendance wasn't mandatory but he didn't record lectures so you would need to go so he can tell you what to highlight.

Best class and professor I have had at Purdue by a long shot I actually felt like I was learning and was interested in the material. He cares about students and is very approachable. Tells you exactly what will be on 50% of the test and the other 50% I would compare to anatomy class challenge wise.
Amazing teacher and great class structure. Just go to class and you will be fine. Study the lecture and you will do well. Very approachable and does not try to trick you on exams.

Fantastic professor; great course structure. Exams are content-dense, but he indicates important topics from his lectures to reduce your study burden. Guest lecturers are entertaining and informative; homework deepens understanding of his topics without feeling overwhelming. Fair, if not generous grader that keeps outcomes--and students--in mind.

Dr. Shannahan is a great professor! He gets great guest speakers. The class is not difficult. As long as you show up and pay attention, you will do just fine! Additionally, exams are open note. He is very accessible and responds quickly to emails.
He says tells you every question that is on the exam but her does not record lectures. Go to class and you will get 100s.

Overall, a great class! Interesting lectures with several guest lecturers, including the surgeon general. Dr. Shannahan is an overall nice guy who cares about his students, and gives good feedback on assignments and how to do well in the course. The course itself isn't anything terrible, an easy A or B if you show up and do all the work.

Dr. Shannahan is very fun and makes toxicology seem pretty interesting. Definitely go to lectures, as he explains there what information will be on the test. One of the most enjoyable lecturers I've had!

Dr. Shannahan is a nice guy, and in his lectures he is very specific about what will be on the exams. I was nervous taking this class, but he's made it very enjoyable. He has not graded the homework very hard so far, and the assignments are not especially difficult.
Overall a really well organized class and I really enjoyed the material and lectures. Shannahan is a nice guy and I would definitely take a class with him again!

Excellent professor!! Dr. Shannahan really cares about his teaching and is passionate about toxicology. I was a bit nervous to take the class but he was very approachable and helpful. There's 3 exams, including the final, in the class and 4 homework assignments. The class is slightly curved.
You will be fine if you try. The instructor is nice in person but not so much over email.
He was nice
Mobolaji is goated take his class. He is very kind and teaches well while always being encouraging.

Class was organized despite a few last minute changes. Lots of busy work assignments with few that are actually going to make you better at presentations. Finding live audiences is a bit of a hassle so make sure to be ready.

Professor Mobolaji was the best professor I have had at Purdue so far. He is so passionate about the course and knows that most of us are there just because it's required so his main goal is "just listen to your classmates and learn." (He says that a lot).
