It's hard not to get an A in this course. Take him if you can get him!
Very easy class and even nicer professor. Makes coming to class enjoyable and will respect your time and values. I would recommend taking any class that he teaches.
Great professor and very easy grader; however, he does not do the best job with responding to emails.

He truly represents what every teacher should strive to be. He cares about each students improvement and makes a direct effort to connect with all those in his class. He is my favorite professor on my schedule. Best guy!

Professor Mobolaji is super chill and always happy. He really wants the best for his students, and he is very understanding of his students. He grades super easy and gives amazing feedback. He lets students re-do presentations with no penalty if they blank the first time. He promotes a fun and stress-free environment. He is a great com professor.

Genuinely one of the best professors I've had at Purdue! She clearly cares about her students and is compassionate about the topic she teaches. She's also very sweet and kind! So many extra credit opportunities are given too to compensate for any missed classes.
If I had to choose a public speaking teacher, I would undoubtedly choose her. I'm an international student, and I received tremendous help and encouragement in her class.

Prof. Renkert is one of the best professors I've had at Purdue. She makes the class both interesting and easy for a gen-ed. She is clearly passionate about what she teaches and cares deeply for her students. I found her class to be my favorite this semester, and I think most people would be lucky to take a class with her.
Great teacher, I took the class because it was required, but she was so inspirational and amazing. I loved how driven she was by her research and her topics! She is super sweet!
Best professor I have had at my time at Purdue, actually cared about us learning.

I absolutely loved Dr. Renkert for SCLA 102. We rarely had homework, just short readings to prepare for class. She is a food anthropologist, so our speeches were geared towards food history and culture. She had great stories to tell from her trips to South America. 10/10 would absolutely take another class of hers again.

Can sometimes assign too many readings for homework, but she is learning to reduce it. The class feels a little off-topic for a public speaking class but, it's not too hard either way. Three solo speeches/presentations and one group presentation. Nice professor with lectures that give the class some variety. Try to participate when you can!
Very understanding and lenient! Grades very well too!
She is very nice and caring. However, she tends to talk a lot and sometimes go off-topic.

He's a funny and kind guy. The test are hard but he does give you the question before hand. The questions are out of a bank of about 450 though. It's an extremely hard class. Free response are easy if you pay attention but you will never know what he'll throw on their do you gotta memorize it all.

Dr. Pappas is very kind and actually really funny when you go to class. However, there were not a ton of opportunities to earn points. His MCQs on exams are given ahead of time and he will pick a certain amount to show up on exams. Short answers are impossible though...Try to do well in lab if you struggle in lecture, that's about to save my grade.

Dr. Pappas is a great professor who clearly enjoys what he teaches. Provides practice multiple choice questions you can use to practice/memorize before exams as he will reuse those same questions, and free response questions are based off his learning objectives. This made the course significantly easier to study for.

Dr. Pappas is a great person and lecturer. He keeps the lectures entertaining and makes the topics exciting! Would absolutely take another class with him, however, the exams are very difficult. He gives you everything you need, but that means memorizing 400+ questions. The open-ended is quite difficult, too. Study at least 2 weeks in advanced.

TAKE IT ELSEWHERE!!! Do not waste your time, money, and effort with him!! He is such a lousy professor because he truly does not care. He gives you 400 MC questions to memorize for each exam which if you get them all right you get a C on the exam. The free response are so hard that you don't have time/ can't guess what he will he throw on there.

He says on the first day that this class is a weed out class. He is a lazy and terrible professor who does not care about us learning anything. Take it anywhere else but here!! Do not put yourself thru misery! Your grade is based off the exams and if you don't do too hot on the exams- good luck. You memorize 400 MC questions to just get a 70%
I took nucl 502 and had a very difficult time with the exams and slides. His accent is very thick, which is not helpful either, but he is very kind and caring about his students.

Professor did not teach the course content well - packs huge amounts of information into lectures then expects you to memorize them for his exams. Overall, his exams are the majority of the grade but are very difficult to study and prepare for. While he is a caring and smart person, he is a poor teacher.

His exams aren't just hard, they're ridiculous! In his review he simply summarizes the 14 lectures he expects you to know for the exam, and then he has like 5 in depth question on very specific topics.

Revankar is arguably the nicest professor in Nuke. He cares about his subject and wants to help. But, he does have an accent, and when he gets excited, you absolutely can't understand him. Didn't have his exams because he was in Japan, which are apparently hard. He's very understanding if something comes up. Nice guy, but I couldn't understand him.
Find out who is grading labs- ours was cake. Also, get someone's midterms from the year before; they're very similar.
Very accommodating, will do whatever he can to help you succeed. Also very passionate about classics!!

Professor Barnes is genuinely so passionate about teaching history. He is by far one of the best teachers I have met. If you ever have any issues do not be afraid to let him know as he always does his best to help. The exams require some practice with maps however the class itself is fun and interesting.
Dr. Barnes does a great job teaching greek myths and stories. He is very harsh on test regrades and the cutoff for an A is 94%!

Super friendly professor. The exams are tough to study for though because there are a lot of terms you need to learn and I thought the content of this course was so boring. You also have to memorize a bunch of maps of ancient civilizations for some reason.
Helpful in every sense of the word
Unhelpful in every sense of the word

I took SLHS 306 with him which was a very good class. He loves the subject and was very knowledgeable about it, but it was a TOUGH class. His final was two-fold, one 14 page packet with cumulative material and one 12 page packet with the last unit material. He made the material very interest just a tough class. Go to class for the clicker points!

He's helpful, caring and puts a lot of effort to teach the class. The course content is fantastic always in relation with real life examples. In the process, the class becomes challenging.   Worth it!

I like Dr. D he was an interesting professor and lectures could sometimes get off topic but there were fun class discussions and such. Hand taking notes was required but it's not that bad. Projects are easy, and the exams aren't too bad.

A great professor and lecturer, although technology is banned in class and lecture attendance is mandatory, he leads a very engaging and interesting class, really helped with my note-taking skills too. The assignments aren't a lot of work but his exams are hard and require 6+ hours of studying each, just take good notes and you'll be fine.
Great teacher. Actually cares

Mandatory attendance, but lectures constantly went off-topic and felt disorganized. The class environment was unnecessarily rigid, with high expectations for student attention but little effort put into making lectures engaging or useful. Often felt more like a power dynamic than an actual learning experience.

Don't let the reviews scare you---Dejoie isn't easy but he's not incredibly difficult; just come to class, pay attention, and take notes. MGMT382 is a 3 credit class, but I would prepare to treat it like a 4-5 credit class. As long as you pay attention, put in a bit of effort, then you can probably get a B+ or A- if you're super locked.

Lectures are no-tech/no slides, handwritten note yourself, with limited study material. Reviewing is very difficult. Exams are closed-note and test very detailed points from what he said on class you must memorize exactly. Videos are provided to learn coding html, but you will need to write out the exact code during exam. Do Not Recommend!

Dr. D is an effective lecturer, who, if paid attention to, provides clear conceptualizations through use of personal storytelling. I found that paying attention in class and taking concise notes helped me to succeed. He is old-school, and prohibits technology, but I felt that minimizing distractions helped me to stay focused.

Dr. D is an effective lecturer who explains concepts well. His work sessions prepare you well for HW assignments. Exam is a big portion of the grade, is fully closed‑book/notes, and is quite difficult, with some very specific details tested--I think he takes pride in its difficulty. But he does give partial credit. I did learn a lot in the class.

Mr. Dejoie deeply cares about MGMT 382 and has a lot of time under his belt, however, this class is painful to sit through sometimes. NO TECHNOLOGY in addition to no lecture slides makes you writing in your notebook every thought he spurts out since it's most likely going to be on the exam. If you miss a class you better get the notes from someone.

RUN! You HAVE to take notes to do well. Practically no resources to learn if you miss class. All short-answer exam: questions require you to remember his specific ramblings/reasoning, not just "correct" answers (despite open-ended questions) - notes from previous sections can be different. No technology allowed - handwritten notes only. Very boring

Please put all the content in the PowerPoint presentation instead of relying on the professor to lecture, and don't even take notes. Not everyone is a native speaker. I have never seen an exam that is all short answer questions. The worst professor is the one you should choose. If you accidentally choose him, run away!

Absolutely HORRIBLE professor. He would ONLY reach out on Piazza and he would respond to issues with a lot of verbiage but little to no guidance. He loves to gloat about how hard his exam is but that is because it is all closed notes and difficult to prepare for. Mind you this is a Python course that can be for beginners. AVOID.

Group Projects are super easy but only worth a small part of your grade. His lectures are 10x more boring than any other teacher I've ever had. The tests (70% of grade) have questions that make you quote what he said in class which makes studying for them almost impossible. He loves to brag about how hard his class is and wants you to fail.

This professor made the course 10x more difficult than it should be. The material of this course is not bad, the way that it's structured is terrible. It doesn't feel like he wants everyone to succeed, even the peer evaluation is designed in a way where someone has to lose points even if everyone did equal work. Terrible

Switch to a different professor. There are a total of five assignments including the two exams worth 70% of your grade.  By the end of the semester I had grades for my first exam and first project.  Lectures are confusing as he goes on personal tangents often. The exams aren't too bad as they're purely based on lecture. Your notes are everything

By far one of the worst professors at Purdue. If you miss one of his lectures, you better hope the material covered that day isn't on the exam. His lectures are extremely boring and not engaging at all. If you need help, I suggest asking one of the TAs instead because he will grill you on how you don't understand the material.
Very specific questions on exam. He also doesn't curve the class average to a 3.0.

Dr. D expects you to go to every single lecture, and if you miss class, he won't provide appropriate resources for you to learn the material. The lecture slides cover less than 10% of what you see on exams. His lectures are unbearable to listen to since he goes on tangents on his days at Texas A&M and on personal tales with no relation to class.

No slides or outside resources for topics, and you have to go to class everyday or else you won't make it. Exams are the hardest I've ever taken since it's off of what we talked about in class and it's all open answer with absolutely NO hints. He is very intellectual but lectures are so heavy it's hard to know everything we are expected to know.

There are no slides at all, the exams are just based on his rambling for the entire class. The exams are worth 70% of your grade and the exam makes you refer straight from what he says in class. I wouldn't take this class if you can't learn from someone just sitting and talking.

All the content for the class is basically him rambling and you just have to try and keep up. Outdated lecture style. Often goes off on tangents and tests were hard. Project criteria makes no sense and you cannot equally grade groupmates. Avoid this professor, other lectures with different teachers have easier tests and a much easier overall class.

Some people don't like Dr D, but I enjoy being in his classes. He is very straight foreward and will tell you how it is and what he expects from you. If you follow along through the progression of the course and genuinely learn the code you will be fine. Don't work with a partner / copy others, just learn it.

Who would set a due date on Sunday at 12 am... Beware!!! The discussion board is due every week on Sunday "12 AM". Why can't he just tell students that it is Saturday 11:59 pm. I was too focused on "Sunday" and didn't realize it's actually due the day before. I don't know why but I feel like this is just tricking students into the wrong deadline.

The exams are challenging but in my section there was a significant curve that helped me out on one of the exams. Three group projects, but none of them take very long to complete. A few warnings: follow all directions on assignments carefully, and take detailed notes. Like, be way way way more detailed than you usually would.

Took this class online and it was very disorganized. There was no clear schedule for him uploading lectures. His lectures were not necessarily related to the material, but him talking about unrelated topics. Exams are said to be based on lectures, but the exam format makes them difficult.

The class is difficult no doubt, but prof Elliot does a great job of presenting all the material you need to know during lectures. The homeworks also line up with each lecture (a set of problems for every lecture; yes its a lot), so it is helpful to reinforce the concepts covered that week. If you go to lecture and do the homework you will be fine.

I'm a senior and have never seen any professors at Purdue being sooooooo organized. Very timely lectures, clear slides, HWs are sorted and numbered to the slide notes so you can just go review that specific day. HWs are generally harder that the Exams but he let you resubmit. this man loves his job and cares about U

Professor Elliot goes out of his way to curve his class to a C average. This means that even if you do better than his abysmal test averages (~40), you may still find yourself clawing for a decent grade. His lectures are hard to get through and attending OH is practically mandatory. Do yourself a favor and take this class with Melloch instead.

Exams were really rough and he ended up doing a backwards curve at the end of the class (a C went from requiring a 65 to a 67). That being said, he was a decent instructor and seemed to care about his material.
Exams are difficult, make sure to attend lectures and all of your recitations, the recitations greatly help understanding and memorizing.

Dr. Elliot is generally a good professor, especially for a lecture of about 900 students. I found his lectures to be a mix; sometimes they were good and easy to understand, but some lectures were equation heavy without really explaining what the equations meant. And of course, some were flat out boring. Overall, I think he does a good job.

Professor himself is ok. The material in this class is inherently complicated and difficult to understand, but the way that the class was taught doesn't make it any easier. You can look at the slides, but they're not very informative, and you can go to lecture, but he'll gloss over examples because he doesn't have enough time to do them.
He's OK. Nothing great, nothing bad.

He's aiight. The lecture slides aren't organized by him and don't really help, but he's definitely knowledgeable on the subject. Learn the content through the homework and don't copy directly from chegg, and you'll be fine.

His lecture is somewhat boring and hard to follow in logic and arrangements, and its contents are not enough for you to do your homework and exams. I basically learned the entire course by reading the textbook myself

Lecture slides are good but lectures are somewhat boring (not engaging). iClicker questions at the end of lecture and during recitation. As with most of these classes, do past exams and have a good crib sheet and you will do well.

The class was hard but Professor Elliot was very nice and helpful in office hours. Go to class because there are pop quizzes. Do a lot of practice exams and ask him any questions on past exams that you were confused by. He is very helpful and wants everyone to succeed.

Professor Elliot does not explain things well in lectures. Recitations are boring and also don't explain the subject matter. Homework isn't too bad, but there's a lot of it. Clicker quizzes at the end of lectures

Professor Elliot cares so much about his students. His notes aren't the greatest and aren't posted on Blackboard but if you go to his office hours he's extremely helpful and cares a lot about his students.

I barely passed this class with a C+. The class is exponentially easier if you take with Prof. Malloc. This class was harder than passing kidney stones. The profs notes were sub-par and handwriting seemed like he was inflicted with Parkinson's. His explanation of topics were too calculus heavy that did not help understand any of the material.

Lecture notes are written in the class and the hand writing is terrible and the explanation of concepts even worse. Exams and homeworks are tougher than stones & there are pop up quizes especially on days of low attendence. Average grade in my class was C and best of the students goy B's. Exams are not practical & tested your ability to do calculus

One of my least favorite professor's I have had a Purdue. As a person, he seems very nice and caring, however don't let that fool you. He is an extremely poor lecturer, and his tests are written obnoxiously hard (averages mid 30's) where you are not even able to demonstrate your knowledge of the subject. Homework's are awful and not helpful either
Very good professor, kind and helpful but the text book wrote by another prof. at Purdue is so bad with so many mistakes

Can't lecture. Says everything and what he writes down you can't read. I did well in the prerequisite class but after listening to lectures my understanding of those topics had decreased. Started reading the book during lectures instead of listening and did much better. Can teach transmission lines tho, meaning he's probably not a bad 201 teacher.
Old guy, good professor.
Professor Elliot is very fair and cares about his students.  His tests are pretty easy and follow the past tests he gives as practice.

His curves are the worst curves ever. Avoid! He is clear and whatnot; however, you do not get the grade you deserve with him. He probably curves the class upward instead of downward  sometimes and for sure he did not curve the class at all this time.

You need time to get used to him, maybe something like 15 lectures. Hes way too good outside of lecture, go to his office hours and you'll see why. His exams are pretty easy, but then again you need a bit of time to get to know his style. Once you know it, though, the class will be cake.

Awesome professor! Explains really well. Uploads lecture figures before the lecture, so you don't need to draw all the figures in notes. Very helpful during office hours. Just attend the lectures and go to office hours, and you will excel in this class!
ECE 201 is to this date on of the hardest classes I have taken at Purdue.  Professor Elliot did a great job of explaining the material and making it interesting.

COM 217 with Professor Goodrich was one of the most helpful classes I've taken for improving my communication skills. She created a supportive and engaging environment where I felt comfortable speaking. Her feedback was always clear and detailed. She treated every student with genuine care and respect, making the classroom feel safe and inclusive.

Goodrich was my favorite professor this semester. Her class was very fun and engaging and most of the class is spent doing ungraded activities with your peers. No long essays or long reading at all. Mostly speech based as for grades.

Com217! Initially, I was not confident in my speaking abilities and presentation skills. Dr. Goodrich was always open to providing helpful, encouraging feedback and making sure that I felt included and supported, along with my peers. Dr. Goodrich is an incredible lecturer, and is truly there for her students. Highly recommend taking her class!!
Interesting lectures. Homework is sometimes complicated.
Interesting professor and impressive lectures.
Professor Goodrich is very easy to understand and provides lots of feedback to make sure you succeed in class!
What I really appreciate was that she always provided clear grading rubric with detailed requirements. She is also a conscientious professor
Professor Negin was an outstanding teacher. Her lectures were consistently engaging and easy to follow, and she always made sure the assignment guidelines and rubrics were explained clearly.
This course is very international-student-friendly. The workload is well-balanced, the grading is generous, and most importantly, the content is professional and rich.

I took her SCLA 110 and 111. We had 1–2 homework assignments weekly—not too hard, but the workload isn't light. Got an A in 110, but an A- in 111 because I got only 50% participation for the second half, despite attending, participating, and submitting everything. The grading felt unclear.

I took both SCLA 110 and 111 with Dr. Goodrich and enjoyed her classes. She makes discussions engaging, explains things clearly, and genuinely cares about students. The field trips were a great bonus, making the course even more enjoyable. Highly recommend taking her if you get the chance!
Always ready to help whenever you need it and goes all out

I took SCLA 101 and SCLA 111 with her. She is an amazing professor who tries to understand her students individually. She will be lenient with your grade if you work hard and are honest about your work. She really helped me with my Presentation skills. Overall, I would recommend choosing her as your professor.
She is enthusiastic to teach class and willing to help and share her knowledge with her students.
She's really kind and caring. I took both SCLA 110 and 111 with her. As long as you do the homework and give effort, it's an easy A. She explains things clearly and makes the class enjoyable.
I took her SCLA 110 & 111 classes during my first year and it was amazing

The professor is really good for this class. I took 2 classes with her (SCLA 110 and SCLA 111).
If you participate and show efforts, it's almost guaranteed to get an A or an A+.

His lectures are actually very informative and assigns a lot of group work throughout the semester. He does not take attendance, but ensures you are in class by having you complete the group projects in person throughout the sem. The class can really help you to get to know yourself and others more, as he covers leadership concepts.

Professor J is great! Caring, compassionate, unique. His class would have been great if literally 95% of the students weren't lost in their smartphones all through class or if more participated or gave him the respect he deserved. He remained enthusiastic despite the lack of participation. He was appreciated for sure by me. Highly recommend.

Dr. Plaskoff had a very fun and easy class. The textbook is unneeded, as his lectures are very informative. Attendance is not recorded, though, so having flaky group mates will make this class much harder. He's super understanding, though. One of my favorite teachers so far.

Willing to tell the truth about people, but the most caring and entertaining teacher I've ever had. Lots of people have independently said this as well.
Class was fairly easy as long as you pay attention.

Very kind and passionate guy. I'm not sure if I really learned anything since a lot of it seemed like common sense, but it was needed for my major. Attendance isn't mandatory but sometimes there are group projects in class so you shouldn't skip too often. The textbook is lowk irrelevant.

Great guy, you can tell he is passionate about the topic. Lectures can get a bit boring after the first few weeks but you honestly don't have to listen that much. Tests are all online, there is a few in class group assignments that are very easy. You don't need to buy the textbook. Do the assignments and do decent on tests and you will get an A.

Extremely kind, interactive, and engaging professor. Class is straightforward and if you just go to lectures and complete assignments you'll get an A for sure. Course material is interesting and insightful mostly but lectures can get long and tiring though since they're usually nonstop 75 minutes of lecture.

Dr. J always comes to class ready and willing to answer questions and explains things very well. The first few classes are very important to attend as they build up the foundation for EVERYTHING in this class. You have group assignments, but they are easy and quick. Homework is graded very leniently and as long as there's effort you'll get credit.

Literally, go to class, turn everything in on time, listen to a certain extent, and it's a free A. I think the biggest thing is like, don't ask for extensions; he really doesn't give them, and you have to show up when there is a (random) group assignment; otherwise, it isn't necessary. He also talks about his personal life a lot, but it's chill.

Attendance is not recorded but there are group projects that you will not understand or be able to do if you don't go into class. He doesn't tell you when they are until the day of. There are 3 tests, and no final. The tests were easy (imo) and done on Brightspace. He gives everyone chocolate at the end of the semester which is nice.

Dr J. is my favorite professor I've ever had at Purdue- he truly cares so much about his students and what he's teaching and it makes you want to come to class. Harder to be personal when he's in a bigger lecture, but I've had him in smaller classes and he's awesome!

It is a tough lecture to sit through. Content is both easy and mildly interesting, but Professor Plaskoff's attitude toward students makes it very hard to like him. Class itself is easy enough and useful to real life. Attendance is not kept but there are group assignments on random days due in class, so I would recommend going.

Easy class. You are put into small groups that you will work with on assignments and you also have weekly individual assignments called RACO's. Very interesting material that you will take with you into your career. Tip: He HATES when people have phones out and he will call you out in the middle of lecture in front of everyone if you have one out.

Dr. J was an awesome professor. He doesn't like the average class setting, he prefers people to get to know one another as well as himself. He uses groups to make people get closer. Tests are online. If you plan to skip make sure it is not a group project day because the assignments are due in class. Overall, awesome guy! Also, he makes truffles!
Good class but the group project at the end is annoying

She is a very good professor and is very understanding and respectful. She is not a tough grader and gives great feedback after speeches. She is also very understanding of students not enjoying public speaking and makes sure everyone is comfortable with each other and tries extra hard to get rid of this fear many students have.

There is an array of instances where rather than owning up to her mistake to being vague, she gives you half credit and tells you to resubmit. For your final grade, you have to go back and forth in an email chain with her for her to realize her mistake even when all evidence was presented in the first email. She demonstrates severe incompetency.
I would definitely reccomend taking this COM class. It is obvious how much she cares about her students and is a fair grader.

She is a very kind and caring person. Her lectures are clear and well done. She will sometimes go off topic and it will be a fun conversation for the class. Public speaking can be hard and she understands that. So when grading she will definitely tell you what you did right and what you don't need to work on. Along with what you need to work on. !!
She is such a sweet professor and really cares about her students. As long as you practice a little bit for your speeches and follow the rubric you will do well.
Not Mean!!!
She is amazing!

Hannah is a great instructor! She's very kind, and shows it! When teaching material she doesn't move too fast or too slow, she maintains a reasonable pace. In my opinion, this class is fairly easy when you put in the effort to complete the tasks on time and practice. If you follow the rubric for the presentations, you should do fairly well.
Pretty cool

Hannah was such an amazing instructor. She truly cares about her students, their well-being, and how they can improve in her class. She grades fairly and gives clear feedback. If you put in some effort and follow the rubrics to a T, you will do well.

Prof. Salazar's class may not be the easiest class, but it was enjoyable to attend! She is very encouraging and her feedback is constructive. I'm glad that I'm done with the course, but it was genuinely fun (especially the in-class activities)!

I didn't think she was mean. The class wasn't the easiest thing ever, but not too bad. She gives very good feedback IMO, her grading comments always make me happy. Just look at the rubrics for the presentations and you will get the points.
Mean
Makes class interesting and fun to go to. Writing assignments do not have strict rules about structure so you can write with your own voice as long as your grammarr isn't bad

Dr. Robson creates an environment that allows for people to make great friends and have civilized, intelligent conversation. Every book for his class was a good read, and Dr. Robson is quite forgiving if you don't read. He's very kind, considerate, funny, and if you have him on your schedule, you've hit the jackpot.
he is okay, a good teacher. Class is chill and lots of reading but duable

Professor Robson is an amazing professor who truly cares about each of his students. Don't be shy to participate in the class discussions, make sure to not procrastinate on the essays, and make sure to buy the books early and print out the readings ahead of time.

Thomas Robson is the best English teacher I've had. He genuinely cares about his students and their well-being, offers many extra credit opportunities, and allows sick days without grade penalties. Your grade reflects your effort. Read the syllabus, bring materials, and arrive on time, as participation depends on attendance and readiness.

Very good Professor for SCLA 101. Homework was intermittent but not too much. A little bit hard on grading for papers, but these papers are not an overwhelming portion of the grade. Utilize office hours, and do extra credit. Lot of discussion in class, make sure to participate. Need to buy books, get them as early as possible.
BEST English teacher/professor I've ever had! Super easy classes with interesting discussion adn absolutely pressure-free.

Amazing professor who always made your time in class enjoyable with engaging lectures and fun papers that instill creativity in the students. Difficult grader but if you attend extra help and apply yourself you grade will reflect that. Also many extra credit opportunities. If you have the opportunity to take his class, take it.

Genuinely loved attending his classes as he always found some way to make them fun. He allowed us gain extra credit by attending any cultural event on campus (worth up to 5%), and published the grading rubrics long before the assignment deadline. If you have the option to take Dr. Robson's class, I highly recommend that you do.

Much of the class is spent reviewing and discussing the reading from the current text, and Dr. Robson does a great job ensuring that we understand the text before we go into deeper discussion. The writing part of the class is simple and straightforward for the most part, but make sure to go to office hours to check. Do extra credit.

Good teacher, he actually cares that you are learning how to write better. I had to get five books for the semester, two of which were new to the school book store and were quite expensive, 20-30 bucks. Although the They Say book is quite informative. He is a tough paper grader but gives lots of opportunities for extra credit by watching plays.

Dr. Zhang is an amazing professor. From day one she oriented the course around professional development, giving extra credit to go to events and engage with employers. She brought in alums to give chats. There are weekly cases with a quiz, a group presenting on it, and discussion. (attend) Sales simulation at end is good to test concepts/compete.

Took her class last year and just remembered how caring she was after having a bad experience recently with another instructor in the different course. She truly looks out for her students that show interest and is accessible outside of class. Also didn't have class on mondays (reading day) and the content wasn't that hard. Definitely worth it

Professor Zhang as a person is great. However, the average for the exams this semester about a 60%. The lectures she gives are not related to exam. The formatting of the exams goes as follows: A. Answer 1 B. Answer 2 C. Answer 3 D. All of the Above E. A and B. F. None of the above. Her grading is also 10% increments -- (90-100=A), (80-90=B), etc.

I think the main problem with this course is the way it's set up. 60% of your grade is based on two exams that are really hard for no reason. Other than that, Prof. Zhang is lovely just doesn't set you up for the material ahead.

Exams are super hard because of the answer choices; the average for the first exam was a 60%, and no curve was placed. I feel like her lecture don't help you learn the material. Just read the textbook.
She's a great professor—very kind and caring toward her students. The course isn't too difficult; just make sure to read the textbook and complete every quiz, and you'll be able to get a high grade.

All she does is talk about Elon Musk and Donald trumps tarrifs. Not to mention she loves China and won't stop talking about it. I've honestly had enough listening to her. Sounds like CNN or MSNBC. Take her class if you want to hear her bogus Chinese political rants.

She was alright, just make sure to do your homework and show up to every single class for participation points (sign a sheet). Study for the weekly quiz because it's like 85% of your grade. It takes max 2 hours to study so don't be lazy. Go to her office hours for extra credit and you'll get an easy A. No exams.

Ignore the course I selected, I took MGMT 324(Marketing) with this lady. She was by far the worst professor I have ever had. 80% of your grade was based on 10 difficult 30 question quizzes every week - no exams. Lectures were useless and she barely tried to explain the content. Was genuinely shocked when she said she's been a professor for 12 years

Kind, caring, and overall great professor. Answers emails in a timely manner, is always available to help and happy to answer questions. She genuinely cares about her students and gets to know them. Attendance is mandatory but the classes were pretty engaging and easy to pay attention during. One of my favorite retail professors at Purdue.
She has no clue what she is teaching at all, she just giggles and says okay over and over. She is very sweet just clueless.

Grades papers like she is an English teacher, not a retail teacher. Wish her presentational skills weren't so choppy and text book answered. REALLY wish her lectures wouldn't always start with a sentence about the weather.

A very fair professor, may be the best professor I have ever had when it comes to answering student questions.  Very good lecturer, optional homework, mandatory attendance and fair exams.  Would take this same class with him again.

Professor Ott did an amazing job of explaining concepts that were boring or challenging. He offers optional homework problems that align very close with exam material. He also posts practice exams that are super similar to the format of the mid terms. If you go to class, ask questions, and participate you should have no problems succeeding.

The material for this course is difficult and boring; I did not enjoy taking it in the early morning. However, the proffesor is great at explaining each topic, caring and effective at answering questions. I did feel surprised on any exams. Overall, great instructor, but you signed up for accounting, so be prepared to feel eepy.

If you do not have much prior accounting knowledge, this class may be challenging. The course is worth 400 points, with exams making up the majority of the grade, so exam performance is HIGHLY important. Quizzes are straightforward, and staying on top of the material is essential.
Professor Ott teaches exactly what you'd want to learn in this course. The material is overall difficult, but if you go to lectures and pay attention it should be no problem.

Only exams and quizzes are graded. Material is not easy nor hard to understand, you have to go to lectures, pay attention in class, and do the homework to do well. If you do those things, it'll be hard to get anything less than a B, since each exam is curved. Ott is good at what he does, he knows the material and can answer questions well.

Professor Ott is one of the best professors I have had here in my time at Purdue. His lectures are the most useful out of any class I take currently. Exams can be challenging but there is a curve and he provides a coupious amount of practice problems.

Pretty easy class overall. Professor Ott is a very nice guy and gave ample practice material when preparing for exams. There are like 7 at home quizzes that are very similar to in class problems, the 3 part project is very easy if you just follow the syllabus. Exams were very similar to the review material he posts which is very nice.

The content learned in this class is a joke.  Its simple topics that they try to overcomplicate.  Pro Shi was alright, the homeworks are easy, and most of class is spent reviewing the content from the previous day.
Talks down to students, impossible to access outside the class. Responds to any emails in a derogatory tone. Most possibly the worst experience I've ever had at Purdue.

I've found her to be rude and condescending. Ask her a question and she doesn't even look up from her phone. Email her and she replies like she's royalty who has been disturbed. It's honestly a travesty for Supply Chain Students that she teaches a core course for them, that must SUCK. Stay away, if you can.

Easily the best professor I've had here at Purdue. Even though this semester was different because of COVID, she still went above & beyond for her students. She genuinely cared about us & wanted us to succeed. The online video lectures were short & sweet & the in person lectures were extremely helpful.

She is very well prepared for class and she will be honest if she doesn't know the answer to something related to the lecture, which is unusual. She could go a bit deeper in the practicality of some topics but overall is very good as an intro class.

Really hard to focus, but if you know what is going on you will get a good grade. Exams are really long and you only have an hour - definitely a struggle. But they are just like the practice exams. Make sure you know your stuff or you will run out of time.

Nice guy but the course is horridly run. Communication is at an all time low, basically no materials were released for the final even though he said he would, grades for the project are nowhere to be seen. The course itself is fun, yet difficult, but the TAs and instructors make it nearly unbearable.
Dr. Swabey is a good person, but he is not that organized, and there were many unexpected changes to the course during the semester. I wish the class were more organized.

Prof. Swabey presents the material well. He tends to go on tangents, but they are generally relevant to the content and provide some interesting context/background. Most of the work in 337 comes from the labs and not the lectures/exams, which focus mostly on high-level concepts.

ADHD if he was an ECE professor.
I love the man and he is very smart but the lectures were useless and the course was very disorganized specifically the amount of work.





Bonus point your GTAs' name start wth a J, change your section, like immediately!
I am traumatized by this class.

Very bad professor. His lectures have literally zero enthusiasm, very robotic.. Pretty much reads off the slides in the most boring way possible... does not even have his own slides, since the whole course is adopted from Berkeley. Not sure why people like Prof. Bullins even get into teaching when it's clearly not their thing.

While I did not prefer the lectures (got a bit mundane). Prof brian is a very knowledgeable person and is very passionate about the topic. Talking to him outside of the class got me interested in a lot of his projects

The classes were very boring, with attendance dropping to around 15%. Grading was unclear, with poorly explained expectations. The content was very broad and shallow, lacking practical depth or applicability. For this material, CS188 from UC Berkeley is a better option, with clearer lectures and free online resources.

Pretty good professor who taught 471 for Spring 24. There were 4 homeworks for the entire semester, with no quizzes, so a straightforward workload. One midterm, one final. The material was well-presented, but the final was a killer compared to the practice exam. Luckily Prof. Bullins was a fair grader and his grading was generous. Would recommend.
